LOCAL AND PRIVATE ACTS
OF THE
STATE OF KENTUCKY ,
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN AND HELD IN
THE CITY OF FRANKFORT ON MONDAY ,
THE THIRTY -FIRST DAY OF DE-
CEMBER , EIGHTEEN HUN-
DRED AND EIGHTY-
THREE .
J. PROCTOR KNOTT , Governor.
JAS . R. HINDMAN , Lieut. Gov'r and Speaker of Senate.
CHAS . OFFUTT , Speaker of the House of Representatives.
J. A. MCKENZIE , Secretary of State.
P. W. HARDIN , Attorney General.
CHAPTER 800 .
AN ACT to prohibit the saleof spirituous, vinous,or malt liquorswithin
one mile of the school-house in common school districtnumber seven,
in Hart county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful hereafterforany person
to sell,by retailor otherwise, any spirituous, vinous ,or
malt liquors within one mile of the school -house known
as " Thompson Academy ," in common school district
number seven (7),Hart county; and itshallbe unlawful
for the county judge of said county to grant a licenseto
LOC . L.,VOL . II- 1

2 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
any person to sellany such liquors,in any quantity,with-
in said boundary ; and such license, if granted ,shall be
void,and no protectionto the person obtaining the same .
§2 . Any person violating the provisions of this act
shall be fined in the sum of one hundred dollars for each
and every offense, to be recovered by indictment by the
grand jury of said county ,or by warrant before any jus-
ticeof the peace for said county .
§3 . Any previous act conflictingwith thisact ishereby
repealed.
§ 4. This act shallnot become a law untilitisratified
by a majority of the legal voters of the said common
school districtnumber seven ,who may vote for or against
it at an election to be held in said district as hereinafter
provided for; and for that purpose ,the judge of the Hart
county court shall, by an order entered of record in said
county court, order an election to be held at Thompson
Academy ,in saiddistrict,on Saturday,theseventhday of
June , one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four, and
appoint two judges and a clerk to hold said election,
who shallbe firstduly sworn to faithfullydischarge their
duties as such . Said clerk shall prepare a poll-book for
said election, with two columns , one " for the sale of
liquor," and the other " against the sale of liquor" in com-
mon school district number seven , within one mile of
Thompson Academy ; and the clerk shall ask each voter
offeringto vote,"Are you for or against the sale of liquor
within one mile of Thompson Academy ,in common school
district number seven ? " and if the voter votes for the
sale of the liquor,his vote shallbe so recorded; and if he
votes against the sale of the liquor, his vote shall be so
recorded. And the officersholding said electionshallcer-
tifythe resultof said election, and return the poll-book
thereof to the clerk of the Hart county court,who shall
carefullypreserve same in his office; and on Wednesday
next after the said election, the county judge , county
clerk,and sheriffof Hart county shall meet at the county
court clerk'sofficeof said county , examine ,count ,and
compare the said poll-book ,and certifythe resultof said

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 3
vote,and thesame shallbe enteredof recordinsaidcoun-
ty clerk'soffice; and ifa majorityof allthe votespolled
at said election is against the sale of liquor within one
mile of Thompson Academy ,in common school district
number seven ,as shown by said certificate, this act shall
become a law and be in fullforce and effect. But any
person previouslylicensedto sellliquorsin one mile of
said academy in said districtshall be exempt from the
provisionsof thisact,and may continue to selluntilthe
expiration of said license. 1
§5 . The electionherein provided for shallbe held be-
tween the hours of eight o'clockin the morning and five
o'clock in the evening . Notice of said electionshallbe
given by postingwrittenor printednoticesthereofatsaid
Thompson Academy ,and at four other public places in
said district, for at least twenty days next before said
election .
CHAS . OFFUTT ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives.
JAMES R. HINDMAN ,
Speaker of the Senate.
Approved April 15, 1884.
J. PROCTOR KNOTT .
By the Governor :
J. A. MCKENZIE , Secretaryof State.
CHAPTER 801 .
AN ACT for the benefitof common schooldistrictnumber fifty-eight,
Wayne county .
WHEREAS ,By oversightof the commissioner of common
schools for Wayne county , thirty-one pupil-children were
omitted in the census report of common school district
number fifty-eight,forthe schoolyear ending June thir-
tieth, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four; there-
fore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Superintendentof Public Instructionis
hereby authorizedto draw his drafton the Auditor of

4 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Public Accounts ,in favor of J. W. Sallee,commissioner
of common schools for said county , for the sum of forty-
four dollarsand thirty-three cents ($44.33), on which
shall be paid thirty-nine dollars and forty-eight cents
($ 39.48), the full amount of unbonded interestdue the
county aforesaidon itssurplus bond ,and four dollarsand
eighty-fivecents($ 4.85),out of the common schoolfund
for the present school year ending June thirtieth, one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-four .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1884.
CHAPTER 802 .
AN ACT to prohibit the sale of intoxicating liquors in the town of
Merrimac , or in three miles thereof in Taylor county .
Be itenactedby the General Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any person to sellor
vend any spirituous,vinous , or malt liquors, or a mixture
of either,in any quantity, in the town of Merrimac , or
within three miles thereof, in Taylor county .
§2 . That any person violatingthe provisionsof this
act shall be subject to the penalties and punishments
which are imposed by statute upon persons guilty of re-
tailingand tippling.
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 15, 1884.
CHAPTER 804 .
AN ACT giving John C. Richardson,sheriffof Boyd county,untilthe first
Monday in May , one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four, to execute
a bond for the collection of the State revenue .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That John C. Richardson,sheriffof Boyd county,
be,and is hereby,given untilthe firstMonday in May ,
one thousand eighthundred and eighty-four,to execute

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 5
his bonds for the collection of the State revenue for the
year one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four; which
bond shallbe executed as required by law , and when ex-
ecuted ,he and his sureties shall be liable thereon as if
executed on the firstMonday of January last.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1884.
CHAPTER 805 .
AN ACT to incorporateJake Rice Lodge , number six hundred and six, of
Ancient York Masons .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Milton Sweatman , Minyud Holbrook ,and A.
M. Burton , as trustees, and theirsuccessorsin office,be ,
and they are hereby ,constituted a body -politicand cor-
porate , under the name and style of Jake Rice Lodge ,
number six hundred and six,of Ancient York Masons ,and
that the officersand members of said lodge and theirsuc-
cessors shallso continue and have perpetual succession,
and by the name and styleaforesaidare made capable in
law to contract and be contracted with ,sue and be sued ,
plead and be impleaded , and answer and be answered ,
in all the courts of this State and elsewhere ; to make
and have a common seal , and the same to break , alter,
or amend at pleasure.
§ 2. The said corporation shall have the right to take
and hold by purchase ,gift,devise,or bequest,real or per-
sonal estate,to an amount not exceeding five thousand
dollars, and dispose of and convey the same at their
pleasure .
§3 . The business and affairsof said corporation shall
be under the management and control of the trustees
aforesaid, and theirsuccessors, whose duties shallbe such
as are prescribed by a majority of the members of said
lodge .
§4 . The members of said corporation shallhave power
to pass such by -laws,rulesand regulations, as may be
necessary forthe safe-keeping of the property and other

6 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
interests of said lodge ,not inconsistent with the Consti-
tution of the United States or of the Constitution of this
State .
§5. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 15 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 807 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Jas.W. Johnson, clerkof the Rowan circuit
court .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That afterthe passage of this act itshallbe law-
ful for Jas . W. Johnson ,clerk of the Rowan circuit court ,
to practice law in all the courts of thisCommonwealth ,
except the Rowan circuitcourt.
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 15, 1884 .

CHAPTER 809 .
AN ACT authorizingand permitting G. W. Duncan to change the " State
Road ," in Trigg county , leading from Canton to Aurora .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That G. W. Duncan be,and he ishereby,author-
ized and permitted to change that portion of the " State
Road " in Trigg county ,leading from Canton to Aurora ,
as follows,viz : Commencing at a point about fiftyyards
east of said Duncan's residence,leaving the old road to
the south ,and running parallel with the old road about
four hundred yards to where it intersectssaid old " State
Road " again ; upon condition that said G. W. Duncan
shallfirstopen saidproposed new road and put same in
good condition and repair for public travel before closing
the old road .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 15, 1884 .
CHAPTER 810 .
AN ACT toamend an act,entitled' An actrelatingto the Old State Road
and Ripple Creek Turnpike Road Company , in Campbell county , and
to authorizea saleof a portion of said road to pay debts."
WHEREAS , By an act of the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , entitled"An actto amend
thecharter of the Old State Road and Ripple Creek
Turnpike Road Company ," approved February the six-
teenth,one thousand eight hundred and seventy-two ,said
company was authorized to borrow not exceeding twenty-
fivethousand dollarsfor the purpose of paying itsdebts
and to complete itsroad to the house of John Black , in
Campbell county , and to issue bonds thereon ; and
whereas ,said company was authorized to mortgage or
convey in trustfor the securityof saidloan thatportion of
itssaid road commencing at the Newport and Alexandria
Turnpike Road and extending to the house of said John
Black,a distanceof about eightand three-eighthsmiles;
and whereas,on the firstday of May ,one thousand eight

8 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
hundred and seventy -two , said company did issue its
said bonds for twenty -five thousand dollars,in denom-
inations of one hundred dollarseach ,due and payable in
ten years,with coupons attached,bearing interestat the
rate of ten per cent.per annum ,payable semi -annually ;
and whereas ,on the same day ,to-wit : on the firstday of
May one thousand,eight hundred and seventy-two ,said
company executedand deliveredto Benjamin Smith its
certain deed , in writing, whereby it conveyed to him
that portion of its road above described, to be held by
him in trust, as securityfor the payment of said bonds ,
and the interestthereon ; and whereas ,on the twenty-
sixth day of August,one thousand eight hundred and
seventy-three, an action was institutedin the Camp-
bell chancery court, wherein Benjamin Smith , trusteeas
aforesaid,and others were plantiffs,and said company was
defendant ,for the purpose , among other things, of en-
forcing said trust; and whereas ,such proceedings were
therein afterwards had , in said action , as resulted in a
sale by the master commissioner of said court of all of
said bonds to John W. Schneider ,Joseph H. Horner , and
W. M. Ware ; and whereas , by an act of the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , approved
April the fifteenth, one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-two ,entitled"An act relatingto the Old State
Road and Ripple Creek Turnpike Road Company ," the
circuitcourt of Campbell county was authorized and em-
powered to cause to be sold said road ,with allitsprop-
erty, rights, interests, franchises, privileges, and appur-
tenances ; and whereas ,on the sixth day of June , one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-two , an action was
begun in saidCampbell circuitcourt,wherein said John
W. Schneider,Joseph H. Horner,and W. M. Ware were
plantiffs,and saidcompany and Benjamin Smith,trustee
asaforesaid,were defendents; and whereas,such proceed-
ings were therein afterwards had as resulted in a judg-
ment in favor of plantiffsagainst said company , for
twenty -fivethousand dollars, with interestat the rate of
ten per cent.per annum from the firstday of May ,one

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 9
thousand eight hundred and seventy -two ,until paid , and
theircoststhereinexpended,lessone thousand fivehun-
dred and ten dollars, as of June the twenty -third, one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-two , and a sale of
that portion of said road above described ; and whereas ,
said portion of said road was , pursuant to saidjudg-
ment , sold by the master commissioner of said court on
the fifteenthday of August ,one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-two ,for the sum of four thousand one hun-
dred and fifty-six dollarsand ninety cents,and which said
sale was afterwards duly confirmed ; and whereas ,there
is stilla large indebtednessowing by said company to
said parties and others ; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the circuitcourtof Campbell county be,and
the same ishereby ,vested with allthe rightsto sellthe
remainder of saidturnpikeroad,under the same restric-
tions and for the same purposes , as authorized by said
act of April the fifteenth,one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-two .
§2 . The purchaser or purchasers at said sale shall be
invested with the same rights as vested in the purchasers
under said act,except that they shallorganize under the
name of the Claryville, Grant's Lick and Butler Turn--
pike Company .
§3 . Said company , when organized , may consolidate
with the Campbell County Turnpike Company ,and the
Newport and ClaryvilleTurnpike Company ,or eitherof
them ,a majorityof the stockof the respectivecompanies
consenting thereto .
§4 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 15, 1884 .
CHAPTER 811 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Versaillesand Midway Railway Company .

§1 . That a corporationishereby created,to be known
as " The Versaillesand Midway Railway Company ," by .

which name itmay contractand be contractedwith,sue
and be sued , have and use a common seal, alter and

estate,in fee-simple or otherwise,not exceeding two hun-
dred acres,and all personal property necessary,conven-
ient,or suitablefor the purposes of the company ; shall
have perpetual succession, and all other rights,immuni-
ties,and privilegesthat other railroadcompanies have ,
use,and enjoy .
§2 . The capital stock of said company shall not ex-
ceed fivehundred thousand dollars,in shares of one hun-
dred dollarseach ,which stock may be subscribed for and
taken by any person,individual,corporation,or company ;
and as soon as two hundred and fiftyshares of stock are
taken ,the company may effectan organization by the
election of five directors, who shall elect out of their
number a president,as hereinafterindicated.
§3 . Said corporation is authorized to build,construct,
and operate a railroadfrom the town of Versailles, Wood-
ford county ,Kentucky ,to a point on the Louisvilleand
Nashville Railroad at or near the town of Midway , with
power in saidcompany to extend the road from that point
to some point on the Cincinnati Southern Railway in
Scottcounty,Kentucky ,and alsowithpower in saidcom-
pany to extend said road from the town of Versaillesto
the town of Lawrenceburg ,Anderson county ,Kentucky ;
the company to selectthe most feasibleroutes. They
may build the road upon any gauge they desire,either in
whole or in part,and may double the track,or use both a
broad and narrow gauge in whole or in part. They may
build and operate the main line from Versaillesto Mid-
way ,and saidextensions,or eitherof them ,or any part
thereof .
§4 . That Thos .F. Forman ,Jno . A. Steele,Robt . Wal-
lace,R. G. Berry,Ed . M. Wallace , H. C. McLeod ,Jno . S.
Withrow ,Jno . A. Rogers ,R. S. Starks,W. E. Rogers ,and
James F. Calloway , or such of them as may act, are
hereby appointed commissionersto open books and re

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 11
ceive subscriptionstosaidcompany ; which books may be
opened at any time or place any one of them may choose ,
without any motive; and any one of saidcommissioners
may apply to, and receive a subscriptionfrom , any com-
pany , corporation,or person ,town ,city,district,or county
authorized to subscribe,upon any terms or conditions not
repugnant to law ,as such subscriber chooses to impose ;
the finalacceptance of said subscription upon the terms
or conditionsimposed to be left,however ,subjectto the
ratificationof the president and directors,and to be bind-
ing upon the subscriber after acceptance or ratification
by said presidentand directorsat any time within one
year afterthe subscription. And in case any of the com-
missioners aforesaid do not act,those who will may fill
the vacancy or vacancies by electionor appointment .
§5 . That allpersons,companies,and corporationswho
subscribe for stock in said company , and who reside, at
the time of their subscription,in any town , county ,pre-
cinct, or other territorialdistrictwhich may hereafter
subscribe and tax itselfin aid of said road,shall,upon all
tax assessed against such subscriber,be credited by the
amount he has subscribed to the extent he has paid his
individualsubscription; which shallbe done by the presi-
dent drawing an order to the tax collectordirecting a
delivery of the tax receipt; for which orders so drawn
said collector shall receive credit, but not commission ,
when taken by him from such subscriber,but from no one
else : Provided,however,That said orders shallnot be re-
ceived in payment of any one's tax except the person
actuallymaking the subscription.
§ 6. The company may acquire,by deed or lease,any
coal land or mines ,and may deal in coal at theirdepots
by purchase elsewhere and sale and delivery at the same
and surrounding country .
§ 7. The following sectionsof an act,entitled"An act
to incorporate the Cumberland and Ohio Railroad Com-
pany ," approved February the twenty-fourth,one thou-
sand eighthundred and sixty-nine,are now adopted and
made part hereof,so far as not inconsistentwith ,or repug

12 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
nant to,the provisionsof thischarter,viz: sectionsthree,
four, five,six,seven ,eight,nine,ten, fifteen,sixteen, sev-
enteen .
§8 . The board of directors may frame and adopt by-
laws for the regulation of the business of the company ;
fix the rates for freight and passenger transportation,
subject to such general laws of this Commonwealth as
now exist, or may be enacted for the regulation of such
rates .
§ 9. The company may receive, by donation or pur-
chase , all lands necessary for its road -bed , side-tracks,
depot ,and warehouse ,and other legitimatepurposes ,and
also materials necessary for the construction and mainte-
nance of the railroad and its appurtenances ; and if the
lands or materials necessary for the road -bed ,side-tracks,
depots,warehouse grounds cannot be procured through
voluntary agreement of the owners thereof,said company
may have the same condemned , and pay therefor in the
manner now provided by law .
§ 10. The said company may selland convey ,rent or
lease itsroad,privileges,and franchisesto any other rail-
road company ; or itmay buy or lease any other connect-
ing railroad and operate the same . It may connect or
consolidate with any other railroadbuiltor to be built in
orthroughthe Stateof Kentucky or any adjoiningState,
so as to form a continuous or connected line of railroad .
§11 . The Legislature reserves the right to regulate
rates of freightsand passage over the railroadhereby in-
corporated, and over any railroaditmay acquire by pur-
chase,consolidation,or otherwise,and the rightto amend
or repeal the charter hereby granted , or the charter of
any railroadconsolidated or otherwise acquired.
§ 12. That the employes and operators of the road ,and
personsreceivinginjuryto themselvesor propertyby said
road , are hereby given a lien on the rollingstock and
other personal property and assets of said company for
the payment of their wages ,or the amount of any con-
tractsenteredinto by such employes or operatorswith
said company .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 13
§13 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 15, 1884 .
CHAPTER 812 .
AN ACT toincorporatethetown ofBagdad, in Shelby county.
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The town of Bagdad ,in Shelby county ,is hereby
incorporated,and the limits of said town shall be one-
fourth of a mile in alldirectionsfrom the railroaddepot ,
as now located in said town .
§2 . The municipal concerns of said town ,with the
government and control thereof,shall be vested in three
trustees, a policejudge and a marshal ; the trustees and
police judge and marshal shall be elected annually on
the firstSaturday in May by the qualifiedvotersresident
within the boundaries of the town ,and the firstelection
shall be held on the firstSaturday in May ,one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-four; the officersto hold said
election shall be J. E. Hume , W. B. Long , and Hardy
Bohannan , two of whon shall act as judges and one of
whom shall act as clerk of said election . The result of
said electionshall be certifiedby said officers, who shall,
before acting, be sworn to faithfullydischarge the duties
of said offices,and shallbe filedby them with the trustees
elected,and the same shallbe kept on fileby said trustees.
The trustees, police judge ,and marshal so elected shall
be sworn ,before acting as such,to faithfullydischarge the
duties of theirrespectiveoffices.
§3 . The trustees of said town shall elect one of their
number chairman of the board,and they shall have
power to pass such ordinances and by -laws for the gov-
ernment of said town as they may think proper ,not in
conflict with the Constitution and laws of the United
States or the Constitution and laws of this State .
§ 4. The police judge of said town shallhave the same
powers for enforcing the ordinances and by -laws of said
town as are now vested in the police judge of the town of

14 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Shelbyvilleby the charter of Shelbyville for the enforce-
ment of the ordinances and by -laws of the town of Shel-
byville. The power of saidpolicejudge shallbe thesame
in penal and criminal matters in said town as are vested
by law in justices of the peace in this State; and the
powers of the marshal shallbe the same in likematters
as are vested by law in constables in this State ; but
nothing herein shall authorize any of said officersto levy
any tax
§5 . This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 16 , 1884.

CHAPTER 816 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to incorporate Central
University ."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The board of curators of Central University shall
consistof the chancellor and fifteencurators,which cura-
tors shall be elected, after this act takes effect, by the
Synod of the Presbyterian Church in Kentucky ,now in
connection with the General Assembly of the Presbyterian
Church in the United States, commonly known as the
Southern PresbyterianChurch; and of the persons so
elected three shall be elected to serve one year , three to
serve two years,three to serve three years, three to serve
four years, three to serve five years. Vacancies in said
board shallbe filledby the board itself,untilthe next reg-
ular meeting of the Synod . The Synod shall hold the
electionof curators at itsregular annual sessions; but if,
during any year ,the Synod ,for any reason , failto elect,
the curatorspreviously elected shallcontinue in officeun-
tiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified. Two -thirds
of the curators must be members of the Alumni Associa-
tion, and also citizensof Kentucky . The chancellor of
the University shallbe electedby the curators,shallbe ex
officiomember of the board of curators, and , when pres-
ent , its chairman .
§2 . The board of curators is hereby invested with all
the rightsand powers ,and on itare imposed allthe obli-
gations ,duties,and functions now by law vested in and
imposed on the curatorsof CentralUniversityand on the
trusteesof Central University.
§3 . The chancellor and all professors, instructors,
teachers, and officersof Central University, in all its

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 17
departments,and of allthe Universityschools,shallbe
elected and removed by the board of curators.
§ 4. Eight members of the board of curators shallcon-
stitutea quorum for the transaction of business; but said
board may appoint from their own number an executive
committee of not less than five, of whom the chancellor
shall be one ,and chairman of the committee ; which com-
mittee shall have , when the board of curators are not in
session, allthe rights and powers residingin the board :
Provided ,however,That the acts and doings of said com-
mittee shallbe reported to the board at itsnext meeting ,
and be subject to itsapproval .
§ 5. The corporation shallsue and be sued ,convey and
accept conveyances , receive donations , enter into con-
tracts,and execute officialinstruments,under the name of
" The Central University of Kentucky ," and by that name
and styleshallbe known .
§ 6. All contractsmade by the trusteesof CentralUni-
versity,or by the curators of Central University, or by the
Alumni Association,or by any person for the benefit of
Central University, and all subscriptions, conveyances,
donations , made or received for the benefit of said Uni-
versity, shallbe enforceableby the board of curators
elected under this act .
§ 7. This act shallnot take effectuntilithas been ac-
ceptedby the Alumni Associationof CentralUniversity,
and by the PresbyterianSynod of Kentucky ,commonly
known as the Southern PresbyterianSynod ; and when
thisact isaccepted ,then allparts of the act to which this
is an amendment inconsistentwith the provisions of this
act shallstand repealed; and when the board of curators
elected under thisact shall have been organized , the cu-
ratorsof CentralUniversityand the trusteesof Central
University existing under the act hereby amended shall
cease to have or to exercise any powers ,duties, or func-
tions under or by virtue of said act,except so far as may
be necessary to turn over to the board of curators the
books,papers,assets,and propertyin theirpossessionor
held by them .
LOC . L., VOLI - 2
Approved April17,1884.

ison county court clerk'soffice.

CHAPTER 820 .

liquorsin the town of Skilesville, and in the common schooldistrict,
in Muhlenberg county,of which saidtown isa part.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth

§1 That hereafterno spirituous,vinous,ormalt liquors
shall be sold in the town of Skilesville and in the com

20 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
mon school district, in Muhlenberg county , of which

lentprescription,shallbe punished by a fineof not less

urday inJune,one thousandeighthundred and eighty-four.

And if a majorityof saidlegalvoters,votingon the ques-
tion of approving or disapproving this act,shallvote in

and eighty-four ,appoint two judges ,a clerk,and a sheriff
of saidelection,who shallhold said election. If any so ap-

icate to be entered on the order -book of his court .
