CHAPTER 821 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Buzzard Roost Fence Company , in Daviess and
Henderson counties .
WHEREAS , A large portion of that sectionof land in
Daviess county, Kentucky , and that part of Henderson
county ,Kentucky , adjacent thereto, east of Green river,
and south of the Ohio river, bordering thereon, is, and
has been subjectto disastrousoverflows from both of said
rivers; and whereas ,especiallyfor the lastfew years,the
floods of said rivers have demonstrated the fact that fenc-
ing of the separate tractsincluded in the bounds herein-
afterdesignated isimpracticable,and many farms in said
bounds are now without fencing or any means thereon out
of which fences can be constructed; and whereas ,itisbe-
lieved to be to the interest of the land -owners in said
bounds that one common fence should be constructed
and kept in repair, and the cost of same should be appor-
tioned equitably among the land owners in said bounds
according to their respective interests; and for thispur-
pose itisnecessary that such rulesand regulationsshould

22 LAWS OF KENTUCKY ,
exist as will protect the common fence from damage or
injury, by negligent or evil-minded persons ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That all persons who are now owners of land ,
and such other persons in their stead as may hereafter
acquire titleor ownership of land within the following
boundary , viz :
§2 . Beginning on the bank of the Ohio riverat Gran-
villeGuthrie'supper corner; thence southwardly with
the Roost road to the south margin of the Berry road at
Mrs. Nelson's southeast corner ; thence with the Berry
road westwardly along the south margin of said road to
W. M. Fuqua's east line; thence with said line south-
wardly to said Fuqua's southeast corner in the Lancaster
road ; thence westwardly along the north margin of said
road to said Fuqua's southwest corner ; thence south-
wardly with William Sands ' east line to his southeast
corner ; thence westwardly along the north margin of the
county road bordering on the south line of the lands
owned by William Sands and W. W. Herr , to W. W.
Herr'ssouthwestcorner; thence southwardlyalong J. H.
Rudy's east line to his southeast corner; thence west-
wardly along the south line of the lands owned by J. H.
Rudy ,J. J. Berry ,James E. Smith , the land known as the
E. T. Berry farm , and the land of H. D. Bellew , to said
Bellew's southwest corner ; thence with and along the
north margin of the Berry road to the point where it
crosses the Booneville road near B. T. Kelly's; thence
with the Booneville road ,along the west side thereof,
southwardly ,to the Henderson road ; thence westwardly
with and along the north margin of said road to Green
river ; thence down said river to the line of the Scuffle-
town Fence Company ; thence with the lineof saidcom-
pany to the Ohio river; thence up said river to the
beginning .
3. That they be, and are hereby, incorporatedand
constituteda body -politic,under the name and styleof the
" Buzzard Roost Fence Company ;" by which name and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 23
titlethey shall have power and authority to sue and be
sued,contractand be contractedwith,and do any and
allactswhich an individualperson might do,in further-
ance of the objects of thiscorporation, viz : to build and
construct and keep in repair, from year to year ,one com-
mon fence, on the line of the boundary described, and
constructand establishgates at such pointswhere said
common fence crosses the public highway .
§ 4. The businessofsaidcorporationshallbe controlled
and managed by a board of officers,viz: president, sec-
retary, and treasurer,duly electedby the land -owners in
saidboundary,any two of whom shallconstitutea quo-
rum for the transaction of business , and whose term of
office shall be one year from the firstelection, and suc-
cessively thereafter until their successors are elected
and qualified.
§5 . Every person who is the owner of land in said
bounds at the time of an election shall be entitled to one
vote for each one hundred dollars assessed value of land
owned , and may vote in person or by written proxy . In
cases of decedents' estates,executors, administrators, and
guardians shall be entitledto vote as prescribed. Ama-
jority of the votes cast shalldecide the result.
§6 . The board of officersshall have power to make
and establish all needful rules and regulations for the
management and control of the business of said com-
pany ; and may enact by laws and impose penaltiesor
finesfordamage or injury to said common fence.
§ 7. It shallbe the duty of the secretary to keep in
a book for that purpose a listof the names of the land-
owners in said bounds ,together with the number of acres
of land owned by each , and the assessed value of same
as shown by the assessors' books ; and ,to raise funds for
the construction of said common fence,the board of offi-
cers shall have power and may levy an ad valorem tax
upon the lands in the bounds aforesaid,under said assess-
ment of that year or the previous year .
§ 8. The treasurershallcollectand accountforand pay
over allassessmentsmade and deliveredto him ,as pro-
>

24 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vided for in the next preceding section of this act; and
he shall have the same powers by law and the same
mode of procedure to make said collections which consta-
bles have in the collectionof claims placed in theirhands
for collection. And , in addition to the power conferred
on the treasurer to make collectionof said assessments,
in case of failureor inabilityso to do ,the same shallbe
a lien on the land so assessed,and enforceable against
same as judgments of justicesof the peace are enforced
by the sale of land . It shall further be the duty of the
treasurer to collectallfinesand penaltiesimposed by the
board of managers of said company , and pay over the
same and allother money coming into his hands under
the order of the official board .
§ 9. It shallbe unlawful for any person to break or
throw down said common fence,damage or wantonly or
negligentlyleave open said gates belonging to said com-
pany ; and any person so offending shall forfeitand pay
to said company ,for each and every such offense,not less
than fivedollarsnor more than twenty dollars,and ,in ad-
dition thereto,shallbe liable for all damages which may
ensue from such violationof the company's rights,recov-
erable before any court of competent jurisdiction.
§ 10. H. C. Elliott,Jno . M. Herr ,Thos .J. Childress,and
Nat .G. Stanley are hereby appointed commissionersfor
the purpose of organizing said company , any three of
whom may act. Said commissioners , after giving ten
days ' notice,in writing,posted at two or more of the most
public places in the boundary aforesaid, shallmeet at
the place ,on the day named in said notices,between the
hours of ten in the forenoon and two in the afternoon,
and proceed to hold an election for president,secretary,
and treasurer, and the person obtaining the largest vote
forthe respectiveofficesshallbe duly elected thereto,and
the said company shall be thereby and then duly formed
and organized . All subsequent electionsshallbe held on
the firstMonday in June of each year. Any vacancy
occurringin the officeof president, secretary,or treas

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 25 .
urer,shallbe filledby the other two untilthe next regular
election .
§11 . The Scuffletown Fence Company may , by a vote
of a majority of its members ,consolidate with this com-
pany , a majority of this company assenting thereto, and
the consolidated companies shall be known by the name
of this company , and shall have all the powers , rights,
and privilegesset forthin theirrespectivecharters.
§ 12. The board of officersmay prescribe the time
within which stock shallnot be permitted to run at large;
and any stock found roving about within the boundary of
said company afterthe time prohibited,shallbe taken up
and kept at the expense of the owner until the owner
shallcome and pay chargesforthe keeping,and such ex-
pense shallbe a lien upon the stock; and the board of
officersmay establishone or more pounds forthe keeping
of such stock; they may impose such finesand penalties
and establishsuch rulesand by-laws as may be necessary
to carry out the object of this act. When any stock shall
be taken up ,a written or printed descriptionof said stock
shallbe posted in two conspicuous places in said bound-
ary; or,if the owner is known , a notice given to him or
her will be sufficient. If the owner of the stock shall
refuse to pay the charges, or if no owner shall appear
within twenty days,then the company shallhave the
rightto sellsaidstock,afteradvertisingat leastfivedays,
and afterdeductingallexpenses,keep the remainder sub-
ject to the order of the owners .
§ 13. This act shall take effectwhenever itshallhave
been ratifiedby a majority of the qualified voters and
land -owners residing within the boundary herein pre-
scribed and voting at an election to be held as soon as
may be afterthe passage of this act; and to ascertainthe
sense of such voters and land -owners ,itshallbe the duty
of the county judge of Daviess county to cause pollsto
be opened at Oakfort and at the pointheretoforeknown
as Lancaster's store, on the Buzzard Roost road , near
the land of Carrice and Simmons ,to appoint officersof
and fixa day for said election,and to give ten days ' no-

26 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ticethereof,by noticesposted at the two voting places
aforesaid,and published at leasttwice before said election
in the Owensboro Weekly Messenger ; and said election
shallbe conducted ,and the pollsthereof compared , as in
elections for Representatives, and the certificateof the
result of said election shall be returned to the county
court and spread upon the records thereof.
Approved April 17,1884 .
CHAPTER 822 .
AN ACT to organizeand establisha system of common and graded schools
in the town of Dover , and in common school districtnumber two , in
Mason county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the boundary of common school districtnum-
ber two , in Mason county ,be enlarged and extended so
as to include allthe inhabitants within boundary : begin-
ning on the Ohio river,the corner of Mason and Bracken
counties; thence with the boundary line between those
counties a distance of two and one -half miles ; thence
four and one -half miles east ; thence two and one -half
miles north to the Ohio river; thence down the river to
the beginning ; which boundary shall be and remain the
boundary of said common school districtuntilthe same
ischanged by an act of the Legislature of Kentucky .
§2 . Said schooldistrictshallbe a body politicand cor-
porate ,under the name of " The Trustees of the Dover
Graded and Common School ," and as such , and by that
name ,may sue and be sued ,contract and be contracted
with,and exerciseallpowers necessaryto carryout and
effectuatethe purposes for which it is organized . The
titleto all common school property within said district
shallvestinsaidcorporation,and itisherebyauthorized
to use, occupy ,and control allsuch property , and to take
titleto any propertythat may hereafterbe conveyed to it
for the use and benefit of the common schools of said dis-
trict, and to draw and expend in the support of such
schools so much of the common school fund of the State

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 27
as may be due said district,from year to year ,under the
common school laws regulating the distributionand ex-
penditure of said fund.
§3 . Said corporation shallbe under the exclusive con-
trol and management of a board of five trustees, to be
elected by the persons resident within said districtquali-
fied by law to vote for common school trustees. The
first election for trustees under this act shall be held at
the school-house in Dover ,on the firstSaturday in June ,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four, as, and in
the manner school trusteesare now required to be elected
by the general laws of the State. So soon as these trus-
teesare chosen,they shallqualifyby taking the oath as
prescribed for common school trustees, and draw lotsto
ascertain which of them shall hold officefor one year ,
which for two years,which for three years ,which for four
years,and which for fiveyears; itbeing the intentand
purpose of this act to require one trustee to be elected
annually ,on the firstsaturday in June ,for the term of
fiveyears afterthe firstelection. Should a vacancy oc-
cur at any time ,by death or resignation,such vacancy ,
for the unexpired term ,shall be filledby the remaining
members of the board .
§4 . The trusteesshallorganize,within two weeks after
theirelection,by electingone of theirnumber chairman,
and such other subordinateofficersas they may deem
proper; and they may requireany of saidofficersto give
bond ,with personal security,to insure a proper discharge
of such duties as may be assigned them . The board is
authorizedto pass such by-laws as it may deem proper
or necessary to carry out the purposes of thisact,not in-
consistent with the Constitution or laws of the State .
§ 5. Itshallbe the dutyof thisboard toorganizeand
maintain a system of common and graded schools within
said district,and to have the same taught for ten months
in every year , to which every white childin said district,
within the common school age , shall have free access,
and the rightto attend free of charge at all times, as he
might have the same rightswere thisschool taught under

28 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and pursuant to the provisions of the laws of the State
which regulate the common schools of the State. This
board of trusteesis also authorized to organize and main-
tain a separate common school forthe benefit of the col-
ored children within said district .
§ 6. Itshallbe the duty of the board to have a census
taken and the number of scholars within the common
school ages resident within said districtascertained, in
the month of April,every year , and to report the number
to the Superintendent of Public Instruction,and to make
such reports of their school as are required from school
trusteesunder the general laws of the State.
§ 7. The said board of trustees is authorized to levy
and collectan annual poll-tax, not exceeding two dollars
on each poll,alsoan annual tax not exceedingfiftycents
on each one hundred dollars in value of all the taxable
property within said district, as the same is assessed for
Statepurposes,also,the net proceedsof allfinesimposed
for infractionsof the criminal and penal laws of the
State, within said district,afterpaying costs and commis-
sions, shall be paid to said trustees, for the use of the
schools within said district. That collected from the
white people shallbe used in the maintenance and sup-
port of the schools established for the benefit of white
children, and that collectedfrom colored people shallbe
used for the maintenance and support of such schools as
may be established for the benefit of colored children.
The tax thus levied shall be listed with the sheriff of the
county of Mason , whose duty itshallbe to collectthe
same and pay the money to the board of trusteeswhen
collected, and he and his suretiesshall be responsible
therefor,as in case of other taxes : Provided,however,That
the provisions of section seven ,so far as itapplies to tax-
ation,shall not take effectuntil ithas been ratifiedand
approved by a majority of the legallyqualifiedvoters
residingwithin said district,and voting at any electionfor
the consideration of that section, and the county judge of
Mason county and the justicesof the peace of Dover pre-
cinct,are authorized to arrange for and order an election

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 29
for the consideration of said section,giving public notice
by printed handbills, at least ten days before said elec-
tion ; and if a majority of the legally qualifiedvoters,
voting at said election,vote in favor of the tax , as pro-
vided in section seven , it shall thereafter be in full force
and effect .
§ 8. The board of trusteesis hereby authorized to es-
tablish schools on such plan as they deem best; to alter
and change their plans as often as they wish ; to employ
teachers and discharge the same ; fixand change salaries,
and generally to manage and control the common schools
within said districtas they may deem it to be to the in-
terest of the public,but in conformity to law and to the
rights of allpartiesinterested. Said trusteesshall make
an annual settlement with the county court of Mason
county , and in such settlement show what moneys they
have received and paid out under the provisions of this
act, and produce vouchers for allpayments made .
§ 9. The trusteesshall have the power to admit schol-
ars from other districtsto the school,and to assesstheir
tuitionfor the privilegeof attending the same .
§ 10. This act shalltake effectfrom the date of itspas-
sage .
Approved April 17, 1884 .
CHAPTER 823 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the public roads in Sulphur precinct, in Henry
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . Itshallbe the duty of the officersof the electionin
August ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,to
open a pollat the voting place in Sulphur precinct,in
Henry county,and ask each voterwhether he votesforor
againstworking the county roadsin saidprecinctby tax-
ation,and to recordthe votes; and ifa majorityof those
votingupon thisquestionin saidprecinctvotein favorof
working the roadsby taxation,then theprovisionsof this

30 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
act shall be in force in said precinct,otherwise the pres-
ent law in relationto publicroads shallbe in force.
§2 . If the majority vote to work the roads by taxation,
the county judge of Henry county,in conjunction with
the magistrateof saidprecinct,shall,at the firstterm of
the county court after the tax has been voted,appoint a
superintendentof county roads in said precinct, who
shallhold his officeduring the pleasure of the appointees ,
and whose duty it shall be to oversee all county roads
within said precinct,keep them in good repair,remove
all obstructionswhich may be placed upon them ,and
open or change any road that the county court may direct,
as provided in chapter ninety-four,articleone,of Gen-
eral Statutes .
§3 . The superintendentshallhire as many hands as
may be necessaryto work in a proper way the county
roads in said precinct and oversee them while at work ;
shallpay thesmallestwages possibleto securegood hands,
and require them to work nine hours every day ; may
hire the necessary teams , wagons , and plows , and pur-
chase alltoolsand implements necessaryto aid in work-
ing the road , which ,when purchased ,shall belong to the
precinct: Provided, That the amount expended in said
precinct in one year ,under the provisions of thisact,shall
not exceed the amount raisedin said precinctby taxa-
tion for thispurpose .
§4 . The superintendentshall annually make to the
countyjudge a fullreportshowing the amount of money
received by him ,the roads he has worked ,the number of
hands employed and wages per day paid ,the names of
those who work out part or allof theirtaxes,the charac-
ter of toolsand implements purchased and pricespaid
therefor, the amount expended for hire of teams ,& c., the
number of days he was actuallyemployed ,and allother
expenses actuallyincurredby him in the dischargeof his
duty; and he shallbe paid a reasonablecompensation for
hislabor,tobe fixedby the appointees,not to exceed two
dollarsper day .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 31
§5 . The levy courtshall,when theyfixthecounty levy,
assess a tax of two dollars and fiftycents on each male
person over the age of twenty -one , and a tax of twenty-
five cents on each one hundred dollars ' worth of taxable
property in said precinct. This tax shallbe collectedby
the sheriffat the same time and manner as the county
levy,and he shallpay the tax collectedunder thisact as
soon as collectedto the superintendent of said precinct,
and shall be liable on his official bond for all failures to
collect or pay over, as herein provided ,the same as in
actions against him concerning the collection and pay-
ment of the county levy, and shall receive the same pay
a3 for collectingState revenue .
§6 . The superintendent,before entering upon the dis-
charge of his duty ,shall execute, with good security, to
the county judge ,a bond conditioned for the faithfulper-
formance of allthe duties imposed upon him by this act.
Said bond shall be of the same character as the bonds
executed by the sheriff, and shall be recorded by the
county clerkin his office, and he and his suretiesshallbe
liable on said bond to any persons aggrieved by a breach
of its condition .
§7 . The superintendent shall,in working roads, give
the preference,first,to turnpikes that have been turned
over to the county , then to other leading roads in the or-
der that they are the most traveled and necessary for the
convenience of the public; and it shall be the duty of
the superintendent of said precinct to give the refusal
of employment upon any of the roads he works to the
persons residentin said precinct, and who have been as-
sessedfortaxationby virtueof the provisionsof thisact,
provided such persons can be employed at the same price
as other hands of equal efficiency; and the certificateof
the superintendent given to such persons shallbe taken
by the sheriffin payment of,or as a creditupon ,theroad
tax due by such persons . The certificateshallshow the .
number of days he has labored and price paid per day .
He shall also hire teams ,& c .,to aid in working roads,
from tax -payers under thisact,provided he can get them
4

32 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
as cheap as from other persons,and shallgive them a
certificatein payment of such hire,which certificateshall
be taken by the sheriffin payment of hire, or as a credit
upon the road tax due by such persons. The certificate
shallshow what was hiredand pricepaid therefor.
§ 8. The superintendent shallnot buy or speculate,di-
rectlyor indirectly,in claims due persons for services or
articles furnished under this act , and he shall take from
every person to whom he pays money a receiptshowing
the actual amount paid,and said receiptshallaccompany
his report to the court.
§9 . The county levy court,before levying the county
levy in any year,shallfirstascertainthe amount neces-
saryto be raisedfor county purposes,exclusiveof road
claims,and shallonly levy on persons livingin saidpre-
cinct a sufficienttax to pay theirpro rata part of the nec-
essary expenses of the county exclusive of road claims .
Itshallbe theduty of the sheriffto furnishsaidsuperin-
tendent with a listof the delinquents in said precinct,and
said superintendent shallhave the same power now given
surveyorsof roads to callthem out and allow them one
dollarforeach fullday'swork untiltheirtax ispaid. Such
delinquents,on failureto work , when notifiedby the su-
perintendent,shallbe liableto the finesand punishments
fixedby the General Statutes of Kentucky .
§ 10. All roads in said precinct shall be worked under
the general law for road -working as is provided in the
General Statutes of Kentucky ,untilApril first,one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-five, at which time the
superintendent herein provided for shallcommence work
under this law .
§11. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 17 , 1884 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 33
CHAPTER 824 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Southwestern Natural Gas Fuel Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly ofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. P. Devon ,C. W. Stone,C. H. Noyes ,J.
O. Marshall , John E. Hamilton , R. B. Carothers, J. J.
Hetsch , and L. E. Casey , and theirassociates,successors,
and assigns,shallbe ,and are hereby ,created a body -poli-
tic and corporate,by the name of the Southwestern Nat-
uralGas Fuel Company ,with power and in thatname to
contract and be contracted with,to sue and be sued , to
plead and be impleaded, to answer and defend, in all
courts and placeswhatsoever,as a naturalperson; and
to have and use a common seal,and to change and renew
or alterthe same at pleasure; and to ordain and put in
execution such by -laws , rules and regulations, for the
government of its affairsas it may deem expedient and
proper ; but the same may be altered or amended , at any
regular or calledmeeting,by a majorityof the paid-up
stock so voting , after a motion to that end shall have
been entered at any regular or called meeting held not
lessthan thirtydays before: Provided,however,That such
by -laws ,rules and regulations,shall not be inconsistent
with the Constitution and laws of this State or of the
United States .
§2 . That the capital stock of said company shall be
fixedby the corporationaforesaid,but shallnot exceed
the sum of one hundred thousand dollars , divided into
shares of fiftydollarseach . When the sum of five thou-
sand dollars is subscribed and paid up , said company
shallhave authority to organize and commence business.
The shares of said stock shall be personal property, and
transferableon the books of the company according to
the by-laws; but the company shallhold a lien on the
shares of any stockholder who may be indebted to it,and
such shares shall not be transferable without the con-
sent of the president and directors until such debt shall
have been paid or discharged. Each share shallentitle
LOC . L.,VOL , H - 3

34 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
theholderto one vote,in person or by writtenproxy,in
allelectionsof officersand at all meetings of said com-
pany for any purpose .
§3 . That said company shallhave the right,through
its directors,to take from its officersand agents bonds for
the faithfuldischarge of duty and for the safety and se-
curity of itsfunds and property.
§ 4. There shallbe elected, at each annual meeting of
the stockholders, a president, vice president, secretary,
treasurer ,and five directors ; and this number of directors
may ,by a vote as herein provided,be increasedto nine,
who shall hold their officefor one year and untiltheir
successors are elected and qualified,a majority of whom
shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business .
Vacancies that may occur in the term of any directoror
other officermay be filledby the board . The annual elec-
tion afterthe firstone ,which may take place at any time
afterorganization,shallbe held at the officeof the com-
pany ,in the cityof Covington,on the second Monday in
January of each year . At all meetings of the directors
they shallvote in person and not by proxy .
§5 . That the business of said company is limited to
furnishing and supplying the city of Covington and its
inhabitants and persons and corporations located near to
said city,and the cityof Newport and itsinhabitants and
persons and corporationslocated near to said city,and
the city of Ludlow and its inhabitants and persons and
corporations located near to said city, and other towns
and villagesin Kenton or Campbell counties,within five
miles of eitherof said cities,with motive power and fuel
from natural gas , obtained by drillingor otherwise,and
conveyed through pipes,mains ,and by other means , and
may charge thereforsuch priceand compensation as may
be agreed upon between said company and the individu-
als or corporations contracting with it; and in aid of its
object itmay acquire and hold such real estate as may
be necessary for the location of its works and the carry-
ing into effectthe purposes of itsorganization, including
the right of way for its pipes,mains , over, on , or under

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 35
privateproperty; and the body hereby incorporatedshall
have the exclusiveprivilegeof conducting the business
above described within and adjacent to said citiesfor the
term of twenty-fiveyears; but a non -user of the privi-
leges of thisact of incorporation for fiveyears shallwork
a forfeiture .
§ 6. Said company ,afterhaving firstobtained from the
city council,and had entered on the journal of said coun-
cilan order to that effect, shallhave the privilegeto lay
itspipes and mains and other appliancesthrough and
along any street, alley, or highway of said city from
whose council said privilege has been so had ,or through
or along any public road or highway outsideof said
cities,permissionbeing firsthad thereforfrom theproper
authorities: Provided, That in no way shall eitheror any
of said streets,roads,or highways be ,by said privileges
to said company , permanently obstructed in the use of
the same to the public or to any individual; and said
company is hereby requiredto replaceand put in repair
any street, road , or highway through which its line of
pipes,mains ,& c.,may pass; but itis allowed to tempo-
rarilyobstruct, for a reasonable time , in laying its line
and repairing the same .
§7 . The private property of stockholders shall not be
liablefor the debts of the corporation.
§ 8. Itishereby made the duty of saidcompany tofur-
nish to the State Geological Department of the State of
Kentucky a record and samples of any and all borings
for eithergas or oil, showing the differentstrata and the
thickness of each strata of rock .
§ 9. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 17,1884.
CHAPTER 825 .
AN ACT forthebenefitof Webster and Hopkins counties, relatingto the
public roads in said counties .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The county judge of Webster county shalldivide
all of the roads of said county into precincts, and as

36 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
often as may be necessary appoint a surveyor in each
precinct,whose duty itshallbe to superintendthe roads
therein,and seethatthe same isworked and kept in such
repair as the agreement of the contractors thereon with
the county court requires. No surveyor shallbe allowed ,
unlessby consentofthe court,to resignsuch place under
two years,excepthe remove from the precinct. Any sur-
veyor may be removed at any time by said court.
§2 . The clerk of the county court shall, within ten
days after the appointment of a surveyor of said road ,
delivera copy of the order to the sheriffof the county
(or to a constableiftherebe no sheriff),containinga full
and complete description of the precinct, and take his
receipttheretor. Such officershall, within fifteendays
thereafter,delivera copy of the order and a description
of the precinct to the surveyor,and return the originalto
the clerk'sofficeof the Webster county court with the
time of the service indorsed, which shall be evidence of
the factsstatedtherein. The clerkshallpost up in his
office,once in every year , a listof allthe suveyors in the
county,designatingthe precinctof each surveyor. The
clerk,sheriff,or otherofficerfailingto perform the duties
prescribedin thissection,shallbe finedtwo dollarsand a
half for each neglect .
§ 3. Each surveyorof a publieroad shallbe furnished
with a copy of the contract for the working of the road
in his precinct. He shallreport to the county judge any
failureupon the part of the contractor thereon . He shall
certifyto the county judge when the contract has been
properlycomplied with. He shallbe exempt from jury
service, and from poll-tax for road purposes during his
continuancein saidoffice. For a failureto perform any
of his dutiesprescribedby thisact,said surveyor shall
be fined therefor not less than five nor more than fifteen
dollars for each offense , to be recovered by warrant in
the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; and the
quarterlycourt shallhave exclusivejurisdictionof all
proceedingsagainstsurveyorsof roads.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 37
§ 4. The county judge shall,at the court-house door in
Dixon ,on the firstMonday in Aprilin each year (and at
such other times as he shall find necessary),let out the
working and keeping in repair the public roads in Web-
stercounty ,to the lowest and best bidder,for such length
of time as he may choose (not exceeding his own term of
office),after due advertisement of two insertionsin the
county paper, ifthere be one, and by printed notices
posted up for at leastfifteendays before such lettingat
the voting places of each voting precinct in the county :
Provided , That the fund raised by the levy court under
this act shallbe sufficientto work all of the public roads
in said county , and if not sufficient, then it shall be used
at such places and for such roads as the county court may
think best,having due regard to the public good and the
wants of the differentparts of the county .
§ 5. Each contractor shall execute to the Common-
wealth a bond ,with good security,to be approved by the
county judge ,in double the amount he is to receivefor his
contract, for the faithfulperformance of said contract,
and said bonds shallbe filedat the next regular term of
the Webster county court afterthe letting,which shallbe
noted of record and the bonds kept on fileby the clerk of
the court in his office in bundles marked “ contractors '
bonds ;" and certifiedcopiesofsaidbonds shallbe compe-
tent as evidence , and the county judge shall deliver to
each surveyor a copy of the contract for working the
road in such surveyor's precinct. Any contractor who
shall fail,in whole or in part, to complete his contract
within the time and in the manner prescribed therein, or
who , having contracted to keep any part of the public
roads in good order for traveling or hauling ,or any bridge
or culvertin good repair,and who shallfailto do so,shall,
for every failure, be deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor , and
upon conviction shallbe fined not lessthan ten nor more
than one hundred dollars .
§ 6. All fines assessed under this act shall be collected
as otherfines,and shall[inure] to the benefitof the road
fund of Webster county, and shallbe paid out by the
sheriffunder the ordersof the countyjudge.

38 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 7. The countyjudge shallprovidework on the roads
of the county or on the streetsof towns forsuch persons
convictedof penal offensesas may ,by orderofthe proper
authority,be required to work on roads or streets, or to
work out fines assessed against them on the roads or
streetsunder the provisions of general or special laws ,
and may ,when necessary,put a balland chain upon any
such persons to prevent their escape , and if necessary ,
may employ some suitable person to oversee and guard
them while at work .
§ 8. The county judge shallletout the construction or
repairing of the bridges in Webster county not included
in the agreement of any contractor, or the levy court may ,
in theirdiscretion,appoint a specialcommissioner to let
out any bridge or bridges,and provide for his compensa-
tion therefor, and such county judge or special commis-
sioner shall be held liable for any failurein regard to
any bridge so let by them respectively, and the court
may require bond with surety from the special commis-
sioner .
§ 9. That alldelinquent capitationtax-payers of Web-
ster county shall be required to work out their taxes at
such times on the roads in their several precincts as the
surveyor thereof may require,for which work such delin-
quents shallhave creditat the rate of one dollar for each
fullday's work performed by him . For failingor refus-
ing to comply with the order of said surveyor when
notified, all such delinquents are made liable to the
penalties now imposed by law for persons failing or re-
fusing to work on roads. The several surveyors are
authorized and empowered to collectthe taxes due from
such delinquents in their several precincts,if they shall
elect to pay the same rather than work on the roads,
and the same shall be held and applied as part of the
road fund ,and reported and paid over by said surveyor
to the county judge , taking and filingwith the county
clerk his receipttherefor;and said clerkshallgive to said
surveyora certifiedcopy of saidreceipt,which shallhave
the same effectin his hands as the original.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 39
10. The county court of Webster county may open
roads not exceeding sixtyfeet in width , or may increase
the width of established roads to sixty feet, anything in
the general laws to the contrary notwithstanding .
§ 11. That the courts of the justicesof the peace in and
for Webster county shallhave jurisdictionconcurrent with
the quarterly court of all proceedings against road con-
tractors under this act, and it shall be the duty of any
justiceor the county judge upon his own knowledge or
upon complaint of any surveyor,or upon the information,
under oath,of any citizen, that any part of the public
road is out of repair, to issue his warrant against the de-
linquent contractor,returnable to his own or some other
justices' court,or to the Webster quarterlycourt,and upon
the execution and return thereof such proceedings shall
be had as are had in other Commonwealth cases. In pro-
ceedings under this act the defendant shall have no
right to claim his district, and both the Commmonwealth
and defendant shallhave the rightof appeal from justices'
courts to the quarterly court,and from the quarterlycourt
to the circuit court .
§ 12. Itshallbe the duty of the county attorney to pros-
ecute allwarrants under thisact,and forsuch prosecutions
he shall receive twenty -five per cent. of the finesrecov-
ered , and upon his failurethe court may appoint some
attorneyin his stead,who shallreceivesaidcommission
for his services . All fines assessed under this act shall be
collected as other fines,and shall inure to the benefit of
the road fund of Webster county , and shall be paid out
by the sheriff,or other collectingofficer,under orders from
the county judge .
§ 13. It shallbe unlawfulforthe county judge,county
attorney,sheriff,collectorof the revenue ,or any surveyor ,
to become ,directly or indirectly,interested in any con-
tractfor working roads, or building or repairing bridges ;
and itshallbe unlawful for either of said officersto buy
or become interested in any claim growing out of said
work or contract. For a violationof thissection,either
of said officersshall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and ,

40 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
upon indictment and conviction thereof,shall be fined
not less than fiftynor more than five hundred dollars.
This section shall be given in charge to the grand jury
by the judge of the Webster county court.
§ 14. Any person who shallwillfullyor negligentlyin-
jure,destroy,or obstruct any of the said public roads or
bridges, or any of the culverts or ditches on said roads ,
shall be fined not less than five nor more than twenty-
five dollars, to be recovered in like manner as the fines
prescribedherein for surveyors, and shallalso be liablein
double damages to the county , or any person aggrieved
or injured,to be recovered in any court having jurisdic-
tion of the amount . It shallbe the duty of surveyors to
reportpromptlyto the countyjudge any violationof this
section .
§ 15. The county court,a majority ofthejusticesagree-
ing,shall, on some regular county court day ,assessa tax
of not more than fifteen cents on each one hundred dol-
lars' worth of property assessedfor revenue purposes ,and
a capitation tax not exceeding two dollars on each per-
son in said county required by the general law to work
on roads .
§ 16. The ad valorem and capitation tax provided for
in section of this act shall be collected as other revenue
due the county is collected,and under the same obligations
by the collectingofficer,and shallbe paid out by said col-
lecting officerto road contractors on the order of the
judge of the Webster county court.
§ 17. Chapter ninety-four of the General Statutes is
hereby adopted and made part of this act,except when
itcomes in conflict with the provisions of this act ; and all
other road laws passed for Webster county are hereby
repealed .
§ 18. That an electionshallbe held in Webster county
on the firstMonday in August ,one thousand eight hun-
dred and eighty-four,at which two pollsshallbe opened ,
one " for the road law " and the other " against the road
law ;" and if it shall be found that a majority of those
who voted upon the question have voted for the law ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 41
then this act shall be in full force ; should there not be a
majority for said law at said election,then the county
court may ,at any regularsubsequent election,have polls
opened as provided for in thissection; and ifat any such
election there is a majority of persons voting in favor of
thislaw ,itshallthen be in fullforce; and whenever said
law is adopted ,the county court may , as provided in sec-
tion six,proceed at once to levy the tax and order itscol-
lection .
§19 . That it is hereby made the duty of the county
court, for at least one month before any vote istaken , as
provided in section eighteen,to have this act published
in the county newspaper ,if there be one , and also have
the same printed and posted up at one or more places
in each precinct before said election, and also at or near
the pollson the day said electionis held. Said election
shall be held as other general electionsare held,by the
same officers,and the pollsshallbe compared and re-
sultannounced by the same board who compare the polls
in general elections, two of whom may act : Provided,
That a failureto comply with any part of thissectionby
said court shallnot make void the adoption of this act if
a majority vote for it.
§20 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 17 , 1884.
CHAPTER 826 .
AN ACT to establisha road law for Lawrence county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . Applications shall be allowed for opening roadş
only for the convenience of traveling to the court-house ,
to a public warehouse , or an established town , landing ,
ferry, mill, iron-works, house of public worship, poor-
house,coalbanks,oilwell,railroaddepot or station.
§2 . When any person shallmake applicationto the
county court to have a new road opened ,or a former one
alteredordiscontinued,itshallappointtwo or more com-

42 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
petent persons to view the ground along which the road
isproposed to be conducted ; theirduty shallbe to view
the old road and new route where a change is proposed ;
if a discontinuance is proposed thereto,review the road
which isproposed to be discontinued.
§3 . Before such view shall take place, the viewers
shallbe sworn to faithfullyand impartiallyto executethe
duties assigned them . The viewers may examine other
routes than that proposed , and may report in favor of the
one which theypreferwith theirreasonsforthepreference,
describing the route so laidout by bounds and by general
courses and distances as near as they can ; they shall re-
port the convenience and inconveniences which will re-
sultas well to individualsas the public,from the opening
of such road , the alteration or discontinuance of a road .
§4 . The viewers shallreport the names of all proprie-
tors and tenants of land over which a road or alteration
in a road isproposed by them to run . The court may di-
rect an engineer or surveyor to attend the viewers , and
make out a map or diagram of the routes viewed , and
reportotherfactsrelatingto such change. Itshallbe the
duty of such viewers to ascertainfrom such proprietoror
tenant of lands whether they can obtain the rightof way ,
and upon what terms, and reportthe saidterms to the
county court; to all of such persons as receive notice of
such reviews from the reviewersshalldispensewith a
summons against such ones .
§5 . Upon the reportofthe viewersto alteror establish
a road , unless the opinion of the court be against the ap-
plication, itshallaward processto summons any pro-
prietorand tenantswho have not had noticefrom the
reviewers over which the viewers may propose the road
to run,to show cause,if any he or they can,against the
same . If any of the proprietorsare non -residentsof the
State, a warning order shall be made , warning the non-
residentto show cause ,ifany ,againstthe same to the next
regular monthly term of said court, and afterthe expira-
tionof thirtydays from making said order,shallauthorize
the condemnation of the lands .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 43
§ 6. Upon the report of the viewers or the process exe .
cuted ,if the court has enough before itto enable itto fix
upon a just compensation to the proprietorsand tenants
over whose land the road or alterationof a road ispro-
posed to run , and they are willing to accept what the
court deems just,it may determine the matter without a
writ of ad quod damnum .
§7 . By leave of the court the report of the viewers
may be amended by them , if it be found to be defective,
or if they shall so desire,at any time before finalaction
thereon .
§ 8. A writ of ad quod damnum shallbe awarded , if de-
siredby any proprietor or tenant, if the court see cause
for awarding the same ; such writ shall command the
proper officerto summons and empanel a jury of freehold-
ers of the county ,not relatedto any of the partiesand not
residingwithin two miles of the proposed road,to meet
on the land of the proprietorsor tenants over which the
road isproposed to be established or changed , at a cer-
tain time and place, of which notice shall be given to
them by the officer.
§ 9. The jury,afterhaving been sworn by the officer,
shall view the lands of the proprietoror tenants so named ,
and ascertainwhat shallbe a justcompensation to them ,
or eitherof them , forthe landsproposed to be taken,for
the additionalfencingand actualvalue of the landspro-
posed to be used ,and if any damage willresultto the re-
mainder of such tractbeyond the peculiar benefitswhich
will be derived to such residue from the road . If a per-
son claiming damages has only an estatefor lifeor years,
and the remainder in fee belongs to another, the jury
shall apportion the damages between them .
§ 10. If the jury shall not be sworn on the day speci-
fied,or one shallbe sworn and disagree and be discharged,
the officershall execute the writ on such other day as
he may appoint,noticethereofbeing given to the parties
interested. If the inquest cannot be completed in one
day ,the officershalladjourn the jury from day to day till
completion. When completed it shallbe signedby the

44 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
jurors, and returned by the officerwith the writ to the
clerk's office whence it emanated .
§ 11.The court shall,upon the report,inquest,and other
evidence , determine whether the road shall be established
or altered as recommended by the viewers ; and may , on
the report of viewers and evidence ,determine whether a
road shall be discontinued .
§ 12. Before an order shallbe made appointing viewers
to discontinue a road ,the person applying shallgive one
month's notice in writing, posted at the front door of the
court-house ,and three other public places in the vicinity
of the road .
§ 13. When a road isopened or altered,the county court
shall levy the amount of damages at the next court of
claims, or provide means for payment as provided in an
act approved February twenty -third, one thousand eight
hundred and seventy -six, and the legal cost of the pro-
ceedings shall be adjusted and allowed at the regular
court of claims ; and the cost of the defendant shall have
expended in his defense, except that when the records
show that the sum allowed by the jury to the defendant
is not more than the court has consented to allow him be-
fore awarding the writ of ad quod damnum ,such defend-
ant shallbe adjudged to pay the cost occasioned by such
writ .
§14 . But the court may open or altera road on condi-
tion that allor a part of allof a sum required to be paid
to the proprietor and tenant,and the cost of procedure ,
shallbe paid by the person applying for the same ,or on
condition that the applicant shall wholly or in part open
or alter the same .
§ 15. If the court shallbe of opinion that the road ought
not to be opened or altered,the applicant shall be ad-
judged to pay the costof the proceedings; but the court
may ,on his own volition,order a review of a main lead-
ing road without applicationof an individual.
§ 16. The county court shallhave power to open or
change any county road through any lands,lot,or prem-
ises,except through any cemetery or graveyard ,provided .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 45
it shallpay the proprietorof the same a reasonable com-
pensation therefor.
§ 17. It shall be in the discretionof the county court,
in opening or changing a county road , to prescribe the
width ,so itbe not lessthan nine and not more than eigh-
teen feet, and cleared not less than fifteen feet and not
more than thirtyfeet, and overhanging substances to be
cleared fifteenfeethigh; but the court may ,on proper
evidence ,increase the width of any road near a town not
exceeding fiftyfeet,by paying the proprietorsof lands
over which it runs a reasonable compensation , as now
provided by law .
§ 18.The county court shalldividethe roads in the
countyintoprecincts,and appointa surveyorin each
precinct, whose duty it shall be to superintend the roads
therein,and see that the same is clear and kept clear and
in good repair, and ditch and kept ditched on one or
both sides of the same ,when so ordered by the court or
road commissioner . No surveyor shall be allowed to re-
sign his place as such until after the expiration of two
years , and untilhe shall make and subscribe an affidavit
that his section of road is in good condition, such as re-
quiredby hisorder; and thecourtmay requireotherproof
unless it shall be made manifest that it will redound to
the best interest of the road : Provided, That the court
shall not be required to reappoint any one who has al-
ready been appointed; but allappointments made here-
aftershallbe made in compliance with thisact.
§19 . All male persons over sixteen and under fifty
years of age ,who are able to do ordinary labor, except
itinerant ministers of the gospel , shall be assigned to
work on some road .
§20 . Every person so assigned to work on a road ,
either in repairing,changing ,or opening the same , who
shallfailto attend,with good and sufficienttools,such as
required of him by the surveyor ,without reasonable ex-
cuse,when requiredby the surveyor of his precinct, or
who shallattend and failto perform good labor while in
attendance ,or to furnisha good substitutewho will per

46 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
form good labor,accordingto the orderof the surveyor,
shallbe fined two dollars and fiftycents for each day or
part of a day he so failsto work or hire the work done ,
to be recovered before the Lawrence county court or any
justiceof the peace of Lawrence county,by a warrant
or summons ,afterfivedays' notice. In case the defaulter
shallbe an infant,the fineshallbe paid by the father,
guardian ,or person in whose serviceshe is engaged in at
the time of such delinquency; and the court shallhave
power to enforce the payment of the father, guardian ,
& c.,by a rule and fineagainst such one .
§ 21. Eight hours shallconstituteone day's work . Any
person refusingto perform eight hours' good labor shall
not be entitledto any creditfor any part of a day ,unless
the surveyor shall call out all or part of his hands in his
precinct to remove an obstruction or to make repair or
change in road which willnot requirethat length of time ,
or unless by consent of the surveyor; then such one shall
be allowed for the time actuallyengaged : Provided, That
no person shallbe required to labor more than ten days
in any year: And provided,That no one shallbe required
to labor more than sixteen hours in any one week .
§ 22. It shallbe the duty of the county court to desig-
nate in each order appointing surveyor or superintendent
the width of the road to be dug and cleared .
23. Any person who shallwillfullyor unlawfullyfill
up any ditchin the county road,or shallfillup any cul-
vert or obstruct the roads with fencing,trees,timber , or
other obstruction,shallbe fined not to exceed fiftydollars
for each offense, and , moreover ,be liable to Lawrence
county for any damages sustained thereby. Each twenty-
four hours shallconstitutea separate offense.
§ 24. Fines collectedfor failingto comply with section
twenty of thisact shall be paid to the surveyor or super-
intendent, where the delinquency occurred, and shall be
by him expended on his section of road . But if he shall
be released or removed before the same shall have been
expended , then he shall pay the same over to his suc-
cessor within thirtydays ,and the various surveyors shall,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 47
once in each twelve months , make out a complete report
of all moneys coming into his or their hands ,to be filed
with the county court; and no surveyor shall be allowed
to resignuntilhe shallmake a complete report," sworn
to," of all moneys coming to his hands and how he has
expended the same ; and such surveyor or superintendent
shall expend allsuch money coming to his or their hands
within sixtydays afterhe receives the same ,provided his
road needs the expenditure . Any surveyor or superin-
tendent unlawfully appropriating any such money to his
own use,or willfullyfailingto account forthe same and pay
itover as required herein,shallbe guiltyof embezzlement ,
and confined in the penitentiarynot lessthan one nor
more than two years .
§25 . Each surveyor shallerect,or cause to be erected,
and keep up at the forks or crossing of every road , an
index on a post or tree,with plain inscriptionsin large
letters,designating the most noted place which each road
leadsto; the costof same shallbe paid out ofthe county
levy.
§26 . The clerk of the county court shall, within ten
days after the appointment of reviewers , surveyor, or
superintendent,deliverto the sheriffof Lawrence county
the same , with such copies as may be required, and take
his receiptfor the same ; each order appointing a surveyor
shallgive a complete descriptionof the precinct,of road ,
and hands . The sheriffshall, within ten days thereafter,
delivera copy of such ordersto each superintendentand
surveyor,or reviewer,and returnthe same to the county
court clerkwith hisreturn on the same . The county court
clerk shallpost up at the frontdoor of the court-house,
once in each year,a completelistof allsurveyorsofroads
inthe county,designatingthe precinctof each surveyor,
and shallfurnishthe grand jury a listof surveyors,desig-
nating the precinctof each surveyor; and he shallbe
allowed a reasonablecompensation therefor,to be paid
outof thecounty levy. Any clerkorsheriffwho shallneg-
lectto perform hisor theirdutiesunder thisact shallbe
fined twenty dollars.

48 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§27 . When any wheel carriage,plough ,draught horse ,
oxen , gear , or driver shall be necessary for the making ,
altering,or repairing of any road ,bridge, or causeway or
culvert,if the surveyor or road commissioner cannot ob-
tain them by contract,or that they cannot be obtained in
his road precinct,any justiceof the peace may ,by his war-
rant, empower the surveyor to impress such wheel car-
riage,plough ,draught horse, or horses or oxen ,gear and
driver as may be necessary,belonging to any person , and
use the same in constructing, repairing his road ; and
any person who is not a hand in the said precinct whose
team ,plough ,& c., isimpressed or contracted for,shall be
paid for out of the county levy,and when the horses,
teams , or implements are furnished by a hand in the dis-
trict, he shall have credit therefor on his taxes or work
due from him at a reasonable price.
§ 28. No surveyor shall call out hands to work on the
road on the firstday of any county or circuitcourt or
electionday . Two days'previous notice,eitherwritten or
verbal, of which the oath of the surveyor or person ap-
pointed by him to warn the hands shallbe prima facie
evidence , shallbe given of the day a hand is required to
work . A surveyor may callout allor a part of his hands
other than regular work days ;such hands shallhave credit
for the time actually engaged . The surveyor may apoint
some competent person , who is liableto work the road in
his precinct,to warn the hands , and such one shallhave
a creditfor one day's time for such services.
§ 29. If a bridge, causeway , or culvert shall be neces-
sary on any road ,the surveyor shallcause the same to be
made and kept in repair, and for that purpose may , with
the consent of the owner of land ,cut so much timber ,
take earth or stone, as may be necessary to do the same ;
but if the consent of the land -owner cannot be had , it
shall be in the power of the surveyor or road commis-
sioner to summons two good disinterested citizens, land-
owners , to appraise the same , and then they shall be
authorizedto take the same and use itfor such purpose ,
and the value of the same shallbe provided for at the next

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 49
court of claims. Ifitis two onerous forthe hands to per-
form ,the county court shallprovide for the same .
§30 . The county court may exempt any person from
working on the roads on account of infirmities.
§31 . The surveyor of each road shallgive to each
hand a certificateof the number of days or hours actu-
ally worked by any hand under him. If any surveyor
shall give to any hand any such certificatewithout the
person named shall have actually performed good labor
for the time named in the same ,he shallbe fined not less
than ten nor more than fiftydollars for each offense .
§32 . If the surveyor of a public road shall fail to
keep his road in good repair, according to the order ap-
pointing him , or shall failto report any delinquency
occurring under section twenty of this act within a rea-
sonable time to the county court clerk of Lawrence
county , or to some justice of the peace ,he shallbe fined
not less than ten and not exceeding fiftydollars: Pro-
vided, That if such prosecution be commenced for failing
to keep in good repair his road ,he shallnot be liable,
provided that he has expended allthe means placed at
his disposal, and that he has reported that fact to the
county court or the road commissioner .
§33 . All surveyors shallbe required to work in obedi-
ence to the orders of the road commissioner ,or any engi-
neer in his employment ; and for a failureor refusalto do
so ,shallbe liableto same penalty as for failing to keep
his road in repair .
§ 34. It shall be the duty of the county court,at the
May term,one thousand eighthundred and eighty-four,
and each year thereafter,to appoint a competent man as
road commissioner for Lawrence county ,who shall hold
his officefor one year , and until his successor be ap-
pointed and qualified; and he shallbe required to take
an oath thathe willfaithfullyand impartiallydischarge
all the duties devolving on him as such ; and he may be
removed for a failureto perform his duty ,by a rule en .
tered of record,and a copy served on him twenty days
LOC . L., VOL 1-4

50 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
1
before the time to which he is required to show cause
why he shallnot be discharged .
§ 35. It shallbe his duty to inspect allthe roads in the
county as fast as is practicalafterhis appointment , and
ascertain their condition, and where he shall find any
road out of repair,to callon the surveyor of the same ,
and ascertain how much labor and means under his con-
trolis unexpended , and for that purpose shallhave power
to swear him ; and ifthe means and labor of hands given
him has not allbeen expended ,itshallbe his duty to call
out the surveyor to work the districtof road of which he
has control, and the surveyor shall call his hands out
within a reasonabletime and repairit,or make culverts,
ditches,or throw it up in such manner as the commis-
sioner may direct: Provided, That he shall not be re-
quired to expend more than nine days of his hands ' time
at any one season,leaving the remainder of their time
so as to enable him to use that in making any needed.
repairsduringthe remainder of the year. Any surveyor
who shallfailor refuse, without a good excuse ,to obey
the order of the commissioner , shall be liable to the same
penalties as for failingto keep his road in good repair.
§36 . If the labor of hands and the expenditures of
money derived from delinquents will not be sufficientto
repair,change , or build the roads in such manner as re-
quired by this act and the orders of the road commis-
sioner,it shallbe the duty of the road commissioner to
ascertain, as near as he can,what itwill costin each
voting precinctto place the respectiveroads in good con-
ditionin the same over and above the labor of hands ,and
filehisreport,in writing,relatingto each precinct; upon
the said reportbeing filed,it shallbe the duty of the
county court to lay a levy on the taxable property in
each voting precinct sufficientto place the roads in good
repair,change,or build the same . The levy shall,in no
event, exceed forty cents on each one hundred dollars'
worth of taxable property in such precinct in any one
year under thissection: Provided, That the levy shallbe
made on or beforethe Julyterm in each year; and such

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 51
moneys so leviedand collectedshallbe expendedin the
precinctwhere collected: Provided,That thecountycourt
shallhave power to prescribethe mode and manner by
which tax-payers may work out theirtaxeson the county
roads .
§ 37. Upon the order being entered of recordsof the
county court laying the levy, it shall be the duty of the
clerk to certifythe same tothe sheriffof Lawrence county
for collectionin ten days after the order is entered, and
thereuponitshallbe the duty of the sheriffto collectthe
same ,in the same manner as the county levy and public
dues are now collected,except as provided hereinafter;
and he and his sureties shall be liable for the amount so
leviedand collected,as they are now by law forthe col-
lectionof the county levy and public dues : Provided,
That if the county court shall prescribe how tax payers
may work out taxes, and designate a person or persons to
certifythe satisfactionof such taxes to such tax payers
when worked out,that the certificateshall be taken up
by the sheriff,and he shallgive him a receiptstatinghow
his taxes are paid,and upon his filingsuch certificate
with his statement ,shall be his voucher to the extent of
the same .
§ 38. For the purpose of more effectuallycarrying out
this act, and to secure good easy grades on the main
roads,the commissioner will be authorized to employ a
competent civilengineer to, and agree with him as to his
compensation [to] aidin the work ; but such employment
shallbe by and with the consent of the county court.
§39 . If it shall be ascertained that the labor and
money raised by taxation will be insufficientto make
good roads in such precinct,then the money raised by
taxationas provided in sectionsthirtysixand forty-four,
itshallbe expended on the roadsdesignatedby the coun-
ty court,having reference to the main and leading roads.
§ 40. The building of roads,or repairing or changing
of same ,shallbe done , when done ,by the expenditure of
money arisingfrom taxes,in the mode and manner pre-
scribedby thecommissionerand the county court.

52 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 41. If the county court sittingas a court of levy and
claims for Lawrence county , a majority of the justicesof
the peace concur therein,shalldeem itto the best interest
of the road system , it shallhave power and authority to
issuethe bonds of Lawrence county ,bearing not exceeding
seven per cent,interestper annum ,the interestto be paid
annually , semi -annally, or in such manner as may be
prescribed by the court,to be redeemed at any time at the
pleasure of the county , not beyond ten years from their
date : Provided, That in the ordering theirissualit shall
prescribe the time of their redemption , and the rate of
interest, said bonds to be disposed of in the manner pre-
scribedby the county court: And provided, That they
shallnot be disposed of at lessthan ninety-five cents on
the dollar : And provided further, That the amount of
bonds issued shall not exceed thirtythousand dollars dur-
ing any one year : And providedfurther, That not exceed-
ing thirtythousand dollarsin bonds shallbe outstanding
at any one time . If the county court shall issue said
bonds as provided in thissection,itshalllay a levy on all
thetaxablepropertyof Lawrence county,not exceeding
forty cents on each hundred dollars' worth of taxable
property,and cause the same to be collectedin the same
way that the county levy and public dues are collected,to
provideforthe payment of intereston same ,and provide
a sinking fund to pay offthe principalof said bonds :
Provided, That ifbonds are issued under thissection,then
no levy shallbe made as provided in sectionthirty-six:
Provided further, That a strictaccount shallbe kept of the
amount of bonds issued for the benefitof each precinct,
and bonds disposedofforsuch precinctshallbe expended
in the same ,and alltaxes collectedfrom such precinct
shallbe set apart to pay the interestand principalof
bonds to the amount disposed of for the same .
§42 . When it shallappear to the county court that the
roadsleadingthrough any precinctisof too greata bur-
den on the tax-payer of the same ,and it shallappear
thatthe road is a main leading one,and the publicne-
cessitydemands a well-graded road, then the county
court shallenter an order directing to what extent such

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 53
precinct shallbe aided from a common fund arisingfrom
taxation on the taxable property of the whole county ;
and for the purpose of enabling the county court to do so,
itshallbe in the power of the court of claims of Law-
rence county to lay a levy and cause to be collected a
tax of not exceeding ten cents on each one hundred dol-
lars' worth of taxable property in Lawrence county .
§ 43. Ifbonds are issued,itshallbe the duty of the clerk
to keep an account with each precinct,and charge all
bonds up to the respectiveprecincts; and he shallkeep
blank certificatesfor surveyors,as provided in section
thirty-one of thisact; and for all such services he shall
be allowed a reasonable compensation .
§ 44. That if any one be able to work on the road has
cattle,horse, or mule team ,then it shallbe in the power
of the surveyor to warn such one to bring with him such
team . Any person so furnishing two yoke of cattleand
wagoner ,scraper,two horses or mules , wagon ,scraper,
or plow ,shallbe allowed two days forany such team and
plow , wagon , or scraper,on his time as a hand . Any
person who is a hand on a road and an owner of team ,
plow , wagon , or scraper, and being required to bring the
same on the road in obedience to the order of the sur-
veyor , without a reasonable excuse , shall be fined five
dollarsfor each day he so fails.
§45 . Any hand liable to work on a road may ,on or
beforethe day he isrequiredto work ,pay to the surveyor
of his precinct one dollar, and that shallexonerate such
one for that day ; and the surveyor shall be required to
expend the same and be under the same penaltiesaspre-
scribed in section twenty -four of thisact.
§ 46. The commissioner shall be allowed a reasonable
compensation for his services,in such way and manner
as may be prescribedby the county court.
$ 47. The Lawrence county court shallhave , and jus-
ticesof the peace,concurrentjurisdictionwith the crimi-
nal or circuitcourt,jurisdictionover alloffensesprescribed
in this act,except as prescribed in section twenty -four ;
and thisact shallbe given in charge to the grand jury of
Lawrence county .

54 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§48 . That the hands living in the corporate limits of
Louisa shallbe required to keep up and in good repair
allroads leading out of Louisa ,to a point one half mile
beyond the corporate limitsof the same .
§ 49. That thisact shallapply to the county of Law-
rence .
§ 50. To take effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 17, 1884 .
CHAPTER 827 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Horace C. Branham ,sheriffof Hardin county .
WHEREAS , Miles Petty, colored, was charged in the
county of Hardin , near railroad station Sonora , with
committing a rape on the daughter of James Vanort , of
said county ; said Petty was arrested and in the custody
of Horace C. Branham ,sheriffof Hardin county , and be-
fore he could deliver said Petty in the jailof said county
a mob of some fiftymen in the neighborhood where the
crime was said to have been committed were in search of
the prisoner,threatening to hang him ,and no doubt would
have done so if kept in the county aforesaid. The sher-
iff, Branham , was ordered to take the prisoner, by the
county judge,to the Louisvillejailforprotection,which
he did,with aid,by crossing the Ohio riverat Rock Horn
into Indiana , thence to Louisville, at great peril,trouble,
and expense,and was three and one-half days accom-
plishing the trip; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor be , and he ishereby ,directed to
draw his warrant in favor of Horace C. Branham , the
sheriff,for the sum of ninety-nine ($99) dollars,to com-
pensatehim forhisservicesand the guard and theirex-
pense,horse hire,hotelbills,& c.
§2 . That thisact be in force from itspassage .
Approved April 17, 1884.

CHAPTER 830 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Drake's Creek and Allen Springs Turnpike
Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Ewing G. Bell,C. J. Larmon ,Wm .Greathouse ,
T.J Sweeney , L. W. Kirby ,and E. M. Davidson ,and their
associates and successors,be ,and they are hereby , cre-
ated a body -politicand corporate, under the name and
styleof the " Drake's Creek and Allen Springs Turnpike
Company ," and by thatname and styleshallhave power
to contract and be contracted with, sue and be sued ,
answer and be answered , plead and be impleaded , in all
courts and places in this Commonwealth ; and may have
and use a common seal,and may alteror change the same
at pleasure .
§2 . The said company is hereby authorized and em-
powered to construct a turnpike, macadamized ,or,gravel
road,in itsdiscretion,from a pointat or near Sweeney &
Potter's mill,on Drake's creek,or at or near the present

56 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
terminusof theRow's MillTurnpike Road running thence
to the Allen county line at some point in the neighbor-
hood of Allen Springs ; said road to be located on the
most practicableroute between said points; and in order
to raisethe funds necessary to construct said road ,said
company shall have power to raise a sum by the issue
and sale of the capital stock, not to exceed thirtythou-
sand dollars, to be divided into shares of fiftydollars
each .
§ 3. That the books for the subscriptionof stock may
be opened in the city of Bowling Green , and at such
other places as may be deemed best, under the direction
of the persons hereinbefore named ,allof whom are here-
by made commissioners for the purpose of obtaining the
capital stock of said company . They may insertan ob-
ligationin the subscriptionbook and listssubstantiallyas
follows: " We ,whose names are hereunto subscribed,sev-
erallypromise to pay to the Drake's Creek and Allen
Springs Turnpike Company the sum of fiftydollarsfor
each share of stock in said company set opposite our
names ,in such manner and proportion and at such times
as shallbe required by said company . Witness our hands
this - day of — , 188- ." When the sum of three
thousand dollarsshall have been subscribed to the capi-
tal stock of said company , it shall be the duty of said
commissioners named in this act to give notice , in such
manner as they may think proper , of a meeting of the
stockholdersof said company , at such time and place as
may be specifiedin said notice, for the purpose of elect-
ing a president and directors. Five directors shall be
elected, and the directorsshallelect one of their number
president. One vote shallbe allowed foreach share of
stock; and the presidentand directorsshallcontinuein
officefor one year ,and until their successors are elected
and qualified. The time and place of allelections after
the firstelection shall be fixed by the president and di-
rectors,and a majority of the board shall be competent
to transact business .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 57
§ 4. So soon as saidcompany isorganizedby the elec-
tion of a presidentand directors,as above provided, they
shallbe a body -politicand corporate,by the name and
styleabove stated,and by thatname shallhave perpetual
succession and all the privileges and franchisesincident
to a corporation; shallbe capableof holdingtheircapital
stock, and taking and holding , by purchase or gift, all
such lands, tenements, and hereditaments, real or per-
sonal property,as may be necessaryfor the prosecution
of itswork or the objectsof thiscorporation
§5 . The president of said company may call meetings
of the board of directorswhenever he may thinkproper,
and on application of any two directorsto him , the board
of directors shall have power to elect a secretary and
treasurer, and such other officersas they may think nec-
essary,and to fillsuch vacancies as may occur in said
board during their term of office. Said board shall re-
quire of the treasurer,before he assumes the duties of
his office, a bond for the faithfuland honest discharge
of his duties, and to faithfullyaccount for all moneys
that may come to his hands as such treasurer,which bond
and securityshallbe approved by the board . Said board
shall keep a record of their proceedings ,which shall be
signed by the president; and in the absence of the presi-
dent the board may electa presidentpro tem. Said board
shall have power to make all necessary by -laws and reg-
ulations for the government of said company ; and may
sue for and recover, in the name of said company , from
delinquent stockholders,the amount due and unpaid upon
any share or shares of stock in said company .
§6 . The presidentand directorsshallfixand regulate
the elevation and grade of said road ,the width , and part
thereof to be covered with stone or gravel ; and may fix
the rates of toll; and may change and alterthe same at
pleasure ; but said rates shall not exceed the rates pre-
scribed by chapter one hundred and ten of the General
Statutes ; and when three consecutive miles of said road
shallhave been completed and examined and approved by
any two justicesofthepeace of Warren county,who shall

58 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
examine the same on application of the president of said
road ,a toll-gate may be erected, and tollcollected under
such rules and regulations as shall be prescribed by the
president and directors, and under the general law of the
State ; but until said road is completed for five consecu-
tivemiles,thetollcollectedshallbe in proportionas the
length of road finished nears to five miles .
§7 . The president and directorsof said company , and
theiremployes ,are hereby authorized to take the consent ,
in writing,of all persons who may consent to grant the
rightof way for said turnpike road ,and the use of mate-
rials for building and repairing said road ; and when
eitherthe rightof way or any of the materials necessary
for the constructionof said road cannot be acquired ,by
consent or purchase , said president and directors may
apply to the Warren county court for a writ of ad quod
damnum to assess damages which may be sustained by
the owner of said land ; and the jury ,in assessing the
damages , may take into consideration the advantages
resulting to the party or parties claiming damages by
reason of the construction of the road ; and upon the
payment of the damages assessed,or the tenderthereof,
itshallbe lawfulfor said company and itsemployes to
make said road,and to dig and carry away any stone or
other material necessary for the construction or repair of
said road; and said company may acquire,by purchase
or by writ of ad quod damnum , as above set forth, land ,
not exceeding ten acres, for the purpose of erecting a
toll-house on said road; and said company may procure
from the Warren county court the right of way over any
road over which the said county has control.
§ 8. The presidentand directorsof said company may
make and declare equitable dividends of the surplus
earning of said company among the stockholders, ac-
cording to their respective shares, after paying the ex-
penses of said company .
§9 . Certificatesof stock in saidcompany shallbe signed
by the president,and countersigned by the secretary; shall
be deemed personal estate, and shall be transferable on

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 59
the books of the company ,in such manner as the presi-
dent and directorsshallprescribe.
§ 10. The capitalstockof said company shallbe used
in the procurement of the right of way , materials, & c.,
and in constructing said road ,and in defraying all legiti-
mate expenses incident to the construction of said road ,
and for no other purpose whatsoever ; and the stock-
holders in said company shall only be bound to the ex-
tent of their stock in said road .
§11 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after itspassage .
