CHAPTER 834 .
AN ACT providing for the improvement of Broadway or Dunkirk Road in
Jeffersoncounty .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1.WHEREAS , A largemajorityof personsowning prop-
ertywithin a distance of eight hundred feet on eitherside
of the Broadway or Dunkirk road ,from the western bound-
ary line of the city of Louisville to the eastern line of
E. Kettig'sland ,have petitionedthisGeneral Assembly to
pass an act to enable the county court of Jeffersoncounty
toimprove said Broadway or Dunkirk road,by taxing or
assessingthecostofsaidimprovement upon therealestate
benefitedthereby. Now , in accordance with the prayer
of said petition, it is enacted that the land embraced
within and described by the boundary linebeginning at a
pointin the western boundary lineof the cityof Louis-
villein the centre of the Broadway or Dunkirk road , and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 61
measuring eight hundred feet north and eight hundred
feet south along the western boundary of said city, thence
westwardly in two parallellines each eight hundred feet
from the centre of said Broadway or Dunkirk road to the
east lineof E. Kettig'sland,is hereby createda tax dis-
trictfor the improvement of said road , and George C.
Buchanan , James Kell, Theodore H. McGee , Patrick
Brown ,Peter Speth, John Heck , and H. J. Lewis, are
hereby appointed commissioners, who , or a majorityof
whom ,shallappoint a competent engineer,with power to
remove him at pleasure,and fixhis compensation by the
month ,who shall,under said commissioners , superintend
the work hereinafterprovided for.
§2 . The said commissioners shall advertisefor propo-
sals to construct the necessary culverts, and to grade
and macadamize said Broadway or Dunkirk Road . The
macadamized portion to be of a width of twenty feet,
and the grading to extend twenty feet on each side of the
macadamized portion; and said commissioners may ad-
vertise and let the construction of culverts , grading , and
macadamizing of said road separatelyor together, as
they may find to be for the best interest of the property,
and shall award the contract in every case to the low-
est and best bidder or bidders, as the case may be .
§3 . The party or partiesto whom the contract is
awarded shall enter into a written contract with said
commissioners,withgoodsecurity,tobe approvedby said
commissioners ,for the fulland faithfulcompletion of the
work according to the specificationswhich shall have
been prepared by the engineer,which contract and speci-
ficationsshallbe filedin the Jeffersoncounty court.
§4 . Upon the completion of as much as one thousand
lineal feet of the work according to the contract, the
engineer shallgive public notice in one of the daily news-
papers published in the city of Louisville, and name in
said notice a day on which he and the commissioners
aforesaid will attend and inspect the part of the work
reported as completed ; and on the day named ,said engi-
neer and said commissioners ,or a majority of the same ,

62 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shall attend and examine the work ; and it is made the
duty of allpersons owning real property in said district
to attend and examine said work on said day ; and ifsaid
work has not been done according to the contract and
specifications,itshallnot be accepted; but if, on inspec-
tion,it be found by said engineer and a majority of said
commissioners that the improvements has been made in
accordance and compliance with the contract and speci-
fications, then it shall be accepted , and said engineer
shallgivelikenoticeand have likeinspectionfor or upon
each section of one thousand feet as the same shall be
completed ; and when each of said sections shall have
been accepted ,the said engineer and commissioners shall
at once reportthe same , in writing,as completed, and
made in accordance with the contract , which shall be
final and conclusive on that subject .
§5 . Upon such reportbeing made ,it shallbe the duty
of said Jefferson county court to cause to be ascertained
the names of the owners of each and every square foot
of land within the tax district described in the first sec-
tion hereof, and to assess the entirecost of said improve-
ment of said road , including the compensation allowed
the engineerand otherfees and costs,equallyupon each
squarefootof land within said district,and a liensupe .
riorto allother liensexcept tax liens and liensof record
inJeffersoncounty clerk'soffice,shallexistforthe same
on saidproperty;and saidcourtshallapportionsaidcost
of saidimprovements to the owners of realestatewithin
said districtaccording to the number of square feet owned
by each person respectively, exclusiveof roads, lanes,
or other avenues in said district .
§ 6. The amount of money ascertained to be due from
each property-owner by the Jeffersoncounty court,and
which,as the costof saidimprovement isapportioned to
theproperty-owners respectively,shallbear interestat the
rateof sixper cent.per annum untilpaid,and suitsmay
be brought for the enforcement of lienshereinbefore given
in the Louisvillechancery court, which court shallhave
fullpower and authorityto correctany and allerrorsin

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 63
the apportionment or proceedings of the said commis-
sioners, engineer, or county court,to the end that each
square footof land withinthe boundary aforesaid(public
roads,lanes,and other avenues only excepted)shallbe
caused to bear an equal part of the cost of said improve-
ments . Ifany oftheliensgivenshallbe enforcedby judg-
ment of the court,and sale made thereunder ,the defend-
ant ,his heirs or personal representatives,shall have the
right to redeem the same at any time within fiveyears
from the date of such sale,by paying to the plaintiffor
his representativethe amount of the judgment and costs,
and costs with six per cent, interestthereon from , the
date of such judgment ; but if not redeemed within the
five years,the titleto such land shallrestabsolutelyin the
purchaser .
§7 . After said Broadway or Dunkirk road is improved
as herein provided for,itshallbe a public road.
§8 This act shallnot become a law or be binding on
the lands ,or owners of the lands mentioned in the firstsec-
tion thereof, until after the said act shall firstbe submitted
to a vote of the persons owning the lands in the district
designated by the firstsection of thisact as a tax district,
and shallbe accepted by a majority of said persons own-
ing said lands in said districtvoting in favor of the same
at an electionto be held for said purpose in said district.
§ 9. The time and place of said electionshallbe fixed
by an orderof the Jeffersoncounty courtupon the peti-
tion of twelve of the owners of said lands .
$ 10 The orderofsaidcourtshall,forthirtydays prior
to the said election, be published in two daily newspa-
pers,one inthe Englishand theotherintheGerman lan-
guage ,in the cityof Louisville,Kentucky ,and a copy of
the orderofsaidcourtshallbe postedforthirtydays prior
to saidelectionatthreeprominentplacesinthesaiddis-
trict,and saidpetitionersto pay the costsofsaidpublica-
tions .
§ 11. The officersof said electionshallbe persons own-
ing lands and residingwithin the said district, and shall
be appointed by said county court.

64 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 12. The said election to be otherwise held, and the
resultdetermined , as is by law provided in State elec-
tions .
Approved April 17, 1884 .

CHAPTER 840 .
AN ACT to prevent trespassin the county of Woodford .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That if any person ,with dogs or gun , go upon the
lands of another for the purpose of hunting game ,with-
out the permissionof thepersoninpossessionof the land,
he shallbe guiltyof trespass,and ,on conviction, be pun-
ished by a fineof not lessthan fivedollarsnor more than
twenty -fivedollarsfor each offense.
§2 . That if any person shall ride or walk over the
lands of another , after receiving a written notice from
the person in possession of the land through the agency
of another ,he shallbe guiltyof trespass, and ,on convic-
tion,be punished by a fineof not less than five dollars
nor more than fifteen dollars for each offense .
§3 . That thisact shallnot apply to persons hunting
foxes with hounds .
§ 4. That this act shall apply only to the county of
Woodford .
§5 . That this act shall take effectfrom and afterits
passage .
Approved April 17,1884 .
CHAPTER 841 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheIrvineand Clay CityTurnpikeRoad Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a company ishereby created,under the name
and styleof the Irvine and Clay City Turnpike Road
Company ,which shallbe a body-politicand corporate,
forthe purpose of constructinga turnpikeroad from the
town of Irvine, in Estillcounty, to the town of Clay
City, in Powell county .
§2 . That the capitalstock of said company shallbe
fiftythousand dollars,which may be increasedor dimin-
ished at the pleasure of the company ,divided into shares
of one hundred dollars each .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 67
§ 3. Books of subscriptionshallbe opened by C. W.

City Turnpike Road Company the sum of one hundred
dollars foreach and every share set opposite our names ,
in such manner and at such times as shall be by them
required under the law incorporating said turnpike road
company ."
§4 . When ten thousand dollars shall have been sub-
scribed to the capital stock,it shall be the duty of the
commissioners named to give notice,in such manner and
at such time and place as they may deem proper , of a
meeting of the stockholders for the purpose of electing

for each share of stock; and the president and directors
shall continue in officeuntiltheirsuccessorsare qualified.
The time and place of election, after the first,shall be
fixed by the president and directors, and shall occur
once in each year ; a majority of the board shalltransact
business , and shall be convened by order of the presi-
dent ,or any three of the directors.
§ 5. As soon as the company is organized ,by the elec-
tion of officers,the president and directorsshallbe a body-
politicand corporate,in fact and law ,by the name and
styleof the Irvineand Clay CityTurnpike Road Com-
pany ,with the privilegesand franchisesof a corporation;
shallbe capable of holding theircapitalstock,and the
increase and profitthereof, and holding by gift,purchase ,
or otherwise , anything necessary for the prosecution of
their work ,or the objectsof their corporation. They
shallhave power to contractand be contractedwith,sue
and be sued ,in allcourts of law and equity ; to have and
use a seal,and generally to do alland every act and thing
lawful for a corporation to do to effectthe object for
which said corporation was created.

68 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§6 Said corporationshallfixand regulatethe grade
of said road , and its covering with gravel or stone ; but
said grade shall be not less than twelve feet wide , and
there shall be not less than ten feet of gravel or stone in
width on said road ; may designate the place of tollgates,
fix the rates of toll; may regulate and change the place
of saine ; but such rates shallnot exceed those prescribed
by general law . They shallhave power ,afterfivemiles
have been completed of said road , to erect gates and
collect tolls, and apply the same to the completion of
the road .
§ 7. It shall be lawful for the officersand employes of
the company , with theirtools and appliances for locating
or making said road ,to enter upon the lands over and
contiguous to which the intended road shall pass,having
firstgiven notice to the owner or occupants thereof.
They shallhave the right to take and receive the right of
way over and through the land where said road shall be
located. If in any instance they cannot procure the right
of way , and cannot agree with the owner or owners of
the lands through which said road is to pass as to dam-
ages said owner will sustain by reason thereof,then the
president shall apply to the county court of the county
wherein the land liesfor a writ of ad quod damnum to
assess the damages which the owner thereof may sus-
tain because of the location of the road upon his,her,
or their premises ; and upon the payment or tender of
the damages assessed, itshallbe lawful for the company
toopen and make said road ,open and use rock and gravel
quarries upon the line of said road ,and the lands contigu-
ous thereto,and do all work pertaining thereto; and in
assessment of damages ,the advantages and disadvantages
of said road to the owner or owners of said land through
which the road runs , shall be taken into consideration .
§ 8. The president and directors shall give notice, as
theymay deem proper, of the amount of callon each
share of stock,and the time and place of itspayment .
§ 9. The presidentand directorsmay appoint annually
such officersas they may deem necessary,with such com-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 69
م
pensationastheymay deem just; among whom the treas-
urer shall give bond in such sums as they may prescribe,
with surety to be approved by the board , to pay over

and the board of trustees of Irvine or of Clay City,by

to the capitalstock of said company ,and levy a tax to pay

hundred dollarsper mile for said towns ,or eitherof them ;
and any other corporation or individual may subscribe

§ 11. This act shallbe in effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 17 , 1884.

AN ACT to amend an act, entitled " An act to incorporate the Falmouth
Turnpike Road Company , in Pendleton county ," approved April twenty-
first, one thousand eighthundred and seventy-three.

ident and directorsof said company , as now provided for

§2 .The presidentand directorsof saidcompany now

sage ofthisact,allrecordsand propertyof saidcompany

CHAPTER 843 .
AN ACT toprohibitthe saleof intoxicatingliquorin the countiesof Pike,
Letcher , and Martin .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any person,persons,
or corporationto sellany spirituous,vinous or malt liq-
uors,or a mixture of either, in the counties of Letcher ,
Pike , or Martin ; and any person thus offending shallbe
deemed to have been guilty of a high misdemeanor , and
upon indictmentof a grand jury and convictionthereof,
shall be fined in any sum not lessthan one hundred dol-
lars nor more than two hundred dollars .
§ 2. If any person has a knowledge of a violation of
this act, and shall filehis or her affidavitwith a magis-
tratefor county judge or policejudge of the county in
which such offense has been committed , disclosing the
fact, the officerwith whom such affidavitwas filed shall
immediately issuehiswarrant forthe arrestof the offender,
and give him or her a speedy examining trial; and if he
shallbelieve such person to be guilty,he shallgrant such
personbailinthe sum of one hundred dollars,to answer
such indictment as may be returned against such person
by the grand jury of said county .
§ 3. All finesand forfeituresaccruingunder the provis-
ions of this act shall inure to the benefit of the common
school fund of the State .
§4 . This act repeals alllaws authorizing county judges
or other officersto grant licenseto any person or corpo-
ration to vend ardent spiritsin said counties.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 71
§5 . This act shall take effect from and after the fif-
teenth day of June , one thousand eight hundred and
-eighty-four.
Approved April 17, 1884 .

poses,and to provide forthe payment of same .

lows : nine thousand dollars to redeem the bonds issued

CHAPTER 845 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kennedy's Creek and Bedford'sStationTurnpike
Road Company ,in Bourbon county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a company is hereby formed ,for the purpose-
of constructing a turnpike road from a point on the farm
of H. Clay Hutchcraft, in Bourbon county , on the east
sideof the Paris and Clintonvillepike,about opposite the
school-house ; thence down Kennedy's creek to Bedford's
Station, on the Kentucky Central Railroad Extension ;
thence crossingthe railroad, and thence down Kennedy's
creek to a point on the Paris and Winchester Turnpike at
or near the bridge over Kennedy's creek on said last
named pike; said company to be known as " The Kenne-
dy's Creek and Bedford'sStation Turnpike Road Com-
pany," and by thatname shallbe a body-politicand cor-
porate,with power to sue and be sued,to contractand be
contractedwith,to plead and be impleaded.
§ 2. The capital stock of said company shall be seven
thousand five hundred dollars, divided into shares of
twenty -five dollars each .
§3 . That H. Clay Hutchcraft , Ben . F. Bedford ,J. W.
Harmon ,Eli Penn ,John C. Clay , Sidney P. Clay ,James-
Craig,and J.Walker Muir are hereby appointedcommis-
sionersto perform the duties of this act; and they shall
take subscriptionsto the capital stock of said company
by takingfrom the subscriberssuch obligationas,in their
discretion,they may think best.
§3 . When twenty -five shares of said stock are sub-
scribed, said commissioners ,or a majority of them ,shalb

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 73 :
notifythe saidsubscribersthata meeting of the subscrib-
ers to the stock of said company will be held at a time
and place ,as they may designate in said notice, for the
purpose of choosing officers; and in the electionof such
officers each shareholder shall be entitled to one vote for
each share of stock held by him , and he may vote in per-
son or by proxy in writing.
§4 . The width of said road shallnot exceed sixtyfeet,
and the part covered with metal shall not be less than
sixteen feet,unless the Bourbon county court of claims.
shall,in making itssubscriptionof stock to said company ,
agree to a lesswidth .
§ 5. The officersof said company shallbe a president,
five directors , a treasurer , and secretary . Said board of
directorsshallkeep a true record of all theirproceedings
in a well -bound book , which book shall at all times be
accessibleto each shareholder for inspection, upon appli-
cation therefor to said board .
§ 6. So soon as said company shall be organized , the
presidentand directorsshallpossessand be investedwith
all the powers , rights, and privileges of turnpike road
companies under the laws of thisCommonwealth , and
may do allthe acts necessary for letting,contracting,and
completing said turnpike road; and shallbe subject to all
the duties and qualifications,restrictions,penalties,fines,
and forfeituresas are given to and required of the Paris
and Winchester and Kentucky River Turnpike Road Com-
pany ,and forthatpurpose sectionseight,nine,ten,eleven,
thirteen,sixteen,seventeen ,nineteen,twenty ,twenty -one ,
and twenty -two of the act incorporating the last-named
company ,approved February twenty-fifth,one thousand
eight hundred and forty-eight, so far as not inconsistent.
with the provisions of this act,are hereby enacted and
made a part of thischarter.
§7 . The said company shallhave the power to erect a
toll-gate or gates on the line of said road ,and to charge
such tollsfor the distance traveled as are allowed by the
general laws of thisState.
§ 3. This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
after itspassage .
.
:
Approved April 17, 1884 .

CHAPTER 846 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe sale of or trafficin spirituous, vinous, or malt
liquorsin the town of Providence, Webster county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That it shallbe unlawful for any person to sell
or barter, or sufferitsold or bartered at his instance,any
spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors, or any intoxicating
drinks ,in the town of Providence , Webster county , Ken-
tucky ; and any person so offending shall, on conviction,
be subject to a fine of not less than ten nor more than
one hundred dollars for each offense,to be recovered
either by indictment in the Webster circuitcourt, or by
warrant in the name of the Commonwealth in the police
courtof Providence,or by warrant beforeany justiceof
the peace forWebster county. One -half of the finescol-
lected under the provisions of thisact shallbe paid to the
trustees of the Providence common school district,and be
appliedin aid of common schoolstherein; the otherhalf
shallbe paid to the trusteesof the jury fund forWebster
county .
§ 2. It shall be unlawful for the trustees of the town of
Providence to issue any licensefor the sale of spirituous,
vinous ,or malt liquors in said town , and any license so
issued shall be void .
§3 . Before the provisions of this act shallbe in force,
an election shall be held in the town of Providence , to
take the sense of the legal voters of said town on the
question as to whether spirituous, vinous ,or malt liquors
shallbe soldtherein. Said electionshallbe ordered by
the Webster county court at a time and place ,in said
town ,to be named , of which not less than fifteendays '
notice shallbe given by written or printed notice,posted
at fourpublicplacesin the town limits; saidcourtshall
appoint the officersto hold said election,consistingof the
sheriffor hisdeputy,and two judges and a clerk,who
shallresidein Providence. In one column of the poll-
book , at said election, shallbe entered the names of those
in favor of the sale of spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors;

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 75
and in the other column shall be entered the names of
those against the sale of spirituous,vinous ,or malt li-
quors . Those voting against the sale of spirituous, vi-
nous , or malt liquors shall be construed and counted as
voting in favor of thisact. The officersconducting said
election shall certifythe result,and cause the poll-book
to be returned within three days aftersaid electionto the
Webster county court clerk'soffice, and thereupon,or
within three days thereafter,the county judge , county
clerk, and sheriff, or any two of them , shall count and
compare the vote, and if it shall appear that a majority
of the legal voters, voting at said election,are against
the sale of spirituous, vinous ,or malt liquors,they shall
make a certificateof the fact,showing the vote as given ,
which shall be entered of record in the order -book of the
Webster county court,and theprovisionsof thisact shall
then be in fullforce. Upon the ratificationof this act
by the votersof Providence,as hereinprovided,the Web-
ster county court shall issue no license for the sale of
spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsin the town of Provi-
dence ,and any license so issued shallbe void.
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 17 , 1884 .

CHAPTER 847 .
AN ACT to enlarge and definethe duties, authority, and powers of the
commissioners for the Court-house District, in Campbell county, and
authorizingthe payment of certain fees.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the commissioners for the construction and
maintenance of a court-house in Newport , in Campbell
county,appointedby act of the Legislatureof thisState,
approved April seventeen, one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-two ,shallnext,afterthe annual payments and
expenses provided for in section four of said act,pay out
of the proceeds and collectionsof money made each year ,
by virtue of said act,the following fees and expenses ,to-

76 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
wit : Allfees due the jailerof the cityof Newport since
the firstof January , one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-two , which, under the law , were required to be
paid out of the county levy; the fees of said jailer for
services to be rendered attending the county and quar-
terlycourts at Newport ; for furnishing fuel, lights,& c.,.
for the county courts at said place, not exceeding two
dollars per day ; for putting a prisoner in irons,besides
the cost of the irons, fiftycents; for keeping and diet-
ing prisoners in the jail at Newport ,when confined for
cause other than felony or contempt of court, unless-
confined for violation of the ordinances of a city, town ,
or municipality,in that case the fees shall be paid by
such city, town , or municipality ; seven ninths of the
allowance annually made to the sheriffand clerk of the
county for ex officioservicesrendered by them; expenses
of holding elections in said courthouse district, includ-
ing cost of poll-books ; all of said payments to be
made annually ,firstpaying the amount due said jailer
since the firstof January , one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-two , as hereinbefore provided; and they shall
pay the county judge and county attorneya salaryeach
year for services rendered in said district,to be ascertained
and fixed by them , upon proof of said services.
§ 2. In the event of a deficiency of funds in the hands
of said commissioners to make the annual payments of
the feesand expenseshereindirectedto be paid,or upon
their failureand refusal to pay said fees, said county
court at Newport shall levy and assess a tax, annually ,
on all the property in said court-house districtin said
county , which may be subject to assessment and taxa-
tion for State revenue purposes ,on which the same lien
shall exist for payment of said tax, and which tax so
levied shall be paid by the owners of the property so
levied on and assessed . Said tax shall not exceed in
any one year the sum of two cents on the one hundred
dollars' valuationof said property. The property as-
sessed in said districtfor State revenue purposes , and
valuation thereof , shall be taken as that assessed for the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 77
purposes of thisact. Said annual taxes , when so levied
and assessed,shall be collected by the sheriffof Camp-
bell county by the same process of execution, distress, or
otherwise , as he is authorized to collectthe State reve-
nue ; and he shall have the same fees as are allowed him
for collectingthe State revenue ,and shall be under the
same obligationsand penalties as are imposed on him in
making collectionsof the said revenue . He shall pay
over said taxes,when collected,to the county treasurer,
who shall hold said taxes separate and to the credit of
said court-house district, or said court may appoint a
treasurer for said districtfunds,taking good and suffi-
cient bond ,with security, for its safe-keeping and dis-
bursement , which shall be on the order of said court.
The sheriff,before he collectssaid tax,shallgive bond in
said court,with proper conditions for the collection of
said tax, and for paying the same to the treasurer,and
he shallsettlehis accounts when required by said court .
For failure to make collectionof said tax,or pay over
the same ,the same remedies and proceedings may be had
against said sheriffand his sureties as are allowed by
law for delinquencies in making collectionof State reve-
nue ; and the proceedings shallbe in the name of Camp-
bell county court at Newport .
§3 . This act shall be in force from and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 17, 1884 .

ments ,and should any sums remain in theirhands after

as herein provided should be inadequate to liquidatesaid

of said Ormsby Avenue and Fourteenth street,or on the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 85
hereafter be authorized by the general council of the
city of Louisville.
§7 . Said corporation shall have the right to regulate
the transportation of freight cars and locomotives over
itstrack or tracks,as the interestof said corporation may
require,within the limitsof the terms prescribedby the
aforesaid ordinance of the general council of the city
of Louisville,or any ordinance which said council may
hereafter ordain; and shall also have the right to con-
tract with such other companies ,corporations,or natural
persons for the use of their tracks,buildings,depots,and
elevators upon such terms and conditions as said corpo-
tion may deem best.
§ 8. That any shipper or shippers over or along the
line of the railway track or tracks of the Central Trans-
fer Company shallbe entitledto side-tracks and switches,
upon such terms and conditions, within the limits and
terms prescribed by the general council of the city of
Louisville in the aforesaidordinance , or other ordinances
which the saidgeneral councilmay hereafterordain,as
may be agreed upon between said shippers and said Cen-
tralTransfer Company .
9. The capital stock of said corporation shall be fifty
thousand dollars($ 50,000) divided into shares of one hun-
dred dollarseach , which stock may be subscribed for and
taken by any natural person , corporation or company ,
which capitalstockshallbe personalproperty,and shall
be free from all claim of dower or curtesy, and may be
ansferred on the books of said corporation by the holder
or his legallyconstitutedattorney.
§ 10. The corporators as named in the firstsection of
thisact shallbe ex officiodirectors of said Central Trans-
fer Company , and shallserve untilthe second Monday in
January ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty- five,and
until their successors are elected and qualifiedas in this
act provided . And in order to continue the succession of
the presidentand directorsof said company ,three direct-
ors shallbe electedannuallyon the second Monday in
January of each year by a majorityof the stockholders

86 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
in interest, at such place as the directorsmay designate ;
and if for any cause the stockholders failto elect a board
of directorson the day prescribed in the charter,then the
directors in office shall hold over until the succeeding an-
nual electionday ,and untiltheirsuccessorsshallbe elected
and qualified; and should a vacancy occur in the board by
death or otherwise at any time , the remaining members
of the board may fillsuch vacancyor vacancies. And
the directorsshallelectone of theirnumber as president,
and other officers, at theirfirstmeeting after the annual
election of directors .
§ 11.The directorsofsaidcompany shallhave power to
adopt such by -laws for the government of the company ,
its officers,agents and employes in the general manage-
ment of the business of the corporation, as,in the opin-
ion of such directors, may be lawful and proper ; and to
alter and amend or repeal the same in accordance with
the provisions thereof.
§ 12. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 16 , 1884 .

sand eight hundred and seventy six, entitled"An act to authorize the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY , 87
CHAPTER 854 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Union Improvement Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That S. F. J. Trabue , W. G. Hamilton,and W.
H. Adams , their associates, successors, and assigns, be ,
and they arehereby,createda body-corporateand politic,
under the name and styleofthe Union Improvement Com-
pany ; and by thatname shallhave perpetualsuccession,
and be capable of suing and being sued ; may have a com-
mon seal,and alterthe same at pleasure ; may have power
to contract and be contracted with,and make by -laws for
the government of the company ,its officers, agents, and
employes in the general conduct and management of the
affairsand businessof the company ,as may be needful
and proper ,and alter,amend ,or repealthesame at will.
§2 . The capitalstock of said company may be any
amount not exceeding one million of dollars,divided into
shares of one hundred dollars each ; said stock shall be
deemed personal property,and shallbe transferableon
the books of said company in such manner as may be
prescribedby the by -laws thereof. The directorsof said
company may receivereal or personalpropertyin pay-
ment for subscriptionsto the capitalstock, upon such
terms and conditions and at such valuation as may be
agreed upon by the directorsor a majority of them and the
subscribersrespectively.
§3 . The persons named in the firstsection of thisact
shall constitute the first board of directors of said com-
pany ,and may serve as such directorsuntilthe firstmeet-
ing of the stockholdersthereof; at such firstmeeting , and
at every annual meeting thereafter,the stockholders shall
elect not less than three nor more than seven directors of
said company ,as may be prescribedby the by -laws there-
of, who shall continue in office until their successors are
elected and qualified. No one shallbe elected a director
unless he is the owner of stockin said company at the
time of his election . At all meetings of stockholders of
said company ,the stockholders shall be entitledto one

88 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vote for each share of stock so held, which may be cast in
person or by written proxy . The board of directorsshall
choose one of their number president, and appoint such
other officersand agents of said company as may be pre-
scribedby the by -laws thereof. It shall require a major-
ity, includingthe president,to constitutea quorum of the
board of directors for transaction of business .
§ 4. Said company shallhave power to purchase ,hold,
grant ,sell,convey ,mortgage ,lease,rent,use,acquire,and
dispose of any real or personal estate, in this State or
elsewhere; may engage in mining and selling coal, and
in mining and manufacturing iron and other ores and
minerals ,timber and lumber ,and transporting the same
to market ; and may erect and build or purchase such
buildings, mills, furnaces, engines, machinery, and fix.
tures as may be needful and proper for the successful
operation and prosecution of the business of said com-
pany . Said company shallalso have power to contract
with individuals or corporations for the construction and
equipment of railroads,bridges,and telegraph lines,and
all buildings,erections, and machinery necessary or ap-
purtenant thereto; and may construct,operate,or main-
tain other works of public or private improvement ; and
may receivein payment thereforproperty,money ,prom-
issory notes, bonds , stocks, or other securitiesissued by
individualsor corporations,and may sell,negotiate,and
pledge the same ,as may be deemed best by said com-
pany .
§5 . The stockholders of said corporation shallnot be ,
or be made individuallyliablefor itsdebts or liabilities.
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 17, 1884 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 89
CHAPTER 855 .
AN ACT toprohibitthe sale,directlyor indirectly,of any spirituous,vi-
nous ,ormalt liquors,or intoxicatingdrinks,inShiveley'sand Johnsontown
precincts, in Jeffersoncounty, provided a majority of eitheror both vote
in favor of it.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any person to sell, or
indirectlyin any manner dispose of,any spirituous,vi-
nous ,or malt liquors,or any intoxicatingdrink thereof,
to any person in Shiveley voting precinct,in Jefferson
county ,or inJohnsontown precinctinsaidcounty.
§2 . Any person violating the provisions of this act
shall, upon conviction thereof before any court of com-
petent jurisdiction, be finedin any sum not lessthan
twenty -fivedollarsnor more than fiftydollars.
§3 . Provided,however, That an electionshallbe held in
each of the above precincts on the thirtiethday of April
next . The county judge of said county shallappoint two
judges , a clerk, and sheriff, to hold the election at each
of the precincts named herein ; and the electionshall be
held from seven o'clock in the morning untilsix o'clock,.
afternoon; and the sheriffshallbe required to put at
least three notices in each precinct ten days before the
election in conspicuous places,that such an electionwill
be held on the thirtiethApril,one thousand eight hun-
dred and eighty-four.
§ 4. If a majority of those voting vote for thisact,that
is,no spirituous or vinous liquors, & c.,shall be sold in
thisprecinct,then this act shall be lawful ,or if one pre-
cinctvote for thislaw and the other against it,itshallbe
a law in the one so voting .
§5 . This act shallbe in force on and afteritspassage ..
Approved April 17, 1884 .
CHAPTER 856 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof J. P. Pierce, of Crittenden county .
WHEREAS , J. P. Pierce,as sheriffof Crittendencounty,
in May , one thousand eight hundred and eighty-one,

90 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
arrestedin said county James Millis,and conveyed him
to Pulaski county, and deliveredhim to the presiding
judge of the circuitcourt (then in session) of said coun-
ty,in pursuance of an attachment which issuedagainst
said Millis as a witness in said court on behalf of the Com-
monwealth vs. Robert A. Nunnelly ,and others,charged
with murder ; and whereas ,the distance traveled by said
Pierce to and from Pulaski county was seven hundred
miles, and the actual traveling expenses of the prisoner
was twenty-two dollarsand fortycents ($22.40),which
was paid by said Pierce; and whereas, he has never
received any compensation for his services, nor has he
ever been reimbursed for expenses of said Millis, there
being no law authorizing the Auditor to pay the same ;
therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor be, and he is hereby ,directed to
draw his warrant on the Treasurer in favor of the said
J. P. Pierce for the sum of ninety-two dollars and forty
cents,in payment of his services and expenses incurred
in arresting and delivering the said Millis as aforesaid .
§2 . This act to take effectfrom and afterits passage .
Approved April 17, 1884.
CHAPTER 857 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to amend the charter of the
Beaver Creek and Cumberland River Coal Company , organized under
chapterfifty-six of the General Statutes," approved February twenty-
fourth, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-two .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That , in addition to the rights and powers con-
ferredupon the Beaver Creek and Cumberland River Coal
Company by an actof thisGeneralAssembly,approved
February twenty-fourth,one thousand eight hundred and
-eighty-two ,and entitled"An act to amend the charter of
the Beaver Creek and Cumberland River Coal Company ,
-organizedunder chapter fifty-six of the General Stat

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 91
utes ," the said corporation,for the preservation and main-
tenance of the public peace and good order, is hereby
authorizedto appointand employ ,at theirown expense,
a special and proper policeforce,who are hereby author-
ized,and whose duty it shall be , at all times of the day
and night, within the boundaries of the land owned by
said corporation,and allterritorylying within three miles
of any part of said land , to preserve the public peace ,
prevent crime, protectrightsof persons and property,
guard the public health, preserve order, arrest offenders,
and generally to obey and enforce all criminal laws of
this Commonwealth and of the United States ; and for
such purposes they shallhave fullpower and authority to
pursue and arrest,anywhere within Pulaski county ,any
and allpersons suspected of,or charged with,having com-
mitted any crime or offensewithin the limitsaforesaid.
§2 . That every person appointed by the authoritiesof
the said corporation to act as such policemen shall,before
entering upon the discharge of his duties,execute a bond
before the county court of Pulaski county and take the
same oath of officeas isprescribedby chaptereighty-two ,
sections thirteen and fourteen ,of the General Statutes of
this Commonwealth for police officers; and such oath of
officeshall be administered by the judge of the county
court of Pulaski and entered on the record of said court .
§3 . That all persons arrested by any member of such
police force for any offenseshallbe committed to the
county jail,as is provided in all other cases for similar
offenses,and shallbe subjectto fineand imprisonment,or
both,in likemanner as ifthe arresthad been made by
officersor persons duly qualifiedunder and in pursuance
of the general laws of this Commonwealth to make an
arrestfor a similarcrime or offense. It shallbe the duty
of every justiceof the peace or police judge in Pulaski
county to try any person or persons so arrested and
charged with crime or offense,in like manner as if he or
they had been arrested by a sheriff, constable, marshal ,
policeman,or otherministerialofficerauthorizedto make
such arrest; and such justiceof the peace or policejudge

92 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shallaward such fineor punishment,or both,as iftheper-
son orpersonsso finedor punished had been adjudged or
convicted upon arrestmade by a sheriff,constable, mar-
shal,policeman , or other ministerialofficerauthorized to
make such an arrest.
§4 . That the members or persons constituting such
police force shallhave the same privilegesand the same
power to arrest wrong -doers, and preserve the public
peace , and shall be treated in like manner as sheriffs,
constables,marshals,policemen,or otherministerialoffi-
cershave or are,and as ifthey had been duly electedor
appointed in pursuance of the general laws of thisCom-
monwealth ,and shallbe subjectto thesame rights,duties,
and penalties as such sheriffs,constables,marshals ,police-
men ,or other ministerialofficers,or as persons so elected
or appointed .
§5 . That said Beaver Creek and Cumberland River
Coal Company ,in additionto the rightsand powers here-
toforeconferredupon it,shallhave the rightand power
to purchase other coal mines or coal.companies within
this Commonwealth , or consolidate itscapital stock with
the capital stock of any other coal mines or coal com-
pany or companies owning or operating any coal mine
or mines now in operation, or which may hereafterbe in
operationin thisCommonwealth ,upon such terms and
conditions,both as to manner and time of payment , or
consolidation or exchange of stock, as may be agreed
upon between it and such company or companies ; and
said Beaver Creek and Cumberland River Coal Company
shall have power to issue itsown capital stock in pay-
ment of such purchase ,or in pursuance of any agree--
ment that may be effectedbetween itand such company
or companies fora consolidationof itscapitalstockwith
the capital stock of such company or companies ; and it
shall be lawful for any coal company or companies in
this Commonwealth to sellits or their property, or con-
solidateitsor theircapital stock with the Beaver Creek
and Cumberland River Coal Company ,upon such terms
and conditions as may be agreed upon between the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 93
boards of directors of such companies : Provided, That
in no event shallsuch purchase or consolidationbe law-
ful or effectualuntila majorityin interestof the stock-
holders of said Beaver Creek and Cumberland River
Coal Company and such other company or companies
shall ratifysuch purchase or consolidation,in person or
by proxy ,at a meeting held by each for such purpose ,
of which meetings ten days' notice,in writing, shallbe
mailed by order of the board of directors of each ,stat-
ing the object,time , and place of holding such meeting .
6. This act shall be in force from and after its pas-
sage .

greatlyby the charityof hisneighbors; therefore,

of Kentucky :

is hereby , exempted from State and county taxes.

pany , of Nicholascounty.

CHAPTER 862 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the turnpike roads in Mason county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe the duty of the county judge of the
county of Mason ,upon the petitionof a majorityof the
land-owners on each sideof any turnpikeroad in Mason
county,and withinone mile of it,to make an order,and
enter the same of record, appropriating all road taxes
owing from the people along the line of said road , on
each side of it,and within one mile of it,to keeping such
road in repair; but this act shall not apply to any road
that ismore than fivemiles long .
§2 When an order is made appropriating the road tax ,
and requiringthe same to be used in keeping any road in
repair under the provisions of section one of this act, it
shallbe the duty of the county court of Mason county to
appoint the president of the turnpike company owning
said road surveyor thereof, and itishereby made his duty
to dischargeallthe dutiesof a surveyorof a public road
under and pursuant to the provisions of the General Stat-
utes ,within the boundaries in which the road tax isauthor-
izedto be expended,to wit,one mile on each sideof the
road . Such surveyor shallhave allthe powers and author-
itiesconferred on surveyors of roads by chapter ninety-
four of the General Statutesof Kentucky and acts amend-
atory thereof, and for a violation of his duties shall be
subjectto allthe penaltiesand punishments to which
surveyorsof roads are made subjectby the generallaws
of the State .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
[Became a law without thesignatureof the Governor .]

pany , for the purpose of working said turnpike, at the

ous acts in regard to Bardstown ," approved March thir-
teenth, one thousand eight hundred and seventy-eight,

erty assessedunder the laws of the State of Kentucky

CHAPTER 869 .
AN ACT to protect the property of the Short Route Railway Transfer
Company , of Louisville.

of Kentucky :

or other water -craft,of any descriptionwhatever ,or for

land and Davis ,the said Sutherland was to collect the

situated thereinfor the years one thousand eight hundred
and seventy-nine,one thousand eight hundred and eighty,
and one thousand eight hundred and eighty-one , and
when so appointed such receiver shall have the same
power to collectsuch taxes as the sheriffand other offi-
cers have to collectthe State revenue and county levy
taxes and ad valorem taxes under the laws now in force
in the State of Kentucky .
§2 . That said receiver, when appointed , shallhave the
righttoappointone ormore deputiesto collectsaidtaxes,
with the same power that he would himself.
§3 . That he shall have the rightto listsuch taxes as
may be due on said back taxes with any sheriff, con-
stable,or collectingofficerof thisState,forthe purpose

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 101
of collectingthe same , who shall have the right to col-
lectthem as the said receiver is authorized by the act.
§ 4. That the said receiver shall have the further time
of three years,from the passage of this act, to collect
said taxes by levy or distraintor garnishee.
§ 5. This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 19 ,1884.
CHAPTER 872 .
AN ACT incorporatingthe Fourth Avenue Highland Park Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Woodford H. Dulaney ,John T. Moore ,Charles
Bremaker , and Delaney E. Stark, who are the owners of
a tract of land in Jeffersoncounty ,laid out by them for
park purposes ,and known as the Fourth Avenue High-
land Park , and their associates and successors,be ,and
they are hereby,created a body-politicand corporate,
under the name and style of the Fourth Avenue High-
landPark Company ,forthe purposeof owning,improv-
ing,and managing the said park ; and with power to sue
and be sued ,contract and be contracted with,to have a
common seal,and to exercise all powers incident to the
purposes of the said corporation; and with perpetual suc-
cession so long as the said corporation shall continue to
admit to the said park all well-behaved people free of
charge , at reasonable hours ,and under reasonable regu-
lations: Provided,That the franchiseshereby granted shall
determine and the said corporation shall be dissolved
when it shall discontinue the said free admission .
§2 . The affairsof the said corporation shallbe managed
by a board of directors, composed of five stockholders,
and by a presidentand secretaryand treasurer,to be chosen
by the said board , who shall be elected annually by the
stockholders,at such time and place as the by -laws of the
saidcorporationshallappoint; and shallcontinuein of-
ficeuntiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified. Any

102 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vacancy in thesaid board,or in the said offices,may be
filledby the board of directors,and a majority of the
members of the said board shall constitutea quorum for
the transaction of business .
§3 . The capital stock of the said corporation shallbe
limited to a maximum amount of five hundred thousand
dollars,divided into fivethousand shares of one hundred
dollars each .
§4. No turnpike, or countyroad,or publicpassway
shallbe opened through the said park ,so long as the road
now open through and around the western part of the
said park shallremain open .
§5 . During the existence of this corporation,and so
longas itshallcontinueadmittingtothesaidpark allwell-
behaved people free of charge , at reasonable hours, and
under reasonable regulations,no toll-gate shallbe placed
on any road leading from the said park to the cityof
Louisville,lessthan two hundred feet south of the north
line of the said park.
§6 . That nothing contained in thisact shallin any way
affect any judicial proceeding now pending ,having for
its object the establishinga road through said grounds ,
nor the existingfranchises or rights of the Jeffersonand
Bullitt Turnpike Company , or the National Turnpike
Company .
§7 . All laws and parts of laws in conflictherewith are
hereby repealed .
§ 8. This act shalltake effectat itspassage .
Approved April 19 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 874 .
AN ACT to incorporate the City Fire and Marine Insurance Company of
Louisville .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That George S. McKiernan ,George W. Anderson ,
James S. Pirtle, Adolph L. Schmidt, Samuel A. Miller,
Wm . Patterson, jr., Samuel L. Avery, Wm . Lindsay,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 103
Henry Warren , Alfred Pirtle, M. R. Wheat , Lewis G ,
Wells ,and James A. Leech ,together with theirassociates,
successors,and assigns,be ,and they are hereby ,made a
corporationand body -politicunder the name and styleof
the City Fire and Marine Insurance Company of Louis-
ville,and shallcontinue untilthe firstday of January,
one thousand nine hundred and fifty; and by that name
are made capable in law to have,purchase,or receive,
possess,enjoy,and retain,to them and theirsuccessors,
lands,rents,tenements , goods ,chattels, public or private
securitiesof any kind ,quality, or nature whatsoever ,not
exceeding at any one time the sum of five hundred thou-
sand dollars, and to sell and dispose of the same at any
time or in any manner ; and to sue and be sued , plead
and be impleaded , answer and be answered ,defend and
be defended,in any courtof record,or in any otherplace
whatsoever ; also to make and use a common seal, and
the same to break , alter, and renew at pleasure; also to
ordain and establishsuch by -laws as may be necessary
and convenient for the government of said corporation,
not contrary to law .
§ 2. The capital stock of thiscompany shallnot exceed
one hundred thousand dollars, and shallbe divided into
shares of fiftydollarseach: but the capital stock may be
increased by vote of the stockholders,from time to time ,
to two hundred and fiftythousand dollars.
§3 . The corporatorsnamed in thisact,or any threeof
them ,shall, within twelve months from the passage of the
same ,open books forthe subscriptionof stock,and when
two thousand shares shallhave been subscribed,they shall
give notice of a meeting of the subscribers,when ten dol-
larsshallbe paid in hand on each share subscribed for,
and the company shallbe organized when the remaining
forty dollarson each share shall be secured,to be paid at
the time and in the manner that shall be designated by
the share-holders who have paid in ten dollars on each
share subscribed for. The corporators named ,or any of
them , who shall fail to subscribe for stock , shall cease to
be members of the company ; and allsuch subscribersas

104 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shallfailto pay ten dollarson each share subscribed for
on the day designated in the second notice,shallcease to
be stockholders,and the stock subscribed for by them may
be subscribed for by others, under the direction of the
person or persons who received the originalsubscription.
And in case any subscribershallfailto pay any callmade
by the board of directors(who are hereby authorized to
make such calls) for the unpaid balance of his subscrip-
tion,and remain in default for the space of sixtydays ,
the board may ,in theirdiscretion,forfeithis stock for the
benefitof the company . And the board of directorsmay ,
in theirby -laws ,prescribethe manner in which the capi-
tal stock shall be increased ,but in no event shall it exceed
the sum of two hundred and fiftythousand dollars.
§ 4. The management of saidcompany shallbe under
the direction and control of a president and twelve di-
rectors,who shallbe stockholders,and each share of said
stock shall entitle the holder to one vote , and the shares
may be voted by proxy , or by the administrator or exec-
utorof deceased owner . The stockholdersshall(meet]
atthe officeof the company on thefirstMonday in July of
each year , and elect a president and twelve directors
who shallcontinue in officeuntilthe firstMonday in July
of the ensuing year ,and until their successors shall be
elected, of which electionprevious notice shall be given
in one or more newspapers printed in Louisville; and in
case of the death ,resignation,or removal from the city
of the president or director,the remaining directorsshall
fillthe vacancy for the residue of the year . None but a
citizen of this State and a share holder shall be eligible-
as president or director.
§ 5. The president and directors shall elect one of the
directorsvice president, and shallhave power to appoint
such officersand agents under them as shallbe necessary
for executing the business of said company ; to require
and take bond and security for the discharge of their
respectiveduties and trusts. The president and six di-
rectors shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of
business,or seven directors without a president,one of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 105
whom shall be chosen president for the time being , in
the absence of the vicepresident.
§6 . Whenever the sum of one hundred thousand dol-
larsshallbe paid,they shallhave fullpower and author-
ity, in the name of said company , to make insurance
against fire, on the general conditionsand principles
of fireinsurance,to the fullextentnow enjoyed by other
companies now incorporatedforthe same purpose by the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and to make all kinds of
insurance on vessels,boats,and craftsof every descrip-
tion,on the interiorrivers,lakes,bays,bayous,canals in
the United States,or on thehighseas,as wellas on goods ,
wares , merchandise, produce,specie,freights,and every
description of property transported by land or by water
within the United States,or to or from any foreignport
or country ,or on the high seas,on the general conditions
and principlesof marine insurance,and to do and per-
form all things relatingto the said objects,conformable
to the provisions of this act and the laws of the Com-
monwealth .
§7 . The form of certificates of shares and manner of
transfershallbe regulatedby the by laws of the corpora-
tion,and a lienis hereby created in favor of the corpo-
ration on the stock of each share-holder for any claim that
the company may have forthe unpaid partof his shares
or other debts due or arisingto said company ; and no
transfershallbe made except by the sanction of the board
of directors. The company shall have the right to pur-
chase the stock of any stockholder who withdraws his
business from the company ,or whose business is unsatis-
factory to president and directors,at a discount of five
per cent,upon the value of the stock as shown by the
books of the company .
§ 8. At any regularmeeting of the stockholders(a ma-
jorityof the stockconsenting)it may be determined to
declare a portion of the profitsof the company by way
of returnpremium to such policy-holdersas the stock-
holders at such meeting may direct.
§ 9. On the firstMonday inJulyin each year a fairand
clear statement of the affairsof the company shall be

106 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
made out and exhibited to the stockholders , and a divi-
dend of such proportion of the profitsbe made as a ma-
jority of the president and directors shalldecide ; but in
no case shallthe capital stock be diminished.
§ 10. In no case shallthiscorporation exercisethe busi-
ness of banking by issuing notes as an incorporated bank ,
but they may loan theirmoney on public or private secu-
rities: Provided, That no more money shall be used as
aforesaidthan is actuallypaid in on the capitaland the
premium on hand .
§ 11. Should any electiondirected to be held under the
charter of said company ,from any cause ,be neglected to
be held on the day designated ,or to be held on a subse-
quent day ,the corporationshallnot from that cause be
dissolved; but any and all electionsmade in good faith
shall be held and deemed valid, as if held on the day
designated by the act of incorporation.
§12 The stockholders, in person or by proxy , repre-
senting a majority of stock in the said insurance com-
pany ,may at any time make ,alter,or amend the by -laws
of said company , and may , by the by-laws ,increase or
reduce the number of directors from time to time , and
change the time of the annual electionof president and
directors, and the time of their going out of office, and
change the time for making semi -annual statements to
the stockholders,and the time fordeclaring dividends.
§ 13. Said company may purchase or erect a building
in which to have an office; and such building shall,at a
faircash value ,be estimated and treated as assets,under
the laws of Kentucky regulating fireand marine insur-
ance companies .
§ 14. This act shalltake effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 19, 1884 .

CHAPTER 879 .

including the residence of J. L. Manzy ; thence and in-
cluding the residence of J. M. Gill; thence and including

B. Reevis (colored); thence to thebeginning,and includ

ing the said late residence of C. D. Lewis ,deceased ; that

with ,plead and be impleaded ,defend and be defended ,

tees, who shall hold their officeuntilthe firstMonday in

! 111LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . That allthelegislativepower and authorityofsaid

concerns thereof, and the government and control of

ney ,a treasurer,a clerk,and assessor,and such other offi-
cers as the board of trusteesmay ,from time to time,direct
and appoint .
§5 . That an election shall be held in said town on the

board of trusteesmay appoint ,and of which at leastten
days ' previous public notice shall be given by written or
printed notices,posted in at least four public places in
said town by the town clerk.
§6 . That at said electionthere shallbe elected by the
qualifiedvoters of said town fivetrustees,a police judge ,
and a marshal ,and the person or persons (qualified to
hold such positions) having the highestnumber of votes
cast at said election for either of such offices shall be

cept the policejudge and marshal,which shallbe for

ivelycontinue in officeone year,and untilthe appoint-
ment and qualificationof theirsuccessors; but the board

men , firemen, surveyors,gaugers , weighers , and inspect-
ors,and otherlikeofficers,to continuein officeduringthe

a failureby the people to electany officersherein re-
quired to be elected,the board of trusteesmay appoint

members of the board of trustees; but no officer who
has been electedby the people shallbe removed ,except

heard in his defense ; and the board of trustees shall have

such trial; and shall proceed , within ten days , to hear

lected, or may hereafter refuse or neglect, for ten days
afterdemand ,to account for and pay over to the person
orpartyauthorizedto receivethe same any publicmoney

not ,at the time of his election,a citizen and a voter in

ive officeshall have an equal number of votes for the
-same office, the election shall be determined by lot, in

the morning and six o'clock in the evening .
§ 14. That every free male citizenof the United States
over twenty -one years of age ,residentof thisState two
years,or inthe county of Todd one year,or in saidtown
six months ,shallbe qualifiedto vote for any town officer
electiveby the people .

ishingillegalvotingat the State electionsshallapply to

ject,however ,to allof the provisionsof thisacttouching

qualifications,elections,and returnsof itsmembers ; and

by the by -laws or ordinances .

§ 17. That the outgoing town clerk shall,immediately

quiredby thetreasurer,allfinesor othermoneys received

appointed shallbe vestedwith allthe powers and per-
form all the duties of chairman ,except in regard to act-

ceive as compensation for his services such sum as the

jects submitted to him by the board of trusteesor itscom-
mittees. He shall,for his services,receivethirtyper cent.

ent and prosecutes. For other legalserviceshe shall
