trustees .
§6 . That the treasurer shall receive all moneys be-
longing to the town , and keep an accurate account of
all receipts and expenditures in such a manner as the
board of trusteesmay direct. All moneys shallbe drawn
from the treasury by warrant from the chairman or act-
ing chairman of the board of trustees,countersigned by
the clerk. Such warrant shallspecifyforwhat purpose
the amount specifiedtherein is drawn . The treasurer

116 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shallexhibittothe board of trustees,on the firstMonday
in May of each year , and oftener if required, a full and
detailedaccount of allreceiptsand expendituressince
last report, and also the state of the treasury, which
account shall be filed in the office of the clerk .
§7 That itshallbe the duty of the marshal to attend
all sessions of the police court, and to give the town
attorneypreviousnoticeof allproceedingsin thatcourt,
to which itishis duty to attend, and to attend to procur-
ing evidence in cases in which the town or board of trus-
tees of Elkton isa party within the limitsof the town ; and
in allmatters of a criminal nature arising under the by-
laws or ordinances of said town or the Commonwealth of
Kentucky ,he shallpossessthesame powers as sheriffsand
constables. He shallexecute allprocess,civiland crimi-
nal, issued and to him directed by the chairman of the
board of trustees,the police judge ,the presiding judge of
the Todd county court, or a justice of the peace . He
shallpossess the power and authority of a constable of
this Commonwealth ,and his powers for discharging his
duties shallbe co -extensive with Todd county . He shall
be entitled,as compensation ,to the same fees allowed by
law to constables and sheriffsfor similar services, col-
lectable in the same manner as sheriffs' and constables '
fee -bills are . For other services , he shall be entitled to
such fees and compensation as the board of trusteesmay
fix by by -law or ordinance . He shall be liable in the
same cases,way ,and manner ,and to the same extent, as
sheriffsand constables. He shall perform such other
duties not inconsistent with this act, and make such
returnsand reportsof his acts,as may be prescribedby
by-law or ordinance,and as hereinafterdescribed. He
may or may not be appointed collector; but if he is, he
shall be required to give, in addition to the marshal's
bond ,a bond as collector.
§ 8. It shall be the duty of the collector to collectall
taxes and assessments which may be levied by the board
of trustees, and perform such other duties as may be
herein prescribed or required by the board of trustees :

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 117
Provided, That the board of trusteesshall have power to
require alllicense money to be paid directlyto the treas-
urer,or to appoint some other person than the collector
to collect the same .
§ 9. The assessor shall perform all the duties which
the board of trustees may ,by by -law or ordinance , pre-
scribein relationto the assessingof property for the
purpose of levying the taxes imposed by the board of
trustees .
§ 10. That the board of trustees shall have power ,
from time to time,and at alltimes,to require further
and otherduties,and fixthe compensation of allofficers
whose duties are herein prescribed, and prescribethe
powers and duties of allofficersappointed or elected to
any officeunder this act, whose duties are not herein
specificallymentioned,and fixtheircompensation.
§ 11.The policejudge, treasurer, collector, and mar-
shal shall severally,before they enter on the duties of
their respective offices,execute a bond ,payable to the
board of trustees of Elkton ,in such sum , with such con-
ditions and sureties, as the board of trustees may ap-
prove, conditionedthat they willfaithfullyexecute the
duties of their offices, and pay over to such persons ,
and at such times as they may respectivelybe entitledto
the same ,all moneys or property that may come to their
hands as such officers,which bond , with the approval of
the board of trusteescertifiedthereonby the clerk,shall
be filedand carefullykept in the officeof the clerk of
the Todd county court.
§12 . That the board of trusteesof Elkton, or any
person injuredby the breach of any or eitherof said
bonds, may ,in any court having jurisdiction, prosecute
suit or motion against said officerby whose breach of
duty they have been injured, and his sureties,or any or
eitherof them ,or the personalrepresentativeor heirsor
deviseesof any ,or eitherof them ,as now provided by
law for sheriffsor constables,and the bonds , or either of
them ,shall not be void or satisfiedby the firstor any
other recovery,untilthe board of trusteesof Elkton ,and

118 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
every person aggrievedby the acts or omissionsof such
officer,shallhave been recompensed .
§13 . That if any person ,having been an officerin said
town ,shallnot,within ten days after notificationand re-
quest,deliverto his successorin officeall the property,
papers,and effectsof every descriptionin hispossession,
belonging to said town ,or appertainingto the officehe
held,he shallforfeitand pay for the use of said town
one hundred dollars,besides allcosts and damages for his
neglect or refusal so to do .
ARTICLE IV .
Police Courts .
§1 . That there shall be established in said town a court
to be styled the " Elkton Police Court ," which shall be
heldby one policejudge,who shallbe electedas herein-
before provided .
§2 . That the Elkton policecourtshallhave exclusive
originaljurisdictionof allprosecutionsforviolationsof
theby laws or ordinancesof saidtown ,withouttheinter-
vention of a jury, unless demanded by defendant; and
concurrentjurisdictionwith justicesof the peace of all
pleas of the Commonwealth arisingwithin the limitsof
saidtown . Said court shallhave power and authority to
take recognizances from persons charged with offenses
cognizable before said court, to appear and answer the
same ,that the circuitcourts have like power to take the
bond as forfeitedand pronounce judgment thereon and
enforce fullcompliance with the same ,and shallhave the
power of two justicesof the peace in examining courts.
§3 . That the judge of said court shallbe a conserva-
tor of the peace , and may order arrest for all offenses
against the laws of the Commonwealth or by -laws and
ordinances of the town , and for those committed in his
presence may order arrestwithout warrant,the person
to be dealt with according to the laws of this Common-
wealth or the by -laws and ordinances of the town . If he
thinksitnecessary,he may detainany person or persons

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 119
so offending in custody in the county jail or other safe
place .
§4. That the policecourtmay be holden at such place
as the board of trusteesmay designate,or,they failingto
designate or provide a suitable place, at such a place in
said town as the judge may select. The judge shallhave
power to fixthe time forholdingcourtforthe trialof
-criminalor penal casesas,in his discretion,the business
before him may require.
§5 . That the policecourt shallbe a court of record,
and the judge thereof shall act as clerk; and certified
copies of the records of said court,over the signature of
the judge , shall be evidence in any court in this Com-
monwealth .
§ 6. That all proceedings in and process from said
court shall be in the name of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky . The process shallbe made returnable to the police
court ,and may be directed to the town marshal ; but the
judge may direct the process to any sheriff, constable,
jailer, coroner , or policeman , and the person executing
the process of said court shallbe entitledto the same fees
for likeservices,and to the likeremedy for the collection
thereof,as the sheriffsand constables of this Common-
wealth , and shallbe subject to like finesand penaltiesfor
like offensesand failures,recoverable in the same manner .
§7 . That the fines and forfeituresrecovered in the
policecourt in favorof the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
and those in favor of the town of Elkton ,or of the board
of trusteesof Elkton , are hereby granted to the town of
Elkton .
§ 8. That allfinesor forfeituresrecovered in the police
court are to be collectedby the marshal or otherofficerto
whom executionor capiasprofine isdirected,and to be
paid by him to the town treasurer; but before the issual
of the execution or other finalprocess,the defendant may
pay the fineor forfeitureto the judge of the policecourt,
who shallpay the same to the town treasurer.
§ 9. That the defendant may have the right, at any
time,to replevy,forthreemonths ,any judgment ,fine,or

120 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
forfeitureof thepolicecourt,or execution,or otherfina
processthereon, by giving bond , with good security,in
the same manner as allowed by law in similar cases in
favor of the Commonwealth .
B
§ 10. That a return of " not found " on a capiaspro
fine, or " no property" on an execution issued on any
judgment in the policecourt,shallauthorizean attach-
ment out of equity in favor of the board of trustees of
Elkton against the defendant or defendants,in the same
manner that a return of no property authorized an at-
tachment in equity in judgments in the circuitcourts.
§ 11. That in alljudgments for fines rendered by the
policecourt,whether in favor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky or the board of trustees of Elkton , and which
shallnot be paid at the time of renderingthe same ,an
execution against the estate of defendant may be issued,
or a capiasprofinemay be issued,requiringthe imprison-
ment of the defendant in the county jail, or the defend-
ant may be placed at work on the streetsor public works
of said town ; but the imprisonment or work shall not
exceed one day for each one dollar of said fine.
§ 12. That appeals shall lie from the decisions and
judgments of the police court to the Todd quarterly court
in all cases where the fine exceeds ten dollars and is less
than fiftydollars, exclusive of cost, and to the Todd
circuitcourt where the fine is fiftydollars or upwards ,
exclusive of cost; and where the punishment is im .
prisonment alone, exceeding ten days, an appeal shall
lie either to the Todd quarterly or circuitcourt. In all
cases of appeal in criminal or penal cases,the execution
of the judgment shall not be suspended unless the de .
fendant,within ten days afterthe renditionof the judg-
ment , causes to be executed ,before the judge or clerk of
the courtto which the appeal is taken,a covenant,with
good security,in all respects as required on appeals to
such court in criminal and penal cases by the Criminal
Code of Practice of Kentucky .
§ 13. That the policecourt shallalways be open for
hearing and determining such penal and criminal cases

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 121
as arewithinitsjurisdiction; and may make such rea-
sonablerulesand regulationsforthe speedy hearing and
determining of all such cases as to afford reasonable
noticeto the parties,and a prompt administrationof jus-
tice,
§ 14. That the provisionsof the Criminal Code of
Practice shall regulate the proceedings of said court in
criminaland penal cases; and the provisionsof the Civil
Code of Practice shall regulate the proceedings in civil
cases .
§ 15. That the fees of said judge , whether acting as
judge or clerk or otherwise,shallbe the same in all cases
as are now or may hereafterbe given by law to officers
performing like services; and fees and costs shall be
taxed in cases in said court to the same extent,in the
same manner ,and under the same regulations,as in courts
having similarjurisdiction.
ARTICLE V.
General Powers and Duties of the Board of Trustees.
§1 . That the board of trustees shall hold stated meet--
ings, and the chairman may call meetings by notices to
each of the members of the board of trustees ,either writ-
ten or oral; but if written,such notices must be served
personally or leftat theirusual place of abode or busi-
ness . A majority of the persons elected trustees shall
constitute a quorum .
§2 . That the board of trustees shall have the manage-
ment and controlof the finances,and allthe property,
real, personal,and mixed ,belonging to the corporation ;
and shallhave power to contract and be contracted with
for and on behalf of said town ; and shall have power to
provide for the erection and repair of all needful public
buildingsforthe town ; and to provide forthe inclosing
or improving allpublic grounds belonging to the town ,
and shall likewise have power within the jurisdictionof
saidtown by by -law or ordinance-
First. To restrainand prohibitalldescriptionsof gam-
ing and fraudulentdevices,and allplayingof dice,cards,

122 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and other games of chance whereat anything of value is
bet, won ,or lost.
Second. To regulate the tax for the selling of any
ardent spiritsor otherintoxicating,fermented,vinous,or
malt liquorsby any hotel-keeper,tavern-keeper,or inn-
keeper in said town under and by virtueof the present
statutorylaws of thisCommonwealth : Provided, That ,
in additiontothe presentState tax,the tax (under this
act,if such trafficbe toleratedin the town ) upon licensed
hotel, tavern, or innkeepersshallnot exceed one thou-
sand dollars for a year ; and the tax upon any merchant
or merchants to sellsuch liquors in said town shall not
exceed two hundred and fiftydollars,in addition to the
amount now required by law .
Third. To prohibit the giving away of any spirituous,
vinous ,malt ,or intoxicating liquors to any minor or ap-
prenticewithoutthewrittenconsentof hisorherguard-
ian,parent , master ,or mistress. Said trusteesshallhave
power to licenseor authorizeany saloon or coffee-house
keeper to sellspirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors, and re-
quire each of them to pay an annual tax not exceeding
one thousand dollars a year ,and no such licenseshallbe
transferable .
Fourth. To license,tax,regulate,suppress,and prohibit
billiardtables,pigeon -hole tables,Jenny Lind tables, and
allsimilartables and contrivances,nine or ten-pin alleys,
ball-alleys, and shooting galleries.
Fifth. To license,tax, and regulate auctioneers,distil-
lers, brewers ,and pawnbrokers , and to tax or lay duties
on the sale of goods at auction.
Sixth. To license,tax,and regulatehawkers ,peddlers,
and all itinerantvenders of medicines or other goods ,
wares , or merchandise ,whether at auction or otherwise ;
also allagents of insurance and lotteryoffices,and agen-
cies doing or pretending or offeringto do business in said
town , and to prevent any business that kind being done
without license .
Seventh. To tax, regulate,suppress,and prohibitallex-
hibitionsof common showmen , shows of every kind by

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 123
itinerantand other persons, exhibitionsof natural or arti-
ficialcuriosities, caravans, circuses, theatricalperform-
ances,and allotherexhibitions(exceptby the citizensof
the town or county).
Eighth . To authorize the chairman of the board of trus-
tees,or other proper officerof the town ,to grant and issue
licenses,and direct the time and manner of issuing and
registeringthereof,and the feesto be paid therefor. No
licenseshallbe granted forlessthan one year,and not
lessthan one dollarshallbe requiredto be paid forany
license under this act; and the fee for issuing the same
shall not be more than fiftycents.
Ninth. To preventand suppressany riot,rout,or breach
of the peace,noise,disturbance,or disorderlyconduct or
assemblage .
Tenth. To establish and regulate markets and other
public buildings, and provide for their erection and de-
termine their location and authorize their erection in the
streets of said town or other suitable place or places
within the town limits .
Eleventh. To regulate,license,or prohibitbutchers,and
to revoke their license for malconduct in the course of
trade .
Twelfth. To prevent horse-racing,immoderate riding or
driving in the town ,and to authorizepersons immoder-
atelyridingordrivingtobe stoppedby any person,and
to punish or prohibitthe abuse of animals ,and to compel
persons to fasten or secure their horses, mules ,oxen ,or
other animals , attached to vehicles or otherwise , while
standing or remaining in the streets.
Thirteenth. To regulate the keeping or conveying of
gunpowder and other combustible or dangerous mate-
rials .
Fourteenth. To prevent the incumbering of the streets,
sidewalks, lanes, alleys, and public grounds with car-
riages,carts,sleighs,sleds,wheelbarrows,boxes,lumber ,
timber ,fire-wood ,brick,stone,post-awnings ,signs,or any
substance or material whatever .
Fifteenth. To preventany obscene,indecent,or profane
exhibition or conduct .

124 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Sixteenth. To restrainand punish vagrants and prosti--
tutes .
Seventeenth. To restrain and regulate or prevent the-
running at large of horses and swine ,and authorizing
the distraining, impounding , and sale of same for the
penalty incurred and cost of proceedings , and also to-
impose penalties on the owners of such animals for a
violationof any by -law or ordinance in relationthereto.
Eighteenth. To prevent and regulate the running at
large of dogs , and to authorize the destruction of the
same when at large contrary to the by -laws or ordi-
nances .
Nineteenth. To regulateand preventtheplaying of ball,
flyingofkites,or any otheramusement orpracticehaving
a tendency to annoy persons passing on the streetsor
sidewalks,or to frightenhorses or teams .
Twentieth. To make regulationsto prevent the spread
of contagious diseases in the town .
Twenty-first. To have exclusivepower over the streets,..
alleys, and sidewalks , and remove or abate any obstruc-
tions and encroachments therein or thereon .
Twenty -second. To abate and remove nuisances , and
punish the offenders thereof by penalties of fine and
imprisonment , and to define and declare what shall be
deemed nuisances,and to authorize the summary abate--
ment thereof .
Twenty-third. To appoint watchmen and policemen,
and prescribetheirduties,power ,and pay .
Twenty -fourth. To purchase land for and locate ceme-
teries, eitherwithin or without the town limits. They
shall have the power to lay out said cemeteries in lots,
and sellthe same ,to improve the cemeteries,and to exer-
cise fullcontrol over the same , and to enforce the proper
regulationand management thereof, and to punish all
trespassesthereonby finesand penalties.
Twenty -fifth.To appoint inspectors,weighers ,and gaug-
ers, and regulate theirduties and fees.
Twenty-sixth. To provide the town with water in any
manner they may deem proper, and to establish,make ,
and regulatepublicpumps ,wells,and cisterns.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 125
Twenty-seventh.To establishand regulatepublicpounds .
Twenty-eighth. To authorizeand regulatethe use of
locomotive engines within the town ; and to grant to any
railway company the right of way through the streetsof
the town ; and to direct and control the location of rail-
way trades and depot grounds, and prohibitrailway
companies from doing storage and warehouse business,
or collectingpay for same .
Twenty-ninth. To erect,control,rent out,and keep in
repair public scales in said town when deemed neces-
sary .
Thirtieth. To exclusivelyregulate,control,repair,grade,
pave , macadamize , amend ,and clear the streets,alleys,
bridges,sideand cross-walks ,and open ,widen ,straighten,
extend and vacate streetsand alleys.
Thirty-first. To establishhospitalsand pest-houses,and
make regulationsfor the government thereof, and discon-
tinue the same at pleasure.
Thirty-second. To do allactsand make allregulations
which may be necessaryor expedient for the preserva-
tion of health and the suppression of disease.
Thirty-third. To prohibitany work or businessbeing
done on theSabbath day,and to punish any person found
at or engaged in any work or business on that day ,ex-
cept itbe the ordinaryhousehold officesof dailyneces-
sityor other work of necessityor charity : Provided, That
persons who are members of any religioussociety,who
observe as a Sabbath any other day in the week than
Sunday or the ChristianSabbath,shallnot be punished,
if they observe as a Sabbath one day in each seven .
Thirty-fourth. That the board of trustees shall have
power to make , publish,ordain, amend ,and repeal all
such ordinances, by -laws , and police regulations, not
contraryto the Constitutionof thisCommonwealth , for
the good government and order of the town , and the
trade and commerce thereof, as may be necessary or
expedient to carry into effectthe powers vested in the
board of trusteesor any officerof said town by this act;
and enforceobservanceof allrules,ordinances,by-laws,
police,and other regulations made in pursuance of this
..

126 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
act, by penalties not exceeding a fine of one hundred
dollars,or imprisonment not exceeding fiftydays,or both,
forany offenseagainst the same .
Thirty-fifth. The saidtrusteesmay provide forthe use
of said town a fire-engine , organize a firecompany ,and
make necessary regulations for the prevention of dam-
age by fire.
Thirty sixth. That the board of trusteesof Elkton shall
have exclusive power to grant all licensesfor which pro-
vision ismade by thisact, and the amount to be paid for
any licenseforwhich provisionismade by thisactshallbe
fixedby the said board of trustees.
Thirty-seventh. That the board of trustees shall have
power to regulate and licensepublic hacks ,drays ,carts,
and other public vehiclesin said town , also to license any
stallion,jack,orbullthatmay be stoodwithinthelimitsof
said town .
ARTICLE VI .
Taxation .
§1 . That the board of trustees of Elkton shall have
power ,subjectto the exceptionshereinaftermentioned ,
toannuallylevyand collecttaxes,not exceedingfiftycents,
on each one hundred dollars' worth of all the real estate
in said town , includingthe improvements on such real
estate,and upon allpersonal estate,includingstoresand
groceries as well as notes,bonds , stocks,and other evi-
dences of debt : Provided, That in listingthe money ,notes ,
and so forth of a citizen,he may deduct from the gross
amount due him an amount equal to the debts due from
him .
§2 . That there shallbe exempt from taxation under this
actalllands,stockand farming implements used exclu-
sivelyforfarming purposes, and within the boundary of
said town ,unless said land should be at any time divided
into town lots, then they are to be taxed as other property.
There shall also be exempt from taxation under thisact,
allpublicpropertybelonging to saidtown ,the county of
Todd ,the State of Kentucky ,any church,publicschool,
allgrounds attachedto any church or publicschool; all

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 127
parsonages or grounds dedicatedsolelytosuch uses,and
any cemetery belongingto saidtown .
§3 . The board of trusteesshallalso have power to
annually assess and collect a tax,per capita, upon every
and each male inhabitant of said town over twenty -one
years of age , of not exceeding two dollars, and a tax not
exceeding one dollaron each dog over one thatany per-
son may keep ,or sufferor permit tobe kept,on hisprem-
ises .
ARTICLE VII .
Opening Streets.
§1 . That the board of trustees of Elkton shall have
power to lay out public squares or grounds, streets,
alleys, and highways , and to alter, extend , widen , con-
tract, straighten, open , or continue the same ; and such
board of trusteesshallcause allstreets, alleys,highways ,
public grounds , or squares laid out by them , to be sur-
veyed and described, and a descriptionthereof, to be
recorded in a book to be kept for that purpose by the
clerk of said town , showing particularlythe proposed im-
provements ,and the real estaterequired to be taken ; and
such streets, alleys, and highways shall, when opened ,
be public highways .
§2 . That whenever the public convenience may ,in the
opinion of the board of trustees, requireany streetor
alley,lane or highway to be opened ,laid out,extended ,
changed , straightened, or altered,or any public ground
or square to be opened or laid out,they may obtain or
purchase from the owner or owners thereof such real
estate as may be necessary therefor,and obtain from such
owner or owners a conveyance of said real estate,which
conveyance shallbe recorded in the officeof the clerk of
the Todd county court.
§3 . That in all such cases where the board of trustees
cannot ,or do not ,obtain or purchase such real estate,
and receiveconveyance therefor, they shallhave power
tocause tobe filedin the Todd county court a petition,
in which the board of trustees of Elkton shall be made
plantiffs,and allthe owners of such realestate,and the

128 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
guardian of infant owners , shall be made defendants ;
but it shall not be necessary to swear to said petition.
Said petition shall describe the street, alley,lane, high-
way ,public ground , or square that is sought to be laid
out,altered,extended ,widened ,or straightened; the char-
acter of the proposed improvements ,where situated, and
the names (it known ) of the owners of the real estate
required to be taken ; if any of said owners are non-
residents, and which ; if any are infants,and who are the
guardians of such infants, and if such there be ; and all
such owners and the guardians of said infants,if resi-
dents of thisState,shallbe summoned actually,as re-
quired by the CivilCode of Practice in ordinary actions;
and if non -residents, shall be warned , as required by
said Code ; and guardians ad litem shall be appointed to
defend for said infants , who shall answer ; and an attor-
ney shall be appointed for any non -resident defendant
or defendants ,in the way and manner required by said
Code of Practice,and his dutiesshallbe as thereinpre-
scribed ; and after all the defendants shall have been
so actuallyor constructivelysummoned before the court,
and the answer of the guardian ad litem, and the report
of the attorney for the non -residents shall have been
filed,a writ of ad quod damnum shall be awarded by the
court and directed to the sheriff of Todd county ,to be
executed and returned as required by law for writs of
that nature for opening roads in this State . On return of
the writ duly executed ,the court shallorder the damages
assessed to be paid by the said board of trustees,out of
the funds in the treasuryof said town , to the party or
partiesentitledthereto,and shallorderthe street,alley,
highway , or public square or ground to be laid out and
opened ; upon the payment of said damages it shall be
public property,and under the controlof the board of
trusteesof Elkton: Provided, That at any time before
such ordershallbe made tolay outor open any suchpublic
square or ground,street,alley,or highway ,the board of
trusteesmay abandon the proceedings,being responsible
for cost,and in such event the public square, ground ,
street,alley,or highway shallnot be opened or laidout.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 129
ARTICLE VIII .
Grading,Paving,and Improving Streetsand Alleys.
§1 . That the board of trustees shall have power to
grade, pave ,improve, protect,and ornament any public
square or ground now or hereafterlaid out.
§2 . That the owners in front of, or upon whose prem-
ises the board of trustees shall order and direct curbing
and sidewalks to be constructed,repaired,or relaid,shall
make ,repair,or relaysuch curbingor sidewalks at their
own expense,and in the manner ,with the materials,and
in the time prescribedby the board of trusteesby ordi-
nance or otherwise ; and if not done in the manner ,with
the material,and within the time so prescribed,the board
of trustees may cause the same to be constructed, re-
paired,or relaid,and assessthe expenses thereof,by an
order to be entered in the record of their proceedings ,
upon such lots respectively,and collectthe same by war-
rant and sale of the premises ,as in the case of taxes ; a
suit may also be maintained by the board of trusteesof
Elkton against such owner or owners for such improve-
ments .
§3 . That allthe provisionsof the preceding sectionshall
apply with equal forceto allpersonswhose duty itisto
remove or abate any nuisance ,and to allproperty charge-
able with removing or abating any nuisance ,where the
board of trusteeshave incurred any expense in removing
•or abating same ; that no person shallbe required to make
a sidewalk in front of his or her premises until afterthe
street in front of such shall have been curbed for the side-
walk thereof, at the cost and expense of the town ,and of
suitable material for the sidewalk ordered to be made .
ARTICLE IX .
Collection of Taxes and Assessments .
§1 . That the board of trusteesshallhave power ,by
ordinance ,to prescribe the form of assessment rolls, and
prescribe the duties,and define the power of assessors;
and to make such rules,and give such directionsin rela-
LOC . L.,VOL . 1-9

130 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
1
tion to attending,revising, or adding to the rolls, as they
may deem proper and expedient .
§2 . That the annual assessment rollsshallbe returned
by the firstday of Julyin each year; but the time may
be extended by order of the board of trustees. On the
return thereof, the board of trustees shall fix a day for
hearing objectionsthereto, and the clerk shallgive notice
of the time and place of such hearing ; and any person
feeling aggrieved by the assessment of his property may
appear at the time specified, and make his objection.
The board of trustees shallhave power to supply omis-
sions in said assessment rolls, and for the purpose of
equalizing the same , to alter, add to,take from , or other-
wise correct or revise the same .
3. That when the assessment roll shall have been
corrected and revised, the same shall be filed,and an
order confirming the same shallbe entered by the clerk.
The board of trustees shall thereupon ,by an ordinance
or resolution,levy such sum or sums of money as they
deem proper ,not exceeding the amounts authorized by
this act , and direct the collector to collect the same .
§4. That alltaxesand assessments,generaland special,
levied or assessedby the board of trusteesunder thisact,
shallbe a lienupon the real and personal estateupon
which the same may be imposed or assessed until such
tax shall be paid ; and no sale or transferof real estate
shall affect the lien thereon , and no property shall be
exempt from saleforthe payment of such tax or assess-
ment , except such as is by thisact exempt from taxation.
Any personalpropertybelonging to the debtor may be
taken for the payment of taxes, and sold by the collector,
aftertendays'noticeof such saleposted up atfourpublic
places in said town .
§5 . That the collectorshall pay all taxes and assess-
ments as fastas collectedinto the town treasury; and his
powers ,duties,and liabilities, in case of default or mis-
conduct,shallbe prescribedby the board of trusteesby
ordinance .
§6. That in case of the non -payment of taxeslevied
or assessments assessed under this act upon real estate,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 131
so much of the premises as may be sufficientto pay the
same may be soldto the highestbidder,at publicauction,
forcash in hand ,at any time withintwo years from the
confirmation of the assessment by the board of trustees,
which shallbe enteredat largeupon the recordskept by
the clerk,directing the collectorto sell, particularly de-
scribing the delinquent premises to be sold, and the tax
or assessment for which the same shallbe made , a certi-
fied copy of which order,signed by the chairman of the
board of trustees and the clerk , shall be delivered to the
collector, which shallconstitute the process upon which
such sale shall be made .
§ 7. That thecollectorshallthen advertisesuch prem-
ises for sale,by posting written or printed notices at four
public places in said town for ten days priorto such sale,
describing the said premises ,and giving the name of the
owner (when known ),and the severalamounts of taxes
or assessments thereon, and the time and place of sale.
§ 8. The collectorshallbe allowed such fees for selling
as the board of trustees may prescribe. The clerk shall
keep a record of such sales,which shallbe open to pub-
licinspection at allreasonable times.
§ 9. That the rightof redemption,in allcases of sales
of real estate for taxes or assessments,shallexist to the
owner or his heirs for the period of two years from the
day of sale,on payment of the amount ,and twenty per
cent .thereon,for which it was sold, and alltaxes accru-
ing subsequent to the sale. If the estate of any infant
or feme covert or lunatic be sold under this act as afore-
said, the same may be redeemed at any time within
two years aftersuch disabilityshallhave been removed .
In case of redemption , the money may be paid to the
purchaser,or forhim to the town clerk,who shallmake
a special deposit thereof with the treasurer, taking his
receipt therefor. If not redeemed ,the board of trustees
shall,upon the returnof the certificateof sale,or proof
of itsloss,direct the collector or his successor to execute
a deed to the purchaser ,conveying to the purchaser the
premises so soldand unredeemed as aforesaid. The col

132 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
lector may charge a fee of one dollar for every deed so
made ,and an amount sufficientto pay other necessary
cost of such deed , to be taxed as other cost
§ 10. If at any saleof realor personal estatefortaxes
or assessinents,no bid shallbe made for any parcel of
land, or any ground ,or any chattels,the same shall be
struck off to said board of trustees for the benefit of
said town , and thereupon the board of trusteesshall re-
ceive from the officermaking the sale a certificateof the
sale thereof,and they shallbe vested with the same rights
as other purchasers at such sales, and ,for the benefit of
the town , and the collectorshall execute to the purchaser
a certificate of sale for all real estate sold for tax .
§ 11. Any deed , or duly authenticated copy of the rec-
ord thereof, made for real estate sold for taxes or assess-
ments ,shall be prima facie evidence of the power and
authorityto sellthe same ,that allthe proceeding in refer-
ence to such sale and levying the taxes or assessments
for which the same were sold were regular.
§ 12. That where partiesfailingor refusingto pay taxes
or assessments own personal property,remedies against
that shallbe exhausted before any sale of real estate can
be made .
ARTICLE XI .
Borrowing Money .
§ 1. For the purpose of creatinga fund formaking such
improvements as may seem necessary,or forthe purpose of
assistingin any public improvement ,the board of trustees
are hereby authorized to issuebonds of said town payable
to bearer,and not more than twenty -fiveyears afterdate ,
and bearing interestat a rate not greater than six per
cent . per annum , payable semi -annually , principal and
interestto be made payable at such place as the board of
trusteesshall,by ordinance,designate,and which shallbe
named in the face of the bonds , and same may be of any
denomination not lessthan fiftydollars,and not greater
than five hundred dollars, and shall have coupons for
interest attached. The bonds shall be signed by the
chairman of theboard of trustees,and countersignedby

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 133
the clerk, and shall have the corporate seal of the town
attached; and the coupons shall be signed by the clerk
alone ; which bonds so issued may be sold, pledged , or
disposed of by the board of trusteesas to them may seem
best for the purposes aforesaid and for the interestof said
town ; and for the payment of same ,principaland interest,
allof the revenue of said town shallbe pledged : Provided,
That not more than twenty -five thousand dollars of said
bonds shallbe issued in any one year , and the amount of
same outstanding shall not at any time exceed twenty-
five thousand dollars: Provided further, That before any
bonds shallbe issued under thisact,the question whether
same shallbe issuedshall,by order of the board of trus-
tees, be submitted to the qualifiedvoters of said town
at some regular election, or at such other time as the
board of trusteesmay fix; and the board of trusteesshall
cause notices of said submission and of the time thereof,
and place or places,to be posted at four public places in
said town for at leasttwenty days before the time of sub-
mission ; and the election to determine whether said bonds
shallbe issuedshallbe held by the same officers,appointed
in the same manner , between the same hours , and in all
respects conducted in the same manner as electionsfor
town officersare providedby thisactto be held;and the
officersholding such election shall, at the close of the
polls,make out and sign,at the footof the poll-book used ,
a full certificateof the time such election was held , and
the correctstateof the pollswhen closed; and saidpoll-
book shallbe disposed of and preserved as is required in
other elections in said town ; and at the firstmeeting of
the board of trustees after such election , the aforesaid
certificateof officersof same shall be spread in fullon
their records ; and no bond shall be issued under this act
unless a majority of allthe qualifiedvoters of said town
shall have cast their vote for such issual .
§2 . That the board of trustees shall, out of the reve-
nues of said town hereinbefore provided to be levied and
collected, pay said bonds and coupons as they respect-
ively falldue; and for that purpose may ,out of said

134 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
revenue ,create and set apart a sinking fund ; but before
so doing shall appoint three resident tax-payers of said
town a board of sinking fund commissioners , who shall
be sworn as other officersof said town , and hold their
officesduring the pleasure of the board of trustees; and
said commissioners shall, before acting or entering on
the discharge of their duties, with such suretiesas the
board of trusteesmay accept and approve ,execute to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , for the use of said town ,a
bond for the faithful discharge of their duties . Said com-
missioners shall take charge of said sinking fund , and
keep the same securelyand profitablyinvested,untilre-
quiredfor payment as aforesaid,and shallcontrolsame
under the advice and direction of the board of trustees.
ARTICLE XII .
Miscellaneous Provisions .
§ 1. That the board of trustees shall have the corporate
boundary of said town carefullysurveyed from the be-
ginning point, and said boundary corrected,platted, and
laid off; and the same , duly approved and indorsed by
the board of trustees , shall be filed for record with the
clerk of the Todd county court, and the originalpre-
served in his office .
§2 . That the board of trustees, at leasttwo weeks be-
fore each annual assessment, as hereinbefore provided ,
cause tobe postedin two publicplacesinsaidtown ,a full
and complete statement of the receiptsand expenditures
from the date of the lastannual report,together with the
source from which the former were derived and their
mode of disbursement ,together with such other informa-
tion as may be necessary to a fullunderstanding of the
financial concerns and conditions of the town .
§3 . Every ordinance,by -law , or regulation imposing
any penalty,fine,imprisonment, or forfeiturefor a vio-
lationof itsprovisions,shall, afterthe passage thereof,
be published one week by posting up written or printed
copies thereof in four public places in said town .
§ 4. That itshallbe the duty of the board of trustees
at alltimes to keep the streetsand alleys,lanes and high

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 135
ways ,in saidtown ,in good orderand condition,andfor
every failureto do so they shallbe liableto indictment
and fineof not less than five nor more than twenty -five
dollars .
§ 5. That all property, real,personal,or mixed ,now
belonging to the town of Elkton , or the board of trustees
of Elkton,ishereby vestedin the board of trustees,as
created or provided for by this act.
§6 . That merchants and business men may ,during
the day ,and only during the day , have the privilegeof
occupying the sidewalk fronting theirbusiness houses for
the purpose of receiving or expressing their goods for
sale .
§7 . That the Todd county court shall not grant a li-
cense to any person or persons to sellspirituous, vinous ,
malt ,or intoxicatingliquorswithin said town , unless the
person applying for such licenseshallproduce to the
county court a certificatefrom the board of trusteesthat
the town tax requiredby thisact has been paid by such
applicant .
§ 8. That the presentpolicejudge and marshal of Elk-
ton shallbe continued in office,and exercise allthe duties
and functionsof theirrespectiveofficesunder thischarter,
untiltheirsuccessorsare duly electedand qualified,as
herein provided .
§ 9. That nothing in this act shall be taken as repeal-
ing,modifying,or in any way changing,an act of the
Legislatureof Kentucky , approved March sixth, one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-two , and entitled
" An act to regulate the sale of spirituous, vinous , and
malt liquors in the town of Elkton , Kentucky ,or within
one mile of the corporate limitsthereof.
§ 10. That all acts or parts of acts inconsistenthere-
with are hereby repealed, and this act may at any time
be altered,modified,or repealed by the General Assembly
of Kentucky .
§ 11. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 21, 1884 .

136 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 880 .
AN ACT toprovideforthe constructionand repairof publicroadsinOld--
ham , Trimble , Hardin , and Ballard counties.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That at the regular electiontobe held on the second
Tuesday in November , one thousand eight hundred and
eighty -four ,again at the regular election on the firstMon-
day in August , one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
six,and bienniallyafter the last named date, there shall
be elected one superintendent of roads in each magis-
terial district in Oldham , Trimble , Hardin , and Ballard
counties,or in eitherof them which may ratifythe pro-
visions of this act in the manner hereinafterprescribed;
who shallhold his officefor two years , and untilhis suc-
cessor is duly elected and qualified; whose duty itshall
be to take charge of and superintend the construction
and repairsofallroads within his district,as hereinafter
specified,
§ 2. He shalltake charge of all roads,highways , and
bridgesin hisdistrict,and cause the same to be kept in
as good repairas the prudent use of the means in his
hands will permit ; he shall execute all orders of his
county court for opening , changing ,locating, or vacating
any road or highway in his district; he shallhave con-
trol of all funds of his districtfor road , highway , and
bridge purposes; he shallsee that all the roads, high-
ways , and bridges are of the width required by law .
§ 3. His term of office shall commence on the firstMon-
day of the month next after his election; and before en-
tering upon the discharge of his duties he shallexecute
bond to the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,with good se-
curity, for the faithfuldischarge of all his duties under
thisact; such bond to be approved by said county court,
on which bond he and his suretiesshall be liableto any
one aggrieved ; and before entering upon the discharge of
hisduties,he shalltake an oath that he will faithfullyand
impartiallydischargethedutiesof hisoffice.
§ 4. The justices of the peace and the county judge ,
composing the court of claims for said county ,shall,att

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 137
the December term , one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-four, of the county court in each of said counties,
and afterwards at the regular term of the court of claims
in each year,levy and assessa poll-tax forhighway pur-
poses ,to be known as commutation road tax , of two dol-
larsupon each male residentof such county who isby
law liableto taxationfor other county purposes,except
such as the county court may ,for sufficientreasons, ex-
empt from the payment of such tax; and shall also levy
a road tax of not exceeding twenty -fivecents per annum
upon each one hundred dollars' worth of property liable
to taxation for State revenue as listedby the assessorof
said county forcounty and Statepurposes,which assess-
ment and levy shall be certifiedby the clerk of said
county court to the sheriffof said county , and said sher-
iff shall collect said tax as other taxes are collected ; and
all moneys so collected shall be kept separate for each
magisterialdistrict,and paid over by the sheriff: that is,
the amount collectedfrom each districtshallbe paid to
the superintendents of said district. All persons exempt
from poll-tax,as providedin thissection, shallprocure
from the county clerk a certificateof such exemption ,
and shallproduce the same to the sheriffwhen called
upon for his tax . Said sheriffshall, before collecting
said tax,execute a bond to the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , with one or more good and sufficientsecurities,to
be approved by said county court, for the faithfuldis-
charge of his duties under this section, and for the
prompt payment of said tax to the proper parties.
§5 . Said tax shallbe due and collectableon the first
day of January in each year , and the sheriffshall pay
the same to the superintendents- one halfon or before
the firstday of March ,and the otherhalfby the firstday
of July in each year- taking theirreceiptsforthe same ,
which shall be vouchers for same in his settlements . The
sheriffshallbe allowed fiveper cent.commission for col.
lecting said taxes , and shall settle his accounts for same
as now required by law in relation to county levy, ex-
cept he shallbe required to settlein the month of July

138 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
in each year , and may return his delinquent listas to
said taxes as now required by law as to county levy and
revenue delinquents,but shallreturn said delinquent list
to the said county court by the June term thereof in each
year .
§ 6. The districtsuperintendent shall,in the months of
April,May , and June of each year , firstput allthe high-
ways of his districtin good ordinary repair, and , with
such othermeans as may be in hishands,proceed to do
such other work upon such portions of the public roads
most traveled,and where it will be of greatest benefit to
the public ; such as judicious ditching,draining, making
fills, grading , building culverts, bridges, and such other
work as shallto him seem prudent,and to the bestinter-
est of the public; and if the funds in his hands are
sufficient, may turnpike or gravel any portion of said
roads mostly used by the public, and at such places as
are hardest to keep in repair; and shall expend all the
means in his hands applicableto such purposes,as here-
inbefore stated,by the fifteenthday of November in each
year .
§ 7. Itshallbe the duty of the superintendentof each
districtto employ laborers, mechanics , and teams , at
specifiedwages ,such as is usual in his districtfor such
work ,at the lowest price he can obtain said hands and
teams,and such employes shallwork eight hours each
day .
§ 8. The superintendent shall keep the time of the
hands employed by him , and for cause may dismiss any
hand , and he shall pay them the price agreed upon ,
taking receipts therefor,which shall be vouchers in his
settlement with the county court.
§ 9. The superintendent shall,upon entering upon the
discharge of his duties,become the custodian of all the
tools,material, and other propertypertainingto roads,
which have been held by the differentsurveyorsin his
district,and shall,as faras practicable,collectthe same ,
and provide a suitableplace or places for theirsafe-keep-
ing; and he may , out of the road funds in his hands ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 139
purchase such other tools or materials as may be neces-
sary for the use of roads in hisdistrict; and forallarticles
so purchased he shall take a receipt from the vender of
the same , which shall be a voucher in his settlement;
such toolsand materialshallbe the propertyof such dis-
trict .
§ 10. The superintendent shall, at the expiration of his
term of office, make out a schedule of all property be-
longingto hisdistrictappertainingto roads,and turn the
same over to his successor in office, taking his receipt
therefor,which scheduleshallbe copied upon a book to
be kept for road purposes in his district; he shallalso de-
liverto his successor allmeans on hand for road purposes ,
taking his receipt as a voucher for same .
§ 11. The superintendent shall,within one month after
the expiration of his term of office, and oftener, if re-
quiredby the county judge,make a complete settlement
of allhis accounts with the county judge of said county ,
showing allmoneys received for road purposes as super-
intendent of roads,and also for all moneys paid out and
expended by him under the provisions of this act, and
produce and filewith his settlement receipts and vouch-
ers for each item of expenditure ; which settlement and
vouchers shallbe filedin the county court of said county ,
and laid over one month forexceptions; afterwhich time ,
if no exceptions be filedthereto, the same may be con-
firmed and ordered to be recorded . If exceptions be filed
to said settlement,they shallbe heard and determined by
said court as exceptions to executors' and administrators'
settlements are required by law to be heard and deter-
mined . It shall be the duty of the county attorney for
said county to be present and represent the tax-payers of
said county when said settlements are made , and fileex-
ceptions thereto if he should find any errorstherein; and
he shallrepresent the county on the trialof same . Either
party shallhave the right to appeal from the judgment of
the county court to the circuitcourt of said county ,upon
the exceptions,when the amount in controversy is twen-
ty-fivedollarsor over,to be taken as appeals in other

140 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
cases are taken ,as now required by law ,except that when
the county attorney shall desire to appeal ,he may do so
without bond or security; and for a failureof the super-
intendent to make his settlement as required by thissec-
tion,he and his suretiesshall be liable to a fine of fifty
dollars on his officialbond ,besides being liablewith his
suretiesfor any damages the county or any person may
sustain .
§ 12. The superintendent,or any other person by his
order, may enter upon any land adjoining or in reach of
any highway which is being improved , and thereupon
make any ditches,drains,or dams , and may dig and re-
move any gravel,stone, earth,sand ,or cut and remove
any wood or trees that may be necessary for the proper
construction,repair,or preservation of any such highway ;
and any person aggrieved may apply to the superintend-
ent for an assessment of damages occasioned thereby ;
and in such case the superintendent shall appoint three
disinterestedpersonsin said districtto view the locality.
where the grievance was committed ,and assess the dam-
ages in twenty days after their appointment ; they shall
firsttake an oath to faithfullydischarge their duties;
and they shallreport,within five days aftertheir assess-
ment , to the superintendent, having firstgiven notice
thereof to the complainant ; and the superintendent shall
pay said damages out of any means in his hands for road
purposes ,unless he should deem such award unreasona-
ble,in which case he may reduce the amount ; and such
complainant shall have the right to appeal from said
award to the circuitcourt of the county , upon the same
terms and conditions,and in the same manner as appeals
are taken from inferior courts to the circuit courts .
§ 13. Every districtsuperintendent shall have the right
to administer oaths in allcases touching and necessary to
the discharge of his officialduties .
§ 14. The officeof surveyor of roads ishereby abolished,
and alllaws or parts of laws or acts now enforced in said
counties, where reference is made to surveyors of roads
or highways ,the same shall hereafter be considered as

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 141
referringto the districtsuperintendent,so faras the same
can be made applicable. Said superintendentshallbe
subject to the same fines,to be recovered in the same man-
ner,for any failureor willfulneglect of duty on his part,
as is denounced by law against surveyors of public roads.
§ 15. The superintendent shall, as nearly as possible,
distribute the work on the roads equally in all parts of
his districts .
§ 16. That allthe tax-payers in said county shallhave
the right to work out their road tax under the rules and
regulationsas provided in sectioneightof thisact, pro-
vided the personsowing the tax shallapply to the super-
intendent to do such work , and will do the same at the
prices usually paid other hands in the district,and at such
times and places as the superintendent may direct.
§ 17. The superintendent shallbe allowed a reasonable
compensation , not exceeding two dollarsper day of eight
hours ,for his services; and he shall keep his time , and
make out a sworn statement of the same at the time he
makes his annual settlements, and when so made , shall
be allowed as a credit in his settlement .
§ 18. When a public road , or any part thereof,shall lie
wholly or inpart upon the line between two magisterial
districts, the county court of the county in which the
same shalllieshall,by an orderenteredof record,desig-
nate the districtin which such road or part thereof shall
be included .
§19 . It shallbe the duty of the sheriffsof said counties
to open a poll at the regular electionfor State and other
officerson the firstMonday in August , one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-four,atallthe votingplaces in each
of said counties,forthe purpose of taking the sense of the
qualified voters as to whether or not this act shall be in
force. To each person who may be qualifiedto vote for
State officersthe questionshall be propounded as to
whether he is or is not in favor of the ratification and
enforcement of thisact,and his answer shallbe recorded
accordingly by the regular officersof election. The result
of the vote so recordedshallbe certifiedby the officersof

142 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
election to the county court,and if a majority of all the
votes cast in said counties,or eitherof them , shallappear
from such certificate to have been cast in favor of the rati-
ficationof thisact,such certificateshallbe spread at large
upon the order-book of the county court,and the provis-
ions of this act shall thereafter be in full force in said
counties,or in eitherof them in which such majoritymay
be given . The sheriffsof said counties shall give notice
of the electionprovided for in thissection,by causing to
be printed and circulatedat leasttwenty copies of thisact
in each voting precinct in said counties,at least twenty
days before said election, or he may , in his discretion,
cause thisact to be published by two weekly insertions,in
a newspaper published in said counties, at leastfifteen
days before said election,adopting the cheapest and best
mode .
Approved April 19 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 881 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Franklin county court to issuebonds to build a
new jail.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Franklin county court is hereby author-
ized to issue bonds of the said county ,not exceeding fif-
teen thousand dollarsin amount ,at not longer date than
fifteenyears ,to bear interestnot exceeding six per cent.
per annum ,which bonds may be in such amounts ,and
principaland interestpayable at such places,as the said
county courtmay direct,theinterestto be paid annually.
§2 . To meet the annual intereston said bonds ,and to
pay allthe expenses of issualthereof,and the payment
of principal and interestwhen due ,it shallbe the duty
of the county court,annually ,to levy an ad valorem tax
upon the property subjectto pay State taxes, which,
when added to the poll-tax, shallbe sufficientto defray
the necessarycounty expenses and the expenses before
mentioned .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 143
§3 . Said county bonds shallbe orderedto be issuedby
a courtconsistingof a majorityof allthe justicesof the
peace of said county , and shallbe signed by the county
judge , and attestedby the county clerk, with the seal of
the county affixed; and the said bonds shall have the
necessarycoupons attached thereto, and said coupons
shallbe signed by the clerk of said court.
§4 . Said county bonds shallbe denominated Franklin
county jailbonds .
§5 . The sheriff or county collector for said county
shallbe regulated and governed ,in the collectionof the
tax imposed by this act, as the same is regulated and
controlled in " An act to authorize the Franklin county
court to issue bonds for the aid of turnpike roads and
other purposes," approved March third, one thousand
eight hundred and seventy -six.
§6 . That the said county court may ,at its discretion,
place in said bonds a stipulationthat the same may be
paid off at the pleasure of said court.
§ 7. Said bonds shallnot be sold for lessthan their par
face value ; and said bonds shallbe placed in the hands
of the treasurer of Franklin county to be sold; and the
proceedsof saidbonds shallbe appropriatedto the build-
ing of a new jail for Franklin county ; and said treas-
urer shall be liable for the same under his officialbond
as treasurer .
§ 8. Provided,however, That before said tax is levied,
the qualifiedvoters of said county of Franklin shallvote
thereon,at theirrespectivevotingprecincts,at such time
and place as the county court may direct,after thirty
days ' notice thereof.
§ 9. This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 18, 1884 .

144 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER- 882 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Carrolltonand Worthville Railroad Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Allen Donaldson ,J. W. Butts,Peter Lostut-
ter,Cyrus Hanks , G. W. Anderson, W. A. Fishback,E.
Hafford ,R. H. Stanton , W. E. Pratt, J. G. Ginn ,A. N.
Jett,E. A. Gullion, E. D. Salyers, G. S. Dean , Robert
Humphrey ,R. B. Dean , F. H. Gaines, be,and they are
hereby,createda body -politicand corporate,by the name
of the " Carrollton and Worthville Railroad Company ,"
with all the powers and authority incident to corpora-
tions, and such as are vested and created by thisact; and
shallhave perpetual succession of members ,by the name
and styleof the Carrollton and Worthville Railroad Com-
pany .
§2 . That the Carrollton and Worthville Railroad Com-
pany is hereby authorizedand empowered to construct
and operatea railroadfrom Carrolltonto some point on
the line of the Louisville and Nashville Railroad , Short
Line Division, not furthereast than Liberty Station,nor
furtherwest than English Station.
§3 . The said company ,in and by theircorporate name
of the Carrolltonand WorthvilleRailroadCompany ,may
contractand be contractedwith,sue and be sued,plead
and be impleaded , answer and be answered ,defend and be
defended , in allcourts; with power to acquire ,hold, and
use allsuch realestate,and fixturesand personalestate,
as shallbe necessary to construct and operate their rail-
road; and they may have, adopt,and use a common seal,
and alter and renew the same at pleasure; and shall
have ,use,and enjoy allthe rightsand powers and priv-
ilegeswhich other railroadcorporations in thisState may
lawfullyhave,enjoy,and exercise.
§4 . The capital stock of the Carrollton and Worth-
villeRailroad Company shall be two hundred thousand
($200,000) dollars, divided into shares of one hundred
dollarseach ,and be issued and transferredin such man-
ner as may be prescribedby the board of directorsin the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 145 .
by -laws of the company . The Carrollton and Worth-
villeRailroad Company is authorized to receive subscrip-
tions of stock,payable in land , and receive conveyances
for same ,which may be sold and conveyed by the com-
pany for itsuse and benefit.
§5 . The business and affairsof the said company shall
be managed by a board of directors,consisting of seven
members , each of whom shall be a stockholder , one of
whom ,chosen by a majorityof them ,shallbe president
of the company . The firstboard of directorsshall con-
sist of seven of the incorporators named herein in the
firstsectionof thisact,to be chosen by a majority of the
incorporators, who shall, by subscription, become stock-
holders ; the directors so chosen to be at the time stock-
holders. If any of theincorporatorshereinnamed refuse
or failto act, then a majorityof the others may act.
After the electionof the firstboard of directors,ifany
of them refuse to act, or resigns, or failsto serve, a
majority of the others may fillthe vacancy by electing
some other stockholder; and the board of directorsmay
fillall vacancies happening by death, resignation, or
otherwise in theirboard . The said incorporators,or any
of them , or the board of directors,or any of them , may
open books of subscription to the capital stock of the
said company ,or at any time or place,with or without
notice; and the same shallbe binding on the company
and the partiesthereto. The company may organize so
soon as a majority of the incorporators herein named
become stockholders, and choose from theirnumber the
firstboard of directors; and thereafter, so soon as ten
thousand dollars($ 10,000)shallbe subscribed,noticeshall
be given by the directors,or any one of them ,of the time
and place of holding an election for another board of
directors,who shall be chosen by a majority in interest
of the stockholders,to serve one year ,or untiltheirsuc-
cessors are elected .
§6 . That the said company may take,by giftor pur-
chase,or inpayment of subscription,and holdin fee-sim-

ple, any lands or personalpropertynecessaryforsaid

per annum ,and may issuethe bonds of the saidcompany ,

villeRailroad Company such sums as they may decide on ;

:
council of any city,within thirtydays thereafter,to cause

read company . Itshallbe theduty of the judge of said

in the name of said county or town or city or civil

the tax, and take such bonds from them as they may pre-
scribe .

§ 14. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 21 , 1884.

Hickman .

of Kentucky :

passage .

trictshallbe that made by the assessor of said county in
said districtin his assessment annually for State revenue
purposes .
§3 . The taxes that may be levied under this act shall
be collectedby the sheriffof Campbell county,or by a
specialcollector,who may be appointed by said board
of trustees,at the option of the board . The officerwho
may undertake the collectingsaid tax shall, before col-
lectingthe same ,execute ,in the Campbell county court,
a good and sufficientbond , with approved security, to
said board of trustees, for the collectionand payment
over of saidtax so levied. The taxes,afterhaving been
paid into the treasuryof said district,shallbe paid out
on the order of the said board of trustees .
§4 . The board of trusteesshallfurnish such collecting
officerwith a copy of the order of the levy of tax made
annually on or before the firstday of June in each year,
and it shallbe the duty of such officerto settleand pay
over to said board of trustees the money collectedfrom
time to time ,as such board shallrequire and direct; and
he shallmake finalsettlement for the year not laterthan
April firstnext afterhe shallhave receivedthe copy of
the order of levy of said tax each year . If a collector
shallbe appointed,saidboard shallfurnishhim a listof
assessments made of property in the districtfor the year
by the county assessoron or beforethe firstof July next
after the levy of tax shall have been made ; but if the
LOC . L.,VOL . II- 11

162 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sheriffshallcollect,he shallcollectfrom his tax-book for
the year furnished by the county clerk for revenue pur-
poses .
§5 . The said collector, in making said collectionsof
said tax, shall have the same powers ,and may use the
same processallowed by law to sheriffsforcollectingthe
State revenue or county levy; and ,as compensation ,shall
have fiveper cent on the amount collectedand paid over
into the treasury of said district.
§6 . The same lienforthe payment of said taxes shall
attach to the property assessed as for the payment of the
State revenue , and the officercollectingsaid tax shallbe
subjectto the same obligations, liabilities,and penalties
as providedby law againstsheriffsto enforcethe collec-
tion of the State revenue and county levy; and the board
of trusteesshallhave the same remedies and powers to
enforce said collectionof said tax for any delinquency of
said collector or sheriff for failing to collect said tax in
due time , or for failingto pay the same over as herein
provided,as are provided by law in casesof failuresor
delinquenciesof sheriffsto make collectionsand pay-
ments of Staterevenueor countylevy; and forallsuch
delinquencies upon the part of such collectoror sheriff,
his suretiesshallbe responsiblewith such officeron their
bond to said board of trustees,who may sue ,or proceed
by motion on saidbond ,in any courtof competent juris-
diction .
7. This act shall be in force from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 21 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 901 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Botlandand Beech Fork TurnpikeRoad Com-
pany .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That B. H. Lewis,Milton Greer,Austin Wimsatt ,
S. B. Coleman , and John A. Simpson , and their success

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 163
ors and associates,be , and are hereby ,created a body-
politicand corporate,under the name and styleof the
Botland and Beech Fork Turnpike Company ; and by
such name may sue and be sued, and hold and enjoy
allthe rights,privileges,and powers incident and belong-
ing to similarcorporations in thisCommonwealth .
§2 . The objectand purpose of said company ,and for
which they are hereby invested with corporate powers , is
to construct and operate a turnpike road ,to commence
at a point on the Bardstown and SpringfieldTurnpike
Road ,at or near the town of Botland , and to run thence
to a point on the Beech Fork of Salt river,oppositeor
nearly so to the lands of John A. Simpson , on said river,
and in Washington county .
§3 . Said persons are authorized to open books and
receive subscriptions to the capital stock of said com-
pany at any time and placethey may deem proper. Said
stock shall be divided into shares of fiftydollars each ,
and the entire capital stock shall not exceed a sum suf-
ficientto construct said road , and to erectthe necessary
toll -houses thereon .
§4 . When , in the judgment of said incorporators, a
sum shall have been subscribed sufficient to build twe
miles of said road ,they shall call a meeting of stock-
holders, after having given due notice thereof; and at
such meeting said stockholders shall elect from their
number a president and five directors. Said directors
and president shall respectivelyhold theirofficesforthe
period of one year,and untiltheirsuccessorsare elected
and qualified. The time and place of election, after the
first,shall be fixed by the president and directors, and
shalltake place once in each year. A majorityof the
board shalltransact business, and shall be convened by
the president, or any three of the directors.
§5 . The management and control of the affairsof said
company shallbe intrustedto the president and directors;
and they shallhave power to appoint a treasurerand sec-
retary,and totake bond forthefaithfulperformanceof
theirduties; and said company shallbe capable of held

164 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ingitscapitalstock,and theincreaseand profitthereof,
and holding by gift,purchase ,or otherwise anything nec-
essary for the prosecution of their work , or the objects of
itsincorporation; and they shallhave power to contract
and be contracted with; to have and use a seal; and gen-
erallyto do alland every act or thing lawful for a corpo-
rationto do to effectthe objectforwhich saidcorporation
was created .
§ 6. "Said corporation shall fix and regulate the grade of
said road , and its covering with stone and gravel; and
may designate the places of toll-gates, and fix the rate
of toll,but such ratesshallnot exceed those prescribed
by the general law ; and aftertwo miles of said road is
completed,they shallhave power to erecta toll-gate,and
collecttolls,to be applied to the building of said road.
§7 . Said company shallhave the powers , and be sub-
ject to the provisions of chapter one hundred and ten of
the General Statutes, and amendments thereto, except
as herein differentlyprovided ; and in addition thereto, if
said company shallbe unable to contract with the owner
of any land or materialnecessaryforthe company's use,
they may secure the right of way in the manner and
upon the terms prescribed in an act of the General As-
sembly of Kentucky, approved April the eleventh, one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-two , providing the
mode for condemning land for the use of railroad and
turnpike companies ; and may secure the necessary mate-
rialsas now provided by law .
§ 8. This actshallbe in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 21, 1884 .
CHAPTER 902 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the town of Jamestown, in Russel
county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the territoriallimits of said town shall be
extended as follows: Beginning at a pointon the James-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 165
town and Albany road , on the southwest line of said
town ; thence with said road to the corner of G. F. Jones '
lots; thence with his(Jones')lineto a stakeon saidroad;
thence ,leaving the road , running with Jones' line to a
stone corner; thence a straightline from Jones' corner to
the south corner of A. P. Simpson's lot; thence with the
old town boundary to the beginning .
§2 . That the countyjudge of Russellcountyshallhave
the power and authorityto appoint trusteesof said town ,
when a vacancy occurs or the trustees failto elect; that
said trustees appointed shall hold their officeuntil the
next regular election or until their successors are ap
pointed or elected and qualified as such .
§3 . That it shall be lawful for the county judge to
appoint a marshal for said town , and he have the same
powers and privilegesthat the original charter granted
them when elected by the voters of said town ; and that
he hold his office until his successor is elected or ap-
pointed and qualifiedas such,as requiredby sectionfive
in said charter .
§4 . That itshall be unlawful for the county judge of
Russell county , the trustees of Jamestown ;or any other
person or persons ,to grant a license to any person or
persons to sellby retailany spirituous,vinous, or malt
liquors within the limits of the town of Jamestown , or
within one mile thereof .
§5 . That any person or personssellingsuch liquorsby
retailshallbe deemed guilty of keeping a tippling-house ,
and forthe firstoffensebe finedtwenty- fivedollars,to be
recoveredby indictmentby a grand jury or upon a war
rant of any court having jurisdiction; and foreach sub-
sequent offenseshallbe finedfiftydollars,tobe collected
as other fines in similar cases , and inure to the benefit of
the town of Jamestown .
§6 . That itshallnot be lawful for any druggist in said
town limitsto sellany such liquorsin any quantity what-
ever ,unless itisupon a written prescriptionfrom a regu-
dar practicing physician then in regular practice,and that
said patient is under his treatment ,and that the same is

166 LAWS OF KENTUCKY :
necessary, and only one sale can be made on the said
prescription: Providedfurther, That such druggistor his
clerkmay sellwine to any member of any churchfor
sacramentalpurposes by the person who buys the same
leaving a written statement of the amount wanted and
the purpose for which itis to be used .
§7 . That allactsor partsof actsin conflictwith this.
act are,so far as they affectthisact,repealed.
§ 8. That thisact take effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 21 , 1884.

poster'spostedatfourpublicplacesin thetown ,and notice

dollar and fiftycents. Whenever any levy of taxes,

petent person to be appointed by the board of trustees,

venient for inspectionby the tax payers thereof; and the

villeTurnpike , and by one insertion of a notice in one

Louisville, twenty days before any levy is made based

board of trusteesshallhave power to make corrections,
and correct any irregularities; and , after said notices
have been given , and a reasonable time allowed for ob

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 171
jections thereto,the said board of trusteesmay appoint a

town ; and when so appointed or directed, he shall have
the same power to levy, sell, distrain, and collectthe

for, and collect State taxes; and the general laws now

ofsaidtown ; and the officerorpersonmaking saidcollec-
tion shall settle his accounts with the board of trustees

ified voters of said town ; said consent to be either ex-
pressed in writing, or at any electionheld in said town .
§7 . The said board of trustees shall keep a full and

cause to be kept an accountof allmoneys expended,and

eeedings of the board shall be subject to inspection by

§8. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof trustee

thority thereof, to-wit : Every male inhabitant, twenty-
one years of age or over, who shall have resided twc
years in the State,or one year in Jeffersoncounty,and
i

sixtydays in the town of Crescent Hill, next preceding

nance must providethatallpersonswho ,by operationof

tion,but who may not be so at the time of the registra-

sary to have a registrationto regulate the electionat any
time,whether at an annual or at a specialelection,they
shallby ordinance prescribethe number of days , not less
than thirtydays ,before the day of election,which the

and shallfix an hour between half-past seven o'clock,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 173
theywillattendthe place of registration,on each one of
the fivedays beforethe lastday fixedfor registration;
and two of the judges shall remain at the place named
one hour ,or longer if necessary, on each of said days , to
receiveand record applicationsof allpersons entitled
to register; and public notice shall be given of the ordi-
nance ,of the names of the judges , of the place where

be given by handbills, written or printed,posted at four

German and one Englishdailynewspaper publishedin the

beticalform , and deliver the same to the judges of the

lowed to register,such as " not yet of age ," "not yet sixty
days in town ;" and in case said person shall,on the day

allowed tovote. The officersof the electionshallcertify
the returns of the election, and give certificatesto the

174 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
•personselected,which shallentitlethem toqualify; but
in case of a contest itshall be determined by the county

town ; and the contest shallin allrespectsbe governed by
theprovisionsof articleseven,chapterthirty-three,Gen-
eralStatutesof Kentucky ,so far as the same relatesto
county officers.
§ 11. Said board of trusteesshallhave power ,by ordi-
nance , to prevent and punish nuisances within the town

or laws of the State shallbe passed.
§ 12. No ordinance shall become effectual until it shall

ville, and copies of the ordinance , written or printed,

§ 14. This act shalltake effectfrom its passage : Pro-
vided,however, That nothing in thisact shallin any way

pany ; nor shallanything in the said charter be so con-

CHAPTER 904 ,
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act regulatingdefenses in actions
of trespassin Carrolland Trimble counties," approved March the twenty-
eighth, one thousand eight hundred and seventy-two .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled"An act regulating defenses
in actions of trespass in Carroll and Trimble counties,"
approvedMarch the twenty-eighth, one thousand eight
hundred and seventy-two ,be amended by adding thereto

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 175
the following : when a partition fence has existed, or
may hereafterexistby agreement or acquiescencebe-
tween two or more persons,neitherpartyshallremove
the same without the consent of the other, except be-
tween the firstof December and the firstof March ensu-
ing .
§2 . No such change as named in the last section shall
be made unless three months ' previous notice,in writing,
shallbe given to the oppositepartyby theperson desiring
to make the same .
§3. Whenever a divisionfenceexists,each partythere-
to shall be required to keep a good and sufficientfence,
such as willturn ordinaryunbreachy stock,on his por-
tion of the line; and in case he neglects or failsto do so,
the party so failingshallbe liablefor allthe damage the
other party may sustainfrom the trespassingof cattle or
other stock over said divisionfence,and the party sus-
taining the injury may bring his action and recover the
same in any court of competent jurisdiction.
§4 . If one part owner of a division fence shall fail,
afterreasonable notice,to repair the same as far as need-
ful and proper, the other part owner may proceed to
repair the same , and recover from the recusant the value
of the repairs so made .
§5 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 21 , 1884.
CHAPTER 905 .
AN ACT to incorporateOphelia Council, number eleven,of Order of Chosen
Friends, at Cadiz, Trigg county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the members of " Ophelia Council, number
eleven, of Order of Chosen Friends," at Cadiz , Trigg
county,Kentucky ,and those hereafteradmittedto mem-
bershipin saidcouncil,be,and they are hereby,incorpo-
rated,in the name and styleof " Ophelia Council,number
eleven, of Order of Chosen Friends;" and in that name

176 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and styleshallbe capable of contracting and being con-
tracted with,suing and being sued ,pleading and being
impleaded ; and shall have the power of acquiring and
holding, by purchase or otherwise, real and personal
property for the benefitof said council,not exceeding
ten thousand dollars; and may at any time selland con-
vey same , or any part thereof, and reinvestor dispose of
the proceeds ; and to pass and adopt such by -laws for its
government as itmay deem proper,not inconsistentwith
its charter, and with the Constitution and laws of the
United States and of this State. The said council may
adopt a seal, renew and break same at pleasure; may
build and erect,own and hold,or purchase a hall,and
such other buildings as they may see fit. They may
loan,or cause to be loaned, any money that they may
have on hand ,and collectthe same by suit or otherwise
in theircorporatename ,or transactany other business
theymay deem proper and right, or may have power
and authorityto investtheirmeans in lands,bonds,or
stocks,and hold ordisposeof same at pleasure.
§2 . That to the officersof saidcouncil,and theirsuc-
cessorsin office, under the directionof a majority of the
members of said council, is confidedthe management
of the affairsof said corporation,who , or a majority of
whom ,shallhave power to make allcontractspertain-
ing to the personal and real property of said council,
and in conveying any real estate which may belong to
same ,they shalljoinin the conveyance.
§3 . That the service of any process upon the chief
officer of the said council shall be deemed and held as
sufficient notice to said council . That the General As
sembly reserves the right to amend , alter, or abolish this
charter at any time,upon the request of a majority of all
the members of said council .
§4. This acttobe in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved April 19, 1884 .
