CHAPTER 906 .
AN ACT to amend the road laws of Rowan county .
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be the duty of the county court of
Rowan to immediately ,afterthe taking effectof this act,
to appoint one road commissioner forsaid county,who
shall hold his officeuntil his successor is appointed and
qualified. It shallbe the duty of saidroad commissioner
so appointed ,on receipt of notice of his appointment to
said office,or at the next county court of his county there-
after, and before entering upon the dutiesof his said
office, to take an oath or affirmation for the faithfuldis-
charge of his duties as road commissioner of said county ,
and give bond and security,to be approved by the county
courtand payable to theCommonwealth ,and conditioned
for the faithfuldischarge of his duties as road commis-
sioner; on which bond any person consideringhimself
aggrieved may sue ,and one recovery shall be no bar to
other suit or suits .
§2 . It shall be the duty of the road commissioner to
lay off said county into road districts,which he may alter
and change at pleasure,and to appoint one road overseer
for each districtso laid off by him ; and he may remove
said overseer at pleasure; and itshallbe the duty of the
severaloverseersso appointedby the road commissioner
to cause the roads in their respective districtsto be im-
proved and kept in good repair,and see thatallthe labor
or money due to the roads is faithfullyapplied to the
roads of their districts,
§3 . The said road commissioner shall be a body -cor-
porate,and by the name of the Road Commissioner of
Rowan County may sue and be sued,may plead and be
impleaded,defend and be defended,answer and be an-
swered; and by the name and styleaforesaidmay con-
tract and be contracted with in relation to any of the
objects, direct or incidental, for which he may be ap-
pointed; and he and his successors shallbe considered in
LOC . L.,VOL . II- 12

178 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
law a body in perpetuity: Provided,however, That any
person may and shall,for the malfeasance ,trespasson ,or .
conduct incompatiablewith the powers granted by this
act of incorporation on the part of said commissioner ,
have the right to sue such commissioner and his securi-
ties in theirindividual right; and in that character shall
they alone be liablefor said wrongs hereinbeforemen-
tioned .
§4 . Every male person residingin the county,over the
age of eighteen years and under the age of fiftyyears ,
shallbe compelled to furnish work on the public roads,
by himselfor substitute,four days ineach year; and,in
addition to the aforesaid road labor,a levy of one ,two ,
or three days ' work in each year for each one hundred
dollars' worth of propertyassessedforStaterevenue pur-
poses,saidwork to be leviedby the commissioner of pub-
lichighways according to the requirements of the roads ;
and each person herein required to work on the public
roads shallfurnishsuitabletoolsforthe labor so required.
It shallbe the duty of the overseer to give persons liable
to work on the public roads at leastthree days ' notice of
the time and place of working said roads in said district;
and if such person should failto attend and work the
time required of him by law , to the satisfactionof the
overseer,or furnish aforesaid four days' labor by substi-
tute,he shallforfeitand pay to the overseer of his district
two dollarsfor every one day of said work so delinquent;
or shouldsuch personsas may be liableto work any time
on aforesaidlevyon propertyfailto work the same tothe
satisfaction of the overseer of the districtwhere the work
properlybelongs,he shallforfeitand pay to the overseer
of the district where such work belongs one dollar and
twenty fivecents for every one day so delinquent,to be
recovered in the name of the Commonwealth , as ishere-
inafterprovided for,which amount so recovered shallbe
appliedto thedistrictwhere thelabororiginallybelonged.
§5 . There shallbe a taxleviedforthepurpose ofbuild-
ing and maintainingbridgesand for incidentalexpenses
thatmay occurin performingthedutiesconnectedthereto,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 17
andforallsurveyingand engineering,printing,advertis-
ing,and books ,blanks ,& c., necessary for the same , and
forthe enforcement of road and bridge laws ,and for the
salaryand pay of the said road commissioner and his asso-
ciates; and the county court of said county shalldeter-
mine the amount of the tax to be collectedforthe purposes
herein mentioned , and shallbe governed by the estimates
and recommendations of the said road commissioner ; and
thefund arisingfrom said tax levy shall be known as the
county road and bridge fund . The tax herein mentioned
shall be levied from the assessment rolls as assessed for
State revenue purposes , and said tax shallbe collectedby
the sheriff at the same time and manner he collects taxes
for State revenue purposes . The said sheriffshall give
bonds,made payable to the Commonwealth , with good
securities, conditioned for the faithfuldischarge of the
duties as required of him by thisact for the faithfulcol-
lectionand disbursing of said road and bridge fund ,accord-
ing to the requirements of this act, which bonds shall
be approved of by the county court of said county , the
same as otherbonds of the said sheriff; and on which , for
any failureof said sheriffto collector disburse said road
and bridge funds according to the requirements of thisact,
it shall be the duty of the road commissioner , in his cor-
porate capacity,to institutesuitin the county court of
said county against said sheriffand his securities,and to
prosecute said suit to finaljudgment for such default of
said sheriff.
§6 . That the county judge and justicesof the peace in
said county be , and are hereby , authorized to renderjudg-
ment out of theirregular term time against any hand that
shall hereafterfailto work said roads as required of them
by thisact,by due proof being made thereof, before said
judge or justicesof the peace ,of such failue. The over-
seer shallbe a competent witness to prove any factbefore
any judge or justiceon trialof said cause ; and proof of
person having had three days'notice,verbalor written,
given to him or leftat his residence, or with his agent ,or
with any ofhisfamilycompetent of informinghim of the

.180 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
time and place of working the roads, will be sufficient.
The penalty forfailingor refusingto work the roads shall
be as heretoforeprovided; and capiasprofinemay issue
until same is satisfied .
§7 . Any person claiming exemption from road work
on account of age ,must present to the overseer, if he
require it,his affidavitstating that he is over fiftyor
under eighteen years of age ,sworn to before some officer
authorized to administer an oath ; and the road commis-
sioner is hereby ,authorized to administer said oath that
may be required in the enforcement of the said road law
in said county .
§ 8. It shall be the duty of each and every road over-
seer to report to the commissioner of his county , and
settleup hisaccounts as road overseer,on or beforethe
firstday of December of each year , which report or set-
tlement shallbe verifiedby the oath of said overseer.
They shall also report the condition of the roads in their
districts; and if any overseershallfailto so reportand
settleup with saidcommissioner,or having settled,there
be any laborerdelinquent in his districtat the time of his
settlement,he shallhimselfbe responsibleforsuchdelin-
quents ,unless he can show to the satisfactionof the road
commissioner that such delinquencywas not a faultof
his. It shall be the duty of the road commissioner , on
or before the firstMonday in January of each year , to
reportto the county court any overseerthat may be de-
linquent for the previous year , or in making his settle-
ment with said road commissioner as requiredby law ;
and said court shallcause a rule to issue against such
overseer; and afterfivedays' notice to him , shallrender
judgment againsthim forthe fullamount of labor due
the road in his district,unless he shall answer and show ,
by proof,thatthe saidlaborhas been appliedto theroad
in good faith, or that the same could not be obtained
afterproceeding as directedby law; but shallrender
judgment for all costs,if he shall failto settlebefore he
was reported to said court. On such judgment rendered
by saidcourta capiaspro finemay be issuedagainstsaid

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 181
overseer,from time to time, until the same shall have
been satisfied. Said suit shall issue in the name of the
road commissioner ,who shall be a competent witness to
prove any fact on the trialof said suit. Any person
liable to do road work on the public roads may be ap-
pointed overseerby the said road commissioner; and
upon his receiptof his commission,or noticeof his ap-
pointment as overseer of certain districts, he shall be
held by law to be the overseer of said district,and liable
to allthe penaltiesof thisact: Provided, That no person
shall be required to serve as overseer more than two
years out of any three. The road commissioner shall
allow each overseer,in his settlement with him ,one dol-
lar per day for each day necessarilyspent on or for the
benefit of the road in his said district,over and above the
four days required of him by law ; and , for the time as-
sessed upon property against him , to be paid out of the
delinquent fund of his district, ifthere be any ; and if
there should be no delinquent fund belonging to his dis-
trict,then he shallpay them out of the county road and
bridge funds .
§ 9. The road and bridge fund of said county shallbe
under the control of the road commissioner of said coun-
ty, and paid out on his order, which shall be proper
vouchers in the hands of the sheriff for the same . It
shallbe the duty of the road commissioner , firstof all,
to settlethe incidental expenses of the enforcement of
the road law in said county ,and give his orders for the
same on the said road and bridge fund . The residue of
the said road and bridge fund shallbe applied by the
road commissioner to the improvement of roads and
building and repairing of said county bridges,which im-
provement and buildinghe may have made by contract
or otherwise,as he shalldeem proper: Provided,however,
That when the amount to be expended shallexceed fifty
dollars , there shall be associated with him two other
persons,freeholdersand citizensof the county,to be ap-
pointed by the county judge ,who shallnot be interested
in the improvement to be made , and who ,while acting

182 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
as counselor or adviser,shallbe under oath for the faith-
fulperformance of theirduty as such; and when said
improvement shallhave been completed to the satisfac-
tion of the commissioner and said advisers, where the
amount shall exceed fiftydollars,the commissioner shall'
draw his order on the sherifffor the money so expended
forbridges or other improvements ,directingitto be paid
out of the said road and bridge fund of said county .
which order the sheriffshallpay when it ispresented to
him , and there are funds in his hands to that amount .
The said commissioner shall, on or before the firstMon-
day in January in each year , make and deliver to the
county court a true report of the condition of the road
and bridge fund of said county ,showing the amount that
has been collectedthe previous year ; alsothe amount that
has been expended , and what such expenditures were for

§10 . The county judge ,county clerk, and road com-
missioner,or any two of them , shallconstitutea board to
settlewith the sheriff, on or before the fifteenthday of
December of each year , the receipts and disbursements
of the road and bridge funds of said county by him , and
make such allowance to said sheriff as may seem rea-
sonable,which allowance may be kept out of said road
and bridge fund by said sheriff.
§11 . The road commissioner shall be allowed a rea-
sonable compensation for his services as road commis-
sioner. He shallkeep a correct account of his services,
and make out a detailed account of the same ,and pre-.
sent itto the county judge and clerk,who shallaudit said
account,and directthe sherifftopay tosaidcommissioner
the amount allowed out of said road and bridge fund not

§ 12. It shallbe the duty of the road commissioner of
saidcounty to procure and keep in hisofficea book or

books,in which he shallcause to be enteredthenames of
all the road overseers, with the date of their appoint-
ment ,and the boundaries of allthe road districtsin said

missioner shall also cause an abstract of this act to be

as publicproperty,and deliverthe same to hissuccessor

fulllistof alltaxable property within said boundary , and
the name of the owner of the same ; also the names of

§14 . That in case of any vacancy by death ,removal ,

sioner herein named ; and upon the appointment and

his representatives,shall deliver to his successor all the

books and papers belonging to the public,or which was

§ 15. In allmotions or suitsbrought under the provis-
ions of thisact by the road commissioner,the attorney
forthe Commonwealth , if in the circuitcourt, and the

cute the same ; and the clerk, sheriff, and other officers

§ 16. Whenever it shallbecome necessary,in the im-
provement and repairsof the public roads and bridges,
for the county to have the service of teams ,or to have
timber,stone, earth, or other materialfor said purpose,
and the same cannot be obtained by contract or other-
wise ,then the overseer whose duty itis to have the pub-
licroads kept in repair,may press any team or teams ,cut

sioner for settlement, and deliverthe same ,when called
upon ,to the owner of saidpropertyso taken; and when

Provided,however,That in no case shallthe overseer take

or from within the yards of any farm buildings,or any

may work theirroads with teams ,wagons ,plows,road-

working ,he shallgive due notice to persons having road
work to perform to appear on the road at specifiedtime

and place of working saidroads,with team ,wagon ,plow ,
road-scraper,or with any other tools,allor any part of
them , that they may have in their possession, as may be

son or persons furnishing said teams and tools. And any

§ 17. That whenever itshall become necessary for the

to change the track of said roads to either side of the old

ested freeholders, and have them value the damage that

§ 18. The public roads,when necessary,may be kept
open fiftyfeetwide; and in no case shallthey be closed
up to lessthan twenty feet. Any person closingup said

186 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
roads to a less width ,or willfullyfillingup a ditch of
said roads,or tearing out a water -break , or otherwise

by thegrand jury of saidcounty,and fined,in the discre-
tion of the jury, any sum not exceeding fifteendollarsfor
overseer,and fiftydollarsforotherofficers,forthe willful
neglectof the dutiesrequired of them by thisact over and
above the responsibilitythatthey may incur from such neg-
lectof duty . In said action,finesrecovered against officer
or otherperson,arisingunder thisact,shallbe paid over
by the officercollectingthe same to the sheriffof said
county,to be held and paid out by him as part of the
road and bridge fund of said county ; and the officercol-
lectingand paying over the same shall forthwith report
the same to the road commissioner ,with the amount so
collectedand paid over to said sheriff.
§19 . That all former amendments to the road laws of
the county of Rowan , and all general laws conflicting
with thisact, as far as the aforesaidcounty is concerned ,

§20 . This act to take effectafteritsratificationby the
legal voters of Rowan county , said vote being cast at
a regular election .
Approved April 19, 1884 .
CHAPTER 907 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Bowling Green and Greenville Turnpike Com-
pauy .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . Frank W. Jackson, W. H. Blokley, L. Stallord,
Garrett Stewart , Thomas Dial, and Jas. Thompson , and
their associates and successors,be ,and they are hereby ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 187
createda body -politicand corporate,under the name and
styleof theBowling Green and GreenvilleTurnpike Com-
pany,and by said name and styleshallhave power to
contract and be contracted with ,sue and be sued , plead
and be impleaded,in allcourtsand places in thisCom-
monwealth ; and may have and use a common seal, and
may alteror change the same at pleasure.
§2 . The said company ishereby authorizedand em-
powered to construct a turnpike,macadamized , or gravel
road, in itsdiscretion, from a point at or near where the
Bowling Green and Greenville road leaves the Bowling
Green and Morgantown road ,near the residence of
Johnson where Robert Hollend formerly lived, running
thence by Frank W. Jackson's,and terminating at or near
a point where the present road intersectsthe old Greenville
road. Said road to be located on the most practicable
route between said points; and in order to raisefunds
necessaryto constructsaidroad,saidcompany shallhave
power to raisea sum ,by the issue and sale of the capital
stock, not to exceed thirtythousand dollars,to be divided
into shares of fiftydollarseach .
§3 . That the books for the subscriptionof stockmay
be opened in thecityof Bowling Green ,and such other
places as may be deemed best, under the directionof the
persons hereinbefore named ,allof whom are hereby made
commissioners for the purpose of obtaining the capital
stockof saidcompany ; they may insertan obligationin
thesubscriptionbook and listssubstantiallyasfollows: We
whose names are hereunto subscribed,severallypromise to
pay the Bowling Green and GreenvilleTurnpike Company
the sum of fiftydollarsfor each share of stock in said
company setoppositeour names ,insuch manner and pro-
portion,and at such times,as shallbe requiredby said
company . Witness our hands this day of
one thousand eighthundred and eighty---. When the sum
of three thousand dollars shall have been subscribed to
the capitalstock of said company ,it shallbe the duty of
said commissioners named in this act to give notice, in
such manner as they may think proper,of a meeting of

188 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the stockholdersof saidcompany ,at such time and place
asmay be specifiedinsaidnotice,forthepurposeofelect-
ing five directors ; one vote shall be allowed for each
share of stock ; the directors when thus elected shall elect
one of theirnumber president of said company , and the
president and directorsshallcontinue in officeforone year ,
and untiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified. The
time and place of allelectionsafterthe firstelectionshall
be fixedby thepresidentand directors, and a majorityof
the board shallbe competent to transact business.
§ 4. So soon as saidcompany is organizedby the elec-
tion of a president and directors,as above provided ,they
shallbe a body -politicand corporate,by the name and
styleabove stated; and by that name shallhave perpet-
ual succession,and allthe privilegesand franchisesinci-
dent to a corporation; shallbe capable of holding their
capitalstock, and taking and holding, by purchase or
gift, all such lands, tenements , and hereditaments , real
or personalproperty,as may be necessaryfor the prose-
cutionof itswork or the objectsof thiscorporation.
§5 . The president of said company may callmeetings
of the board of directorswhenever he may thinkproper,
and on application of any two directors to him ; the
board of directorsshallhave power to elect a secretary
and treasurer,and such other officersas they may think
necessary, and to fillsuch vacancies as may occur in said
board during their term of office. Said board shallre-
quire of the treasurer, before he assumes the duties of
his office,a bond for the faithfuland honest discharge of
his duties,and to faithfullyaccount for allmoneys that
may come to his hands as such treasurer,which bond and
security shall be approved by the board . Said board
shallkeep a record of theirproceedings,which shallbe
signedby thepresident; and ,in the absence of the presi-
dent , the board may elect a president pro tem. Said
board shallhave power to make allnecessaryby -laws
and regulationsfor the government of said company ,
and may sue for and recover,in the name of said com-
pany ,from delinquentstockholders,the amount due and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 189
unpaid upon any share or shares of stock in said com-
pany .
§ 6. The president and directors shall fix and regulate
the elevation and grade of said road ,the width and part
thereof to be covered with stone or gravel,and may fix
the rates of toll,and may change and alter the same at
pleasure ; but said rates shall not exceed the rates pre-
scribed by chapter one hundred and ten of the General
Statutes . And when three consecutive miles of said road
shallhave been completed,and examined and approved
by any two justicesof the peace of Warren county,who
shallexamine the same on applicationof thepresidentof
said road ,a toll-gate may be erected,and tollcollectedun-
•
der such rulesand regulationsas shallbe prescribedby the
president and directors,and under the general law of the
State. But untilsaid road is completed for fiveconsec-
utivemilesthe tollcollectedshallbe in proportionas the
length of road finishedbears to fivemiles.
§7 . The president and directors of said company ,and
theiremployes,are hereby authorizedto take theconsent,
in writing ,of allpersons who may consent to grant the
right of way for said turnpike road,and the use of ma-
terialsfor building and repairing said road ; and when
either the rightof way or any of the materials necessary
for the construction of said road cannot be acquired by
consent or purchase,saidpresidentand directorsmay ap-
ply to the Warren county courtfora writof ad quoddam-
num to assess the damages which may be sustained by
the owner of said land,and the jury,in assessingthe
damages ,may take into consideration the advantages re-
sultingtothepartyor partiesclaimingdamages by reason
of the constructionof the road; and upon the payment
of the damages assessed, or a tender thereof,it shallbe
lawful for said company and its employes to make said
road , and to dig and carry away any stone or other ma-
terialnecessaryfor the constructionor repairof said
road,and said company may acquireby purchase,orby
writ of asad quoddamnum ,as above setforth,land,not ex-
ceedingone acre,forthepurpose of erectinga toll-house
on said road; and said company may procure from the

190 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Warren county court the rightof way over any road over
which said county has control.
§ 8. The presidentand directorsof said company may
make and declare equitabledividends of the surplus earn-
ings of said company among the stockholders according
to their respectiveshares, after paying the expenses of
the company .
§9 . Certificatesof stockin saidcompany shallbe signed
by the president,and countersigned by the secretary; shall
be deemed personal estate, and shall be transferableon
the books of the company in such manner as the presi-
dent and directorsshallprescribe.
§ 10. The capital stock of said company shallbe used
in the procurement of the right of way , materials, and
so forth, and in constructing said road , and in defraying
alllegitimateexpenses incident to the constructionof said
road , and for no other purpose ; and the stockholders in
saidcompany shallonly be bound to the extentof their
stock in said company .
§ 11. This act shalltake effectfrom its passage .
Approved April 19, 1884 .
CHAPTER 908 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kentucky Mining , Manufacturing, and Con .
structionCompany .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That W. R. Minish,W. A. Leitch,J.S. Gibson,W.
H. Lowdenback ,T. S. Hartford,Noel Smoot , O. S. Gib-
son,and such otherassociatesas the above corporators
may adopt ,and their successors, are hereby created a
body-politicand corporate,named the Kentucky Mining ,
Manufacturing,and ConstructionCompany ; and by that
name shall have power to contract and be contracted
with; to acquire and hold property,real and personal,
and the same to convey ; to sue and be sued in allcourts
and placesas a naturalbody forever; to have and use a
seal; and to make all necessary by -laws for the govern

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 191
ment of, and to expedite the business of the said com .
pany .
§2 . The said company shallhave power to purchase,
acquire, and hold such real estate, mining privileges,
rightsof way ,personalproperty,and othereasements,as
itmay deem necessary or expedient forits business. It
shallhave power to dig,bore ,mine ,or in any way search
for iron,lead, coal, salt, oil,concealed or hidden treas-
ure,or any other mineral product or deposits; and shall
have power to erecthouses,mills,machinery,telegraph
and telephone lines,turnpike and railroads,or to take
stock in said roads , and trade in all kinds of merchan-
dise, stocks, & c.,and to exercise any proper power to
carryout fullythe powers grantedby thisact.
§3 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe two
hundred thousand dollars($200,000),dividedintoshares
of twenty dollars each , and may be increased to three
hundred thousand dollars($300,000) when ,in the opin-
ion of the board of directors, the business of the com-
pany shalldemand it; and said stockshallbe personal
property .
§ 4. The principalofficeof the company shallbe kept
and held in the town of Gratz,Owen county,Kentucky .
§ 5. The persons named in the firstsection of thisact
shall constitutethe firstboard of directors; they, or
majorityof them, shallhave power to provide and open
books for subscription to stock, provide and issue said
stock as the by-laws of the company may provide,and
prescribetheterms of subscription,the times and man-
ner of payment , and grounds of forfeitureof the stock
subscribedand not paid in as requiredby call; and they
may eitherforfeitthe stock of defaulting stockholders or
collectthe same by suitas they may elect.
§ 6. The said firstboard of directors are authorized to
issue stock in such amounts ,not exceeding one half the
capital stock, and selland dispose of the same upon such
terms and conditionsas they may consider proper; to
purchase mines,mineral rights,rightsof way ,buildings,
roads , machinery , or other franchises, or to defray other

192 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
necessary expenses incidentalto and forthe proper work-
ing and management of the company .
§7. Said company may receivesubscriptionsin money ,
notes,bonds , securities, mines , lands ,mining privileges,
or other property .
§8 . The board of directorsmay appoint such agents ,
eitherin or out of the State,as they may find necessary,
and remove the same at pleasure.
§9 . The president and secretary of the company shall
be appointed by the board of directors, and may be of
their number .
§ 10. Provision shallbe made by by -laws for the annual
electionby the stockholders of a board of directorsof not
less than seven members ; and such election shall be an-
nuallyheldin the town ofGratz,Kentucky,on thefirst
Friday in November . Each share of stock shallentitle
the holder to one vote .
§ 11. No person holdinglessthan fiveshares of stock
shallbe eligibleto officeas a director of said company .
§ 12. All officerselected shall hold officeuntil their
successorsare electedand qualifiedas provided by the
by -laws .
§ 13. Only thepropertyof the company shallbe liable
for the debts or other obligationsof the said company .
• § 14. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 19 , 1884 .

194 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 911 .
AN ACT authorizingthe county court of Logan county to build a new
court-house or fire-proof clerks' offices,eitheror both , as they may elect,
and levy taxesto pay forsame .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That the county courtof Logan county,a majority
of the justices concurring ,ishereby authorized and em-
powered to erect a new court-house or fire-proof clerks'
offices,either or both , as it may desire or elect to do , at
any time ,at a cost not to exceed ,for both,the sum of
fiftythousand dollars.
§2 . For the purpose of paying for same ,the county
court is hereby authorized to levy an ad valorem tax on
all the property in said county ,subject to taxation for
revenue purposes, not exceeding twenty (20) cents on
each one hundred dollars' worth of property,and a per
capitatax on each person in said county,subjectto poll.
tax,not exceeding one ($1) dollarper head; eitheror
both of said taxes may be levied in the discretionof said
court,but fora period of not exceeding four years. Said
per capitatax shallbe in addition(iflevied)to the regu-
lar levy for county purposes ,
§3 . That said court is hereby authorized and empow-
ered,at any time and on any terms it may deem best,
to sell,eitherpubliclyor privately,the house and lotof
ground now owned by Logan county , and used as clerks'
officesand sheriff'soffice, in Russellville,Kentucky ; and
also the present court-house building and ground , and
convey the same ; and the proceeds of sale arisingfrom
eitheror both of said pieces of property shallbe ap .
plied in liquidationof the indebtedness incurred in build-
ing eithera new court-house or fire-proof clerks'offices,
or both .
§ 4. That the sheriffof Logan county shallbe,by vir-
tue of his office, collector of the tax herein authorized
to be levied, and shall be responsible for the same on
bond executed ,with good and sufficientsurety, to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , for the faithfuldischarge

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 195
of his duties in collectingand accounting for said taxes,
and paying over the same to such person or persons as
the county court may order, upon which bond suit may
be brought in the name of the Commonwealth for the
use of said county court. His compensation shall be
fixedby said court,but shallnot exceed the amount paid
for collectingthe State revenue : Provided, If the sheriff
aforesaid shall failor refuse to collectsaid tax,then said
court isauthorized to appoint a collector,fixhis compen-
sation,take bond as aforesaid, and prescribe his powers
and duties .
§5 . The building or buildings authorized to be built
under thisact may be erected on any propertynow be-
longing to Logan county , and situated in the town of
Russellville, or that may hereafterbe acquired by the
county forthat purpose .
§ 6. This act shall not take effect or be in force until it
shall have been submitted to the legal voters of Logan
county , and shallbe voted for by a majority of those vot-
ing ; and for the purpose of taking the sense of the people ,
said court ishereby authorized at any time , and as often
as they may deem itbest,to order an electionto be held
in saidcounty,upon atleastthirtydays' noticeprintedin
each of the newspapers published in Logan county ,and
by handbillsdistributed,at which electionthe following
questionmay be submitted to the qualifiedvotersof said
county ,viz: " Are you for or against a new court-house
and fire-proofclerk'soffices?" which saidquestionmay be
submitted at any electionheld under thisact.
§7 . Any election held under thisact shall be held by
same officersand under the same restrictionsand penal-
ties as electionsare now held forState and county officers;
and if at any election held under this act a majority of
the legal votes cast at said election shall be in favor of
building a new court-house or fire-proof clerk'soffices,or
both ,then this act shall be in fullforce, and said court
shallproceed to carrythesame intoeffect.
§ 8. All laws in conflictherewithare hereby repealed,
so far as the purposes of thisact are concerned .
Approved April 19 , 1884 .

196 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 912 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe JeffersontownCemetery Company .
WHEREAS , On the firstday of August , one thousand
eight hundred and sixty-four,C. T. Wells and wife con-
veyed unto Dr. W. W. Senteny ,Dr. P. P. Fenley ,and Jas.
M. Tucker , as trustees,allof Jefferson county , four acres
of land , more or less, near Jeffersontown, Kentucky , to
be used as a burial ground ; and in said deed , which is
recorded in the Jeffersoncounty clerk'soffice,Dr. W. W.
Senteny ,Dr. P. P. Fenley , and James M. Tucker are ap-
pointed trustees,with power to sellsaid lots in parcels
of twenty feet square each ,the lotshaving been laid off
and numbered before the purchase of the said ground
was made by the said trustees; the proceeds of the sale
of said lotsto be paid to the said C. T. Wells :
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Dr. W. W. Senteny, Dr. P. P. Fenley,and
James M. Tucker , and their successors,be ,and they are
hereby , made a body -politicand corporate in law , under
the styleand firm of the JeffersontownCemetery Com-
pany ; and shallbe able and capable in law to have and
use a common seal,to sue and be sued ,plead and be
impleaded , and do allother things incident to a corpora-
tionstheyshallhave power to give certificatesforalllots
that are sold or may be sold, which certificateshall vest
the purchaserwith title. This titlemay be transferred
by the consent of a majority of the trustees, but in no
other way shallthey be transferred.
§2 . The trustees shall have power to appoint , from
time to time,such superintendent, sexton,or other offi-
cers as they may think necessary. The said lots shall
not be used for any other purpose than burial lots, and if
appliedforany otheruse,the titleshallrevertto the cor-
poration.
§3 . If any person shallwillfullyand without lawful
authority violate any of the graves of the dead , or de-
face or remove any tombstone or monument , or cut ,
break , or injure any tree, plant, or shrub, or other orna-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 197
!
[
ment of said cemetery ,they shallbe deemed guilty of a
misdemeanor , and besides being liableto the corporation
or the owners of the lotsfor the injury done ,they shallbe
fined not less than ten nor more than one hundred dol-
lars for each offense,recoverable by warrant before any
justiceof the peace in Jeffersoncounty,or he shallbe
imprisonednot lessthan fivenor more than fiftydays.
§4 . If any vacancy shall occur in the board of trus-
tees,by death or otherwise, the lot-holders may fillthe
same by an election to be held afterten days ' notice, to
be postedin threeconspicuousplacesin the town of Jef-
fersontown , by an order of the remaining trustee or
trustees , to be entered of record in the books of said
company .
§ 5. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 19 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 913 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof W. H. H. Thompson , of Laurel county.
WHEREAS ,John Mathews ,of Laurel county ,was legally
adjudged to be a pauper lunatic on the twentieth day of
July ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-three,by the
Laurel county court, and ,upon applicationof said court,
he was refused admission to the asylums at Lexington
and Hopkinsville,on account of no vacancy in said asy-
lums , and said Mathews having ,by order of said court,
been placed under charge of W. H. H. Thompson , of
London ,Laurel county ,to be provided for while awaiting
reply to applicationfor admission to the asylums, and
while under care and charge of Thompson he was taken
sick on July twenty-seventh,and died August thirteenth,
one thousand eighthundred and eighty-three, and said
Thompson having furnished him with lodging and board
and attended him in his sickness; therefore,to reimburse
said Thompson on account of said Mathews ,
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor be ,and he is hereby ,directed to
draw his warrant on the Treasurer in favor of W. H. H.

198 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Thompson ,for thirty-six dollars($36),to be paid out of
any money not otherwise appropriated .
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved April 19, 1884.
CHAPTER 914 .
AN ACT to prohibit the sale of intoxicatingliquors in the Cromwell
magisterialdistrict, in Ohio county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful to sellwhisky , brandy ,
wine ,ale,or beer,in any quantities,in any saloon or bar-
room ,or otherplace,in public or in private,within the
Cromwell magisterial district,of Ohio county .
§ 2. That it shall be unlawful for any druggist to sell
any of the liquorsnamed in section one of this act with-
in said district,except as the same may be prescribed by
a regular practicingphysician.
§3 . That itshallbe unlawfulforany physicianto pre-
scribe any intoxicatingliquorswithin said district,except
in cases of actual sickness .
§ 4. That any person or persons violating any of the
provisionsof thisact shall,on convictionby a competent
jury,be finedin the sum of fiftydollarsforeach offense.
§ 5. That the finesmentioned in section four of this act.
shallbe collectableby warrant ,in the name of the Com-
monwealth , in the police court of the town of Cromwell .
§ 6. That the foregoing fines shall be for the use and
benefitof the said town of Cromwell ,and that said fines,
when collected, shallbe paid to the treasurerof said
town .
§7 . That thisact take effectfrom and afteritspassage :
Provided, That nothing herein shallbe construed to inter-
ferewith any saloonor bar-room untilthe licensesof the
same shallhave expired.
Approved April 19, 1884 .

CHAPTER 921 .
4
AN ACT to prohibit the selling,loaning, or giving away any spirituous,
vinous, or malt liquorsat or within one mile of the church or school-
house inAdairville, in Logan county .

of Kentucky :

to sellor loan any spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsat
or within one mile of the churches and schools in the
town of Adairville, Logan county .
§2 . That any personviolatingany of the provisionsof
this act shall be fined for each offense not less than twen-

days,or both,at thediscretionof a jury.

CHAPTER 922 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Walnut StreetBaptistChurch,of Owensboro,
in Daviess county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. N. Mason , C. L. Nall, J. D. Robinson ,
and C W. Gordon ,trusteesof the Walnut StreetBaptist
Church , in Owensboro , Daviess county, Kentucky ,and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 203
theirsuccessorsin office,be,and they are hereby,created
a body-politicand corporate,by the name and styleof the

have perpetual succession,and a common seal, or may
use theirindividualsealsor scrolls; and may sue and be
sued,plead and be impleaded, in any court of law or
equity in thisState.
§2 . That when any vacancy in said board may occur
by death,resignation,or otherwise,or by other disquali-
ficationto act,to be judged by the church at a regular
business meeting ,it shallbe competent for the members
of said church to fillsuch vacancies at any regular busi-
ness meeting . Said church shall also have power to
remove from officeany one or more of said trustees, a
majorityof allthe members presentat any regularbusi-
nessmeeting concurringtherein,and may proceed to fill
such vacancy in the manner provided above for filling
* vacancies : Provided, That no removal or appointment
of a trustee shall take place until notice of such inten-
tion has been given at a previous regular business meet-
ing of the church .
§3 . That the above named trustees and their success-
ors shall have full power to buy , to take by devise or
bequest,or by giftor deed , any real estate not exceeding
the amount allowed by law to churches,and any per-
sonal property,and to hold, convey,and otherwise dis-
pose of the same ,with allthe powers incidentto religious
-corporations .
§ 4. That said trustees and theirsuccessors shall, and
they are hereby,vestedwith fullpower to borrow money
forthe purpose of erecting church buildings or other im-
provements for church purposes,or to pay offand dis-
charge indebtedness of said church for repairing and
building church or church property, and to mortgage the
real estateand church property and improvements to
secure borrowed money ; to selland convey the title
which they now hold,or may hereafteracquire or hold
in trust for said church , in any lot of ground , together
with the improvements thereon,whenever and to whom

204 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the said church ,at a regular business meeting , shalldi-
rect,or make any other dispositionof the same as said
church may orderor direct,noticehaving been given at
a regular business meeting of such sale or disposition,
and not otherwise,and to do and perform allacts com-
mon to limited corporations of likeorder,not to conflict
with the laws of this State .
§5 . This act to take effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 19 , 1884.
CHAPTER 923 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the Casidy Creek Turnpike Road , in Nicholas
county; preventing stock from running at large on said pike.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shallbe unlawful for any stock to run at
largeon CasidyCreek Turnpike Road ,in Nicholascounty.
§2 . Any person or persons being the owners of,or hav-
ing the custody of stock of any kind ,who shall sufferor
permit any of them to run at large on any portion of the
line of turnpike road belonging to said Casidy Creek
Turnpike Road Company , shallbe fined,for each and
every offense, not less than one dollar nor more than
twenty dollars,to be recovered by warrant in the name
of the Commonwealth ,beforeany judge or justiceof the
peace which the turnpike road runs .
§3 . Unless said fine is paid or replevied,the stock so
running at large shallbe subjectto the fineand costs; and
an execution shallbe levied upon it, and the officershall
proceed to collect same as in case of other executions .
The fine so paid shallgo to the trustee of the jury fund
in the county in which the offense was committed .
§4 . This act shalltake effectand be in force from the
firstday of June ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
four,and notice shallbe posted at three of the most pub-
lic places on line of said turnpike road for two weeks
before the day thislaw take effect.
Approved April 19, 1884.

CHAPTER 926 .
AN ACT enablingthe sheriffsof Meade and Breckinridge countiestomore
efficientlycollectthe State revenue and county taxes.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe the duty of the sheriffsof Meade
and Breckinridgecountiesto advertise,before the tenth

206 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
day of August in each year ,by written or printed posters,
to be posted in conspicuous and public places,and not
lessthan four of which are to be in a voting precinct,
stating the proportionate amount of revenue due from
the sheriffon the firstday of October,the proportionate
amount due on the firstday of December ,and the full
amount due the State on the firstday of April. The
postersshallalsodesignate the times when and the places
where the sheriffwill meet the tax-payers ; not more than
two places in any one precinct shall be named or desig-
nated . The posters shallalso fixthe time of meeting the
tax-payers at the court-house in the officeof the sheriff.
§2 . The sheriffshallhave one appointment in each pre-
cinctin the months of September and October , and shall
likewise have another appointment in each precinct in
the month of November .
§3 . It shall be the duty of the tax-payers to meet the
sheriff, or his deputy , at the appointed places of meeting
and pay their taxes; and upon the failure or refusal of
the tax-payer so to do , at the time and place designated
by the poster, before the firstday of December ,such per-
son so refusing or so failingshall be required to pay said
tax to the sheriffat the court-house in his,the sheriff's,
office,before the firstday of March .
§ 4. Upon the refusalor failureof any person to pay
his tax before the firstday of March ,such person so re-
fusing or so failing shall pay to the sheriffan additional
tax,or penalty, of five cents on every one hundred dol-
lars' worth of such person's taxable property.
§5 . The sheriffor his deputies shallbe at hisrespective
places of appointment,as fixedby his posters,not later
than ten o'clock,beforenoon , and shall not depart there-
from before the hour of three, afternoon; and upon the
failureof said sheriff,or hisdeputy ,to meet the tax-payer ,
as providedin thisact,he shall,upon indictmentfound,
and convictionhad ,be finednot more than fiftydollars
for each offense .
§6 . The fullamount of the tax paid as penalty,less
the sheriff'scommission ,shall become and be a part of
the State revenue ,

§ 7. This act shalltake effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 19 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 927 .
AN ACT toamend an act, entitled" An act to incorporatethe Richmond ,
Boonesboro , and Otter Creek Turnpike Road Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Richmond , Otter Creek , and Boonesboro
Turnpike Road Company isreleasedand foreverrelieved
from keeping up and maintaining so much of the Rich-
mond ,Otter Creek ,and Boonesboro Turnpike Road as ex-
tends from a point on said road ,near Otter Creek ,Mad-
ison county , five miles from beginning of said pike, in
Richinond ,to the terminus of said road at the mouth of
Stony Run , a distance of about three miles.
§ 2. That the Richmond ,Otter Creek , and Boonesboro
Turnpike Road Company shall only be required here-
aftertokeep up and maintain,accordingtoitscharter,the
aforesaidfivemiles of road beginning at Richmond ,and
shallnot,in any way ,be liablefor any failureto keep up
and maintain the three miles of road next to Stony Run ,
in Madison county,or for injuriesor accidentsthatmay
hereafter occur on said three miles of road .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 19, 1884 .
CHAPTER 928 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to amend and reduce into one
the severalacts in regard to the city of Lebanon ," approved February
nineteen, one thousand eighth hundred and eighty-four.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act,entitled" An act to amend an act,
entitled 'An act to amend an act and reduce into one the
severalactsin regardto the cityof Lebanon , " approved
February nineteenth, one thousand eight hundred and

208 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
eighty-four,be , and the same is,amended as follows: In
allcivilcases that may be brought in the police court of
Lebanon , of which the circuitcourt has concurrent juris-
diction with the policecourt,the judge of the police court
shall be allowed to charge the same fees as are now by
law allowed to clerks of circuit courts for similar services .
§2 . In allcases in the police court of which the circuit
court has concurrent jurisdictionwith said court,there
shallbe taxed for the benefitof the successfulparty the
same attorney'sfees as are taxed by law in circuitcourts.
§3 . The board of trusteesof Lebanon may allow the
policejudge such salary as they deem proper .
§ 4. The provisionsof articlethree,chapter twelve , of
the General Statutes shall apply to a change of venue
from said police court in allcivil, criminal,and penal ac-
tions, whether said actions be under city ordinance or
otherwise .
§ 5. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 19, 1884.

§ 37. 1. There shall be in the town a court styled
the " Franklin Police Court ," which shall be held by
a police judge , elected as hereinbefore provided , and
having the qualificationsprescribedby law fora justice
of the peace .

Constitution of this State, enter upon the duties of his
office .

officialacts,and shall faithfullypreserve the records of
his office, and deliver same to his successor on demand ;

judge.

and for any failureherein,or otherwise,he shallbe sub-

inalproceedings; and his certifiedcopies of the official
acts,records, and proceedings thereof shall be evidence
and have the same effect as records of justices of the
peace .
5. In addition to the powers and duties conferred

terly courts for the trialof civilcauses in the town of

rulesof proceedings governing justicesof the peace shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 235
CHAPTER 930 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Hardin county to take stock in
turnpike roads .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That ,upon the writtenapplicationof a majority
of the real estate owners living along the line of any
projected turnpike road in Hardin county , and within
one and one half miles of said road , made to the county
judge of saidcounty,saidjudge shallappointthreedis-
creet persons living along the line of said road , and
interestedin the construction of the same ,whose duty it
shall be to procure subscribers to stock for construction
of same ,to the amount of one half the necessary cost,
which cost shall not exceed two thousand dollars per
mile .
§2 . When one half of the necessary stock shall be
pledged by solventsubscribers,then the county court,a
majority of the justicesof the peace concurring therein,
shall subscribe stock in every mile of such road to be
builtin said county ,to the amount of one half the cost
of building said road ,but not to exceed one thousand
dollarsper mile.
§3 . For taking stock in such roads,the county court
shallapply such funds as may be on hand ,afterpaying
the bonded debt of said county , and when no such funds
shall be on hand , the said court shall issue the bonds
of the county of such denominations as the court may
choose,not lessthan fiftydollarseach,in printedform,
under the seal of the county , signed by the presiding
judge of saidcourt,and countersignedby the clerk,pay-
able when and where the court may elect,with annual
or semi -annual interestcoupons ,at a rate of interestnot
to exceed six per cent. per annum ,and to be sold at not
less than their par value, for the purpose of raising
money to pay forsaidturnpikestock.
§4 . To provide forthe payment of the principaland
interestof saidbonds,the saidcourtishereby authorized
to levyan ad valorem tax,not to exceed twenty centson

236 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the hundred dollars,on all the property in said county
subjectto taxation for county or State purposes , for each
and every year that such tax may be necessary; and the
proceeds of said tax,together with the dividends accruing
to the county on itsstock in said turnpike roads,shallbe
paid to the treasurerof the county ,who shallapply itin
payment of the principaland interestof the saidbonds ,
under the directions of said court .
§5 . The court shalldesignate the county treasurer,or,
if there be no such officer, a special treasurer to receive
and pay out the funds mentioned in section four of thes
act; and such treasurer shall, before receiving any of
said funds,execute a good and sufficientbond , with good
security,approved by the court,forthe faithfulperform-
ance of his duties .
§6 . The sheriffor collectorof taxes for said county ,
and his sureties,shall be responsible on their bonds -for
the collection of the taxes herein authorized , in the same
manner they are responsiblefor the ordinary county levy;
and shall have and exercise the same powers in the col-
lection thereof as they have by law for collectingthe
county and State taxes; and shall, under the same pen-
alties,pay over the same to whom the courtmay appoint
treasurer, and at such times as the court may require.
§7 . The directorsof any turnpike road to be builtunder
thisact shall be selectedin any manner the stockholders
in the same may choose : Provided, however, That the pre-
sidingjudge of said county shallbe ex officioa member of
the board of directorsof any turnpike road in the county
in which the county owns stock,and shall represent the
county , and cast the vote or votes to which the county
may be entitledin allthe meetings of said directors.
§ 8. The said board of directors shall decide as they
choose how much stock shall represent a vote in their
meetings .
§9 . All laws or parts of laws in conflictwith this act
are hereby repealed.
§ 10. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 21 , 1884.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 237
CHAPTER 931 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe CentralKentucky Telephone Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. G. Proctor,G. W. Welsh , jr., George D.
Weaner ,W. P. Walton ,W. S. Miller,B. M. Burdett,Chris-
topher Lyons , W. R. Williams,L. F. Hubble ,and J. W.
Alcorn , and their associates and successors, be, and they
are hereby ,created a body corporate and politic, under
the name and styleof " The CentralKentucky Telephone
Company ," with power ,under saidname ,to contractand
be contracted with,sue and be sued; to have perpetual
succession,and a common seal,and such other powers as
herein granted .
§2 . The said company shallhave power and authority
to construct,maintain,operate,telephonelinesbetween
and within any of the towns ,villages,private residences,
and citiesof the State of Kentucky ; may buy or lease
any telephone lines already constructed in the State of
Kentucky ,and may buy or lease such instruments as they
may deem necessary to carry on and conduct the business
of sending and receiving messages by telephone .
§3 . Said company may acquire rightsof way for the
said lines in the same manner as now provided by the
law of this Commonwealth for the acquisitionof rights
of way by turnpike road companies .
§ 4. Said company inay fixthe rate of charges for the
transmission of messages over theirlines.
§5 . The capital stock of said company shall be one
hundred thousand dollars,to be dividedintotwenty thou-
sand shares of fivedollarseach,to be paid in cash,par
value,or in stock of Lancaster,Danville,and Stanford,
or Stanford and HoustonvilleTelephone Companies ,by
transfer,at par value .
§ 6. The business of said company shall be managed
by a president and board of nine directors,who shallbe
chosen on the firstMonday in July, each year, by the
vote of the share-holders,at which election, to be held
by three persons appointed by the board of directorsin

238 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
office,each share shall be entitled to one vote,and the
persons receiving the greatest number of votes shall be
elected president and directorsfor the ensuing year ,and
until their successors are qualified .
§7 . The presidentand directorsof the company shall
be stockholders in the company , and shalltake an oath
before some officer authorized to administer an oath that
they will faithfullyperform the duties of their officebe-
fore they enter upon such duties; the certificateof the
officeradministering the oath to be entered on the books
of the company .
§ 8. The president and board of directors shall have
power to appoint a treasurer,secretary, and such other
officersas they may deem necessary or expedient for the
transaction of the business of the company , and may
require such bonds of them , payable to the company , as
they may deem necessary to secure the interestand rights
of the company ; and may make such by -laws ,rules and
regulations,for the proper carrying out of the provisions
of this act, not inconsistent with the Constitution and
laws of the United States and of this State .
§ 9. The principal place of business of said company
shall be at Danville,Kentucky , at which place all elec-
tions and business meetings shall be held,unless other-
wise ordered by the board of directors.
§ 10. A majority of the board of directorsshallconsti-
tute a quorum to transact business.
§ 11. Within sixty days after five hundred dollars of
stock is subscribed, in money or stock of the other tele-
phone companies above named ,the said corporators shall
calla meeting of the stockholders,to be held at Danville,
Kentucky ,for the purpose of electinga presidentand
board of directors,which electionshallbe heldby two
persons named by the corporators. The persons so elect-
ed shallhold theirofficesuntilthe firstMonday of July,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-five, and until
theirsuccessorsare qualified.
§ 12. The persons subscribing forstock in thiscompany
shall sign an obligation in substance as follows,to-wit :

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 239
" We ,the undersigned,hereby agree and promise to pay
to 'The CentralKentucky Telephone Company ' fivedol-
larsfor each share of stock set oppositeour names ,re-
spectively,in cash,or by transferof stock in the Lancas-
ter,Danville,and Stanford Telephone Company ,at par
value ,or by transferof stock in the Stanford and Hous-
tonvilleTelephone Company , at par value, as may be
indicatedby writingafteror below our respectivenames ,
on or before the firstday of June, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-four."
§ 13. The said company shallat no time create a debt
exceeding one thousand dollars.
§ 14.Stock shallbe transferableonly on the books of
the company ,the old certificatetaken in and a new one
issued .
§ 15. The privatepropertyof the individualstockhold-
ersshallbe exempt from the liabilitiesof the company .
§ 16. Said company may buy or lease a sufficient
amount of real estate to enable them to carry on the
business of the company .
§ 17. The share-holders may cast their votes, at any
election of officers,by written proxy .
§ 18.This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 21,1884 .

gart, their associates and successors, be , and they are
hereby, made a body-corporate, with the name of the
LouisvilleCar Trust Company ; and as such may sue and
be sued,adopt a corporateseal,enact rulesand by-laws,
and may do and perform allother acts usual or neces-
sary in effectingthe objectsofthe corporation,not incon-
sistent with the laws of this State or of the United States .

240 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§2 . The said company shallhave a capitalstock of
not lessthan twenty -fivethousand dollars,the amount of
which may be fixed from time to time by the directors,
not exceeding two hundred thousand dollars. The cap-
ital stock shall be divided into shares of one hundred
dollars each ,and subscriptions thereforshallbe payable
as agreed upon ,or as may be calledby the directors. In
all meetings of stockholders each share shall be entitled
to one vote, which may be cast by proxy .

authorized to open books for subscription to the capital
stock; and whenever as much as twenty -five thousand
dollarsshallhave been subscribed, they shallcalla meet-
ing of the subscribers for the election of officers, and

where ,and may make and enter into such contracts in
relationthereto as any individual might , and may assign,
set over,pledge , or mortgage any such rolling stock, or
any obligations received by itfrom any sale or lease of
such rolling stock, and may make , issue, and sell its
bonds or certificates, secured by such rolling stock or
obligationsreceived therefor.
§ 6. The saidcompany may execute and sellitsnego-
tiablebonds,payable at such time and place as may be
determined , which may bear interestat a rate not to ex-
-ceed six per centum per annum ,payable semi-annually,
and may secure the payment thereofby pledge or mort-
gage of any property it may own , real, personal, or
mixed .

§7 . This act shalltake effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 21 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 933 .

Paris .

of Kentucky :

Mrs. Carrie Hanson ,Mrs. Emily Parker ,and Mrs. Mary
Turney ,Mrs. Lucas , and Mrs. SalliePullen,their associ-
ates and successors,be , and they are hereby ,created a
body -politicand corporate, under the name and styleof

purposes of the organization; may have and use a com-
mon seal, and the same to alter at pleasure; and adopt
such constitution, by -laws , and rules of regulation that
are not contraryto the laws of thisStateas may be nec-
essary to carry out the purposes of this organization;
and the same shall not be changed until one months '
notice, in writing, shallbe given at a regular meeting of
the association .
§2 . The objectof thisorganizationis to provide for
the comfort and proper care of poor , worthy , indigent
women and children,under such limitationsand restric-
tions as the by -laws may fix. The said home shall be
located in the city of Paris, or within one half mile of

convey the same as a natural person . Said home shall

LOC . L., VOL . II- 16

months aftertheir organization,or untiltheir successors

laws .

efitof said home all gifts, bequests,and conveyances of
propertyof every kind,and apply the same ,or the pro-
ceeds thereof, to the use of said home ,in such manner as
may be determined by the board of directors. The board

dent and vice president; shallalso elect a secretary and
treasurerfrom theirown number , or may elect from out-
side. They shallhave power to appoint all agents,as-
sistants,and servants that may be necessary to conduct
the affairsof said home properly,and may have power
to discharge them , or any of them , as they may deem
necessary .

power to make such appropriationsout of the county
fund for the support and maintenance of the home as the
majorityofthemagistratesofthe county may determine.

after its passage .
Approved April 21 , 1884.

CHAPTER 936 .
AN ACT to amend and reduce into one the several acts in relation to the
town of Shelby City, in Boyle county.
ARTICLE I.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the corporatelimitsof the town of Shelby
Citybe,and are,establishedasfollows: Intheform of a

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 247
square,whose sidesshallrun north,south,eastand west,
and measure a half mile,and whose center shall be the
center of the Danville and HustonvilleTurnpike Road ,
and the Knoxville Branch of the Louisville and Nashville
Railroad , at theircrossingin said town .
ARTICLE II.
§ 1. That upon the firstSaturday in May ,annually,an
election shall be held at some convenient point in said
town for a board of five trustees of said town , at which
election the male inhabitants of the town of the age of
twenty -one years and over,who shallhave been bonafide
residentsof the town one year next preceding the elec-
tion,and allthe male bona fideresidentsof the county of
Boyle of the age of twenty -one years and over, who shall
be the owners of real propertylying within the town ,
upon which there has been an assessment of taxes equal
to the poll-tax imposed upon the male residents of the
town ,shallhave the right to vote.
§2 . Said electionshallbe advertisedby noticesposted
at two or more public places in said town at least one
week beforethe election; the trusteesshallappointtwo
discreetpersons to act as judges of the election,and also a
clerk and sheriff, citizensof the town ,who shalltake an
oath before some person qualifiedto administer oaths, to
faithfullyperform theirduties; and itshall be the duty of
the judges to compare the poll-books ,and return them to
the clerk of the board of trustees,and make a statement
in writing, signed by them , of the number of votes re-
ceived by each candidate ,and certifythe names of those
elected .
§3 . No person shall be eligibleto the officeof trustee
who has not resided within the town one year next pre-
ceding his election,and who is not a freeholdertherein,or
who is directly or indirectlyinterestedin any contract
with the town .
§4 . That the trustees' term of office shall continue for
the next twelve months after their election , or until their
successorsare duly electedand qualified,and shalltake

248 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
an oath before some officerempowered to administer
oaths that they will faithfully,and without affection to
any one, discharge.the dutiesthat may devolve upon
them ; three of whom shallconstitutea quorum to trans-
act business .
§ 5. In said board of trustees and their successors in
officeshallbe vested the fiscaland prudentialaffairsof
the town ; and they shall be a body -politicand corporate;
and by the name and styleof the " Board of Trustees of
the Town of Shelby City " shallbe known ,and shallhave
perpetual succession; and may sue and be sued in all
courts of law or equity; may contract and be contracted
with, and do and perform all other corporate acts under
said name .
ARTICLE III.
§1 . The board of trusteesshallhave power to make
by -laws and ordinances for carrying into effect all the
powers herein granted for thegovernment of the town ,
and to do allsuch things as properly belong to the police
of an incorporated town ; to regulate the time and places
of theirmeetings , and determine the rules of their pro-
ceedings,and to enact laws to compel the attendance of
members at any meeting called by the chairman or two
members of the board; to appoint a chairman, clerk,
treasurer, assessor, and attorney of the town ,should they
deem it necessary to appoint the last named officer; to
employ a competent policeforce,and fixtheirfees and
emoluments ; to fillall vacancies of members of their
own body , or of officersappointed by them ; to make
rulesand regulationsfor the cleanliness,good order, de-
cency , and decorum of the town ,forthe preservation of
the health,peace,lives,and propertyof the inhabitants
of the town ; to clear the streets, alleys, and sidewalks of
all obstructions; to erect and sink cisterns, wells, and
pumps ; to cause to be removed and abated all nuisance ,
and regulatethe storageof any combustibleor unwhole-
some material; to cause any chimney , flue,stove-pipe ,or
fire-place to be changed and repaired,so as to remove
any cause of danger that may exist; to suppressalltip-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 249
:
pling-houses,houses of ill-fame ,disorderlyhouses,retail-
ingof spirituous,vinous,and maltliquorswithoutlicense,
and gaming of everydescription,whereby money or other
valuablesare won or lost; to impose penaltiesfor any
violation of the ordinances of the town , or of misde-
meanors committed and occurring within itslimits,or for
the neglect or refusal of any person to conform to any
regulations or orders of the board of trustees, or of the
officersof the town ,within the scope of theirauthority,
powers , and duties granted by thischarter and the laws

nances of the town of Shelby City, and are finedtherefor
by policecourtof same ,and failto pay saidfines, in the
Boyle county work house at Danville.
§2. That the board of trusteesshallhave power and
authorityannuallyto assess,levy,and collecta tax on
allrealand personalestatewithinthelimitsof the town
subjectto taxationforStaterevenue,not to exceed forty
centson one hundred dollars' worth of property; also to
levy and collecta poll-tax,not exceeding one dollar and
fiftycents,on allmale persons in the town over the age
of twenty -one years ; to tax and license all shows and
exhibitionsof allkinds in any sum , not exceeding twen-
ty-five dollars for such exhibition on any one day ; to
license and regulate the sale of spirituous,vinous , and
