one hundred and fiftydollarsper annum ,or to withhold
such license,at theirdiscretion; but no such license,when
granted ,shalltake effectuntil the dealer shallpay to the
clerk of the Boyle county court the tax imposed, and
execute the bond required by the laws of the State; to
suspend indefinitely,or for a limitedtime, any license
they may grant ,whenever they may be satisfiedthat said
tavern-keeper or coffee-house keeper, or other dealer,

250 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
has permitted unlawful gaming , disorderlyor indecent
conduct to be practiced or committed in his house , or has
permitted any person to drink to intoxication therein; to
suppressand prohibitthe settingup and use for hire of
billiard,Jenny Lind,or pigeon holetables,or any similar
contrivance within said town .
§ 3. For any tax or back tax levied under thisact there
shall exist a lien in favor of the trustees upon all the
property of the tax-payer until the taxis paid , which
shall not be defeated by gift,devise, sale, alienation, or
any means whatever ; and in case the owner of any tax-
able propertyshallfailto pay his taxes beforethe first
day of October in each year , it shall be the duty of the
tax collectorto sell, for cash in hand , at some public
place in said town , afterten days ' notice,by printed or
written advertisements, posted in three or more public
places in said town ,so much of the personal,mixed ,or
realpropertyof the person so in defaultas willpay off
and discharge the taxes of such defaulterdue the trus-
teesand expensesof sale,which sale shallvestthe pur-
chaser with the titleand right to hold the same ; and
personal and mixed property so sold shall be delivered
at once to the purchaser : Provided, however, That the
owner of the real estate so sold may , within one year
from the date of the sale, redeem such real estate by
paying to the treasurer of the town ,or clerk of the board
of trustees,for said purchaser, the purchase money and
twenty per cent,thereon ; but if he or she failsto re-
deem the real estate, as hereinbefore set forth, within
one year , the chairman of the board of trustees shall
convey,by deed to the purchaser,or hisheirsor assignee,
theestateso purchased,under hisofficialtitle; and said
deed shallvest the full fee-simple titlein the purchaser ,
who ,to obtain possession,shallbe entitledto the same
remedies as are prescribed by law in case of a purchase
under a sheriff'sdeed . The collectingofficer, when he
sellsrealestatefortaxesunder the provisionsof thisact,
must and shallexecute to the purchaser a certificateof
purchase ,stating the amount for which itwas sold, the
1

LAWS OF , KENTUCKY . 5
date upon which itwas sold, and as accurate description
of the propertysold as he can furnish,which certificate
of purchase the purchasershallfilewith the clerkof the
board of trustees of the town for record, and the clerk
of said board shallimmediately record said certificate
of purchase in a book to be kept forthat purpose; and
should the purchase money ,with the twenty per cent.
thereon , be paid to said clerk or treasurer within the
twelve months ,he shallmake a note of it on the margin
of said book opposite where said certificateof purchase
is recorded . The board of trustees shall make all need-
ful rulesand regulationsto carry into effectthe fore-
going provisions.
§ 4. Persons upon whom a fine has been imposed by
the policecourt of saidtown may be confined in jail,
or a lock-up,or in the work -house of Boyle county,if an
arrangement of that kind is made by the board of trus-
teesof saidtown ; or they may be worked on the streets
with ball and chain ,until the fine and costs shall be
paid ,unless discharged by law , or paid by work at the
rateprescribedby ordinance. All finesand forfeitures
accruing to the town shallbe ,when collected,paid over to
the treasurer of the town .
§ 5. The board of trustees of said town may , from
time to time , direct and require the owners of lots or
parts of lots,in said town , when the same fronts on a
streetor alley, to cause the same to be curbed and paved
with sidewalksin frontof theirrespectivelots,of such
width ,and of such materials, and in such manner ,and at
such elevations,as the board may direct; and also to re-
pair the same in such mode and manner as they may
deem expedient. If the owner or owners of said lots
shallfailor refuseto have the same repaired,curbed,
paved, graded, macadamized, or furnishedwith side-
walks ,within the time and manner prescribed by said
trustees, it shall be lawful for said board of trustees
to have the same done at their own cost and charges ;
and one -half of the costs and charges thus incurred and
expended by said trustees,shall be taken and be a tax

252 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
upon said lots, and the owners thereof respectively, in
proportion to the amount which the costs of the whole
improvements under said order leaves to the length of said
lotsrespectively. Notice of such order or directionfor
repairing,grading , macadamizing , or furnishing it with
sidewalks,shallbe given by writtenor printedbillsposted
up in fourpublicplacesin said town ,one of which shall
be in the neighborhood of the streetto be repaired for at
leastthirtydays before said work is to be begun ; and
said notice must also statethe time at which said repairs
must be completed , which must be a reasonable time in
which to do the work ordered by the board to be done .
In allcases of making sidewalks and pavements ,streets,
under thisprovision,the town must pay one-halfthe ex-
pense of same .
§6. That whenever the publicconveniencemay ,in the
opinion of the board of trustees,requireany street,alley,
lane,or highway to be opened ,laidout,extended ,changed ,
straightened or altered,they may obtain,by purchase from
the owners thereof, such real estateas may be necessary
therefor; that in all cases where they cannot obtain by
purchase such real estate,they shallhave power to con-
demn the same by causing a writ of ad quod damnum to
be issued by a court of competent jurisdictiondirected to
the marshal of saidShelby City,on a petitionfiledby said
trustees,making all parties owning any interest in the
lands sought to be condemned partiesdefendant to said
petition .
ARTICLE IV .
§1 . It shall be the duty of said board of trustees to
keep a journal of theirproceedings , and at the request of
any member of said board the ayes and nays on any
question shall be called and recorded . No member of
said board shallvote on any question in which he has a
private interest. It shall be the duty of said board to
keep an account of all moneys levied,collected, and dis-
bursed ,and state on theirjournal for what each sum was
disbursed; and shallat alltimes keep theirjournal open
to the inspectionof the citizensof the town . To cause

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 253
allordersand proceedingsof the board to be entered on
thejournal,and to be signed by the chairman or chair-
man protempore. To requireallofficersof thetown,that
bonds are requiredof by law ,to execute bonds,in ade-
quate penalties,with sufficientsecuritiesfor the faithful
discharge of their respective duties. Said bonds shall
be made payable to the board of trusteesof the town of
Shelby City,and may containsuch stipulationsand con-
ditions as the board may require and receive, and when
executed,shalloperateas a lienupon alltherealand per-
sonal estate of such officersand theirsureties, until their
conditionsare respectivelycomplied with ; and fora breach
of such conditions, and the unlawful act of officers, the
trustees, or any person aggrieved, may sue and recover
againsttheofficersand theirsureties,and have appropri-
ate judgment and execution.
ARTICLE V.
§1 . The board of trusteesshallappoint a clerk,whose
duty itshallbe to preservethe books,papers,and records
belonging to the office. He shallkeep a regular journal
of the proceedings of the board , and record all its acts
and resolutions, and draft all agreements between the
board and other persons . He shallfurnish attestedcopies
of all acts and orders passed by the board when required
to do so by persons entitledto demand the same . He
shall draw his order on the treasurer for all sums due
persons on claims allowed by the board. Upon the re-
turn of the assessor'sliststo the board , he shall hand
over one of said lists,with the additions and corrections
made by the board , to the collector, together with the
order fixingthe rate of assessment , and also a listof the
persons and property by whom and upon which taxes
are due and unpaid , and the amount of each claim re-
spectively. He shallperform alldutiesproperlyapper-
tainingto his office, and he may be removed at the
pleasure of the board .
§2 . The board of trusteesshallappointa treasurerof
the town ,who shalltake an oath to faithfullyperform

254 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thedutiesof hisoffice,and executea bond ,in a penalty
not less than one thousand dollars . The treasurer shall
receiveand receiptfor allmoneys paid to the board; he
shallpay no money without a copy of an order of the
board making the appropriation,signedby theclerk; and
he shall file all such vouchers with his settlement with
the board . His books shallat alltimes be open to the
inspectionof the officersand citizensof the town . The
treasurer is authorized to receive and receiptfor alltaxes
thatmay be paid to him before the firstof October,an
nually. He shall,on or beforethe tenthday of October,
annually ,report to the clerk of the board a true listof
all the taxes and moneys received by him . He shall,
whenever required,report to the board the condition of
the treasury, and shallat alltimes be ready for a settle-
ment of his accounts ,
§3 . The board of trustees shall annually appoint an
assessor ; and no one who is not qualified to vote for
trusteesshallbe eligibleto said office; he shalltake an
oath of officein substance the same as county assessors.
It shallbe the duty of the assessorto callupon alltaxa-
blepersonsinthe town ,and make out a truelistof their
taxableproperty,with the value thereof,which listshall
be made on the oath of the party, to be administered by
the assessor . Said lists shall be so taken as to include all
real estate, and every species of personal property,ex-
clusive of household furniture,and allmale persons over
twenty-one years of age. If any person shalltailto give
a listof his or her property,the assessorshallmake a list
from the bestinformationhe can procure. Two faircop-
ies of said lists,arranged in alphabetical order,shallbe
completed and returned to said board on or before the
firstday of June , annually . Upon the return of the as-
sessor'slist,the trustees shallgive notice that any person
or persons who may feelthemselves aggrieved by an ex-
cess of valuationmay appear beforethem ,at a stated
meeting, to be held for that purpose, on or before the
firstof July, annually; and, upon satisfactoryevidence
being made to them , they may change such valuation.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 255
The assessorshallreceive for his servicessuch compensa-
tion as the board shall determine after the lists are re-
turned .
ARTICLE VI .
§1 . The police court of Shelby City shall remain ; it
shall be a court of record,composed of one judge ,and
shallhave a marshal,and may have a prosecutingattor
ney . No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof police
judge unless he be over twenty-one years of age, and
shall have been a residentof the town one year next pre-
ceding his election. The qualificationof a marshal shall
be the same as those of a sheriff.
§2 . The police judge and marshal shall be elected by
the qualified voters of the town , at the time and in the
manner prescribed by law for holding electionsforcounty
judges and sheriffs,and forthe periodsprescribedby the
forty-firstsection,articlefour,of the Constitutionof the
State; and for holding the same ,the board of trustees of
the town of Shelby City shallmake allnecessary orders,
and appoint the necessary officersto conduct the same .
§3 . Said court shall have jurisdictionof all causes ,
civil, criminal,and penal,in which justicesof the peace
have jurisdiction; and that in all criminal and penal
cases he shallhave thejurisdictionof two justicesof the
peace ,and of allmisdemeanors occurringand committed
within the town .
§4 . Said courtshallalsohave the power to cause the
arrest of all offenders for breaches of ordinance , or the
penal and criminallaws of the State,and commit or dis-
charge, or admit to bail,if bailable, and take recogni-
zances, with surety, for appearance before the proper
tribunal; and to impose such fines and penalties as may
be prescribedby law forbreaches of the saidordinance.
Said judge shalltake an oath of office, in substance the
same as county judges . Said court shallhave power to
impose a fine to the amount of one hundred dollars.
§ 5. Said courtshallbe governed in itsrulesof proced-
ure by the Code of Practice,and appeals from itsjudg-
ments may be taken as thereinprescribed.

256 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§6 . Persons arrestedunder capiaspro fine, or in cus-
tody,upon whom a finehas been imposed,shallbe com-
mitted to jail,the lock-up , or work -house , untilthe same
shallbe paid,unlessdischargedby law ,or paid by work
at the rateprescribedby ordinance,in or outof the work-
house .
§7 . A warrant to tryany person forany allegedoffense
withinthe jurisdictionof the policecourt,may be issued
by any magistrate in Boyle county , and shallbe returna-
ble before the police judge ,if for a violation of an ordi-
nance of the town ; and if, from any cause, the police
judge cannot try the case,or failsto attend or hold court,
the case may be tried before the officerissuing the war-
rant, or any other magistrate in the county , who may
issueallappropriatemesne and finalprocessin cases
cognizable by said court, or which have been adjudi-
cated .
§ 8. Said court, whenever it is necessary,may detain
offenders in custody over night,or if such persons are
drunk , until they are sober, in the county jail, or other
safe place that may be prepared by order of the board of
trusteesof the town as a prison.
§ 9. That a returnof not found on a capiasprofine,or
of no propertyfound on an executionoffierifaciasissued
from thepolicecourt,shallauthorize,in any courtofcom-
petentciviljurisdiction,the same procedure forthesatis-
factionof the judgment that may be had in civilcases
aftera returnof no propertyfound: Provided, Defendant
shallhave the right, at any time, to replevy, for three
months , any judgment , fine, or forfeiture, in the same
manner as allowed by law in similar cases in favor of
the Commonwealth .
§ 10. The process of said court shall be directed to
and executed by the marshal, unless, forspecialreasons
the judge cause it to be directed to another, which may
be to any sheriff, constable, policeman , or marshal . It
shallrun in the name ofthe Commonwealth ofKentucky ,
and may go intoany county of the Commonwealth ,and
saidmarshal or otherofficermay executeitanywhere in
the State, or make , by indorsement on the process, a

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 257
specialbailiffto executeit,and the bailiffshallhave the
right to execute it,and the fees therefor the same that
the marshal has by law . The policejudge shallbe en-
titled to the same fees that are allowed justices of the
peace for similarservices.
§11 . The marshal of the town ,before he enters upon
the duties of his office, shall take the oaths of office, in
substance the same as those prescribedby law for sher-
iffs, and execute bond ,with sufficientsecurity, in a sum
not less than one thousand dollars, conditioned as may
be required by the board of trustees. It shallbe his duty
to execute and make due return of all notices and all
processes legallydirected to him , and receive and collect
finesand forfeituresdue and payable to the town ,and
account for and pay over the same at the time and in
the manner requiredby law; and may command and
take with him , if need be, the power of the town to
aid him in the execution of his office . He shall attend
the meetings of the board , and serve and execute all
orders and allnoticesissued or made by them . He shall
have allthe powers ,and shallbe liableto allthe respon-
sibilities,of sheriffsin the execution of his office.
§ 12. The marshal shallbe ex officiocollectorof all
taxes due the town; but the board of trusteesmay ap-
point another collectorof taxesforthesame reasonsthat
the county court may appoint a collectorof revenue in
the stead of the sheriff .
§ 13. A vacancy in the officeof marshal shallbe tem-
porarilyfilledby the board of trusteesuntilthe next suc-
ceeding August election, and until the successor then
elected shall qualify. The marshal shallbe allowed the
same fees and commissions that are allowed to consta-
bles for similar services ; and for all other services , shall
receivesuch salaryor feesas the board of trusteesmay
fix .
§14 . The board of trusteesof the town may appoint
and contractwith an attorneyat law to prosecuteany
case that may come up in the policecourt,and pay him
LOC . L., VOL .H - 17

258 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
whatever sum may be agreed upon for such services, or
a certain per cent. of the fine, should one be assessed
and collected on a trialof the case,or they may employ
him by the year at a stated salary,or a certain per cent.
of the finescollected. They may also employ an attor-
ney to attend to any other legal business in which the
town may have an interest,and allow him a feetherefor,
and draw an order on the treasurerfor payment of same .
ARTICLE VII .
§1 . That the present trustees, and allother officersof
the town , shall continue in officeuntil their successors
are elected and qualifiedunder this act,and are hereby
invested with allthe powers ,rights, and privilegeswhich
the trustees and officers to be elected under this act will
possess .
§2 . That this act shall not invalidate any legal act
done by the board of trustees of Shelby City, or by its
officers, prior to the passage of this act; and all ordi-
nances and regulations now in force in said town , not
inconsistent with this act, shall remain in full force until
altered, modified, or repealed by the board of trustees
after this act shall take effect .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 21, 1884.
CHAPTER 937 .
AN ACT creatinga new voting precinctin the county of Larue .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there be ,and ishereby ,created a new voting
precinct in the county of Larue ,composed of the voting
population living within the following described terri-
tory in said county ,viz : Beginning at the mouth of Sul-
phur Lick,on RollingFork of Saltriver; running thence
with the line of said Lick ,on the north side thereof, to
the head of same ; thence in an eastwardly course to the
boundary lineof Taylor and Larue counties; thenceeast

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 259
wardly with said boundary line to the old Washington
county corner,within seventy -five or one hundred yards
of the old crooked sour woods ; thence down Salt Lick
to Rolling Fork ; thence with the course of same to the
beginning .
§2 . The voting place in said precincthereby created
shall be located at or near the residence of Jefferson
Price, until otherwise decided upon at a regular election
by a majority of the qualifiedvoters living in said pre-
cinct .
§3 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage; and allacts or parts of acts in conflictwith this
act are hereby repealed.
Approved April19, 1884.
CHAPTER 938 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Highland Land and Lumber Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That John C. Gaddis,James M. Southgate, H. H.
Southgate , R. S. Robson , and their associates, be , and
they are hereby, created a body-corporate, under the
name and style of " The Highland Land and Lumber
Company ," of Campbell county ; and under said name
said corporation may contract and be contracted with ,
sue and be sued; and may have a common seal, which
they may alterat pleasure.
§2 . The principalbusinessof the company shallbe the
manufacture and sale of lumber ; and for that purpose it
may acquire and purchase timber and timber lands, in
thisState or elsewhere,and may erectand operate all
necessarymills,machinery,and buildingsfor the proper
prosecution of said business.
§3 . Said corporationmay mine for and sellany min-
eralswhich they may find on any of the lands owned by
them . They may also use any of said lands for stock-
raisingor for agriculturalpurposes.

260 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. The capital stock of the company may be in any
sum which may be fixedby the board of directors,not to
exceed the sum of one hundred thousand dollars; but
within that limit,the board may at any time increase or
decrease the capital stock of the company ; not,however ,
to interferein any way with the creditorsof the com-
pany .
§5 . The stock shall be divided into shares of one hun-
dred dollars each ,transferable only on the books of the
company as may by by -law be prescribed.
§ 6. No stock of the company may be sold or trans-
ferred without the owner thereof firstgiving to the then
members of the company an opportunityto purchase the
same .
§7 . The officersshall be a president, secretary, and
treasurer, and a board of five directors, who shall be
electedannually,upon the firstMonday in April,for a
term of one year ,and until their successors are elected
and qualified. Each share of stock may cast one vote.
At meetings of the board of directorsno officermay vote
unless he be also a member of the board ,except the pre-
siding officerin case of a tie.
§ 8. The private property of members of the corpora-
tion shallbe exempt from allliabilityfor corporate debts.
§ 9. The corporation may appoint and employ allagents
and servants necessary for said business,and may make
allnecessary by -laws ,not in conflictwith the laws of this
State or the United States .
§ 10. This act shallbe in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 19, 1834.
CHAPTER 939 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Harrison county court to levy an ad valorem tax
to build a bridge acrossSouth Licking river.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Harrison county ,a ma-
jorityof the justicesof the peace of said county being

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 261
present and concurring ,is hereby authorized to contract
forthe building,and to have built, a bridge across South
Licking River ,at or near Robertson Station, on the Ken-
tucky Central Railroad ,as said court may determine ;and
to levy an ad valorem tax upon each one hundred dollars'
worth of taxable propertyin said county to pay forthe
same .
§2 . That the tax provided for in this act shallbe as-
sessed and collected as other taxes are collected.
§3 . This act shall take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 19 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 940 .
AN ACT to incorporateMiss Tipton's Select School for Ladies, in Paris.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Maria Tipton and S. E. Tipton,and their
successors and assigns,shall be a body politicand cor-
porate,by the name and styleof " Miss Tipton'sSelect
School for Ladies,in Paris,Kentucky ," and as such shall
have perpetual succession, with the right and privilege
of suing and being sued ,contracting and being con-
tracted with , and of doing any and all other acts of a
legal and natural person .
§2 . The aforesaid incorporators shall have the power
of electing and appointing a president, board of three
directors,treasurer,and secretary,and such other officers
as in theirdiscretionthey may think rightand proper,
and as they may determine by their by -laws , rules and
regulations .
§3 . The purposes of the said school shallbe to organ-
ize and maintain in the city of Paris a seminary for the
proper training and education of young ladies, misses,
and boys ,and to do all other acts necessary and proper
forthe successful and efficientmanagement and control
of said school; they may ,by their board of directorsor
president,employ teachersor professors,make allneces-

262 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
*
sary rules, by -laws , and regulations for the government
of said school,and do allother things necessary to secure
a thorough course of English ,industrial,and classicaledu-
cation in said school .
§4 . The president and board of directors shall have
thepower to confer degrees and grant diplomas,signed
by the said president and board of directorsand faculty
of said school .
§ 5. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved April 19,1884 .
CHAPTER 941 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Roup's Mill and Plum Spring Turnpike
Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 That a company is hereby incorporated,under the
name and styleof the Roup's Mill and Plum Spring
Turnpike Company ; and by that name itshallhave per-
petual succession; may sue and be sued , contract and
be contracted with , and is clothed with all the powers
conferred by the general law on turnpike companies in
this State .
§2 . That the following persons are appointed corpo-
ratorsto receive subscriptionsof stock to said company ,
to-wit : W. H. Bluvett, M. M. Smith , R. D. Stone , W.
E. Phalen , T. T. Roup , and John W. McFarland , and
they are authorizedto open books and receivesubscrin-
tions of stock at any time and place they may deem best.
§3 . That the capitalstock of said company shallbe
divided into shares of twenty -fivedollars each ; and the
entire stock shall not exceed the amount necessary to
properlyconstructa turnpike road from the junctionof
the Salt River Road with the Louisville and Nashville
pike,near E. T. Roberts',by the bestroutealong or near
said Salt River Road to Plum Spring, and to supply a
toll-house and grounds , if necessary,at which tollmay
be collectedat the rate now allowed by law .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 263
§4 . That when a thousand dollars of stock is sub-
scribed ,the said corporators,or such of them as may act,
shall,at such time and place as they deem proper,call
a meeting of the stockholders,and hold an election for
a president and three directors,to hold their officesfor
one year ,and untiltheir successors are elected and qual-
ified . Each stockholder to have one vote for each share
of stock at allelections. They shallhave power.to make
all needful by -laws and rulesthat may be necessaryto
carry out the purposes of thiscorporation,one of which
shall fixthe time and place of holding the annual elec-
tion of president and directors.
§5 . That the president and directorsshallhave power
to let out the building of said road , upon such terms as
they may deem best forthe interestof said company ; and
they may take shares of stock, and allow itto be worked
out in the construction of the road ,upon such terms as
they may fixor prescribe.
§6 . That the president and directorsmay appoint one
of their number treasurer and secretary, and take bond
from him ,with good surety,to faithfullydischarge the
duties of his office. He shall keep a fullrecord of the
proceedings of said board,and an account of the stock
and of the receiptsand the expendituresof said com-
pany ,open to the inspectionof any stockholderin said
company .
§ 7. That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 19 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 942 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Trimble countycourt to levy an ad valorem
and poll-tax,and issuebonds to build a new court-house in saidcounty .
WHEREAS , The Trimble county court has appropriated
the sum of thirteenthousand dollarsfor the purpose of
buildinga new court-house in saidcounty; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Trimble county be , and
itishereby,authorizedto issuethe bonds of saidcounty,

264 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
not to exceed thirteenthousand dollars,forthe purpose
of building a new court-house in said county .
§2 . Said bonds shall bear interest at a rate not exceed-
ing sixper cent,per annum , and shallbe due and paya-
ble in twenty years ,but redeemable at any time in the
pleasureof said county courtaftertwo years from date.
§3 . Said bonds shall not be for a sum less than one
hundred . dollars each ; shall be signed by the county
judge, attestedby the county court clerk, and stamped
with the seal of said court. They shallbe made payable
to bearer,and pass by delivery, and the interestshallbe
payable annually .
§4 . Said bonds shall not be issued and delivered until
said court house is completed according to the specifica-
tionsand contract,and received by a building committee ,
to be appointed by said court .
§5 . For the purpose of paying offsaid bonds,and to
enable the county court to provide for the payment of
the principal and interestof said bonds ,it may levy an
annual ad valorem tax of not exceeding twenty cents on
each one hundred dollars' worth of taxable property in
said county , and an annual poll-tax of one dollar on
each tithable in said county , which shall be collected and
accounted for by the sheriff as all other taxes .
§6 . The sheriffof said county shall,before collecting
said tax,give an additional bond ,with good security, to
be approved by said county court,in substance and form
as is now requiredby law for the collectionof county
taxes ; and said sheriff and his sureties shall be liable on
said bond , and mayay be proceeded against as is now
allowed by law on similar bonds , which bonds shall be
executed and renewed every two years , or oftener if
required by said court .
§7 . Said sheriffshallbe allowed and receive five per

CHAPTER 943 .
AN ACT to authorizethe citycouncilof Stanford to issuebonds to pay off
the outstanding indebtednessof the cityof Stanford.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the citycouncil of Stanford, Kentucky , a
majority of the councilmen being present and concurring ,
may cause the bonds of saidcity,not to exceed in the
aggregate the sum of one thousand dollars($ 1,000),to be
executed in printed form ,signed by the mayor of the city,
and countersignedby the cityclerk,payable when and
where the council may elect, with annual or semi -annual
interestcoupons at a rate not exceeding six per cent .per
annum ; and each coupon thereunto attached shall be
signed by the clerk of the citycouncil.
§2 . The bonds authorized to be issued by thisact shall
be sold at such time and in such manner as the council
may direct, and the proceeds thereof shall be used in
paying off the present indebtedness of the city.
§ 3. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 19 , 1884.

CHAPTER 947 .

ions of said company , to be denominated , respectively,
first division , second division , and third division . The

4
countsand reportsshallbe made of the number of mem-
bersand the profitsand lossesof each division.

ter Mason may become a member of the second and
thirddivisions,itsuch person shallbe recommended for
membership by two Master Masons .

jorityvote of the board of directorsand the payment of

pealed .

§ 10.The capitalof said company shallbe taken and
consideredas the securityrequiredby law for the faith-
fulperformance of itsduties,and other securityshall not
be requiredupon itsappointment to any of the offices
or duties mentioned herein,except when required by the
courtsor by partiesin interest; but any court having
jurisdictionto make or approve such appointments shall
have power ,before making or approving the same ,and
any time thereafter,to examine the officersof the com-
pany ,under oath or affirmation,as to the securityafore-
said , and the amount and extent of its fiducial liabilities;
and ifdeemed advisableby said court or courts to ap
point a suitable person to investigate the affairsand
management of said company ,who shallreportto such
court the manner in which itsinvestments are made ,and
the securityofferedto those by or forwhom itsengage-
ments are held,the expense of such investigationto be
paid by said company .
11. In the exercise by said company of the powers
herein authorized as guardian , executor, administrator,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 281
* committee of lunatics,or of any office or duty imposed
by the courts on or otherwise committed to said company ,
itshall be subjectto the same duties and responsibilities,
shall have the same powers , and shall receive the same
compensation,as may be fixedby law with relationto
individuals holding similar offices or trusts, except as
herein otherwisespeciallyprovided. The exerciseof the
other powers , and the performance of other duties by
said company ,may be,as to compensation and other-
wise,mattersof contractwith thepartiesinterested.
§ 12. Said corporationmay ,from time to time,declare
and pay dividends; but such dividendsshallbe paid only
from the net earnings of the company .
§ 13. This act shalltake effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 22 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 951 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe American Trust Company , of Louisville.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. T. Duncan , P. B. Reed , W. W. Thum ,
James S. Ray ,J.S. Dorey ,John S. Miller,Jas.A. Leech ,
Arthur M. Rutledge,and theirassociates,successors,and
assigns,who shall hereafter become stockholders in the
company hereby incorporated,be,and they are hereby,
createdand made a body-corporateand politic,under the
name of the "American Trust Company ," of Louisville,
and by such name shallhave perpetualsuccession,sue
and be sued ,contract and be contracted with,and have
allthe powers hereinaftergiven ; and may have and use
a common seal,and alterthe same at pleasure.
§2 . The capital stock of said corporation shallbe five
hundred thousand dollars, divided into five thousand
shares of one hundred dollars each , with the privilege
to increase the capitalby a vote of the stockholders
(those holding two -thirdsof allthe capitalstock assent-
ing thereto), at an annual or special meeting ,to any sum
not exceeding one milliondollars($ 1,000,000).

282 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . The corporators,or a majority of them named in
the firstsection of this act, shall have power to open
books for subscriptionsto the capital stock at such times
and places as they may deem expedient; and when not
less than one thousand shares shall have been subscribed
and twenty per centum thereon shallhave been paid in,
the share-holders may electa board of not lessthan nine
directors and begin business,which number may be in-
creased at any time by a vote of the stockholders owning
two -thirdsin value of all the stock subscribed and paid
for, to any number not exceeding fifteen; the directors to
serve as hereinafter provided, and until their successors
are duly elected and qualified. And the directorsof said
company , when it shall have been organized , may , and
they are hereby authorizedand empowered to,have and
exercise,in the name and behalf of the company ,allthe
rightsand privilegeswhich are hereby given,subjectonly
to such liabilitiesas other share-holders are subject to,
which liabilitiesare no more than for the payment to the
company of the sums due ,or to become due ,on the shares
held by them . And should the capitalstock at any time
be increased ,the stockholders at the time of said increase
shallbe entitledto a pro rata share of such increase upon .
thepayment of the pricefixedby the board of directors.
§ 4. The principal place of business of said company
shall be in the city of Louisville. All the directors of
said company shallbe citizensof the State of Kentucky
and reside therein .
§ 5. The board of directorsshalldivideditselfby lotinto
three classes,as nearly equal as may be . The term of
officeof the firstclassshallexpire at the end of one year ,
that of the second classat the end of two years, and that
of the thirdclassat the end of threeyears ; and at the ex-
piration of the firstyear ,and annually thereafter, there
shall be chosen a number of directorsequal to the num-
ber of directorsin the class whose time will then expire,
who shall hold their officefor three years, or until their
successorsare electedand qualified. Vacancies occurring
in the intervals of election shall be filledby the board
of directors. The annual meetings of stockholders for

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 283
the purpose of electingdirectors and attending to other
business properlybrought beforesuch meeting,shallbe
held at the officeof the company in Louisville on the
second Tuesday in January of each year ,under such rules
and regulations as may be provided in the by laws ; and
if for any reason such annual meeting is not held, the
directors then in office shall hold over until the next an-
nual meeting,or a specialmeeting may be calledand held
forsaid purpose,in such manner as may be provided in
the by -laws .
§ 6. The directorsshallelectfrom theirnumber ,at the
firstmeeting of the board after their election, and an-
nually thereafter, a president and vice president, who
shallhold their officesfor the term of one year , and until
their successors are elected and qualified; and may ap-
point a secretaryand treasurer,and such other officers,
clerks, agents ,and employes as the business of the com-
pany may require,fixtheircompensation and prescribe
theirduties All electionsfor directorsshall be by bal-
lot, and every stockholder shall be entitledto one vote
foreach share of stockheld by him; but no person shall
be eligible as director who is not a stockholder to the
amount of twenty shares. At any meeting a quorum of
the stockholders shall consist of stockholders owning at
least one -third in value of all the capital stock, and a
record of the proceedings of all such meetings shall be
kept .
§7 . The board of directors shall make all by -laws
necessary for conducting the business of the corporation,
and shallkeep a record of its proceedings . The direct-
orsshallhave power to requirepayment of the amount
remaining unpaid on the stock of the corporation at such
timesand in such proportionsas they shal thinkproper.
The said assessments to be made as the by -laws of said
corporationshalldirect. The corporationshallhave a
lienon the stock for any amount that may be due there-
on .
§8 . The capital stock. to the extent of one hundred
and fiftythousand dollars($ 150,000),and in the event of

284 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
an increase over five hundred thousand dollars,then the
same proportionof the capitalstock paid in, shallbe
invested in such securities, at such rates of interestas
they promise to pay , as are authorized by law for trus-
tees, and from time to time shall be reinvested in the
same , and shallbe kept separate and apart from the
other investments,and designated upon the books of the
company as the" trustsecurityfund;" and itshallbe the
duty of the proper officersof the company annually,on
or before thefifthday of January,or oftenerif required
by the court,to filein the Louisville chancery court a
detailed statement , under oath or affirmation, of said
investments; and said trustfund shallbe speciallyand
primarily liable for the obligations of said company as
executor , administrator, guardian , receiver, assignee, or
trusteein any othercapacity,and shallnot be liablefor
any otherdebt or obligationof the company untilallits
liabilitiesin regard to its trusts as aforesaid shall have
been firstfullymet and discharged . The residue of the
capital,and other money in the hands of the company ,
may be loaned or invested in such manner as may be de-
termined by the by -laws of the corporation; but nothing
herein shallbe construed to permit said company to dis-
count paper,or to engage in thebusinessof banking.
§9. The capitalof said company shallbe taken and
consideredas the only securityrequiredby law for the
faithfulperformance of itsduties; and other securityshall
not be requiredupon itsappointment to any of the offices
or dutiesmentioned herein,exceptwhen requiredby the
courts or by partiesin interest; but any court having
jurisdictionto make or approve such appointments shall
have power ,before making or approving the same ,and
from time to time afterward ,to examine the officersof
thecompany ,under oath or affirmation,as to thesecurity
aforesaid, and appoint a suitable person to investigate
the affairsand management of said company , who shall
report to such court the manner in which itsinvestments
are made , and the securityofferedto those by or for
whom itsengagements are held; such investigationshall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 285
be made as often as orderedby the court,the expenses
of said investigationtobe paidby the company .
§ 10. The said corporationmay be appointed and may
act as guardian of infants,as executor,or administrator,
or committee of lunatics,as receiver,assignee, or other
trustee,whether appointed by deed,by lastwilland tes-
tament , by any court within this Commonwealth ,or in
any other manner not inconsistentwith law . Itmay re-
ceive and hold in trust,estate,realand personal,includ-
ing the notes,bonds ,obligations, and accounts of estates
and individuals, of companies and corporations,private
or public; and may , in itsfiduciarycapacity, purchase,
collect,adjust,and settle,sell,and disposeof the same in
thisState or elsewhere. Itmay act as agent or attorney
in the leasing,conveying,and managing real and per-
sonal estate, receiving and collecting rents and other
moneys ,in the issuing and countersigning certificatesof
stock, bonds , or other obligations of any corporation,
association,municipality,State,or otherpublicauthority;
and may receiveand manage any sinkingfund therefor,
and may accept and execute all such trusts, of every
description,not inconsistentwith the laws of thisState,
as may be committed to itby any person or persons,or
by any corporation,or by any court of record of thisor
any other State. It shallhave a trustdepartment,and
all its business of a fiduciarycharactershallbe kept
separate and apartfrom the otherbusinessof the com-
pany , in a separate set of books , and a separate ac-
count shall be kept with each estate committed to its
care .
§ 11. In the exercise,by said company ,of the powers
herein authorized,as guardian,executor, administrator,
committee of lunatics,or of any officeor duty imposed
by a court,said company shallbe subjectto the same
dutiesand responsibilities,shallhave the same powers ,
and shallreceivethe same compensation,as may be fixed
by law with relationto individualsholding similar offices
or trusts,except as herein otherwisespeciallyprovided.
The exerciseof the otherpowers,and the performance of
other dutiesby said company may be,as to compensa-

286 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tion and otherwise, matters of contract with the parties
interested .
§ 12. Said corporation may purchase ,erect,or rent a
building having a commodious and strong safety vault ,
containing a number of small compartments or boxes ,
designed and adapted forrentingto personswho desire
to have a place of safedepositforvaluables; and said
corporationis hereby empowered to rent out the boxes
in such safety-vault to such persons and for such sums
of money and for such times as may be agreed between
such corporation and such persons as may jointly or sev-
erally rent one or more of said boxes or compartments .
§ 13. Said corporation is hereby empowered to receive,
forsafe-keeping,any valuablesthat may be intrustedto
it, receiptingfor the same by any of itsofficersdesig-
nated by its by -laws so to do , and may guarantee such
specialdeposits.
§ 14. Said corporation may , until it secures a safety-
vault for the valuables intrusted to it, rent or hire boxes
in the safety-vault of any other company or corporation ,
or rent a room or rooms for its use until it shall erect ,
rent,or purchase a building.
§ 15. The said corporation is authorized to take,hold,
have,and enjoyallsuch estates,real,personal,and mixed,
as may be obtained by the investment of itscapitalstock,
and allothermoneys or fundsthatmay come intoits
possession in the course of itsdealings and business,and
the same to sell,grant, mortgage ,lease,and dispose of at
pleasure, and to execute , acknowledge , and deliver all
deeds and other instruments of writing concerning the
same : Provided,however,That the said real estate so to
be held by itshallbe only that occupied as its officeand
place of business, not to exceed fiftythousand dollars,
and such as may come into itspossession in satisfaction
of any debt due to it upon any mortgage ,decree,or judg-
ment .
§ 16. Said corporationmay declare and pay semi-an-
nual dividends from the net earnings of the company ;
but before declaring said dividends,it shallreserve from

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 287
said net earnings not less than ten per cent , and shall
continue so to do untilthe amount thus reserved equals
twenty per cent. of the paid -up capital stock.
Approved April 22, 1884 .

CHAPTER 953 .
AN ACT to incorporate the IllinoisSouthern and Paducah Railway Com-
pany .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the IllinoisSouthern and Paducah Railway
Company ,a corporationformed and existingunder the
general incorporation law of the State of Illinois,be ,
and it is hereby ,made a corporate body in this Com-
monwealth , with power to sue and be sued ,contract and
be contracted with, and with such additionalpowers as
may be conferredon said company in this act; and for
said objects and purposes the said railwaycompany is
vested with perpetual succession.
§2 . That the IllinoisSouthern and Paducah Railway
Company shallhave the rightto buildand operatea rail-
road in the county of McCracken , and in the city of Pa-
ducah , from the terminus of any railroadbridge or trans-
ferthat may be builtor established across the Ohio river,
in said county , to any station or depot in said city, or
junction with any other railroadcompany with which it
may connect ; and may carry and transferitslocomotives ,
cars,freight, and passengers across said river; it being
the intent and purpose of this act to enable and author-
izethe saidcompany to build,own ,and operatea lineof
railway from some convenient pointin the cityof Padu-
cah to any such bridge or transferlanding ,so as to con-
nect the same with a line of railway in the State of Illi
nois by bridge or river transfer, and may establishand
constructsuch depots, sidings, turn-tables, shops, and
offices, as may be deemed expedient . And with like
powers may construct and operate a lineof railroadfrom
the cityof Paducah to such point on the Tennessee State

288 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
lineas saidcompany may selectbetween the Tennessee
riverand the town of Fulton , on the lines of the States
of Kentucky and Tennessee. And may purchase,lease,
or otherwiseacquire realand personalpropertyforthe
said railroad tracks, transfer landings, depots, sidings,
shops,offices, and other purposes of the said company .
And forthese purposes may condemn land,stone,earth,
gravel,and such other things as may be necessary for
the construction,repair,and operationof said railroad,
in the mode and manner provided in an act approved
April eleventh,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
two ,entitled" An act to prescribe the mode of condemn-
ing lands for the use of railroadand turnpike companies ,"
and such amendments to said act as may be passed by
the Legislature of Kentucky : Provided, That one director
shallbe a residentof the Stateof Kentucky .
§3 . The said IllinoisSouthern and Paducah Railway
Company may issue and sellits negotiable bonds ,bear-
ing a rate of interestnot exceeding six per centum per
annum ,payable at such times and place as it may de-
termine ,and may secure same ,or other obligations,by
mortgage upon itsproperty,rights,and franchisesin this
Commonwealth ,eitherseparatelyor in connection with
its property,rights and franchises out of this Common-
wealth .
§ 4. That the city of Paducah is vested with power to
subscribeto the capitalstock of said railway company
such sum as may be agreed on between the said city and
the railroadcompany ,not to exceed fiftythousand dol-
lars,and pay for the same in negotiable bonds of said
city,at par ; said bonds to be made payable to bearer at
the officeof the treasurerof saidcity; to bear any rateof
interest,not to exceed five per centum per annum ; the
bonds to be of the denomination of one thousand dollars
each ,and payable at such time as may be agreed on , to
bear interestfrom date,payable semi -annually; the bonds
to be signed by the mayor ,and the coupons to be signed
by the clerk of said city of Paducah . Before any such
subscriptionshallbe binding on saidcitythe citycoun-
cilshallsubmit to a vote of the legal voters of said city

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 289
|
an ordinance fixing the amount to be subscribed ; and it
shallbe voted for by a majority of the voters voting at a
general or specialelection; and the mayor ,clerk,and city
attorneyshallconstitutea board to compare the votes and
certifythe result.
§5 . The saidcompany shallhave the righttobuildand
operate itsrailroad over and along such public ways in
the cityof Paducah as may be permittedby the council
of said city,and upon such regulationsas said council
may ,by ordinance ,require.
§6 . The IllinoisSouthern and Paducah Railway Com-
pany ishereby authorizedand empowered to subscribe
stockinthe Paducah and IllinoisBridge Company .
§7 . The Legislature reserves the rightto regulate the
charges for passage and freight on said railroad, or by
thecompany over itsroad.
§ 8. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 22, 1884.
CHAPTER 954 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Covington Mutual Life InsuranceAssociation.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Frank P. Helm ,Jas. B. Casey,Henry Mers-
man ,Geo . G. Perkins, and E. J. Hickey ,their associates
and successors,be,and they are hereby,createda body-
politicand corporate, with perpetualsuccession, under
the name and styleof the Covington Mutual LifeInsur-
ance Association, the objectof said company being to
provide financialaid to the widows ,orphans,heirs,lega-
tees,and assigns of deceased members . The said com-
pany shallhave power to sue and be sued,contractand
be contracted with ,and to hold such real estate and other
propertydeemed necessaryfor itsuses and purposes by
itsboard of directors, not to exceed in value one million
of dollars, and with power to selland convey and other-
wise dispose of the same ; also to make and use a com-
LOC . L.,VOL . II- 19

290 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
mon seal,and to break and alterthe same , and to make
such by -laws and regulations as may be deemed neces-
sary and appropriate for carrying out the purposes of this
act .
§2 . The principal officeof said company shall be in
the cityof Covington,in thisState; and upon the callof
one or more of the incorporatorshereinbefore named ,the
saidincorporators,or a majorityof them ,may meet and
organize the said company by electinga board of five
directors,who shallhave the management and control of
said company and its affairs, and shall, of their own
number , elect, as principalofficersof said company , a
president, vice president, secretary, and treasurer, the
terms and emoluments of the same to be fixed by the
by-laws of the company . The company may appoint
such other officersand agents as itmay deem proper and
necessary for the transaction of itsbusiness.
§3 . The board of directorsshalldecide upon allappli-
cationsfor membership , and upon allclaims for death
benefits in said corporation. The said company shall
have power ,and are hereby authorized,to issue certifi-
cates of membership in sums of from one dollar to ten
thousand dollars, entitlingthe beneficiarynamed therein
to receive such sum , upon the death of a member , as
may be named in the certificate,the said amount to be
collectedby levying an assessment upon the entiremem-
bership in good standing at the time of the death of said
member as hereinafterprovided; and said benefitshallbe
paid from the amount so collected,and no otherfund or
property of the company shallbe liablefor the same ; but
in no event shallthe sum paid said beneficiaryexceed
the amount collectedby one assessment upon allmem-
bers in good standing at the date of the death of such
member .
§4 . There shall be three separate and distinctfunds
collected from the members of the said company ,to be
known as the " mortuary or assessment fund ," the “ secu-
rityfund ," and the " expense fund ." The mortuary fund
shallbe createdby an assessment collectedfrom each

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 201
member ,according to a graded rate of assessments,to be
fixedby the by -laws of the company ,and in the mode and
manner therein provided; and should there, at any time ,
be a deficit, the company shallhave the power to make
a deficiency assessment pro rata on such members as are
liablethereforby reason of the insufficiencyof previous
assessments against them . The said fund shallbe held
sacredfor,and shallonlybe applicableto,the payment
of benefits accruing from death among the members of
the said association,for the taxes assessed against said
fund ,and forthe costs and expenses of suitsin protect-
ing saidcompany againstsuspectedfraudulentclaims to
such benefits; and the said mortuary fund shall,as col-
lected,be depositedin such nationalor charteredState
banks as may ,from time to time ,be selectedby the said
board of directors as the depository of said fund , and
the same shallonly be withdrawn,in whole or in part,
upon checks signed by two of the principalofficersof
the said company . Should a surplusmortuary fund ac-
crue or be created,the same shallbe invested either in
bonds of the United States or the State of Kentucky , or
the city of Covington , or such other securitiesas insur-
ance companies are allowed by law to invest in; and said
bonds are to be held by said depository untilrequired for
the payment of claimsagainstthe said company ,when
the said company shallhave power to withdraw and sell
or disposeof saidbonds forsuch purpose.
§5 . The securityfund shall be created by an assess-
ment upon the members at the time ,in the manner ,and
in the amount fixedby the by -laws,and shallbe placed
in saiddepositoryorin thehands oftrusteesappointedby
the board of directors(who shallbe removable at the will
of said directors), and said depository or trustees shall
invest said fund in such securities as are hereinbefore
named , and as shall be agreed to by said directors, and
shallhold the same for the protection and benefitof the
persistingmembers in the manner provided by saidby-
law; and saidfund shallbe subjectto no otheruse except
that of the necessaryexpense of managing and protect-
ing the same .

292 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§6 . The expense fund of said company ,in which the
members shallhave no interest, shallaccrue from and con-
sistof the membership fees and annual dues collectable
from the members in such sums , at such times , and in
such manner as shallbe prescribedby the by -laws of said
company . The amount of said annual dues shallbe
stated in the application for membership ,and in the cer-
tificate, and shall not be increased thereafter . All the ex-
penses and claims upon or against the company ,except
the death benefits,taxes, and costs,and expenses specified
in sectionsfour and fiveof this act,shall be paid out of
said expense fund .
§7 . Any person may , in his applicationfor member-
ship,designatethe beneficiaryof the benefittoaccrue on
hisdeath,provided such beneficiaryshallhave an insur-
able interestin the lifeof the insured; and ifthe benefici-
ary so designated be other than himself,the benefit shall
not be liablefor any debt of such member .
§ 8. Every member who shallfailto pay his mortuary
assessment or annual dues ,or to make his deposit to the
securityfund, or any of them ,as prescribedby the by-
laws,shallforfeitmembership in the said company ,and
in such case certificateof membership shall become null
and void. Allmoneys paid on account of such member-
ship shall be forfeitedto said company ,and no benefit
shallaccrue or be payable to any person upon the death
of such member .
§9 . Benefits accruing on death among members shall
be payable at the officeof the company in Covington ,
Kentucky , within ninety days after the receipt of satis-
factoryproof of such death ; and should payment of any
such benefitbe refused,an action or suitfor the recovery
thereof can only be commenced within twelve months
next after such refusal .
§ 10. On or before the firstMonday in January of each
year,itshallbe the duty of the principalofficersof said
company to make a statement of the condition of its
mortuary and securityfunds, showing the receiptsand
disbursements of the same , and the balance thereof re

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 293
maining on hand for the fiscalyear ending the thirty-
firstday of December next preceding,and to submit such
statement to the Insurance Commissioner of this State .
It shall be the duty of said Commissioner to verifythe
same , and he shall be entitled therefor to a reasonable
fee,not exceeding one hundred dollars per annum , to be
paid by the company . A printed copy of such statement
shallbe mailed to each member of the company . If,at
any time ,the Insurance Commissioner shall have cause
to believe that the company is in any way violating its
charter,he may make or cause to be made an examina-
tion of said company , and if he shall find that there is
such a violationas,in his judgment , shalljustifyhim in
so doing ,he is hereby authorized to take such steps to
annul thischarter as are now allowed by the law estab-
lishing the Insurance Bureau to be taken against other
insurance companies .
§ 11. The said company ,being of a purelybenevolent
character, itshallnot be subject to the laws of this State
governing life insurance companies , except as herein
provided .
§ 12.The secretaryof saidcompany shallhave charge
of the records, books , and collectionsthereof; and shall
give bond to the company in such sum , and with such .
sureties, as the board of directors shall determine ; and
the secretary and treasurershall be required to execute
good and sufficientbond to secure the faithfulapplica-
tion of the funds which may come to their respective
hands,which bonds shallbe approved by the judge of
the circuit,chancery , or county court of Kenton county .
§ 13. The said company shall pay city, county, and
State taxes on allrealestateand other propertyowned
by itas private individualsare required to do .
§ 14. The tenure and terms of officein said company ,
the mode of fillingvacancies , the amount of benefits,
mortuaryassessments,membership fees,annual dues,and
allother matters pertaining to the business and objects of
thecompany ,not contraryto law ,may be fixedand reg-
ulated by the by -laws of the same .

294 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§15 . This company is exempted from the payment of
licensefeesof everykind,exceptas provided forherein.
§ 16. None of the officers, incorporators, or members-
shallbe liablefor the debts of the company .
17. This act shallbe in force from and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 955 .

eity," approved February nineteen, one thousand eight hundred and

ruary nineteen,one thousand eight hundred and seventy-

>
§2 . That before thisact shalltake effecta majority of

tions to vote at any general elections,shall, at an elec-

before acting, shall take an oath before some officer
authorizedto administeroaths that they willfaithfully
perform theirduty under this act. Said judges shall
decide who is entitledto vote, and if they disagree,the
sheriffshalldetermine the question; and the sheriffshall
preserve order at said election. The clerk shall record
the votes as given for or against the sale . The elections
shall be held between the hours of seven o'clock in the
morning and six o'clock in the evening . When the vote
istaken,the poll-book shallbe certifiedby said officers,
with the resultof the election,to the council of said city,
who shall enter upon its record the resultas certifiedof
said election .
§4 . If,from any cause ,said election is not held at the
time and place named in thisact,then said county judge
shall fixanother time and place for same , and give ten
days ' notice thereof,by written or printed notice,posted
up at said court-house door .
§5 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 25 , 1884.
CHAPTER 956 .
AN ACT providing for making indexes of deeds and mortgages in the-
clerks' officesat Newport and Alexandria, in Campbell county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Campbell county shall
have authorityto cause to be made such new cross-in-

296 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
dexes as may be found necessary of alldeeds and mort-
gages in the county clerk'sofficeat Newport , in said
county ,and such indexes shall remain in and be for use
in said office .
§2 . Said countycourtshallalsohave authoritytomake
such new cross-indexes of deeds recorded in the county
clerk'sofficeat Alexandria,in said county,containedin
deed books from A to Z ,inclusive,as may be found neces-
sary to be made ; and such indexes shall remain in and
be for use in said office .
§3 . For the purpose of determining the necessity for
making such new cross-indexes at the clerk'sofficeat
Newport ,the justices of the peace residingin the Court-
house districtin said county , or such of them as may at-
tend,afterbeing dulysummoned ,shallform a partof said
court .
§ 4. For the purpose of determining the necessity for
making such new cross-indexes at the clerk'sofficeat
Alexandria, the justicesof the peace residingsouth of
the Court-house district,in said county ,or such of them
as may attend,afterbeing duly summoned ,shallform a
part of said court.
§5. The court,at eitherNewport or Alexandria,upon
being satisfiedof the necessityof making such new in-
dexes,inwhole or inpart,as the case may be,shalladver-
tisein a newspaper publishedin Campbell county,or in
a newspaper of general circulationin said.county, by at
least three weekly insertions,and in such other manner
as said court shalldeem proper , for sealed bids to per-
form said work ; and said court shallletthe work to the
lowest and best bidder, if the work should be let; but
said court is to have the right to rejectany and allbids.
Upon any one of said bids being received,saidcourt is
to take a good and sufficientbond ,with security,forthe
faithfulperformance of the work to be done. After the
said work shall have been done according to the con-
tract, and received by said court,the cost of making said
indexes of the deeds and mortgages in the officeat New-
port shallbe paid forby the court-house commissioners

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 297
in said county,out of any surplusannual funds in their
hands as commissioners . And the cost of making the
new cross-indexes of deeds ,from book A to Z , inclusive ,
inthe officeat Alexandria,shallbe paid out of the coun-
ty levy from that part of Campbell county south of the
Court -house district. The court of claims,the justices,or
such of them as may be present,residing in the part of
said county south of said Court -house district,sittingwith
the county judge ,shallhave power to levy and collect,in
the same manner as the county levy is now made and
collected,such additionalamount on the realpropertyin
saiddistrictas may be necessaryto pay for the indexes
made for the office at Alexandria .
§6 . Upon the refusalor failureof the said court-house
commissionersto pay the amount necessaryforthe mak-
ing of said indexes for the officeat Newport ,said county
court, composed of the county judge and a majorityof
the justices of said Court -house district, sittingat New-
port,shallhave power to levy and cause to be collecteda
tax on the real property in said districtsubject to tax for
general revenue purposes, sufficientto pay for making
said indexes and costs of collection , and no more .
§7 . The assessments made in both of said districtsfor
general revenue purposes shall be taken as the assess-
ment of the propertyunder thisact,and the same shall
be collectedby the sheriffas other taxes are,and be ap-
plied to the payment for making said indexes at each
place, respectively, under the direction of the county
court. Said sheriffshall give to said court good and
sufficientbonds for the collectionof said taxes,both at
Newport and Alexandria ,and for the collection of said
taxes in the respective districts.
§8. This act shallbe in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 22, 1884 .

CHAPTER 957 .

Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Claiborne Lisle, S. A. B. Woodford , J. L.
Allen, John A. Juely,C. B. Ecton, and Jesse E. Gordon ,
together with their assigns and successors,be , and they
are hereby,appointedand constituteda corporationand
body -politic, under the name and style of the Farmers '
Bank of Winchester ; and as such,and in that name ,may
sue and be sued,plead and be impleaded ,answer and be
answered ; and that they shallhave power to purchase ,
hold,possess,sell, transfer,or convey,by deed or other-
wise , public or private securitiesof any kind; also to
make and use a common seal, and the same to alterand
renew at pleasure .
§2 . The capitalstock of said corporation shallbe fifty
thousand dollars, but may be increased at any time to
one hundred and fiftythousand dollars,or any sum there-
under , by vote of a majority of the stockholders, duly
certified,and the certificatethereof signed and acknowl-
edged by the presidentand cashier,and recordedin the
clerk'sofficeof the Clark county court. Said stock shall
be divided into shares of one hundred dollars each .
§3 . Any two of the corporators named in thisact may
open books in Winchester for the subscriptionto the cap-
italof saidcorporation; and when two hundred and fifty
shares of said stock shall have been subscribed on said
books , a meeting of the stockholderswho thus subscribe
shall be called, to be held in Winchester , when , if sub-
scribersholding a majorityof said stock so subscribed
are present,an organization shallbe effectedby the elec-
tion of not less than five nor more than nine directors,
as the majority of the subscriberspresent may deem best;
and from these directors the directors themselves shall
choose a president,allof whom shallhold officeuntilthe
next regularelection,which shallbe on the firstMonday
in January following; and on the firstMonday of Janu-
ary in each year thereafter,between the hours of ten, A.
1

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 299
M. ,and one, P. M. ,at said corporation'sbanking-house
in Winchester , an election of a board of directors, of
the number prescribedby [the] by -laws of saidcorpora-
tion,shallbe chosen,who shallselecta presidentfrom
theirnumber ,and shallhave power to choose a cashier,
clerk or clerks, and such other officersor employes as
they may deem necessary,and pay to them such salary
or compensation as may be agreed on by the partiesin-
terested. Said board of directors,by vote of a majority ,
shallmake callsfor the payment of capitalstock sub-
scribed to said bank , and collect same by suit in the

from the cashierand such other officersas they think
proper,forthe faithfuldischargeof theirrespectivedu-
ties; and upon any such bond recoverymay be had for
a breach thereof, or for any embezzlement or breach of
trust of said officer .
§ 4. A majority of the board of directors shall be a
quorum to transact business,and a failureto attend the
meetings of said board four consecutive meetings ,either
regular or called, unless absent from Clark county or sick,
shallbe deemed a vacation of said office,and a successor
may be electedby the remainder of said board .
§5 . Each board of directors and its president shall
hold untiltheirsuccessorsare duly electedand qualified.
Stockholdersatallelectionsshallvotebyballot,and prox-
ies shallnot be voted unless obtained ten days before the
electionwhen they are offeredto be voted. Directors and
allotherofficersshalltake an oath faithfullyto discharge
theirdutiesbeforeenteringupon the dischargethereof.
§6 . Whenever the sum of twenty -fivethousand dollars
shallbe paid in,and the furthersum of twenty -fivethou-
sand dollarsshallbe subscribed,whether paid in or not,

300 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
saidcorporationmay proceed to transacta generalbank .
ing and financialbusiness; and may lend money as a
naturalperson at common law ,discountpromissorynotes,
buy and sellexchange ,stocks,bonds ,and other securities,

and elsewhere ; and the promissory notes made payable

§7 . The form of certificateof stock,and the manner of

erty,withinClark county,to securethe payment ofa loan

§ 11.Said corporationshallat no time have an indebt-
edness,over and above itsdeposits,that shallexceed its

CHAPTER 958 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheEastHickman and JessamineCountyTurnpike
Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
a§1 . That a company may be formed and created
body-politic,by the name and styleof the East Hickman
and Jessamine County Turnpike Company ,for the pur-
pose of buildinga turnpikefrom a point on the Lexing-
ton and Tate's Creek Turnpike Road ,near East Hickman
Church , to intersectthe Nicholasvilleand Union Mills
Turnpike Road at thefarm of Barton Shelby.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 303
§2 .The capitalstock of said company shallbe six
thousanddollars,to be dividedintosharesof fiftydollars
each .
§3 . Books forthe subscriptionof stockshallbe opened
by Thomas A. Davis , John A. Johnson ,John P. Davis ,
George S. Mitchell,John Hinds , T. F. Mitchell, who are
hereby appointed commissioners for said purpose; and
the subscribersto the stock of said company shall enter
into an obligation as follows,in the books of said com-
missioners : We , whose names are hereunto subscribed,
promise to pay the president and directors of the East
Hickman and Jessamine County Turnpike Road Com-
pany the sum of fiftydollarsforeach share of stock by
us subscribed,and set oppositeour respectivenames ,in
such proportions,and at such times, as shallbe required
by said corporation. When the sum of four thousand
dollarsshallhave been subscribed to the capital stock of
said company ,it shall be the duty of any two or more
of the commissioners named in thissectionto give notice,
in such manner as they may deem expedient,for a meet-
ing of the stockholders,at such time and place as the
noticemay specify, for the purpose of electinga presi-
dent ,four directors , and a treasurer . One vote shall be
allowed for each share of stock; and the officersso elected
shall continue in officefor one year , or until their suc-
cessors are elected and qualified. The manner of giving
notice, and the place of all elections after the first,be
fixedby the presidentand directors; but the day of elec-
tion shallbe the same in each year : Provided, The stock-
holders may not change the time of thisannual meeting ,
a majority of allthe votes concurring .
§ 4. Every presidentand directorsof said company ,be-
fore he enters on the duties of his office, shall take an
oath before some person authorized to administer oaths
that he will well and truly discharge the duties of his
said officeto the best of his judgment , without partiality
or prejudice.
§5 . As soon as said company is organized by the elec-
tion of officers, the presidentand directorsshallbe a
body-politicand corporate,in factand in law ,under the

304 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
name and styleaforesaid; and by that name shall have
perpetualsuccessionand privilegesand franchisesinci-
dent to a corporation; shallbe capable of holding their
capital, and the increase and profitsthereof; and of
taking and holding,by purchase or gift, allsuch lands,
teneinents,hereditaments,and realor personalproperty,
as may be necessaryfor the prosecutionof theirwork
or the objectsof thecorporation. They shallhave power
to contract and be contracted with, sue and be sued ,
plead and be impleaded, answer and be answered , in
any court of law or equity; also to have and use a com-
mon seal,and generallyto do any act,matter,or thing
which a corporationmay lawfullydo to effectthe objects
forwhich thiscorporation was created.
§6. The presidentand directorsshallfixand regulate
the grade and elevation of said road , which elevation
shall not exceed four degrees; the width of the part to
be covered with stone; shall designate the place for the
erectionof a gate; may fixthe rates of toll,regulate and
change the same ; but the tollshallnot be greater than
the law permits on other turnpikeroads in thisState;
they may enlargethe capitalstock to such an amount as
theymay deem necessary,and open subscriptiontherefor
in such manner as they may direct.
§7 . The presidentand directors,with theirsurveyors,
engineers,and chain-carriers,are hereby authorizedand
empowered to enterin and upon the lands and inclos-
ures, public roads and highways , in and through over
which the intended road may pass,and to examine and
surveythe ground most proper for that purpose; to ex-
amine quarriesor beds of stone,gravel,and othermate-
rialnecessaryforthe constructionof saidroad.
§ 8. It shallbe lawful for the president and directors,
with theirsuperintendents,engineers,and workmen ,with
tools, instruments, carts, wagons , and other carriages,
and theirbeastsof draught or burthen,to enterupon the
lands in and over, contiguous, and near to which said
road shall pass,having firstgiven notice of theirinten-
tionto the owners or occupiersthereofor theiragents:
Provided, That if the presidentand directorsshallnot

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 305
agree with the owners of saidland through which said
road is to pass as to the damages the owner or owners
may sustain by the road passing through land ,the pres-
identand directorsshallapply to the county courtof Jes-
samine county fora writof ad quod damnum to assessthe
damages which may be sustained by the owner or own-
ers of said land ; and upon payment or tender of the
damages assessed,itshallbe lawful for the president and
directorsto open and make said road ,and dig and carry
away any stone or other material necessary for the con-
structionor repairing of said road .
§ 9. The president and directorsmay require payment
of the stock subscribed , at such times and in such install-
ments as they may deem necessary ; and any stockhold-
er failingto pay the amount of the callupon him for
thirtydays afterthe same shallbecome due and paya-
ble,shallpay ,in additionthereto,one per cent,on said
amount ,and two per cent. per month for every month it
remains unpaid thereafter.
§ 10.The presidentand directorsshalldelivera certifi-
cate,signed by the presidentand countersignedby the
treasurer,and sealed by the seal of the corporation, to
each share-holder for each share by him or her subscribed
and held, which certificateshall be transferable on the
books of saidcompany ,in person or by attorney; but no
share shall be transferred until all arrearages thereon are
paid ,the originalcertificateof the share or shares trans-
ferred shallbe surrendered, and a new certificategiven
to the purchaser, who shallbe entitledto allbenefits
and privilegesthat would have occurred to the original
owner .
§ 11. The treasurerof the company ,before he enters
on the dutiesof hisoffice,shallgive bond and security,
as the presidentand directorsmay require, payable to
them , conditioned to pay over any amount in his hands
to the order of the president and directors.
§ 12. The countycourtof Jessamine county shallhave
power ,a majority of the justicesconcurring therein, to
