subscribea sum ,not exceedingone thousand dollarsper
mile,to the capital stock of said road ,to be regarded as
other stock .
§ 13.Itshallbe lawfulforthepresidentand directors
of the said company ,forthe more thoroughlygood gov-
ernment of the same , to make any set of by-laws not
inconsistent with the provisionsof this act and the Con-
stitutionand laws of Kentucky .
§ 14. No person who is not a share-holder in said cor-
porationshallbe allowed to vote at any electionor hold
any officein the company ,nor shallany stockholdervote
or be entitledto any rights of a member of said com-
pany unlessthe whole amount due and payable as afore-
said on each share held by him shallhave been paid
agreeable to the requisitionsof the president.
§ 15. Any person holding stock to the amount of one
hundred dollarsshallbe exempt from work on any county
road .
§ 16. This act to take effect from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1884.
CHAPTER 959 .
AN ACT to incorporateFunk Seminary ,at Lagrange , in Oldham county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Funk Seminary ,an institutionof learning,
be,and the same ishereby,incorporatedand established
at Lagrange ,Oldham county,Kentucky ,to be known by
the name of Funk Seminary .
§2 . That S. E. DeHaven , B. Wile, Jos. Sauer, and
Isaac T. Risley shall be , and they and their successors
in officeare hereby,constituteda body -politicand corpo-
rate,to be known by the name of the Trustees of Funk
Seminary ; and by thatname shallhave perpetualsucces-
sion and existence,and a common seal,which they may
alterat pleasure; and by said name ,and in theircorpo-
rate capacity,may sue and be sued,plead and be im

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 307
pleaded in allcourtsof law and equity; contractand be
contractedwith; they shallhave power ,in the advance-
ment of the interestof said institution,to raise money by
donation or subscription.
§ 3. The permanent officersof said board shallconsist
of a president,secretary,and treasurer. The board shall
prescribethe duties and responsibilitiesof the officers
provided for in this section; shall, in case of the death
or resignation of any principal,electhis successor; shall
have power to prescribe the course of study, and to elect
assistant teachers .
§ 4. The principal shall have power to confer degrees
and grant diplomas, signed by the principal, president,
and secretary of the board of trustees.
§ 5. No provision of thisact shallbe construed so as to
effectin anywise the special act in relationto the com-
mon school district, number twenty -five, Lagrange ,Old-
ham county , approved April fifteenth,one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-two .
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved April 22 ,1884.
CHAPTER 960 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Deposit Bank of Albany .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a bank is hereby establishedin the town of
Albany , Clinton county ,Kentucky ,by the name and style
of the Deposit Bank of Albany ,to be capable of contract-
ing and being contracted with, of suing and being sued ,
and having a common seal.
§2 . The stock in said bank shallbe deemed personal
property,and shall be assignable according to such rules
as the board of directors shall from time to time establish .
§3 . That the capital stock of said bank shallnot be less
than twenty thousand dollarsnor more than fiftythousand
dollars, and divided into shares of fiftydollarseach .

308 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. That the privatepropertyof the individualstock-
holders shallnot be responsiblefor the corporate debts.
§5 . Said bank may receivedepositsof gold, silver,
bank notes, and other notes which may be lawfully circu-
lated as money , and repay the same in such manner and
at such times as may be agreed upon with depositors by
special or general contract, and may deal in loaning
money ,exchanging promissory notes, and other evidences
of debt ; take personal or other security and real estate,
for the payment of debts made with or due the bank .
§ 6. The said bank may acquire, hold, possess, use ,
enjoy, and occupy allsuch real estate,goods and chattels,
as may be convenient and necessary for the transaction
of itsbusiness,or which may be conveyed to it as surety
for any debt, or purchased in satisfactionof any debt ,
judgment ,or decree of a court having jurisdiction, and
selland convey the same ; and make all needful by -laws
forthe government of said bank not inconsistentwith the
Constitution and laws of the United States or the State
of Kentucky .
§7 . Said bank may receive deposits from minors and
married women ,whose checks or orders for such deposits
shallbe good against said parties, and payments made to
them for such deposits shallbe good and lawful.
§ 8. Dividends , if any , shall be declared on the first
Monday in January and July each year .
§ 9. It shallnot be lawful for said bank to issue notes
or billsto be used as a currency.
§ 10. The rate of intereston deposits and loans shall
not exceed at the rate of six per cent .per annum .
§ 11. That B. M. Yates,T. V. Stephenson,W. F. Harri-
son,G. W. Gwinn ,and J. F. Brents ,or those of them will-
ing to act, are authorized to organize said bank , issue
certificatesof stock,and manage the affairsof said bank
as directorstillthe firstSaturday in January ,one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-four,when the stockhold-
ers of said bank shall electfivedirectors,who shallelect
one of their number president, and an election shallbe
held annuallytoelectdirectors; but if,from any cause,an

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 309
electionisnot held at the time above named ,the election
may be held at another time by giving reasonable notice.
Directors shall hold over and manage the affairsof said
bank untiltheirsuccessorsenter upon a discharge of their
duties .
§ 12. The board of directorsmay appoint such officers
and agents as they may deem necessaryto carryon the
business of the bank , and take bond with surety from
such officersand agents for the faithfulperformance of
theirrespectiveduties. The directorsshallbe stockhold-
ers, and they are authorizedto fillall vacancies when
any occur from any cause .
§13. The rightto alter,amend ,or repeal thischarter
ishereby retained, and itshallexpire in thirtyyears from
the date of itsorganization.
§14 . That this act take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 961 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Owenton High School, in Owen
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of trustees of the Owenton High
School shallhave the same power to submit the question
of local taxation in aid of the common school in said dis-
trict, as is now vested by law in the trustees of common
schools by virtue of chapter eighteen, General Statutes,
and the acts amendatory thereof, and in addition to the
powers conferred by the charter,shall have the same
powers and responsibilitiesas common school trustees
under the general laws of this Commonwealth .
§2 That the money thus raised by taxation may ,in
addition to the objects specified in section five, article
two , chapter eighteen, General Statutes, or to the ex-
clusionthereof, in whole or in part,be applied to the

310 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
payment of intereston any bonds hereafterissued by
saidboard, forthe purpose of raisingmoney to purchase
a new site,and build and furnish a new school-house ,or
to repair or refurnish the present school house ,and to
the providingof a sinkingfund,not exceedingsuch pro-
portion annually of the amount of such bonds as one
year shallbear to the whole number of years such bonds
have to run from their date ; and the said board shall
have discretionarypower as to whether or not such sink-
ing fund shallbe created.
§3 . That said bonds shallbe so issued as that not more
than one thousand dollarsshallfalldue within any one
period of four years from theirdate ,and shall not ex-
ceed in the aggregate six thousand dollars; but it shall

Such partialpayment amount to one hundred dollars.

in July.
§7 . That section seven of an act, entitled"An act to
charter the Owenton High School ," approved February
fourteen, one thousand eight hundred and seventy-three,

the question of said tax shall be submitted to and de

cided by the qualifiedwhite electors,and collected from

CHAPTER 971 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Clay City Bank .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Benjamin Crawford , W. M. Beckner , and G.
B. West ,be,and they are hereby,constituteda corpora-
tion and body -politic, under the name and style of the
Clay CityBank ; and as such may sue and be sued,con-
tract and be contractedwith, plead and be impleaded,
and do such other acts and things as may be proper and
necessary in the transaction of the business which said
corporation isherein authorized to do .
§ 2. Said corporationishereby authorizedand empow-
ered to establisha depositand savingsbank at Clay City,
in Powell county,Kentucky ,with a capitalstock of not
lessthan ten thousand dollars,which may ,by vote of the
board of directors,be increased to fiftythousand dollars:
Provided ,however, That no increase shall be voted until
the amount of increasehas been subscribedforby good
and solventpersons. Said bank may receivedepositsof
gold, silver, bank notes,and other notes which may be
lawfullycirculatingas money , and repay the same in
such manner , at such times, and with such interest as
may be agreed upon with the depositorsby specialor

316 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
general contract; may deal in the loaning of money ,
exchange, promissory notes, bonds, stocks, billsof ex-
change , and other evidences of debt; take personal and
other securitiesfor the payment thereof,and dispose of
such securitiesin accordance with the provisionsof an act,
entitled"An act forthe benefitof the incorporated banks
of Kentucky ," approved March the sixteenth,one thou-
sand eight hundred and seventy -one ; and the promissory
notes made negotiable and payable at itsbanking -house ,
or any bank and inlandbillswhich may be discountedby
it,shallbe , and they are hereby ,put upon the footing of
foreign billsof exchange , and like remedies may be had
thereon, jointly and severally, against drawers and in-
dorsers .
§3 . The corporators herein named may ,at any time ,
open books for subscription to the capital stock of said
bank ; and whenever ten thousand dollars shall have been
subscribed thereto by persons satisfactoryto them , they
shall call the stockholders together , and elect a board of
directors, composed of five stockholders,who shall elect
one of theirown number president,and continue in office
until their successors have been elected and qualified.
An election of a like number of directors of said corpo-
ration shall be had the firstMonday in January of each
year , at the banking -house of said corporation in said
Clay City. Said directorsshall take an oath before an
officerauthorized to administer oaths to faithfullydis-
charge their duties as such; may enact such by -laws ,
rules and regulations as they may deem proper in the
management of the affairsof said corporation,and are
not inconsistentwith the laws of the land ; may create
such offices,and appoint thereto such officersand employ
such employes and servants,as the business of said cor-
poration,in theirjudgment ,requires,and take from them
bonds for the faithfuldischarge of theirduties,with secu-
ritysatisfactoryto said board of directors. Said board
of directors shall meet at least once a week , on a day
and at an hour to be fixed by the by -laws ; and in case
either of said directorsshould fail,for four regular con

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 317
secutivemeetings,to attend saiddirectors' meetings,his
name may be dropped from the listby vote of the ex-
amining directors,or a majorityof them ,and a successor
elected in his stead. Said bank may begin business
whenever said ten thousand dollars shallhave been paid
to the cashier of said bank .
§ 4. Said corporationmay acquire, hold, and forever
use,occupy , and enjoy all such real estate, goods and
chattels, minerals, timber , or mining interests, or farm
produce ,as may be convenient for the transaction of its
business,or which may be conveyed or mortgaged to iton
securityor indemnity for any debt,judgment ,or decree,
and selland convey the same by deed duly executed by
itspresident and cashier. Any person conveying or mort-
gaging any personaltyor movable propertyto said cor-
poration, to secure or indemnify itagainst lossby reason
of a loan of money or forbearance to collectsame ,who
shall remove same from this State or willfullydestroy or
impair same so as to affectitsvalue,or shallconceal same
so that it may not be found or taken possession of when
called for by said corporation, shall be deemed guilty of
a misdemeanor ,and fined not less than fiftynor more
than fivehundred dollars,and confined in the county jail
for a period of not lessthan ten nor more than one hun-
dred days .
§5 . At all electionsheld under the provisionsof this
act,stockholdersmay vote in person or by proxy; but no
person shallactas a proxy who isan officerofsaidbank
and no proxy shall be voted which was not executed ten
days before said election. The yeas and nays may be re-
quired,and must be enteredof record,open to inspection
from any one , on any question before the directorsor
stockholders at the request of one director or stock-
holder .
४
§6 . Certificatesof stockforso much as has been paid
forshallbe issuedto the holdersand owners thereofby
the presidentand cashier, and shares of capitalevi-
denced by such certificates,shallbe held in law as per-
sonalproperty,and be assignableand transferableas the

318 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
by-laws may prescribe. Certificatesof depositshallbe
obligatory on said corporation, and shall be transferable
or assignableas prescribedin the by -laws. Said bank
shallnot have or incur an indebtedness, exclusive of de-
posits, exceeding the amount of itscapitalstock, and
should it incur an indebtedness greater than this, the
directorsvotingfor same ,if shown by the record,and if
not shown by the record, all the directors,shallbe indi-
vidually liablefor such excess to the person or persons
to whom said corporation is indebted for the amount of.
§7 . Itshall be the duty of the president of said corpo-
ration,on the firstday of January in each year ,to pay to
the Treasurer of this State fiftycents on each share of
stock held and paid for in said bank ,which shallbe in full
of alltax or [bonus]
§ 8. This act shall be in effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 972 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe ExcelsiorClay and Iron Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That George Davis , Ludwig Schmidt , Thaddeus
Bennett,Thomas E. Tynes ,and John Tynes ,theirasso-
ciatesand assigns, be, and they are hereby, created a
body -politic and corporate, with perpetual succession,
under the corporate name of the Excelsior Clay and Iron
Company ; and by that name may contract and be con-
tracted with ,sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded , in
allcourts; may have and use a common seal,and alter
and change the same at pleasure; may purchase , hold,
and own land ,leases,oiland other mineral grants,or any
other depositin the earth of any kind,character,or de-
scription,such aspotter'sclay,fire-clay,crucibleclay,coal,
ironore,stone,or any other substancethatmay be upon
or under the surface of the earth,and may selland con-
vey the same to any person or corporation; may hold,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 319
own , and possess any personal property, and may sell,
lease, or assign the same in such manner as may be
deemed necessaryfor the successfultransactionof the
businessof the company ; may make allneedfulregula.
tions for the government and management of the affairs
of the company , from time to time, as may be deemed
necessary .
§ 2. The object of thisact of incorporation is to enable
the corporation to mine and manufacture fire,crucible,
and allother kinds of clay or deposits in, upon ,or under
the surface of the earth; rock and petroleum oil, stone
and cannel coal,stone in all its formations ; to bore for
and procure natural gas ,and to manufacture gas ; to re-
fineoils; tobore forand procuresaltwater,and manufac-
ture saltin allitsforms; to sink mining shafts,and build
and construct roads and railways from theirland , prop-
erty,and works to any railway or navigable water -course ;
to build boats and barges of allkinds,or to buy and own
the same ; to build and erect upon theirlands or leased
lands cooper-shops , machine and manufacturing shops ;
to build tanks , stores, wharves , platforms, and depots ;
and shallpossess,in additionto the specificpowers here-
in enumerated,allthe powers conferredupon similarcor-
porations under the laws of thisCommonwealth ,including
the rightto lay down pipes and pipe linesto conduct
oil, gas - natural or manufactured - from their works to
any point in the State of Kentucky .
§3 . The capital stock of said corporation shallbe five
hundred thousand dollars , divided into shares of one
hundred dollarseach ; shall be held as personal property,
and transferablealone upon the books of the corpora-
tion; and the corporation shall have a lien thereon for
any unpaid calls and demands due upon the shares of
any subscriber or holder. The stockholders,a majority
of said stock voting in the affirmative,may ,at any reg-
ular or called meeting ,by a vote increase the capital
stock of said corporation to one milliondollars.
§4 . The affairsof said company shallbe managed by
a board of fivedirectors,a majority of whom shall con

320 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
stitutea quorum for the transaction of business. The
said directorsshall elect one of their number president,
and may electa treasurer, secretary, general superin-
tendent,and such otherofficersas they may deem expe-
dient. The said board of directorsmay fix the salaries,
and prescribethe dutiesof any officeror agent they may
select or appoint . No person shall be a directorunless
he is a stockholder. The five persons named as incor-
porators in this act shall constitute the board of direct-
ors, and shall continue in office until their successors are
elected and qualified; and the board of directorselected
by the stockholders shall continue in officeone year , or
untiltheirsuccessorsare electedand qualified. Atall reg-
ular elections for directors each share of stock shall entitle
the holder thereof to one vote,which he may castby writ-
ten proxy. The said corporators, or any of them ,may
open books forthesubscriptiontothecapitalstockof said
company at such times and placesas any threeof them
may electand deem rightand proper.
§5 . The board of directorsof saidcompany are here-
by authorizedto open officesfor the conduct of their
business at such place or places as they may deem
proper; and to make such rulesand regulationsto con-
troltheissueand transferand payment of stock at their
principalofficesas they may deem proper; and to pass
any rule, by-law , or regulationdeemed necessary and
properby saidboard of directors.
§ 6. The said company shallhave power to own and
hold, by purchase or otherwise,such real and personal
estate as may be necessary for the proper carrying on
the businessof the company ; and may selland convey
the same at pleasure; and may borrow and raisemoney
on such terms,and securethe same in such way as may
be prescribedby theby laws,or determined by the board
of directors;and to thisend they may secure the pay-
ment of any bond issuedby the company upon the real
and personal property of the corporation.
§7. Said company may organizeand proceed to busi.
ness when the sum of twenty thousand dollarsshallhave

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 321
been subscribed to the capital stock; and the board of
directorsshallhave power to cause the payment of such
stock in such calls,and at such times as they may deem
proper ; and may by by -laws provide for the forfeitureof
any stock upon which any callduly made shallnot be
paid within thirtydays afternotice,or may proceed to
enforce the payment of such callby suit in any court of
competent jurisdiction.
§ 8. No stockholder shall be liable for the debts and
liabilitiesof said corporation to any greater amount or
extent than the balance due upon unpaid stock, which
he may have subscribed to the capital stock thereof.
1
Approved April 22, 1884 .
CHAPTER 973 .
AN ACT toinhibittheowners or baileesof horses,cattle,orhogs,or other
animals,from allowing, suffering, or permitting them to go at large
in Carroll county , and to make the owners or baileesof such animals
liableforalltrespassesand injuriescommitted by them .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for the owner or owners ,
or bailee or bailees,having the custody , control, or man-
agement ,of horses, cattle,sheep ,hogs ,or other animals ,
to allow ,suffer, or permit them , or any of them ,to run at
large or in any highway in said county; but itshallbe
the duty of the owner or owners ,bailee or bailees,thereof,
to safelyand securely take care of such animals ,and keep
them upon his,or her,or theirown premises,exceptwhen
drivingthem from one place to another,and when driv-
ing them to market .
§2 . That if the owner or owners ,bailee or bailees,of
the stock and animals mentioned in the first section of
thisact,shallallow,suffer,or pernit them or any of them
to run at largein saidcounty,or upon any otherthan his
own land,or upon any of the publichighways of said
county ,or upon the land of any other person or persons
whether the same is inclosed by a lawful fence or not,
LOC , L., VOL . 11-21

322 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the owner or owners , manager or managers , bailee or
bailees, of such animals shall be liable to the owner ,
lessee, or tenant having the management ,possession,or
control of such lands, or to any or either of them , for
such damages as they or either of them may sustain
by the trespass of such animals as aforesaid; and the
person or persons sufferingsuch damages shall also
have a lien upon such animals to secure the damages
aforesaid, and the costs and expenses of impounding ,
feeding , and caring for said animals until the damage ,
costs,and expenses aforesaidare paid as hereinafterpro-
vided ; the damages to be recovered and lien aforesaid to
be enforced in any court of competent jurisdictionin
said county .
§ 3. That the owner ,tenant,lessee,or other person hav-
ing the possession of any land or lands upon which the
cattle,stock,or other animals mentioned in the firstsec-
tion of this act shallor may be found trespassingor
doingdamage ,may lawfullyseize and take possessionof
such animals,and he may lawfullyimpound and safely
and securely hold them (in the meantime furnishing them
with sufficientwater and pasturage or teed) untilthe per-
son or persons liablefor the damages done by them shall
pay the same and a reasonable compensation forwatering ,
feeding,or pasturingthem :Provided,however,That itshall
be the duty of the person or persons impounding or de-
taining such cattleand other animals as aforesaid,to give
to the owner or owners thereof notice of their detention
in a reasonable time after impounding the same : And
providedfurther,That in case the owner or owners of such
animals shall, after having received notice of their deten-
tion as aforesaid,failor refuse to pay the damages done
by said animals and a reasonablecompensation for im-
pounding ,feeding,and taking care of said stock,the per-
son damaged thereby may ,in any court having competent
jurisdiction, recover of the person liable for such dam-
ages ,the damages done by said stock, and a reasonable
compensation for impounding , feeding,and taking care
thereof; and by appropriate proceedings in said court may
enforcehislienupon saidstock.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 323
§ 4. That in case any person or persons shalltake and
impound any such cattleor other stock found trespassing
or doing damage as aforesaid, and shallfail, neglect, or
refuse to furnish them , or any of them , with sufficient
pasturage ,feed, or water , or shall in any manner injure
or damage any of them ,he,she,orthey shallbe liableto
the owner or liable thereof for any damage done to or
sufferedby said stock or other animals ,to be recovered
inany courthaving competent jurisdictionthereof.
§5 . But this act shall not take effect or be in force
until it shall firstbe submitted to the legally qualified
voters of Carrollcounty as hereinafterprovided .
§ 6. That it shallbe the duty of the sheriffof Carroll
county to cause a poll to be opened in every voting pre-
cinct in Carrollcounty ,on the firstSaturday in May ,one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,for the purpose
of taking the sense of the qualifiedvoters of said county
as to whether or not they are in favor of the adoption of
this act . Said election shall be held in the same man-
ner,by the same officers,and under the same penalties,
and the returns shallbe made and vote compared ,as in
county elections.
§ 7. It shallbe the duty of the sheriffaforesaidto give
at least two weeks ' notice of the time and purpose of
holding said election,by printedhandbillposted in at
leastfiveof the most public places in each voting pre-
cinct in said county , and by causing said notice and this
act to be published twice in the Carrollton Democrat for
at least two weeks before the time of holding said elec-
tion .
§8. Ifa majorityofthe votescastat saidelectionshall
be in favor of the adoption of this act, then the com-
paring board shallduly certifysaid factto the county
judge of Carrollcounty,who shall, at the firstregular
term of the Carrollcounty court held thereafter, cause
said certificateto be duly recorded in the order-book of
said court; and this act shallbe in fullforce and effect
from the date of its adoption .
§ 9. Should thisact failto receivea majorityof votes
in itsfavor, then, in case there shallbe a majority of

324 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
votes in any voting precinct in said county in favor of
this act , then it shall be in force and effect in such
precinct,and such fact shallbe certifiedand recorded as
provided in section eight of thisact.
Approved April 22, 1884.
CHAPTER 974 .
AN ACT to incorporate" The People'sMutual Assurance Fund ."
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Asher G. Caruth ,Jas.S. Phelps , W. R. Kin-
ney ,Maj . Ed . Hughes ,Chas .Godshaw ,C. J. Enright, and
A. W. Hyde , and theirassociates and successors,be , and
they are hereby,createda body -politic,under the name
of "The People'sMutual Assurance Fund ," with power
to sue and be sued , contract and be contracted with , and
shallhave perpetual succession, with the rightto hold all
realestateor otherfunds necessary,in the judgment of
the board of directors,for itsuses and purposes; and may
disposeof the same ; and may make and use a common
seal, renewable and changeable at pleasure; and may
make such by -laws and regulations as may be deemed
necessaryfor fullycarryingout the purposes of thisact
of incorporation .
§2 . The principalplace of business of said corporation
shallbe in Louisville,Kentucky ,and any sixof the incor-
poratorsnamed in[the] precedingsectionmay ,in person
or by proxy, upon five days' notice, publishedin any
paper printed in Louisville, proceed to organize and
elect a board of directorsand other officersprovided for
herein .
§3 . The affairsof said corporation shall be managed
by a board of directorsof not lessthan six(6) or more
than twenty (20),sixof whom shallbe residentsof Jet-
ferson county , and constitute a local board , who shall
manage the affairsof the corporation,and a majorityof
whom shallconstitutea quorum ; the remaining fourteen
( 14) shallconstitutean advisoryboard,and may residein

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 325
any portionof the United States. They shallbe chosen
by the members of the corporationupon the second Mon-
day in January of each and every year,and shallhold
theirofficesuntiltheirsuccessorsare qualified; vacancies
in the board of directorsmay be filledby the board itself;
and any directorso elected shallhold his officeuntilthe
subsequent annual meeting ; and in case no election is
held on the day named , the president, by order of the
board of directors,may call a meeting for such purpose ,
upon ten days'notice,publishedas provided forin sec-
tion two . This board shall elect from their number a
president, vice president, and secretary, or such other
officersas they deem requisitefor the management of
the business of the corporation, and may , by by -laws ,
regulate the duties of each .
§4 . The object of this corporation is to create and
provide a beneficiaryfund for the families or relationsof
deceased members ,or for the benefit of members in sick-
ness ; and the board of directors shall determine who
shallbe members ,upon what terms they will be received;
may rejectallapplicationsfor membership ; fixand de-
termine the sums to be paid by members , either upon
admission or as monthly dues ; and control and invest
the funds as they deem best; divide or prorate any per-
manent fund among the members ,according to theirrights
and equities; may requirebonds forthefaithfulperform-
ance of theirduty from the officerschosen to manage the
business; and do all others acts necessary to fullycarry
out the benevolent purpose of the corporation.
§5 . The members of the associationshallbe arranged ,
as they are recorded , into divisions; no division shall
exceed two thousand members ; and the funds accruing
from each division may be kept or divided, as the board
of directorsmay require.
§6 . Upon the decease of any member of thisassocia-
tion,the fund to which hisfamilyisentitledshallbe paid
as may be designated in the application for membership ;
thisbeing changed by death ,or otherwise impossible, it
shall go , first,to the widow and infant children; second ,

326 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tohismother and sisters; third,to hisfatherand brothers;
fourth,to hisgranchildren; fifth,tohislegalheirs.
§7 . The fund due deceased members shall not be sub-
ject to the claims of creditors, and shall not be reached
by attachment,garnishment,or other process of law ,so
as to divert from the families of such members .
§ 8. There shallbe an admission fee of six dollars,and
such quarterly dues as may be required by the board of
directors; and,upon the death of a member ,an assess-
ment of one dollaror more ,as may be determined to be
just.
§9 . The members shall be divided into classes ac-
cording to theirseveralages; those from eighteen (18)
to thirty(30) shallconstitute one class; those from thirty
(30) to forty(40) another; those from forty(40) to fifty
(50) another ; those from fifty(50) to sixty(60) another ;
those from sixty (60) to seventy (70); and by a by -law
the board of directorsshallfixthe quarterlydues and
assessments payable by each upon the death of a mem-
ber .
§ 10. Upon receiving notice of the death of a member ,
the secretary or other officershallsend notice of same to
each surviving member and demand , within thirtydays ,
the assessment upon each . If any member shall failto
pay,within thirtydays afterdue notice,he shallforfeit
allrightsin the associationand claim to itsbenefits, and
can only be reinstated by a majority vote of the board
of directors, and the payment of all arrearages and
costs; a notice addressed and mailed to the member at
post-officenamed in the applicationformembership ,or
other addresssubsequentlyentered on the books of the
corporation,shallbe consideredprima facienoticeforall
the purposes herein.
§ 11. New members may be admitted to any division, at
any time,by the board ofdirectors,upon the terms which
they deem just.
§ 12. The fund due deceased members shallbe paid
within sixty(60) days afterlegalnoticeof the death of
such member is given the association; and such legal

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 327
notice of death shall be the claim of the beneficiary of
deceased member ,giving name ,age,place,and circum-
stance of death,accompanied by the sworn statementof
the physician or minister and undertaker officiatingat the
funeral of such member that he died as set out in the
claim . Unless such claim is asserted within one year
from the death of a member , any right of recovery shall
be forfeited. The amount paid by the association as a
beneficiary fund to deceased members shall not exceed
two thousand ($2,000) dollars,and shallnot be lessthan
one dollar from every member of the division in which
deceased member was registered. The living members
shallbe bound in law to pay allassessments made and
dues ; but they may withdraw from the association by
giving a written notice to the secretary or chief officer
of the association of such intention. After giving this
notice,all liabilityfor assessments and dues shallcease
and determine ,as shall also all liabilityof the associa-
tion .
§13 . The members shall in no way be liable for the
debts of the corporation.
§ 14. This corporation shallbe liableto taxation,State,
municipal,on allitsproperty,personaland real,as other
property in this State is taxed under the laws of Ken-
tucky; that the said corporationshall,annually,make a
report,in writing,to the Insurance Bureau of itsprop-
erty, real and personal,money ,choses in action,deposits,
reserved fund, cash balances ,on the tenth day of Janu-
ary in each year .
§ 15. This act to be in force from itspassage .
Approved April 22 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 975 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kentucky Building and Loan Association.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That James McKenna ,James R. Morgan ,Joseph
David ,and Reuben McDonald ,their associates, success-

328 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
.
ors, and assigns,be , and they are hereby , constituted a
body-corporate, under the name and styleof the Ken-
tucky Building and Loan Association ; and by that name
may sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded ; shallhave
succession ; have and use a common seal, and alter or
change the same at pleasure; may acquire and hold real
and personal estate, and selland convey the same ; and
shallpossess such other powers as are consistentwith the
objects of this association .
§2 . The officersof the associationshallbe a president,
vice president, a secretary, and such other subordinate
officersas may be deemed necessary, and five directors.
In election of officers,and in the transaction of other busi-
ness , each member shall be entitled to one vote for each
share he may hold : Provided, That women and infants,
members of thisassociation, may vote by proxy ; married
women and infants by their husbands and guardians ,
otherwomen by any member of the associationselected
by them ,in writing,to act forthem .
§3 . The capital stock shallbe any amount fixed upon
by the association,not exceedingfivehundred thousand
dollars, to be divided into shares of four hundred dollars
each,and to be paid by the stockholdersin weekly install-
ments of one dollar,or a lessamount ,each week , for each
share of stock,until said installments,with their net ac-
cumulations , shall amount to said sum of four hundred
dollarsforeach share; and afterthat,itscorporate powers
and existence shallonly continue to settleup itsunsettled
business and affairs .
§ 4. The corporators above named shallopen books for
the subscriptionof said stock, and may adopt ,so far as it
has progressed,the subscriptionof said stock in anticipa-
tion of this act ; and whenever three hundred shares shall
be subscribed, said association may go into operation.
Other shares may subsequentlybe subscribed,untilthe
whole stock is taken , the subscribersthereto being re-
quired to pay the back installmentscorrespondingwith
those paid by the originalsubscribers, with interestat
such rate,not exceeding six per cent., as the association
may prescribein itsby-laws: Provided, That no stock

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 329
holder shall acquire or hold at any time more than
shares of said stock . Said stock shall be transferable on
the books of said associationas may be provided in its
by -laws .
§ 5. As soon as practicable, after the association is
ready to go into operation, as provided in section four of
thisact,the corporators shallcalla meeting of the stock-
holders, giving reasonable notice of time and place of
such meeting ,for the electionof officers, who shallserve
for one year ,and until their successors are elected and
enter upon the discharge of theirrespectiveduties. An-
nually thereafter,at such time as may be fixed by the
by -laws to be adopted by the stockholders,the officers
shall be elected ; and said stockholders shall have author-
ityto pass and make such by -laws for the government of
said corporation as may be necessary, not inconsistent
with the laws of this State or of the United States .
§6 . Said corporation may provide in itsby -laws what
penaltiesshallattach for failureto pay installmentsof
stock, at the times fixed therefor; and shall have full
power to enforce such penalties, whether the same shall
be by fine or torfeitureof such shares. It may also pro-
vide upon what terms members desiring to do as may
withdraw from the association; and it may also direct
what officersshall be required to execute bonds , with
security, forthe faithfulperformance of theirduties, and
the amount of penalty in such bonds ; and said bonds ,
when executed ,shall be fullybinding upon the obligors
thereto; and it may require quarterly or semi annually ,
in advance ,a payment of five cents upon each share ,
per week , to meet current expenses ; and also fix an
initiation fee of one dollar on each share .
§7 . The object of said associationbeing to enable its
members ,by theirsavings and accumulations ,to acquire
homes and other property,itmay receive deposits in such
sums and at such times as it may provide by itsby -laws ,
and may make loans at any rate of interest,not exceed-
ing legal interest,and on such terms as to securityand
repayment as said associationshall,from time to time,.

330 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
prescribein itsby -laws. No premium shallbe exacted
formaking any loan,but the associationmay make loans
in the order in which applications are made ; or, if more
than one application is made on the same day ,or for the
loan of a particularsum ,may determine by lot among the
applicants; and the deposits of money in said association
shallbe in all things held and treated as their separate
property .
§ 8. It shall be the duty of the secretary of the asso-
ciation,in January of each year , to pay in to the Auditor
of Public Accounts the sum of fifty cents on each one
hundred dollarsof the paid-up capitalstock, and this
shall be in full of all taxes .
§9 . In addition to the mortgage or other lien or secu-
ritywhich may be given by a stockholder,when a loan
is made to a stockholder to secure any loan made to him ,
a firstlienis given to the association upon the interest
and stock of such stockholder in said association to se-
cure it in said lien or indebtedness . The board of direct-
ors of said associationis authorized to invest, from time
to time , any part of the money of the associationthat
may be on hand in excess of the amount required for the
time for loans and retirement of stock in bank stocks or
bonds of any kind, and may loan any part of itsfunds to
stockholders upon the securityof theirstock in the asso-
ciation as collateralto secure payment of such loan , and
may loan any part of itsfunds to persons not stockhold-
ersupon such terms and conditionsas it may prescribe.
§ 10. This act shalltake effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved April 22 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 976 .
AN ACT creatingthe officeof collectorof taxesfor McCracken county,
leviedand to be leviedby the county court of sa'd county for the exclu-
sivepurposesof the county, and to pay itsdebts, and definingthe duties
of such collector .
WHEREAS , The county of McCracken owes a large
bonded indebtedness,createdheretoforefor the purpose

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 331
of aiding in building railroads,on which the county has
to meet the annual interestby the levy of a sufficienttax
upon theproperty of the county ; and whereas ,said county ,
by its county court,under existing laws ,levies other ad
valorem taxesforother county purposes,togetherwith a
poll-tax; and whereas , under existing law said county
has a county treasurer, whose duty is to give bond and
approved surety as such , and who is made the custodian
of the county's funds, and the disburser of the same under
the directionof the county court;and whereas ,the county ,
itis believed,can more efficientlyand conveniently col-
lectitspublic dues ,control and disburse the same ,if it
has itsown county collectorof itstaxes; therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the office of " Collector of Taxes of Mc-
Cracken County " be ,and the same ishereby ,created; that
saidcollectorshallbe electedby the McCracken county
court,composed of the county judge and a majority of
the justices of the county , sittingas a county court,at
at such time in the month of June in every year as they
may be in session, or be calledtogetherby the county
judge ,which he may do for the purpose of electingsaid
collector ; that the collector shall be elected for the term
of one year , with the power and duties as hereinafter
provided; thathe shallbe requiredby saidcourt,within
fifteendays after he is elected, before acting as such
county collector,to execute a covenant to McCracken
county ,with good securityto be approved by said county
court,for the faithfuldischarge of his duties under this
act,which may be in substance as follows: " Whereas ,
--has been elected by the McCracken county
court , on the day of , collector of McCracken
county for the collection of all its taxes for county
purposes and publicdues forthe year --- : Now ,the
said ---- , as principal, and --- , his
securities, hereby covenant to and with said county of
McCracken that he shallwell and trulyperform allhis
duties as such collector, and that he shall well and faith-
-

332 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
fullycollectand pay over to the treasurerof McCracken
county allthe taxes and public dues of said county levied
by thecounty courtthereofand collectableforcounty pur-
poses,forthe year -- , according to law ; and that he will
at alltimes hold himself amenable to the legal orders of
said court concerning the same , and that he shall settle
his accounts with the commissioner appointed by the .
courtforthatpurpose whenever notifiedby order of the
court to do so,and especiallyat the end of his official
term ."
§ 2. That said collector, with the approval of said
county court, may appoint one or more deputies, who
shallhave the same officialpower as himself; but he and
his sureties shall be liable on his officialbond for the mis-
conduct or default of such deputy to the extent Mc-
Cracken county may sustaininjurythereby. That said
collectorand deputies shallbe sworn in open court,be-
foreenteringupon theirduties,to faithfullydischargethe
same according to law .
§3 . That the county assessor'sbook forthe year , when
revised as the general law provides, and is returned by
the assessorto the county courtclerk'soffice,shallbe the
legal assessment and basis upon which taxes of Mc-
cracken county for all county purposes ,including poll-
taxes, shall be ascertained and fixed against every listor
tax-payer liableto pay county taxes of allkinds whatso-
ever; and upon the return by the assessorof his book to
said clerk'soffice,itshallbe the duty of the clerk at once ,
with all reasonable dispatch, to make out the collector's
tax-book for the year ,showing the valuation in the ag-
gregate of each tax-payer's property as assessed,and the
amount of taxes for every purpose , and poll-tax,if any ,
every tax-payer owes the county respectively; and he
shall at once deliver the same to said tax-collector,and
take his receipttherefor, and said collectorshallhave the
power , and itshall be his duty , to collectsaid taxes with
or without the sale of property ; and he shall have all the
power and rightto levy upon and sellthe property of the
tax-payers to satisfytheir respectivetaxes that sherifftsof

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 333
thisCommonwealth have to levy upon and sellproperty
for taxes .
§ 4. That when said tax-collectorshall receive into his
hands said tax-book,he shallstand charged as debtor to
the county of McCracken in the fullamount of all the
taxes shown by the tax-book as due said county for that
year , and itshall be his officialduty to collectthe same
and pay the same over to the treasurerof McCracken
county within one year from hisqualificationas such tax-
collector; and it shall be his further and especial duty ,
afterhe receivesthe tax-book , to pay over to the county
treasurer, at the end of each and every month during his
term of office,hismonthly collectionsof taxes,or oftener
ifhe see proper.and take the treasurer'sreceiptsthere-
for, which shall be vouchers in his favor, and for which
he shallhave credit with the county in his settlementwith
the county court'scommissioner .
§5 . That in case said collectorfailto collectallof said
taxes without any faultof his,and withoutbeing ableto
do so afterexercisingallreasonable vigilance and energy
on his part to collect,and he shall show to the county
court by satisfactoryevidencethatat no time during his
term of officehe had it in his power to have collected
such taxes,then and in such cases the county court shall
allow the collector credit to the extent of the amount due
from such recusant tax-payers in the settlementsof his
accounts as collector .
§ 6. That in case the collector shall have made his
leviesupon propertyfortaxes during his officialterm of
officewhich he shallnot have completed by a salebefore
his term expires,he may ,afterthe expiration of his term ,
have and exercise the same power as he before possessed
for the purpose of completing such levies by a sale of
the property so levied on ,in like manner as sheriffsmay
do with execution process in theirhands levied,but with-
out sale when their officialterms of officeexpire: Pro-
vided ,however ,That said collector and his sureties shall be
liableupon his officialbond to McCracken county forthe
payment of alltaxes which said collectorshallnot have

334 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
paid over to the county treasurer,for which he shall not
have been allowed a credit by the county court, under
sectionfiveof thisact,on or before the expiration of one
year from his qualificationas collector; that all actions
and proceedings upon the officialbond of the collector
under this act shall be prosecuted in the name of Mc-
Cracken county .
§7 . That all laws making the sheriffof McCracken
county , by virtue of his office, collectorof the county
levy and other taxes levied for the purposes of said coun-
ty, and all laws which require the sheriffof said county
to execute a bond for the collections of such taxes , are
hereby declared inoperative as to said county of Mc-
Cracken and the sheriff thereof ; and all such taxes for
said county hereafter shall be collected by the county
collectorunder and in pursuance to the provision of this
act .
§ 8. That a lienishereby declaredand given the coun-
tyof McCracken upon thepropertyof McCracken county
and the tax-payers thereofforthe payment of the afore-
said taxes,subordinate only to the lien of the Common-
wealth foritstaxes; and a lien is also declared and given
said county upon all the property of said tax collector
owned by him at the time he executes his officialbond ,or
which may be acquired by him during his term of office,
forthe satisfactionof any liabilitywhich may accrue to
said county upon such bond in favor of the county against
said tax collector .
§ 9. That said tax collectorshallhave the power to sell
the real and personal estate of the tax-payers for their
taxes,for cash in hand , afterhaving firstadvertised the
sale as requiredby law forthe sale of similarproperty
under execution ; but the sale of real estate shall be made
atthe court-house door,in Paducah ,upon the firstday of
some county court, circuit,or common pleas court, to be
held for McCracken county; and itshallbe the duty of
said collector to preserve and return to the clerk's office
of McCracken county court all his levies upon , and his
written returns of, all sales of real estate sold by him ;

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 335
and when so returned,itshallbe the duty of the county
court clerk to file and preserve the same in his office,
which shallbe and remain on fileas public records of his
office, and copies thereof, so certifiedby him , shallbe
competent evidence in allcourts.
§ 10. That the officeof collectorof McCracken county
aforesaid shallbe compatible with the officeof sheriffof
said county ; and the county court,composed of the judge
and a majority of the justices, may electthe sheriffof
said county,or another,in theirdiscretion,the collector
of the taxes aforesaid .
§ 11. That in case a vacancy in the officeof collector
shall occur by the death , resignation, or removal from
the county of the incumbent , it shallimmediately be the
duty of the judge of said court to convene the justices
of the county , who ,or a majority of whom , shallat once
proceed to fillsuch vacancy by the electionof some fit
person to fillsuch vacancy , who shall,before acting,give
such covenant, with good and approved surety,for the
discharge of his duties as may be prescribed by the coun-
ty court, to suit the exigency of the case,who shall also
be duly sworn according to law ,and who shallhave full
power ,and itshallbe hisduty,to finishand complete the
collectionsof that year under similar responsibilitiesas
hereinbefore prescribed in this act; but the duties and
responsibilitiesof the incumbent who shall fillthe va-
cancy shallbe commensurate only with the unpaid taxes
at the time he qualifies,and which it shallbe his duty to
collect and account for.
§ 12. That the county court of said county ,composed
as aforesaid, at the same term the county collector is
elected in every year ,and before he is elected and qual-
ifies,shallfix, by orderof court,the compensation of the
collectorforthe collectionof the county taxes for that
year, which shall not be less than three nor more than
six per cent.of the amount he shallcollectand pay over
to the county treasurer: Provided,however, The collector
may retainin his hands his per centum compensation,
estimated upon the whole amount collected, and receive

336 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
a credittherefor(if he shallpay over to the treasurerall
the restcollected) in his settlement with the county court
commissioner .
§ 13. That the county courts of McCracken county
may be legallyheld, and allorders and business therein
had and transacted under the provisionsof thisact, with-
out having the justices of the county called together or
associated with the county judge in the holding of the
county courts, save and except in the election of said
county collector, and in the fixing of his compensation .
For these purposes only it shallbe necessary for the jus-
ticesof the county to be present and presidingwith the
county judge .
§ 14. That said county court,in taking the officialbond
of the collector, shall require his sureties,before accept-
ing them ,to be good and solvent,and in the aggregate ,
with theprincipal,afterpaying alltheirliabilities,worth
in propertyin thisCommonwealth subjectto execution
of reasonable value equal to the whole amount of allthe
county taxes to be collectedfor the year by the collector;
and the county court may require the collector, and his
suretiesproposed,to severallyverify,by writtenaffidavits,
the value of theirproperty respectivelyso subject to exe-
cution, which affidavits,when so made and offered,shall
be filedand remain in the county court clerk's office as a
part of the public records of the same .
§ 15. That upon the failureof the collectorto pay over
to the treasurer his monthly collectionsas required by
the foregoing provisions of this act,he and his sureties
shall be liableupon his bond to an action by the county
against them for each and every such failure, and a re-
covery may be had therefor to the extent of the sum
collectedand not paid over ; and also, upon the expira-
tion of the officialterm of the collector,he and his sure-
ties shall be liable upon his bond for whatever amount
of allthe taxes collectableby him for the year , as shown
by his tax-book ,he has not theretoforepaid over to the
treasurer, or received a creditfor as delinquent taxes, or
for which a judgment shall not then have been rendered
against him and his suretiesas aforesaid.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 337
§ 16. That in collectingtaxes,and in levying on prop-
erty to satisfythe same ,it shallbe the duty of the col-
lectorto firstlevy upon personalpropertyin likemanner
as sheriffsare requiredby law to do. That no property
of the tax-payer shall be exempt from levy and sale to
satisfythe taxes named in this act; and in no case shall
the collector receive a credit in his settlement with the
county ,as fordelinquent taxes,if,by the exercise of vig-
ilance and reasonable diligence,he could at any time
during his term of office have made the taxes out of
personal property of the tax-payer ; but in case he could
not have done so, and he shall levy upon and sell real
estate to satisfythe same , which shall be bought by an
individual,he may make such purchaser a deed therefor;
but in case no individual shallbuy and the county has to
bid in and buy the realestateforthe taxes(which by its
agent itmay do),the county shalltherebyacquire a lien
only upon the real estate so bought by itforthe satisfac-
tionof itssaid taxes,with interestfrom day of sale,and
the costs of selling; and also for the costs of the equit-
able suitwhich it may instituteto enforce the lien it
acquiresby purchase under the tax sale; and to the
extent the county shallso buy real estatefor taxes the
collector,in his settlementwith the county court,shall
have a credit for the amount of the taxes for which the
real estate isso sold and bought by the county .
§ 17. Be it furtherenacted, That alldelinquent taxes for
which the collectormay have received credit,in his set-
tlements with the county court, shallthereafterbe collect-
able by subsequent collectors, who shall have the same
powers to do so as in case of othertaxes collectableby
them .
§ 18. That this act shall take effect and be in force from
itspassage .
LOC . L.,VOL . II- 22
Approved April 22 , 1884 .

338 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 978 .
AN ACT to incorporatePaducah Mutual Building Association.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Adam Alt, Robert B. Hall , W. W. Roark ,
Nicholas Greif, Charles Thompson , G. B. Hainline , and
Edward C. Bristol, and their associates and successors ,
are hereby created a body -politic and corporate,by the
name of " Paducah Mutual Building Association," with
fullpowers to contract and be contracted with, to sue and
be sued, to plead and be impleaded , and to adopt a con-
stitution,by -laws , and a common seal.
§2 . Said association may electone of their members
president and one secretary,to hold theirrespectiveoffices
for a term not lessthan one year , which may be fixed by
by-law . The presidentshallpresideat the meetings of
said associationwhen present,and in his absence some
member shallbe selectedto preside by a vote of the asso-
ciation; and the president shall be the keeper of the
common seal, and shall attestall contracts made by the
association ,and in cases of real estate,he shall attest with
his signature and the seal. The secretary shall also
attestall contracts concerning real estate, and all other
contractsmay be attestedwithout sealby the president
or secretary .
§3 . Said associationshallhave power and authority to
purchase and hold realestatein the cityof Paducah ,not
to exceed , however , at any one time , a greater quantity
than twenty lotsof unimproved land; this,however ,not to
include the lotsowned by the individual members of said
association ; and shall have the right to erect and con-
struct on the lots of the association , or the individual
members thereof,buildings suitablefor residentpurposes ,
and to sellsaid propertythus erected and constructed,
and to take to said associationmortgages from purchasers
for any part of the purchase price,and any and alldues
owing said associationby the purchaser of any improved
lot sold by said association.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 339
§4 . Said association shall have the right to sell and
convey any real estateowned by it,by deed of convey-
ance signed and acknowledged by the president and sec-
retary,and take the writtenobligationsof purchasers to
pay the purchase-money by installments; and may , by
appropriateby-laws,provideforthe payment of weekly
or monthly dues to meet accruing intereston allcontracts
bearing interest,not to exceed six per cent.per annum ,
and in such sums and amounts only as will effectthe full
payment of purchase price of any improved lot sold by
said associationwithin fifteenyears from day of sale.
§5 . Said association may elect a board of directorsin
manner to be provided by suitableby -laws ,to consist of
not more than eight members , and to hold their officesfor
a term to be fixed by said by -laws ,whose duty itshallbe
to investigatethe books , contracts, and property of the
association, and report same to the association,and to
counsel and advise with president and secretaryin regard
to purchase of real estate and erecting buildings .
$ 6. The president may callthe board to meet him at
any time to advise upon matters and business of the
association,and he shall,in allcasesof thepurchaseof
realestate,advisewith the directorsbeforeany purchase
is made .
§7 . Said association shallhave the rightto require of
the president and secretary good and sufficientbond for
the faithfulperformance of all their duties, and for the
payment of allmoneys belonging to said associationthat
may come to theirhands respectively; and saidassocia-
tion has the right,by suitable by -laws ,to prescribe the
duties of the president and secretary and directors not
herein prescribed.
§ 8. That for a violation of the provisions of eitherof
saidbonds,said associationshallhave the rightto pro-
ceed upon said bond in any court of McCracken county
having jurisdiction.
§9 . Said association shall have power and right to
provide for fillingvacancies in officeor board of direct-
orsby suitableby -laws; and the privatepropertyof the

340 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
members of the association shall not be liable for the
debts of the association .
§ 10. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 22, 1884 .
CHAPTER 980 .
AN ACT to amend an act approved April tenth, one thousand eight hun-
dred and eighty-four, amending the charterof the Districtof Highlands ,
in Campbell county .

public highways ; therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 983 .

treasurer .

of Kentucky :

be, and the same is hereby , established.

pointinent; but ,before he shallenter on the discharge of
his duties,he shallbe requiredto enterinto bond ,with
approved surety,for the faithfulperformance of his du-
ties,and take the oath now required of other officers; and
he shallhold the said officefor the term of two years,and

rence county court, and pay the same out as directed by

ceeded against in the same manner for a failureto settle,

CHAPTER 985 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Woodstock , in Pulaski county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the town of Woodstock ,in Pulaski county,
be ,and the same is hereby , incorporated, with the fol-
lowing boundaries: Beginning at Mary Patent's,includ-
ing her ; running thence in an easterlydirection to the
house known as the Howard Garner place, including;
thence south to James Watson's , including; thence to

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 343
Aden Isaacs', including; thence a southwest course to
John Price's, on Buck creek; thence with the creek , and
up same to Anderson Todd's ,including; thence to David
Todd's , including; thence to the beginning ; and with such
streetsand alleysas now exist,and such other public
grounds as may be laid out by the trustees. Said trus-
tees to lay offand define said streetsand alleysand
public grounds ,and a plat and survey of said town ; and
in making said plat and survey of said town , said trus-
tees may lay offthe boundaries of said town differently
from those above specified,but not to include a greater
area ; and the plat and survey ,when made , shall be re-
corded in the county clerk'sofficeof Pulaskicounty.
§2 . That Houston Osborne , W. H. Isaacs, V. L.
Thomson ,John Price,and IsaacWeaver ,are hereby ap-
pointed trusteesof said town , who shall hold their office
untilthe firstMonday in June ,at which time,and annu-
allythereafter,thereshallbe an electionby the qualified
voters of said town of fivetrustees,to serve for one year ,
and until theirsuccessors in officeare duly elected and
qualified; before they enter upon the discharge of their
duties,said trusteesshalltake an oath before some officer
authorized to administer oaths that they will faithfully
discharge their duties as said trustees to the best of their
abilityas trusteesof said town ,and the oath required
by the Constitutionof thisState.
§3 . The said trusteesshallbe a body -politic,known by
the name and styleof " The Board of Trustees of Wood-
stock," and by thatname be capable of contractingand
being contracted with ,of suing and being sued; that said
trusteesmay have and use a common seal,which they
may alterat pleasure; and shallhave allthe power and
privileges,and be subjectto the same dutiesand liabili-
ties, as provided by the general law in relationto towns
incorporated in thisCommonwealth .
§4 . That at an electionof trusteesof said town ,on the
firstMonday in June next,there shallbe a policejudge
and a town marshal elected for the town ,each for the term
of two years . The jurisdictionof the police judge shall

344 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
be concurrentwith that of justicesof the peace of Pu-
laski county in issuing warrants and hearing and passing
upon cases of persons charged with criminal or penal of-
fenses,and the powers of a single justice in examining
trials; and shall have jurisdictionconcurrent with jus-
tices of the peace in civilmatters, where the defendants
reside within the corporate limits of the town of Wood-
stock; the said court shallhave jurisdictionfor the trial
of all persons upon charges of the violationsof the by-
laws or ordinances . The marshal shall have the same
powers and liabilitiesas a constable,and his fees shall
be the same for like services; and shallexecute a bond ,
with good and sufficientsurety, to the Commonwealth of
Kentucky ,to be approved by the board of trustees,and
shallembrace similarstipulationsand conditions as bonds
required by law to be executed by constables; and said
marshal shallcollectalltaxes which may be leviedby
said board of trustees, and for that purpose shall have
similarpowers as allowed by law to sheriffsin making
distraintfor such taxes ; and shall pay over same as he
shall be directed by said board of trustees . He shall be
liableon his officialbond for the due collectionand pay-
ment of said taxes .
§5 . The board of trustees shallhave power to grant
coffee-house license, with privilege to sellspirituous, vi-
nous ,or malt liquors by drink or otherwise in said town ,
and to charge and collect a tax therefor,the amount of
such tax to be fixedby order of the board , and when col-
lected,shallbe for the use of said town ; but no privilege
shallbe exercised under said license untilthe tax upon
licenserequired to be paid to the State have been paid .
All finesfor breach of the by -laws or ordinances of said
town shallbe for the benefit of said town ,and shall be
paid to such person or persons as the board of trustees
shall direct .
§6 . The board of trustees shall have power to order
an election for the purpose of voting a tax upon the
property of said town , not exceeding twenty -five cents
on each one hundred dollarsin value in one year , and a
poll tax not exceeding one dollar upon each tithablein

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 345 .
said town in one year , which tax shall be collected by
the marshal ; and with a view to the levying of such tax,
the board of trustees shallappoint an assessorto take a
listof such propertyand tithablesin saidtown .
§7 . The police judge shallbe entitledto the same fees
as a justiceof the peace for similar services,except that
he shall be entitled to the sum of one dollar for issuing
warrant of arrest against any party for violationsof the
ordinances or by -laws of said town .
§ 8. The board of trustees shallhave power to make
and enact such by laws and ordinancesas may be nec-
essary for the government of saidtown , and have full
power to enforcesuch by laws and ordinancesby fines
and forfeitures,to be recovered by warrants against the
offenders, to be tried before the police judge of said
town .
§9 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 22, 1884 .

CHAPTER 989 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof intoxicatingliquorsin common schooldis-
trictnumber thirty-three, in Metcalfecounty .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
-of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person to sell
spirituous,.vinous, or malt liquors within the limits of
common school districtnumber thirty-three,in Metcalfe
county .
§2 . Any person violatingthe provisionsof this act
shallbe fined not less than twenty nor more than sixty
dollars, in the discretion of a jury , which fine shall be
recoverable on indictment in the circuit court of said
-county , as finesfor tipplingand keeping tippling-houses .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 347
§3 . A pollshallbe opened at the August election,in
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,at the voting
precinctin which saidschooldistrictissituated,at which
time and placeall of the qualifiedvotersin said school
districtshallhave the rightto vote for or against thisact.
If a majorityof thosevotingshallvote forit,then itshall
become a law ,otherwise itshall not.
§ 4. The officersof the electionshallmake on the poll-
books a certificateof the result of the vote ; and the board
to compare the polls in said county shall make two cer-
tificatesof the resultof said vote, one to be recorded by
the county court clerk in the order-book , and the other to
be kept on fileby the circuitcourtclerkas otherrecords.
§5 . The sheriffof said county shall, at least twenty
days before said election, advertise that thisvote will be
taken at two public places in said school district, and at
two other public places in said voting precinct.
§ 6. Should a majority of those voting cast their votes
for this act,then it shall take effectfrom and afterthe
time said certificateshall be recorded by said county
court clerk; and in the event a majority should not vote
for this act ,then it shall be void and of no effect.
Approved April 24, 1884.

CHAPTER 991 .
AN ACT to prevent the saleof spirituous, vinous,or malt liquorswithin-
two milesof Lewis Chapeland Highland Church,inWarren county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any person to sell
spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors,or any mixture thereof,
at Lewis Chapel or Highland Church ,in Warren county ,
or within two miles of either; and it shall be unlawful
for any person within said limitsto sellany articlewhat-
soever,and under pretense of such sale to give away any

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 349
§2 . Any person violating the provisions of this act
shall,upon conviction,be finedin any sum not lessthan
ten and not exceeding fiftydollars,to be recovered by

tucky ,in any court of competent jurisdiction.
§3 . This act shallbe submitted ,at some electionheld
in said districts, to the qualified voters resident within
said districtsas defined in thisact; and forthe purpose of
holding said election,the county judge of Warren county
is required to appoint two judges and a clerk in each of
said districts,who shall hold said electionat any time in
said districts, after having firstgiven forty days' public
notice of said election,by ten or more written or printed
notices,to be posted in each of said districts. And after
said election is held,the judges thereof shall count and
certifythe vote to the county court clerk of Warren
county; and if, on the counting of saidvote,itshallap-
pear that a majority of allthe votes cast at said election,
in eitherof said districts,shallvote in favor of thisact,it
shall be in force and effectas to such district,when the
majority of the votes are so cast in favor of it.
§4 . The vote herein authorized to be taken may be
taken in either of the districts as herein defined , and the
judges so appointed shalldetermine who are legal voters
and entitled to vote at said election . Each district shall
pay the cost of the electionheld therein under thisact.
Approved April24,1884.

enty-eight, and return the same , upon proper certificates,

CHAPTER 996 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Paducah Hotel Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Charles Reed ,George Langstaff, John Orm ,
Moses Bloom ,John W. McKnight ,Ebridge Palmer ,John
Seganfetter,Louis Kolb,John O'Brian,and R. Loeb ,and
their successors and assigns,be , and they are hereby ,in-
corporated in the name and style of the Paducah Hotel
Company ; and in that name and styleare empowered to
contract and be contracted with as natural persons; to sue
and be sued in the courts of this State and elsewhere ;
and to make such by -laws for the government and busi-
ness affairsof said Paducah Hotel Company as it may
deem proper ,provided the same are not inconsistentwith
the Constitution and laws of this State nor of the United
States; and said company isvestedwith perpetual suc-
cession .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 353
§2. That the saidPaducah HotelCompany isvested
with power to purchase and own one or more lotsin the
cityof Paducah ,and erect thereon a hotel,with allneces-
sary and proper buildings connected or near thereto,as
may be deemed properor useful,and to rent and lease
the same upon such terms and for such length of time as
itmay thinkproper; thatthe capitalstockof said com-
pany shallbe one hundred thousand dollars, or such sum ,
and no more ,than is necessary to purchase the lots and
erect the hotel and other buildings and improvements as
is herein authorized ; the stock to be divided into shares of
one hundred dollars,and may be paid within such times
and on such terms as may be agreed on by the persons
subscribing the same ; and the company and each incor-
porator named therein is authorized to open books and
receive subscriptions to the stock of said Paducah Hotel
Company ; and to enable said Paducah Hotel Company
to purchase the lots and erect thereon a hotel and other
improvements ,the said company is empowered to issue
itsbonds in such sums and amounts as may be agreed on
or thought proper,payable in such time and to such per-
sons as the company may think proper ,bearing any rate
of interestnot exceeding six per centum per annum ,pay-
able semi -annually in said city of Paducah ; but in no
event shall the bonds so issued exceed the actual costs of
the ground and improvements to be erected thereon ; and
to that extent the said Paducah Hotel Company is em-
powered to execute a deed of trust upon its real estate,
improvements,and otherpropertytofullysecureallbonds
issued under this act,so as to create a prior lien on all
said property to secure the payments of allbonds issued
under thisact; and said company may sell,hypothecate,
or exchange said bonds,as it may deem proper,in the
purchase of said lotsand erection of said improvements .
§3 . That the affairsof saidcompany may be managed
and controlledby an executive committee of three stock-
holders, to be elected by the stockholders of said com-
pany , at such time and place and for such length of time
as theby-laws of saidcompany may prescribe. And said

executive committee , when elected, are to purchase the

cure the same by deed of trust,and do and perform all

essary or proper after its organization, and within the
scope and meaning of this act and such by -laws as may
be adopted by said company .

CHAPTER 998 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Mineral Land Company of Virginia, Kentucky ,
and Tennessee, to construct, equip, and operatea railroad.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Mineral Land Company of Virginia,
Kentucky , and Tennessee ,in addition to the powers and
privilegesgranted in itscharter,approved April nine-
teenth,one thousand eight hundred and eightytwo , is
hereby authorized to construct, equip , and operate a rail-
way from the Louisvilleand NashvilleRailroad,or the
Kentucky Central Railroad, through the counties of
Laurel , Knox , and Bell, to the Virginia line; and may
also extend a branch of said road up Cumberland river
towards Pound Gap ,so as to connect with the Kentucky
Union Railroad ; and itmay also build and construct and
operate such other branch roads in Knox , Bell, and Har-
lan as may be necessaryto developthe landsnow owned ,
or which may hereafterbe owned , by said company in
said counties; and the said company is hereby vested
with all powers necessary to construct,equip , and ope-
rate lines of railway as aforesaid,and such as are inci-
dent to corporations engaged in constructing, equipping ,
and operating railroads.
§2 . And for the purpose of constructing said railroad
and branches ,the said company may accept subscriptions
to its capital stock in lands, material, timber , mineral
privileges,labor,and money , upon such terms and condi-
tions as may be agreed on between said company and
the subscribers thereof .
§3 . If said company and the owners of land on or
through which said road and branches may be located do
not agree with referenceto the rightof way or the ac-
quirement of lands for depots and other purposes of said
company in building and operating said railroadand
branches ,said company may acquire said right of way
and lands forother purposes,as isnow providedby law
for condemning rightsof way ,etcetera,forrailroadsand
other corporations.

356 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. That it shall be lawful for the said company to
consolidate their said railroad with any other railroad,
or any branch thereof, whether now or hereafterincorpo-
rated, with the consent of such other railroad company ;
and it shall be lawful for any other railroad company ,
now or hereafter incorporated,to consolidate their rail-
road ,or any branch thereof,with the road of this com-
pany , with the consent of thiscompany ; which union
may be consummated upon such terms and conditions as
may be agreed on by and between the directors of the
respectivecompanies. And the company thus consoli-
dated shall become vested with all the rights, powers ,
and privilegesgranted to said companies before the con-
solidation thereof .
§5 . And the directorsof said company may ,from time
to time ,borrow money to be used in the construction of
said railroad and branches ,and for the other purposes of
saidcompany ,and they may issuebonds therefor,bearing
not exceeding six per cent.interestper annum ,in such
denominations as they may think proper ,and make the
same payable at such times and places as they may think
proper ; and they may make said bonds receivablefor all
debts due the company .
Approved April 28, 1884.

of said county as a pest-house ; and there being doubt as

of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 1001 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Clark county to subscribeto the
capitalstock of turnpike roads.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
Kentucky :
§1 .The county court of Clark county, Kentucky (a
majority of the justicesof the peace of said county

358 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
concurring therein),be ,and it is authorized, to subscribe
to the capital stock of any turnpike road which may be
built in said county , not exceeding fifteenhundred dol-
lars per mile for each mile hereafter constructed; and
said courtisauthorizedto make such subscriptionupon
such terms and conditions as said court shall deem best .
§2 . If said county court shallnot be able to pay the
whole or any amount of any subscriptionwhich may be
made under the provisionsof thisact,itmay issueand
sellthe bonds of saidcounty forthe amount (or any part
thereof) subscribedas aforesaid,and to be payable at
such times and places as may be fixed by said court,but
not to bear a rateof interestgreaterthan six per cent.
per annum . Said bonds shallbe signedby the presiding
judge and clerk of said court, and shall not be sold for
less than their face value .
§3 . The county judge of said county shall call the
justicesof said county together, for the purpose of mak-
ing the subscriptionsprovided forin thisact,whenever
requestedto do so in writing, signed by at least ten
freeholders of said county ,but they shall not be called
togetherfor such purpose more than twice in any one
year .
§ 4. The county judge of said county shallbe a mem-
ber of the board of directors of any company that re-
ceivesa subscriptionfrom saidcountyunder the provisions
of this act,and shallhave allthe rightsand privilegesof
other directors. The capital stock of such companies
shallbe divided into shares not exceeding three hundred
dollars each; and each stockholder shall be entitled to
one vote for each share ; and the county judge is author-
ized to cast the vote of said county upon allquestions
arisingbeforethe board of directorsof such companies
in regard to the construction and management of said
road or otherwise .
§5. This act to take effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 22 , 1884.
