CHAPTER 1005 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the LouisvilleBanking Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe lawful for the Louisville Banking
Company to consolidatewith any other bank in the city
of Louisville,and Stateof Kentucky ,upon such terms as
may be agreedon by theboardsof directorsof saidbanks ,
and be ratifiedby the stockholdersof the saidbanks at a
meeting of said stockholdersto be held for that purpose ,
ten days ' notice of said meeting having been previously
given by noticein some daily paper published in said
city. And any stockholderin eitherbank who ,for any
reason,shallfailto attend said meeting,may ratifyand
consent to such consolidationby writing, duly signed and
witnessed by the cashier of the bank in which he or she
or they may be stockholders, and whether married
single. Stock held in trustmay be representedby the
trustee,and stockheld for infantsby guardians may be
represented by such guardians .
or
§2 . When terms of consolidation shall have been
agreed upon by the boards of directors,and ratifiedby
stockholders, as provided in the preceding section, the
consolidation may be completed in the following manner :
The new bank shall be known as the Louisville Banking
Company ,or ifthe boards of directorsprefer,the name
of the other bank may be added ; and in such name said
new bank may contract and be contracted with ,sue and
be sued ,have ,use ,and alterat pleasure a common seal;
and succeed to allthe privilegesand franchisespossessed
by the two consolidating. Said two banks so agreeing to
consolidateshallthen convey ,transfer,and assign alltheir
real and personalproperty, claims, debts,dues, and de
mands to said new bank , an inventory of which shallbe
entered in favor of each of said banks on the books of the
new bank , Stock certificatesshall then be issued at once
to the holders of stock in said two banks ,for an amount
of stock equal in the aggregate to one half the value of
the assetsso turned over,divided into shares of one hun

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 363
dred dollarseach , and distributedamong the stockholders
of said two banks in proportion to the amount owned
by each ,and to the relativevalue of the assets of each
of saidtwo banks,givingscripcertificatesforportionsof
shares ; and so soon thereafter as the remainder of said
assets so transferredcan be realized, additional like cer
tificatesfor shares of capital stock shall be issued,and
distributedto those entitledas aforesaid,untilsaid assets
are capitalized, not, however , in excess of one million
five hundred thousand dollars .
§ 3. As soon as the firstshares of capitalstock shall
have been issued as provided in the preceding section,
said new bank may begin business,with the rights, pow-
ers,and franchisesprovided in the firstsection of thisact;
and itsaffairsshall be controlledby a board of directors
consisting of the members of the boards of directorsof
said two banks , who shall serve as such until the first
Tuesday in February , one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-five,on which day ,and annuallyon thatday there-
after,the stockholders shallelectfrom among themselves
a board of directors of the same number . Said board thus
constitutedshallappoint a president,two vice presidents,
and such cashiers,tellers,clerks,servants,and agents as
they deem proper , and fix the compensation of each ;
make allproper by -laws for the government and the con-
control of the business, and remove any of said officers,
agents, or servantsat pleasure, and may appoint from
their number an executive committee of such members as
they deem best,who ,when said board is not in session,
shall have , forthe time being ,the powers of said board of
directors .
§ 4. Itshallbe the duty of said board of directorsto
see that accounts are faithfullykept to show the assetsof
each of said banks entering said consolidation,and until
the same isfullyrealized, and that certificatesfor shares
are issued to share-holders in proper proportion,so that
the assets of each of said banks may be capitalized in
favor of its former share-holders; and if any surplus of
assets shallremain afterissuing stock to the limit herein

364 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
before fixedof one millionfivehundred thousand dollars,
the amount of such surplus may be equalized as far as

dated bank ,or distributedamong those entitled as may
be agreed upon .
§5 . The safety vault now owned by the Louisville
Banking Company may be leased,sold, or otherwise dis--
posed of as determined by itsboard of directors,or if de--
sired,the same may pass intosaid consolidation,and its
value be capitalized, on such terms as may be agreed

ecute bond , with good security,to discharge such duties

thousand eight hundred and seventy-eight,so far as the

of an act , entitled "An act to amend an act , entitled 'An
act to establishan institutionof learning in the town of
Corinth,Grant county ,to be known as the Corinth Acad-
emy , " are alsohereby adopted and made a part of this

ployed by the trustees,unless he or she shallhave a first-

common schoolteachersin thisState; and saidprincipal

duct .

one barrel .

CHAPTER 1010 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof liquorsin Ballardcounty ,and providing for
a vote on that question .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawful for any person or persons
to sell,loan,barter,or trafficin spirituous,vinous,or malt
liquors,in any quantitywhatever, and for any purpose
whatever, eitherdirectlyor indirectly, in the county of
Ballard .
§2 . Any person violatingthe provisionsof the preced-
ing sectionshall,upon conviction,be finedin any sum not

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 367
less than one hundred nor more than five hundred dol-
lars,in the discretionof th jury trying the case.
§3 . This act shall not take effect and be in force until
the same shallbe ratifiedand approved by a majorityof
the votes cast at the election hereinafter provided for in
thisact. Itshallbe the duty of the clerkof the Ballard
county court to prepare poll-books for each of the voting
precincts of said county ; and in preparing said books the
clerk shall make one column marked at the head thereof,
on each page , " For the sale of spirituous, vinous, and
malt liquors," and another column marked at the head
of each page ,"Against the sale of spirituous,vinous,and
malt liquors;" and the clerk shall deliversaid poll-books
to the sheriffof said county , which shallbe deliveredby
the sheriffto the clerk of the electionin each precinct at
leasttwo days before said election.
§4 . Itshallbe the duty of the clerk of said electionto
propound to each individual who offersto vote at said
electionthe question : "Are you for or against the sale of
whisky in the county of Ballard ? " and his vote shallbe
recorded according to his answer . It shall be the duty
of the officersto be governed by the laws regulating the
conduct of officersof general elections. The saidpoll-
books shallbe returned to the clerk of the Ballard county
court on Monday after said election, and on Wednesday
followingthe officerswhose duty itisto compare thepolls
in generalelectionsshallcompare the pollsat thiselec-
tion; and if it shallappear,from a comparison of the
polls,thata majorityof thevotescastat saidelectionare
againstthe sale of said liquors,then they shallmake a
certificateof that fact, which shall be signed by all or
a majority of said comparing board ,and filedwith the
county clerk,and said clerk shall record the same in his
office; and then this act shall be in fullforce and effect.
§ 5. In order to carry out the objects and purposes of
thisact,itishereby made the duty of the Ballard county
court to order the sheriffto give twenty days ' notice,by
printed or written posters,in each of the voting places at
said election,of said election; and said court shall ap

368 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
point the officersto hold said election at the May term
of said court , Said election shall be held on the Satur-
day , seventh day of June , one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four ; and at said electionitshallbe unlawful
for any person to vote who is not eligibleto vote for Rep-
resentativeto the General Assembly .
§6 . Nothing in this act shall be construed to interfere
with the privilege of any person who is sellingliquors
under a license properly granted tillthe expiration of
such license .
§7 . This act,in so far as it authorizes the vote to be
taken ,shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage .
§ 8. That if a majority of allqualifiedvoters voting at
said electionvote forthe sale of whisky ,then allacts and
partsof actsprohibitingthesaleof whisky in saidcoun-
ty are hereby repealed .
Approved April 22, 1884.

A trusteemay ,by writtenfilewith thetrustees,resignas
such trustee,and the vacancy shallthereupon be filledas
herein authorized .
§5 . That said trustees shall be known as " The Trus-
tees of the Town of Shady Grove ," and by that name
may sue and be sued,contractand be contractedwith,
plead and be impleaded with in allcourtsof thisCom-
monwealth ; they shallelectone of theirnumber chairman
of the board ,who shall preside at all theirmeetings ,but
in case of the absence of the chairman at any meeting ,or
when the officeof chairman becomes vacant ,the trustees

372 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shallelecta chairman protem,in the firstcase,and in the
second a chairman for the rest of the term for which the
trustees have been elected; they shall also elect a clerk,
who may be a member of the board ,or some other citizen
of the town ,and he may be removed at the pleasure of
the board ; the record of the board's proceedings shall be
valid,however ,if entered by any member of the board ,or
any one elsein theirpresence,and signed by the chair-
man or chairman pro tem.;and copies and transcriptsfrom
the recordskept by the trusteesshallbe evidence in all
courts and places where the same shall be necessary ; the
trusteesappointedhereinmay meet to organizein any
house they may deem proper in Shady Grove ,and their
successorsthereaftershallmeet for that purpose at such
places in the town as may have been procured for their
use ,or at such places as they may select; at the meeting
of the trusteesfororganization, the oldest member elected
shall be chairman until a regular chairman be elected
which shall be the firstthing done after the meeting .
The trusteesof said town shallhold regular monthly
meetings in the town on the firstSaturday in the month ,
untilby actionoftheboard some otherday be fixedupon ;
they may hold specialmeetings at such times as they may
determine upon ,and the chairman may callspecial meet-
ingswhen ,in hisopinion,itbecomes necessary,by notify-
ing the trustees thereof .
Y
§6 . The legislativepower and authority of said town
shallbe vestedin saidtrustees; they shallhave the power
to pass allneedful by -laws and ordinances for the effect-
ual administration of rightand justicein said town ,and
for the better government thereof, and the better protec-
tionof the propertyof the citizens,unlessrestrainedby
this charter, or the Constitution or laws of this State,
and fixsuch penaltiesfor violationof any of such ordi-
nances and by -laws,not exceeding a fineof fiftydollars,
as they may deem the good order and welfare of the town
may require. All ordinances passed by the trusteesmust
be made public by them by being posted at two or more
places in said town for at leastfivedays. They shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 373
have fullpower and authority to cause the streetsand
alleys in said town to be opened , and kept open and in
good repair,so faras to them may seem best; macadam-
ize,turnpike,or lay with planks such streetsand alleys,
or parts thereof,as to them may seem proper. They
shallhave the power and authority to cause the sidewalks
in said town to be built or made , and the same repaired
and kept in good order; but beforepaving or planking,
or repairing,they shallascertainthe costthereof,and no-
tifythe owners of the lotsor lands abounding on or ad-
jacenttheretosuch sidewalks; and if any owner of any
such lots or lands failto pay his or her portion for pav-
ing,repairing such sidewalks,forten days afterbeing no-
tifiedof the amount due from them , he or she,the same
shall be collected as hereinafter described .
§7 . The said trustees shall have power to prevent all
kinds of stock from running at large in the town , and by
ordinance require the owners of stock to prevent the
same from running at large in the town ,under such fines
as the trustees may say seem proper ; they may also by
ordinance provide that any hogs ,cattle,mules ,or other
stockfound running at largein the town ,may be taken
up and kept by the town marshal untilthe owner thereof
can be proceeded against, and thus held and subjectto
the payment of the fine imposed by the court,by proper
orderof the court forthat purpose tryingthe case, and
the cost of proceedingand expenses of taking up and
keeping the same , which shall be ascertained and fixed
by the judgment of the court.
§8 . The trustees shall have the power , and it shall
be their duty , to provide , by ordinance , with necessary
penalties, to keep the streetsof said town clean of all
obstructions of any kind whatever ; and they may , by
appropriate proceedings in their names as said trustees,
in any court having jurisdiction,cause allor any of the
streetsand alleys in said town that now closed or en-
croached upon , by fencing or otherwise, to be opened ,
and thereafterto be kept in theirfullwidth .
§9 . They shallhave power to licenseshows and pub-
licexhibitionsin the said town ,and may fixthe license

374 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
feethereofat any sum at theirdiscretion; but thisshall
not interferewith generallaws requiringState or county
license,nor apply to any schoolexhibitionor other pub-
licexhibitionof entertainments in the town ,and confined
to it.
§ 10. They may ,ifthey see proper,prevent all public
auctions or sales in the streetsof said town ,or license
the same : Provided, That thissectiondoes not apply to
any saleor auction orderedby any courtor judge there-
of,or any administratoror executor,or other publicoffi-
cerin the dischargeof hisduty as such.
§11 . They shallhave power to levy and collectsuch
ad valorem tax upon the realestatein the town annu-
ally, not to exceed fiftycents on the hundred dollars
worth thereof,and also a poll-tax of not to exceed one
dollarand fiftycents on each male citizenin the town
who are twenty-one yearsofage; the saidtaxesso levied
and collectedshallbe expended by the trusteesin repair-
ing and keeping the streetsin good repair,and executing
such otherdutiesas are requiredof them by thischarter.
§ 12. The trusteesshallappoint and remove at pleas-
ure a town assessor,who shall assess allthe real estate
in the town at its fair cash value , in the currency of

make out and return a complete listof allmale citizens
of the town at the time of assessment over twenty one
years of age; he shallmake a fullreportto the trustees
at such times as they may direct; hisreport[to] show the
realestateassessed, againstwhom ,and give a descrip-
tion thereof; show the lots, size, and so forth, where it
can be done .
§13 . There shallbe an electionin said town for town
marshal on the second Saturday in May ,one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-four,at same time trustees are
elected, and every two years thereafter. The marshal
shallhold hisofficeforthe term of his qualifying,giving
bond untilhis successoris electedand qualified. E. F.
Stevens is appointed town marshal of said town until
the second Saturday in May ,one thousand eighthundred

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 375
and eighty-four, and untilhis successoris elected and
qualified.
§ 14. The marshal of said town shallbe a conservator
of the peace,and perform such other dutiesnot herein
named as may be imposed by the by-laws and ordinances
passed by the trustees; and it shall be his especial duty
to preserve the peace in the said town ; arrest,with or
without warrant,allpersons he sees or knows to be en-
gaged in breaking the peace,or disturbingthe peace of
the town , or violatingthe laws of the land, and take
them before a justiceof the peace or a policejudge ,to
be dealt with according to law ; he shallhave the same
power to callto his assistance,in suppressingdisorderly
persons and make arrests,that constables have ; he may
execute summons ,executions,arrests,and any otherpro-
cessesissuedby the policejudge, take replevinbonds
and bonds authorizedto be taken by constablesunder
similarwrits,and may execute any processesthatmight
be executedby a constable,and shallmake properreturns
of allprocessesput in hishands; but he cannot be com-
pelled to receive processesin civilbusiness to be executed
outside of his civildistrict; but if he receives such ,he
shall execute the same .
§ 15. The marshal of said town shallbe ex officiocol-
lectorof allthe taxes levied in said town by said trustees;
he shall pay over to the trustees all taxes and moneys
collectedby him or coming to hishands as such collector;
and said marshal shall be liable on his official bond for
any failureof duty as such collector.
§ 16. The marshal of said town shall make arrestsin
allcases where a constable or sheriffmight ,for violation
of the laws ,as well as for violations of the town Before
enteringupon the dischargeof his duty as marshal,he
shallgivebond ,with security,to the Commonwealth ,to
be approved by the county court,thathe willfaithfully
discharge his duty ; on which bond he and his sureties
shallbe liableto any one aggrievedby itsbreach; he
shallalsotake an oath to faithfullydischargehisduties.
§ 17. That a policecourt be,and the same is hereby,
establishedin said town ; the judge of said court shallbe

376 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
electedby the votes of said town ; he shallhold his office
for two years from his election, and until his successor
is electedand qualified. That W. S. Williams,of said
town ,be ,and is hereby , appointed the firstjudge of said
court, and shallhold his officeuntilthe second Saturday
of May ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,and
until his successor is elected and qualified,and on that
day ,and everytwo years thereafter,at same time town
marshal is elected ,there shall be an election in said town
by the qualifiedvotersthereof for judge of the police
court. The judge hereinappointed,and the judge here-
after elected, before entering on the discharge of their
duties of said office, shall, before some officer, take the
oath to faithfullydischarge the duties of his office, and
give bond to the Commonwealth , with good surety, to be
approved by the county court, that they will well and
truly perform their duties as judge of the court; which
bond shallbe filedin the county court,and proceedings
may be had thereon by any one aggrieved by itsbreach .
§ 18. The police judge of said court shall hold his
court in said town for the trial of civil business on the
second Saturday in every month ; and shallhave the same
civiljurisdictionas a justiceof the peace have in that civil
district, and co -extensive with the district; and the same
rulesof pleadingand practiceshallapply as in justices'
courts, and processes, writs, attachments ,executions,and
other writs issuedby justicesof the peace ,may be issued
by the police judge , and same proceedings had thereon
as issued from a justice: Provided, That allwrits may
be directed to any constable of said county ,or the town
marshal of said town ; also all processes issued by said
judge,and may be executed by eitherof saidofficers,or
by the sheriffor other officersauthorized to execute such
writs and processes; any writs appropriateto the busi-
ness in said court may be issued by said judge , whether
named herein or not, and the proper proceedings had
thereon as if issued from any other court of likejuris-
diction. The said judge shall have the same jurisdic-
tion in forcible entry and detainer that justices of the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 377
peace have under the Civil Code of Practice. Summons
shall be issued some time before trial in said court as in
justices' courts,and a return day shall be fixed in such
cases. The said judge shall open his court at any time
forthe trialof misdemeanors and other public offenses
over which he may have jurisdiction. He shallhave the
same jurisdictionover riots, routs, and breaches of the
peace thatjusticesof the peace have ,and shallbe gov-
erned by the same laws and rules in such proceedings ,
and shallissuethe writsand processesthatjusticesof the
peace may issue in such cases : Provided, That allsuch
writsand processesmay be directedto and executed by
the town marshal,any constable,or the sheriff, and shall
proceed upon as if issuedby a justiceof the peace.
§19 . That in all cases of prosecutionsfor public of-
fensesbailmay be allowed and taken as isauthorizedin
such cases before justicesof the peace ,and on forfeiture
of bailthe same proceeding may be had as in such courts.
§20. The said policejudge shallhave authorityand
jurisdictionto hold examining courts,and hold to bail in
allcases,eitherwhen the offensecharged be misdemeanor
or a felony, and shallproceed as an examing court under
the Criminal Code of Practice .
§21. The saidpolicejudge shallopen hiscourtsat any
time for the trialof persons charged with a violationof
the by -laws or ordinances passed by the trusteesof said
town , and shallhave jurisdictionof allsuch matters ,and
shallissueand arrestor summons against any person vio-
latingany such ordinancesor by -laws of saidtown ,when
the judge has reasonable grounds ,from information on
oath of another,or from his own knowledge , or informa-
tion of a peace officer, or from a trustee of the town .
The time of trialshall be fixed in the writ,which shall
not be lessthan two days from the date of the summons ,
and if it be an arrest warrant , it shall be returned for
the within; the party arrested, on being brought before
the judge , shall immediately be tried, unless the case is
continued to some other time ,in which case bail may be
given with surety,as in cases of trialsof misdemeanors ,

378 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
which ,ifforfeited,shallbe proceededon againstthe sure-
tiesand principalas in casesof forfeituresof bail in the
criminalcourt,exceptthatthe summons shallbe return-
able beforethepolicejudge at such times as he may fix,
and thenbe tried,and judgment be given or renderedac-
cordingto law . All proceedingsin saidcourtfor viola-
tion of the ordinance or by -laws of said town shallbe in
the name of the Commonwealth , and allfines or forfeit-
ures received in said court for a violation of ordinances
and by -laws shallbe paid over to the trusteesof said
town ,and held and used by them for the benefitof said
town . And on alljudgments fora violationof saidordi-
nances or by -laws executions shall issue in the name of
the Commonwealth ,or capiasprofinemay be had ,as in
cases of such writs when issued from justices' courts or
judgments in misdemeanor cases.
§22 . The trusteesmay and shallhave the power to
regulatethe sale of any and allspirituous, vinous, or
malt liquors,or any otherintoxicatingagent,whether it
be a proprietarymedicine or beer,insideof the incorpo
ration, inflictingsuch finesand penaltiesas they may
deem necessary and hereafter may fix.
Approved April22,1884.
CHAPTER 1014 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Home InsuranceCompany , of Louisville.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That J. M. Robinson, W. C. Hall, SilasF. Miller,
John S. Long ,T. M. Sullivan,H. P. Forwood ,JuliusBam-
berger, W. T. Grant , O. T. Sutfield, J. B. Wilder , and
Daniel E. Dogherty ,together with their associates, suc-
cessors,and assigns,be,and they are hereby, appointed
and made a corporationand body -politic, under the
name and styleof the "Home Insurance Company , of
Louisville," and shallcontinue in force for fiftyyears
from the passage of thisact.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 379
§ 2. That by that name are made capable in law of
suing and being sued , contracting and being contracted
with,pleadingand being impleaded in allcourtsof law ,
and of having and using a common seal,and regulating
theiraffairsby the establishmentof proper by -laws ; to
have,purchase,or possess,enjoy and retainlands,rents,
tenements,goods,chattels,publicor privatesecuritiesof
any kind,quality,or nature whatsoever,and to selland
disposeof the same at any time or in any manner ; and
said company may investmoney in securities,according
to law regulating fire,marine ,health,accident,livestock,
and allothers except lifeinsurance companies .
§3 . That the capitalstock of thiscompany shallnot
exceed one million dollars ,and shall be divided into shares
of one hundred dollars each .
§4 . That the corporators named in this act,or any two
of them ,shallopen books for the subscriptionof stock;
and when one thousand shares or more shall have been
subscribed,they shallgive notice of a meeting of the sub-
scribers,when twenty-fivedollarsshallbe paid in hand
on each share subscribedfor; and the company shallbe
organized when the remaining seventy -five dollars on
each shareshallbe secured,tobe paid at the time and in
the manner thatshallbe designatedby the share-holders
who have paid in twenty-fivedollarson each share sub-
scribedfor. The corporatorsnamed ,or any of them ,who
shallfailto subscribe for stock,shallcease to be members
of the company ; and allsuch subscribersas shallfailto
pay twenty -five dollars on each share subscribed for on
the day designated in the second notice,shallcease to be
stockholders,and the stock subscribedfor by them may
be subscribed for by others, under the direction of the
person or personswho receivedthe originalsubscription;
and the board of directorsmay , in theirby -laws, pre-
scribethe manner in which the capitalstock shallbe
increased, and the manner in which such increase shall
be paid in,or so securedto be paid in; but in no event
shallthe capitalstock exceed one milliondollars, nor
shallthe company commence businessuntilone hundred
thousand dollarsshallhave been paid in.

380 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§5 . That the officersof said company and the manage-
ment thereof shall be under the direction and control of
a president and twelve directors,who shallbe stockhold-
ers, and each share of said stock shall entitle the holder
to one vote,and the shares may be voted on by proxy
or by the administrator or executor of deceased owner ;
and the stockholders shall meet at the office of the com-
pany on the firstMonday in April of each year and elect
a president and twelve directors,who shall continue in
officeuntilthe firstMonday in April of the ensuing year ,
and until their successors shall be elected , of which elec-
tion previous notice shallbe given in one or more news-
papers printed in Louisville; and in case of the death ,
resignation,or removal from the cityof thepresidentand
director, the remaining directors shall electsome person
to fillthe vacancy for the residue of the year .
§6 . That the president and directorsfor the time being
shallhave power to appoint such officersand agents
under them as shall be necessary for executing the busi-
ness of said company ,and to allow such compensation as
may be agreed upon ,and to require and take bond and
securityfor the discharge of their respective duties,and
trusts,and to fix the compensation of the president; and
the president and six directors shall constitutea quorum
for the transaction of business, or seven directors with-
out a president, one of whom shall be chosen president
for the time being .
§ 7. The president and directorsshallhave power and
authority,in the name of the company ,to make allkinds
of insurance upon vessels,boats,or crafts navigating on
the waters of the United States or the high seas; also
against firein town or country ,and upon the transpor-
tation of goods , wares ,and merchandise , and to do and
perform all matters and things relating to said objects,
subject to the regulations and restrictionsof the share-
holders .
§ 8. On the firstweek in July,and on the firstweek in
January in each year, half-yearly statements shallbe
made of the situation of the capital and of the state of
1

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 381
the accounts of the company , which shallbe laid before
the share -holders , with the amount , number , and charac-
ter of the risk undetermined , and such other matters and
things as shallbe necessaryfor the information of the
share-holders; and such dividends of the profitsmay be
made as the president and directorsmay think advisable,
but they shallnot diminish the capitalstock. The share-
holders may , at the halfyearly meetings , pass by -laws
and ordinances for the regulation of the business of the
company and the regulationof the presidentand direct-
ors, as they shall think proper , which shall not be re-
pealed,altered,or amended except by the share-holders;
and none but a citizen of this State and a share -holder
shallbe eligibleas president or director.
§9. Every writ may be served on the corporationby
executingthe same on presidentor secretary,which shall
authorizea judgment by defaultifthe corporationfailto
appear .
§ 10. The saidcompany shallhave a lienon the share
or shares of such share holder for any debt or demands
which such share-holdershallor may owe said company ,
and they(the said presidentand directors) shallrefuse
the privilegeto any share-holder in said company to
transfer his, her, or their share or shares of stock until
all debts and demands the said company may have
against such share-holder shallbe fullypaid; and such
lienmay be enforced by a written notice to such share-
holdersto pay such debt or demands within thirtydays;
and ifthe same be not paid,itshallbe the duty of the
presidentand directors,and they are hereby authorized,
to advertise the sale of such share or shares of stock for
the space of thirtydays ,and if not paid within said thir-
ty days,to sellthe same ,at publicauction,to the highest
bidder, for cash; and on the payment of the purchase-
money ,the president and directors shall have issued to
the purchaser a certificateor certificatesof stock; and
on the application of the former owners , and the sur-
render of his, her , or their certificate or certificates of
stock, the said president and directors shall give him ,

382 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
her,or them credit,after deducting the costs and charges
of such sale, for the residueof the money for which
such share or shares sold on the debt and demands
of the said company against such share-holder ; and
when such share or shares shall sell for more than the
debt and demand , including costs and charges of such
sale of the said company , the presidentand directors
shallpay over the residue,ifany ,to such share-holders.
This sectionshallnot debar the company from any rem .
edy at law or in equity againstshare-holders for any
debt or demands which said company have or may have
against them .
§ 11.The presidentand directorsof saidcompany shall
prescribe the form of certificatesof shares of stock and
the manner of transferring such certificates; but the
transfershallbe made in a book to be kept forthatpur.
pose by said company at theirplaceof doing businessin
said cityof Louisville; which transfershall be made in
the presence of,and attestedby,eitherthe presidentor
secretaryof the company ; and a transfermay be made
by such share-holder in person, or by his, her, or their
agent,the power of attorneyto such agents to be duly
proven and certified, and lodged with the presidentor
secretary of the company .
§ 12. The General Assembly hereby reservesthe right,
from time to time,to repealor modify thisact.
§ 13. The stockholders, in person or by proxy, repre-
sentinga majorityof stock,may at any time make ,alter,
or amend the by-laws of saidcompany ; and may ,by the
by -laws , increase or reduce the number of directors,from
time to time ; and change the time of the annual election
of presidentand directors,and the time of theirgoing
out of office; and change the time formaking semi -annual
statements to the stockholders, and the time for declaring
dividends; and also may ,by the by -laws,declarea por-
tionof the profitsto certainpolicy-holdersor customers
of saidcompany ,by way of returnpremium .
§ 14.Said company may purchase or erecta building
in which to have an office; and such building shall,at a

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 383
faircash value,be estimatedand treatedas assetsunder
the laws of Kentucky regulatingfireand marine insur-
ance companies .
§ 15. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage, and remain in force subjectto the power of the
General Assembly as aforesaid.
Approved April 24, 1884.
CHAPTER 1015 .
AN ACT to authorize the board of councilmen of Frankfort to issue bouds
forschoolpurposes.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
ofKentucky :
§1 . Whereas , the board of councilmen of the cityof
Frankfortis authorizedby the citycharterto build,pur-
chase ,or procure any additional grounds or buildings
which may be necessary for common school purposes;
therefore ,to enable the board of councilmen to realize a
fund sufficientto procure a suitablebuilding,and neces-
sary furniture, fixtures, and apparatus for the colored
public school of the city,the said board of councilmen
may issuebonds of the cityto the amount of ten thou
sand dollars,of the denomination of fivehundred dollars
each,with coupons attached,forthepayment of interest,
which interestmay be at any rateper annum notexceed-
ing sixper cent.,and shallbe payable semi-annually,to-
wit: on the firstday of January and July,at theFarmers '
Bank ofKentucky ,Frankfort,Kentucky ; saidbonds to be
payable twenty years afterdate at said Farmers' Bank
of Kentucky . The bonds to be signedby the mayor and
countersignedby the clerk, with the corporateseal of
the cityattached, and the coupons to be signed by the
clerk; which said bonds,so issuedas aforesaid,shallbe
put upon the market by the mayor and sold,and from the
sale thereof shall be realized the said fund as aforesaid :
Provided , That the said bonds shallnot be sold for a less
sum than their face value .
§ 2. The board of councilmen shall,from year toyear,
levy upon the taxable propertyin said city,and collect

384 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
an additional tax sufficientto pay the interest on said
bonds , and redeem and pay off the same when they shall
become due ,which said taxes, as levied and collected,
shallbe kept separate and apart from the other revenues
of the city, and shallnot be used for any other purposes
than the payment of the interestand principal of said
bonds .
§3 . The fund realized from the sale of said bonds shall
be kept separate and apart from the other funds of the
city, and shall be styled the Colored School Building
Fund , which fund shall not be used for any other than
the purpose herein named .
§ 4. When a suitablebuilding and necessary furniture,
fixtures,and apparatus shallhave been procured for the
coloredpublicschoolof saidcity,the balance of the said
building fund ,if any shall be then remaining ,shall be
placed to the credit, and be made part,of the fund for
the support of the colored public school.
§5 . The board of trustees of the public schools and
the committee on education of the board of councilmen
shall constituteand be styled the School Building Com-
mittee. The chairman and secretary of the board of
trusteesof the publicschoolsshallbe respectivelychair-
man and secretary of the said school building committee ,
which building committee shall have power to expend
the said colored school building fund for the purpose
atoresaid. The said fund shall be disbursed upon order
of the said committee ,and upon warrants signed by the
chairman and secretary thereof.
§ 6. The bonds provided for in thisact shallnot be sub-
ject to taxation for citypurposes .
§7 . All acts or parts of acts in conflictherewith are
hereby repealed.
Approved April 25, 1884 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 385
CHAPTER 1016 .
AN ACT to recharterthe town of London ,Laurel county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That allacts and parts of acts incorporatingthe
town of London ,in Laurel county,Kentucky ,be,and the
same are hereby ,repealed, and said town ishereby re-in-
corporated: Provided,however, That thisact shallin no-
wise affectthe propertyor rightsof individualsof said
town ,or the liabilitiesof said town as a corporation; but
allrightsand liabilitiesshallremain as ifthisact had not
been passed .
§2 . The boundary of said town shallbe as follows,viz:
Beginning at a stoneon the Whitley branch,thencenorth
forty, west five thousand eight hundred and ten feet to a
stone; thence north fifty, east three thousand five hun-
dred and eighty feet to a stone; thence south forty, east
five thousand eight hundred and ten feet to a stone;
thence south fifty, west three thousand five hundred and
eighty feetto the beginning .
§ 3. The trusteesof said town now in office, viz: T. J.
Canifax,A. R. Dyche , E. H. Hackney , H. J. Williams,
and C. W. Jones,shallremain so,and shallhave allpoм-
ers granted to them by the laws herein,and be subject
to allliabilities,and dischargeallthe dutiesimposed by
this act,in the same manner as if they had been elected
under it,untiltheirsuccessorsare duly electedand quali-
fied .
§ 4. That on the second Saturday in December , one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-four, and annually
thereafter,there shallbe elected fivetrustees,by the legal
voters of said town , who shall hold their officefor one
year ,and untiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified;
who , before acting, shalltake the constitutionaloath,
which may be administered by any officerof this State
authorized to administer oaths . The officer administer-
ing the oath shallmake a certificateof same ,which shall
be spread on the records of the said board . Said trus-
LOC , L., VOL . 11-25

386 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tees, after qualifying, shall organize by electing one of
theirbody chairman of said board , who shall have con-
current jurisdictionwith justices of the peace in said
town ,and issuehis warrants,try cases,issuecapiaspro
fine,and do any and all acts that a justice of the peace
or police judge might do if present; and also a clerk
shall be elected at same time . In case a vacancy may
occur in said board ,they may fillsaid vacancy by ap-
pointment , at any meeting when a quorum of three are
present. The board shall elect a treasurer, who shall
give a bond ,to be approved by the board ,for the faithful
performance of his duties.
§5 . The saidboard of trusteesshallbe a body-politic
and corporate, and shall be known by the name and
styleof the " Board of Trustees of the Town of London ,
Kentucky ;" and by that name may sue and be sued,
contract and be contracted with , and have all power
given corporations under the general laws of Kentucky .
A majority of said board shallconstitutea quorum for
the transaction of business; and , in the absence of the
chairman,theymay electone of theirbody presentchair-
man pro tem. Said officerpro tem. shall sign the min-
utes of the previous meeting when read and adopted ,
and shallhave allpower that the permanent chairman
would have were he present and presiding.
§ 6. Said board of trustees shall have power to make
allby -laws that they deem necessary,but not to conflict
with the Constitution of the United States or of Ken-
tucky , forkeeping good order, and for the benefit of the
society of said town , and for improvement of streets,
and alleys, and sidewalks of same .
§7 . That the trustees are hereby authorized to force
streetsand alleysthrough any portionof saidtown when ,
in theirjudgment ,it is necessary for the convenience of
said town ,by paying the owner for any land or property
damaged thereby,to be condemned in same manner that
landsare condemned forpublic roads under the general
law of the State .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 387
§ 8. That said trustees shall have power to levy and
collect an ad valorem tax of not exceeding twenty -five
cents on the one hundred dollars' worth of real and per-
sonal property in said town ; also may levy and collecta
capitationtax of not exceeding($ 1) one dollaron each
male citizenresidingin said town over twenty -one years
of age . This tax, when collected, to be applied to the
improvement of the streetsand alleysof said town ,and
such other legal uses as the board may deem proper
that the assessment of the property of said town , and
citizensowning property therein, shallbe made annually
as on the tenth day of January , same as the State law ,
by an assessorappointed by the board of trustees,with
the authority,instructions,and restrictionswhich the as-
sessor of the property of Laurel county has ; and when
said assessorreturnshis assessmentfor any year,if, in
the judgment of the board , or a majority of them , any
one has failed to value his property properly,they may
summons said person or persons to appear before the
board to show cause,if any,why theirpropertyshallnot
be valued higher, and have the same power given them
as is given to supervisors of tax for the differentcounties
of this State .
§9 . Said trusteesmay employ an attorney,whose duty
it shall be to prosecute all persons for violation of the
by -laws of said town ,and in case that the board may
deem it best to have counsel ; and said attorney shall
receive thirtyper cent. of all fines and forfeitures, and
such additionalpay as the board may agree to and allow
when he appears and prosecutes for the said board .
§ 10. The said board of trusteesshallnot grant license
of any kind to any person or persons,with the privilege
of sellingspirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsin any way
in said town .
§ 11. And that the county judge shallnot grant license
of any kind in said town to any one to sellspirituous,
vinous, or malt liquors. The board may grant tavern
license,withoutthe privilegeto sellliquors,or the county

388 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
judge may license taverns,without the privilege to sell
liquors .
§ 12. The board of trustees shall have authority to
licenseauction sales,peddlers,shows , and exhibitionsin
theaters or halls, and fix the amount of tax thereon , as
they deem best by theirby -laws .
§ 13. They shall not have power to license any gam-
bling-house,or any house where money or other thing is
won or lost .
§ 14. That the board of trustees shall have power to
say what are nuisances on public property or elsewhere
in said town ,whether erected or being erected. On com-
plaint by any person of said town ,itshallbe the duty of
said trusteesto summons the party and hear proof before
theirchairman or police judge , and ifthey deem it best,
they may give the party notice to abate same ; and if
not done within five days thereafter,they may fine him
from one to twenty dollarsfor every day he shallkeep
said nuisance ; or if, from the knowledge of the board ,
any one isguiltyof having a nuisance on hisproperty,
or on the streets or alleys,they shall give the offender
notice to abate same , without hearing proof, with the
same penalty as above mentioned .
§ 15. That saidboard shallhave power to declare any
house in said town a nuisance to societywhere gambling
or card-playing is carried on ,or anything that is detri-
mental to good society, and fix the fine,not to exceed
that in section fourteen .
§ 16. That any one keeping spirituous, vinous , malt ,
and intoxicating liquors, or mixtures; thereof,to sellor
dispose of, directlyor indirectly, within the limitsof said
town ,in houses or on the streets, shall be guilty of run-
ning a public nuisance to society, and upon conviction
shallbe fined from fiftydollarsto one hundred dollars, or
may be imprisoned in the county jailfrom one to six
months , or both fined and imprisoned , in the discretionof
the chairman or policejudge or jury before whom the
case istried; and on the offenderfailingtopay orreplevy,
saidfinemay be worked out on the streetsas providedin

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 389
the General Statutes; and it shall be a part of the judg-
ment that the offender give bond to said board of trus-
tees,in the sum of five hundred dollars,that he will not
sell or give to any person spirituous, vinous , or malt
liquors,or mixture thereof,in said town , again , contrary
tolaw . And on hisfailureto give said bond ,ifin business
house ,the board of trusteesshall have power to lock up
hisplace of business,and keep same abated ; and if said
bond be forfeited, suitmay be brought in any court hav-
ing jurisdiction,in the name and for the benefit of said
board . The board shallhave power to make allby -laws
in regard to peddling whisky , and so forth, on the streets
or other places within the limits of said town , and fix
such penalties as they may deem proper .
§17 . There shallbe elected on the second Saturday in
December ,one thousand eighthundred and eighty-tour,
and bienniallythereafter,by the qualifiedvoters of the
town of London ,a policejudge,who shallhold hisoffice
forthe term of two years,and untilhissuccessoriselected-
and qualified, who shall take the same oath taken by
county judges ,and execute bond in the Laurel county
court in the same manner that justices of the peace do
under the State law . No person shall be elected to fill
the officeof police judge who is not twenty -one years
of age ,and who has not been a residentof said town for
one year preceding his election. The electionof said
police judge shall be certifiedto the Governor of Ken-
tuckyby the officersholdingthe election,whereupon the
Governor shall commission said judge in the same man-
ner that county judges are commissioned ; and at any
time that the officeof police judge shallbecome vacant ,
the board of trustees, and in case of no board the county
judge , shall appoint and recommend a person to the
Governor , who ,when commissioned and qualified,shall
have the same power as if elected untilthe next regular
election, and until his successor iselected and qualified.
The policejudge of said town , or,in his absence, the
chairman of the board of trustees,shall have exclusive
jurisdictionof all offenses against the ordinances and

390 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
by -laws of said town , and concurrent jurisdictionwith
the county judge and justices of the peace in criminal
and penal offenses committed in Laurel county ; and the
policejudge shallhave concurrentjurisdictionin civil
cases with justicesof the peace of Laurel county; but
for civilcases,he shallkeep a separate docket , on which
such cases may be set for trial. and some specifiedday to
be setby said police judge in the months of April, July,
October,and January of each year,and in such trialhe
shall be governed by the same laws as now control
county judges and justices of the peace in Laurel coun-
ty; and he shallbe a conservator of the peace of Laurel
county; and any civilprocess issuedby him may be re-
turned before the county judge or any justice of the
peace in Laurel county. The policecourtshallbe styled
the " Police Court of the Town of London , Kentucky ;"
and same is hereby declared a court of record, and shall
have allsuch powers of preserving order,punishing con-
tempt of its authority, and enforcing obedience to same ,
as are possessed by the county judge . All finesimposed
by said police court for offensescommitted in said town
against itsby -laws shallbe for the benefit of said town ,
and shallbe paid over by the officercollectingsame to
the treasurer of the board of trustees within ten days
aftersame is collected. All warrants issued by the police
judge of saidtown shallbe in the name of the Common .
wealth of Kentucky ,for the benefitof the board of trus.
tees of the town of London , and shall be executed by
the marshal of said town ,or any other peace officerof
Laurel county ,to whom they may be delivered and re-
turned to said police court for trial; said police court
shallhave power to take bailfor the appearance of any
person charged with offenseswithin itsjurisdictionas
defined in thisact, at a subsequent day for trial, in ac-
cordance with the Code of Practice,or may indorse on
the warrant the bail which may be taken as the law
requiresof officersmaking arrestunder the State laws
forhisappearance on a day fixedby such officer; and in
case any person shall failto appear ,in discharge of his
or her bail,may declaresame forfeited,and issuesum

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 391
mons against such person or persons and theirsuretiesto
appear and show cause why judgment shallnot be ren-
dered against them for the amount of said bond ; and if
no good reason is shown , within ten days afterservice of
summons ,said court may give judgment for the amount ,
on which judgment a capiaspro fine may issue, and
when collected,the same shall be paid to the treasurer
of the board of trustees of said town : Provided, That
said bail shallnot be exceeding the amount of the fine
and costsin any case. The fees of said policejudge
shall be the same in all cases as the county judge of
Laurel county,Kentucky . Either party may appeal to
the quarterlycourt in civilactions,and to the circuit
court of Laurel county in criminalor penal actions,in
any case where ,under the Civil Code of Practice, each
party would have the rightto an appeal to the quarterly
court from justices'court,or from quarterlycourt to cir-
cuit court. which appeal shall be taken in the same
manner and time ,and on the same conditions , as from
justices' or quarterlycourt. Said police judge shallhave
power and jurisdictionto enforce the laws of this State
against vagrants, gypsies, peddlers, tipplers, nuisances,
and all other violations of the laws of this State com-
mitted within Laurel county ; and to imprison drunken
and boisterouspersonsuntilsober; and to requirebonds
to keep the peace, and to be of good behavior. The
said policejudge,ifsatisfiedthat a public offensehas
been committed inviolationof the by-laws or ordinances
of said town ,or the statutoryor penal laws within the
county of Laurel,he may summons before him any per-
son he may think proper forexamination,on oath,con-
cerning it,to enable him to ascertain the offender, and
issuehiswarrant forhis arrest; and if saidpolicejudge
has reasonable grounds for believing that any person or
persons are keeping spirituous, vinous,or malt liquors,
or any mixturethereof,in any house,barn,or otherplace
in said town or county ,contrary to law ,forany other pur-
pose than forhisor her own familyuse,and isdisposing
ot saine by sellingor giving,he shallhave power to issue

392 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
a search warrant directed to the sheriff, constable ,or mar-
shal of said town to search such premises ,and if spirit-
vous liquorisfound,itshallbe taken by said officer,and
delivered to the said police judge ; and if, on trialof the
offender,itshallappear that he or she was or had been
sellingor disposingof same ,in any way ,the saidpolice
judge shall make an order directing the officerto pour
same out and destroy it; and the offender shall be fined
for such violation,and pay all cost of officers, and so-
forth; and the offender shallhave no pay for such liquor,
or have any cause of action against said officer, board ,
or police judge for the value of same ; said search war-
rant shallstatewhat premises are to be searched,and
saidsearchshallbe made in the day -time. And W. R.
Ramsey ishereby constitutedpolicejudge of said town
untilhis successor isduly elected or appointed and qual-
ifiedas herein provided for.
§ 18. At the regularelectionon the second Saturday in
December ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,
and annually thereafter, there shall be elected by the
qualifiedvoters of said town a marshal , who , within ten
days after his election,in addition to the oath required
by the Constitution and laws of the State,shalltake an
oath faithfullyto discharge all duties under this act and
the by -laws of the board of trustees of said town , and
execute bond ,with approved security,to be approved by
the board of trustees,conditionalfor the faithfulperform-
ance of his duties, and that he will in due time collect
and pay over to the proper persons alltaxes which it
shallbe his duty to collect; also allfines, assessments,
forfeitures, or other dues of said town placed in his
hands for collection, and that he will, in good faith,
to the best of his skill and ability, execute all such
duties as may be lawfully required of him by the board
of trusteesor policejudge of said town , or other offi-
cer, imposed by thisact for breach of the by -laws of
said town ; and he may execute processes issued by any
officerof this Commonwealth for any infractionor viola-
tion of the criminal laws of thisState, and for these pur

LAWS OF KENTUCKY 393 .
poses may go to any part of Laurel county ; and said
marshal shall have concurrent jurisdictionwith consta-
bles of said Laurel county , Kentucky ,in civiland crimi-
nal cases : But provided, That before he shallhave power
to executeprocessesin civilcases,he shallexecutebond,
with approved security, in the Laurel county court, in
the same manner that constables do . He shall have all
power to levy,distrain, and sellproperty for alltaxes,
fines, assessments, or forfeituresor other dues of the
board of trustees, as sheriffs have for the collection of
county levy and State tax, and shall have the same
compensation or commission, and any additionalcom-
pensation which the board may allow; and said marshal
is hereby made the collector of the board of all taxes,
fines, and so forth, unless otherwise ordered by said
board ; and upon his failureto collectand pay over same
to those entitled, as above stated, he and his sureties
shall be liable in the same manner that constables of this
State are, and proceeded against in the same way . The
said marshal shallhave power to sellany realestateto
pay any town tax or assessment against its owners in
the same manner and on the same terms that sheriffs
have under the State law on executions,when he shall
be unable to findpersonalpropertyin saidtown to pay
same ; and shall return the authority under which he
made the levy and sale,with his return indorsed thereon ,
to the clerk of the circuitcourt of Laurel county , who
shall record same in the same manner that execution
sales are required to be recorded; and said clerk shallbe
entitled to the same fees as in other similar services .
Such real estate shall be subject to redemption in the
same manner and in the same time and on the same
terms as real estate sold under executions ; and if not
redeemed withinone year,shallbe conveyed to the pur-
chaser by the marshal in the same manner as real estate
is conveyed by the sheriffs; and for making said deed
the marshal shallhave a fee of two dollarsand fifty
cents of the purchase -price of said estate; and said mar-
shal shallhave the same power to levy, sell, and col

394 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Jectunder any execution that sheriffshave in thisState .
The marshal of said town may appoint a deputy , who
shallbe a residentof said town of London ,and the said
marshal shallbe responsiblefor the action of said dep-
uty; but said deputy shall have no jurisdictiononly in
cases of a violation of the by -laws or ordinances of said
town of London . It shallbe the duty of the marshal to
arrestallpersons who violateany of the by -laws of said
town , ordinances,or the charter of same , respecting the
peace and good order and tranquilityof said town or
any of itsinhabitants; and forthispurpose he may sum-
mons , when he deems necessary, any or all the male
persons of said town to aid him in making said arrests;
and any person or persons refusing to obey such sum .
mons shall be fined not less than two dollars nor more
than twenty dollars,to be recovered on warrant issued
by the chairman of the board of trustees or police judge
of said town . Said marshal shall, at all times , arrest
drunken and disorderly persons in said town without
waiting for warrant , and bring them before the police
judge , chairman of the board of trustees, or justice of
the peace ; and , in the event all are absent from said
town ,he shall have power ,and is hereby authorized, to
put them in jailuntilsober,and then bring them out for
trialbefore some peace officer; or said marshal , in the
absence of a peace officerto try any offender, may take
bond of said offenderfor his appearance for trialin the
same manner that bails are taken when warrants are
issued. The marshal shallattend the sittingsof the po-
licecourt, and perform allduties required of him by said
court or by the by -laws of the board of trustees. The
marshal ,John T. Hatcher ,now in office, shallhold said
office, with allpowers under this act,untilhis successor
is elected and qualified,but shallnot have civiljurisdic-
tion until he executes bond as herein stated ; and at any
time, if there is a vacancy in the officeof marshal ,for
any cause , the board are hereby authorized to appoint
untilthe next regular election; and when the appointee
has executedbond, as heretoforerequiredof elective
L

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 395
marshals , he shallhave allpower that one elected would
have .
§ 19. If at any time , from death , resignation, or other-
wise ,there are not as many as a quorum of three trustees
in officeand acting, it shallbe the duty of the judge of
thecounty courtto appoint at leasta quorum ,and when
qualifiedas herein required,by taking oath,shallhave
allpower as though elected. The board of trusteesshall
have power to appoint a marshal,who shallact as such
untilhis successor is elected and qualified.
§ 20. It shallbe the duty of the board of trustees, be-
fore any regular election, to appoint two judges and a
clerk to hold the election, who shall be sworn as now
required by law , who shallbe paid by the board the same
fees that are now paid officersfor holding State elections.
21. Itshallbe the duty of the secretary of the board
to have notice published one week beforehand of the
election of the board of trustees, police judge ,and mar-
shal; but a failure on the part of the secretary in this
respect shall in no way affectthe election if held, and
officers above are elected ; and , in the event there is no
board to appoint said officersof the election, the county
judge shallappoint them .
§22 . The board of trustees shallkeep the streetsand
alleys in good repair,and for that purpose may appro-
priate such money as taxes, fines, and so forth; and , in
addition to that,may work each and every male person
between the ages of sixteen and fiftysuch number of
days each as the board may deem proper in each year.
§ 23. To be in force from and afteritspassage .
Approved April 24 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 1017 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Beargrass Woolen Mills.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Charles Bonnycastle Robinson and William
Meade Robinson ,and their associates,successors, and as

396 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
signs,be,and are hereby,created a body -corporateand
politic, with perpetual succession, under the corporate
name of the " Beargrass Woolen Mills;" by which name
itmay sue and be sued ,contract and be contracted with ;
itmay have a seal,and alteror change the same at pleas-
ure; acquire, hold, and transferall real and personal
property necessary forthe conducting of itsbusiness, and
forsecuringdebts and liabilitiesto it as fullyas though
itwas a natural person ; and also shall have the rights,
powers ,and privilegesordinarilypertaining to corporate
bodies of like character. It may ,by its directors, make
all rules and by -laws necessary for the management of
its affairs,not inconsistent with the Constitution and laws
of this State or of the United States .
§2 . That the capital stock of this company shall be
one hundred thousand dollars,to be divided into shares
of one hundred dollars each ; and the stockholders may ,
whenever they deem itproper,increasesaid stock,from
time to time , until it shall reach two hundred and fifty
thousand dollars. The said company may carry on its
business whenever two hundred and fiftyshares of stock
have been subscribedand paid in,in cash,or such prop
erty,realand personal,at a fairvaluation,as the corpora-
tionmay requireforconductingitsbusiness. Certificates
of stock shallbe issued to each stockholder,signed by the
presidentand countersignedby the secretary,which shall
be transferableby the holder; but no such transfershall
be valid,exceptbetween thepartiesthereto,untilthe same
is regularlyentered upon the books of the company ,so
as to show the name of the persontowhom and by whom
made , the date of the transfer, and the number of the
shares transferred .
§3 . The businessof thiscompany shallbe conducted
by a board of not less than two nor more than seven
directors, to be chosen by the stockholdersfrom their
own number ,and who shallvacate theirofficeswhen they
ceaseto be stockholders. They shallhold officefortwo
years from the regular election,and untiltheirsuccessors,
duly chosen,shallenterupon the duties of theiroffices;

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 397
but whenever a majority of the newly -elected directors
shallenterupon theiroffices,then allthe members hold-
ing over shall cease to act. The directors shall have
power to fillany vacancy in the directoryuntilthe regu-
lar election. They shallappoint one of theirnumber
president and one vice president,and appoint a secretary
and a treasurer,combining any two or three of these offices
in one person ,if they think itdesirable; and may engage
such other subordinate officersas they may deem neces-
sary; and may fixthe salaries,and times and manner of
payment of all officers, and shall prescribe the duties of
all such ; but the directorsmay ,at any time ,remove any
officer for cause A majority of the directorsor stock-
holders shall constitute a quorum to transact business,
whenever a meeting of eitherisheld. The times of hold-
ing allelectionsshallbe fixedin the by -laws of the com-
pany , and each share of stock shallentitlethe holder to
one vote in all elections or matters submitted to a vote of
the stockholders, and all tievotes in the directoryshall
be so submitted .
§ 4. The business of this company shall be dealing in
wool and cotton and other textiles,and in manufacturing
their products, selling, buying , and trading in woolen ,
cotton ,and mixed yarns ,textilefabrics, and knit goods
of all kinds , and the material used in the construction of
the same ,and shall be carried on in or near Louisville,
Jefferson county ,Kentucky ; but itshallhave the right to
establish agencies and officeselsewhere to assistin carry-
ing out the object of the company .
§5 . The directoryshall declare and pay to the stock-
holders,from thenetprofitsof thebusiness,such dividends
or dividend as, in theirdiscretion,is wise and prudent .
§6 . The private property of the corporators and stock-
holders of thiscompany shallbe exempt from allliability
for the debts or liabilitiesof the company .
§ 7. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April25,1884.

398 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1018 .
AN ACT to incorporate the town of Woodbine , in Whitley county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Woodbine ,in Whitley county,and alsothat
part of said town that may be in Knox county,be,and
the same is hereby , incorporated, the limits of which
shallextend one mile every way from the present resi-
dence of William Sears .
§2. The prudential,fiscal, and municipalconcernsof
said town , with the government and control thereof, is
hereby and shall be vested in fivetrusteesnamed in this
act,and their successors, who shall be elected annually
on the firstSaturday in December ,by the legally quali
ified voters of said town . Said trustees, when elected ,
shallhold theirofficefor the term of one year , and until
their successors shall have been elected and qualified;
and when a vacancy shall occur from any cause , the
board of trustees shall have power to fillthe vacancy
until the next annual election . No trustee shall be under
twenty -one years of age ,and shall have been a resident
of said town not lessthan one year preceding his elec-
tion .
§3 . That the trusteesherein named , and theirsuc-
cessors, shallbe a body-politicand corporate,and shall
be known by the name and styleof " The Board of Trus-
tees of Woodbine , Kentucky ," and by that name shallbe
capable in law of contracting and being contracted with ,
suing and being sued , pleading and being impleaded , an-
swering and being answered , in all courts and places,
and in all matters and things whatsoever ,and with all
the powers and rightsincident tocorporate bodies having
perpetual succession; and may have and use a corporate
seal,and change , alter,and renew the same at pleasure .
§4 . That said trustees, aftertheir election and qualifi-
cation,shallelectone of theirnumber chairman ,who shall
presideover the deliberationsof said board when con-
vened , and shall have power to convene same when ,in
hisopinion,theinterestof thetown demands it. Itshall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 399
be hisduty to see that all the ordinances and by -laws of
said town are duly executed. They shallelectone of
theirnumber clerk,who shallkeep a true recordof all
the proceedings of the board ,which shall be open to the
inspection of any person desiring to see the same , and
perform such other services as the board may require.
They shallelectone of theirnumber treasurer,who shall
receive allmoneys collected, and pay the same as he may
be directed by the board . It shall be the duty of the
treasurer to render to said board ,at some regular meet-
ing in the months of April,August ,and December , and at
such other times as he may be directed by the board , an
account of all moneys received and paid out by him ,
showing when ,to whom , and for what purpose paid , and
from whom , when ,and on what account received; said
treasurershallexecute bond , with approved security, for
the faithfulperformance of hisduty , payable to the board
of trustees of the town of Woodbine and their successors
in office,in such penalty as said board may direct.
§5 . The regular meetings of said board shallbe on the
firstSaturday in each month , and called meetings may
be held when called by the chairman ,and a majority of
the board shallconstitutea quorum for the transaction of
business ; and all business transacted at a called meeting
shallbe as valid as if at a regular meeting ,provided three
of the trusteesare present.
§6 . That said trustees, or a majority of them , shall
have power to make or receive allnecessary conveyances
in relation to said town ; they shall have power to open
streets, alleys, and passways in said town , and have
power over same ; and may selland convey any lotsor
ground belonging to said town ,provided said sale shall
be approved by four of the trustees,and apply the pro-
ceeds thereof to the benefit of said town ; and they may
directthe improvement of the streetsand sidewalks as
they may deem most beneficial to the interestsof the
town ; they shallhave the power to levyand collectan
ad valorem tax on the real propertyin saidtown ,includ-
ing any and all real estate belonging to any railroad

400 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
company that may be in said town ,not exceeding fifty
cents on the one hundred dollars' in any one year ; and a
polltax on each male citizen over twenty -one years of
age , not exceeding two dollars in any one year ,provided
said male citizenshallremain , or shallhave remained , in
saidtown long enough to be a legalvoterthereof. They
shallhave power to tax auction sales,shows , and exhibi-
tionsfor money or profit,such sums as they may deem
proper and just, or as may ,from time to time, be fixedby
the by -laws or ordinances of the board .
§7 . They shallhave the right to tax, and the sole and
exclusive rightto license,alleys for nine or ten-pins,bil-
liards,Jenny Lind or other tables for recreationor amuse-
ment , and all other houses of publicresortwithin said
town , or within two miles thereof, except gambling
houses or houses of illfame ,and shall have the right to
fixthe tax thereon in any sum not exceeding one hundred
dollars. They shall have the sole and exclusive right to
licensepersons to sellspirituous,vinous , or malt liquors,
or any other intoxicating liquors or beverages , within
said town or within two miles thereof , and to tax the
same not less than two hundred nor more than five hun-
dred dollars; but they shall have no right to license any
person to so sellwithin said limits, when the sale of
either spirituous, vinous , or malt liquors, or any other
intoxicating liquors or beverages ,or allof same ,is pro-
hibited by law in Knox and Whitley counties,or either
of said counties,or prohibited by any local law or local
option law ,within the limitsof said town , or within any
votingprecinctwithinwhich any partof saidtown isin.
cluded : Provided, That no such license shall be granted
except at a regular meeting of said board , at which a
quorum of the trusteesshallbe present and vote to grant
said license,and then said license shall be approved by
the policejudge before any one shallbe authorized to sell
thereunder. No trusteeshallbe allowed to vote on any
question granting license if he has any interest,directly
or indirectly, in procuring said license,or is to be bene-
fitedin any way ,directlyor indirectly,by the same ; nor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 401
shall the police judge approve the license if he has , or
is to have , any interest,direct or indirect, contingent or
remote , in or under the same . And no person shall be
allowed to sellspirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors,or other
intoxicating liquors or beverages , within said town or
two miles thereof, unless licensed as hereinbefore stated;
and any one so sellingcontrary to the provisions of this
section shall be fined not less than fiftynor more than
one hundred dollarsfor each offense. It isprovided that
nothing in this act shall be so construed as to dispense
with any licenseor tax required by law to be paid into
the Treasury, or jury or other fund of the State; and
before granting any license the board shall be satisfied
that the applicant is of good moral character.
§ 8. Said trustees,before entering upon theirduties as
trustees,in addition to the oath prescribed by the Con-
stitution, shall take an oath that they will faithfully,
without partiality, favor, or affectionto any one , dis-
charge the duties of theirofficeas trusteesof the town
of Woodbine ,which oath may be administered by any
officerauthorized by law to administer oaths .
§ 9. That saidtrusteesshallhave the power to remove
any member continuouslyabsent from the meetings of
said board forfour months ,by three trusteesvoting there-
for; and shall have the authorityto remove the chair-
man ,clerk,treasurer,or streetoverseer,and electothers
to theplacesof thoseremoved ,when such actionmay be
deemed advisable .
§ 10. They shallhave the rightto electa streetover-
seer,and pay him such compensation as they may think
proper for such servicesas he may render; and ifthey
thinkproper,they may authorizethe town marshal or his
deputy , to perform such duties as may be required of a
streetoverseer,and pay him such compensation as they
may think proper for servicerendered by him . They
shallhave the authority to callout for labor on the streets
and alleys of said town ,or to construct walks ,culverts,
or bridgesin said town ,or to buildand keep in repair
-a. prison-house for said town , allthe able-bodied male
LOC . L.,VOL . II- 26

402 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
residents of said town ,between the ages of sixteen and
fiftyyears,not exceeding twelve days in each year; but
at least two days' previous written notice shall be given
of the time and place of working by the streetoverseer,
or the person acting as such overseer : Provided, That in
lieu of said work ,the sum of seventy- five cents shall be
accepted from each hand , for each day's work , if such
sum be tendered on or before the day of working , or
within fivedays thereafter; and each person required to
work may be allowed to furnish an acceptable substi-
tute . And any person who shallfailor refuse to labor,
or furnish a substitute,or tender the seventy-five cents
per day , shallbe fined one dollar and fiftycents for each
day he may have been notifiedto work ,to be recovered
in the police court of said town ,by warrant in the name
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,forthe use and benefit
of the trustees of Woodbine ; and if any person against
whom such a fine is assessed shall failto pay the same ,
it shall be lawful for the police judge of said town to
place him at labor on the streetsand alleys,or prison-
house , under direction of the town marshal or overseer
of the streets,to work out said fine and all costs, at the
rate of one dollar per day for each day's labor.
§ 11. That the trustees shall have authority to build
and erect a safe and commodious prison-house or lock-
up in said town ,of such form and dimensions as they
shalldeem advisable, which shallbe placed in charge of
the town marshal , and used for the confinement of all
offenderswho shallbe consigned thereto by the police
judge , and for the safe-keeping of such persons as may
be consigned to work on the streetsand alleys of said
town when not at work .
§ 12. In addition to all the powers now conferred by
law upon thetrusteesof towns,theyshallhave the power
to abate any and all nuisances in said town ; to remove
any dangerousorcombustiblematerialsfromsaidtown;
to remove ,and order to be removed ,alldefective chim-
neys and flues; to set out shade and ornamental trees,
build sidewalks and streetcrossings, and repair and keep

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 403
the same in order; to open new streetsand alleys, and
to purchase and condemn land for that purpose , and to
widen and extend streets and alleys, when necessary ;
to maintain , care for, and provide for all poor people
residing in said town ; and do all acts and things nec-
essary and expedient for the preservation of the health
and property and livesof the citizensof said town ,and
to protect them from abuse and insult of drunken or
other persons,when the acts of said board are not con-
traryto the Constitution and laws of this State and of
the United States .
§13 . That the marshal appointed by thisact,and his
successor, when elected and qualified,shallbe a conserv-
ator of the peace throughout the counties of Whitley and
Knox , and shall have power and authority to execute all
original, mesne , and finalprocess issued by the police
judge of Woodbine , or other tribunalor officer,as a con-
stable may now or hereafter have ,provided the said pro-
cess shall have been directed to him as the town marshal
of Woodbine ; in doing which ,he may go to any part of
Knox or Whitley counties,and shall be governed in the
service and execution of process by the same rules, regu
lations, and laws that now govern , or may hereafter gov-
ern, constables in the service and execution of process,
and shallbe alike liablefor property held by virtue of a
process , or for money collected by him . It shall be his
duty to serve allprocess and preceptsto him directed,
and make due return thereot ; collect all taxes of said
town ,executions,and otherdemands which may be put
in his hands to collect, and account for and pay over the
same to whomsoever may be entitledthereto, under the
same rulesand regulations required of sheriffsin the col-
lection of the revenue ,and constables in the collection of
executions and other demands ; he shall have the same
power , and be entitledto the same fees for collectingthe
town tax and levy,that sheriffsare forcollectingthe State
and county levy,and in allother cases the same fees al-
lowed constablesor sheriffsforserviceperformed in simi-
lar cases; he shallbe vested with allpower and authority

404 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
which is given constables or sheriffsin executing process
to them directed, and ,if need be ,to summon the aid of
Knox or Whitley in executing any process to him direct-
ed; and any person failingor refusing to aid him , when
so summoned in the county where the process is to be
executed, may be finedby the court, if sittingin said
county, not exceeding five dollars, and imprisoned not
exceeding six hours . Unless the trusteesotherwise direct,
he shallhave charge of the town prison or lock-up ; and
it shall be his duty to arrest, with or without a warrant ,
allriotous,drunken,or disorderlypersons in said town ,
and take such personsforthwithbeforethe policejudge
for trial. For the care,custody ,and control of prisoners
placed in his charge for confinement ,or for work on the
streetsand alleysof said town ,he shallbe allowed a rea-
sonable compensation by the board of trustees. He shall
pay monthly to the treasurerof the board of trusteesall
money due said board , and he shall make a settlement
with , and pay to the treasurer, all moneys in his hands
due the board, at any time demanded by the trustees.
Before entering upon his duties,he shalltake the usual
oath of office, and shall execute a special bond to the
board of trustees of Woodbine ,conditioned that he will
pay over, on theirdemand , all taxes,dues , and demands
listedwith him for collectionby said board . He shall
also execute in the Whitley county court clerk'soffice
such bond as is now required of constables; and when he
goes out of office, shall deliverto his successor such of-
ficialpapers as may need further officialaction. That
said marshal may appoint one regulardeputy marshal,
who shalltake an oath as required of the marshal .
§ 14. There shallbe a policecourt in saidtown ,to be
presided over by a policejudge , who shallhave the same
qualifications,be elected at the same time , and hold his
officefor the same period,as the members of the board
of trustees. He shallhave originaland exclusivejuris-
dictionof alloffensescommitted againstthe by-laws and
ordinances of said town , and shall have the same and
likejurisdictionin allciviland criminalactionsor pro

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 405
ceedingsin Whitley county that the county judge of said
county now has , except where said judge has by law ex-
clusivejurisdiction; and shalltake such oath of officeand
givesuch bond as isnow requiredby law ofpolicejudges
of towns . He shall have power to hear and determine ,
in a summary manner ,all offenses against the by -laws
and ordinancesof saidtown : Provided, That ifthe pen-
alty for violation of such by -law or ordinance exceed.
twenty dollars, he shall cause a jury to intervene,pro-
vided one is demanded , but not otherwise .
§ 15. If any person against whom a penalty or fine is
assessedin said court shallfailto pay the same ,together
with the costsof the proceedings,saidpolicejudge shall
have authority to direct the confinement of such person
in the town prison-house or lock-up , or work him upon
the streetsuntilsaid fine and costs be paid , at the rate of
one dollar per day for each day confined or worked . He
shallhave power and authority to compel witnesses to
attend and testifyin causes pending before him ,or to give
theirdepositions, and to enter judgments and award ex-
ecutions accordingly, and to fine and imprison for con-
tempt : Provided, The fine in no case shall exceed five
dollars, and the imprisonment not longer than twenty-
four hours. He shallhave power and authority to grant
injunctions, attachments , restraining, orders,writs of ne
exeat and habeas corpus, and receive returns of the same ..
He shallkeep his own records,and shallbe subjectto
the same penaltiesimposed upon justicesof the peace for
violationof duty; he shallkeep a docket of causesin the
order in which they are tried,showing the various steps
taken therein,the judgment ,issual, and return of execu-
tions,and shallsafelykeep allpapers in every case tried
by him ,and make and keep a complete index to his rec-
ords and execution book ; he shall be allowed the same
