CHAPTER 1040 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Pretty Run Turnpike Road Company , in Clark
and Bourbon counties .
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company be formed and created a body-
corporateand politic,by the name and styleof the Pretty
Run Turnpike Road Company ,for the purpose of build-
ing a turnpike road , beginning at the Winchester and
Paris Turnpike Road , on the line between Edwin P.
Bean and James H. Bean , deceased ; running said line
along a lane; continuing along said lane to a point on
the line between James D. Gay and Thornton Lewis ,
deceased ; thence through saidGay's farm to John Evans '
farm; through said farm to dirt road ; thence to some
convenient point on North Middletown and Thatcher's
MillTurnpike Road .
§2 . That the capital stock of said company may be
one thousand dollars, to be increased or diminished as
the company may desire, to be divided into shares of
fiftydollarseach .
§3 . The books for the subscription of stock shall be
opened by James D. Gay , Charles Swift, Ed . P. Bean ,
Nelson P. Gay ,Samuel McDaniel , and John Evans ,who
are hereby appointed commissionersforsaid purpose, at
such times and places as they may deem proper . When
the sum of six hundred dollars shall have been subscribed
to purpose aforesaid,itshallbe the duty of the commis-
sionersmentioned in thissection,or a majority of them ,
togive notice,in such manner as they may thinkproper,
for the meeting of the stockholders, at such time and
place as the noticemay specify,fortheelectionof a pres-
ident and two or three directors . One vote shall be
allowed for each share of stock, and the president and
directorsshall remain in officeone year , and tilltheir
successors are qualified. The times and places of all
electionsshall, afterthe first,be fixedby the president
and directors; and the majority of the board shall be
competent to transact business.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 457
§4. So soon as saidcompany isorganizedby the elec-
tion of officers, the presidentand directorsshallbe a
body -politicand corporate, in fact and in law ,under the
name and styleof the PrettyRun Turnpike Road Com-
pany; and by that name they shallhave power to con-
tractand be contractedwith,to sue and be sued,plead
and be impleaded , answer and be answered ,in any court
of law , equity, or elsewhere ; and do all other lawful
things to carryout the purpose forwhich thiscorpora-
tion was created .
§ 5. Such president and directorsshallfix and regulate
the elevation and grade of said road ,width and the part
thereof to be covered with stone or gravel ; and may fix
the rates of tolls,not to exceed the rates now fixed by
law .
§6 . The president and directors, with their surveyor ,
and so forth,are hereby empowered to enter upon the
lands and inclosures, and upon the highways through
and over which thisroad may pass,and to examine the
ground most suitablefor theirpurpose; and may exam-
ine quarries,gravel beds , and so forth, necessary for the
construction of said road .
$ 7 . It shall be lawful for the president and directors,
with theirworkmen ,tools, wagons , beasts of draught , to
enter into the land in or contiguous and near to which
said road may run ,having firstgiven notice to the owner
or their agents : Provided, The president and directors
shall not agree with the owners of the land through
which said road is to pass as to damages which said
owners are to sustain by the road running through their
lands, the presidentand directorsshall apply to the
county court of the county in which theirlands are sit-
uated for a writ of ad quod damnum ,to assessthe dam-
ages which the owners of said lands may sustainin
consequence of the road having passed over the lands,or
of any stone, gravel, land for toll-gate house, or other
thing taken from such owner by reason thereof.
§8 . The president shallgive notice,in such manner as
he may deem proper,the amount of callon each share

458 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of stock,and of the time of payment ; and ifany person
shallfailto pay his amount of stockfor the space of
thirtydays afterthe payment thereofis demanded of
him ,he shallpay intereston said callat the rate of eight
per centum per annum untilpaid .
§ 9. The counties of Clark and Bourbon may subscribe
for stock in said company ,regardless of the distance it
may be constructed in eithercounty .
§ 10. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 28 , 1884.
CHAPTER 1041 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Motier and Peach Grove Turnpike Road Com .
pany , in Pendleton county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company may be formed,under the name
and styleof the Motier and Peach Grove Turnpike Road
Company ,in Pendleton county, for the purpose of con-
structinga turnpikeroad from Motier to the Campbell
county line, said road to be located as agreed upon by
the board of directors .
§2 . That the capital stock of said company shallnot
exceed fifteenthousand dollars, and shall be divided into
sharesof twenty-fivedollars.
§3 . That J. H. Comes ,S , G. Ware , and Wm . Garvey
are appointed commissioners,whose duty it shallbe to
procurea book,in which the subscribersof stock in said
company shallenterintothe followingobligation: " We ,
whose names are hereto subscribed , do bind ourselves to
pay to the Motier and Peach Grove Turnpike Road Com-
pany the sums of money set opposite our names ,in such
proportionand at such time as shallbe requiredby the
president and directorsof said company ."
§4 . That as soon as a sufficientamount of stock has
been subscribed to build one mile of said road ,itshallbe
the duty of said commissioners to give notice of a meet-
ing of the stockholdersof said company at some con

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 459
venientpointforthe purpose of choosingofficersforsaid
company ,which shallbe given by noticesposted up at
three of the most publicplaces along the line of said
contemplated road ,for at least fivedays previous to the
meeting ; and at said meeting at least two of said com-
missionersmust be present and superintendthe election.
§5 . There shallbe elected fivedirectors,who shallhold
their officefor one year from the day of election, and
untiltheirsuccessorsare duly elected and qualified. The
said board of directors shallelecta president and treas
urer out of their number , and such other officersas they
may deem necessary. The said treasurer, before enter-
ing upon the discharge of his duties,shall execute bond ,
with good security,to be approved by the directory, for
thefaithfulperformance of saidoffice.
6. That the president and directors shall severally
take an oath faithfullyto discharge the duties of their
respectiveoffices.
§7 . That said road shall be opened according to the
county specifications; that when one mile of said road is
completed the company may erect a gate on the same ,
and appoint a gate -keeper to serve at their pleasure,and
shall have the right to collect at said gate tollson the
travel on said road at rates per mile not exceeding the
rates now authorized to be charged by the General Stat-
utes .
§8 . That they shallhave power to acquire,hold ,and
use, for the purpose of carryingout the objectsof the
corporation,such real and personalpropertyas may be
necessaryto constructand operatesaidroad.
§ 9. That the privatepropertyof the members of the
corporationshallnot be liablefor corporatedebts.
§10 . The stockholders shall be members of the corpo-
ration , and entitled to one vote in the election of direct-
ors for each share of stock held,and may cast said votes
by written proxy .
§ 11. That no person shall be eligiblefor director in
said company who is not a stockholder in the same . The
board shall have power to fillall vacancies that may

460 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
occur in their body , and the electionfor directors after
the firstelectionshallbe on the firstSaturday in May .
§ 12. That said company may sue and be sued ,plead
and be impleaded ,contract and be contracted with , and
in alllitigationshallbe dealt with as natural persons.
§ 13. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 28, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1042 .
AN ACT to prevent obstructionsof turnpike roads in Anderson county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any officerof any
turnpike board in Anderson county to allow any person or
personsemployed forthe purpose of haulingor breaking
metal for the repair of any turnpike road in said county ,
to allow any person or persons to leave to be broken , or

CHAPTER 1044 .
AN ACT for the benefit of Y. L. Ford , collector of State revenue for the
county of Daviess for the year one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
three .
WHEREAS , The Auditor of Public Accounts did,on the
eleventh day of December ,one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-three, appoint Y. L. Ford collectorof the
State revenue for the county of Daviess for the year one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-three; and whereas ,
by reason of the lateness of said appointment , together
with the high water thathave floodeda great portionof
said county since said appointment ,said Ford has not
been able to collect alf of the revenue due from said
county for the year one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-three; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Y. L. Ford , collectorof State revenue for the
county of Daviess forthe year one thousand eighthun-
dred and eighty-three, have the further time until the
firstday of June , one thousand eight hundred and eigh-
ty-four, in which to collect and pay into the Treasury
the revenue due from Daviess county for the year one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-three : Provided, The
suretiesof said Ford will,on orbefore the firstday of
May , enter their consent in open court in the Daviess
county court to the extension of the time herein given
said Ford to pay said revenue, which consent shallbe
entered of record in said court,and certifiedto the Aud-
itor of Public Accounts .
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved April 28, 1884 .

vious to the day of election.

Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCommonwealth

of Georgetown , the capital stock of which shall not be

in created, who , or a majority of whom , may meet at

town , of which one month's previous' notice shallbe

tions of stock to said corporation.

sionersshallgive preferenceto the stockholdersof the

Farmers ' Bank of Kentucky until three hundred and fif-
ty -nine thousand dollars' worth of stock is subscribed :

-ersshallconstitutea body -politicand corporate, with the

years ,and may contract and be contracted with,sue and
be sued ,plead and be impleaded ,and may exercise all

closed,to the president and directorsof the Georgetown
Branch of the Farmers ' Bank of Kentucky , who are
hereby constitutedand appointed directorsof the Farm-
ers' Bank of Georgetown ,untilthe firstSaturday in May
next ensuing ,and until their successors shallbe elected.
An annual meeting shall be held by the stockholdersat
theirbanking-house in Georgetown ,on the firstSaturday
in May ,at which seven directorsshallbe elected, who
shallhold theirofficefor one year and untiltheirsuccess-
orsshallbe elected. Meetings of the stockholdersmay be
-calledat any time by a majority of the directors. In all
meetings of the stockholders each person shall be en-
titledto one vote for every share of stockheld by him .
The directors may choose one of their number as their
presidentand fixhis compensation. May appointa sec-
retaryand such other officersas they may require, fix
theircompensation ,prescribe theirduties, and take bond
and suretyfor the faithfuldischarge thereof; may pre-
scribethe manner and times of paying in the stock,and
shall have the general control of the affairsof the bank .

472 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
No person shall be a director who is not a stockholder.
The directorsshall annually appoint two or more stock-
holders to attend and hold the annual election. At any
meeting of the stockholdersa committee may be ap-
pointed ,whose duty it shall be to examine into the con-
dition of the bank ,and make report on same ,which shall
be subject at alltimes to the inspectionof allthe stockhold-
ers. Stock shallbe voted in person ,or by written proxy
to a stockholdernot an officer, and it shallrequire a ma-
jorityof the stock present and voting to carry any prop--
osition .
§6 . The business of said bank shall be to receive
money and bank notes on deposit, and may allow the
depositorssuch interestthereon as may be agreed upon ,
not exceeding the lawful rate of interest; to deal in gold
and silvercoin,bullion,billsof exchange ,and promissory
notes; to buy and sellthe stock of other companies , and
the bonds of this State and of the United States ; but it
shallnot issuenotes or bills,or certificatesof deposit, or

dorsers thereon .

clare dividends of the profits from the business of the

and be refusedpayment thereof on demand made for
the same according to the terms of deposit, and without
lawful excuse ,said bank shall pay to the depositor the
amount of his or her deposit,and fifteenper cent. dam-
ages on the amount thereof, to be recovered by action in
the Scott circuit court .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 473 .
11. Said bank may purchase and hold,selland con-
vey , any real or personal estate which may be necessary

of the bank to the extent of stock owned and held by

amine into the condition of affairsof the bank by any

town , Kentucky .

on cach share of stock subscribed, which shall be in full

thereof,shallbe sentencedto confinementin the peniten-
tiary of the State for a period of not less than two nor

take an oath faithfullyand impartiallyto dischargethe
duties of theirrespectiveofficesunder thischarter.
§19 . This act shallbe in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 28 , 1884 .

he shallexecute a bond to the company ,with sureties,

will faithfullyperform all the duties of treasurer, and
pay over as directedby the board any money in his
hands belonging to the company , and will make fullset-
tlement of his accounts , when required by said board ;

476 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and on going out of officewill deliverto his successor
all books ,papers ,property,money ,or other thing in his
hands belonging to said company . This bond shall be
recordedin his record-book,and preservedby the secre-
tary .
§ 8. That said company shallhave the right to estab-..
lish toll-gates on said road , and charge and collecttolls
as authorized by the General Statutes.
§9 . That the capital stock of said company shall be
divided into shares offifty dollars or twenty-fivedollars
each , as the firstboard of directors elected hereunder
shall determine , which determination shall be entered on
their record -book .
§ 10. That said board of directorsshallhave power to
make allby -laws ,rules and regulations, for the govern-
ment of themselves and officers and affairs of said com-
pany ,not inconsistentwith public law .
§ 11. That at any general electionthe stockholders may
change the time of holding the regular election for di
rectors .
§ 12. That Judge James T. Woolfolk , Dan . Roberts ,
Green Craycroft, Ben . Clarkson , and S. M. Wrather are
hereby made commissioners to organize said company ;
and they , or any of them choosing to act, may open
books for the subscription of stock,call an election for
the election of the firstboard of directors,and do such
other acts and things as may be necessary to completely
organize said company .
§ 13. The Meade county court of claims,a majority of
the justicesthereof being present and concurring therein,
may subscribe not more than seven hundred and fiftydol-
lars of stock to each mile of said turnpike road ; and for
the purpose of paying the same ,said court of claims may
make a levy upon the taxable property in said county .
§14 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and .
afteritspassage .
Approved April 28, 1884...

CHAPTER 1054 .

ston county court,to borrow money to pay for building a bridge in said

creek,at or near Carrsville,in said county ,not to exceed
fivethousand dollarsin value ; and whereas , the present
General Assembly has ,by its act, authorized said county

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 481
eight hundred and eighty-four, or any subsequent year ,

CHAPTER 1063 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Pineville,Barbourville,and Woodbine Turn-
pike Road Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That a company be,and ishereby,incorporated,for
the purpose of building a turnpike road from Pineville, by
way of Barbourville,to Woodbine ,or some other point,
one or both,on the Knoxville Branch of the Louisville
and Nashville Railroad ,under the name and styleof the
Pineville, Barbourville, and Woodbine Turnpike Road
Company ; and by that name and styleshallhave per-
petualsuccession,sue and be sued,and be a body-politic
and corporate.
§2 . That the capital stock of said company shall be
dividedintosharesof twenty-fivedollarseach,and the
capitalstock shall not exceed seventy-fivethousand dol-
lars .
§3 . That George M. Adams , John H. Wilson ,James
T. Gibson , John D. Jarvis, and J. M. Unthank , be ,and
they are hereby , appointed commissioners to open books
and receivesubscriptionsat such times and places as

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 455
any three or more of said commissioners may deem ex-
pedient; and when five thousand dollars shallhave been
-subscribed to said capital stock, said commissioners , or
any threeof them ,shallcalla meeting of the stockhold-
ers by a general public notice forten days before the time
of meeting ,at a time and place to be specified in the
notice,when the stockholdersshallproceed,inperson or
by writtenproxy,to choose a presidentand fivedirectors,
in whom and their successors shall be vested all the cor-
porate powers herein granted ; in which voting or elec-
tion every stockholder shall be entitled to one vote for
each share owned by him .
§ 4. Afterthe board isthus organized,an annual elec-
tion of president and directors shall be held,each and
every year ,on the firstMonday in May ,in the manner as
hereinbefore indicated , the said officers to hold their
officesuntil their successorsare lawfullyelectedand
qualified; and no person shall be eligibleto the officeof
president or director unless he be the owner , in his own
right,of stock in said company .
§5 . That said company may constructsaidroad, or
have it constructedby contractwith other parties,in a
manner deemed best by said company ; and should the
-company constructthe road,they may provide thatstock
may be paid for in work in the construction of said road
in such manner as may be agreed upon ; and as soon as
five thousand dollars shall have been subscribed to the
stockof said company ,and the company organized,the
construction of said road may be commenced ; and they
may determine at what point on said road work shall
begin ; and the number of gates and rates of tollshallbe
the same as is provided by the general laws on the sub-
ject, except that as soon as three miles of said road shall
have been completed , the directors are authorized to
erecta toll-gate acrosssaid road,at any pointthey may
select,and collectfulltollfrom all persons traveling on
said road as now ,or as may hereafterbe,provided by
law .
§6 . The presidentand directorsshallappoint one of
theirnumber treasurer,who shallexecutebond,with ap

486 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
proved security,and be responsibleforallthe company's
moneys received by him , and shall pay out the same as
provided by the president and directors, and keep an ac-
count of all his transactions ; and the board of directors
shallkeep a fairrecord of theirproceedings , which at all
times shallbe open to the inspection of stockholders.
§7 . That said board of directorsshallhave allthe rights,
powers ,and privileges, and may do allacts and things
necessary for letting, contracting,completing ,and man-
aging said turnpike road , and may locate the same , and
determine itswidth and grade ,on such route or routes as
theydeem proper,subjectto sectionone of thisact.
§ 8. And all stock subscribed shall be paid at such
times and in such manner as may be indicated by the
board of directors .
§9 . That the directors of said company shall have
power to employ allneedful superintendentsand engi-
neers,and make surveys, and order any routes deemed
expedient by them ,and acquire by contract the right of
way forsaidroad,and the materialforbuildingthesame;
and in case a satisfactoryagreement cannot be made ,
theymay apply for a writ of ad quoddamnum ,and have
lands, gravel beds,quarries, and timber condemned ac-
cordingtothe generallaws upon the subject. They shall
have power to enter upon any lands to survey out said
road , and to examine any gravel pits, or quarries,or tim-
ber necessary for the constructing or repairing of said
road. And they shall have the right to use any county
or State road forthe purpose of constructing said turn-
pike and itsbranches ,and said State or county road may
be used by said company as the road-bed for any turnpike
made by them ,and to condemn the same a writ of ad quod
damnum shallnot be necessary.
§10 . That ifany person shallgo around any toll-gate
on said road with intent to avoid the payment of toll,
he shallbe fined ten dollars,to be recovered by a war-
rant before any justiceof the peace,forthe benefitof
said company .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 487
§ 11. That the directorsshallhave allthe powers and
privilegesallowed turnpikeroad companies by law that
may be necessaryto enable them to constructand oper-
ate said road .
§12. This act shall take effectfrom its passage .
Approved April 28, 1884.
CHAPTER 1064 .
AN ACT to incorporateAdvance Seminary .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an institutionof learning be ,and the same
ishereby , incorporated and establishedat Medina , John-
son county, Kentucky , to be known by the name of

pal .

dents amenable for their moral conduct during the entire
term forwhich theirnames are registeredishereby recog-
nized .

afteritspassage .
Approved April 28, 1884 .

488 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1065 ,
AN ACT to repeal the charterof the town of Uniontown ,and allamend-
ments thereto, and to re-incorporatethe same .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That all acts and parts of acts incorporating the
town of Uniontown ,or establishingor regulating a police
court therein,be , and the same are hereby , repealed, and
the said town is hereby re-incorporated : Provided,how
ever, That this act shallin nowise affectthe property or
rightsof individualsof saidtown ,or the liabilitiesof said
town as a corporation; but allrights and liabilitiesshall
remain as if thisact had not been passed .
§ 2. The boundaries of said town shall be as follows :
Beginning on the Ohio river at low -water mark , at a
stake twenty-fivefeet below the mouth of the slough
immediately below the Funk pork-house property, and
running thence south fifty-five degrees ,east far enough
to intersect the line of Seventh street furthest from the
river; thence in a northeast direction with the line of,
and including Seventh street, to the eastern corner of
block thirty-seven ; thence in a direct line to the corner
of lot number two hundred and two ,the corner of Fran-
cisburg; thencein a northeasterndirectionwith the line
of Francisburg far enough to include out-lots numbers
-- ; thence with the line of said out-lotsto Highland
creek; thence with Highland creek to low -water mark to
the beginning .
§3 . The board of councilmen now in officeshall re-
main in office,and may exercise all the powers granted
to the board of councilmen of said town by thisact, and
shallbe subject to allliabilitiesand discharge allthe du-
ties imposed by this act, in the same manner as if they
had been elected under it, until their successors are elect-
ed and qualified.
§4. The fiscal,prudential,and municipal affairsof said
town shall hereafter be vested in seven councilmen , who
shall be styled the board of councilmen of Uniontown ,
all of whom shall be elected at the same time ; and an

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 489
electionof councilmen shall be had on the firstMonday
in August , one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four ,
and every two yearsthereafter,by the qualifiedvotersof
said town who shall have resided therein not less than
six months before such election: Provided,however, That
no person shall be allowed to vote at any election for
councilmen or other town officerswho has not paid all
town taxes againsthim or hisproperty. No person shall
be eligibleto officeas councilman of said town unless he
has resided in said town for one year immediately pre-
ceding his election, and is a housekeeper , and above
twenty -one years of age , and the owner of real estate in
his own right, situated in said town ; nor shallany per-
son be eligibleto the officeof councilman who is a vend-
or of spirituous,vinous , or malt liquors,in quantities less
than a quart, under a licensefrom the authoritiesof said
town . The councilmen shall hold their offices for two
years from their election, and until their successors are
electedand qualified. Beforeproceedingto act as such,
the councilmen shalltake the oaths prescribedby the
Constitutionand laws of thisState, which may be ad-
ministeredby any justiceofthe peace,policejudge,clerk
of a court,or notarypublic in Union county. The offi-
cer administering the oath shallcertifythe same to the
board of councilmen ,and such certificateshallbe spread
upon the recordsof the board. The board of council-
men of saidtown shallbe a body-politicand corporate,
known and styledas above specified; and by thatname
may contractand be contractedwith,may sue and be
sued,and may have and use a common seal,and may
alter the same at pleasure. They may take and hold
real and personalpropertyby purchase,devise,bequest,
or donation , for the use and benefit of said town , and
may lease or selland convey the same , and may lease or
sell and convey any property, real or personal , now
owned by them or said town : Provided, however, That
they shall not lease or sellany real estate except on an
affirmative vote of not less than five councilmen ,and the
names and votes of those voting for and against the lease
or saleshallbe enteredupon the recordsof the board.

490 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§5 . The board of councilmen , at their firstmeeting
afterthey are qualified, shallorganize by electing one of
theirnumber president of the board for the term forwhich
the councilmen are elected. In case a vacancy shall
occur in the board of councilmen ,they may fillsuch va-
cancy by appointment for the remainder of the term ; and
should a vacancy occur in any other officein the town ,
eitherby failureto elect or otherwise,they may fillsuch
vacancy in the same manner and for the same term as in
case of councilmen . They shallannually appoint a clerk
of the board ,who may be one of the board elected or
not as they may see proper , and shallannually appoint a
town treasurer. They shallrequire from the treasurer of
the town a bond , with good and sufficientsurety,condi-
tionedforthefaithfulperformance of his dutiesas treas-
urer, and forthe accounting for and paying over,accord-
ing to the orders of the board of councilmen , allmoney ,
securities, or other property, or evidences of debt, that
may come to his hands belonging to the town . Said
bond shall be executed to the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky,and may be sued upon by the board of council-
men ,or by any person injured by a breach of its condi-
tions, or any of them , and it shall be approved by the
board of councilmen .
§6 . The board of councilmen may fixby theirby -laws
the time and place of regular meetings ,and the mode of
calling special meetings ; a majority of the councilmen
shallconstitutea quorum for the transaction of business,
but a less number may ,by an order entered of record,
compel the attendance of a quorum at any regular meet-
ing; and in the absence of the president those present
may electa presidentprotem. The officerpresidingshall
sign the minutes of the preceding meeting ,when approved
by the board , and shall put all questions and preserve
order, but shall not vote on any proposition, except in
case of a tie vote . The board of councilmen shall have
power to provide by by -laws for the punishment of any
one guilty of disorderly conduct in their presence ; and
shallhave power to provide forthe government of their

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 491
own body by by-laws; and they may compel the attend-
ance before them of any officerof said town .
§7 . The titleto the streetsand alleys, and to allpublic
grounds in said town ,shall be , and is hereby , vested in
the board of councilmen of said town , and their success-
ors; and they may maintain any appropriateaction to
prevent any obstructionof or damage to said streets,
alleys, or public grounds , shade trees, or other property
belonging to said town ; and may recover damages for
any injury to any of said streets, alleys,public grounds ,
or othertown property. The said board of councilmen
shall have power to build and maintain a town prison
and work -house ; they shallhave power to licensesaloons
and coffee-houses,with the privilegeof sellingspirituous,
vinous,or malt liquorsby the drink,and requiretavern-
keepers to pay a tax to the town , the same as saloons
and coffee-houses ; and to licensemerchants to sellspirit-
uous ,vinous ,or malt liquorsin quantitiesnot lessthan a
quart ,and not to be drunk on or about the premises ; to
licensewagons , hacks, or othervehiclesplying in said
town forhire,other than those run by keepers of licensed
livery-stables; to licenselivery-stables,auctioneers,butch-
ers, peddlers,store-boats, other than those sellinglime,
coal, salt, or stoneware to dealers; to licensemenageries ,
circuses, shows , and other exhibitions to which an admis-
sion fee ischarged ; to license billiardtables,pool tables,
pigeon tables, ten-pin alleys, shootinggalleries, and all
other places or contrivances of amusement kept or run
for hire; and the power to license wharves and docks.
The board of councilmen shall have the power to re-
quire the payment of a tax for each license issued or
granted , and to prescribe by ordinance the amount of
tax for each license. Licenses to auctioneers,peddlers,
menageries ,circuses , shows , and exhibitions ,and to store-
boats,may be for any length of time not less than one
day nor more than one year ; all other licenses shall be
for one year,and no licenseissuedor granted by the
authorities of said town shall be transferable . The board
of councilmen shall have the power to make rules for

492 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the government and regulation of such saloons, coffee-
houses,taverns,and otherplaceswhere spirituous,vinous,
or malt liquors are sold or kept for sale,billiardtables,
pool tables, pigeon tables, tenpin alleys, shooting-gal-
leries, or other places or contrivances of amusement ;
and said board of councilmen shall make ordinances,
with adequate penalties for the violation thereof,for the
punishment of any one engaging in any business or
doing any act for which a license is required, without
firsthaving obtained license therefor,and paying the tax
required forthe same ; and for the punishment of any one
guiltyof any violationof the rulesprescribedby virtue
of the powers given in thissection.
§ 8. In addition to the powers given in the preceding
section,the board of councilmen shall have power to re-
quire,by ordinance,allsaloons,coffee-houses,and other
placeswhere spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsare kept
for saleby the drink,to be closedand kept closed,and
no liquorsof any kind to be soldor otherwisedisposedof
therefrom or drank therein,between the hours of twelve
o'clockmidnight and five o'clockin the forenoon ,and on
the Sabbath day,electiondays,and on such otherpublic
days as may be required by ordinance .
§9 . Any person doing business in said town under a
license from the town or the authorities thereof ,who shall
be convicted of any violationof any of the rules or ordi-
nances passed or prescribed by the board of councilmen
in pursuance of the powers granted in the two sections
next preceding ,shallhave his licensesuspended untilthe
fine and costs are paid or replevied; and the court shall
include such suspension as a part of the judgment of
conviction; and upon a second conviction for the same
offense,the court shall,in addition to the fine or impris-
onment ,or both,which may be imposed,adjudge the li-
cense of the offenderforfeited,and licenseshallnot again
be granted to the person convicted for twelve months
thereafter .
§ 10. The board of councilmen of said town shall be
constituteda board of supervisorsof the assessor'sbooks

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 493
of said town ; but they may ,ifthey see proper to do so,
by ordinance , empower the president of the board of
councilmen to appoint a board of three supervisorsfrom
the citizens of the town ,who shall act as such ,instead
of the board of councilmen . The board of supervisors
shall examine the assessments returned by the assessor,
and ,upon notice to the parties affected, may correct the
valuation in any list, when satisfiedsuch valuation is
eithertoo high or too low ; and theirreport shallbe made
and returned to the clerk of the board of councilmen
within two weeks from the time said listis placed in
their hands .
§ 11. The board of councilmen shall have the control
and management of the town cemetery , and the power
to keep the same in repair,and to ornament and improve
itat theirdiscretion; to employ a sexton , and to selland
convey lotsto privatepartiesfor burialpurposes; they
shall provide,by ordinance ,for the punishment of tres-
passers on the same ,or any injury to any monument ,tree,
or shrub therein,or to any inclosure in or around said
cemetery .
§ 12. The board of councilmen shallhave power to
prescribethe placesand manner in which powder ,ben-
zine,and coaloilshallbe storedand sold,and to require
that allchimneys and fluesbe kept in good order, and to
requirethe owner or user of such chimneys or fluesto
repairthe same when necessary; and, upon his or her
failure to do so in ten days after notice, to cause such
repairs to be made at the cost of such owner or user;
and forthispurpose shallhave ingressand egressto and
from allbuildingsthe fluesor chimneys of which have
been ordered to be repaired. The board of councilmen
shallhave a lien upon the premises for the costs of any
repairs made to such flueor chimney ,and by appropriate
action may recover such costs and enforce said lien.
§13 . The board of councilmen shall have power to
investigate complaints of nuisances within said town ,
whether erected or about to be erected , remaining or
permitted to remain,and proving or about to prove, a

494 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
nuisanceto adjacent property-owners or to personsliv-
ingthereon,or to the public. Upon complaint made by
any person,itshallbe the duty of saidboard of council-
men to investigate the matter complained of, firstgiving
reasonable notice to the parties owning , controlling, or
occupying the premises upon which the matter of com-
plaint is situated; and if, upon proof, such things are
proven to be nuisances,they shallso declarethem ,and
order the same to be removed or abated , under such pen-
alty as may be prescribed by ordinance . The board of
councilmen shall, through the marshal of the town ,serve
a written notice on the owners or persons in possession
of property upon which a nuisance is declared to exist,
to remove or abate such nuisance within ten days after
such notice ; and in case of failure to abate or remove
the same according to notice,the marshal shall remove
or abate the same ,or cause it to be done , at the expense
of the owner ,tenant,or person in possession of the prem-
ises; and in the event of the failureor refusalof such per-
son or persons to pay the expenses of removal or abate-
ment ,then the board of councilmen shall,by suitin the
policecourt of Uniontown , recoversaid expenses and
costsof the suit,forwhich eithera fierifaciasor capias
pro finemay issue.
§ 14. The board of councilmen shall have power to
provideby ordinancefor the punishment of indecentor
disorderly conduct , drunkenness , gaming , fast riding or
driving,obstructingstreets,alleys,or sidewalks,injuryto
wells, cisterns, streets, alleys, sidewalks , sewers , shade
trees, or other property belonging to said town or the
board of councilmen thereof; breaches of the peace ; sell-
ing,orkeeping,orofferingforsaleany diseasedor spoiled
meats ,or spoiled vegetables, milk,or other articleof food ;
keepersof or inmates of bawdy -houses,or houses of ill-
fame; firingRoman candles, sky-rockets, fire-crackers,
fire-arms ,or other explosives; profane swearing on the
streets; crueltyto animals; unlawfullyinjuring,defacing,
or removing signs,posters,or advertisements; disturbing
religiousworship , or other lawful assemblies; carrying

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 495
concealed,on or about the person, any deadly weapon .
otherthan an ordinarypocket knife- and any pistolshall
be considered a deadly weapon - without proving thatfact;
and they shallhave power to provide by ordinance forthe
suppressionand punishment of any and allotherirregu-
laritiesor disorders that may be prejudicialto the peace
and good order of said town , and for the enforcement of
regulations necessary or proper for the securityof the
health of the citizensthereof, or the safety of property in
said town , not contrary to the Constitutionor laws of this
State .
§ 15. The board of councilmen shallhave power to open
any new streetor alley,or to open ,straighten,or widen any
old ones,when they may deem itnecessary or proper,and if
they shallbe unable to agree with the owner thereof as to
the land required,or the compensation therefor,the same
may be condemned in the same manner as lands are now
authorized to be condemned by the general laws of this
State. Upon payment by the board of councilmen of
the value and damages assessed,the legal titleto the land
condemned shall vest in the board of councilmen and
their successors . But if they shall deem the damages
assessed more than it would be expedient to pay , they
may abandon the proceedings, being responsiblein that
case only for costs; and upon their abandoning the pro-
ceeding the court shall enter an order annulling the order
condemning the ground .
§ 16. The board of councilmen shall have power to
compel the property-owners of said town to make suit-
able pavements and curbing , of either stone, brick, or
wood ,as the board of councilmen may direct,on the side-
walks in front of theirproperty along the streetsof said
town ,whenever the board may deem it necessary and
proper for the benefitof said town , or whenever the
streetsadjacent to such propertyhave been graded or
paved ; and in the event that any owner of said prop-
erty refuse or failto have the said sidewalks curbed and
paved ,as directedby the board of councilmen , the board
shallhave power to have the same done at the expense

496 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of the owner or owners of such property; and the board
of councilmen , and theirsuccessorsin office,shallhave a
lienon saidpropertyfor the payment of said expense,
with ten per centum per annum interestthereon , from
the payment of said expense by said board untilpaid
by the owner or owners of said property,and for costs;
and forthe purpose of subjectingthe saidpropertyto the
payment of said expense , interest, and costs,the board
of councilmen may sue such property-owner or owners
in any court of competent jurisdiction,and by a judg-
ment of said court have said property, or enough thereof,
sold to pay said debt,interest, and costsof suit; but the
said property-owner or owners shallhave the right to
redeem the same as is now provided for by law in regard
to land sold under execution ; but in no event shall the
owner be compelled to pay more than twenty per centum
of the value of the property.
§ 17. The board of councilmen shall have power to
grade and pave such portions of the riverfront as they
may see proper; and may charge a licensetax for the
privilegeof keeping a wharf at the footof such places
as may be graded or paved , in double the amount of
licensetax requiredof thosekeeping a wharf at points
not so graded or paved ; and they may requirea license
tax,not exceeding two dollars and fiftycents, for each
landing of steamboats discharging freight on the river
front of said town , at other points than at licensed
wharves .
§ 18. The board of councilmen may employ an attor-
ney forsaidtown ,whose duty itshallbe to prosecuteall
violations and infractions of the by -laws and ordinances
of saidtown ,and allviolationsof the criminalor penal
laws of this State committed in said town , of which the
Uniontown policecourt has jurisdictionas granted by
thisact; the compensation of said attorneyfor prosecu-
tionof offendersin said policecourt shallbe thirtyper
cent,of allfinesand forfeituresprosecutedto judgment .
He shall perform such other duties in prosecuting or de-
fending suitsor other proceedings for said town as may

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 497
be required,and shallreceive a reasonable compensation
therefor .
§19 . The board of councilmen shall,in the month of
July preceding the biennial electionof town officers, ap-
point a clerk,sheriff, and threejudges of said election.
Itshallbe the duty of said officersto hold the electionfor
town officersaccording to law ,and the judges and clerk of
said electionshall,at the close of the polls,sign and seal
the poll-books of said election, and return the same to
the board of councilmen ; and after due examination of
said poll-books by the board of councilmen of the poll-
books , they shalldeclare who are elected,and shallgrant
to those elected a certificateof such election; and they
shall have exclusive power to decide all contested elec-
tions or votes in any electionin said town .
§20 . An assessment of property for taxation shall be
made annually by an assessor,to be appointed by the
board of councilmen ,in the same manner and under the
same restrictionsas isrequired by law of the assessorof
Union county under the revenue laws of this State,ex-
cept that such assessment shallbe made in the month of
May or June in each year. Propertyshallbe listedfor
taxation by said town as of the firstday of the month
in which the assessment is directed to be made ; and the
propertylistedfortaxation,and which shallbe taxed by
said town ,shallincludeallproperty,real and personal,
situatedin said town ,not exempt from taxationby this
State under the revenue laws thereof .
§ 21. The assessor, after having taken the listsof all
property requiredto be listedas above, shallrequire
each person residing in said town , on oath,to fix the
amount he or she is worth from all other sources on the
day to which said listrelates, aftertaking out his or her
indebtedness from said amount ; and the assessor shall
take from said amount the sum of one hundred dollars,
and set down and listthe balance for taxationby the
town ,and which shallbe so taxed . This sectionincludes
allpropertyof everysortand description,notes,accounts,
LOC . L., VOL . 11-32

493 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
bonds ,billsof exchange ,choses in action,debts, and de-
mands of every kind ; but does not include land not situ-
ated within the town ,which is taxed where situated .
§ 22. Any goods , wares , or merchandise brought into
said town for sale,after the regular assessment for that
year,by any one [who ] has not been assessedforthatyear,
shallbe assessedin the same manner ,and be required to
pay such proportion of the tax levied for the year as the
remainder of the year bears to the whole year . But this
section shallnot apply to peddlers having a licensefrom
the town . The clerk of the board of councilmen shall
make the assessment contemplated by thissection.
§ 23. The assessor of the town shall place a just and
fairvaluation upon the property listedfor taxation under
the twentieth section of thisact,from which list,together
with the listsdirected to be taken by section twenty -one
of thisact,he shall make two fair copies of the listof
tax payers, arranged in alphabeticalorder,showing the
propertyassessedunder sectiontwenty, and the valua
tion thereof, and the valuation required by section twen-
ty-one ,and the total amount assessed to each ; and said
assessor shall include in said lists the names of all male
residentsof said town ,over the age of twenty -one years .
He shall return said lists to the board of councilmen at
their regular meeting in the month following that in
which the assessment is herein directed to be made .
any one failor refuse to give in and swear to a list,the
list shall be made from the best information the assessor
If
has or can obtain .
§ 24. The board of councilmen shallhave power tolevy
and collect,annually , an ad valorem tax of not exceed-
ing fiftycents upon each one hundred dollarsof value
of the property herein directed to be assessed for taxa-
tion, and a capitation or poll-tax, annually ,not exceed-
ing one dollarand fiftycents upon each male residingin
said town and over the age of twenty -one years .
§ 25. For thepurpose of providingforthe payment of
the present outstanding indebtedness of said town , in-
cluding scriptheretofore issued,the board of councilmen

LAWS OF KENTUCKY , 499
may , for the three fiscalyears next after the passage of
thisact,levy and collect, annually, double the ad valorem
and poll-tax prescribed as the maximum in the lastpre-
ceding section, one -half of which is to be used in liqui-
dating said outstanding indebtedness, and for no other
purpose . And at the close of the saidterm of three fiscal
years the provisions of thissectionshallcease,and be null
and void .
§26 . The board of councilmen shallhave power to re-
quire a license,and tax for same ,upon alldogs owned or
kept in the town , and to order the killing of any dogs
kept or owned in the town upon default of payment of
the required licensetax for thirty days afterthe levy of
the same .
§27 . The board of councilmen may appoint a street
commissioner when they may deem it proper to do so,
who shallhave superintendence of the streetsand alleys
of the town , and other public property situated therein,
and of the care and repair of the same ,
§28 . The board of councilmen shallhave power to dig
wells ,build cisterns,constructsewers ,build station-houses,
work -houses, pest-houses,town prison, and other build-
ings necessary for the use of the town , and may acquire
land therefor, and build pest-houses and work -houses
without thelimitsof the town if they see proper,but not
more than one mile from the town limits. They may
appoint policemen , who shallhave the same power with-
in Union county given herein to the marshal of Union-
town ,except in the collectionof fines and taxes and in
civil cases . They may appoint watchmen for station-
houses,work-houses,and pest-houses,with the powers of
policemen .
§29 . The board of councilmen may requirebond, with
good surety, of any officerappointed by them , condi-
tioned forthe faithfulperformance of the dutiesof his
office, and may prescribethe duties of all officersap-
pointed by them,includingclerk and treasurer,in addi-
tionto the dutiesprescribedby thisact,and may fixthe
rate of compensation to allsuch officers.

500 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 30. No member of the board of councilmen shallbe
surety on the officialbond of any officerof the town ,
and no person acting as an officerof any town election
shallbe eligibletoany officetobe filledby suchelection.
§ 31. There shallbe elected, on the firstMonday in
August ,one thousand eighthundred and eighty-four,and
biennially on the firstMonday in August thereafter,by
the voters of said town qualifiedto vote for councilmen
as requiredby thisact,a policejudge,who shallhold his
officefor two years ,and untilhis successor is elected and
qualified. No person shallbe eligibleas policejudge
who is not twenty -one years old, and who has not been
a resident of the town for one year next preceding his
election, or who does business in said town by vending
spirituous,vinous, or malt liquorsby the drink,or who
keeps a billiard-table,pool-table,pigeon-table,or shoot-
ing-gallery in said town . Any person holding the office
of policejudge of said town who shallremove from the
town , or shallengage in business in violationof thissec-
tion,shallipsofactovacate his office. The policejudge
of said town now in officeshall remain in office, and
may exercise the powers granted to the police judge of
said town by this act,until his successor is elected and
qualifiedas requiredby thisact; and should the officeof
policejudge become vacant at any time, the board of
councilmen may appoint one for the remainder of the
term ,and untilhis successor is elected and qualified.
§32 . The policejudge of said town shallhave ex-
clusivejurisdictionof all violations of the by laws of
said town , and concurrent jurisdictionwith justices of
the peace and the Union circuitcourt of allviolations
of the criminalor penal laws of thisState,committed
within the limitsof said town ,the punishment for which
does not exceed a fine of one hundred dollars, or im-
prisonment forthirtydays,or both such fineand impris-
onment ; and in examining trialshe shallhave the same
powers as two justicesof the peace . In civilcases he
shall have concurrent jurisdiction with justices of the
peace for Union county . The police court shall be

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 501
styledthe " Uniontown PoliceCourt," and is hereby de-
clared to be a court of record ; and the civil terms there-
of shallbe held monthly ,beginning on the firstTuesday
of each month , and continuing until all business before
it is disposed of, not,however ,beyond one week . All
judgments for fines or forfeituresin said police court
shall be for the benefit of the board of councilmen . All
warrants or summons issued by the police judge for
offensescommitted within his jurisdictionshallbe in the
name of " The Commonwealth of Kentucky for the benefit
of the board of councilmen ;" and all process from said
court shall be directed to the marshal of Uniontown ; but
may be executed by him or any constable or town mar-
shal of Union county. Said policejudge shall have
power to take bail forthe appearance of persons charged
with offenses within his jurisdiction, as defined by this
-act, at a subsequent day for trial,or to indorse on war-
rants issuedby him the sum in which defendants may be
admitted to bail by the officermaking the arrest for his
appearance on a day to be fixedby such officer. The
-conditions of the bail bond shallbe as required by the
Criminal Code of Practice of this State . Should the
accused make default in any of the conditions of his
bail bond , the judges shallenter that fact on the records
of the court,and issue summons thereon against the bail,
requiring them to show cause ,upon the firstday of the
next civilterm of the police court,commencing not less
than ten days thereafter,why judgment should not be
rendered against them on the forfeiture for the sum fixed
in the bail bond . The proceedings in said court on sum-
mons against bail shallbe as prescribed by the Code of
Practice. Money collected on judgment against bail, or
forfinesadjudgedin saidcourt,shallbe paid by the officer
collecting same to the treasurer of the town . Money ,
in lieu of bail,may be depositedwith the policejudge,
who shallbe responsible therefor upon his officialbond .
Should the accused make defaultby not appearing,or
should judgment be rendered against him for fine or
costs, the police judge may apply the money in pay-

502 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ment ,making an order to that effecton the records of
the court .
§ 33. The feesof the policejudge shallbe the same as
allowed to clerks of circuitcourts and to justicesof the
peace for similar services.
§ 34. In any case in which the policejudge is absent,
or,from any cause ,cannot act,the president of the board
of councilmen,or eitherjusticeof the peace forUnion-
town precinct,may act in his stead,and shallhave the
same powers and be entitledto the same fees as the po-
licejudge for the same services.
§36 . Either party may appeal from the judgment of
the Uniontown police court in any case when allowed by
the Codes of Practice and laws of this State .
§37 . There shallbe elected,on the firstMonday in Au-
gust,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,and on
the firstMonday in August biennially thereafter, by the
voters of said town qualified as required by this act to
vote for councilmen ,a marshal ,who shall, within thirty
days afterreceivinghiscertificateof election,in addition
to the oath required by the Constitution and laws of the
State,take an oath to faithfullydischargeallhis duties
under this act and the by -laws and ordinances of the
town , and execute bond in the county court of Union
county,the suretiesin said bond to be firstapproved by
the board of councilmen of Uniontown ; said bond shall
be conditioned for the faithfulperformance of his duties,
and that he willin due time ,and according to law ,collect
and pay over to the properperson alltaxeswhich itshall
be his duty to collect; also allexecutions or other claims-
or demands placed in his hands forcollection; and that
he will,in good faith and due diligence,to the best of
skilland judgment ,execute all duties that may be law-
fullyrequiredof him by the board of councilmen,or are
imposed by thisaet; and saidbond shallbe furthercon-
ditioned as isrequired by law forthe bonds of constables..
The marshal shall execute alt process from the Union-
town police court placed in his hands ,and may execute
process issuedby any officerin the Commonwealth of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 503
Kentucky ; and he shallhave the same powers as are
granted by law to constables,in additionto the powers
herein specified,and shallbe subjectto the same liabili-
ties,and may be proceeded against in the same manner .
He shall have the same power to levy,distrain, and sell
fortaxes,includingthe power to attachor garnishee,as
sheriffs have in the collection of the State revenue and
county levy, except as herein specified otherwise; but
garnishees summoned in cases of attachment for town
taxes shall be summoned to appear in the Uniontown
police court,instead of in the county court. The marshal
shallhave the power to sellrealestateforany town tax or
assessment against itsowner ,when he shallbe unable to
findpersonal property in the town to satisfythe same . If
the tax owing by any tax-payer is not paid afterthe tax-
listisplaced in the hands of the marshal ,he shall pro-
ceed to levy on and sell any personal property he may
find in the town belonging to the person from whom the
tax is due ; the sale to be advertised and made as sales of
personalpropertyare requiredby law to be advertised
and made by sheriffsin the collectionof State revenue .
Ifthe marshal can findno personalpropertyin thetown
belonging to the person from whom the tax is due , he
shall return a copy of the tax listof such person to the
board of councilmen , with an indorsement thereon show-
ing the factthat he can find no personal property out of
which he can make the tax. Upon such returnbeing
made by the marshal ,the board of councilmen shallnotify
the person from whom the tax isdue that unless histaxes
are paid within ten days aftersuch notice,the marshal
will be ordered to sellthe real estate of such tax -payer ,
or enough thereof to pay the tax due and the costs of the
notice, advertisement , and sale. In case the owner of
such real estate is absent from the county , or is not
known , service of the notice upon the agent of such
owner ,or upon the party listingthe property,shallbe
deemed sufficient; and in case no agent or person listing
the propertycan be found, publicationof the noticein
a newspaper publishedin Union county for two weeks
shall be sufficient. In all cases of notice sale, fifteen

504 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
6
per cent. of the amount of tax due ,and one dollarmore ,
shall be added to the amount for the costs of notice , ad-
vertisement,and sale. If the taxes are not paid accord-
ing to the notice , the board of councilmen shall direct
the marshal ,by an order entered on the records of the
board ,to sellenough of the real estate of the tax payer
to pay the tax and costsdue . After advertising the sale
forthe space of ten days ,in the manner required by law
for advertising sale of real property under execution,the
marshal shallsell,at the door of the policejudge's office,
in Uniontown ,enough of the propertyadvertisedto pay
the tax and costs due from the owner thereof The sale
shallbe made as salesof land under execution, except it
shall be for cash in hand . The marshal shall return a
copy of the order of the board of councilmen , with his
return of sale thereon, to the clerk of the Union county
court, who shall record the same ; and the purchaser at
such sale shall pay the clerk therefor the same fees as
are allowed him for recording certificatesof sheriff'ssale
of land for taxes. Such real estate shall be subject to
redemption in the same manner and in the same time
and upon the same terms as real estatesold under exe-
cution; and if not redeemed in one year from the sale
thereof, shallbe conveyed to the purchaserby the mar-
shal in the same manner as real estateisconveyed by
the sheriff; and for making such deed the marshal may
charge and collect from the purchaser one dollar and
fiftycents. The marshal shallalso filewith the clerk of
the board of councilmen a report of each sale of real
estate for taxes ,setting forth the land sold, and the name
of the purchaser , together with the amount of tax and
costsproduced by such sale,which reportshallbe pre-
servedby the clerk. The marshal may appoint a deputy.
with the consent of the board of councilmen , and the
marshal shallbe held responsible on his officialbond for
the acts of his deputy . It shallbe the duty of the mar-
shalto arrestallpersonswho may be guiltyof violating
any of the by -laws or ordinances of the town ,or any of
the penal or criminal laws of this Commonwealth , and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 505
for thispurpose may callto his aid any of the male citi-
zens of the town or county ; and any one failingor refus-
ing to assistthe marshal or his deputy , or any policeman
of the town , when called on , shall be fined in such sum
as may be provided by ordinance of the board of coun-
cilmen . The marshal shall attend the sessions of the
police court, and all regular meetings of the board of
councilmen ,and shall perform all such services,not in-
consistent with the nature of his office, as may be pre-
scribed by by -laws or ordinances of the board of coun-
cilmen . The marshal shall be entitled to the same fees
for collecting taxes as are allowed sheriffsfor collecting
taxes ; and for other services shall be entitled to the same
fees allowed constables for similar services . The board of
councilmen may allow him a reasonable compensation
for the performance of services not otherwise provided
for. Should the officeof marshal become vacant at any
time ,the board of councilmen shallappoint a marshal to
fillthe vacancy untilthe next regular electionof council-
men , and he shall hold his officeuntil his successor is
elected and qualified. The marshal now in officeshall
remain in officeuntilhis successor is elected and quali-
fiedas required by this act, and ,together with his deputy ,
may discharge the duties of the officeas though elected
under this act .
§38 . The board of councilmen may ,by ordinance ,re-
quire merchants having licenseto sellspirituous,vinous ,
or malt liquorsby the quart,not to sellany such liquors
on electionor such other public days as they may deem
proper,and shallprescribeby ordinanceadequate penal-
ties for violating such requirements ; and the license of
any one violatingsuch ordinancesshall,upon hisconvic-
tion,be suspended untilthe fineand costsare paid,and
upon a second conviction,such license shall be forfeited,
and the court shall include such suspension or forfeiture
as a part of the judgment , and when the license is for-
feitedby a second conviction,license shall not again be
granted tosuch partyforone year.

506 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§39 . The board of councilmen shall not prescribe a
greater penalty for the violationof any ordinance or by
law than a fine of one hundred dollars, or imprisonment
for more than thirtydays , or both such fine and impris-
onment .
§40 . It shallbe the duty of the board of councilmen to
keep the streets, alleys, culverts, sewers , wells, cisterns,
and other public conveniences , in good repair; and they
shall make and publish annually an account of the
moneys received, and from what source, and of the
money paid out and for what purpose ,and for a failure
to make and publish the statement as herein required,
each member of the board shall be subject to a fine of
not lessthan twenty -fivenor more than one hundred dol-
lars,recoverable upon presentment and indictinentof a
grand jury .
§ 41. License to vend spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors
shallonly be granted by the board of councilmen in open
council . All other licenses shall be granted by the clerk
of the board ,upon presentation to him by the applicant
of the receipt of the town treasurer or marshal for the
amount required by ordinance for such license. The
clerk shall issue license to vendors of spirituous,vinous ,
or malt liquors when ordered by the board of council-
men so to do ,and upon presentation of the treasurer'sor
marshal's receiptfor the amount of license tax required
by ordinance .
§ 42. All money belonging to the town or the board of
councilmen thereof shallbe paid by the officercollecting
the same to the town treasurer, who shall pay no money
out except on order of the board of councilmen ,certified
by the clerk of the board .
§ 43. All by -laws and ordinances of the town now in
force shallso remain untilchanged by the board of coun-
cilmen in pursuance of the powers herein granted .
§ 44. Allactsor partsof actsconflictingwith the pro-
visionsof thisact are to that extent repealed.
§ 45. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage ,
Approved April 28 , 1884.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 507
CHAPTER 1068 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Farmers' Bank of Owenton .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby created and establishedin
the town of Owenton a savings and deposit institution,
which shallbe a body -politicand corporate,by the name
of the Farmers 'Bank of Owenton ; and shall have power
and authorityin thatname tocontractand be contracted
with, sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded , answer
and defend , in all courts and places as a natural person ;
and may have and use a common seal, and alter and
change the same at will; and shallhave all other rights
and powers usual and incident to corporations; and inay
do such acts and things as may be convenient and de-
sirable,provided the same is not contrary to law .
§2 . The property, business, and affairsof said bank
shall be under the management , government ,and con-
trol of a board of directors,to consist of not less than
five nor more than fifteenpersons, as the by -laws may ,
from time to time , regulate and prescribe,one of whom
shallbe electedpresidentof the bank; and A. Cox ,J.
Holbrook ,A. B. Davis , B. E. Garvey ,J. H. Cunningham ,
Wm .Teal , and J. M. Herndon are hereby constitutedand
appointed a board of directorsfor said bank ,to serve as
such untilothers are duly elected and qualified; and they
may electotherpersonswhom they may thinkproper,not
exceeding the limit above specified,to serve with them
as directors; and should any of the personsabove named
fail or refuse to qualify and serve as a director,his or
their places may be deemed and treated as vacant ,and
the vacancy or vacancies may be filledby the remainder ;
and allvacancies occurring in the board of directorsmay
be filledby the other directors; and the board of direct-
ors shallprovide for an electionof directorsby the stock-
holders, within three years from the organization of the
company , and bienniallythereafter; and all elections of
directors by the stockholders shallbe held at the place
of business of the bank , and notice of which shall be

508 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
given in at leastone newspaper published weekly in the
town of Owenton or the cityof Louisvilleforthirtydays
preceding each election; and such election shallbe held
under the inspection of two or more persons,to be ap-
pointed by the board of directors,who shallcertifythe
resultunder theirrespectivesignatures,and a plurality
of votes cast shall elect; and each stockholder shall be
entitled to one vote for each share of stock which he or
she may own , and may cast the same in person or by
proxy; and the board of directorsmay provide a form
forthe appointment of proxies,and specifythe evidence
that shallbe required in execution thereof.
§3 . The capitalstock of said bank shallconsistof five
hundred shares of the par value of one hundred dollars
each , but may be increased, from time to time ,as the
board of directors may deem advisable,to any number ,
not exceeding two thousand shares of the same value ;
and the persons named in the second section of this act
may open books for, and receivesubscriptionsto, the
capitalstockof saidbank,at such times and places,and
subject to such terms and conditions,as they may deem
proper ; and whenever as many as two hundred or more
shares of the capital stock of said bank have been sub-
scribedand paid in,or securedto be paid in,in accord-
ance with the terms and conditions upon which the same
were subscribed, and affidavitto that effect has been
made by itspresidentand cashierbeforethe clerkof the
Owen county court,said bank may proceed to transact
a general banking and financialbusiness; and may lend
money ,discountpromissorynotes,buy and sellexchange ,
stocks , bonds , and securities.
§4 . The said bank shallhave power to make advances
on approved securitiesof any kind; and it may receive
stocks,bonds ,and other things in pledge for the security
of money loaned or debts owing . Said bank may re-
ceive deposits of gold, silver,bank notes, United States
Treasury notes and othercurrency,and pay the same in
kind or as may be agreed by general or specialcontract ;
and may allow intereston deposits,not to exceed the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 509
rates allowed by the laws of this Commonwealth . It
may issuecertificatesof credit, payable throughout the
United States and elsewhere, for the convenience of
merchants and travelers,but shall not issue any notes
or billsto circulate as money .
§5 . The saidbank may acquire and hold,possess,use,
occupy,and enjoyallsuch realand personalproperty,
goods , chattels, and other things, as may be convenient
for the transaction of its business, or which may be
pledged or conveyed to it as security for any debt , or
purchased in satisfactionof any debt,judgment ,or de-
cree,and selland convey or otherwise dispose of the
same as a natural person .
§ 6. The board of directorsof said bank are hereby
authorized and empowered to enact and put in force
such rules,by laws,and regulationsforthe management ,
government ,and controlof itsproperty,business,and af-
fairs,as they may deem expedient,and alter,amend ,and
repeal the same at will; and shallspecifythereinthe
number of directorsnecessary to constitutea quorum for
the transaction of business; they may appoint such offi-
cers,agents,and servantsas they may deem necessary
to conduct the businessof the bank ,and pay them such
sums fortheirservices,and take from them bonds in such
penaltiesto secure the faithfulperformance of theirre-
spectiveduties,as they may thinkreasonableand proper;
and upon any bond thus taken recoverymay be had for
breaches of the conditions thereof . The stock of this bank
shallbe deemed personalproperty,and shallbe only as-
signable in accordance with such rules as the board of
directorsshall,from time to time ,prescribe; and said bank
shallhold a lien on the shares of any stockholderwho
may be indebted to it, and such shares shall not be as-
signablenor transferreduntilthe debt shallbe paid or
discharged .
§ 7. This act to take effectfrom its passage , and re
main in force for thirtyyears from the firstorganization
of the said institution,provided itis organized within five
years from itspassage , and shall be located in the State
of Kentucky .

510 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§8 . Said bank shallpay to the Auditor fiftycents on
each one hundred dollarsof itscapitalstock,at the same
time and manner as providedforthepayment of taxesby
other banks in this Commonwealth ,which shall be in lieu
of all taxes .
Approved April 28, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1069.
AN ACT to amend an act , entitled " An act to amend and reduce into one
the severalacts in relation to the city of Frankfort," approved March
sixteen, one thousand eight hundred and sixty-nine.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

cipalpurposes in saidcity,the councilmay ,by ordinance
orresolution,authorizethe mayor to purchase,at a stated
price, any designatedpropertyfor a specifiedpurpose,

ifadults,the money at saidprice,and make thepurchase,

owners of said property,or any part thereof,in the Frank-

judgments,as willsecurea fairtrialby an impartialjury

tobe condemned ,including damages ,ifany ,occasioned by

CHAPTER 1075 .

forthe purpose of building,controlling,and using a turn-

LOC .L., VOL . II- 33

of the stockholders, and hold an electionfor fivedirectors

president; and they shallhave power to make allneces-
sary by -laws , rules and regulations, necessary to carry

sage .

efit of Nicholas county , of two thirds of the stock in

pany ,other than the stock subscribed on behalf of and

the Maysville and Lexington Turnpike Road ,in Nicholas and Masor
counties .

of Kentucky :

Lexington Turnpike Road Company .

having the custodyor controlof any eattleor stock of

at large upon any portion of the lineof turnpike belong-
ing to said Maysville and Lexington Turnpike Road
Company , shallbe fined for each and every offense not

running at large shallbe subjectto the fineand costs,

shallproceed to collectsame , as in case of other execu-
tions; the fine so paid to go to the trustee of the jury

bon ,or Fayette counties.

four.and noticeof same shallbe posted at each and every
toll-gate on the lineof saidturnpikeaffectedby the law ,

theprovisionsof thisact shallnot apply to any residentof
Bourbon county whose stockmay run at largeon saidpike
ineitherofthecountiesthroughwhich saidpikeruns.

bonds at theirmaturity,and also to pay the intereston

nue tax of said town , and that they be authorized to col-
lectan annual tax of not exceeding ten dollars on each

for the purpose before stated; and that no additionaltax
shallbe levied or collectedby said board of trusteesdur-
ing the time that said bonds are outstanding and in force,

ityof the citizensof Fulton .
§3 . The taxes levied under the authority of this act
shall be assessed and collectedby the town marshal of
said town , and when collected,paid into the treasury of
said town as other revenue ; but before he shall be author-
ized to collectsaid tax, he shall execute good and suffi-
cient bond ,with securitiesapproved by the chairman and
board of trustees,that he willfaithfullyand promptly col-
lectand pay over to the proper person or persons author-
ized to receive the same ,and within the time prescribed,
all taxes levied under this act, which may be intrusted
to him for collecton . Treasurer's Bond . That the treas-
urer of said town ,before he receives any of the aforesaid
money , shallgive bond and security, approved by the
chairman and board of trustees of said town of Fulton ,
Kentucky , for the proper application of the same .
§ 4. The clerkof the saidtown of Fulton shallkeep a
record-book of said bonds , and the date of their issue,
and the date of the payment and cancellationof the same ,
and the date of the payment of allintereston same . Said
bonds shallnot be signed or dated untilthe same are sold,
at which time a record of said sale shall be made . The
said chairman and board of trustees are of themselves ,
and by virtueof theirofficialposition,commissioners to
arrange for the payment of the bonds as they become
due or redeemable , and they shallinvest the proceeds of
said bonds as they may think best,on the streets,side-
walks , and alleys of said town ; and for the purpose of
betterdistributingsaidamount equally,thechairman may
appoint one member of the board from each ward ; to be

known as streetcommissioners,whose duty itshallbe to
supervisethe work in theirrespectivewards; and they
may be allowed a reasonable compensation for theirser-
vices, to be paid from the funds belonging to the said
town as other money drawn from the treasuryof said
town .
§ 5. And that beforesaidtax shallbe levied,or bonds
issued,thequestionshallbe submittedto the legalvoters
of said town ,by said chairman and board of trustees,at
a time designatedby them ,givingatleastten days'notice
of time and place of voting on said proposition. If a
majority of the legal votersvote for the same at said
time ,then said chairman and board of trusteesshalllevy
said tax,and issue bonds in accordance with the provis-
ions of this act .

Approved April 25, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1089 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Chicago ,in Marion county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the town of Chicago ,in Marion county ,be ,
and the same ishereby ,incorporated,and the town limits
shall extend three-quarters of a mile in every direction
from " Smith's store," in said town . The following are
hereby appointed officersof said town : Wm .L. Smith ,
policejudge; Wm .Bullock,marshal; and T. C. Blair,C.
N. Ballard, J. M. Ferrill, J. R. Smith , and Thos . Boon ,
trustees; all of whom shall hold their respective offices
tilltheirsuccessorsare elected and qualified. A majority
of the trusteesshallconstitutea quorum .
§2 . The prudential, fiscal,and municipal concerns of
said town shall be vested in five trustees,who shall be
elected annually , on the third Monday in April, by the
qualifiedvoters of said town , and shall hold their office
for one year ,and until their successors are elected and
qualified; that said trustees and their successors are a
body -politicand corporate, and shall be known by the
name and styleof the Board of Trustees of Chicago;
and by that name shallbe capable of contracting,being
contracted with ,of suing and being sued ,of pleading and
being impleaded , of answering and being answered , of
defending and being defended ,in all courts and places ;
and may use a common or private seal,and do allother
acts which a body -corporate and politiemay lawfully and
rightfullydo ,having perpetual succession.
§3 .The trusteesshallbe electedby the qualifiedvoters
of said town . The qualificationof voters in said election
shall be a residence in said town continuously for six

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 525
months next preceding the election at which the voter
offers to vote,and twenty -one years of age . No person
shall be eligibleto the officeof trusteeof said town who
is not a citizenthereof,and has not been a residentthere-
of twelve months preceding the election; and no trustee
shallbe a party to any contractin which the town of
Chicago is or may become a party. A majority of the
trusteesshallconstitutea quorum .
§4 . That sections five, seven , eight, nine ,and ten , of
article one ,also allthe provisions of articlesthree,four,
and five,of an act ,entitled "An act to amend an act ,and
reduce into one the several acts in regard to the city of
Lebanon ," approved February thetwenty-ninth,one thou-
sand eighthundred and eighty-four,with alltheirprovis-
ions,so faras applicable,are made partof thischarter.
§5 . The trusteesof saidtown ,when theydeem proper,
may employ an attorney to prosecute allproceedings for
breaches of the ordinancesof said town ,and to prose-
cute or defend all actions on behalf of or against said
town ,or in which it has an interest; and may pay such
compensation as shallbe agreed upon by the trusteesand
said attorney.
§ 6. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 25, 1884.

icatingliquorsin Californiavoting precinct, in Campbell county .

§ 2. In case of a vacancy in the officeof police judge
or marshal,by death,resignation,removal,or otherwise,

the chairman of the board of trusteesmay order an elec-

ernor as now required by law to be done , and whose

sentation of said commission to the county judge of said

ment ; but nothing herein shall be so construed as to dis-
pense with the regular annual election of all the officers

judge; and the person so appointed shallmake his re-
turns under oath , and shall be entitled to the same fees
