CHAPTER 1099 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheMt. Tabor TurnpikeRoad Company ,inFayette
county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That B. F. Petitt, Robert McClanyhan , John E.
Muir ,James Simpson ,Mortimer Walker ,C. W. Hardman ,
and their successors , chosen in the manner hereinafter
set forth,be,and they are hereby,createda body-corpo-
rate, under the name and styleof the " Mt. Tabor Turn-
pike Road Company ," forthe purpose of constructing and
operating, upon the macadam plan, a road in Fayette

532 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
.
county , to begin at a point at or near the intersectionof
the Mt. Tabor road with the Lexington and Tate's Creek
Turnpike , and to extend either along or in the general
direction of said road to its intersection with the Rich-
mond and Lexington Turnpike Road .
§2 . Said corporation is hereby granted perpetual suc-
cession, and is empowered in itscorporate name to con-
tract and be contracted with,sue and be sued ,to purchase ,
acquire, own ,sell, mortgage ,or convey , any real or per-
sonal property, or rightsor intereststherein,that may be
needful in constructing,operating,repairing,or carrying
on said turnpike and itsbusiness; to adopt and use a cor-
porate seal, and to do any and all other things that turn-
pike companies are usually empowered to do .
§3 . The provisions of chapter one hundred and ten of
the General Statutes,titled" Turnpike .Gravel , and Plank
Roads ," so far as they are applicable to turnpike roads,
are adopted and made a part thereof.
§ 4. Books may be opened at any time withinten years
from the time this act becomes a law , by any two of the
incorporators,for the subscriptionof stock; and when as
much as two thousand dollars in stock has been taken , a
meeting may be called at any convenient place in Fay-
ettecounty,of which ten days'previousnoticeshallhave
been given,by advertisementin a newspaper publishedin
Lexington ,or by actual notice to allof the subscribers,for
the purpose of organizing and electinga board of direct-
ors .
§ 5.The board of directorsshallconsistof fivepersons,
allof whom shallbe stockholders; and in voting therefor,
or upon any other question in which they have a voice,
stockholders shall be entitledto as many votes as they
have shares of stock . Each share of stock shall be of the
par value of fiftydollars, and the entire capital stock
shallnot exceed six thousand dollars. The corporation
shall not contract or create a greater debt than three
thousand dollars,and the private property of stockholders
shallbe exempt from the payment of corporate debts.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 533
.
§6 . The board of directors shall choose one of their
number as a president, and one of their number as sec-
retary and treasurer, and choose such other officersand
adopt such by -laws and regulations,consistentwith the
laws of the land,as they may deem proper and needful
for the conduct and promotion of the corporation busi-
ness; but no officershall be chosen ,or by -law adopted ,
except by a vote of at leastthree directors. The presi-
dent , or any two directors, may call a meeting of the
board .
§7 . There shall be an annual meeting of the stock-
holdersin Fayette county,forthe purpose of electinga
board of directors , and of the directors for choosing its
officers; and said meeting ,until changed by the board ,
shallbe held on the firstSaturday in June of each year ;
and the directorsand officerschosen at any time shall
hold theirofficeuntilthe firstSaturday in June next suc-
ceedingtheirelection,or such otherday as may be fixed
by the board of directors,and until theirsuccessors are
chosen and qualified.
§ 8. This charter shall be forfeited and void unless the
incorporators shall organize and begin work within ten
years from the date of itspassage .
§ 9. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 24, 1884.
CHAPTER 1100 .
AN ACT to create the officeof road commissioner in Johnson county ,
and to provide for the appointment of the same .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the office of road commissioner be , and is
hereby,created and establishedfor the county of John-
son; thatsaidroad commissioner shallbe appointedand
commissioned by the Governor of this Commonwealth ,
and shallhold his officeforthe term of four years,and
untilhis successoris appointedand qualified. Said ap-
pointment may be made on the recommendation of the

534 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Johnson county court, or of the circuitjudge of the dis-
trictin which said county is situated; and before said
commissioner shallenter upon his duties he shallgive a
good and sufficientbond ,to be approved by the county
court of saidcounty,forthe faithfulperformance of his
duties as road commissioner , and shall take an oath in
saidcourt to the effectthathe willfaithfullyperform all
the dutieshereinenjoinedupon him .
§2 . That said road commissioner shalltake charge of
all the roads in said county , and keep , or cause to be
kept ,the same in good repair; and also to open allnew
roads,or changes in any of the oldroads,in saidcounty,
and erect allbridges,culverts,and make all fills,let all
contractsfor buildingbridges, blowing rocks, and any
and all dutiesthat is now requiredby law of county
judges,and shallcallon the overseersand hands liable
to work on roads in said county ,and in the same man-
ner as the county judge now does under the existinglaw :
Provided ,however , That if any overseer or hands shall
failor refuse to comply with the demands of said com-
missioner,he shallreportthe same to the county court,
and they shallbe proceeded againstas now providedby
Jaw .
§3 . That when itbecomes necessary to open any new
road ,or changes in any old road ,said commissioner shall
call to his aid the surveyor of the county ,and proceed to
review and lay out,by metes and bounds , said new road
or change , and shallreport the same to the county court;
and said commissioner shall have right to agree with
any or allpartiesfor the land over which any new road
or change may run , and the amount to be paid said land-
owner ; thatsaidcommissioner shallreporttothe county
court allagreements with land-owners forrightof way ;
and if approved by the county court,said contract shall
be legaland binding; but ifdisapproved by the county
court ,or no contract made by said commissioner , then
the county court shallissuehiswrit of ad quoddamnum
as is now provided by law ; that said commissioner shall
be furtherempowered to contractor procure any timber,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 535
team , or anything necessary to build or establishany
road , or make any fill,culvert,or erect any bridge in said
county .
§4. That itshallbe the duty of saidroad commissioner
to keep an itemized account of all his acts concerning
roads in said county ,and shall report the same to the
court of claims of said county ; and said court of claims
shallpay the accounts of said commissioner, and shall
pay said road commissioner two dollarsper day for each
day he is actually engaged in said road service, to be
paid out ofthe county levy of saidcounty as otherclaims
are paid .
§ 5. That if said commissioner shall fail or refuse to
perform any of the dutiesherein required of him , he
shall,upon indictment and conviction,be fined not less
than fivenor more than fifty($ 50) dollars. Said fines
and forfeituresshall go to the common school fund of
said county .
§ 6. This act shallonly apply to Johnson county .
§7 . This act shalltake effectfrom Aprilfirst,one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-four.
Approved April 24, 1884.
CHAPTER 1101 .
AN ACT to amend and reduce into one all the acts in relation to the
town of Junction City.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the corporate limitsof the town of Junction
City be and are established as follows: In the form of a
square , whose sides shall measure each three hundred
and twenty poles,whose centre shallbe the centre of the
crossing of the Cincinnati Southern Railway with the
Knoxville Branch of the Louisville and Nashville Rail-
road ,and two of whose sides shall be perpendicular to
and crossing the Knoxville Branch of the Louisvilleand
Nashville Railroad,and the other two sidesparallelto
said railroad .

536 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§2 . That the fiscal,prudential,and municipal concerns
of said town ,with the government thereof,shallbe vested
in seven trustees,four of whom shallconstitutea quorum
to transact business, and all of whom shallhave resided
in said town one year next preceding theirelection.
§3 . That said trustees, and their successors in office,
shallbe a body -politicand corporate,and shallbe known
by the name and style of the " Board of Trustees of the
Town of Junction City;" and by that name may sue and
be sued in all courts.of law and equity, may contract
and be contracted with , and may do and perform all
other corporate acts.
§ 4. That said trustees,on the firstSaturday of June ,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four, and annu-
allythereafter,shall be elected by the qualifiedvoters of
the said town , who shall have resided within the limits
of the town for the periodof twelve months previous to
said election,or who have resided in the State of Ken-
tucky two years,and in said town sixtydays previous to
said election . Said trustees shall hold their office for the
twelve months next succeeding their election, or until
theirsuccessors are duly electedand qualified; and before
entering upon the duties of theirofficethey shalltake an
oath,before an officerempowered to administer oaths,
that they will faithfullydischarge the duties of trustees
of said town during their continuance in office.
§ 5. Said election for trustees shall be held at some con-
venient place in the town of Junction City,to be desig-
nated by the trustees of said town by written notices
posted at threepublicplaces in the town one week pre-
vious to the day of election; and the electionto be held
between the hours of seven o'clock in the forenoon and
six o'clock in the afternoon .
§ 6. That the board of trustees shall, before every
such election,appoint suitablepersons as officersthereof,
viz: Two persons as judges,one to be of one political
partyand the otherof the oppositeparty,and a sheriff
and clerk, with a like politicaldifference: Provided, That
therebe a sufficientnumber of the members of such po

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 537
liticalparties as aforesaidto fillsaid offices,and who will
consent to act as such. That said officers,before they
proceed to hold such election,shallbe duly sworn to faith-
fullyperform theirrespective duties; that the poll-books
of all elections of officers of said town shall be certified
by the officersholding said election,with the names of
those elected, and returned by the clerk of said election,
and deposited immediately with the clerk of the board of
trusteesof saidtown ,who shallimmediatelynotifythose
elected of their said election. Said judges of election
shallmake a writtenstatement,signedby them ,of the
number of votes received by each candidate ,and return
same ,with the poll-book ,to the clerk of the board .
7. That the legal titleto allthe streetsand alleysin
said town and to all real and personal property which
now belongs ,or which may hereafterbelong to said town ,
is vested in said board of trustees, with authorityto hold
real and personal estate,by purchase ,donation ,or other-
wise ,for the use or ornament of the town ; and they are
authorized to use,lease, or sell and convey the same ,as
they may deem expedient .
§ 8 ..That said trustees, after their qualification, shall
have power to fillany vacancy occurring in their own
body . It shall be their duty to elect one of their own
number chairman ,who shallpreside at alltheirmeetings ;
and incaseof the absence of the chairman,theyshallap-
point a chairman pro tempore. In case any trustee shall
remove from the town , or in case of the death of a mem-
ber, the board may ,by resolution passed to that effect,
declare his seat vacant and fillthe vacancy so created.
§ 9. Said trusteesshall have legislativepower to make
by laws and ordinances for the carrying into effectof all
the powers herein granted for the government of the town ,
and to do all things properly belonging to the police of
an incorporated town ; also,
1. To regulate the time and place of their meetings
and change them at pleasure, and to determine their
rulesof procedure .

2. To elect a chairman , clerk, treasurer , assessor of
taxes,collector, and also a competent police force, and

the treasury of the town , and to require of the treasurer,
marshal , assessor, and collectorof taxes bond , with good
security, for the faithfuland proper discharge of their
duties .
3. To fillby appointment for the unexpired term all
vacancies occurring in any of the officeswhich , under

pointment, and allvacancies of officerselectedin any
manner to serve said town .
4. To make rules and regulations for the cleanliness,
good order, decency , and decorum of the town , for the

the inhabitants, and to enforce the same by appropriate
finesand penalties.
5. To adopt and enforce sanitary regulations to pre-
vent the introduction and spread of epidemics and con-
tagious diseases ,
6. To clearthe streets, alleys, and pavements of all
obstructions ,
7. To make and keep in repairalldrains,ditches,and
guttersthat they may deem necessaryto promote the

8. To prohibitand to regulate the storage of any com-
bustible,explosive, or unwholesome material within the

9. To cause any chimney , flue,stove-pipe, fire-place,
projectingwall,or any building, or part of a building, or
other thing,to be so changed ,repaired,or destroyed as
to remove any cause of danger or insecuritythat,in their
opinion,may exist.
10.To cause the owners and occupiers of property
fronting on streetsand alleys,or having streetsor alleys
inthe rearof theirproperty,to keep the same clean and
free from filth.
11.To maintain and carryintojudgment and execu-
tion,by any appropriateaction,allsuitsforthe recovery

of,or for damages for,the detention or taking of or in-
jury to,or destructionof,any propertyor chosesin action,
the title to which is vested in them .
12. To prohibit the erection,and to cause the removal
and destruction,of wooden buildings and other things,

uable improvements in any part of the town .
13. To suppress and punish keepers of tippling-houses ,
houses of ill-fame , disorderlyhouses ; also wholesalers or

or other valuables are won or lost .
14. They shall have power to establishand construct,

watch -house , and such other buildings as they may deem

15. They shallhave power to restrainand prohibitthe
running at large of hogs ,goats,cattle, and other stock.
16. They shall have power to license,tax, or regulate

Jenny Lind, and other similartables, nine or ten-pin
alleys,or ballor pin-alleysof any kind,and of shooting-

17.To restrain,suppress,and punishindecent,obscene,
or profane language, conduct, or exhibitions, drunken-
ness,disorderlyconduct, and to restrainand punish va-
grants,prostitutes,rakes, and whoremongers .
18. To license,tax,and regulate auction salesand the
peddlingof goods,wares,and merchandise.
19. To license,tax,and regulate within said town the
keepers of inns, hotels, taverns, saloons, coffee-houses,
restaurants,grocery merchants ,druggists, and keepers of
allotherplacesforthe sale of spirituous,vinous,or malt
liquors
20. To authorize the clerk of the board to issue license
in any matter referredto in thischarterin which said
trusteesare given power to grant license. The fee for

21.They shallhave power to appropriatesuch sums
as they may deem proper to construct, or aid in con-
structing,pavements , plank roads or walks ,rock walks ,
or crossings, and to acquire titleto and macadamize any
streets thereof .
22. They shallhave power to fixpenaltiesfor the vio-
lationof any of the ordinances of said town in such
sums as they may deem proper, not, however , in any
case to exceed one hundred dollars' fine and ten days '
imprisonment , eitheror both .
23. To provide for the purchase and erection of lamp-
posts and other supplies necessary for lighting the streets
of the town .
24. To require the owners of property lying on any of
the streetsof the town to pave the sidewalks adjacent
thereto, and to keep said pavements in repair,according

25. To extend , widen , or open streetsand alleys; to
acquire titlethereto by purchase ,gift,or by proceedings .
in any court of competent jurisdictionas prescribed in

§ 10. That itshallbe the duty of said trustees-
1. To keep a journal of theirproceedings ; and , at the
request of a member ,the ayes and nays on any question
shallbe recorded. In the meetings of the board no mem-
ber shallvote on any questionin which he has a pecu-
niary interest.
2. To keep an account of allmoneys levied, collected,
and disbursed,and state on their journal for what each
sum was disbursed,and at alltimes keep the same open
to the inspection of the citizensof the town .
3. To require allorders and proceedings of the board
to be entered on the journal, and signed by the chairman
or chairman pro temporeof the board .
§ 4. To require the marshal , treasurer, assessor, and
collectorof the town to execute bond in adequate pen-
alties, with sufficientsecurities, for the faithfuldischarge
of theirrespectiveduties. Said bonds shallbe made pay-
able to the board of trustees of the town of Junction

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 541
City , and may contain such stipulationsand conditions
as the board may require and accept ;and when executed ,

of such officers and their securities until the conditions

cover against said officers and their securities, and have
appropriate judgment and execution .
§ 5. To appoint a clerk,whose duty it shall be to pre-
serve the books ,papers , and records belonging to his of-
fice. He shallkeep a regular journal of the proceedings
of the board , and record all its acts and resolutions , and
draft allagreements between the board and other persons.
He shall furnish attested copies of all acts and orders

fied or attestedcopies of allorders directed to be paid by

order, when presented to the treasurer, with the receipt
of the claimant indorsed thereon ,shallbe paid . He shall
perform allthedutiesproperlyappertainingto hisoffice,
and may be removed at the pleasure of the board .
§ 11. That the said board of trusteesshallhave power
and authorityannually to assess,levy, and collecta tax
on all the real and personal estate within the limits of
said town subject to taxation for State revenue , not to
exceed fortycents on one hundred dollars' worth of prop-
erty; also-
1.To levy and collecta poll-tax,not exceeding one
dollar,on all male persons resident in said town over
the age of twenty -one years.
2. To tax and licensepeddlers, and other itinerant
vendors of medicines , nostrums , goods , wares , and mer-
chandise; also lotteryagents and butchers,and to pre-
vent any such business being done within the town limits
without license,by appropriatepenalties.
3. To licenseand regulatethesaleof spirituous,vinous,
or malt liquors by tavern , hotel,saloon,or coffee-house
keepers , druggists, and other dealers, at wholesale or

retail,in said town ; and to fixthe rate of license tax to
be paid by such as may be licensedin any sum not ex-
ceeding two hundred dollars per annum : Provided, That
no such license is to take effect until the State tax is
paid,and the bond executed which is required by the
statute .
4. Immediately upon the return of the listsby the
assessorof the town ,the trusteesshallappoint a com-
mittee as a board of supervisors, who shallcarefully
examine the tax -listsreturned by the assessor, and cor-
rect the valuation of property as made by him , whether
the same shall have been valued too high or too low ;
make such corrections as they may deem just,and add
thereto any property omitted by the assessor as herein-
before provided , and report to the said board within fif-
teen days aftertheirappointment .
5. That immediately upon the report of the board of
supervisorsbeing made ,the board of trusteesshallap-
portion and levyan ad valorem tax on the propertyso
returned, and a poll-tax on each male inhabitant over

6. Upon the return of the assessor'slist,and afterthe
corrections are made by the board of supervisors, and
approved by the board of trustees,the clerkof saidboard
shall deliver an alphabetically-arranged copy of said
lists, with the additions and corrections made by the
board , to the marshal or collector,together with the or-
der fixing the rate of assessment , and also a listof the
persons and property,and propertyby whom and upon
which taxes are due , and the amount of each claim
respectively.
7. That alltaxes and assessments levied or assessed by
the board of trustees under this act , and all costs and

lienupon allthe realand personalestateof the owner
within the town , whether the tax be levied, voted , or
assessed,untilthe same be paid ,and no sale or transfer
0 real estate shall affect the lien thereon .
8. That the marshal or collector, immediately on the

receipt of said tax-book and lists, shallgive notice there-
of, by posting the same in four conspicuous places in said
town fortwo weeks ,notifyingthe tax-payers thathe will
be ready ,at his office, between the hours of nine and
twelve in the forenoon and two and five in the afternoon ,
forthe periodof six days,to receiveand receiptforsaid

forthe same by levying upon and selling, for cash in

tisethe time,terins,and place of sale,by printedhand

billsposted at the court-house door in Danville and at least

fore the day of sale, and also in some newspaper pub-

taxesin saidtown a rightof redemption,fora periodof

nal owner of the fullamount forwhich the propertymay
have been sold,with ten per cent.per annum interest,and

if the owner at the time of the sale be an infant,married

make to the purchaser or his assignee a deed for said

be subject to redemption .

have a lien upon any such lots or fractions of lots so
improved at the cost of the town untilallsuch costs and
taxes shallhave been paid .
13. Notice of the order directingsaid paving and other
work may be executed on the owners of saidpropertyor

owning suchproperty. Said improvements shallbe made
and repairsdone within two months afterthe serviceof

§ 12. The treasurer appointed by the board shalltake
an oath to faithfullyperform the duties of his office, and
shall execute a bond in a penalty of not less than one
thousand dollars for the faithful discharge of the duties
of his office,and conditionedthat he will,at the expira-
tion of his term of office,deliver allfunds and property in
his hands ,belonging to said trustees,to his successor in
office, and disburse any funds received by him according
to the order of the said board of trustees. It shall be his
duty to receiveand receiptfor all moneys paid to the
board , and pay no money without a certifiedcopy of the
orderof the board making the appropriation,signed by
the clerk; and he shall filesuch copy as a voucher in his
settlements with the board . His books shall at all times
be open to the inspection of the officersand citizens of
the town . The treasurer is authorized to receive and re-
ceiptforallmoney due the town for licensés, fines,pen-
alties,and taxes collectedby the marshal,or money due

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 547
the town from any source. He shall, on or before the
firstday of April,in each year,reportto the clerkof the
board a true listof all the taxes and moneys received
and paid out by him . He shall,when requiredby the
board,reportto them the conditionof the treasury,and
shallat alltimes,on reasonable notice,be ready for a set-
tlement of his accounts with the board ,who shall,at any
time,have power to demand a settlementof him .
§ 13. That there shallbe appointed annually, by the
board of trustees of said town , an assessor . No one
shallbe eligibleto said officewho is not qualifiedto vote
for trustees of the town . He shall take an oath before
entering upon the discharge of his duties similarto that
required of the county assessor. It shallbe the duty of
the assessor to callupon allpersons liable to be taxed ,
or their agents , if known to him , and make out a true
listof their taxable property, w he value thereof ,
which listshallbe made upon the oath of the owner or
his agent , to be administered by the assessor. Said list
shallbe so taken as to include allreal estate and every
speciesof personalpropertysubjectto taxationfor State
revenue purposes situatedin said town , exclusiveof
household furniture owned on the tenth day of January
of the year for which the assessment is made , and all
male residentsof the town at that date over twenty -one
years old. If any person shallrefuse to give a statement
of his property, under oath ,the assessor shallmake out
a listfrom the bestinformationhe can procure. Ifthere
be any estate within the town ,the owners of which are
absent or unknown , or have no known residentagent,
the assessor shallreport the factsspecificallyon his lists,
and the board shallfixthe valuationof such property.
and cause itto be appended to the assessor'slists. Two
complete copies of said lists, arranged in alphabetical
order,are to be returned by the assessorto said board on
or before the firstday of May , with the certificateof the
assessorto each . Upon the return of the assessor'slists,
the trustees shallgive notice that any person or persons
who may feelthemselves aggrieved by an excessive val

548 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
uation may appear before a board of supervisors, who
shall be appointed each year to supervise the lists, at
some time on or before the firstday of June of each
year ; and ,upon satisfactoryevidence presented to them ,
they may change the valuation. Said supervisors shall
also change the valuation of any other property which
they may consider erroneous .
§ 14. The police court of Junction City shall remain .
That there shallbe electedon the firstMonday in August ,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,and every
fourth year thereafter, by the qualified voters of said
town ,a " police judge of Junction City." That said po-
licejudge shallbe commissioned as such , and ,before he
enters upon the discharge of the duties of his office, shall
take an oath to faithfullyand impartiallydischargethe
same ,together with such other general oaths as likepub-
licofficersare required by statute to take . His term of
officeshallbegin on the firstMonday in September after
his election. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof
police judge unless he be over twenty -one years of age ,
and shall have been a citizenof the town for one year
next preceding his election.
،،
1. Said policecourt shallbe holden quarterlyin the
town of Junction City, at such times and places as the
judge thereof shall appoint , for the trialof civilcases,
and shall be known as the police court of Junction
City;" and itshallbe always open forthe trialof crimi-
nal and penal cases, and cases for the breach of town
ordinances .
2. Said court shallbe governed in itsrules of procedure
and practicein civiland criminalcases by the Code of
Practice of Kentucky and the amendments which have
been or shall be made thereto; and appeals from its
judgments may be taken as prescribed in saidCode to the
circuit court .
§3 . Said court shallhave jurisdictionwithin the limits
of the town of Junction City of allcauses ,civil,criminal,
and penal,inwhich justicesof thepeace orcountyjudges
have jurisdictionin theirrespective courts. In allpenal

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 549
and criminalcasesthe said policejudge shallhave the
jurisdictionof two justices of the peace . In all misde-
meanors and violations of by laws and ordinances of
said town , said court shall have exclusive jurisdiction.
Said court shallbe a court of record, and the police
judge shallact as clerk of said court, and issue all nec-
essary precepts,writs,and summons ; he shallkeep a cor-
rect record of the proceedings of said court,and a duly
certifiedor attestedcopy of the same , and of any record
in said court , shall be evidence in all the courts of this
Commonwealth .
4. Said court shallhave power to punish contempts by
fine or imprisonment , not exceeding fivedollars fine and
one day's imprisonment .
5. The process of said court shall be directed to and
executed by the marshal of Junction City,or any peace
officerof Boyle county,or any other county within the
State . It shall issue in the name of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky ,and may go into any county of the Com-
monwealth , and the officerto whom it may be directed,
in any town or county within the State,may execute it,
or give authorityto so execute it by indorsement on the
process. The person so authorized shallhave the right
to execute it, and to have the same fees thereof that are
allowed by law to the marshal .
6. That all fines and forfeitures recovered in said court
are hereby granted to said town ,and shallbe collected
by the marshal or other officerto whom capiaspro fine
or execution is directed, and paid by him to the town
treasurer; but before execution or other final process
issues, the defendant may pay the fine or forfeitureto
the judge of said court,who shall pay the same to the
treasurer of the town .
7. That the defendant shall have the right to replevy
forthree months any judgment , fine,or forfeiturein said
police court before execution or other finalprocess there-
in, by giving bond, with good security, in the same
manner as allowed by law in similar cases within the
jurisdictionof other courts of thisCommonwealth .

550 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
8. That any person convicted of a breach of an ordi-
nance of the town of JunctionCity,and adjudged to pay
a fine,in defaultof payment shallbe confined in work-
house,and kept at hard laborat such work as the board
of trusteesmay prescribe,on the streetsof JunctionCity,
in the work -house ,or elsewhere , under such regulations
as the trustees may prescribe, until said fines and costs
are paid , at the rate of one day's confinement and labor
for each dollarof the fine. All persons sentenced to im-
prisonment by the judgment of said courtfor breach of
the ordinances of the town may be, assigned to labor in
like manner as those imprisoned for non -payment of
fines: Provided, That under thisact a day's labor shall
not exceed eight hours .
9. That the fees of the police judge of said town ,
whether acting as judge ,clerk, or otherwise,except as
herein speciallyprovided,shallbe the same and be col-
lected in a similar manner as the fees of similar officers
under the statue law of the State, as it now exists or
may hereafter be in force; and fees and costs shall be
taxed in said court to the same extent,in the same man-
ner , and under the same regulations , as other courts of
thisState having similarjurisdiction.
10. That all prosecutions for a violation of the town
ordinances shall be in the name of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky ,and inure to the board of trusteesof Junc-
tion City; and said town shallbe entitledto allfinesand
forfeitures recovered in such cases , and have the same
rightto enforce the collectionof the same ,by due process
of law and equitableproceedings,on a returnof no prop-
erty found , as isprovided above in other cases.
11. That the said police judge shall have the same
power to issue all writs, try all causes,require and take
allbonds ,and enter alljudgments in civil,penal ,and crim-
inal causes, as isgiven to police judges in similar cases
by the Civiland Criminal Codes of thisCommonwealth
as they now exist,or may hereafterbe amended ,though
saidpowers be not expresslystatedin thischarter.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 551
12. That the trusteesof said town shallnot be respon-
sible for costs in any penal cases in said court, except
such costs as are collected from the defendant in a case .
13. That if any person be brought before said police
court in a drunken and disorderlycondition,it shall be
the duty of the court to refuse to try him in that condi-
tion; but the court may make an order sending him to
prison not lessthan fivenor more than twenty -four hours
before trial. If a person be arrested at night in a drunk-
en and disorderly condition, the marshal of the town
shall, without process,place him in the watch -house or
work -house during the night for safe-keeping , until he
can be brought before the police judge on the ensuing
day for trial.
§ 15.The board of trusteesmay ,at any time,employ
an attorney at law to prosecute any offendersagainst the
by -laws and ordinancesof the town ,and pay him there-
for a reasonable compensation .
§ 16. There shallalso be electedby the qualifiedvoters
thereof, in the manner and at the time prescribedby law
for the electionof sheriffsin thisCommonwealth ,a mar-
shal for said town , who ,before he enters upon the dis-
charge of the duties of his said office, shall take the
oaths usually required by law for similar officers, and
shallgive bond ,with sufficientsecurity,to be approved
by the trusteesof said town ,conditioned faithfullyto dis-
charge all the duties of said office, and to pay over to
such persons,and at such times as they may be respect-
ivelyentitled to the same , all money that may come to
his hands as marshal . This bond may contain any other
condition which the board may think proper to require,
and shall be made payable to the Commonwealth of
Kentucky .
1.It shallbe the duty of the town marshal to collect
all taxes due said town , and all executions and other-
demands which may be put into his hands for collection,
and pay over the same to whoever shallbe legallyenti-
tled thereto, under the same rules and regulations re-
quired by the statutes of sheriffsand constables in the

552 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
collection of taxes and other demands . For a failure to
discharge any of the duties of his office,he shallbe sub-
jectto the same proceedingswhich ,by law ,may be had
in thisCommonwealth against sheriffsand constables in
similar cases .
2. All bonds executed by said marshal for the faithful
performance of his duties,or those executedby allcol-
lectors appointed by the board , shall be filedwith the
archives of said board , and recorded in fullon the min-
ute -book of said board of trustees .
3. The marshal shall be entitled to the same commis-

CHAPTER 1102 .
AN ACT for the benefitof R. F. Howard ,of Magoffin county .
WHEREAS ,At the October term ,one thousand eighthun-
dred and eighty,of the Magoffin circuitcourt,Calvin How-
ard,of Magoffin county,was found to be a pauper idiot,
and ,by orders and judgment of said court,duly entered of
record and certifiedto the Auditor of Public Accounts ,R.
F. Howard ,of Magoffin county ,was appointed a commit-
tee to take charge of, and provide for and support said
pauper idiot,and was ,by the order appointing him ,to be
allowed therefor at the rate of seventy-five dollars per
annum ; and whereas ,said R. F. Howard , as committee ,
did keep and support said pauper idiot for six months ,
viz: from fifthMarch , one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-two ,to fifthSeptember , one thousand eight hun-
dred and eighty-two ; and whereas ,at said fifthSeptem-
ber, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-two ,the said
court failed and refused to allow to said committee any
part of his claim forsaid services,which amounted to the
sum of thirty-seven dollarsand fiftycents,which sum was
a clear loss to said committee ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of Public accounts be ,and he is
hereby,directedto draw hiswarrant on the Treasury of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky in favor of R. F. How-
ard,of Magoffin county ,as committee for Calvin Howard ,
pauper idiot,for the sum of thirty-seven dollars and fifty
cents, to be paid out of any fund in the Treasury not
otherwiseappropriated,and thereishereby appropriated
forthatpurposethe sum of thirty-seven dollarsand fifty
cents .
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after itspassage .
Approved April 22,1884 .

CHAPTER 1104 .
AN ACT to regulatethe sale of intoxicating, vinous, or malt liquors in
Morgan county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That from and afterthe passage of thisact it shall
be unlawful for any person to sell,vend , give,or loan,
eitherdirectlyor indirectly,any whisky,rum ,gin,wine ,
ale, lager beer, or vinous,spirituous,malt ,or intoxicating
liquorsin the county of Morgan ,in lessquantity than ten
gallons at any one time .
§2 . That any person violatingthe firstsectionof this
act shall, for each offense,be fined not less than fiftynor
more than one hundred dollars, to be recovered by in-
dictment in the circuitcourt or any court having juris-
diction thereof : Provided, That nothing in this act shall
be so construedas to prohibitregular practicingphy-
siciansfrom using or administering intoxicatingliquorsas
medicine in their practice; but any physician giving or
administering intoxicatingliquors,when he does not con-
siderthesame absolutelynecessaryforsuchpersontotake
or use the same , and that there is no other medicine that
would answer and accomplish the same purpose equally
as well as spirituousliquors,he shallbe finedin the sum
of one hundred dollars for each offense, to be recovered

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 555
by indictment in the circuitcourt or any other court hav-
ing jurisdictionthereof.
§3 . That the judge of the Morgan circuitcourt shall
give this act in charge to the grand jury of Morgan
county at each term of said court.
§ 4. This act shall take effect from and after the first
day of June ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four.
Approved April 22, 1884 .

protectionagainstlossby fireon theirhomes ,merchan-

surable; and for such purposes are hereby incorporated

icies,and no longer, and shall at all times be bound by

as such person remains a member , receive equitable ben-
efitspro rata of the accumulative earnings of the com-

enty-fiveper cent.of the premium by depositinghis or

CHAPTER 1107 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Ghent , in Carrollcounty .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That sectionfiveof the act incorporating the town
of Ghent , in Carroll county , approved , the
one thousand eighthundred and fifty-six,be so amended
as to empower the trusteesof said town to erect a work-
house in said town .
§2 . That allpersons who violatethe laws of the State
or the ordinances of said town may ,by an order of the
police judge of said town ,or a justice of the peace for
said district, be committed to said jail or work -house ,
and be compelled to labor thereinfora periodof time,
not exceeding one day for each one dollar of any fine
and the costs adjudged against them for such violation.
§3 . That section twelve of said charter be so amended
that the policejudge of said town shallhave jurisdic-
tionco-extensivewith thatof a justiceof the peace in all
civilactions,both in law and equity, when the amount
in controversy,exclusiveof interestand costs,does not
exceed one hundred dollars,
§ 4. He shall have jurisdictionin all penal offenses
when the fine shall not exceed one hundred dollars, ex-
cept in surveyor of road cases. He shallhave exclusive
jurisdictionof alloffensesagainstsaid town ordinances,
and in the absence of the coronerhe may hold inquests.
§5 . Appeals may be taken from any judgment ren-
dered by said judge for the same amount and in the
same manner as now prescribed by law regulating ap-
peals for justices' courts; and he shall collect a tax of
fiftycents on each suitover fiftydollars,and pay over
and account for the same as justices of the peace are
now required by law to report.
§ 6. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after itspassage .
Approved April 24 , 1884.

court to issuebonds to raisemoney to build a jail," and so forth.

of Kentucky :

intend the erection of said jail," passed at the present

and John H. Catron ,are hereby appointed commissioners

ificationsfor saidjailhave been adopted by said com-

CHAPTER 1109 .
AN ACT to incorporate the George W. Frantz Savings Association, of
Louisville .
WHEREAS ,A number of young men in the cityof Louis-
ville,believingthattheirfutureprosperitymay be found-
ed upon theiraccumulatinga capitalfrom small savings;
therefore ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 565
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Wm .Kopmeier ,Chas . Kopmeier ,W. S. Park ,
F. T. Eisenman ,L. F. Eisenman , J. C. Fisel, Chas . Deck-
el, L. J. Unz ,F. H. Coorssen ,Chas .A. Lang , Albert Reut-
linger, P. H. Bald ,James F. Laufer, R. N. Struck, C. F.
Huhlein , G. H. Dorn , M. J. Fleck , C. H. Buetgenbach ,
Wm . Vissman ,J. B. Ohligschlager, and R. G. Tafel, to-
gether with theirassociates and successors, be , and they
are hereby ,created a body -politicand corporate, under
the name and style of The George W. Frantz Savings
Association,of Louisville,Kentucky ,and with perpetual
succession and authority; by that name are made capable
in law to hold,purchase , receive, possess, and enjoy , re-
tain, sell, mortgage , convey , and otherwise dispose of
lands, tenements ,rents,goods ,chattels, choses in action,
personal property,notes,stocks,bonds ,public and private
securities; to loan the funds of said association upon

or notes with personal or real estate security; contract

to alteror renew same at pleasure; alsoto ordain and
establishby -laws for the government of said association,
not contrary to the laws of the land.
§2 . Officersof said corporation shallconsistof a pres-
ident,vicepresident,secretary,and treasurer,to be elected
by the board of directors; the latterofficesof secretary
and treasurermay be filledby the same person .
§3 . The affairs of said association shall be managed
by a board of twenty-one (21) directors,and the incorpo-
rators herein named shall constitute said board until va-

in the board of directors, such vacancy or vacancies may

thirds( ) of whom shallconstitutea quorum for said pur-
pose .

566 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 5. Said association may receive deposits in any sum
not lessthan fifty(50) cents, and may issuecertificates
of deposit therefor, when said deposit shallamount to the
sum of twenty-fivedollars($25).
§6 . Certificatesof deposit shall not be issued in ex-
cess of two hundred thousand dollars($ 200,000).
§7 . The board of directors may electfrom theirnum-
ber a committee,of which the president,vice president,
secretary, and treasurer shall be members , which shall
consist of not more than ten nor less than five directors,
and be known as the executive committee ; and they shall
perform such duties as said board of directorsmay direct,
and shall serve during such terms as the directorsmay
determine ; and the said board may ,at any time , fillany
vacancy in said executive committee , and may declare
such vacancies in said committee as they may choose .
§ 8. The board of directors,a majorityof whom shall
constitute a quorum ,shallenact such by -laws , rules and
regulations,as they may deem necessaryforthe govern-
ment of the association; and they are authorized to de-
posit money belonging to the association in any good
and solvent bank at the riskof the corporation.
§ 9. At the end of every six months of itsbusiness,the
officers of the association shall ascertain the certificates
of deposit issued,and the executive committee may de-
clare such dividends on such certificates,at the end of the
six months following said ascertainment,as they choose .
§ 10. The certificatesof deposit in said association
shallbe held by the persons to whom same may be
issued,theirpersonalrepresentationsor assigns; but all
assignments ,to be valid,shallbe made upon the books of
the association, and with the consent of the executive
committee .
§ 11. Said association may be dissolved at any time ,
and go into liquidation, upon a vote of two thirds of the
entire board of directors; and , after payment of debts,
the entire estate of said association shall be divided be-
tween those entitled thereto as their interestmay ap-
pear .

§ 12. Privatepropertyof holdersof certificatesof de-
positshallbe exempt from corporate debts.
§ 13. This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .

of Rich Pond .

spirituous,vinous , or malt liquors, or any mixture there.
of, includingany patent or proprietaryintoxicatingbit-
ters, in said town , shall,for each offense,be fined not
lessthan twenty dollarsnor more than sixtydollars; and
each sale under this act shall constitute an offense .
§ 4. Prosecutionsor suitsforany violationof the pro-
visions of thisact may be had in any court having juris-
dictionunder the law , or in the police court of Rich
Pond .
§ 5. This act shall,at the next regular election held
after its passage ,be submitted to the qualifiedvoters of
the town of Rich Pond ; and ifa majorityof the quali-
fied voters voting be in favor of enforcing this act,then
it shall take effect .
Approved April 24, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1111 .
AN ACT to provide forthe appointment of an elisorforGreen county,and
toempower him to executeallwarrants and processesin the name of the
Commonwealth , and to take bail-bonds and do alldutiesinsaid criminal
casesas sheriffsare now authorizedby law to do .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That at any time when the officesof sheriffand
coroner may be vacant in Green county ,the judge of the
judicialdistrictin which said county is located may ap-
point some suitable person , a citizen of said county ,as
elisorforsaid county ; and said elisor,in addition to the
powers now allowed elisors by law , shall have all the
powers of a sheriffin making arrests,takingbail-bonds,
and executing process,criminal and penal ,in said county ,
and shallbe subject to all the liabilitiesand penalties of
a sheriff, as now fixed by law ,in the discharge of said
duties .
§2 . Before entering upon the discharge of his duties,
said elisorshall take the oath required of allofficersby
the laws of Kentucky ; and for his compensation he is
allowed the amount now fixed by law to elisorsfor the
time actuallyemployed ,payable out of the State Treas-
ury: Provided, He shallnot be paid out of the Treasury
for any longer time than thirtydays , as now provided
by General Statutes. And is also allowed to charge ,for
specificservicesrendered by him , such fees as is now al-
lowed by law to sheriffsfor similar service,and collect-
able in same manner ,
§ 3. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 24 ,1884 .

CHAPTER 1113 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the town of Tyrone , in Anderson county..
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the limitsof the town of Tyrone be extended
as follows, to-wit : commencing at the mouth of Wildcat
branch ,near Shyrock's Ferry; thence down the Kentucky
river, including the same , to high-water mark , to the
mouth of Sugar Camp branch ; thence with the line of
Watfill, Dowling & Co. to the corner of the old limits;
then a line so as to make the limits one half mile wide
to the beginning .
§2 . There shall be five trustees elected on the first
Saturday in June of each year ,to hold theirofficesuntil
theirsuccessors are qualified. They and theirsuccessors
shallbe a body -politicand incorporate; to sue and may
be sued in all courts of this Commonwealth .
§3 . They shallhave power to make allby-laws forthe
town for the best interest of the citizens and town , and
to have power to grant licenses to tavern-keepers , sa-
loon-keepers,and coffee-house,to retailspirituous,vinous,
and malt liquorsby the drink,not to exceed one hundred
dollarsper annum ,and allmerchants,pool-tables,billiard-

570 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tables, ten-pin alleys, bowling-alleys, and shows of all
kinds, and not to tax them a greater amount than the
county and State charges them ; and to charge allof the
above-mentioned for doing any of said businessin the
corporatedlimitsof the town , or one fourthof a mile
thereof; and in allcases the Stateor county tax must be
paid .
§ 4. They shallhave power to levy a poll-tax not to ex-
ceed one dollaron each male citizentwenty -one years of
age ,and a tax not to exceed twenty -fivecents on the one
hundred dollars' worth of taxable property in the town
limits,unless a majorityof the qualifiedvotersvote in
favor of an increase of taxes; they may tax dogs , and
hold any of the owners ' taxable property subject to the
dog tax; and in case the owners of dogs refuse to pay
said tax,they may directthe marshal or any of his dep-
uties to killsaid dog or dogs ,as the case may be . All
personshaving paid theirpoll-tax shallbe exempt from
paying county poll-tax.
§5. They shallhave power to fillallvacanciesin any
of the officesof the town ,and to appoint allofficersnec-
essary for the government of the same ,allof which shall
be residentsof the town twelve months previous to said
appointment .
§6 . They shall elect one of their body chairman , to
serve one year ,who shallpreside over alltheirmeetings ;
and they may regulate theirmeetings by meeting once a
month ,and may compel the attendance of theirmembers
by fines,not to exceed one dollarat each regular monthly
meeting .
§7 . They shallappoint officersof elections; and said
elections shall be conducted as other elections for State
electionsare,except no voter shallbe allowed to vote un-
lesshe has paid hispoll-tax previousto any electionfor
said town .
§ 8. They shallhave power to open streetsand alleysof
such width as may be suitable, and in all cases be gov-
erned by the General Statutes of Kentucky on opening
roads and passways of towns and cities.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 571
§ 9. They shall have power to compel owners of lots
to lay suitablesidewalks of brick, stone, or wood , of
such width as they may direct, on any streetsor alleys
of the town ,and compel them by finesor saleof the lots
orlands benefitedby said sidewalks; and ifthe owners
failor refuse, after reasonable notice given by them , to
do saidwork ,then they may have it done,and collectby
saleof the lotsor land so benefited, with the rightto
redeem in two years,by paying twenty per centum per
annum , and all costs; they may have the lots or land
soldby the marshal ,afterten days ' notice.
§ 10. They shallhave power to build a lock-up , and
pay the marshal or keeper such salaryas they may see
proper to pay; and power to use a balland chain to
secure prisoners while working out fines; said work to
be done for the benefit of the town . The treatment of
prisoners in such cases shallbe according to law .
§11 . The chairman shall give notice of all elections
ten days previous, by written or printed notices, and
state in said notices who is entitled to vote at said elec-
tions .
§ 12. They shallhave power to compel allnuisances,
by fine, to be removed , and may declare what a nui-
sance is; and to compel allobstructions of allthe streets
and passways in and through saidtown to be removed
by fine for a refusal, after a reasonable length of time ,
by notice from said trustees.
§ 13. On the same day of election for trustees there
shallbe a policejudge and town marshal elected and
qualifiedas directedby articlefourth,sectionforty-one ,
of the present Constitutionof Kentucky . The police
judge shallhave all the powers of such officer,and con-
current jurisdictionwith justicesof the peace of Ander-
son county . He shallhave power to enforce the law on
all gaming and gaming -houses ,and power to destroy all
gaming instruments, and power to close all disorderly
houses of allkinds,and power to enforce allthe by -laws
of said town .
§ 14. The marshal of said town shall serve all pro-
cesses and preceptsto him directedby saidpolicejudge ;

572 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
he shall collectall taxes for the town , and be allowed
the same fees that sheriffsare allowed for collecting the
county revenue ,and in allother cases he shallbe allowed
the same fees that constables are allowed ; he shall have
power to have as many deputies as he believes necessary
to keep the peace,and dischargethem when he believes
itadvisable so to do . He shallgive bond as hereinbefore
mentioned. He shallhave power to closealldisorderly
houses,arrestallof the proprietorsor parties,and bring
them before the police court of Tyrone ,to be dealt with
according to law ; he shall,in additionto the bond given
to the county court,he shallgive bond to the trusteesthat
he will faithfullydischarge the duties of his office,and
pay over allmoney collectedby him or his deputiesfor
the town ; he shallhave power to sellpropertyfor fines
and taxes for the benefit of the town as hereinbefore
mentioned .
§ 15. The trusteesshallhave power to remove or cause
tobe done,thepresenttoll-gate,now situatedin thetown ,
and power to preventany person or personsfrom putting
up one,or causing one to be put up in the town ,or one-
fourth of a mile of the limits hereof, except by a special
permitby allof the trusteesconcurringtherein.
§ 16. The trusteesshallhave power to pay a salaryto
any officer,and shallemploy an attorneyto prosecutefor
thetown ,and pay him such sums of money as they may
agree upon .
§ 17. All sectionsof the former charter of which conflict.
with thisamended charter are hereby repealed.
§ 18. This act to take effectfrom its passage .
Approved April 24 , 1884 ..

CHAPTER 1114 .
AN .ACT to incorporatethe town of Loretto,in Marion county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the town of Loretto, in Marion county , be ,
and the same ishereby ,incorporated,and the town limits
shall extend three-quarters of a mile in every direction
from the depot building of the Louisville and Nashville
Railroad Company ,in said town .
§2 . That the prudential,fiscal,and municipal concerns
of said town shall be vested in five trustees, who shall be
elected annually , on the firstSaturday in May , by the
qualifiedvoters of said town , and shall hold theiroffice
for one year ,and until their successors are elected and
qualified ; that said trustees and their successors are a
body -politicand corporate, and shall be known by the
name and style of the Board of Trustees of the town of
Loretto; and by that name shall be capable of contract-
ing,being contracted with , of suing and being sued ,of
pleading and being impleaded , of answering and being
answered , and of defending and being defended , in all
courts and places; and may use a common seal,and do
allotheractswhich a body corporateand politic,having
perpetual succession,may rightfullyand lawfully do and
perform .
§3 .The trusteesshallbe elected by the qualifiedvoters
of said town . The qualificationsof voters in the election
of trusteesshallbe a residence of sixmonths in the town ,
and twenty-one years of age. No person shallbe eligi-
ble to the office of trustee of said town who is not a citi-
zen thereof,and has not been a resident thereof twelve
months preceding the election; and no trusteeshallbe a
party to any contract in which the town of Loretto is
or may become a party. A majorityof thetrusteesshall
be a quorum to transact business.
§ 4. That sections five, seven , eight, nine ,and ten ,of
articleone ,also allthe provisions of articlesthree,four,
and five of an act,entitled"An act to amend an act and
reduce into one the several acts in regard to the cityof

574 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Lebanon ," approved February thetwenty-ninth,one thou-
sand eighthundred and eighty-four,with alltheirprovis-
ions,so far as applicable,are made part of this charter;
and allthe powers granted in said act to the cityof Leb-
anon shallbe exercised under thischarter by the town of
Loretto .
§5 . The trusteesof said town ,when they deem proper ,
may employ an attorney to prosecute allproceedings for
breaches of the ordinances of said town , and to prose-
cute or defend all actions on behalf of or against said
town , or in which it has an interest; and may pay such
compensation as shallbe agreed upon by the trusteesand
said attorney.
§ 6. Richard Davis is hereby appointed police judge ,
and JesseAlvey is appointedmarshal,and A. G. Blincoe,
Augustine Mills, W. J. Smith , Henry Lyon , and O. T.
Peterson, trustees, who shall hold officeuntil their suc-
cessors are elected and qualified.
§ 7. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 25, 1884.
CHAPTER 1115 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Meade county to subscribestock
in turnpike roads in said county , and to empower the levy court to
levy tax for the payment of same .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Meade county be , and it
ishereby,empowered to pay to any turnpike company
which has been, or may hereafterbe, incorporatedby
the county court of said county,or by the Legislatureof
the State of Kentucky , the sum of seven hundred and
fiftydollarstoward the erectionand completionof every
mile of turnpikewhich may be made in said county in
manner and form,and upon the condition,and subjectto
the limitations,as hereinafterprovided.
§ 2. Said county court shallnot pay or advance any
money untilthe company making applicationtherefor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 575
shallhave completed one mile of well-constructedand
durable turnpike road ,of gravel or stone,as required by
theirrespectivecharters, nor untilsaidcompany shall
have given in writing a statement showing that said
company has ample means or privatesubscriptionson
hand with which ,and the seven hundred and fiftydollars
per mile to be drawn from the county , to construct and
put in operationat leastthree(3) milesof turnpikeroad..
Before paying said subscription,the said court shallcause
saidroad to be carefullyexamined .
§ 3. Upon the payment of each seven hundred and fifty
dollarsby the said county court, the company to which it
ispaid shallissuea certificateof stockto the county of
Meade for the amount so paid by her ; and said county ,
actingthrough the county court,shallparticipatein the
control and management ,and share in the dividends of
any such road as any other stockholder.
§ 4. Said county court,in making payments and taking
stock hereinbefore provided ,shall not give preference to
any road over any other which is in process of construc-
tion,or is in good faithproposed to be built; but in tak-
ing such stock shallbe as nearly just to every portion of
the county as possible; but in no event shallsaid county
courtpay toward,or saidcounty be a stockholderin,the
roads of said county beyond the sum of thirtythousand
dollars .
§5 . All proceedings touching subscriptionsof stock by
said county shallbe kept of recordin the county court
clerk'soffice; and the county court clerk shallkeep a book
in which shall be recorded the date and amount of each
subscriptionof stock by the county of Meade ,in what
company , and when said subscriptionwas made .
§ 6. All dividendsreceivedby the county of Meade on
the stockheld by her in any turnpikecompany shallbe
paid into the treasury of the county as of the county
named .
§7 . Before said county court shallmake any subscrip-
tion of stockto any turnpikeroad company as authorized
by thisact,saidcourtshallsubmitto the qualifiedvoters

-576 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of Meade county ,at the regular electionto be held in said
county in August ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
four,the questionwhether or not the county of Meade
shall subscribe stock in the turnpike companies in said
county; and if a majorityof thosevoting shallvote in
favor of such subscription, then the county court shall
make the payınents and take the stock as herein provided .
For the purpose of ascertainingthe will of the people on
saidquestion,two columns shallbe opened in each poll-
book foreach precinct in said county at said regular elec-
tion,one headed " for turnpikes," and the other headed
" against turnpikes." Before such question isvoted upon ,
said county court shallcause notice of the factthat said
questionof the county subscribingstockinturnpikecom-
panies to the extent of thirtythousand dollars,to be ad-
vertisedfornot lessthan four(4) differentissuesof the
paper published in Meade county .
§ 8. If the county court shalldeem it best, it may , in-
stead of paying the subscriptionsherein authorizedin
money ,pay the same in the bonds of the county,to be
issued in denominations of one hundred dollars and five
hundred dollars, payable to bearer twenty years after
date , redeemable at any time after five years, bearing
five(5) per cent. interestfrom date,and payable semi-
annually on January firstand July firstof each year,
with coupons attached. Said bonds, if so issued, shall
be sold at not less than par ,or paid to the company in
which the stock istaken at theirface value . Said bonds ,
or theirproceeds,shallbe used in paying subscriptionto
stock as herein provided, and no other purpose. Said
bonds shall show on their face that they are in aid of
turnpikes,and the clerkof the county courtshallkeep a
fullregisterof all bonds sold under thisact,date of sale,
number ,and denomination of bond ,by whom bought ,and
when .
§ 9. The interestof the county in any road to the ex-
tent of itsstock in such road shall at no time be liable for
any debt or debts contracted by such company .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 577
§ 10. That the Meade county levy court,a majority of
the justicesof the peace for said county being present,is
hereby authorized to levy a poll-tax not exceeding one
dollar upon each male citizenof the county over twenty-
one years of age ,and an ad valorem tax not exceeding
thirtycents upon each one hundred dollarsof taxable
property in said county in any one year , for the purpose
of dischargingand paying off the bonds hereinbefore
mentioned and the interest on same .
§ 11. That said taxes shallbe collectedby the sheriffof
said county ,for the same commission and under the same
responsibilityas other taxes are now collected.
§ 12. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afterits passage .
Approved April 25 , 1884 .

CHAPTER 1117 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Merrimac,in Taylorcounty.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the citizensof Merrimac ,in Taylor county ,
be ,and are hereby ,created a body -politicand corporate ,
under the name of the Town of Merrimac , with the
power to contract and be contracted with , sue and be
sued,to plead and be impleaded,to answer and be an-
swered , and to do all such acts and things, eitherat law
or in equity,as bodies-politicand corporate,Laving per-
petual succession,may rightfullydo and perform .
§2 . The limits of said town shall be as follows: Be-
ginning on the northeastat Wesley Vaughn's residence;
thence south to John W. Helm ,including his residence;.
thence southwest to M. L. Douglas ; thence west to Mrs.
Diana Spurling's; thence north to Huston Murrell's,in-
cluding his residence; thence east to Nathan Cox's, in-
cluding his house ; thence southeast to the beginning .
§3 . The officersof said town shallconsistof a police
judge ,marshal ,clerk.

Murrell , R. T. Raikes , are hereby appointed trustees of
said town ,who shall appoint one of theirnumber presi-
dent of the board ,and shallcontinue in officeuntiltheir
successorsare elected and qualified.

day in each succeeding year ,an election shallbe held in
said town for the choice of five trustees of said town .
Said trustees,when elected,shallhave the same powers
and authority which is given under the general laws of
thisCommonwealth to trusteesof towns ; they shalltake

the oathtrulyand faithfullyto performthe dutiesimposed
upon them by law as trustees. Said trustees shall meet
and choose from among their number a president of the

tees,from time to time, to presideat theirmeetings, and
to signallordinancesand by-laws of saidtown by them
enacted . Said trusteesshallalso electa clerk,who shall

§6 . Said trusteesshallhave power to assessa poll-tax,

town ,and to levy an ad valorem tax on the real and per-

tees shall have power to appoint annually an assessor,

nually appoint a collector,to collectthe taxes assessedin

formance of his duties . Said collector shall have all the
powers in the collectionof said tax,by levy,distress,sale,

and otherwise,thatis given by law to sheriffsin the col-

allow said clerk or assessor,treasurer,and collector,a rea-

by the qualifiedvotersof saidtown ,on the firstSaturday
în May ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four, and

summons ,process,and subpœnas ,for any and all viola-

canciesin the officeofpolicejudge,marshal,clerk,treas-
urer, assessor,collector,shallbe filledin the same way .

rum forthe transactionof business, and when the presi-
dent of the board is absent the trusteesmay electone of
theirnumber as pro tem ,whose acts shallhave the same-

of clerk , assessor , collector, and treasurer of said town .

CHAPTER 1120 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Fredricktown and Bear Wallow Turnpike Road
Company ,and toauthorizetheWashington countycourttotakestockin
said company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the personswho may subscribestockforthe
purpose of constructinga turnpikeroad from Fredricks-
town to intersectthe St.Rose and Loretto Turnpike near
Chas .Miller'sfarm,in Washington county,Kentucky ,be .
and they arehereby,created a body -corporateand poli-
tic,under the name and style of the " Fredrickstown and
Bear Wallow Turnpike Road Company ;" and by said
name may sue and be sued,and shallhave perpetualsuc-
cession .
§2 . Said company shallbe under the management and
control of five directors, one of whom shall be chosen
president by said directors, and another shalllikewise be
chosen secretary and treasurer of said company . The
directors shallbe elected on the firstSaturday in May in
each year , except as hereinafterprovided.
§3 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe ten
thousand dollars, or such sum as may be necessary to
build said road, to be divided in shares of twenty five
dollarseach ,and each stockholder shallbe entitledto one
vote for each share owned by him or her. The stock-
holdersshallbe liablefor the corporatedebts only to
extentof theirstock,and theirprivatepropertyshallbe
exempt from the corporate debts.
§ 4. The road shallbe builtwith a grade not lessthan
twenty feet wide, with an elevationof not less than
twelve inches in the center , with a ditch on each side
and outside the grade of sufficientwidth and depth to
drain the road . The metal shall be not less than fourteen
feetwide and ten inches deep,of sound,hard rock,the
bottom rock to be five and one -half inches deep , and
broken so as to pass through a four and one -half inch
ring,with a top -dressing of rock broken so as to pass
through a two -inch ring. The top-dressing may be of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 585
sound ,hard gravel,in the discretionof the board of di-
rectors .
§5 . The board of directors shall locate the road and
determine the elevation .
§6 . Whenever two and one -half miles of said road
shallbe completed, the company may erecta toll-gate
and collectfrom persons travelingsaid road the tollsfor
the distance traveled ; and when the whole of said road
iscompleted ,itmay erect a gate at each end of the road
and collectat leastone -half toll; or itmay erectone gate
and collecttollsas provided by the General Statutesof
Kentucky .
§7 . The rightto condemn land for road -way ,rock for
building and keeping the road in repair, and for toll-
houses and lots,shallbe governed by the generallaws of
Kentucky in relationthereto.
§ 8. E. J. McIntire ,James B. Enser ,Thos .F. McIntire,
Jas.E. Kidwell,J. B. McIntire, F. M. Hill,T. J.Phillips,
Jas. A. Cecil, A. G. Hall,John Reynolds ,Wm .Smith ,and
Joseph A. Tongue are appointed commissionersto open
books forthe subscriptionof stock in said company ,at
such time and places as any three of them may deter-
mine ; and when two thousand five hundred dollars of
bonafidesolventprivatesubscriptionsto the capitalstock
of said company shallhave been received,the commis-
sionersherein named ,or a majorityof them ,may give
written notice to the stockholders to meet at a time and
place stated in the notice,also shallmeet at the time and
place statedin such notice and electdirectorsas provided
in section two of this act ; when said directors shall elect
the other officers named in said section ; when said offi-
cers and directorsshallbe,by some officerauthorized
to administer an oath ,sworn to faithfullyperform their
duties .
§9 . Said board of directors,or a committee appointed
by them ,may continue to receive subscriptionof stock in
said company untila sufficientamount is subscribedto
pay for building said road ; they may also receive con-
veyances to said company of right of way for said road ,
or metal with which to build same .

586 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 10. The stockholders shall pay their subscription in
calls of such amounts and at such times as the board of
directorsmay ,by resolution,direct.
§ 11. If any stockholdershallfailto pay the stocksub-
scribedby him ,or any part thereof, he may ,by a vote
of a majorityof the directors,be excludedfrom the right
of voting until the whole of stock subscribed by him shall
have been paid; and said company may sue for the same
in any court of this Commonwealth having jurisdiction
thereof
§ 12. Said board of directorsmay pass by laws regu-
lating their meetings , time and place of elections, and
allother business pertaining to the building or manage-
ment of the affairs of said road .
§ 13. The county court of Washington county ,a ma-
jorityof the justicesbeing present and concurring therein,
may ,forand in behalf of Washington county,subscribe
for stock in said company in any sum not exceeding one
thousand dollars per mile, upon such terms and condi-
tions that said court may deem proper.
§ 14. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after its passage .
Approved April 30 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 1121 .
AN ACT to incorporate the town of Commercial Point,in Union county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby incorporated the town of
Commercial Point, with the followingcorporate limits,
viz : Beginning at the mouth of a branch on Tradewater
river, it being a corner of I.F. Alloway and L. L. Tal-
bott; thence up said branch a distance of fiftypoles;
thence north to the lineof said Talbott and G. B. Simp-
son ; thence with said line to a ditch on the road ; thence
down said ditch to said river; thence up said river on the
low -water line to the beginning .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 587
§ 2. There shallbe electedannually,on the lastSatur-
day in June ,by the legal qualified voters of said town ,
five trustees, who shall be known as the board of trus-
teesof Commercial Point. Said trusteesshallhave per-
petual succession; shall in their corporate name have
power to sue or be sued ,implead and be impleaded ,
grant and receive, and do such other acts as natural
persons may do ; may make and enforce all needfül by-
laws and regulations for the government of said town ,
not inconsistentwith the laws of this State; and they
shall choose one of their number as chairman of the
board . L. L Talbott, I. F. Alloway , Fred . Alloway ,
Ben . Strauss, and William Buckman are appointed trus-
tees of said town until the firstelection hereunder , and
untiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified; and they
shallhave allthe powers herein conferred upon trustees
of said town . No one shall be eligibleto the office of
trustee who is not the owner of real estate in said town ,
and has not residedthereincontinuouslyforsix months
next preceding the election. Trustees shall hold office
for one year ,and until theirsuccessors are elected and
qualified; and before acting as such ,shall take an oath
to faithfullydischargethe dutiespertainingto the office
of trustee .
§3 . The trustees shall have power over the streets,
alleys, and sidewalks of said town ; and may order the
improvement of the same as they may deem best for the
interestsof the town . They shallhave power to levy
and collect an ad valorem tax of not more than twenty-
fivecents on the hundred dollars,and a poll-tax of not
more than two dollars on the male citizens of said town
of twenty-one years old,which taxes may be leviedand
collectedonce in each year. They shallhave power to
tax auction sales,shows , and exhibitions for money or
profit. They shallhave power to lay offsaid town in
lotswith streetsand alleys,and have the plat thereof
recordedin the Union county clerk'soffice. They shall
have power to fillvacancies in the board of trustees,
police judge ,marshal ,or other town officer. They shall

588 LAWS OF KENTUCKY ,
have power to remove nuisances, suppress tippling-
houses ,and punish those guilty of riots,routs,breaches of
the peace ,disturbingreligiousworship , and other assem-
blies, and such other offenses as may be so declared by
theirby -laws and ordinances,and inflictsuch fines and
penaltiesas theirby -laws impose. It is expresslypro-
vided that said trustees shall not have power to grant
license to sellspirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors in said
town ; and it shall be unlawful hereafter to sell any of
said liquors, or a mixture of either, within the limits of
said town ,except by a regular druggist upon the pre-
scriptionof a regularphysician,which prescriptionshall
be filledbut once . Said trusteesshallhave power to tax
animals running at large in said town . They shall ap-
point annuallya town assessor,and prescribehis duties
and compensation. They shallappoint annually a clerk
of the board of trustees and a treasurer of the town , and
prescribe their duties; and shall require a bond , with
good security, from the treasurer, conditionedfor the
faithfulperformance of his duties,and the payment of
the money thatmay come to hishands.
§4 . All taxes levied and collectedby said trustees, and
all fines and forfeitures for violation of the town ordi-
nances and by -laws ,shallbe paid to the treasurerfor the
benefit of the town .
§5 . No money shallbe drawn from the treasury except
by order of the president of the board of trustees,in per-
suance of the orders of said board .
§6. There shallbe electedby the qualifiedvoters of
said town , on the last Saturday in June , one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-four, and biennially thereafter,
a police judge of said town ,who shallhold his officefor
two years, and untilhis successor iselectedand qualified.
Said judge shall,by virtue of his office,be a conservator
of the peace ,and before he enters upon the discharge of
the dutiesof the officeshalltake an oath to faithfully
and impartially execute all of the duties of the office.
Said judge shallhave concurrentjurisdictionwith jus-
ticesof the peace of Union county ,and be entitledto the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 889
same fees. He shall have jurisdictionwithin the town
of all infractionsof the by -laws and ordinances of said
town ; but in case of vacancy in the office,or absence or
interestof the judge,the nearestjusticeshallhave juris-
diction of offenses against said by -laws and ordinances.
Appeals shallbe granted from said court as isprovided in
appeals from justices' courts. The terms of the police
courtshallbe fixedby theby-laws of the trustees.
§7 . There shallbe electedby the qualifiedvotersof
said town ,on the last Saturday in June,one thousand
eighthundredand eighty-four,and bienniallythereafter,
a marshal for said town , who shall hold his office for two
years ,and until his successor is elected and qualified.
Before enteringupon hisofficehe shalltake the oath and
executethebond requiredof constablesof Union county,
which bond shall be firstapproved by the trusteesof the
town before itsapproval by the county court. Said mar-
shal shallhave concurrent jurisdictionwith constables of
Union county ,and be entitledto the same fees for his ser-
vices. Itshallbe his specialduty to executeallprocess
issued for an alleged violationof by laws and ordinances
of said town ,and to promptly collectalltaxes,fines,and
forfeitures, and pay the same to the treasurer. He shall
discharge such other duties as are fixedby the ordinances
and by-laws ofthetown ,and receivesuch compensations
as are fixedby ordinance or by -law .
§ 8. This act shall take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 30 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 1122 .
AN ACT to regulatethe jurisdictionof judicialand ministerialofficersin
the countiesof Bell,Harlan , Perry , and Leslie.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That ifany person shallfilehis or her affidavit
with a magistrate,county or policejudge ,in either of the
counties of Bell, Harlan, Perry,or Leslie,showing that

590 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
another person , within three months of the time of filing
such affidavit,has been guilty of a violation of the law
prohibiting the sale of ardent spiritsin such county , it
shall be the duty of such officerto immediately issue a
warrant of arrestagainstthe offender,and place same in
the hands of a constable,jailer, or sheriffof his county ,
who shallimmediately arrest the offender,and carry him
before the court issuing the warrant , which court shall
speedily examine the offender; and if such court shall
findthere are reasonable grounds for believing the party
guilty,he shallhold him to bail in the sum of two hun-
dred dollarsforhisappearance in the circuitcourtof the
county in which the offense was committed ,to answer
such indictment as the grand jury thereof may find
against him .
§2 . If such magistrate,police judge , or county judge
fail to issue such warrant , or failto comply with the
other conditions of this act, or if any constable, jailer,
or sheriff shall fail to arrest such offender , if the offender
can be found within his bailiwick,the officerthus offend-
ing shallbe deemed to have been guilty of willfulneg-
lect in the discharge of his officialduties, and , upon
indictment of a grand jury and conviction thereof, shall
be fined in any sum not exceeding one hundred dollars,
and not less than seventy-five dollars, and shallbe re-
moved from office .
§3 . This act shall take effect from and after the first
day of June ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four.
Approved April 30, 1884 .
