CHAPTER 1152 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Wm . Hatchitt,latesheriffof Henderson county .
WHEREAS , On June twenty-sixth, one thousand eight
hundred and seventy -nine,an execution in favor of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky for unpaid revenue due to
the Commonwealth was issuedagainst Ben . F. Gibson
(late sheriffof Henderson county) and the suretieson
his revenue bond , executed in January , one thousand
eight hundred and seventy-eight, for the sum of nineteen
thousand four hundred and eighty-six dollars and fifty-
seven cents, and interestand costs, which fi fa. was
placed in the hands of William Hatchitt, sheriffof said
county , with directions to levy, and said Hatchitt did
levy same on a small amount of personalpropertyand
upon a large amount of real estateof the saidsureties,
the greater part of which real estate had been conveyed
away or mortgaged by the sureties; and at the execution
saleof saidpropertyonly the sum of thirty-threedollars
and eighteen cents was realized, and Hatchitt's legal
commissions only amounted to two dollars,and he was
put to labor and expense in making such levy and in
advertising; and whereas , the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky, through itsproceedings in chancery afterwards,
realized from the sales of a portion of said land , and
from compromises with the sureties aforesaid, a large
sum of money ,and no provision in the statutes author-
izes the payment to said Hatchitt of any adequate com-
pensation for his aforementioned services, and it is
deemed that the sum of one hundred dollars should be
paid to him as a bare act of justice; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts is directed to
draw hiswarrant upon the StateTreasurerfor the sum
of one hundred dollars,payable to the saidWm . Hatch-
itt,as compensation to said Hatchitt for said services.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 30 , 1884 .

CHAPTER 1153 .
AN ACT to legalizethe ordersand judgments of thePendletonquarterly
court .
WHEREAS , W. J. Perrin, late judge of the Pendleton
quarterlycourt,failedto sign the ordersor proceedings
of said court ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. J. Perrin, late judge of the Pendleton
quarterly court,be , and he is hereby , authorized to sign
his name as judge of said court to said orders and pro-
ceedings of said court; and the signing of the signature
of said Perrin as judge aforesaid is hereby made legal,
and the same proceedings may be had on the said orders
and judgments of said court as if same had been signed
by said Perrin at the proper time .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 28 , 1884.

CHAPTER 1155 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Adair County AgriculturalAssociation.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. H Hudson , James P. Dehoney , G. W.
Atkins , G. A. Thomas , H. C. Baker , W. T. Moore , and
W. B. Rowe ,and theirsuccessors, are hereby created a
body -corporate and politic, with perpetual succession,
under the name of the Adair County AgriculturalAsso-
ciation ; and by that name may contract and be con-
tracted with, sue and be sued ; have and use a common
seal, and alterthe same at pleasure. The object and
purposes of saidcorporationshallbe topromote improve

65 LAWS OF KENTUCKY
ment in allbranches in agriculture, and to encourage the
raisingof domesticanimals,and in improving breeds of
same .
§2 . The capitalstockof saidcorporationshallbe three
thousand dollars,and may be increased to ten thousand
dollars,tobe divided intoshares of fiftydollarseach . One
fourthof each share shallbe paid in by firstday of May ,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,the residue
in such amounts at such times as the board of directors
may determine .
§3. The affairsof saidcompany shallbe under the
control and management of seven directors,one of whom
shallbe president; and by such they may appoint other
officersand agents as they deem necessary. The direct-
ors shall be chosen by the stockholders annually on the
firstSaturday in May . The directorsshall have full
power and authority to make all rules and regulations
for the government of the corporation.
§ 4. The said corporation shall have power to lease,
purchase,or acquire,by giftor devise,any real or per-
sonal estate,and hold the same in fee-simple or other-
wise,and sellor convey or otherwisedisposeof same ,or
any partthereof,at any time or in any manner the ma-
jorityof the directorsmay determine .
§ 5. The private property of the stockholdersshallnot
be liablefor the corporate debts of the company .
§6 . The corporation may hold on itsfairgrounds ,near
Columbia , one or more fairseach year ,for the exhibition
of agriculturaland mechanical products and live stock;
and when such fairs are held the board of directors of
said company may appoint as many marshals as they
deem necessary, not exceeding four in number , who
shallbe peace officers,and who may hold their office
during the continuance of the fairwhen they are ap-
pointed; but before said marshals enter upon the dis-
charge of the dutiesof theirsaidofficethey shall,before
the Adair county court,take the oath required by law of
constables, and shall in said court execute bond , with
good security, payable to the Commonwealth of Ken-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 6 : 3
tucky, conditionedfor the faithful, just, and impartial
discharge of theirduty as peace officers, which bond
shall be filed in the office of the clerk of said court .
Any person injuredor aggrievedby any act of said mar-
shalsmay recover on said bond such damages as he may
sustainby said marshals so appointed and qualified.
§ 7. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 28, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1156 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Winchester and Two Mile Turnpike Road Com
pany .

of Kentucky :
§ 1.That a corporationishereby created,to be known
as the Winchester and Two Mile Turnpike Road Com-
pany; and by that name shallhave allthe powers,priv-
ileges,and franchisesenumerated in section two of chap-
terfifty-sixof the General Statutesof thisCommonwealth .

erating same .

right of way or material, or a site of any of its tol!-
houses ,by purchase or agreement ,it may condemn same
as is provided by law for condemning rights of way for
railroads , and so forth.

Pendleton,Sam . F. Moore , W. H. Ragland ,John B. No-
land, JesseG. Hart, PhilipRutlege, James Rutlege, J.

hundred dollars each .

ent with the laws of the land .

CHAPTER 1159 .
AN ACT for the benefitof common school districtnumber fifty-one , in
Jefferson county .
WHEREAS , By oversightof the commissioner of com-
mon schoolsfor Jeffersoncounty, thirtypupil children
were omitted in the census report of common school dis-
trictnumber fifty-one ,for the school-year ending June
thirtieth, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four;
therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Superintendent of Public Instruction is
hereby authorized to draw his draft on the Auditor of
Public Accounts in favor of Abner J. Smith , commis-
sioner of common schools for said county , for the sum of
forty-two dollarsand sixtycents($42.60),on which shalt

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 665
be paid four dollarsand twenty-one cents ($4.21),bal-
ance of unbonded interestdue the county aforesaid on its
surplusbond,and thirty-eightdollarsand thirty-nine cents
($38.39) out of the common school fund forthe present
school-year ending June thirtieth, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty -four .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 29, 1884.
CHAPTER 1160 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to incorporatethe Louisville,
Harrodsburg, and Virginia Railroad Company ," approved March ninth,
one thousand eight hundred and sixty-eight,and to change the name of
saidcompany tothatof theLouisvilleSouthernRailroadCompany .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That section four of the amendment to this act ,
approved March the sixth,one thousand eight hundred
and sixty-nine, which is in the following words: That
any town , city, corporation, or counties are hereby en-
abled and empowered to subscribe for stock in said com-
pany upon the following terms : said stock shallbe sub-
scribedby said town ,city,corporation,county or counties,
upon the consent of the majority of the votes therein
cast,said election to be petitionedforby said president
and directors; and there shallbe ordered to be held by
the countyjudge of said county,by the presidentof the
board of trustees of said town , by the president of the
board of directorsof said corporation, as the election
for county officesis held in said county , as the election
for town officesis held in said town , and as the election
for president and directorsisheld under said corporation,
giving thirty days' notice of said election in each in-
stance : Provided, That as to the city of Louisville, the
general council of said citybe ,and are hereby , author-
ized to order, by ordinance ,in compliance with the char-
ter of said city, an election to be held,after due notice
as provided by the charter of said city,by advertisement
in the papers of said city. In case said cityagrees to

take stock in said road ,itmay be paid upon such terms
and conditions as may be agreed by said company and
the general council of said city; and when the terms and
conditions so agreed upon shall have been voted upon
and carried by a majority of the legal votes cast at said
election,itshallthen be the duty of the mayor of said
cityto issuethe bonds of the said citytherefor,with the
corporate seal of said city affixed, bearing an interest
not less than seven per cent. and no more than eight
per cent. per annum , payable semi-annually at New
York City, to be issued within sixtydays aftersaid elec-
tion, and when issued,to be delivered to the president of
said company "-be amended to read as follows : Section
one . That any town , city, or county ,is hereby enabled
and empowered to subscribe to the stock of said Louis-
ville,Harrodsburg ,and Virginia Railroad Company ,upon
the following terms : Said stock shall be subscribed for
by said town , city,or county ,by the consent of a ma-
jorityof the votes cast at an electionpetitionedforand
ordered as follows: Whenever a petition for such elec-
tionand subscriptionis signed by one-tenth of the tax-
payers of any town ,city,or county ,it shallbe the duty
of the county judges , or courts in cases of
county ,and the president of the board of trusteesin case
of any town , and of the mayor in the case of any city,
to order an election,giving twenty days ' notice thereof
by publicationin any newspaper published in said coun-
ty,provided there be one , and by hand -billsposted in at
least ten public places in such town ,city,or county , of
such election . The officers of the last election held in
such town ,city,or county shallbe the officersof theelec-
tion held hereunder : Provided,however, That in case of
death or disability,officersof the electionshallbe ap-
pointed as requiredby statuteor ordinance forthe ap-
pointment of officersof elections. The petitionshall set
forththe terms of such subscriptionand the conditions of
itspayment ,and the same shallbe setout in the publica-
tion herein provided for: Provided,however, That as to
the cityof Louisville,itshallbe lawful for said cityto in-
or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 667
dorse the bonds of said railroad company to an amount
not exceeding one-tenth of the authorizedcapitalstock
of said company ,guaranteeing the payment of the prin-
cipal and interestthereof. Itshallbe the duty of the mayor
of said city of Louisville,when a petition is signed by
fivehundred of the tax-payers of saidcity,ifpresented,
to issue an order submitting the question of such indorse-
ment tobe votedupon by the people of saidcityof Louis-
ville. The petitionshallsetforththe terms and condi-
tions upon which such indorsement is to be made ; and
when such election is so ordered , the same shall be voted
on as follows: A poll-book shallbe prepared with two
columns ,one headed "For the railroad proposition," the
other headed " Against the railroad proposition." The
officersof the last cityelection in said city shall be the
officersof the electionprovided for herein,and any va-
cancy shallbe filledas now required by law or ordinance .
And ifa majority of the votes cast shallbe forthe railroad
proposition,then itshallbe the duty of the mayor to in-

tween thesaidcityand the saidrailroadcompany ; orthe

setforththerein,and duly passed as providedforin rela-
tion to other ordinances of said city. In case the said

shallnot be lawfulforitto subscribeforany of the cap-
italstock of said railroadcompany .
§2 . The said Louisville, Harrodsburg , and Virginia
Railroad Company is authorizedand empowered to con-
tract with any company or person for the construction of
its said line of railroad, and to make payment for the
same in stock or bonds or other assetsof said corpora-
tion then in possession or thereafter acquired : Provided,
however , That such agreement or contract shall be ap .
proved by three fifthsof the stockholders of said railroad
company of record at the time of making such agree-
ment or contract .

§3 . That the saidrailroadcompany is hereby enabled
and empowered to buy or lease other roads connecting
therewith, or to consolidate with any railway incorpo-
rated under the laws of the State of Kentucky ; and it

in the State of Kentucky , not exceeding fiftymiles from
its main line, and to bond separately said branch or
branches,and to issuebonds forany amount not exceed-
ing twenty -five thousand dollars per mile upon said
branch or branches .

Assembly of Kentucky that, under the authority of an
act, entitled" An act to amend an act,entitled'An act to
incorporate the Louisville, Harrodsburg , and Virginia
Railroad , " approved March eleventh,one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-two ,the said corporation, upon the

firstday of April, one thousand eight hundred and eigh-
ty-two , change its name to that of the Louisville South-
ern Railroad Company ,itishereby enacted that the said
change of name is hereby approved and confirmed,and
that the said corporation be hereafter known and called
the LouisvilleSouthern Railroad Company ; and by said
lastmentioned name shallhave allthe rights,powers ,
and privilegesconferredby the originalcharter,approved
March ninth, one thousand eight hundred and sixty-eight,

upon the Louisville, Harrodsburg ,and Virginia Railroad :

derson,Fayette, Madison , Estill, Rockcastle,Knox ,Bell,
Harlan ,and Laurel ,nor eitherof said counties as a whole ,
shall subscribe for any part of the capital stock of said
company .
§5 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 28 , 1884 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 669
CHAPTER 1161 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county of McLean to vote on the road tax.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the levy court of McLean county shall elect
a superintendent of the roads, in and for each voting
precinct in said county ,at the court of claims ; and each
superintendent thus elected shall enter upon the duties
of the officeon the firstday of January next succeeding
his election,and shall hold the same for two years,and
until his successor is duly elected and qualified; and
before entering on the duties of his office he shall be
sworn to well and trulydischarge the duties devolved
upon him as superintendent of roads. It shall be his
duty to superintend allcounty roads in the precinct,keep
them in good order,build all culverts and bridges,not
exceeding twenty feetin length, on said roads,and open
or change any road or roads that the county court may ,
from time to time ,direct. In the event of a vacancy in
said officeby resignation, death ,or otherwise,it shall be
the duty of the presiding judge of the county court to
fillthe same until the next meeting of the levy court,
when that court shallfillthe vacancy as in regular elec-
tions .
§2 . That each superintendent shall be empowered to
purchase all necessary tools and implements , and hire
all hands necessary for such work at the lowest rates;
and hold alltoolsand implements purchased by him as
county property, and shall deliver the same to his suc-
cessor, and take his receipt therefor, which receipt he
shall filein the officeof the county clerk within thirty
days afteritis received.
§3 . That each superintendent shallmake a fulland
accurate report of his transactions,embracing allmoneys
received and paid out by him , to whom paid,and for
what purpose,togetherwith the number of days he was
actually engaged in the duties herein required, to the
county court,on the firstMonday in October and the first
Monday in February of each year,or at any term of the

670 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
county court,upon reasonable notice; and he shall exe-
cute bond , with good security, to be approved by the
county judge .
§4 . That each superintendent shallbe paid a reason-
able compensation for his services,to be fixed annually
by the court of claims .
§5 . That said levy court, at its annual sessions, shall
assess a tax of not exceeding ten cents on each one hun-
dred dollars' worth of taxable property in said county ,
and not exceeding two dollars on each male citizen of
the age of twenty -one years and over, as a separate fund
for the purposes herein provided : Provided, That allde-
linquenttax-payers may work out the amount due by
them under the supervisionof the superintendent,at the
rate of one dollar per day .
§ 6. That said taxes shallbe collectedby the sheriffof
the county as any other county levy, and under the same
responsibilitiesand obligations; and he shallonly pay
out the same upon the certificateof a superintendent,
indorsed " approved " by the county judge ,or upon the
order of the court of claims ; and he shall make an an-
nual settlement of his accounts with the court of claims
on the firstMonday in October .
§7 . That the county judge shall have this act posted
in not lessthan fiveconspicuousplacesin eachvoting
precinct, and published in the county newspaper one
month before any election at which thisact is voted on
in said county .
§ 8. This act shall take effectwhenever a majority of
the votes cast at a general election shall be in favor of a
road tax; and the county judge of said county shallcause
a vote to be taken at the firstgeneral election,and as
often thereafteras he may think necessary, for or against
the road tax. And all acts or parts of acts in conflict
with thisact are hereby repealed.
Approved April 28 , 1884.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 671
CHAPTER 1162 .
AN ACT to provide for good roads in Todd county, and to regulate tax-
ation in the same .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. Itshallbe the duty of the judge of the Todd coun-
tycourtto dividesaidcounty intoroad precincts,and ,as
oftenas may be necessary,to appoint a surveyorin each
of said precincts,whose duty it shall be to superintend
the roads therein,and to see that the same are worked
and kept in such repair as the agreement of the contract-
or thereon with the Todd county court requires. No sur-
veyor shallbe allowed , unless by consent of the court,to
resign such place under two years,except he removes
fromtheprecinct. Any surveyormay be removed atany
time by said court.
§2 . The clerk of the county court of said county shall,
withinten days afterthe appointment of a surveyorof a
road precinct,delivera copy of the orderof saidappoint-
ment to the sheriffof said county ,containing a fulland
complete description of the said precinct and the roads
therein, and take his receipt therefor. And said sheriff
shall, within fifteendays thereafter,deliver a copy of the
said order to the said surveyor , and return another copy
thereof to the clerk's office of said court , with his indorse-
ment of the fact and time of said service made thereon ;
which return shall be evidence of the facts therein stated .
Said clerk shallpost up in his office,once in every year ,a
listof the names of all the surveyors of road precincts
in saidcounty,to be made as aforesaid,designatingthe
precinctof each surveyor. The clerkor sherifffailingto
performhisdutiesas prescribedin thisact shallbe guilty
of a misdemeanor , and , on conviction thereof, shall be
fined five dollars for each neglect,to be recovered on a
warrant against him in the name of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky before the judge of the quarterlycourt or
any justiceof the peace of Todd county.
§ 3. Each surveyor of a road precinct in said county
shallbe furnishedwith a copy of the contractfor the

672 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
working of the roads in his precinct; and it shall be his
duty to reportto the county judge of Todd county any
failureon the part of the contractor to perform his duties
under said contract, and also to certifyto said judge
when the said contract has been properly complied with .
Said surveyor shall be exempt from jury services, and
from alltaxation for county purposes ,during his term of
service as surveyor as aforesaid. For a failureto dis-
charge any of his dutiesprescribedby thisact,saidsur-
veyor shallbe guiltyof a misdemeanor , and shall, on
conviction thereof, be fined not less than five nor more
than fifteendollars for each offense, to be recovered by
warrant against him in the name of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , in the Todd quarterly court, which court
shallhave exclusivejurisdictionof allproceedings against
surveyors of road precincts.
§ 4. The judge of the Todd county court shall, at the.
court-house door in Elkton ,Todd county ,Kentucky ,on the
second Monday in March of each year , and at such other
time as he shall deem necessary,letout the working and
keeping in repair and improving of the public roads in
Todd county to the lowest and best bidder,for such length
of time ,not exceeding two years ,as he may elect, after
having firstadvertised the time ,terms , and place of said
lettingby at least two insertionsin a newspaper pub-
lishedin Todd county ,if one should be published in the
county at the time , and by a printed notice posted up for
at least fifteendays before such lettingat the voting
place or places in each voting precinct in said county :
Provided , That the fund raised by the levy court under
this act shall be sufficientto work all of the public roads
in said county : And provided further,That if said funds
should not be sufficientto keep in order and good condi-
tion all the public roads of said county ,then, and in that
event,said fund shallbe used at only such places and for
such roads as the said judge may deem best,having due
regard to the public good and the wants of the different
parts of the said county .

§5 . Each road contractorshallexecute a bond ,with
good surety.payable to Todd county,covenantingto per-
formhiscontract,which shallbe setforthand specifiedin
saidbond ,and which bond shallbe approved by the said
judge,and by him deliveredto the clerkof said court,
who shallkeep the same as a part of the recordsof his
office,and on which bond said county may recover of said
contractor,by action in the Todd circuitcourt,alldam-
ages which itmay sustainby the failureof saidcontract-
or to comply with the terms of said contract.

fied, by the report of the surveyor of the precinct for

contract, to make an order on the order-book of said
court directingthe sheriffof said county to pay said con-
tractorthe contractpriceforsaidwork . Said judge may
make an order or orders directing the contract price for
said work to be paid from time to time during the pro-
gress of saidwork ,but shallnever order payment to be
made for any work not actuallyperformed,and shallat
all times,before the completion of said work according
to contract,retain or keep back a sufficientamount of
the said contractprice to insurethe completion of said
work according to contract.
§7 . All fines assessed under this act shallbe collected
as other fines,and shallinure to the benefit of the road
fund of Todd county ,and shallbe paid out by the sheriff
of Todd county , under the orders of the county judge
thereof .
§ 8. The judge of the Todd county court shallprovide
work on the roads of said county,or on the streetsof
towns therein, for such persons convictedof penal of-
fenses as may , by order of the proper authorities, be
required to work on roads or streets,or to work out fines
assessed against them on the roads or streetsunder the
provisions of general or speciallaws, and may , when
necessary,put a ball and chain upon such persons to
LOC . L.,VOL 11-43

674 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
prevent their escape; and, if necessary, may employ
some suitableperson to oversee and guard them while at
work .
§ 9. That all delinquent capitation tax-payers , and
otherswhose taxes cannot be collected,of Todd county,
shallbe required to work out their delinquent taxes, at
such times on the roads in theirseveral road precincts as
the surveyor thereof may require,or as the judge of the
Todd county court may contract for; for which work
such delinquents shallhave creditat the rate of one dol-
larforeach fullday'swork performed by him. For fail-
ing or refusing to comply with the orders of said sur-
veyors, when notified, all such delinquents are made
liableto the penaltiesnow imposed by law againstper-
sons for failingor refusingto work on roads.
§ 10. The county court of Todd county may open
roads,not exceeding sixtyfeet in width ,or may increase
the width of establishedroads to sixtyfeet, anything in
the generallaws to the contrarynotwithstanding.
§ 11.It shallbe the duty of the county attorney to
prosecute all offenders under this act, for which service
he shallreceive twenty -fiveper cent. of the fines recov-
ered; and upon his failure to prosecute, the court may
appoint some other attorney to prosecute in his stead,
who shallreceive the same compensation forhis services.
All fines assessed under this act shall be collected as
other fines, and shall inure to the benefit of the road
fund of Todd county ,and shallbe paid out by the sheriff
under orders of the Todd county court.
§ 12. It shall be unlawful for the judge of the Todd
county court,the county attorney,sheriff,collectorof the
revenue , or any surveyor of a road precinct of Todd
county,tobecome ,directlyor indirectly,interestedin any
contract for working roads, or building or repairing
bridges in Todd county; and it shall be unlawful for
eitherof said officersto buy , or to become interestedin,
any claim growing out of said work or contract. For a
violation of this section, any of said officersshall be
guiltyof a misdemeanor , and , upon convictionthereof,
shallbe fined not less than fiftynor more than fivehun

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 675
dred dollars,to be recovered on indictment of the grand
jury in the Todd circuitcourt; and thissectionshallbe
given in charge to the grand jury of the Todd circuit
court .
§ 13. Any person who shallwillfullyor negligently
destroy, injure, or obstructany of the public roads or
public bridges of Todd county ,or any of the culverts or
ditcheson said roads,shallbe guiltyof a misdemeanor ,
and, upon conviction, finednot lessthan ten or more
than fiftydollars,to be recovered on warrant in the Todd
quarterlycourt, and shallalso be liablein double dam-
ages to Todd county , or to any persons aggrieved or
injured,to be recoveredby suitin any courtof thisCom-
monwealth having jurisdictionof the amount . It shall
be the duty ofthe surveyorof any road precinctin which
any such damage or injuryis done to reportthe same
promptly to thejudge of the Todd county court.
§14 . The court of claims of Todd county shall, at their
regular sessionin each year ,or at such other time as they
may deem best, levy for each year a tax of not more
than fifteen cents on each one hundred dollars ' worth of
propertyin Todd county,which may be assessedforrev-
enue purposes,and a capitationtaxof notexceedingone
dollar and fiftycents on each person in said county who ,
under existinglaws ,isrequired to work on roads .
§ 15. All the taxes provided for in this act shallbe col-
lectedby the sheriffof Todd county,forwhich he shall
be paid the same compensation as he is now allowed by
law for collectingthe county levy; and said sheriffand
the suretieson his bond for the collectionof the county
revenue shallbe responsibleforallthe moneys that come
to his hands under the provisions of thisact.
§ 16. All the taxes collected under this act by an ad
valorem tax, and all fines that may be collected under
thisact,and such part of the taxes collected under this
act by a capitationtax as the courtof claims of Todd
county may , from time to time ,designate, shall consti-
tute a fund ,to be known as the road fund ,and shall,
when collected,be held by the sheriffof Todd county,

676 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and by him paid,when and as orderedby the judge of
the Todd countycourt,accordingto theprovisionsof this
act .
§ 17. The county judge of Todd county ,in advertising
and in making contracts for work to be done on the
public roads of Todd county,shallhave fullpower and
authority to advertise and contract for all such work ,
repairs, and improvements to be done on any of the
publicroadsof Todd county as may ,in hisjudgment , be
for the best interestof said county ; and this power may
likewisebe exercisedby saidjudge in the opening or
widening of any road .
§ 18. It shall be unlawful for the judge of the Todd
county court, or for the court of claims or county court
of Todd county ,to use or apply any of the finesor any of
the money collectedon ad valorem tax under the provis-
ions of this act,for any purpose other than the working ,
repairing, and improving of the public roads of Todd
county as providedby thisact.
§ 19. In making contracts for the working ,repairing,
or improvement ofthepublicroadsasaforesaid,thejudge
of the Todd county court may make such terms with the
contractor or contractors of any such work , as to the
work or labor to be performed by delinquent tax-payers ,
or by personsconvictedof penal offenses,as may be best
forsaid county ; and in case the saidjudge makes any such
agreement astodelinquenttax-payers,itshallbe the duty
of the surveyor of the road precinct in which said delin-
quents live,to assistas far as he isablein carryingout
said contract ,
§20 . The court of claims of Todd county shallnot
have the power to make forroad purposes,or forthe or-
dinary expenses of the county ,any other or greater levy
of taxes, either by ad valorem or capitation tax, than
they are authorizedto do by the fourteenthsectionof this
act .
§ 21.No provisionsof the general law is repealedby
thisact which does notcome inconflictwithitsprovisions.

§ 22. This act shall not take effectunless and until it
isratifiedby a majorityof allof the qualifiedvotersof

tionfor one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four; and
forthe purpose of taking the sense of said voters thereon
at said election, it shall be the duty of the sheriff of

posted at each voting place, and at two other public
placesin each voting precinctin Todd county,and in a
newspaper printed in Todd county ,ifthere should then be

regular electionto be held on the firstMonday in August ,
one thousand eighthundred and eighty-four,at each vot-
ing place in Todd county , to take the sense of the voters

a book suitableforthatpurpose ,one column of which shall

you for or against the road law ? " and itshallbe the duty
of theclerkof saidelectiontoproperlyrecordeach voter's
vote on said question,and of the officersof the election
at each of said voting places in said county to count and

shall be returned to the office of the clerk of the Todd

turned. Said poll-books shallbe compared and the result

electionon saidquestionshallvote forsaidlaw,thejudge
of the Todd county court shallstatethatfacton the record-
book of said court,after which this act shall be in full
force and effect .

678 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1163 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Bowling Green and Dishmon'sMillTurnpike
Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That J.A. Briggs,D. H. Philips,John Dishmon ,B.
W. Burge,W. F. Ennis,F.G. Cox , and J. B. Posey,and
theirassociatesand successors,be,and they are hereby,
created a body -politicand corporate,under the name and
styleof the " Bowling Green and Dishmon MillTurnpike
Company ;" and by thatname and styleshallhave power
to contractand be contractedwith,sue and be sued,an-
swer and be answered, plead and be impleaded, in all
courtsand placesin thisCommonwealth ; and may have
and use a common seal,and may alteror change the same
at pleasure .
§2 . The said company is hereby authorizedand em-
powered to constructa turnpike road,macadamized or
gravel,in itsdiscretion, from the corporate limitsof the
cityof Bowling Green , at some point on the western
boundary thereof, near the residence of J. W. Gorin ,
toward Hadley or Green Castle,in Warren county,pass-
ing near,or in the neighborhood of,Dishmon's Mill,or
any routeto be chosen by the company ,to be locatedon
the most practicableroute between said points; and in
order to raisethe funds necessary to construct said road ,
saidcompany shallhave power to raisea sum by the sale
and issueof itscapitalstock,not to exceed thirtythou-
sand dollars,to be divided into shares of fiftydollarseach .
§3 . That the books forthe subscriptionof stockshall
be opened in the cityof Bowling Green ,and such other
places as may be deemed best,under the directionof the
personsheretoforenamed ,allof whom are hereby made
commissionersfor the purpose of obtainingthe capital
stock of said company . They shall insert an obligation
in the subscriptionbook or list,substantiallyas follows:
" We , whose names are hereunto subscribed, severally
promise to pay to the Bowling Green and Dishmon Mill
Turnpike Company the sum of fiftydollarsforeach share

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 679
of stockin saidcompany setoppositeour names ,in such
manner and proportion and at such times as shallbe re-
quired by said company . Witness our hands , this
day of , one thousand eight hundred and eighty
- . " When the sum of four thousand dollars shall
have been subscribed to the capital stock of said com-
pany , it shall be the duty of said commissioners named
in this act firstto give notice, in such manner as they
may think proper, of a meeting of the stockholders of
said company ,at such time and place as may be specified
in said notice,for the purpose of electing five directors.
One vote shallbe allowed for each share of stock; and
the directors,when elected,shallchoose one of theirnum-
ber president; and the president and directorsshallcon-
tinue in officefor one year ,and untiltheirsuccessors are
elected and qualified. The time and place of allelec-
tions,afterthe firstelection,shallbe fixedby the presi-
dent and directors; and a majorityof the board shallbe
competent to transact business .
§4. So soon as saidcompany isorganizedby the elec-
tion of a president and directorsas above specified,they
shallbe a body-politicand corporate,by the name and
styleabove stated; and by that name shallhave perpet-
ual succession,and allthe privilegesand franchisesinci-
dentto a corporation; shallbe capableof holdingtheir
capital stock, and taking and holding, by purchase or
gift,allsuch lands,tenements , and hereditaments ,real or
personal property, as may be necessary for the prosecu .
tion of itswork or the objects of thiscorporation.
§ 5. The president of said company may callmeetings
of the board of directorswhenever he may thinkproper,
and on application of any two directors to him . The
board of directors shallhave power to elect a secretary
and treasurer, and such other officersas they may think
necessary,and to fillsuch vacancies as may occur in said
board during their term of office. Said board shall re-
quire of the treasurer,before he assumes the duties of
his office, a bond for the faithfuland honest discharge of
his duties, and to faithfullyaccount for all moneys that

680 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
may come tohishandsassuchtreasurer,whichbond and
securityshallbe approved by the board. Said board
shallkeep a record of their proceedings, which shallbe
signedby thepresident; and in the absence of the presi-
dent , the board may elect a president pro tem. Said
board shallhave power to make all necessaryby -laws
and regulations for the government of said company ,
and may sue for and recover,in the name of the com-
pany ,from delinquentstockholders,the amount due and
unpaid upon any share or shares of stock in said com-
pany .
§ 6. The president and directorsshallfixand regulate
the elevation and grade of said road ,the width and part
thereof to be covered with stone or gravel,and may fix
theratesof toll,and may change and alterthe same at
pleasure; but said rates shallnot exceed the rates pre-
scribed by chapter one hundred and ten of the General
Statutes ; and when two and one half consecutive miles
of said road shallhave been completed, and examined
and approved by any two justicesof the peace of War-
ren county,who shallexamine the same on application
of the president of said road ,a toll-gate may be erected,
and toll collected, under such rules and regulations as
shallbe prescribedby the presidentand directors, and
under the general law of the State; but until said road
iscompleted forfiveconsecutivemiles,the tollcollected
shallbe in proportionas the lengthofroad finishedbears
to five miles .
§7 . The president and directorsof said company ,and
theiremployes ,are hereby authorized to take the consent ,
in writing,of all persons who may consent to grant the
rightof way forsaid turnpike road,and the use of ma-
terialsfor building and repairing said road ; and when
either the right of way or any materials necessary for
the construction of said road cannot be acquired by con-
sent or purchase,saidpresidentand directorsmay apply
to the county court of Warren county for a writ of ad
quod damnum to assessthe damages which may be sus-
tained by the owner of said land; and the jury,in assess

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 681
ing damages ,may take into considerationthe advantages
resultingto the party or parties claiming damages ; and
upon the payment of the damages assessed,or the tender
thereof,itshallbe lawful for said company and itsem-
ployes to make saidroad,and to dig and carryaway any
stone or other material necessary for the construction or
repair of said road ; and said company may acquire, by
purchase or by writ of ad quod damnum ,as above setforth,
land , not exceeding two acres,forthe purpose of erecting
a toll-house on said road; and said company may pro-
cure from the Warren county court the right of way over
any road over which the said county has control; and
may procure the right of way over any bridge and ap-
proaches over which said county has control.
§ 8. The presidentand directorsof saidcompany may
make and declareequitabledividendsof thesurplusearn-
ings of said company among the stockholders,according
to their respective shares,after paying the expenses of
said company .
§9 . Certificatesof stockin said company shallbe signed
by the president,and countersigned by the secretary,shall
be deemed personal estate, and shall be transferable on
the books of the company ,in such manner as the presi-
dent and directorsshallprescribe.
§ 10. The capital stock of said company shallbe used
in the procurement of right of way , materials, and so
forth,and in constructing said road ,and in defraying all
legitimate expenses incident to the construction of said
road,and forno otherpurposewhatsoever; and thestock-
holders in said company shall only be bound to the ex-
tent of their stock in said road .
§ 11. This act shall take effectand be in force from .
and after its passage .
Approved April 28, 1884.

682 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1164 .
AN ACT authorizingthe Fulton county court to levy an ad valorem tax for
buildingor repairingthe county jail,and buying a lotfor same .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That thepresidingjudge oftheFulton countycourt
of this State be , and is hereby , authorized to cause to be
summoned the justices of the peace of said county to
attend and constitutea court,at any regular term of said
county court, with fullpower to levy and collectan ad
valorem tax of not exceeding fiftycentson the one hun-
dred dollars' worth of property subjectto taxation for
State revenue purposes,to be appliedto the buying of
lot or lots, and building a new jail thereon,or for re-
building on the present lot, or for any repairs on the
present jail of said county. The powers to levy and
collect said tax shall be given to said justices of the
peace,to be continued from year to year untila suffi-
cientsum shallbe secured to carryinto effectthe pur-
poses of thisact.
§ 2. This act shall be in force and effectfrom and after
its passage .
Approved April 28,1884 .
CHAPTER 1165 .

tonville, in Carroll county ."

of Kentucky :
1. That an act,entitled"An act to incorporate the

named in said act to which this is an amendment : Pro

vided,however, That no such licenseshallbe grantedby

CHAPTER 1166 .
AN ACT to empower the county court of Campbell county to make sub-
scriptionto the capitalstock of turnpike roadsinsaidcounty .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of the county of Campbell ,
a majorityof the justicesof the peace of said county
being present and concurring,be ,and ishereby ,author-
ized and empowered to subscribe stock for and on behalf
of said county to allturnpike road companies which have
been or shall hereafter be incorporated by said county
court,or by the Legislature of the State: Provided,That
said subscription shall not exceed one thousand dollars
for each mile of road within said county .
§2 . Said countycourt,a majorityof the justicesof the
peace of thecountybeing presentand concurring,shall
have power to levy a tax upon allthe propertyin said
county,subjectto taxation for revenue purposes, suffi-
cient for the purpose of paying said subscriptionof
stock; which levy shall in no year exceed the sum of
twenty -five cents on each hundred dollars of taxable
property in said county . Said tax to be collected in
same manner , and by the same officers, under same
liabilitiesand penalties,that the revenue tax isnow col-
lected .
§3 . That the county court shall not levy the tax, and
directitscollection,untilthe company orcompanies wish-
ing to avail themselves of the benefit of the county sub

684 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
scriptionshallpresent to the same a record showing that
therehas been chosen,and sworn in,a presidentand a
board of directors,as requiredby theirseveralcharters,
a surveyof the road or roads made out by a competent
engineer,with the estimated cost of each section and part
of a section,and theirbooks containing subscriptionsto
make each mile of said road or roads to which such coun-
ty subscriptionsapply .
§ 4. The amount of subscriptionper mile,made under
the provisions of this act, to the capital stock of any
company ,shallbe paid to the officersof such company
on the completion of each mile of road; and said officers
shall, before receiving any of said money , execute in
said court a good and sufficientbond ,conditioned forthe
due applicationof the money in accordance with the pro-
visions of this act .
§ 5. Dividends arisingfrom stock subscribed,under au -
thorityof thisact,shallbe appliedin aid of the county
levy .
§6 . Before said county court shall subscribe stock, or
exerciseany of the powers herein contemplated, the
question shallbe submitted to the qualifiedvoters of said
county,whether or not they are in favorof empowering
said court to subscribe to the capital stock of turnpike
roads constructed within said county , at an electionto be
heldateach of the votingplacestherein,on thefirstMon-
day in August ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
four; and if the affirmativeof said propositionshall
receive a majority of votes cast,the said court shallbe
authorized to exercise all the powers conferred by this
act .
§ 7. Itshallbe the duty of the sheriffof said county to
open a poll at each of the voting places in said county ,
as provided for in the sixth section of this act; and said
election shall be conducted , and the returns thereof made
and canvassed , and the resultdeclared,in the same man-
ner and by the same officersas in electionof county offi-
The sheriffshallgive ten days ' notice of the time
and objectof said electionby printednotices,posted in
cers .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 685
at leasttwo publicplaces in the neighborhood of each
voting place,and by at least two insertionsin a newspa-
perpublishedin saidcounty.
§ 8. Said county court shall cause the result of said
electionto be entered on itsorder-book,and providefor
the payment of the expense of the same .
§9 . Said court may ,by order duly entered on itsorder-
book , again submit said question to the qualifiedvoters
of said county ; but itshallnot be submitted oftener than
every two years .
§ 10. That theprovisionsof thisact shallnot apply to
thatpart of said county known as the court-house dis-
trict. And the justicesof the peace residingin said court-
house districtshall form no part of said court for any of
thepurposesof thisact,nor shallany of saidtax be ap-
pliedto any road within said court-house district,nor to

§ 11. This act shall be in force from its passage .
Approved April 28, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1167 .
AN ACT to amend and revisean act incorporatingthe town of Butler,
in Pendleton county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
§1 . That the boundaries of the town of Butler,in Pen-
dleton county,as laid off pursuant to an act approved
February first, one thousand eight hundred and sixty-
eight, the plat and plan of which is of record in the
clerk's office of the Pendleton county court, is hereby
declared to be the extent and limits of said town , and
the inhabitantsthereofa body -politic; withperpetualsuc-
cession; with power to contract and be contracted with ,
to sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded ,defend and
be defended ,in allcourts and places,by the name of the
town of Butler; and to have a corporateseal.
§ 2. That the municipalgovernment of saidtown shall
consist of the board of trustees, composed of four per

686 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sons,and a policejudge,who shallbe ex officiochairman
of said board .
§3 . That said board of trustees and their successors
shallbe a body -politicand corporate,and shallbe known
and called the " Board of Trustees of Butler;" and by
that name shallbe capable in law , and for and in behalf
of said town ,of contracting and being contracted with ,
suing and being sued , of pleading and being impleaded ,
defending and being defended ,in all courts and places ;
and do all other acts,matters,and things which a body-
politicand corporate,having perpetualsuccession,may
lawfully and rightfullydo within the limitsof the powers
herein granted .
§4 . That the other officersof said town shall be as fol-
lows : A police judge , an attorney, a clerk, a treasurer, a
marshal ,an assessor, a collector,and such other officers
as the board of trustees may , from time to time ,direct
and appoint.
Elections .
§5 . That an electionshallbe held in said town annu-
ally,on the firstSaturday in February ,at such place as
the board of trustees may designate, and of which ten
days ' previous notice shall be given by printed notices
posted in at leastfive public places in said town by the
town clerk .
§6 . That at the annual election there shall be elected
by the qualifiedvoters of said town four trustees,a po-
lice judge , and a marshal , and the person or persons
receiving the highest number of votes in the whole town
for either of said offices shall be declared elected .
§7 . That the officerselected by the people under the
provisionsof thisact shallrespectivelyhold theiroffices
for one year ,and until their successors are elected and
qualified respectively. All other officersmentioned in
thisact, and not otherwise specificallyprovided for, may
be appointed by the board of trusteesat the firstregular
meeting thereof after the annual election, or as soon
thereafteras may be ,and respectivelycontinue in office
one year ,and untilthe appointment and qualificationof

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 687
theirsuccessors; but watchmen ,policemen, and firemen
may be appointed to hold officeduring the pleasureof
the board of trustees .
§ 8. No person shallbe eligibleto any officein said
town who is not a citizen thereof, and a qualifiedvoter
under the laws of the State ; and no one shall be eligible
to the officeof police judge ,trustee, marshal ,or attorney
who has not,at the time of his election,resided in said
town at least six months previous to such election.
§9 . That every person electedor appointed to office
under the provisions of thisact,before entering upon the
dutiesthereof,shalltake and subscribe,beforesome offi-
cer authorized to administer oaths,the oath of officepre-
scribed by the Constitution of this Commonwealth , and
filethe same ,duly certifiedby the officerbefore whom it
was taken , with the clerk of the town .
§ 10. That whenever a vacancy shallhappen by death,
removal ,or resignation,or otherwise,in any officefilled
by election,such vacancy shall be filledby an election
to be held within ten days after the board of trustees
have notice of such vacancy. Any vacancy occurring
in an officeappointive by the board of trustees shallbe
filledby appointment .
11. That when two or more candidates for any elec-
tive officeshall have an equal number of votes for the
same office,the electionshallbe determined by lot,in
such manner as the board of trusteesmay direct,and in
the presence of the board of trustees, and at leastthree
other persons .
§ 12. That the board of trustees shall, before every
town election,appoint suitablepersons as officersof elec-
tion, two persons as judges ,and one as clerk; but no
person who is a candidate to be voted for at such election
shallbe appointed as an officerthereof. Officersof town
electionsprovided for by thisact shallperform similar
services,receive likepay ,and be governed in theirduties,
so far as consistent herewith , and be subject to the same
penalties as officersof State élections under the laws of
this Commonwealth .

688 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 13. That all elections by the qualified voters of the
town shallbe held between the hours of eight o'clock in
the forenoon and five o'clock in the afternoon .
§14 . That all male residents of said town over the
age of twenty -one years , who shall,at the time of offer-
ing to vote, have resided in said town three months
previous thereto,and are qualifiedvoters of this Com-
monwealth under the laws thereof , shall be entitled to
vote at any electionheld in said town .
§ 15. That all laws in force in this Commonwealth
punishing illegalvotingat the Stateelectionsshallapply
to illegalvoting at the town electionsin the town of But-
ler,subject,however ,to the provisions of this act touch-
ing the qualificationsof voters.
§ 16. That theboard oftrusteesshalljudge of thequali-
fications,elections,and returns of itsmembers ; and cases
of contested elections for any town officerselective by
thepeople,shallbe heard and determinedby the board of
trustees,under such rulesand regulationsas may be pre-
scribedby the by laws or ordinances.
§ 17. The outgoingtown clerkshallcertifyto the Sec-
retary of State the name of the person elected to the
office of police judge of said town , and the Governor
shall commission said officer.
§ 18. That the poll-books of allelectionsheld in said
town shall be returned by the clerk of election to the
clerk of said town , who shallkeep them on fileas part of
the records of his office,examine and compare the same ,
and issue certificatesof electionto the officerselected,
which certificate,beforebeing delivered,shallbe spread
on the records of the board of trustees .
Powers,Duties,and Liabilitiesof Officers.
§19. That the clerkshallkeep the corporateseal,if
there be one ,and make and keep a record of the pro-
ceeding of the board of trustees,whose meetings he shall
attend; and copiesof allpapers duly filedin his office,
and transcriptsfrom the records of the proceedings of the
board of trustees,certifiedby him ,shallbe evidence in

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 689
all courts . He shall draw all warrants on the treasury
directedby the board of trustees,and shallcountersign
the same , and keep an accurate account thereof in a
book to be provided for that purpose. He shallissue
licenses,and do and perform allother acts and duties
that the board of trusteesmay lawfullyrequireof him .
§20 . That the treasurershallreceiveallmoney belong-
ing to the town ,and keep an accurate account of receipts
and expenditures. All money shall be drawn from the
treasury,when orderedby the board of trustees,by war-
rant,which shallbe signed by the chairman of said board ,
and countersigned by the town clerk; such warrant shall
specify the purpose for which the amount named therein
ispaid . The treasurershallexhibit to the board of trus-
tees, at least fifteendays before the annual election of
each year , and oftener if required, a full and detailed
account of all receipts and expenditures, after the date
of the lastannual report; and also the stateof the treas-
ury ,which account shallbe filedin the officeof the clerk,
and he shall cause the whole of the above to be pub-
lished in a newspaper of said town (if there be one
published) one time .
§21 .That itshallbe theduty ofthe marshaltoattendall
sessionsof thepolicecourt,and to givethetown attorney
notice of any proceedings in said court to which it ishis
duty to attend ; he shall execute and make due returns of
allprocesses,civilor criminal,to him directedby the po-
licejudge,thepresidingjudge ofPendletoncounty court,
or any justiceof the peace of saidcounty; he shallpos-
sess the power and authorityof a constable under the laws
of thisCommonwealth ,and hispowers fordischarginghis
duties shallbe co-extensivewith Pendleton county; he
shall be entitled as compensation to the same fees al-
lowed by law to sheriffsand constables for similarservices,
to be collected in the same manner ; for other services he
shall receive such fees and compensation as the board of
trustees may prescribeby ordinance or by -laws ; he shall
collectallexecutionsand other demands placed in his

1 handsforcollection,and pay thesame overtotheperson
or persons entitledthereto; but he shallnot executefieri
faciasfrom circuitor courts of equal jurisdiction; he shall
be liablein the same cases,way ,and manner as sheriffs
and constablesare,and sued or prosecutedin the same
manner ; he may or may not be appointed collector,but
ifhe is,he shallbe requiredto give,in additionto the
marshal's bond ,a bond as collector.
§ 22. That itshallbe the duty of the collectorto collect
alltaxes and assessments which may be levied by the board
of trustees,and forthatpurposeshallhave allthepowers
of a sheriffto make sales of personal and real estate; he
shall pay over to the treasureralltaxes and other money
as the board of trustees may direct. When the tax -list
isplaced in his hands he shall proceed at once to collect
the same ,and to enforce payment may sellpersonal but
not real estate. Within three months from the time he
receives said list, he shall make out and return to the
board of trusteesa report,showing the amount collected,
the names of those who have paid ,and those who are
delinquent. Whereupon the board of trusteesshallmake
out a warrant commanding the collectorto levy upon and
sellsomuch ofthepropertyofdelinquents,eitherpersonal
or real, or both,as may be necessary to satisfyand pay
the taxes due and cost of sale. On receipt of said war-
rant,the collectorshallproceedto make leviesas thereby
commanded , and after advertising the same by notices
postedat the post-officedoor in said town forat leastfif-
teendays,shallsellthe same at some publicplace in said
town to be designated in said noticesto thehighest bidder ,
and on receiving the price bid, shallgive the purchaser a
certificateofsuch purchase,which,on presentationto the
board of trustees, shall entitlethe purchaser to a deed
from the board of trusteesforany real estateso purchased ,
unlessthe same be redeemed as hereinafterprovided; he
shall make to the board of trustees,at such time and in
such manner as they may direct, a fulland complete
return,showing allhis acts and proceedingsin the exe-
cution of said warrant .

!
§ 23. That it shallbe the duty of the attorney to pros-
ecute offenders against the by -laws and ordinances of
said town , and to perform allother duties incident to his
office,and togive writtenopinionsupon subjectssubmit-
ted to him by the board of trusteesor itscommittees .
§ 24. That the assessor shall perform all the duties
which the board of trusteesmay , by ordinance or by-
laws,prescribein relationto the assessmentof property
forthe purpose of imposing or levying taxes.
§25 . That the board of trusteesshallhave the power
at all times to require further and other duties, and fix
the compensation of allofficerswhose duties are herein
prescribed, and prescribethe powers and duties of all
officersappointed or elected to officeunder this act,
whose dutiesare not specificallymentioned,and fixtheir
compensation .
§26 . That the police judge , treasurer, collector, mar-
shal , and such other officers as the board of trustees may
require,shallseverally,before they enter on the duties of
their respective offices, execute a bond , payable to the
board of trustees of Butler,in such sum ,with such con-
ditions and such sureties as the board of trustees may
approve ,conditioned that they willfaithfullyexecute the
dutiesof theirrespectiveoffices,and pay over to such
persons, and at such times as they may respectivelybe
entitledto the same ,allmoney or other thing that may
come to their hands as such officersrespectively; which
bonds , with the approval of the board of trusteescerti-
fied thereon ,shallbe carefullypreserved by the clerk of
said town ; and the bond itselfshallbe spread upon the
records of the board of trustees .
§27. That the board of trusteesof Butler,or any per-
son injuredby the breach of any or either of said bonds ,
may ,in any court having jurisdiction,prosecutesuiton
motion against the said officerby whose breach of duty
such injury is occasioned and his sureties,or any or allof
them ,or the personalrepresentatives,heirs,or deviseesof
any or allof them ,as providedby law as to sheriffsand
constables ; and no such bond shall be void or satisfied

692 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
!
until the board of trustees of Butler, and every person
aggrieved by the acts,omission, or neglect of such offi-
cers,has been recompensed .
§ 28. That if any person ,having been an officerin said
town , shall not, within ten days after notificationand
request,deliverto his successorin officeallthe property ,
papers , and effectsof every descriptionin his possession
belonging to said town , or appertaining to the office
which he held,he shallforfeitand pay ,for the use of the
town ,the sum of ten dollarsfor every day he may refuse
or neglect so to do , which may be recovered by warrant
beforethe policejudge of said town , or any justiceof
the peace within the districtin which said town is situ-
ated .
Police Court .
§29 . That there is hereby establishedin said town a
court,to be styledthe Butler police court, which shallbe
held by one judge,to be styledthe judge of the Butler
policecourt,who shallbe electedas hereinbeforepro-
vided
§ 30. That the Butlerpolicecourt shallhave exclusive
jurisdictionof all prosecutions for violations of the by-
laws or ordinances of said town , and concurrent jurisdic-
tion with other courts of Pendleton county of prosecu-
tiens for riots, routs, breaches of the peace , and other
misdemeanors committed within said county , where the
penalty imposed by law does not exceed one hundred
dollars' fine and fiftydays ' imprisonment . Said court
shall have power and authority to take recognizances
from persons charged with offenses cognizable before it
to appear and answer the same ,and the same power to
declare the same forfeited, and pronounce judgment
thereon, and to enforce fullcompliance therewith, as
circuit courts have .
§31 . That said court shall have power to issue war-
rants and other processes in the name of the Common-
wealth; to compel the attendance of witnesses; to enter
judgment ,and award and issuewrits forthe purpose of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 693
enforcing its orders and judgments , to the same extent,
and in as full a manner , as other courts of this Common-
wealth of like jurisdiction.
§32 . That said court shallhave jurisdictionconcurrent
with justices of the peace of Pendleton county in civil
cases within the magisterial district in which said town
is situated; and the police judge shall have power to
grant orders of attachment , injunctions and restraining
orders,ordersof delivery,writs of habeascorpus,distress
warrants , and warrants in forcible entry and detainer
cases, under the same circumstances , and under same
rules and regulations,as justices of the peace or judges
of courts with likejurisdictionand powers ,
§33 . That the police judge shall be a conservator of
the peace ,and shallhave power to take recognizance to
keep the peace ;the power of two justicesof the peace in
holding examining courts, to issue subpœnas for wit-
nesses, and to order the marshal to summons juries,and
allotheracts and things necessaryand proper to carry
intofulland complete effectthe powers and jurisdiction
conferred on said judge and said court.
§ 34. That the police court may he holden at such place
as the board of trusteesshalldesignate,or,they failingto
designate and provide a suitableplace, at such place as
the judge may selectin said town .
§35 . That said court shallbe open at alltimes for the
trialof criminal and penal causes within itsjurisdiction;
and the terms for the trial of civil causes shall be held
quarterly, at such times as may be fixed by said court,by
an order duly entered on its order-book ; and until thus
changed,shallbe holden at the times now prescribedby
law .
§36 That said court shallbe a court of record,and the
judge thereofshallact as clerk; and copies of the records
of said court over the signature of the judge shallbe evi-
dence in any court in thisCommonwealth .
§37 . That said court shallhave power to punish con-
tempt by fineor imprisonment ,not exceeding fivedollars"
fineand three days ' imprisonment .

694 LAWS OF KENTUCKY
§38 . That allprocesses from said police court shallbe
in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucy ,and may
be directedto the marshal or any constable,sheriff,jailer,
coroner,or policeman ; and the person executing the pro-
cess of said court shall be entitled to the same fees for
like services,and to the like remedies for the collection
thereof, as the constables of this Commonwealth , and
shallbe subjectto likefinesand penaltiesfor likeof-
fensesand failures,and recoverablein the same manner .
§39 . That all fines and forfeitures recovered in said
courtare hereby granted to saidtown ,and shallbe col-
lected by the marshal or other officersto whom execu-
tionor capiaspro fineisdirected,and paid by him to the
town treasurer ; but before the issual of execution or other
finalprocess,the defendant may pay the fine or forfeiture
to the judge of said court, who shallpay the same to the
treasurer .
§ 40. That thedefendantshallhave the rightto replevy
for three months any judgment , fine, or forfeitureof the
police court on execution or other finalprocess thereon ,
by giving bond ,with good security,in the same manner
as allowed by law in similar cases within the jurisdiction
of other courts of this Commonwealth .
§ 41. That a returnof not found on a capiaspro fine,
and of no propertyfound on a fierifacias,issuedon any
judgment of the policecourt,shallauthorizean equitable
proceeding in favor of the trustees of Butler against the
estateof the defendantor defendants,forthe purpose of
enforcingsatisfactionof saidjudgment ,in the same man-
ner that a return of no property authorizes such proceed-
ings in civilcauses .
§ 42. That upon alljudgments for finesrendered by
said court,and which shallnot be paid, an execution
against the estate of the defendant may be issued, or a
capiaspro finemay be issued,commanding the imprison-
ment of the defendant in the jail or work house of the
town or county until the same shall be paid; but the
imprisonment shallnot exceed one day for each dollar of
such fine: Provided, That said court, in its discretion,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 695
may assign such defendants so finedto hard labor on the
streetsor alleys of said town ,or on some public building
in or belonging to said town ,or at some otherlabor for
the benefitof saidtown ,untilhe shallpay his saidfine
and the cost adjudged against him ,allowing forhis labor
at the rate of one dollarper day . Such defendantshall
not be required to labor more than eight hours in any one
day ; and when not at labor, such defendant shallbe con-
finedas providedin thissection; and forthe purpose of
puttinghim in jailor thework -house,and takinghim out
to labor,a certifiedcopy of the order assigninghim to
work shallbe sufficientauthorityto the marshal ,jailer,or
keeper of the work -house. All persons sentencedto im-
prisonment by the judgment of said court may be as-
signed[to] labor in likemanner as those fined. Where
persons imprisonedas contemplated in thissectionshall
be confined in the county jail,the jailer'sfees shall be
paid by said town .
§43. That appealsshallbe from thedecisionsand judg-
ments of saidpolicecourtto the Pendletoncriminalcourt
in criminaland penal cases,when the fineexceeds twen-
ty-five dollars,or the imprisonment exceedsten days; but
no judgment shallbe suspended by such appeal unless
the defendant,withinfivedays afterthe renditionthereof,
causes to be executed , before the clerk of the court to
which the appeal istaken ,a covenant ,with good security,
in allrespectsas required by section three hundred and
sixty-four of the Criminal Code of Practice; or in civil
cases appeals shalllie to the Pendleton quarterly or cir-
cuit court,the same as appeals from judgments ofjustices
of the peace to said courts.
§ 44. That the fees of the policejudge , whether acting
as judge or clerk or otherwise, shallbe the same in all
cases as are now ,or may hereafter be ,given by law to
officersperforming likeservices; and fees and costs shall
be taxed in cases in said court to the same extent, in the
same way , and under the same regulation, as in other
courts having similarjurisdiction.

696 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
General Powers and Duties of the Board of Trustees.
§ 45. That the board of trustees shall hold stated meet-
ings,and the chairman ,or,in his absence ,the town clerk,
may callmeetings,by notice(verbal or written) to each
of the members . A majority of the members elected as
trustees shall constitute a quorum .
§46 . That the board of trustees shall have power to
make ,ordain, and publish all such ordinances,by -laws ,
police and sanitary regulations,not contrary to the Con-
stitutionof thisCommonwealth ,forthe good government
of said town ,and to promote the health,security,trade,
and commerce thereof, and that may be necessary or
expedient to carry into effectthe powers vested in the
board of trustees,or any officerof said town ,by thisact,
and to amend or repeal the same , and to enforce the
observance of all ordinances, by -laws , and regulations
made in pursuance of this act by penalties,not to ex-
ceed one hundred dollars' fine and fiftydays ' imprison-
ment ,for any offenseagainst the same .
§ 47. That the board of trustees shall have the man-
agement and controlof the financesand allthe property,
real, personal,and mixed ,belonging to the corporation,
and shallhave power to provide for the erection,repair,
maintenance , and government of allpublic buildings for
the town ,and to provide for inclosing and improving all
public grounds belonging to the town , and within the
jurisdictionof said town shallhave power by ordinance
or by -law-
First. To sustainand prohibitalldescriptionof gam-
bling,fraudulent devices,playing at cards,dice, or other
games of chance , whereat anything of value is won or
lost .
Second. To license, tax, regulate, or prohibit billiard-
tables, pool-tables,pigeon-hole, Jenny Lind,and similar
tables,pin-alleys,nine or ten-pin alleys,ball-alleys,and
shooting-galleries.
Third . To license, tax, regulate, or prohibit all con-
certs, exhibitions, shows , circuses, caravans , theatrical
performances , and allother exhibitions and amusements .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 697
Fourth. To prevent and suppress tumults,riots, routs,
and breachesof the peace,noises,disturbances,or disor-
derly assemblages .
Fifth. To restrainor suppressdisorderlyhouses, gro-
ceries,saloons,halls, and houses of ill-fame .
Sixth. To restrain,suppress, and punish indecent ,ob-
scene, or profane language ,conduct ,or exhibitions; and
restrainand punish vagrants and prostitutes.
Seventh. To restrain,regulate, or prohibit the running
at large of horses,swine ,and other animals ,and author-
ize the distraining, impounding , and sale of the same for
the penaltyincurred,and costof proceedings,and to im-
pose penalties on the owner of any such animal for a
violation of any ordinance or by -law in relation thereto.
Eighth . To prohibit the running at large of dogs , and
to authorize the destruction of the same when found at
large, contrary to by -law or ordinance , and to impose
penaltieson the owners thereoffor permittingthem to
run at large .
Ninth. To appoint watchmen or policemen,and pre-
scribe theirduties,powers ,and pay .
Tenth. To establish and maintain a jail or watch-
house , a hospital or pest-house , an engine -house , work-
house ,poor -house ,and market -house ,and make rulesand
regulationsfor the government thereof, and appoint offi--
cers for the conduction and management of the same .
Eleventh. To authorize the clerk to grant and issue
license,and direct the time and manner of issuing and
registeringthereof, and the fees to be paid therefor; but
no licenseshall be granted for more than one year , and
the fee forissuingthe same shallnot exceed fiftycents.
Twelfth. To license, tax , and regulate auction sales,
peddlers, studs, bulls, and jacks; to declare what are
nuisances, and provide for the abatement thereof, and
impose penalties for the creation or maintenance of the
same .
Thirteenth. To license, tax, and regulate within said
town ,and within one mile thereof, innkeepers ,hotel or
tavern-keepers, saloon or coffee-house keepers, grocers,

698 LAWS OF KENTUCKY ..
merchants, druggists, and keepers of other places for
sellingspirituous,vinous , or malt liquors,and to restrain
allsuch persons from sellingwithout license.
Fourteenth . That the board of trustees of Butler shall
have exclusive power to grant all licensefor which pro-
vision is made by this act; but such license shall not
excuse the person obtainingitfrom paying to the State
any tax,and procuring from the county court or clerk
any licensenow requiredby law . That the amount to
be paid for license to sell spirituous, vinous , or malt
liquorsin any hotel, inn,tavern,coffee-house,or saloon,
by the drink, or in quantitiesless than one gallon, for
the period of one year ,shall not be less than five hun-
dred dollars nor more than one thousand dollars; and the
amount to be paid forany other licensesauthorizedby
this act shall be fixed by the board of trustees. Be-
fore any person shall have the right to sell spirituous,
vinous,or malt liquorswithin said town ,or within one
mile thereof, he shall present to the treasurer of said
town a certifiedcopy of the orderof the board of trus-
tees granting him license, and lodge the same with him ,
and pay him the amount charged thereforas fixedby
ordinanceor by -law . If any person shallsellspirituous,
vinous ,or malt liquors within said limits, without first
having complied with the foregoing provision of thissec-
tion,he shallbe deemed guiltyof retailingliquorswith-
out license, or keeping a tippling-house , as the case may
be,under the General Statutes,and shallbe subjectto
indictment in the Pendleton criminal court , and fined as
provided in the General Statutes for commission of said
offenses .
Fifteenth. To provide for the securityof said town
againstfireby purchase of fire-engines and other fire
apparatus , and the organization of one or more firecom-
panies,appoint officers,and make rulesand regulations
for the government thereof, and do all other things and
acts necessaryforthe maintenance of an effectivefire
department .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 699
Sixteenth. To acquire, by donation or purchase , and
receive conveyances forground , eitherwithin or without
the limitsof said town , for a cemetery ,not exceeding ten
acres,fora poor-house and a pest-house,not exceeding
one acre each .
Seventeenth. To make and receive all conveyances re-
lating to said town ; to take,receive,and hold any estate,
real or personal or mixed ,for use, benefit, or ornament
of saidtown ,by purchase,devise,or bequest.
Taxation .
§ 48. That said board of trusteesshallhave power ,sub-
ject to the exceptions hereinafter mentioned ,to annually
levy and collecttaxes,not exceeding one dollar on each
one hundred dollars' worth of allthe property in said town ,
including everything subject to taxation by the State for
revenue purposes , and a head or poll-tax upon every male
inhabitant of said town over the age of twenty -one years
of not exceeding two dollars; and a tax not exceeding one
dollar on each dog that any person may keep or sufferor
permit to be kept on his or her premises .
§ 49. That thereshallbe exempt from taxationunder
this act all public property belonging to said town ,the
county of Pendleton ,the State of Kentucky ,the United
States, any church , college, school, seminary , and all
ground's attached to and dedicated to the use of any such
church, college, school, or seminary, and any cemetery
belonging to said town ,and household and kitchen furni-
ture belonging to a bona fide housekeeper with a family,
not exceeding two hundred dollarsin value.
§50 . That alltaxes and assessments levied or assessed
by the board of trustees under this act, shall be a lien
upon the realand personalestateupon which the same
may be imposed,voted,or assessed,untilthesame ispaid,
and no sale or transfer of real estate shall affect the lien
thereon .
§ 51. That the rightof redemption in allcases of sales
of real estate for taxes or assessments ,shall exist to the
owner or hisheirsforthe periodof threeyears from the

700 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
day ofsale,on payment oftheamount forwhich thesame
was sold, and twenty -five per cent, added , and all the
taxes accruing subsequent to the sale; and if the owner
at the time of the sale be an infant , married woman ,or
person of unsound mind ,said right to redeem shall exist
for a likenumber of years afterthe removal of such dis-
ability. Ifnot redeemed,the board of trusteesshall,on
presentation of the certificateof sale,execute a deed to
the purchaser conveying to him the real estateso sold and
unredeemed as aforesaid .
§ 52. That all fines and forfeitures, taxes and assess-
ments , herein contemplated and provided for, shall be
appropriatedexclusivelyto the use and benefitof said
town , in such manner as the board of trustees may di-
rect .
§53 . That the clerk shall keep a record of all sales
fortaxes and assessments, which shallbe open for inspec-
tion at all times .
Streets,Alleys,Public Squares and Grounds ,and etcetera.
§54 . That the board of trustees shall have authority
and control over the streets, sidewalks, alleys, public
squaresand grounds,now insaidtown,orwhich may be
opened or laid out hereafter,with fullpower to grade ,
pave ,construct,repair,protect, and ornament the same as
they may deem most convenient and beneficialto said
town ,and by unanimous vote may close any alleyin said
town .
§55 . That the owners in front of or upon whose prem-
ises the board of trustees shall order and direct curbing
or sidewalks to be constructed, repaired, or relaid,shall
make ,repair,or relaysuch curbing or sidewalksattheir
own cost and expense,and in the manner ,with the ma-
terial, and within the time prescribed by said board of
trusteesby ordinance or order duly entered on their re-
cords; and if not done in the manner ,and with the mate-
rial,and withinthe time prescribed,the board of trustees
may cause the same to be constructed,repaired,or relaid,
and assess the expense thereof, and collectthe same by
warrant and sale of the premises ,as in the case of taxes;

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 701
a suit may also be maintained by the board of trustees
against such owner or owners for the expense of said im-
provements : Provided, That no person shallbe required
to make a sidewalk in frontof his or her premisesuntil
thestreetinfrontofsuchpremiseshas been macadamized
at the expense of the town .
§56 . That itshallbe the duty of the board of trustees
at alltimes to keep the streets,alleys,lanes,public squares,
and places in good order and condition; and for every
failureto do so,as for every otherwillfulfailureto effi-
ciently exercise the powers herein conferred upon them
to promote the good order,health, safety, and property
of said town and its inhabitants, they shall, for each
offense, upon indictment and conviction in the Pendle-
ton criminal court, forfeitand pay for the use of said
town not less than twenty nor more than fiftydollars;
and such fine shallbe paid by the board of trustees out
of their individual estates .
General Provisions .
§57 . That the property and citizensof said town shall
be exempt from the operation of all road laws now or
hereafter in force in Pendleton county .
§ 58. That allordinances,regulations,by -laws now in
force in said town ,and not inconsistent with this act,
shall remain in fullforce untilaltered or repealed by the
board of trustees after this act takes effect; and the offi-
cers of said town now in office shall remain and continue
in the same for and during the terms for which they were
respectivelyelected,but shallbe governed by thisact.
§ 59. That this act shall not affector invalidate any
act done by the board of trustees,or any of the officers
of said town , nor divest their successors of rights of
property or otherwise,or liabilitywhich may have ac-

cerning said wharf .
