CHAPTER 1171 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Brandenburgand Rockhaven Turnpike Road
Company ,in Meade county, and to authorizethe county to take stock in
same .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby created a corporation,styled
the Brandenburg and Rockhaven Turnpike Road Com-
pany , with perpetual succession; and the right in said
name to contract and be contracted with ,to sue and be
sued ,plead and be impleaded ; to have a common seal,
and all other rightsand powers incident to and neces-
sary to carry into effectthe powers hereinafterspecifically
granted .
§2 . That the business of said company shallbe to
build, manage ,and control a graded and macadamized
road from the " Tan Yard Spring ," or near the same ,in
Meade county ,by way of Meade County Salt Works , to
Rockhaven ,on such route as itsboard of directorsmay
determine on ; and may acquire the rightof way for said
road, and the necessary land fortoll-houses ,stone,gravel,
timber,and othermaterialforconstructingand repairing
the same ,by private agreement or condemnation under a
writ of ad quod damnum .
§3 . That those persons who may subscribe to the cap-
italstock of said company are hereby declared the stock-
holders of said company ; and in all electionseach shall
have one vote for every share of stock by him subscribed
and paid , in accordance with the requirements of the
board of directors .
§4 . That the business of said company shallbe under
the direction and control of a board of five directors,
who shallbe electedannuallyby the stockholderson the
firstMonday in May ,and shallhold officeforone year,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 705
and untiltheirsuccessorsare electedand qualified. In
case of a failure, from any cause ,to hold the electionfor
directorson the day named ,an election for that purpose
may be held at any time on twenty days' noticeby the
board of directors, or the owner or owners of twenty
shares of stock. The notice shallbe written or printed,
and postedconspicuouslyatthecompany's principaloffice,
at each toll-gate on itsroad ,and such otherplaces as may
serve to give public notice.
§5 . That the board of directors shall, on their own
election, immediately electone of theirnumber presi-
dent of the board,who shallpresideat theirmeetings,
and be the executive of said board . The board shall
have power to selectfrom saidcompany a secretaryand
treasurer,and such other officersand agents as they may
deem necessary,to prescribe their duties, and fix their
compensation .
§ 6. That it shallbe the duty of the secretaryto keep
the books and accounts of said company ,and to attend
the meetings of said board , and keep in a substantial
book an accurate record of allitsproceedings .
§7 . That the treasurerof said company shallbe the
custodianof allmoney belongingto it,and shallpay the
same on the order of the board of directors,certifiedto
him by the secretary; but before entering on his duties
he shall execute a bond to the company , with sureties
approved by the board of directors,conditionedthat he
will faithfullyperform all the duties of treasurer, and
pay over as directedby the board of directorsany money
in his hands belonging to the company ,and will make
fullsettlementof his accounts when required by said
board , and on going out of officewilldeliverto his suc-
cessor all books , papers ,property,money ,or other thing
in his hands belonging to said company . This bond shall
be recordedin hisrecord-book ,and preservedby the sec-
retary .
§8.That saidcompany shallhave therightto establish
toll-gates on said road , and charge and collecttollsas
authorized by the General Statutes.

706 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 9. That the capital stock of said company shall be
dividedintoshares of fiftydollarsor twenty-fivedollars
each ,as the firstboard of directorselectedhereunder shall
determine ,which determination shallbe entered on their
record -book .
§ 10. That said board of directorsshallhave power to
make all by -laws ,rulesand regulations, for the govern-
ment of themselves and officers and affairs of said com-
pany ,not inconsistentwith publiclaw .
§ 11. That at any general election the stockholders
may change the time of holding the regular election for
directors .
§ 12. That Jesse McGehn , Frank Ditto,sr.,C. R. Mc.-
Gehn ,J.K. Ditto,and Alonzo Moremen ,are hereby made
commissioners to organize saidcompany ,and they,or any

board of directors, and do such other acts and things as

§ 13. The Meade county court of claims,a majority of
the justicesthereofbeing present and concurring therein,
may subscribenot more than seven hundred and fiftydol-
lars of stock to each mile of said turnpike road; and for
thepurpose ofpaying the same ,saidcourtofclaims may
make a levy upon the taxable property in said county .
§ 14. The board of trustees of the town of Branden-
burg,a majority being present and concurringtherein,
may subscribeand take stock,and levy a tax forthe pay-
ment of same ,not to exceed twenty -five cents on every
one hundred dollars' worth of taxableproperty.
§ 15. This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved April 30 , 1884.

CHAPTER 1182 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the town of New Haven ,in Nelson county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charter of the town of New Haven , in
Nelson county ,be so amended as to authorize the board
of trusteesof said town to grant to hotel-keepers ,and all
other persons in said town ,license to vend ardent spirits
or malt liquors,or the mixture thereof,by the drink , or in
any quantity lessthan five gallons, upon such applicant
or applicants paying to said board of trustees a tax on
such license of not less than fiftydollars nor more than
one hundred dollars. And upon the grantingof any such
licenseby said board of trustees, and payment of the tax
by the applicant or applicants,he or they shallbe entitled
to a license therefor, which license shall be in writing,
and shallstatethe amount of tax paid,date of payment ,
and the privilegesgranted thereunder , and be signed by
the chairman of the board and attestedby itsclerk, and
shall continue in force for twelve months from the date
thereof: Provided, That no license shall be granted for
the establishment or keeping of any coffee-house in said
town : And provided further, That the tax fixed by the
board , as authorized in section one , shall be uniform in
amount upon each class.
§2 . That any person who shall sell or vend ardent
spirits, or malt liquors,or the mixture thereof,by retail,
or in any quantity less than five gallons, within the
corporate limits of said town , without firsthaving pro-
cured the license authorized and required by this act,
shallbe deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor ,and upon con-
victionthereof before any court of competent jurisdiction
shall be fined in a sum not less than ten dollars nor more

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 715
than twenty -fivedollarsforeach offense,and each selling
shall constitutea separate offense; such finesto be used
forthe public benefit of said town .
§3 . That allmoneys arisingunder the provisions of this
act shallbe applied by the board of trusteesof said town
to the improvement of the streetsand alleys thereof, or
to such other public use in said town as said board of
trustees shall direct .
§ 4. This act shallnot affectany licensegranted and in
forcein said town at the date of itsadoption .
§ 5. All acts or parts of acts in conflictwith thisact
are hereby repealed .
§ 6. This act shall be submitted to the qualifiedvoters
of said town at the next regular electionfor town trus-
tees,by the officersof said election, and if a majority of
saidqualifiedvotersatsaidelection,or atany subsequent
election held for town trustees , shall vote for this amend-
ment to the town charter,then this act shall be in force
from and after the date of such election .
Approved April 30, 1884.
CHAPTER 1183 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Oscar McKenzie ,of Morgan county.
WHEREAS ,James Dunnaway was indictedin the Morgan
eircuitcourt for murder ,and desiring a change of venue
in said case,he made application thereforas required by
law , and the judge of that court made an order sending
the case to the Magoffin circuitcourt,a court of a county
out of the judicialdistrictin which the case was pending ,
and the witnesses were recognized in the Morgan circuit
court for theirattendance at the Magoffin circuitcourt,at
which they attended according to their recognizances ;
and whereas,when the case was calledupon the docket
of the Magoffin circuitcourt the judge thereof refused to
take cognizance of the same ,as he was of the opinion
the venue of the case was changed to Magoffin county
contrarytolaw,and he refusedto allowthe witnessesfor
the Commonwealth their fees for attendance and mileage ,

716 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and the judge of the Morgan circuitcourt refusedto make
the allowance for said witnesses because their attendance

the other judicialofficersof the State the witnesses,who
were very needy,were refusedpay forthe serviceswhich
they renderedforthe Commonwealth ,and tothe payment
of which they were entitledby law ; and whereas ,Oscar
McKenzie ,sheriffof Morgan county,paid to the saidwit-
nessestheirattendanceand mileage,which amounted to
forty-one dollarsand eighty-eightcents,theevidenceof
which is filed with the committee on claims in the Senate
of Kentucky ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

hereby ,directed to draw his warrant on the Treasurer of

dollarsand eighty-eightcents,payable out of any money
in the Treasury not otherwiseappropriated.

Approved April 30, 1884 .

inpurchasing the lands and easements aforesaid,at not
lessthan par for the firstmortgage bonds of said rail-
road company ,at a ratenot greaterthan par,secured as
aforesaid,which issuing,exchange,and purchasingbonds
shall be made on the conditions hereafter named .
§ 4. The bonds of the county shallnot be issuedunder
thisact untilauthorized by a vote of the qualifiedvoters
of said county. Said bonds shallnot run longer than
twenty years,and may be redeemed at the optionof the
county, at such short a period as may be determined

718 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
when said bonds issue. Said bonds shall not bear inter-
est at a rate exceeding six per cent.per annum .
§ 5. Before any bonds shall be issued by said county
under thisact, the same must be authorized by a majori-
ty of the legal voters of said county ,voting at an elec-
tion to be held as follows : When a petition,specifying
the amount of bonds to be issued and the terms of the
same in regard to the time and place of payment ,signed
by not less than one hundred and fiftytax-payers and
qualifiedvoters of said county , shallbe presented to the
county court of Adair county ,the said county court,com-
posed of the county judge of said county and a majority
of the justicesof the peace in commission in said county ,
may cause an election to be held at the several voting
places in said county , and shallcause notice of the same
to be published in the newspapers in said county , and
shallalso cause said electionto be advertisedby printed
notices,posted up in at least six public places in each
magisterial districtfor at least twenty days before said
election. At said electionthe sense of the qualifiedvoters
shall be taken for and against the proposition to issue
bonds of the county to aid in the construction of a rail-
road through said county . Those who shallvote in favor
of the proposition shall be so recorded ; those who vote
against it shall be so recorded. Said election shall be
held in the same manner ,by the same officers,and under
the same responsibilities, as in case of elections under
the general laws of the State, and the poll-books shall
be returned by the same officers, and in the same time
and manner as in case of elections under the general
laws ; the poll-books shall be opened and compared by
the officers at the same time and in the same manner
as required in cases of election under the general laws
of the State. Said officersso comparing saidpollsshall
make out a certificate of the number of votes cast for
and against the proposition, which certificateshallbe
copied on the order-book of the Adair county court,and
the originalcertificateand the poll-books shallbe filed
and kept in the county courtclerk'sofficeof saidcounty.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 719
ا
§6. Ifitshallappearfrom saidcertificateof election
so made out and recorded that a majority of the votes
cast at said election were in favor of issuing bonds as
aforesaid, the county court shallproceed to have the
same carriedintoeffect,and shallappointthreeresponsi-
ble tax-payers of said county commissioners,who shall
have power to cause proper bonds to be prepared , which
shallbe signed by the county judge and countersigned
by the clerk, and the county seal affixed,with coupons
attached,signed by the clerk. The commissioners shall
have fullpower to carry into effectthe provisions of this
act in relation to all lands and rights of way , and the
sale or exchange of bonds , and the collectionof interest
on the railroad bonds , and the receiving of the taxes
that may be levied to pay principal or interest on the
county bonds, and provide for payment of intereston
said bonds and principal thereof, under the directions of
the county court; but no conveyance or transfershallbe
made to such railroad company of lands,rights of way ,
and easements untilsuch company shallgive proper cov-
enant to locate ,build , and maintain a suitable railroad
through said county as aforesaid; nor shallthe bonds ,nor
the proceeds of the same ,be delivered or paid to said
company until the grade of said railroad shallbe com-
pleted from terminus to terminus ,when one half may be
paid over,and the remaining half shallbe paid over on
the completionof the saidrailroad,ironed and ready for
operation,from terminus to terminus . The said commis-
sioners shall be a body -politicand corporate,under the
name of the Railroad Commissioners of Adair County ;
and under thatname may sue and be sued,contractand
be contracted with,in reference to all matters arising.
under this act. They shall appoint one of theirnumber
treasurer,who shallgive bond ,beforethe Adair county
court, with good and sufficientsureties, conditioned that
he will discharge his duties as such treasurer,and will
faithfullyaccount for and pay over to those entitled
thereto allmoney which may come to his hands as such ,
and will safelykeep all bonds or other property with

720 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
which he may be intrustedin hisofficialcapacity,and
deliver the same as he may be legallydirected. The
treasurer shall be the custodian of all bonds , contracts ,
deeds , and moneys authorized by this act,or in any way
accrue under the same .
§7 . That whenever itmay be necessaryto raisemoney
to pay intereston the county bonds to raisea sinking
fund to retireor pay the bonds ,the county court aforesaid
shall levy an ad valorem tax on all the property in said
county,real, personal, and mixed,which is liableto be
taxedforStaterevenue purposes,which levymay be made
and collectedon the assessments of property of the coun-
ty forState revenue; but no levy shallexceed fiftycents
on the one hundred dollarsin value of the property
assessed for any one year .
§ 8. Itshallbe the duty of the sheriffof Adair county
to collectall taxes levied under authorityof thisact,and
he shall be liablefor the same on his bond as such given
for levy and dues ,and shall have the same power of dis-
traint,levy,and sale of real and personalpropertyand
attachment of choses as in cases of collection of State
revenue ,and he shallbe entitledto compensation for col-
lectingthe same ,the same commission as forcollecting
the county levy; but the county courtshallhave the right
to demand a special bond of the sherifffor said revenue
if itdeem proper,and may ,in his discretion,appoint a
specialcollectorof said taxesand revenues,and require
of him a bond with sureties,conditioned as in other cases
of tax collections; the sheriffand special collectorsshall
pay over to the treasurerof the railroadcommissioners
alltaxes collectedby them ,and shallbe liableto the same
actions and motions as lieagainst other collectingofficers;
saidtax shallbe due and payable by thecollectingofficer
atthe same time the county levyisdue and payable.
§9. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 30 , 1884 .

CHAPTER 1185 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Peck'sSpring Stock Company ..
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That L. Drane , W. V. Prather, Daniel Durbin,
Achilles Perrin,Frank Gray ,F. B. Gray , W. T. Lafferty,
and theirassociates,be ,and are hereby ,created a body-
politicand corporate, under the name and style of the
Peck's Spring Stock Company ; and as such they and their
successorsshallhave perpetual succession, with power
to sue and be sued in the corporatename in and out of
thisState; to make contracts,acquire,hold,and own such
realestateand personalpropertyas may be necessaryfor
the transaction of the corporate business; to have a com-
mon seal,with the power to change and alterthe same at
pleasure. They shallhave power to make allnecessary
rulesand regulationsfor carrying on the business of said
corporation, not inconsistent with the Constitution and
laws of this State or the United States , and such other
powers as are necessaryand proper forthe existenceof
such corporation.
§2 . The capitalstockof saidcorporationshallbe not
lessthan fiftythousanddollars($50,000)nor more than
one hundred thousand dollars($ 100,000),in sharesof one
thousand dollars($ 1,000) each. The capitalstockof this
company is not to be increased afterthe corporation has
organized,electedofficers,and commenced business.
§3 . Said Peck'sSpring Stock Company shallbe under
the controland directionof five(5) directorsand a presi-
dent , each of whom shall be a stockholder . Said direct-
ors and presidentshallbe electedon the firstMonday in
May of each succeedingyear,and shallhold officeuntil
their successorsare elected and fullyqualified.
§ 4. Said president and board of directorsshallelectall
officersforthe proper conducting of the business of said
corporation; and they shallhold regular meetings at such
times and in such manner as may be prescribedby the
constitutionand by laws of said corporation. Three of
LOC . L., VOL . II- 46

722 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the board shallbe a quorum for the transaction of busi-
ness,and ,in absence of the president,shallselectone of
their number chairman .
§5 . In case of death or resignation of any of the di-
rectors, the vacancy shall be filledfor such unexpired
term by the board of directors, unless otherwise provided
by the constitutionand by -laws . The vacancy caused by
the death or resignationof the president is to be filledby
the electionof another by the stockholders.
§6 The certificateof stock shall be signed by the
president, countersigned by the secretary, and stamped
with the seal of the corporation. They shallbe transfer-
able only upon the books of the corporationand upon
the surrenderof the certificate,with proper assignment
thereon,by the partyto whom itwas issued,said certifi-
cates of stock shallbe taken in,canceled, and a new one
issued in lieu thereof .
§7 . The principalbusiness of thiscorporation shall be
the buying of land in the State of Texas ,and conducting
a cattleand sheep ranch; and may ,upon a two- thirds
vote of the stock,borrow money for conducting said bus-
iness .
§ 8. The profits arising from said business are to be .
reinvested as they arise, in land or live-stock, as the di-
rectorsmay deem best. In no case is a dividend to be
declared except by a two-thirdsvote of the stock.
§ 9. They shallemploy such agents and officersas they
shalldeem necessaryto carryon the business,and pay
them such wages and salariesas theydeem proper and
right .
§ 10. The sale of the ranch may be made by the direct-
ors : Provided, That the sale is indorsed by a two- thirds
vote of the stock ; but at such sale none of the stockhold-
ers are to be purchasers, either directlyor indirectly,
without the consent of all.
§ 11. Each stockholder shall have one vote for each
shareof stockhe owns ,and may vote by writtenproxy.
§ 12. This chartermay be alteredor amended upon a
two -thirds vote of the stock .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY 723
13. Private property of the stockholders shallbe ex-
empt from the debts of the corporation.
§ 14. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 30, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1186 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Creed Hays ,of Clay county .
WHEREAS , Almyra Burriswas ,by proper proceedingin
the Clay county court,on the fifteenthday of July,one
thousand eight hundred and eighty,found to be a lunatic;
and whereas ,she was refused admittance into the lunatic
asylum untilthe fifteenthof May , one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-one , and during said time ,from the
aforesaid fifteenthof July, one thousand eight hundred
and eighty,untilthe fifteenthof May ,one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-one,was kept by said Creed Hays
for the period of ten months ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts draw his
warrant upon the Treasurerin favor of the said Creed
Hays forthe sum of sixty-two dollarsand fiftycentsfor
the keeping of the said Almyra Burris,payable out of
any money in the Treasurynot otherwiseappropriated.
§2 . This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage .
Approved April 30, 1884.
CHAPTER 1188 .
AN ACT topreventstock from running at largein Simpson county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. Itshallbe unlawful forany person owning or hav-
ing care or possession of any horse, jack ,jennet ,mule ,
cattle, sheep , hog , horse, or stock or cattle of any kind ,
to permit the same to run at largein Simpson county

724 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
afterthisact takeseffect; and any person or persons so
offending shall be liable to such penalties and damages
as are herein prescribed and provided for in thisact.
§2 . That whenever any stock of the kind named in
firstsection of thisact shallbe found running at large, it
shallbe the duty of the sheriffor his deputies,or the
constable of the district, or the marshal of Franklin ,
when such stock is found, to seize upon , impound , or
confine said stock,and provide for theirfeeding and safe-
keeping until the owner or owners of such stock shall
have redeemed them by the payment of allfeesand costs
of every kind incident to such seizure,feeding,and keep-
ing same .
§3. Whenever any stock herein referredto shallbe
found upon the premises,or trespassingupon the prem-
ises, possessions, or inclosuresof any person,in whose
districtand precinctthisact may be adopted,whether
owner ,lessee,or tenant, the owner of said stock shall be
Diable for alldamages dues and for all costs incident to
such trespass,damage ,or impounding , recoverable before
any officerof competent jurisdiction(notwithstandingthe
premises or propertyso injuredor trespassedupon may
not be inclosedby a lawful fence,as now requiredby
law),and thepartyinjuredshallhave a lienupon thestock
so trespassingor doing injuryforalldamages ,costs,and
fees; and may alsohave executionleviedupon saidstock,
and the same sold in satisfaction thereof , notwithstand-
ing it(saidproperty) may be exempt from levy and sale
under execution, attachment ,or distress.
§ 4. For taking up stock running at large, or commit-
tingtrespass,the officershallbe entitledto the following
fees: For each horse, jack,jennet , mule ,colt, bull, cow ,
steer,heifer, or calf, one dollar,and for each hog, pig,
sheep,or othercattle,twenty-fivecents.
§ 5. Itshallbe the duty of the officermaking the seiz-
ure to give notice within fivedays (ifthe owner isknown
to him) from date of seizureto the owner ,keeper,pos-
sessor,or keeper of stock; and ifstock found offending
againsttheprovisionsof thisact isnot redeemed within

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 725
.
five days of notice,said officersshalladvertise(ten (10)
days beforeday ofselling) such stockforsaleat thevoting
place where such stockwas seizedupon by him ; the sale
shallbe proceeded with in the same manner as salesare
conducted under executions for the recovery of debt ; the
sale shall be made on a credit of three months ,the pur-
chaser giving bond with approved security; and for sale
and advertisement shall receive the same commission and
fees as are allowed for sales under.execution .
§ 6. Any person finding any stock upon his premises ,
or on premises under his control,shallhave the rightto
take up and hold the same as estrays are now taken up
and held . In case where lands or farms are lying con-
tiguous,therebeing a divisionor partitionfence between
them , the provisions of the General Statutes in such cases
made and provided shallgovern and remain in fullforce.
§7 . When the bond mentioned in the fifthsection of
this act shallbe collected,afterallthe costs and expenses
connected with the seizure, keeping, and sale shallbe
paid by him ,he shallpay the residue,if any , of the pro-
ceeds of the sale over to the owner of such stock,and the
sheriffor officer,whoever he may be ,shallbe responsible
on his officialbond for his acts or shortcomings under
this act .
§ 8. This act shall be submitted to the qualifiedvoters
of Simpson county at the regular August electionof one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-four ; and itshall be
the duty of the officersof said electionto be held on the
firstMonday in August ,one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-four,to open a poll in each precinct or voting place
in saidcounty,and propound to each voterwho may offer
to vote at said electionthe question: " Are you for or
against the stock law ?" and registeror record his vote in
accordance to his response to said question,ifhe desiresto
vote,and does vote thereon; the vote shallbe certifiedand
returned and compared as they are required in electionsfor
State and county officers; and in allthe electionprecincts,
when itshallappear by the certificateof the examining
board that a majorityof those voting voted in favor of

726 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thisstock law ,then this act shall take effectand be in
force in such precincts or voting districtsas appear to
vote for same ; and in such precincts or voting places ,
where itshallappear a majority voted against the same ,
this law shallnot apply to the latterprecinctsor voting
districts. It shall be the duty of the examining board to
make out a certificate,under oath , of the votes cast at said
electionunder the provisions of thisact,giving the num-
her of votes cast in each precinct for and against the
stocklaw ,and deliverthe same to the presidingjudge of
the county court,who shall cause the same to be entered
of record by the county court elerk.
§ 9. Itshallbe the duty of the sheriffof Simpson coun-
ty,at leasttwenty days before the day of the next August
election,in each electionprecinct, in three public places
in said precinct,to advertisethat a vote will be taken on
the firstMonday in August ,one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four ,for and against thisstock law : Provided,
That the sheriff'sfailureto do his duty under thissection
shall not vitiate said election .
§ 10. That any one of the officersmentioned in thisact
who shall, on his own knowledge or information of others,
failto take up such stock as is found running at large,
failsto perform his duty required by thisact,shallbe sub-
ject to a fine of ten dollarsfor each offense.
§ 11. This act shall take effect and be in force from the
day of itspassage .
Approved April 30, 1884.
CHAPTER 1189 .
AN ACT to amend ,revise, and reduce into one the severalactsin regard to-

§1 . The boundary of the town of Shepherdsville, in

ginning on Salt riverat low water mark ,in the western

Turnpike ; thence down the center of said pike to a point

mons ' line to the Mt. Washington road to the line of T.
B. Curry,who now owns theParoquetteSpringsproperty,
and with said lineto John Corb'sline,around his tract
with said line extended to the river at low -water mark ;

ning .
§ 2. The municipalgovernment of saidtown shallcon-

the chairman of the board of trustees of Shepherdsville.
Each trustee shall be owner of at least one or more lots.
of ground in said town of Shepherdsville.
§3 . That said trustees and their successors in office

ed , of answering and being answered , and of defending
and being defended ,in allcourts or places; and may use

§4 . The other officersof said town shallbe a police

to time ,directand appoint .
§5 . The county attorney of Bullittcounty shall be ex
officioattorney for the town of Shepherdsville.
§6 . The firstelection under this act shallbe held the
firstSaturday in May , one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-four. Notice of said electionshallbe given by the

five or more public places in said town ,ten days before
saidelectionisheld. The policejudge shallappointtwo
judges ,a clerk, and sheriff,to hold the same . Said elec

tionshallbe held at the court-house in Shepherdsville,

noon .

or printed notices,posted in fiveor more public places in

shal; and the persons receiving the highest number of

otherofficersshallhold theirofficesat the pleasure of the
trustees :

the oath prescribedby the Constitutionof Kentucky ,and

ner as they think proper.

ersagainsttheby -laws and ordinancesof saidtown ,and

§25 . The assessor shall perform all the duties which

improving or levying taxes.
§26 . The trustees may , by ordinance or by -law , fix

tioned .
27. The policejudge ,treasurer,marshal ,and collector,
and such other officersas the board of trustees may re-
quire,shallseverally,beforethey enterupon the dutiesof
theirrespectiveoffices,execute a bond to the trusteesof
Shepherdsville,conditionedthat they willfaithfullyper-
form the dutiesof theirrespectiveoffices,and pay over
to such persons ,and at such times as they may be enti-
tledto receive the same ,allmoney that may come to their
hands as such officers. The bonds so executed shall be

spread upon the recordsof the board of trustees,and the
originalcarefullypreservedby the clerk.
§ 28. The trustees of Shepherdsville, or any person in-
jured by the breach of any of said bonds , may , in any

on againstthe officerby whose breach of duty such in-
jury isoccasioned ,and his sureties,or any of them ,or the
personal representatives,heirs,or deviseesof any or all
of them , as providedby law concerning sheriffs,consta-
bles,and other like officers. No such bond shallbe void
untilthe trusteesof Shepherdsville,and every person ag-
grieved by the acts,omission, or neglect of such officers,

§ 29. Any person,having been an officerin saidtown ,

§30 . There ishereby establishedin saidtown a court,
to be styledthe Shepherdsvillepolicecourt. Such court
shallbe held by one judge ,to be styledthe judge of the
Shepherdsville police court,and who shallbe elected and
qualified as hereinbefore prescribed. Said court shall
have exclusivejurisdictionof allprosecutionsforallvio-
lationsof the by -laws or ordinances of said town . Said
court shallhave the same penal and civiljurisdiction,

731 LAWS OF KENTUCKY
§ 31. Appeals shallbe from the decisions of said court

ion or judgment of a justice of the peace of Bullittcoun-
ty; and an appeal from said court shallbe taken to the

§32 . The trusteesof said town shallprovide the judge
of said court with an order -book ,execution -book ,docket ,
and all necessary stationery, and it shall be his duty to

§33 . Quarterly terms of said court for the trialof civil

§34 . The police court shallhe held at court-house in

house isoccupied by the county court or the circuitcourt,

§35 . The fees of the police judge shallbe the same in
allcases as are now , or may hereafter be ,given by law
to justicesof the peace of Bullittcounty ,subject to the

§36 .All finesand forfeituresrecovered in said court for
offenses committed within the town limits are hereby
granted to the town of Shepherdsville,and shallbe paid
by the officercollectingthesame to the treasurerof said
town .
§ 37. Upon alljudgments for finesrendered by said
court, and which shall not be paid or replevied at the
time of rendering the same , an execution against the
estateof thedefendantmay be issued,or a capiasprofine
may issue,requiring the imprisonment of the person in
the county jail until said fine be paid or replevied; but
said imprisonment shall not exceed one day for each
dollar of the fine; but said court may ,in its discretion,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 735
assigna person so finedto hard labor for the benefitof
the town untilhe shallpay the fineand costsso adjudged
againsthim ,at the rateof one dollarper day. No person
shallbe required to labor more than eight hours per day ,.
and while at labor he shall be under the control of the
town marshal,who shallbe responsibleupon hisofficial
bond for the balance of the fineremaining unpaid , ifsuch
person ispermitted to escape by reason of the negligence

may be assigned by said court to labor in like manner as

§38 . The trusteesof said town shallhave power ,sub-
ject to the exceptions hereinafter mentioned ,to annually

one hundred dollars' worth of propertyin saidtown ; but
no person shall be compelled to listfor taxation any

dent and tax-payer of said town ; said trusteesmay also

years,and a tax of not more than two dollars on every
dog which any person may keep or sufferto be kept on his
premises in said town ,said tax to be paid by the person
on whose premises the dog is kept . There shallbe ex-
empted from taxation in said town the property exempted
by the general laws of the Commonwealth , and no more ,
except as provided in this section.
§39 . All taxes imposed by said trusteesunder this act
shallbe a lienupon the property,realor personal,of the
person against whom the same is assessed, untilthe same
are paid; and no sale or transferof the propertyshall
affectthe lien thereon untilafterthe expiration of two
years from the time such taxes become due .

§ 40. The right of redemption in all cases of salesof
real estate for taxes shall exist to the owner or his heirs,
or vendees or assignees,for two years from the date of

was sold, with ten per centum per annum and the costs

§42 . The trustees shall make such rules and regula-
tionsconcerning the assessment of property as they may
see proper ; and also for correcting and revising the list
returned by the assessor.
§43 . The board of trustees shallhold stated meetings
at leastonce a month ; and the chairman ,or, in his ab-
scence,the town clerk,may callother meetings by notice,
in writing,to each of the members . A majority of the
members elected shall constitutea quorum . In the ab-
scence of the chairman , any member of the board may

§ 44. The board of trustees shallhave power to make ,
ordain,and publishallsuchordinances,by-laws,and reg-
ulations, not contrary to the Constitution of the United
States or the Constitutionand laws of Kentucky ,for the
good government of the town , and to promote health,

security,trade,and commerce , as they may deem neces-
sary or expedient. They may enforce the observance of
allordinances,by-laws,and regulationsmade in pursu-
ance of thisact,by penaltiesnot exceedingone hundred
dollars' fine and fiftydays ' imprisonment ; but said trus-
tees shallnot attach to any offenserecognized by the
statutes of this Commonwealth a penalty either greater
or lessthan isprescribedby the generallaws.
§: 45 . The board of trusteesshallhave the management
and control of the finances, and of allthe property , real,
personal , or mixed ,belonging to the town ; to provide for
the erection,repair, maintenance of all public buildings

ulentdevices,to license,tax,regulateor prohibitallcon-

isteringthe same , and the fees to be paid him therefor,

ers of such premises for the collection of such assess-

ment and the enforcement of the lien. All real estate

licplaces in the town ; and the board of trusteesmay

CHAPTER 1190 .
AN ACT for the benefit of the city of Carlisle .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the citycouncil of the cityof Carlislebe ,and
itis,authorized and empowered (a majority of said coun-
cilbeing present and concurring) to issue the bonds of
said cityto any amount not exceeding ten thousand dol-
lars, the proceeds thereofto be used exclusivelyin the
purchase of a fireengine or engines , water -hose , hose
reelsand carriage,firehooks and ladders,and such other
things as shall be deemed necessary by said council, in-
cluding the cost of building water cisterns or reservoirs
in said city,to be had and used to aid in preventing or
extinguishing fireswithin or adjacent to said city, and to
provide a house or other place for the safe-keeping and
preservation of said fireapparatus and machinery .
§ 2. Said bonds shall be of the denominations of one
hundred , two hundred , and five hundred dollars; they
shallbe numbered as issued severally,commencing with
the number one; they shallbe made payable twenty-five
years aftertheirdates,with the option on the part of the
cityto pay them at any time afterten years from their
date,bearing interestat a ratenot exceeding six(6) per
centum per annum from date,payable semi -annually ; the
principalwhen itmatures ,and the interestas itaccrues,to
be paid at such place as said common council may cause
to be designated in said bonds. Interestcoupons shallbe
attached to the bonds for each semi -annual installment of
interest,the coupons of each bond to be numbered serial-
ly from one to fifty,inclusive,each coupon to bear also the
same number of the bond to which it is attached . Each
of said bonds shallbe signed by the president of said city
council and countersigned by the cityclerk,and each of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 741
P
r
said interestcoupons shallbe signed by the cityclerk.
Said bonds and coupons shallbe made payable to bearer,
and shallpass by delivery.
§ 3. Said bonds,when prepared,or portionsof them ,
from time to time ,may be sold by said council, or any
committees appointedby it,or any agent it may appoint
for that purpose ,for the purpose of raising money to be
used and expended in and for the purposes and objects
aforesaid: Provided,They shall not be sold for lessthan
par value .
§ 4. Itshall be the duty of said common council,after
the sale of said bonds ,from year to year ,to levy a tax
upon the propertyand so forth,which by law issubjectto
taxation for general citypurposes in said city, so as to
raise an amount sufficientto meet and discharge the accru-
ing current interest on said bonds ; and said common coun-
cilmay also annually levy a tax upon said property forthe
creation of a sinking fund for the payment of the princi-
pal of said bonds , eitherat their maturity or at any time
after ten years from the date of said bonds ; when said
taxes shall be collected and accounted for as other taxes
are authorized by law to be collectedand accounted for
by law as to said citytaxes; and it shall be lawful for
said common council to loan out and make profitthereby
upon said sinking fund , which profitshallbe added to and
constitutepart of said sinking fund .
§5 . Said common council shallhave power to appoint
such committee or agent as it may deem necessary to
make purchases for the city of such fireapparatus as is
contemplated by thisact, or as,in theirjudgment ,neces-
sary to be purchased for the use of said city.
§6 . The said common council shall take bonds , with
good and sufficientsecurity,from such of the cityofficers
or agents as may be charged or intrustedwith the duty of
sellingsaid bonds , and receiving the proceeds, to account
for said bonds and their proceeds , and to faithfullydis-
charge the duties confided to them ; and any person who
shalluse or appropriate,or be a partyto or connive at
the use or appropriation of any of said bonds ,or any

742 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
part of theirproceeds,otherwisethan in the manner and
for the purposes herein authorized, directed, and con-
templated,shallbe deemed and heldto be guiltyof em-
bezzlement,and,upon conviction,shallbe punished as
provided by the law against embezzlement .
§7. This act shallbe in forcefrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 30, 1884.
CHAPTER 1191 .
AN ACT to furnish the judge of the Simpson county court with certain
books .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe the duty of the Secretary of State
to furnishto the judge of the Simpson county court with
a fullset of Kentucky Reports ,except the two volumes
known as Rodman's Reports ; and said Secretary of State
shall certifythe cost thereof to the Auditor , who shall

able to said Secretary .

CHAPTER 1192 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Newton Cogar , of Mercer county .
WHEREAS , Newton Cogar, of the county of Mercer,
State of Kentucky , whose father and mother are both
living,and who .forthe pastfiveyears,by consentof his
father,has been doing businessforhimself,but is yet not
twenty -one years of age , and desiring to engage in mer-
cantileor otherpursuits,and to be bound legallyfor his
debts,and to have creditamong his neighbors ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1.That Newton Cogar ,of Harrodsburg ,Mercer coun-
ty,Kentucky ,son of J. E. Cogar, be, and he ishereby,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 743
authorizedto contractand tradein hisown name ,and to
be bound therefor,in law or equity,as though he was of
full age .
§ 2. That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 2, 1884.
! CHAPTER 1193 .
AN ACT for the benefitof school districtnumber forty-seven, in Hart
county .
WHEREAS , The school house in common school district
mumber forty-seven , Hart county , was burned down ,pre-
venting thereby a common school from being taught in
said districtforthe present school year ; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Superintendent of Public Instruction is
hereby authorized to draw his draft on the Auditor of
Public Accounts in favor of Isaac A. Edwards ,commis-
sioner of common schools for said county , for the sum
of seventy -seven dollars and twenty -two cents ($ 77.22),
which shall be paid to said districtfor the purpose of
paying the teacher of its school for five months ' addi-
tional service before the close of the school year ending
June thirtieth, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
five .
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved May 1,1884 .
CHAPTER 1194 .
AN ACT toincorporatethetownof Walnut Grove,inMorgan county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I.
§1 . That the town of Walnut Grove ,in Morgan county ,
Kentucky ,be,and itishereby,incorporatedas a town ,to
be called and known as Walnut Grove , and that the

744 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
boundaries thereof shall be as follows: Beginning at the
house of James Wheeler , on the west side of Caney
Creek , where the widow Wheeler now lives; thence a
straightline to the house of Isaac Watson , so as to in-
clude him ; thence a straightline to the house of James
M. Benton ,including him ; thence a straight line to the
home of A. J. Patton, includinghim; thence a straight
lineto the beginning .
§ 2. That allthe financialand municipal affairsof said
town shall be vested in five trustees, who shall be elect-
ed every two years ; and no person shallbe eligibleto the
officeof trustee except a resident of said town . Said
trustees shall hold their offices until their successors are
elected and qualified. They shall have power to fillva-
cancies in theirown body , occurring by reason of death ,
removal , or resignation. A majority of said trustees
shallconstitutea quorum for the transaction of business.
§3 . On the firstSaturday of June ,one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-four, and every two years thereafter,
there shall be an election held in said town for the elec-
tion of fivetrustees, a police judge ,and a town marshal .
Ten days'noticeof such electionshallbe given by the
clerk or chairman of the board of trustees,by written or
printed posters.
§4. Said trusteesshallbe a body-corporate, with all
the general powers conferred by the laws of this Com-
monwealth upon corporate bodies of the likecharacter,
forthe maintenance of law and good order of said town ;
may have and use a common seal, and may altersame ,
not exceeding one time to each two years'term of office.
They shallbe known by the name and styleof the Board
of Trustees of Walnut Grove , and as such may sue and
be sued ,plead and be impleaded ; may contract for and
hold property, money , and , not exceeding in the aggre-
gate,five hundred acres of land , convenient to Walnut
Grove ,for public purposes .
§ 5. Until an election is had for town officers,James M.
Benton , A. J. Patton ,James Wheeler ,Dudly Lykins ,and
Lebom Lykins are constitutedtrusteesof said town ,and
shall so continue until the election of trustees and their

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 745
qualification,on the said firstSaturday of June ,one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-four; and ifthischarter be
not granted in time for the ten days ' notice of electionin
one thousand eighthundred and eighty-four, then said
persons may hold theirofficesuntil the election of suc-
cessors on the firstSaturday of June ,one thousand eight
hundred and eighty six. In allcases officersof said town ,
when chosen by town elections, shall,on the following
Monday ,qualify,except the policejudge,who shallqual-
ifyas soon as may be convenient afterreceiving his com-
mission of office from the Governor . The officers in all
caseshereinmay qualifyby takingthe oath of officebe-
fore any justiceof the peace of Morgan county or the
judge thereof .
§ 6. The said trustees,including those persons named
as trusteesin section five,afterhaving sworn to discharge
the duties of their office, shall elect one of their number
chairman , to preside over their deliberations,who shall
be known as chairman of the board of trustees of Walnut
Grove , and as such shallhave a vote only in case of a tie
in the remaining trustees. Said board may alsoelectone
of theirnumber as clerk,who shall,in a well-bound book ,
keep recorded the minutes of the board ; and itshall be
at alltimes open forpublicinspectionin his custodyand
presence. Copies of saidminutes and proceedingsof the
board , under the hand of said clerk , or of the chairman
of the trustees, when there is no clerk, shall be received
as record evidence in courts .
ARTICLE II .
§ 1. The police judge shallhave exclusive jurisdiction
of violations of the by -laws and ordinances of said town ,
and concurrent jurisdictionwith justices of the peace of
allcriminal and penal causes , and shallsitas a court of
examination ,with concurrent jurisdictionof two justices,
in felony cases within the limits of said town . All war-
rantsissuedby him as policejudge shallbe directedto
the marshal , sheriff,jailer,or to any constable of Morgan
county, who shallexecute the same . Before entering
upon the dutiesof his office,said policejudge shallbe

746 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sworn to honestlyand faithfullyperformthe dutiesof the
office,in addition to the oaths required by law .
§2 . Said court shall have exclusive jurisdictionin all
cases where the trusteesshall proceed to condemn land
or materials for the opening , extending streets,alleys, or
other propertyfor public use,and shallhave power to
award the writ of ad quoddamnum and adjudicateupon
the same .
§3 . Said court shall always be open for the trial of
criminal causes,and shall have the same power to sum-
mon and impanel juries, and to adjudge finesin the
same manner , and to not exceeding the same amount , as
is possessedby justicesof the peace; and appeals shall
be had from judgments in similar manner as from jus-
ticesof the peace in criminal and penal causes as tojudg-
ments in cases of writs of ad quod damnum in similar
manner as from county courts .
§ 4. It shall be the duty of the marshal to see that the
ordinances and the by laws of said town are carried into
execution ; shallcollectalltaxes imposed by the trustees,
and shallgive a good and sufficientbond annually , to be
executed before and approved by the board of trustees,
forthe faithtulaccount of all moneys that may come to
hishands,and forthe performanceof hisdutiesof office.
Said bond shall be attested by the chairman or the clerk
of the board of trustees, and shallbe filedby them in the
records of said town .
§ 5. Said police judge , marshal , and trustees, except
the clerk of the board , shall not be allowed any salary,
but they shall each receive the fees of their respective
officesas now fixedand regulated by law for magistrates,
constables,sheriffs, and other officersperforming services
similar to those required of them by thisact.
§ 6. That the fines and forfeituresrecovered and paid
in from all sources within said town are granted to said
town ,excepting so much as goes to the Commonwealth .
ARTICLE III.
§1 . The board of trustees may clear the streets and
alleys,sidewalks, and passways of said town of all ob

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 747
structions,abate and prevent nuisances. They may enact
by -laws for the prevention of the erectionof unsafe chim-
neys,flues,and fires; may requirethe owners of property
fronting on streetsto keep the sidewalks open ; continue ,
enlarge, and alterstreetsand alleys,and lay off squares ,
improve sidewalks , and grade streets, whenever public
necessitymay requireand as thegrowth of the town may
demand .
§2 . In laying off squares and opening streetsand
alleys,if the consent of the owners of the land cannot
be firstobtained ,the trustees may ,after ten days ' notice
to the owners thereof, or their agent , proceed to con .
demn same ,and assess the value thereof before the police
court of said town , according to the general law now or
then existing and in force .
§3 . In case of a vacancy at any time in the officeof
police judge ,the chairman of the board of trusteesmay ,
for the time being ,act as such judge , with all the inci-
dents and powers of such judge. Should there be a
vacancy in the officeof town marshal, one may be
elected by the board of trusteesto act until the vacancy
has been filledby a regular election.
§ 4. The board of trusteesshallhave power to levy
and collecttaxes as follows : an ad valorem tax upon
the property of said town liable for taxation for State
revenue of not exceeding one cent to each dollar's worth
of property in value .
§5 . All personsresidingin the town of Walnut Grove ,
as also allpersons owning real estatesituatedin said
town , and who are legal voters at county and State elec-
tions,shallbe qualifiedvotersat allmunicipal elections
held in and for the town of Walnut Grove .
§ 6. All process in cases in which the police judge has
exclusive jurisdictionshallbe issued in the name of the
town of Walnut Grove .
§7 . The marshal shall have the same power to levy
upon and sellpropertyfortown taxes,and forthe pay-
ment of finesand penalties under the by -laws and ordi-
nances of Walnut Grove , that the sheriff has for the

748 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
payment of Stateand county taxes; and in the enforce-
ment of executionsand writsforfinesand penalties,he
may make sale in front of the post-officein Walnut
Grove ,upon ten days ' notice of time and terms .
§ 8. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 2 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 1195 .
AN ACT to empower the purchaser of the Crittenden county delinquent
tax -lists to collect the same .
WHEREAS ,The Crittenden county court did,on the fourth
day of December ,one thousand eighthundred and eighty-
two , afterhaving advertised,sellat public sale the delin-
quent county tax -listsforthe years of one thousand eight
hundred and eighty and one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-one ,thereby securing an increase of county reve-
nue ; and whereas , doubts exist as to the power of the
purchaser of said delinquent liststo collect the same ;
therefore ,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the purchaser of said listsishereby invested
with the same power and authority for the collectionof
the same as is now conferred upon sheriffsfor the collec
tion of county revenue .
§2 . This act shall be in force from and after itspas-
sage .
Approved May 1, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1196 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Wigginton , in Franklin county .
Be itenactedby the General Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the town of Wigginton ,in Franklin county ,
Kentucky , is hereby incorporated,in the name of Wig-
ginton; and the corporate limits of said town shall ex-
tend one half mile in every direction from George W.
Parker's mill in said town .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 749
§2 . That Wyatt Parker, Dr. J.A. Estis, Geo . W. Par-
'ker ,D. J. Gaines , and John T. Spencer be , and they are
hereby , appointed trusteesof said town , who shallchoose
one of theirbody as chairman and one as clerk. Ama-
jority shall constitute a quorum to transact business.
They shall keep a record of their proceedings ,and such
proceedingsshallbe subjectto the inspectionof any cit-
izen at any time ; and allby laws and ordinances passed
by the board of trusteesshallbe posted at threepublic
places in said town at least ten days before the same
shall take effect . Said trustees shall remain in office un-
tilthe firstMonday in September, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-four , upon which day , and upon the
firstMonday in September in each year thereafter, the
male citizensof said town over twenty -one years of age
shallmeet at a place in said town to be designated by
the board of trustees, and choose fivepersons viva voce to
serve as trustees of said town for one year , and until
their successors are duly elected and qualified; and the
chairman of the board of trustees,or some one appointed
by the board , shallhold the election.
§3 . That all citizens living in said town , or owning
propertytherein,and paying taxesto saidtown ,who are
qualified to vote and hold officeunder the Constitution
and laws of the State of Kentucky , are entitled to vote
and hold officein said town .
§4. That John M. Parker is appointed policejudge,
and S. O. Wigginton is appointedmarshal of saidtown ,
untilthe electionhereinbeforeprovidedforin paragraph
second for the election of trustees of said town , and
who shallhold theirofficesuntilthe firstMonday in Sep-
tember,one thousand eighthundred and eighty-four,on
which day ,and on the same day every two years there-
after,thereshallbe an electionheldforpolicejudge and
town marshal of said town ,who shallhold their offices
for two years,or until their successors are elected and
qualified. Said electionshallbe held at the same place,
by the same persons, and in the same manner , as the
election for trustees of said town ; that before the said

750 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
marshal shallenterupon the dutiesof hisoffice,he shall
execute a bond in the county court the same as consta-
bles are now by law required to execute , and take the
oath prescribed forconstables,so far as the same applies.
§5 . All the officersof said town , including trustees,
shall, before they enter upon the duties of their several
offices,take the oath prescribed by law .
§6 . The police judge shall have concurrent jurisdic-
tionwith justicesof the peace in allpenal [and]criminal
and civilcases,and charge the same feesthat justicesare
allowed for similar services . The marshal shall have
concurrent jurisdictionwith the constables of Franklin
county ,and charge the same fees for their services,and
shall collect all taxes due the town , and pay the same
over to the board of trustees .
§7 . The trusteesare empowered to enact such by -laws
and enact such regulations as may be necessary for the
good government and well being of said town , not in
contravention of the laws of the Commonwealth , and to
fillany vacancy in any officecreatedby this act that
may occur .
§ 8. Said trusteesshall have power to levy taxes on all
persons livingin said town ,or owning propertywithin
the corporatelimits,not exceeding twenty-five cents on
each one hundred dollars' worth of taxable property;
also to impose a tax not exceeding one dollar on each
male citizenover the age of twenty one years ; and such
taxes , and all fines and forfeitures for offenses committed
in said town ,shall be applied by the trusteesin such
manner as will be beneficial to said town .
§ 9. Said trustees may sue and be sued in theircorpo-
rate capacity,in the name of the trusteesof the town of
Wigginton .
§ 10. The police judge shallhold his courts for trialof
civilcases on a certain day , to be fixed by him , in the
months of March , June , September , and December in
each year ; and hiscourt shallbe open at alltimes for the
trialof offensesagainst the penal and criminal laws .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 751
§ 11. The officeof town marshal of Wigginton shall
not be incompatible with the officeof constable of Peak's
Millprecinct,in Franklincounty,Kentucky .
§ 12. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 2 , 1884.
CHAPTER 1197 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Walter Eason , late jailerof Garrard county .
WHEREAS , On the -- day of April,one thousand eight
hundred and seventy -six,one John Forbus ,charged by in-
dictment of the Garrard circuit court with the crime of
horse-stealing, escaped from the jail of Garrard county ,
Kentucky ,and went to the State of Indiana ; and whereas ,
Walter Eason was appointed this State's agent by a
requisitionof the Governor of Kentucky upon the Gov-
ernor of Indiana for the arrestand delivery of said For-
bus ; and whereas ,said Eason did take the requisitionof
the Governor of Kentucky ,and go to the State of Indiana
and procure a requisitionfrom the Governor of the State
of Indiana for the arrest of the said Forbus , and did have
him arrested on the day of— , 18— , and de-
liveredto him (the said Eason ),and he brought him and
lodged him in the jail of Garrard county , and the said
Forbus was ,thereafter,on the thirtiethday of August ,one
thousand eight hundred and seventy -six,convicted of the
crime charged in the indictment and sent to the peniten-
tiaryfor two years; and whereas ,said Eason paid out in
actual expenses in capturing and returning said Forbus ,
the sum of one hundred dollars,and has never been reim-
bursed in any way or to any extent ; now ,therefore,
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky be ,and he is hereby ,directed to draw his warrant
upon the Treasurer of the State of Kentucky ,payable to
Walter Eason for the sum of seventy -five dollars.
§ 2. This act to take effect from its passage .
Approved May 2, 1884 .

752 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1198 .
AN ACT to providefor indexing certainrecordsin Jeffersoncounty .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county clerk of Jefferson county shall
cause to be made out a new general index to the records
of inventoriesand settledaccounts, minute-books (or
order-books of the Jefferson county court), bond and
power of attorneybooks,[and] will-books; and that the
said county clerk have all books which are now on file
in the Jefferson county clerk'sofficethat are in a dilapi-
dated condition re copied ; and he shall receive therefor
a compensation to be fixedby the judge of the Jefferson
county court .
§2 . That the compensation for the aforesaid work shall
be paid monthly as the work progresses, and shall be al-
lowed and paid upon the certificateof the judge of the
Jeffersoncounty court alone, and the same to be paid as
are all just claims against the county of Jefferson and
the cityof Louisville, Kentucky .
§ 3. That whenever any book or books in the Jefferson
county clerk'sofficeshallneed re-copying,the judge of
the Jeffersoncounty court is ordered to direct the county
clerk of the Jefferson county court to cause the same
[to be] done ; and the saidclerkto receivehiscompensa-
tion for such work ,the same to be fixed by the judge of
the Jeffersoncounty court.
§ 4. That for the purpose of carrying into effectthe
foregoing provisions of this act, there shall only be ex-
pended the sum of four thousand dollars,and no more .
§5 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
-sage .
Approved May 2,1884 .

CHAPTER 1199 .
AN ACT to authorizethe judge of the Bath county court to appoint exam-
iners for Bath county .

authorizedand empowered to appoint one or more ex-
aminers for Bath county , in the same manner , and with

ments under sectionfivehundred and fifty-nine of the

Approved May 2, 1884 .
AN ACT for the benefitofR. C. Estill, of Fayette county .
WHEREAS , R. C. Estill, of Fayette county, Kentucky ,
has ,for the years one thousand eight hundred and seven-
ty-seven,one thousand eighthundred and seventy-eight,
one thousand eight hundred and seventy-nine ,one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty, and one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-one ,paid taxes upon his whole tract
of land to the sheriffof Bourbon county ,and has also,
upon partof the same land,in thoseyears,paid taxes to
the sheriffof Clark and Fayette counties. The taxes im-

amounts to the sum of ninety-fourdollarsand thirty-five
cents, and has not been refunded to him ; therefore,

CHAPTER 1201 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Kuttawa common schooldistrict, inLyon
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafter the Kuttawa common school district
shall be bounded as common school district number twen-
ty-nine,embracing the town of Kuttawa , Lyon county ,
Kentucky ,is now bounded ,subject only to such changes
as shall be hereafter made by the concurrent action of
the board of trustees of said district and the common
school commissioner of Lyon county .
§2 . A common school shallhereafterbe taught in said
district for not less than five nor more than ten months
of each year ,at which allwhite childrenbetween the age
of six and twenty years residingtherein shall receive in-
structionwithout any charge therefor.
§3 . The exclusivemanagement , regulation,and con-
trolof said school, and of the school fund received from
the State or nation,and of the fund raisedby taxation
under this act , shall be vested in a board of education , to
be composed of six persons.
§ 4. The present board of common school trusteesshall
be members of said board of education during the term
of officefor which they were elected.
§5 . The members of said board of education of Kut-
tawa shall possess the qualificationsof voters of said
district,and shall be a body -politicand corporate ,under
the name of the Board of Education of Kuttawa .
§ 6. On the lastSaturday in June ,one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-four,an electionshallbe held at some
convenient place in the town of Kuttawa ,between the
hours of nine, ante meridian ,and six,post meridian , for
the purpose of electingfour members of the board of
education .
§7 . At the electionsprovided forherein,allthe white
qualifiedvoters, and any widow or maid who is a tax-
payer , or who has childrenbetween six and twenty years
of age, and , exceptingsex,qualifiedto vote,and being
citizensof said district, shallbe voters therein.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 755
§ 8. At the firstelectionfor members of the board of
education ,the three persons receiving the highest num-
ber of votes shall hold officefor four years; and the one
receiving the next highest number , and the two common
school trustees stillremaining in office,shall hold office
fortwo years,and each shallcontinue in officeuntil his
successor is elected and qualified. Each second year
thereafter,at the time,place ,and manner herein desig-
nated, an electionshall be held for three members of the
board ,to serve four years .
§ 9. Said electionsshallbe held in the manner prescribed
for the election of common school trustees .
§ 10. Within ten days afterthe election,the board of
education shalldeliverto each person elected a certificate
of election .
§ 11. Within ten days afterreceiving such certificateof
election,the person shall make oath before any one quali-
fiedto administer an oath to faithfullydischarge the duties
of his office as member of the board of education .
§ 12. The board of education of Kuttawa shall have
power to contract, sue,and plead as a person ; to levy
and collecta tax of not exceeding fortycents on the one
hundred dollars' worth of taxable property,and a poll-
tax of not more than two dollarsper head ; and to receive
and use any property now in possession of the common
school trustees; to receive conveyances of land , and
buy or erectsuch buildingsas may be necessaryforthe
Kuttawa common school; to appoint members to fill
allvacancies in office; to appoint a secretary,collector,
treasurer, assessor, janitor, examiner of teachers, super-
intendent of the school, and fix the salaries of each ; to
expend the funds receivedby them for schoolpurposes;
to establishand control a necessary common school; to
regulate and prescribe rules for the government of the
same ; to prescribe the course of study therein; to ex-
amine , or cause to be examined , teachers; to reprove ,
dismiss,or expel or suspend any teacher or officersap
pointedby them,forinefficiency,neglect,or violationof
duty , or immoral conduct ; to prescribe text books ; to fix

756 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the number of departments in said school; to grade the
school, and regulate the progress of scholars from grade
to grade , and from department to department ; to admit
to the benefits of said school non -resident pupils and
pupilsnot embraced in the schoollaw ,and charge them
tuition therefor; to make by -laws and rulesfor the gov-
ernment of the board and school ; to enlarge the district
when necessary,by consent of the county common school
commissioner; and such otherpowers as may be neces-
saryand proper to effectthe objectscommitted to their
charge : Provided, That before the tax as herein provided
for islevied and collected,the same shallbe submitted to
the white voters of said common school district, at the
election to be held under the supervision of the present
trustees of the Kuttawa common school,previous notice
thereof having been firstgiven ,by written or printed no-
ticesof the said electionbeing posted at fiveof the most
public places in the said district,forat leastten days next
before the day of the said election,statingthe time ,place,
and object of the said election ; and if at said election a
majority of the votes cast shall be in favor of said tax ,
the same shallbe levied and collectedas provided for in
thisact; but if the majority be against the said tax,then
it shall not be levied or collected . And at said election
the question shallbe distinctlyasked each voter,"Are you
for or against the proposed school tax in this district? "
and his vote shall be recorded according to his answer
and certifiedaccordingly; and the said tax shallbe col-
lectedby the collectorappointed by the said board of
education, as provided in sectionfourteen of thisact.
§ 13. Itshallbe the duty of the board of education ,as
soon as properly organized ,to at once buy the building
known as Kuttawa High School , and the lot on which
the same stands,for a sum not exceeding two thousand
dollars, it being understood that said house and lot can
be purchased for said sum ; but in case the board are
unable to buy said house and lot for the sum above
specified,by the stockholders refusing to sell,it shallbe
the duty of the board to select and purchase suitable

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 757
grounds,and have erectedthereonsuitablebuildingsfor
the school herein provided for; to employ teachers,and
cause a school to be taught therein; to superintend,
regulate , and control the same ; to assess, so far as au-
thorized by this act, a tax each year for the purpose
of paying for said house and lot,or grounds and build-
ings,and to pay a sufficientnumber of teachers therein;
and the miscellaneous expenses of the school; to take
care of the school property ; receive and disburse the
funds pertaining to the school; select suitable teachers;
fix salaries; grade the school; provide fuel; and such
other duties as they are empowered to do by this act;
make suitable reports to the common school commis-
sioner of the county ; to take titleto them and theirsuc-
cessors in their corporate name for all school property ;
to surrender all school property and funds to their suc-
cessors; and do and perform all other necessary duties
incident to the execution of the trust confided to it.
§ 14. The taxes herein imposed shallbe a lienupon the
property of the person or company owing the same ,situ-
ated in said district. The assessor's book shall be deliv-
ered to the collectoron or before the firstday of July of
each year,and he shallat once make -up therefromhis
tax -book and proceed to collectthe taxes. Said taxes
shallbe due on and after the tenth day of July of each
year,and shallbe paid over by the collectorto the treas-
urer of the board of education , as follows : one -third
thereofon thefirstday of September each year; one-third
on the the firstday of November of each year ; one third
on thefirstday ofJanuary following; and shallgive bond
to the board of education for the faithfulperformance of
his duties; and shall have power to collect said taxes,
when necessary,by processof law ,as the sheriffcollects
ordinary taxes.
§ 15. Allpropertyshallbe liableforthe taxesthatare
hereinprovidedfor,thatisliableforStatetax and county
levy .
§ 16.All theprovisionsof thisactshallbe construedas
applyingtothepersonsand propertyof the whitecitizens
only, of said district.

758 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 17. This act shall take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 1, 1884.
CHAPTER 1202 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe LouisvillePress Club .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That R. M. Kelly, Daniel E. O. Sullivan, William
M. Hull, Hawthorne Hill, William M. Finley,Henry S.
Cohn ,Young E. Allison, E. Polk Johnson , Ben . H. Ridge-
ly,John Duncan , and Emmett G. Logan , of the city of
Louisville,and theirassociates,forthe purpose of social
enjoyment , improvement in the professionof journalism ,
and the entertainment of visitorsto the city,be ,and are
hereby ,created and made a body -politicand corporate,
under and by the name of the LouisvillePress Club .
§2 . That the said corporation,by said name ,may sue
and be sued in any of the courts of thisCommonwealth ;
it may contract and be contracted with in all matters
concerning itsbusiness as a club; it may have and use
a privateseal,and break and alterthe same at will; it
may adopt such constitutionand by -laws as may be nec-
essaryforitsorganizationunder thischarter; and itshall
have all the several rights,privileges,and liabilitiesof
incorporate bodies .
3. That the LouisvillePress Club may take and hold,
by gift,purchase ,grant,devise,or bequest,personal prop-
ertyto the value of ten thousand dollars,and realprop-
erty to the amount of twenty -five thousand dollars; but
the said propertyshallbe used as a clubhouse and its
appendages and appurtenances , and for the purpose of
supportingand carryingon said corporationalone; and
the said corporation shall not engage in any other busi-
ness save that of conducting and controlling the club
house .
§4 . That the private property of the members of the
club shall not be liable for its debts, and the club shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 759
at no time be indebted in an amount exceeding two
thousand dollars.
§ 5. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 3, 1884.
CHAPTER 1203 .
AN ACT to amend and revisethe charter of the cityof Mayfield,Graves
county .
ARTICLE I.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That so much of the county of Graves as includes
the whole of the southeast quarter of sectionten,township
three,range one east (in which was laidoffthe old town of
Mayfield,insaidcounty),theadditiontosaidtown of West
Mayfield ,a plan of which isrecorded in the Graves coun-
ty court clerk'soffice,and the houses and lotsof Jesse S.
Thomas ,and B. F. and Martha Cargill; and the persons
residing within said limits and boundaries are hereby
created a body -politicand corporate,by the name and
styleof the " Cityof Mayfield;" and by that name may
have perpetualsuccession, sue and be sued, plead and
be impleaded,defend and be defended againstat law and
in equity, in all courts and places, and in all matters
whatsoever ; contract and be contracted with ; have and
use a common seal,and alter,change ,or renew the same
atpleasure; and generallyhave and enjoyallthe powers
and privileges,and be liableto all the duties and obliga-
tionsof a municipal corporation.
§ 2. That said cityhereby incorporatedis hereby in-
vested with all the lands , tenements , hereditaments , and
appurtenances, allthe goods,chattels, money ,revenues,
taxes of all kinds, judgments, rights, and demands , in
possession and expectancy ,accrued or to accrue ,of the
presentcityof Mayfield; and said cityshallbe bound by
all contracts, and liable for all debts and demands ac-
crued or to accrue ,in the same manner ,and to the same
extent , as said city was or might be bound or liable.

760 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . The cityof Mayfield is hereby dividedinto two
wards , as follows : The firstward shall include all that
part of saidcitywhich lieseastof Prairiestreet; and the
second ward shallinclude allthat part of said city which
lies west of Prairie street; and the board of councilmen
are hereby authorized to change the number and bound-
ariesof said wards as the public necessitiesshallrequire,
always endeavoring to equalize as nearly as possible the
population thereof.
§4. The officersof said city shallconsistof a city
judge , to be styled the judge of the Mayfield citycourt,
three councilmen from each ward , a citymarshal, city
attorney, city clerk,city assessor,city tax collector, city
treasurer, and such other officersas from time to time
may be found necessary to transact the business of the
city. The judge of the Mayfield citycourt,the city mar-
shal,citytreasurer,and cityattorney shallbe elected by
the qualifiedvoters of the city,and the three councilmen
from each ward by the votersof the ward in which they
may be candidates. All otherofficersshallbe appointed
by the board of councilmen , and be liableto dismissal at
the pleasure of the council.
ARTICLE II.
Board of Councilmen .
§1 . That all legislativepower and authority herein
granted and conferred shallvest in a body known as and
styledthe" Board of Councilmen of the City ofMayfield,
Kentucky ," which shallconsistof the judge of the May-
fieldcity court and three councilmen from each ward ;
and the said judge of the Mayfield city court shall be
chairman of the board .
§ 2. It shallbe the duty of the board of councilmen to
provide forthe holding of all elections authorized to be
held by this charter by appointing two judges , a clerk,
and sheriffof electionforeach ward in said city, each of
whom shalltake the oath prescribedby the Constitution
of Kentucky .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 761
§3 . The judge of the Mayfield citycourt and three
councilmen shallconstitute a quorum for the transaction
of any business; or, in the absence of the judge, four
councilmen shallconstitutea quorum ,but a smaller num-
ber may adjourn from day to day ,or time to time, and
may compel the attendance of absent members in such
manner and under such penalties as may be prescribed
by ordinance .
§ 4. No person shall be eligibleto the officeof coun-
cilman who is not a male citizen of the United States
and the State of Kentucky ,of good moral character,who
has not attained the age of twenty-four years, who has
not residedin the cityof Mayfield two years next pre-
ceding the day of his election,who is not a bona fideres-
ident of the ward from which he shall be chosen , or who
is interested, directlyor indirectly,in any contract with
saidcity,the terms,rates,or pricesthereofbeing subject.
to modification or enforcement by said board of council-
men ; and no person shall be eligibleto said officewho ,
at the time of his election, isindebted to said city,either
as principalor surety,upon any bond executed to said city,
or for taxes,or on any other account ; and no collectorof
cityfunds shall be eligible to re-election until he shall
have settledwith said cityand obtained a dischargein
full.
§ 5. Itshallbe the duty of the board of councilmen to
meet withinthreedays afteran electionisheld forjudge
of the Mayfield city court, marshal , attorney,treasurer,
councilmen,or any otherelectiveofficer,and canvass the
election returns for said officers, and inquire into the
qualificationsof the candidatesforallthe offices,and by
resolution declare the result of the election, and decide
who is entitledto the respectiveoffices; and if the board ,
aftera fairand impartialinvestigationof the facts,shall
decidethatany of the personsreceivingthe highestnum-
ber of votes is not possessed of all the qualifications
requiredherein,itshalldeclarethe electionof such per-
son void, and proceed to fillby appointment the vacancy
thus caused .

762 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§6 . The board of councilmen shallbe the solejudge
of the qualifications, election,and returns of the mem-
bers of the board , and try and determine all contested
elections .
7. If any member of the board of councilmen shall
remove from the city,or ifany councilman shallremove
out of the ward from which he was chosen ,or if, from
any cause whatever ,any member of the board of coun-
cilmen shall be deprived of or cease to possess any of
the qualificationsrequiredherein,such member shallfor-
feithis office,and his seat as a member of the house of
councilmen shallbe thereby declared vacant by the board ,
recitingthe cause of the forfeiture; but no such action
shall be had until after the member has had fivedays '
noticefrom the cityattorneyof the intendedproceeding.
§ 8. The board of councilmen shall have control of the
finances,and of all property, real and personal,of the
city; and shallhave fullpower and authorityto levy and
collecta revenue forthe cityof Mayfield; to appropriate
the money of the cityto itsimprovement and current
and necessaryexpenses; to provide for the payment of
itsdebts,and for the general protection,good order,and
