LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 771
ARTICLE III.
City Court .
§1 . First. That the judicialpower of the cityof May-
fieldshall be vested in and exercised by a court,to be
styledthe Mayfield CityCourt,which shallbe held by
one judge,to be styledthe Judge of the Mayfield City
Court , who shall be elected as herein elsewhere pro-
vided .
Second, Said citycourt shallhave exclusiveoriginal
jurisdictionto hear,try,and determine finallyallactions
or prosecutionsfor violationsof the ordinances of said
city; and shallhave concurrent originaljurisdictionwith
the Graves circuitcourt to hear ,try,and determine finally
all prosecutions for misdemeanors against the laws of
thisCommonwealth ,when committed within the corpo-
rate limits of said city, where the fine does not exceed
one hundred dollarsand the imprisonment does not ex-
ceed thirty days ; and shall have the same power and
authority to enforce its orders and judgments as the cir-
cuit court of the Commonwealth now have . The judge
of said citycourt shallhave jurisdictionto hold examin-
ing courts,and as such shallhave itspower ,and perform
the duties of two justices of the peace ; and for his ser-
vices in presiding as such in felony cases,shall receive
the same compensation as is now allowed by law to jus-
ticesof the peace for similar services,to be paid as such
justices' fees are now directedby law to be paid ; and he
is authorized to administer oaths in all cases where jus-
ticesof the peace,or a clerkof a court,are authorized
to do so .
Third. The saidcitycourt,or the judge thereof, shall
have concurrentoriginaljurisdictionwith justicesof the
peace in Graves county in all penal and criminal cases
of which said justices now have or may be given them
hereafter by law ; and shall have concurrent original
jurisdictionin all mattersof law and equity,withinthe
limits of Graves county , with justices of said county in
all action and proceeding for the recovery of money or
personal property,when the matter in controversy,ex-
:

772 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
clusiveof interestand cost,does not exceed two hundred
and fiftydollarsin value .
Fourth. The cityjudge of Mayfield shallhave author-
ityto issueand try writs of forcibleentryand detainer,
and take depositions; and for his services in taking dep .
ositions shall be entitled to the same fees as examiners
are now allowed by law . He shallhave power to grant
injunctions,restrainingorders,attachments ,and writs of
habeas corpus; for issuing and granting of any of which
he shallbe entitledto the same fees now allowed by law
for similar services .
Fifth. All proceeding in, and process from , said city
court, in all prosecutions for a violation of city ordi-
nances ,shallbe in the name of said city; and allprose-
cutions for misdemeanors against the laws of thisState
shallbe in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ;
but all fines, judgments , and forfeituresof said court,
whether forviolationsof city ordinances or laws of the
State,shall be for the use and benefit of said city, and
paidoverby the officercollectingthesame to thetreas-
urer of said city.

thejudge of theMayfieldcitycourtshallbe made return-
able to the city court, or before the judge thereof, and
may be directed to the city marshal ,sheriff, or any con-
stable of Graves county ,and in criminal and penal causes
to any policeman of said city.
Seventh. The judge of the city court shall have the
same power and authorityto enforce order and decorum
in said court, and compel the attendance of witnesses,
and punish for contempt , as the circuitjudge of this
Commonwealth .
Eighth . The citycourt shallbe holden at the cityhall
in said city,or at such other place as the board of coun-
cilmen may ,by ordinance ,designate.
Ninth. The citycourtshallor may be opened everyday
(Sundays excepted), and shall proceed summarily to try
all cases brought before it for breaches of the laws and
ordinances of the city, and allmisdemeanors of which it

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 773
isgiven jurisdictionherein, which may be prosecuted by

tledto demand one,itshallbe the duty of the judge to
order a jury, as hereinafterprovided for,to be summoned
forthwith by the city marshal or some qualified officerof
the county .
Tenth . For the trial of all causes in said court , both
civiland penal , where the amount in controversy does
not exceed two hundred and fiftydollarsin a civilcase,
and the punishment does not exceed a fine of one hun-
dred dollarsin a penal prosecutionor action, the jury
shallbe composed of six persons only, who shallbe paid
fiftycents each for theirservicesin each case,to be taxed
as cost against the defendant on conviction in a prosecu-
tion, and against the unsuccessful party in a civilaction
or proceeding .
Eleventh. In all prosecutions in said court, where the
punishment is imprisonment,the defendantmay demand
a jury; and where the punishment is by fine alone,the
defendant shallhave the rightto demand a jury where the
fine may exceed fiftydollars; and in civilactions where
the amount in controversy is over ten dollars,eitherparty
may demand a jury .
Twelfth. In allcivilproceedingsin saidcitycourt,where
the amount in controversy does not exceed fiftydollars,
the pleadings may be oral, and where the amount in con-
troversy is more than fiftydollars,the pleading shallbe
reduced to writing; but the court may ,for good cause ,on
motion of eitherparty, require the pleadings in any court
to be reduced to writing .
Thirteenth. The pleadings,practice,and mode of pro-
cedure in the Mayfield citycourt shallbe governed by the
Code of Practice in all civil,penal , and criminal cases,
except as herein provided. Nothing in this act shall
be construed so as to deprive a party defendant from
claiming the districtof his residence for the trialof a
civilcase,as now providedby law .
Fourteenth. The Mayfield citycourt shallbe a court of
record , and the records thereof, or certifiedcopies of the

facias,issuedon any judgment renderedin saidcitycourt,

against the choses in action or personal effectsof the

erty" authorizesan attachment in equity on judgment
rendered in other courts in this Commonwealth .

for offenses against the laws and ordinances of the city
and laws of the State, when , from his personal knowl-
edge , orfrom information of the cityor county attorney

na for,and compel the attendance of, any person before

Twenty -eighth. The regular terms of the Mayfield city
court for the trial of civil causes shall be held on the
second Monday of each month of the year , and may
continue from day to day until the business thereof is
disposed of.
ARTICLE IV .
Revenue and Taxation .
1
§1 . First. The board of councilmen shallhave power
and authority to assess,levy,and collect annually an ad
valorem tax,not exceeding one dollar on each one hun-
dred dollars'worth of property,upon allreal and personal
estate within the limits of said city,subject to taxation
for State and county purposes ,to be applied to general ,
local, and municipalpurposes. The board of council-
men may levy a specifictax on all goods brought to the
city to be peddled or sold at auction,and not otherwise
taxed . Said board shall also have power and authority
to levy, assess, and collecta poll-tax,not exceeding two
dollars,on each male inhabitant of said cityover twenty-
one years of age ; and allproperty shallbe listedfor tax-
ation as of the firstday of May of each year .
Second. The board of councilmen shallhave the power
to provide ,by ordinance , for the assessment and taxa-
tionof any store,or otherplace of business,where goods,
wares , and merchandise are sold or exchanged that may .
be opened or commenced , at any time subsequent to the
firstday of May in any year : Provided, That in no case
shall the tax assessed and levied be greater or less than
the ratableamount (as compared with otherassessments)
in proportion to the time the same may be kept open or
continued .

778 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§2 . First. The board of councilmen shall,as soon as
itsmembers have been qualified after theirelection,ap-
point an assessor,who shallhold his officeforone year;
and no person shallbe eligibleto said officewho is not,
at the time of his appointment,a qualifiedvoter of said
city,and has been a bonafideresidentand citizenthereof
for at least two years preceding his appointment . The
assessorshall, before entering upon the duties of his
office, take the same oaths of officerequired by law to
be taken by county assessors, and execute bond , with
good security,to the cityforthe faithfuldischargeof the
duties of his office,to be approved by the board of coun-
cilmen,and entered on recordby the cityclerkon the
journal of the city.
Second. He shall begin the duties of his officeon the
firstday of May each year ,and shallcomplete his listof
taxable property and polls, and return his books ,by the
thirdMonday in May of each year to the cityclerk. He
shalladministerto everyperson listingpropertythe fol-
lowing oath before proceeding to listthe property : "You
do swear that the listof taxable property now about to
be given in by you shallcontain a fulland complete list,
and the best description of the same you can give, of all
and every species of property belonging to you or in
your possession, subject to taxation on the firstday of
May of thisyear , and that no removal of property or
omission has been made ,or any method or device adopted
or practiced whereby to evade the payment of taxes by
you ,and that you willtrue and perfectanswers make to
such questions as may be asked you concerning your tax-
able property, or that of othersin your possession, or
listedby you for them ."
Third . The headings of the tax -book listshall be as fol-
lows ,subject to any addition or change by ordinance of
the board of councilmen ,viz : Name ; males over twen-
ty-one years of age ; number of hogs ; value of hogs ;
number of cattle; value of cattle; number of horses and
mares ; value of horses and mares ; number of mules ;
value of mules; number of studs,jacks,and bulls; value

コ
of studs,jacks,and bulls; number of dogs ; tax on dogs ;
number of wagons , carts, and drays; value of wagons ,
carts, and drays ; number of pleasure carriages, hacks ,
buggies , coaches , omnibuses ,or other vehiclesfor passen-
gers; value of pleasure carriages,hacks ,buggies ,coaches ,
omnibuses, or other vehiclesfor passengers; number of
watches and clocks ; value of watches and clocks ; num-
ber of pianos and other musical instruments ; value of
pianos and other musical instruments; value of gold and
silverplate and plated ware ; value of goods , wares , and
merchandise and stockin tradeon the firstday of May ;
value of bonds , stocks, mortgages , and so forth,not ex-
empt from taxation; value of household and kitchen
furniture; number of citylotsimproved and unimproved ;
value of citylotsimproved and unimproved ; value un-
der the equalizationlaw ; value of any other property
not already mentioned ; totalvalue .
Fourth . The assessor shall, from his own knowledge ,
from the statement of persons listingpropertyfortaxa-
tion, and from such other evidence as he may be able to
obtain, fixa fulland fairvalue upon allthe property
listed with him for taxation , and enter same , with the
value thereof, in the order and manner prescribed herein,
giving also the aggregate value . He shall return the
listof white and coloredpersons separately,but in the
same book .
Fifth. If any person shallfailor refuse to give a listof
his taxable property when legallycalledupon for that
purpose by the assessor,or give a falseor fraudulentlist,
he shallbe reported to the board of councilmen as a de-
linquent .
Sixth. The assessor shall,at the time he returns the
tax-book ,also reportthe names of alldelinquents; upon
the return of the delinquents the board of councilmen
shall assess the property of said delinquents from the
best information it can obtain, and cause the listthus
made to be entered on the tax-book , and the taxes col-
lected thereon as if given in by the person to the assessor;
and shallalso directthe cityattorneyto institutepro

780 LAWS OF KENTUCKY
ceedings,by warrant in the citycourt, against allof said
delinquents; and each of said delinquents,iffound guilty,
shall be fined not less than ten nor more than twenty
dollars; and in all trials under this section the defendant
may testifyin his own behalf. The assessorhaving com-
pleted his list, and made out the tax book in a fairlegi-
ble handwriting ,in alphabetical order,with the amount
of value of each column set down , and also the aggre-
gate thereof correctlygiven , shall return the tax-book to
the board of councilmen , with an indorsement that it
contains a true and fairestimate of allthe taxable prop-
erty in said city, and a correct listof all the taxable
inhabitants thereof .
Seventh. It shall be the duty of the board of council-
men to meet within three days after the return of the
tax-book by the assessor, and examine with care the list
in the tax -book ,and correct errors therein ,whether in fact
or in valuation of property, and indorse on the margin
of said book the changes , if any , made by them ; and
they shall enter on the tax-book any listthat may have
been omitted by the assessor; the board shall then direct
the cityclerk to prepare a notice for each person whose
listhas been increased in any way ,giving the time and
place, and where and when the board will meet for the
purpose of hearing complaint ,and reasons why the list
given by them should not be changed . The clerk shall
deliver said notices to the citymarshal ,to be served by
him on the persons mentioned thereinin the same man-
ner that notices are required by law to be served.
Eighth . After the board of councilmen shall have com-
pleted itsrevisionof the tax book ,itshallbe received by
a resolutionof the board , and be placed in the hands of
the cityclerktobe copied.
Tax -Collector.
§3 . First. The board of councilmen shall,during the
month of May ,or on or beforethe firstMonday in June,
appoint a citytax collectorfor the term of one year ,and
no person shallbe eligibleto the officeof citytax.col

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 781
lectorwho has not been a bona fide citizenand qualified.
voter of said cityfor at least two years prior to his ap-
pointment .
Second, Before entering upon the discharge of hisduties,
the said citytax -collectorshall take the oath prescribed
by the Constitution of Kentucky ,and execute bond to the
city of Mayfield , with good security,to be approved by
the board of councilmen of said city, conditioned that he
shall collect, account for,and pay into the citytreasury
all taxes and public dues directedor authorized by law
to be collected and received by him , and that he will, at
such time as he may be required by the board of council-
men ,report his acts and doings as collector, and account
for and pay into the citytreasury such sums as may be
in his hands at such times as may be prescribed by ordi-
nance , and that he will, when required by said board ,
make a settlement, and pay into the city treasury all
sums for which he may be bound , and that he will faith-
fullyand honestly discharge all his duties as citytax-col-
lector .
Third . It shall be the duty of the city tax-collector,
immediately upon the receiptof the tax-books, to pro-
ceed forthwith to the collection of the taxes then due ;
and , in order to do so,he shall have power and authority
to levy on and sell any personal property of any tax-
payer for the payment of the amount of taxes due from
him to the city,and he shallhave the same power to col-
lect taxes by garnishment as is now given to sheriffsof
thisCommonwealth by sectionsone ,two , three, and four,
articlenine,chapter ninety-two ,of the General Statutes.
The notice prescribed in section one ,as aforesaid, shall
summon the person owing the tax or taxes before the
Mayfield citycourt, and said court shall have the same
power to hear and fullydetermine any garnishment for
taxes due the city as the county court have to hear and
determine such cases for taxes due the Commonwealth .
The collector, before making a levy for the collectionof
taxes,shalldeliverto the tax payer a writtenor print-
ed notice of the amount of his unpaid taxes,and notice

782 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of tax due from a non -resident may be served upon his
agent or attorney, or by sending same through the post-
office, directed to the last known place of business or
residence of such non -resident; and the said citytax-col-
lectorshallhave power , in likemanner , to collectany
and alltaxesdue saidcitywhich may have been hereto-
foreleviedby the citycouncilof Mayfieldwhich are due
and unpaid .
Fourth. The city tax-collector,after he has seized the
propertyofany tax-payer,shalladvertisethesame forsale
foratleasttendays,by postinga noticethereofat at least
three of the most public places in said city, stating the
time and place of sale, and giving a description of the
property. He shallsellthe propertyat publicoutcryto
the highest bidder,for cash,and apply the proceeds to the
payment of the cost of sale and taxes.
Fifth. If therebe no personalproperty found in said
citywhich the collector can distrain for taxes due , and
the same shallnot be paid by the firstday of Septem-
ber, the collector may levy on any real estate belong-
ing to or listedby such delinquent tax-payer ,and sellso
much thereof, for cash, as willpay allthe taxes due and
his cost and commission ; but before sellingreal estate for
taxes , he shall advertise the same for the same time and
in the same manner required by law in sales of similar
property under execution,giving a general descriptionof
the property,the amount of the tax due ,the name of the
owner of the property,and the time,place,and terms of
sale. Such sales shallbe made on the firstday of some
county or circuitcourt for Graves county ,for cash , at the
court -house door ; and if no one will bid for such real es-
tate the amount of taxes due and cost of sale,itshallbe
the duty of the collectorto purchase the same for the city,
bidding therefor the amount of taxes due and cost of sale.
Sixth. It shallbe the duty of the collectorto give the
purchaser of any real estate fortaxes a certificateof pur-
chase,givinga descriptionof the land purchased and the
price paid for same ,which certificateshallbe recorded by
the Graves county court clerk.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 783
Seventh. The owner of such realestate,his agent ,heirs,
or assigns,shallhave the rightto redeem the same from
thepurchaserthereof,by paying thepurchasemoney ,with
interestthereon at the rate of ten per cent.per annum ,
and twenty per cent.damages ,at any time within two
years from the day of sale; any minor , married woman ,
or other person laboring under any disability,shallhave
two years afterthe removal of the disabilityin which to
make such redemption ; but the committee or next friend
of a person of unsound mind shallbe allowed only four
years from the day of sale,or two years afternotice in
writingby thepurchasertoredeemthesaine,thoughsuch
disabilityshouldcontinuelonger. Any person whose real
estatemay be sold by virtue of this act may redeem the
same withinthe time prescribedherein,by tenderingthe
purchase money , with interestand damages ,to the pur-
chaser or his agent ,or, if he or his agent refuse to accept
the same ,or reside out of the county , or is absent there-
from ,he may pay the amount to the county court clerk,
and take his receipttherefor, which shallbe a valid pay-
ment ; and itshallbe the duty of said clerk to receive the
payment and date thereof on the margin of the book
next to the place where the certificateof purchase isre-
corded .
Eighth . When the time for the redemption of any real
estate sold as hereinbefore provided shallhave expired,
the sale shallbecome finaland absolute,and the collector
making such sale,or his successor who may be in office
at the time ,shallexecute a deed to the purchaser thereof.
Ninth. Any person or personsfailingto pay theirtaxes
by the firstday of September , shall pay two per cent.
additionalupon the tax so due and unpaid,and the col-
lectorshallhave the same remedies against such delin-
quent tax-payer as providedby previoussectionsof this
act .
Tenth. Itshall be the duty of the tax-collectorto take
any tax -listhe may findhas been omitted by the assessor;
and he shall have the same power to collectthe taxes
thus listedby him by sale of property or otherwise, as
though the same had been listedby the assessor.

784 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Eleventh . The collector shall be allowed such commis-
sion for the collectionof the taxes of the cityas the board
of councilmen may by ordinance prescribe; and shallbe
allowed such fees for the service of notices of attach-
ment and distraint of property, and sales of property
for delinquent taxes, as are allowed sheriffsfor similar
services .
Twelfth. The collector shall, on the firstMonday in
October ,report to the board of councilmen a listof all
delinquent tax-payers whose taxes cannot be collected
by law ,the amount of which list,when passed upon and
allowed by the board of councilmen , shallbe placed as a
creditupon the amount due from said collector,at which
time he shallalsosettleand pay over to the citytreasurer
the balance due on the tax -books from him ; and for his
failureto make said payment at said time he and his
suretiesmay be sued in the Graves circuitcourt for any
balance which he may own , and ten per cent.damages
thereon , with interest from the date said balance become
due .
ARTICLE V.
City Marshal .
§ 1. First. The chief ministerialofficeof said cityshall
be styled the marshal of the cityof Mayfield ; he shall
be a peace officer, and ex officiochief of police; and shall
be vested with like power and authorityfor making ar-
restsas is now or may be hereafterconferredon sheriffs
of this Commonwealth .
Second. It shall be the duty of the marshal to attend
punctually on allsessionsof the citycourt and the board
of councilmen of said city; to execute all orders and
process of said court and said board which may be ad-
dressed to him; and to perform all other duties that may
be prescribed by ordinance or pertain to the office of
marshal ; and he shallbe entitledto charge the same fees
for his servicesin the citycourt as are now allowed by
law to sheriffs for a similar service .
Third . The marshal shallhave the same power as con-
stables to execute warrants and collect debts within the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 785
citylimits as constable; and be allowed to collectthe
same feesas constablesforsimilarservices; and he may
execute any process from the Graves circuit,quarterly,
and county courts,and courtsof justicesof the peace of
Graves county : Provided, He shallnot be compelled to
executeany such processbeyond the limitsof saidcity.
Fourth . The board of councilmen may ,by ordinance ,
fixand provide for other fees to be paid said marshal .
Fifth. The marshal shall enter upon the duties of his
office on the firstMonday in May succeeding his elec-
tion; but before doing so he shall execute a covenant to
the city of Mayfield, in the same form of the official
bonds of sheriffsof this Commonwealth , and take the
oaths requiredto be taken by sheriffsof thisCommon-
wealth ; and said bond shall be taken and approved by
theboard of councilmen,and spread upon the recordsof
the board , and the fact that said bond was approved
and the oaths taken by the marshal noted on the said
record .
Sixth. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof city
marshal who is not twenty -five years of age , and has
been a continuous residentand citizenof said cityfor at
leasttwo years prior to his election; nor shallany person
be eligibleto said officewho is indebted to said city on
any account whatever; nor shallthe citymarshal be eli-
gible to re-election until he shallhave settledwith said
cityfor all moneys and funds which may have come to
his hands ,and paid over the same to the treasurer,and
obtained a finalquietus therefor.
Seventh. It shallbe the duty of the city marshal to set-
tle with the board of councilmen every three months ,and
pay over to the treasurerall fines, moneys , and other
funds belonging to the city which he may have in his
hands .
Eighth. It shallbe the duty of the citymarshal espe
ciallyto suppressallfights,riots,breachesof thepeace,
and unlawful and disorderlyassemblies,and to apprehend
and take beforethe Mayfield citycourt allrioters,disor-
LOC . L.,VOL .II- 50

786 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
derlypersons,disturbersof the publicpeace in said city,
allpersons found drunk on the streetsor public thorough-
faresand placesin the city,allsuspectedfelonsand per-
sons fleeingfrom justice; and ,if necessary for that pur-
pose , he may summon and call to his assistance such
personsas he may need [to] enforcehisauthority; and he
shall forthwith take all persons arrested by him before
the city court for trial, except at night, when he shall
have the power to commit such partiesto the jailor the
citylock-up untilthe next morning ; and it shallbe the
duty of the jailerof Graves county , or the keeper of the
citylock-up,to receivesuch personsin theircustody,and
keep them untilthey may be releasedby due process of
law .
City Attorney.
§2 . First.It shallbe the duty of the cityattorneyto
attend to allsuits and prosecutions in behalf of the city
in which the cityisinterested,eitheras plaintiffor de-
fendant,and to perform such otherdutiesas may be pre-
scribedby ordinance of the board of councilmen .
Second. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof city
attorney who has not been ,for one year prior to his elec-
tion,a bonafide residentand citizenof saidcity,and for
one year a licensedand practicingattorney-at-law .
Third. He shalltake the oath of officerequiredby the
Constitution of the State of Kentucky , which shall be
noted of recordin the Mayfieldcitycourt,and receivefor
hisservicesthirtyper cent.of alljudgments forfinesand
forfeituresrendered by the Mayfield citycourt,and such
otherfeesas the board of councilmen may ,by ordinance,
prescribe.
Fourth. He shallenter upon the discharge of his duties
on the firstMonday in May after his election, and con-
tinue in officeuntilhis successor is elected and qualified.
City Treasurer.
§3 . First. It shallbe the duty of the city treasurer to
receiveallmoneys of the city, and to pay out and dis-
burse the same only upon warrants drawn by the clerk,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 787
and countersignedby the chairman of the board ofcoun-
cilmen, and afterthe claims have been allowed and
ordered paid by the board of councilmen .
Second. Before enteringupon the dischargeof the du-
tiesof his officehe shall execute bond , with good secu-
rity,to be approved by the board of councilmen,forthe
taithfuldischarge of his duties as treasurer, and that he
will pay over to the proper [person],when requiredby
the council to do so,allmoneys received by him as such
treasurer,and take the oath of officeprescribedby the
Constitutionof Kentucky ; said bond shallbe spread
upon the record of the board of councilmen , and its
approval noted thereon .
Third. He shallat alltimes keep a regular and correct
account of allreceiptsand disbursementsof the treas-
ury , which shallat alltimes be open to the inspection of
the board of councilmen ,or itsproper committees ; and
he shall report the amount of money in the treasury to
the board of councilmen every three months , and such
other times as the board may require, and at the close of
his term of officemake a complete and fullstatement of
the receiptsand expenditures of the preceding year , and
of the state and condition of the treasury and financial
status of the city. He shall perform such other duties
pertaining to his officeas the board of councilmen may
direct, and receive for his services such compensation as
may be fixedby ordinance .
Fourth. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof city
treasurerwho has not been a bona fideresident and citi-
zen of said city at leasttwo years prior to his election.
Fifth. The citytreasurershallenter upon the discharge
of the duties of his officeon the firstMonday in May
after his election , and continue in office until his suc-
cessor is electedand qualified. The citymarshal and
treasurer,together with their sureties,shall be made lia-
ble, by suit in the circuitcourt,for any sum found due
the city from them ,which they,or either,has failedto ac-
count forand pay over as directedby the citycouncilor
board of councilmen .

788 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
City Clerk .
§4 . First. The board of councilmen shall, at its first
meeting in May each year ,appoint a cityclerk,who shall
at once enter upon the discharge of the dutiesof his office,
and continue therein untilhis successor is appointed and
qualified; but before entering upon the duties of his
office,he shalltake an oath forthe faithfuldischarge and
performance of the duties of his office.
Second. Itshallbe the dutyof the cityclerkto preserve
the books ,papers ,records,and all documents belonging
to theoffice. He shallkeep a regularand correctjournal
of the proceedings of the board of councilmen ,and record
all the acts, resolutions,orders, by -laws ,and ordinances
passedby saidboard; furnishcertifiedcopiesof the same ,
and any otherrecordsof his office,when calledupon by
any person entitledto demand the same ; draw warrants
on the treasuryforallsums of money due to persons on
claims allowed by the board ; and when the assessor's
books have been revised and corrected,and the assess-
ment of taxes made for the year , he shall make a true
copy of said book , with the amount of taxes due from
each person set opposite his name ; and he shalldeliver
said copy ,together with the order fixing the rate of as-
sessments,to the citycollector,within thirtydays after
the passage of said order,and take his receiptforthe
same ,which shallbe entered of record on the journal;
and he shall perform such other duties as the board of
councilmen may prescribe by ordinance , and be allowed
such sums for his services as the board may deem just
and proper .
Third . All the records in the officeof said clerk, when
copiedand certifiedby him ,shallbe receivedas evidence
in any court in thisCommonwealth .
Fourth. The saidclerkshallhave the same power and
authority to administer oaths, and to give certificates
thereof, as county and circuitcourt clerks of this Com-
monwealth now have,and shallbe entitledto charge the
same fees as said clerk for similar services .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 789
Fifth. After it has been determined by the board of
councilmen who has been elected judge of the Mayfield
city court,city marshal , city treasurer,and cityattorney,
and entered of record upon the journal, the cityclerk
shall,in accordance therewith,give to each of said offi-
cers a certificateof his election; and also send to the
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky a certifi-
cate statingwho has been elected judge of the Mayfield
citycourt, who shall issue and forward to such judge a
commission as such withoutdelay.
ARTICLE VI .
Elections .
§1 . First. The firstelection under this charter shall be
held on the firstMonday in April,one thousand eighthun-
dred and eighty-four, at which time the three councilmen
from each ward ,the judge of the Mayfield citycourt,the
citymarshal ,the cityattorney,and the citytreasurer,shall
be elected; and all subsequent electionsfor the above-
named officersshallbe held on the firstMonday in April
of each year .
Second . All persons who are qualifiedvoters under the
Constitution of Kentucky and of the United States,and
who shallhave resided in the citysix months previous to
the day of the election and sixty days in the ward in
which they offer to vote, shall be entitled to vote for
councilmen ,judge of the Mayfield city court,city mar-
shal, cityattorney, citytreasurer, and allother officers
elected by the popular vote of said city,and on allother
questions submitted by the board of councilmen to the
qualifiedvoters of the citizensof Mayfield .
Third. The Board of councilmen shall,by ordinanceor
resolution, designate a voting place in each ward , at
which places allelectionsby the qualifiedvotersof the
city shallbe held,as provided in thischarter.
Fourth. Any person who isnot authorizedby thischar-
ter to vote in cityelections,who shallvote at any elec-
tion for any candidate for a cityoffice,or on any question
submitted to the qualifiedvoters of the city,shallbe fined

790 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
not lessthan twenty-fivedollarsnor more than fiftydol-
lars,or imprisonedin the jailof Graves county not less
than thirtynor more than fiftydays foreach offense,or
both so fined and imprisoned .
Fifth. Should the board of councilmen failto appoint
officersof election,or,when appointed ,should said offi-
cers failto attend for thirty minutes after the election
should have begun ,or,if attending,should failor refuseto
act,then the judge of the Mayfield citycourt shallappoint
such officersas arehereinrequiredto act; or,shouldsuch
officers,afterhaving been appointed,or any of them ,fail
or refuseto act,then the officeror officerswho have been
appointed and do attend may fillsuch vacancies ; and the
officersthus appointed shall constitutethe board of offi-
cers for such election, and proceed to hold the same .
Sixth. The board of councilmen shalldesignate by ordi-
nance the hours and manner of holding all city elections
and the returns thereof; and the officersholding such
electionsshallreturnthe poll-books.properlycertifiedby
them ,to the cityclerk,on the same day or the day after
the election is held .
Seventh. The vote at allcityelectionsshallbe cast viva
voce,but persons who are deaf and dumb shallbe per-
mitted to vote by ballot.
Eighth. When inthe cityelectionsprovidedforby this
charter,two or more candidates shall receive equal and
the highest number of votes forthe same office, the elec-
tion shallbe determined between them by lot.
ARTICLE VII .
General Provisions .
§ 1. The severalofficersnow holdingofficesin the city
of Mayfieldshallcontinueto hold theirrespectiveoffices
untilthe terms for which they were elected or appointed
shallhave expired,and theirsuccessorsare duly elected
and qualifiedas providedherein; and in the dischargeof
the dutiesof theirofficesshallbe governed by the provis-
ions of this charter from and after the day it goes into
effect .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 791
§2 . All general laws relatingto citiesand towns , and
not in conflictwith the provisionsof thischarter,shall
apply to the cityof Mayfield.
§3 . The marshal,clerk,treasurer,councilmen,and all
othercityofficers,are hereby prohibitedfrom buying,dis-
counting , or dealing in any way in vouchers or claims
againstthe city,under the penaltyof a fine of fiftydol-
lars for each offense .
§ 4.The board of councilmen may ,by ordinance ,change
the forms herein prescribed for the assessors' books ,if it
shall see proper to do so,so as to make the same more
effectualand convenientlyto exhibitthe variousvalues
subject to taxation, and so as to ascertainany other facts
it may deem necessary or desirable. The board of coun-
cilmen may change the time of assessing,levying,collect-
ing,and paying over the citytaxes,or either; and also
change the time of appointing the citytax-collector.
§ 5. This act shallbe a public act,and be judicially
noticed,and be liberallyconstrued by all courts in this
State; and in pleading itshallnot be necessaryto recite
the by -laws and ordinances of the city; but itshallonly
be necessary to refer to their date , title, and section.
And the factthat any person is a citizenof Mayfield shall
not render such person incompetent as a witness or juror
in any suitto which said citymay be a patry .
§ 6. The members of the board of councilmen shall
enter upon and begin the duties of their officeupon the
firstMonday in May aftertheirelectionof each year.
§7. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage ,and thereafterbe thecharterof thecityofMayfield.

ilar corporations .

dollarfor each share of stock subscribed for,in cash ,or

CHAPTER 1205 .

the various actsin regard to the town of Chester,in Mason county "

elected and qualified .
§3 . That the board of trustees of the town of Chester
shallhave power to levy and collecta tax upon the
property in saidtown ,not to exceed in any one year one
per centum on the assessedvalue,and to levyand collect
a poll-tax of one dollar on the male inhabitants of said
town over twenty -one years of age ; said tax to be ex-
pended on the streetsand alleysof said town ; said tax

798 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to be levied and collectedfor the year of one thousand
eighthundred and eighty-four.

who have resided in said town for six months preceding
the election at which they offerto vote are declared the
qualifiedvotersof Chester: Provided, That no person
shall be entitled to vote in such election who cannot
exhibit the tax-collector'sreceipt for all taxes assessed
against him under the provisions of thisact.
§ 5. All finesand forfeiturescollectedby the officersof
Chester for violationsof the city ordinances shall be for
the use and benefit of said town .
§6 . That the board of trustees of the town of Chester
have power to erect and operate a work -house in or near
said town ,and forthat purpose may purchase or lease the
necessary ground ,and erect suitablebuildings thereon for
the safe-keeping of prisoners incarcerated for a violation
of the ordinances of said town or any statute law com-
mitted within said town or within one -half mile of the
limitsthereof. The board of trusteesshallhave power to
appointa keeper and necessarywatchmen forsaidwork-
house ,and provide for theirpay .
§7 . All persons convicted of a violation of the ordi-
nances of said town before the mayor or a magistrate,
and adjudged to pay a fine, in defaultof payment shall
be confined in the work -house ,or such place of confine .
ment as the trusteesmay provide,and kept at hard labor,
as the trustees may direct,or may be worked upon the
streetsor alleysin the town of Chester,by the regulations
governing the said work -house ,and untilsaid fineispaid
in fullat the rateof one day'simprisonment foreach one
dollar fine and cost .
§ 8. When any drunken or disorderlyperson isarrested
by any peace officerwithin the town of Chester,or within
one -half mile of the limits thereof,for any violation of
the law or ordinances of said town , it shall be lawful
for such officerto confine said person or persons in such
place of confinement as the board may provide untilhe
can be brought before the mayor of Chester or some jus

tice of the peace to be dealt with according to law ; but
such person shallnot be so confined for a longer time

rest be Sunday ; then such person shallbe taken before
the court or magistrate by twelve o'clock,noon ,Monday .

clarevacant hisplace,and appoint hissuccessoruntilthe
next regular election.
§12 . This act shall not take effectuntil it has been
ratifiedand approved by a majorityofthelegallyqualified
voters voting at the next regular or at a special election
ordered for the consideration of this amendment ; and this
act shallbe printedin plaintype and postedin fivecon-
spicuous placesin the town of Chester,with the order of

pany shallbe citizensof the Stateof Kentucky ,and re-

pon bonds securedby it,which bonds shallbe negotiable

ed by resolutionof the board of directors;saidreceipts

are issued. Such storage receiptsor certificatesmay be

ered to the holders of the same upon their demand and

CHAPTER 1217 .
AN ACT to amend an act to incorporatethe Cincinnati, Green River,and
Nashville Railroad Company ," approved February twenty-third, one
thousand eighthundred and eighty-two .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the firstsection of an act, entitled" An act
to incorporate Cincinnati, Green River, and Nashville
Railroad Company ," approved February twenty-third,
eighteen hundred and eighty-two ,be amended as follows,
viz: That saidcompany shallhave power to build,equip,
operate, and maintain a railroad, with single or double
track,with all necessary turnouts, side-tracks, switches ,
and depots ,from a point on the Tennessee line, in the
counties of Allen,Monroe ,Cumberland ,or Clinton;thence
through any one or allof said counties, and through any
ofthe followingcounties,viz: Barren, Metcalfe,Adair,
Green , Taylor, Russell, Casey, Marion , Lincoln, Boyle,
Garrard ,Jessamine , Madison , and Clark, so as to connect
with the Kentucky CentralRailroad, or any other rail-
road in said counties; and the said company shallhave
the right to build branch or branches from its said rail-
road through any of the said counties,and through any
of the following counties,viz : Washington ,Nelson ,Spen-
cer,Bullitt, and Jefferson to the city of Louisville; and
may ,by and with the consentof the mayor and common
council,extend theirroad into the city of Louisville.
§2 . That section second of said act be amended as
follows: That the affairsof said company shall be man-
aged by a board of nine directors, who shall be elected
annuallyby the stockholders; thatsaidboard of directors
shallelectone of theirnumber president,and may elector
appoint a secretary and treasurer, and such other officers
and agents and servants as they may deem necessary and
proper,and may take from them such bond,with such
surety,as they may deem proper. No person shallhold
the officeof directoror presidentunlesshe be a stock-
holder .
§4 . That section four of said act be repealed, and the
following be inserted and substituted in lieu thereof :

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 819
That when any lands, right of way ,stone,earth,gravel,
timber,or material necessaryforthe use and purpose of
constructing said road or branches , cannot ,for want of
agreement with the owner ,or for other cause,be pur-
chased or obtained, the same may be condemned and
taken by said company ,as provided by an act,entitled
" An act to prescribe the mode of condemning lands for
the use of railroad and turnpike companies ," approved
April the eleventh,one thousand eight hundred and eigh-
ty-two . The presentorganizationof saidcompany shall
be deemed legaland valid,and the presentboard of di-
rectorsshallhave the rightto provide the manner and
terms of subscribingto the capitalstock of said company .
§ 5. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 5, 1884.

urer shall be the custodian,upon his officialbond , of all

mission of the districtsof the county which vote upon

ified .

CHAPTER 1224 .

tieswhetheror notthey willtaxdogs,therevenuederivedtherefromto
go to pay forsheep killedby dogs .

of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itis hereby made the duty of the assessors.
of the countiesofBoyd and Lawrence ,in each and every
year ,to take from every person assessed by them for tax-
ation a listshowing the number of dogs owned by such
person or under hisor her control,or in hisor her posses-
sion,or upon his or her premises, and the color or sex of
same .
§ 2. Every personowning,controlling,orhavingsame
on his or her premises ,in said counties,shalllistthe same
with the assessorsof said counties each year ; in his list
he shall statethe number of dogs so owned or controlled

832 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
by him or her,or thathe or she has possessionof,or that
staysupon hisor her premises,the colorand sex of same ,
and shallmake oath to same . Any person violatingthis
sectionshall be fined in any sum not less than ten nor
more than twenty dollars,which finemay be recovered,
either by warrant before a justice of the peace of said
counties,or by indictment in the circuitor criminal court
of said counties .
§ 3. Upon every dog owned or controlledor in pos-
sessionof, or on thepremises of,a party in said county,
such party shallpay an annual tax as follows: upon each
male dog ,the sum of fiftycents; and upon each female
dog ,the sum of one dollar and fiftycents,which tax shall
be paid to the same officer, and at the same time the
county levy tax of said countiesispaid: Provided,how-
ever, Not more than one tax shall be collectedupon any
one dog for each year .
§ 4. Whoever keeps a dog in the counties aforesaid,
upon which the tax herein provided forhas not been paid ,
shallbe deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon con-
viction shall be fined not less than five dollars nor more
than ten dollars,to be recovered by warrant before a jus-
ticeof the peace of said counties.

said countiesupon which the tax has not been paid;
and for killing same ,he shall be allowed out of the funds
by thisact twenty-fivecents, upon proper proof of the
correctness of his claim; the same proof being required
as in case wherein sheep has been killed and paid for.
There isno pay allowed forthekillingof young pups or
dogs before they are old enough to killsheep .
§ 6. The taxes, and allfinescollectedunder the provis-
ions of this act,shall constitutea sheep fund , and when
collected,shallbe paid intothe hands of the treasurer,or
any one acting as such ,of the said counties , who shall
keep a separateaccount of the same ,and shalldispose
of itas here and afterprovided.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 833

law fora failureto collector pay over the county levy;

same as he receivesfor collectingcounty levy. When

jure the same ; and may recover for such injury any
amount which the court or jury tryingsame , from the
evidence,believehe isentitledto recover; and any per-
son stealingsuch dog shallbe deemed guiltyof petit
larceny .

aging or killing of his sheep by dogs ,shall make out a

the number of sheep killed or damaged ,and the value

such,on orbefore the firstday of January followingthe
year in which the sheep was killedor injured,and shall
be preserved by the treasurer,and the statement and affi-
davitsfiledpresentedtothe justiceof thepeace,as inthis
sectionprovided,shallbe kept by saidjusticein hisoffice.
The justiceof the peace shallbe paid by the partypre-
senting the statements and affidavitstwenty-five (25)
eents forthe certificategiven to such party .
§ 10. The treasurer,or one acting as such,of said coun-
ties,shallreceivethe taxes herein provided for,and all

rectaccount of the same ; and on or before the fifteenth

ment showing how much money has come to his hands

12. All fines and forfeituresunder this act shall be

insaidcountiesin which thereisnow collectedby law a

§ 14.All actsand partsof actsprovidinga tax on dogs

jorityof the legallyqualifiedvoters of the respective

CHAPTER 1226 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Wingo High School Company , locatedat Winge ,
inGraves county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. M. Andrew ,W. T. Plumlee,R. B. Wright,
Ben .J. Wesson ,and Henry George ,and their associates,
successors, and assigns, be, and are hereby,declared a
body -politicand corporate ,under the name and styleof
the Wingo High School Company ; and by that name
shall have perpetual succession; may contractand be
contractedwith,sue and be sued,plead and be impleaded,
in all courts and places; have a common seal,and alter
or amend same at pleasure; may make all necessary by-
laws for the transactionof the business and government
of said company , not inconsistentwith laws of the State
of Kentucky or United States.
§2 . The capitalstock of said company shallnot be less
than fifteenhundred nor more than fivethousand dollars,
to be divided intoshares of twenty -fivedollars,subscribed
and paid forin such a manner as saidcompany may ,by
its by -laws ,prescribe.
§3 . The stockholders of said company shall annually
elect a board of five directors, no two of whom shallbe
of the same religiousdenomination ; one of whom shall
act as president,who shallhold theirofficeforone year,
or until their successors are duly elected. Said election
shall be held annually the firstSaturday in July. Each
stockholder shall be entitled to one vote for each share of
stock he or she may own ,eitherby person or proxy.
§4 . Said election shall be held at the school-house .
All voting shallbe by ballot. The individualreceiving

838 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the highest number of votes cast shallbe declared elected
to the officefor which he was voted for. The secretary
shallsuperintend and hold each election,and make known
the result .
§5 . Any vacancy that may occur in any of the offices
by death ,resignation,removal ,or refusal to act,shallbe
filledby the remaining directorsfor the unexpired time.
§ 6. The board of directorsmay receive donations of
money or property for the purpose of assistingin the
erection of school buildings,forthe purchase of libraries,
chemical apparatus,for the employment of teachers,or
for other purposes connected with said school; which
said sums so donated ,subscribed, and paid, shallbe used
by said directorsfor the benefit of said school in such
a manner as a majorityof the directorsmay thinkbest.
§7 . They may purchase lands as directedby the com-
pany ,and superintend the same for the advancement of
education ; shallhave fullcontrol of and management of
the school,and allpropertytheretobelonging.
§ 8. Shallbe theduty of the presidentto presideat all
meetingsof the directory,and perform such otherduties
as may be assignedby the by -laws of saidcompany . It
shallbe theduty of the secretarytokeep a faithfulrecord
of alltheproceedingshad and performed,such otherduties
as may be requiredof him by the by -laws. Itshallbe
the duty of the treasurer to collectall money connected
with saidschool,and pay same out by orderof directors,
signedby the president; he shallalsobe requiredto give
sufficientbond forthe faithfulperformance of his duty .
§ 9. A majorityof the directorsin officeshallat all
times constitutea quorum ,and may meet at such places
as they may selectfor the transacting of business.
§ 10.The saidcompany shallcause a book tobe opened ,
subjectto theinspectionof any member of the company ,
which shall contain the names of all stockholders of said
company and the number of sharesowned by each; and
the said shares may be transferredon said book in the
manner to be prescribedby theby -laws of saidcompany .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 839
§11 . The individualpropertyof thestockholdersshall
not be subjectto the liabilitiesof the corporation.
§12. This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 1, 1884 .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That that portionof Jessamine county embraced

mouth of Big Hickman creek; thence with saidturnpike
road to county road number three(known as Hill'sMill
Road); thencewithsaidroad to Nicholasvilleand Paint

thousand dollarsto the capital stock of the Sugar Creek
and Watts' MillTurnpike Road Company .

CHAPTER 1230 .
AN ACT to prevent stock running at largein Harrison county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. Any person being the owner of stock,or having
the custody or controlthereof,who shall sufferor permit
the same to run at large,or to be outside of his or her
inclosure,or premises under his or her charge ,shall be
fined in any amount not less than one dollar nor more
than twenty dollars, to be recoveredby warrant in the
name of the Commonwealth before any justiceof the
peace of Harrison county : Provided, The owner or person
having the custody or controlof said stock had notice
that it was so at large .
§2 . Any person whose premises or property may be
injuredby stockof another while at large,shallhave a
right of action against any owner of such stock, not-
withstandingthe premises or property so injured shall
not be inclosedby a lawful fence as now required by law ,
forany damages he may have sustainedfrom saidstock;
and shallalsohave a lienupon saidstockso trespassing
to satisfyany judgment he may recover.
§3 . Any person findingany stockon his premises,or
on premisesunder hiscontrol,shallhave the rightto take
up the same ,and hold them as estraysare now by law
taken up and held.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 851
§ 4. Whenever any stock of any kind shall be found
running at largeon any of the publicroads or streetsor
alleysin saidcounty of Harrison,or on the premises of
another than the owner or custodian of said stock,with-
out his consent ,it shall be the duty of the sheriffor his
deputy,or constableof said county,to seizeupon and
impound such stock,and provideforitskeeping untilthe
owner or custodianthereofshallredeem it,by paying all
fees,costs,and properchargesincidentto such procedure.
§5 . The feesof an officerforperformingthe services
mentioned in the preceding section shallbe fiftycents for
each head of stocktaken up and impounded by him ,and
all reasonablecharges forkeeping the same ,to be paid
by the owner before he shallbe entitledto the possession
thereof .
§ 6. The officermaking the seizure shall, ifpracticable,
within two days thereafter,give notice to the owner of
the stock he has so taken up ,together with a statement
of the costs and charges ; and if said stock is not re-
claimed by the owner within five days from said notice,
the officerhaving the custody thereof shalladvertise the
same at two or more public places in the magisterialdis-
trictin which said seizurewas made by posting notices;
and unless said stock shall be reclaimed by the owner
within five days after the posting of said notices, said
officershallexpose saidstockto saleat publicauctionat
some public place in said magisterial districtmost con-
venient to the owner . Said sale shall be made on a
credit of three months . The officermaking the sale shall
take bond , with good surety, for the price, payable to
himself; which bond shallhave the forceof a judgment ;
and , after paying allcosts and charges incident to said

year ,for the trialof civiland penal cases,under the gen-

shalof the town of Home Valley,in Muhlenburg county,

CHAPTER 1232 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors,or within
one mile thereof,in the town of Slaughtersville,in Webster county.
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any person to sellor
barter,or sufferit sold or bartered at his instance,any
spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors,or any mixturethere-
of, in the town of Slaughtersville, or within one mile
thereof from the corporate limits of said town ,in Web-

county line; and any person so offending shall, on con-
viction,be subjectto a fineof not lessthan fiftynor more
than one hundred dollars for each offense,to be recov-
ered eitherby indictment in the Webster circuitcourt, or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 857
by warrant in the name of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky in the policejudge'scourt of Slaughtersville, or
by warrant before any justice of the peace for Webster
county . One -half of the fines collected under the pro-
visions of this act shall be paid to the trustee of the
Slaughtersvillecommon school district, and be applied in
aid of common schools therein ; the other half shall be
paid to the trusteeof the jury fund of Webster county.
§2 . Itshallbe unlawful for the trusteesof Slaughters-
villeto issueany licenseforthe saleof spirituous,vinous,
or malt liquorsin said town ,and any licenseso issued
shall be void .
§3 . Before the provisions of the foregoing sections
shall be in force , an election shall be held in the town of
Slaughtersvilleto take the sense of the legal voters of
said town and boundary which is embraced in this act,
which boundary shallbe settledbefore the vote is taken ,
and shall extend one mile on an air-line trom the corpo-
rate limitsof said town , but not to run into Hopkins
county , on the question as to whether spirituous,vinous ,
or malt liquors shall be sold in said town ; said election
shallbe advertisedeitherby writtenor printednotices,
posted up in at leastfiveof the most publicplaces in
said town for at leastten days before said election,which
shall be held on the firstMonday in June ,one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-four,between the hours of seven ,
ante meridian, and kept open untilsix, post meridian.
The officersto hold said election shall be the same that
hold the electionsfor police judge , marshal ,and trustees
for the town of Slaughtersville, In one column of the
poll-book at said election shall be entered the names of
those in favor of the sale of spirituous, vinous , or malt
liquors, and in the other column shall be entered the
names of those against the sale of spirituous,vinous ,or
malt liquors. Those voting against the sale of spirituous,
vinous ,or malt liquorsshallbe construed and counted as
voting in favor of thisact. The officersconducting said
election shallcertifythe result,and return the poll-book ,
as now provided by law forthe returnof pollbooks for

858 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the town of Slaughtersville, and thereupon, or within

votingat saidelectionare againstthe sale of spirituous
vinous ,or malt liquors,they shall make a certificateof
the fact showing the vote as given ,which shall be entered
of record in the order-book of the Webster county court,
and the provisions of this act shallthen be in full force
as herein provided . The Webster county court shallissue
no license for the sale of spirituous, vinous, or malt
liquorsin the town of Slaughtersville,or within one mile
of the corporate limits of said town : Provided, This act
shall not be construed so as to interferewith any license
which may be in force at the time of thiselection.
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 1, 1884 .

zens of voting districtnumber nine,in Hart county ,shall

shall act .

pointed to hold said election in the same manner as is

§9 . The county judge shall fillall vacancies in the

riesas he may think just and proper , but not more than

urer,who shallreceive,in addition to the amount allowed

dred and seventy.

ville shallhave no rightto make any charge against,or

ing in saidcourt in which said cityis a partyor has an
interest; and in lieuthereof,the clerkof the said citycourt
of Louisvilleshallhereafterbe paid by the cityof Louis-
villethe sum of one thousand dollarsperannum ,payable
quarterly,as fullcompensation forallfees and costsfor
services rendered by the clerk of said court to the city of
Louisville ; that the clerk of said court shall be entitled
to have ,charge ,and receive from the defendant ,as costs
only , on conviction, fees not exceeding two dollars in
each suit,action, or proceeding in said court; that other-
wise than as providedby thisact,the clerkof saidcourt
shall be entitledto have ,charge ,and receivethe same fees
as are now allowed by law to the clerksof the circuitand
county courts of thisCommonwealth for similarservices.
The clerk of said court shall furnish his own deputies,
and allbooks and stationeryfor his office,at his own ex-
pense .
§2 .That hereafterthe marshal of the citycourt of Lou-
isvilleshallhave no rightto make any charge against,or
collection from, the cityof Louisvillefor any services
rendered by him as marshal in any suit,action,or pro-
ceeding in saidcourtin which saidcityisa party or has
an interest; and in lieuthereof,the marshal of said city
court of Louisvilleshallhereafterbe paid by the cityof

876 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Louisvillethe sum of one thousand dollars per annum ,
payable quarterly,as fullcompensation for all fees and
costs for services rendered by the marshal of said court
to the city of Louisville; that the marshal of said court
shallbe entitledto have ,charge ,and receive from the de-
fendant,as costs only, on conviction,fees not exceeding
two dollars in each suit, action, or proceeding in said
court; that otherwisethan as provided by thisact, the
marshal of said court shall be entitledto have , charge ,
and receive the same fees as are now allowed by law to
sheriffs of this Commonwealth for similar services . The
marshal of said court shallfurnish his own deputies,and
allbooks and stationeryforhisoffice,at hisown expense.
§3 . That the prosecuting attorney of the city court of
Louisvilleshallbe entitledto have ,charge ,and receive,as
compensation for his services,thirtyper cent.of allfines
and forfeiturescollected in the city court of Louisville
during his term of office,either made to the cityor the
Commonwealth ,which shallbe in lieuof allsalaryand
fees of any kind .
§4 . That when a suit,action,or proceeding in the city
court of Louisvilleisdismissed,for the reason the defend-
ant was wrongfully arrested,or the prosecuting witness
failsto appear and testify, the court may dismiss such
case at the costs of the prosecuting witness, and such
witnessbe requiredtopay the costs,and,forthatpurpose,
may be proceeded againstby the impositionof a finenot
exceeding fivedollars, or as in cases of contempt .

the jurisdictionit now has by law ,shall have exclusive
jurisdictionof all prosecutions or proceedings for the
punishment of the keepers of all bawdy or other dis-
orderly houses within the limitsof the city of Louisville.

of the citycourt of Louisville, the marshal of the city
courtof Louisville,and prosecutingattorneyof the city
courtof Louisville,shallnot take effector go intoopera-
tion untilafterthe expiration of the term of officeof the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 877
presentclerk, marshal, and prosecutingattorneyof the
city court of Louisville.
§7 . That the fees and emoluments of the present clerk
and marshal of the citycourt of Louisville shall be and
remain as now allowed by law during theirpresentterm
of office, except that the claims of said officersagainst
the city of Louisville for fees or for services shall not
exceed the amount of money collected by the city of
Louisvillefrom the citycourt of Louisville,or paid into
the city treasury as the proceeds of fines or forfeitures
imposed or adjudged by the said citycourt of Louisville;
the said cityin no event to pay said clerk and marshal
more than is received by the said cityfrom the fines and
forfeitureshad in said court during theirpresent term of
office .
§ 8. That all acts or parts of acts in conflictwith this
act are hereby repealed.
§ 9. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after itspassage .
Approved May 8 , 1884 .

CHAPTER 1255 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Cynthiana and Unity Turnpike Road Com-
pany .
WHEREAS , The Cynthiana and Raven's Creek Turnpike ,
in Harrison county , was sold by an order of the Harrison
circuitcourt,which sale was authorized by an act of the
General Assembly of Kentucky ; and whereas,the same ,
with allof itsrightsand franchises,was purchased by A.
D. Moore , E. D. Cason , John Shawhan , C. Walton , A.
Killer, Thos .B. Ashbrook , and Samuel J. Ashbrook ; and
whereas , itisrepresented that said road isnow owned by
Samuel J. Ashbrook , Benjamin F. Martin ,and the estate
of Thomas V. Ashbrook; and whereas, itis represented
that they desire to have an act authorizing a corporation
to be formed,with power to purchase and operate said
road ; wherefore , for remedy thereof,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth.
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Samuel J. Ashbrook, Benjamin F. Martin,
Artemesia Ashbrook ,Felix Ashbrook ,jr., Earl Ashbrook ,
SallieAshbrook,and Mary Ashbrook,and theirsuccess-
ors, be , and they are hereby , created a body -corporate
forthe purpose of purchasing and operating as such that
certainturnpikeroad in Harrison county,Kentucky ,ex-
tending from the west end of the bridge over South Lick-
ing river,at Cynthiana ,in a northwestern direction be-
yond Unity,in said county,about seven miles,being the
road in said county known as the Cynthiana and Raven
Creek Turnpike Road . Said corporation shallhave per-
petual succession; and as such may contract and be con-
tracted with,sue and be sued ,and have all the powers
necessary to the purpose for which formed which could
be exercisedby naturalpersons. Itmay have a seal,but
until one is adopted may execute its corporate powers
under the corporatename ,attestedby the president, or
such other person as the board may authorize.
§2 . The capital stock of said company shall be the
sum of fourteen thousand dollars,divided into shares of
one hundred dollarseach; and the company may organ

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 879
izeand electofficers,and operate said road,as soon as
one half of said stock is subscribed .
§ 3. The officersof said road shallconsistof a presi-
dent and a board of two managers. The presidentand
one manager may constitute a quorum for the transac-
tion of business; but all officialacts shall be supported
by the vote of at least two of these officers. This board
may electa secretary and treasurer, and may ,if they so
desire, requirehim to give bond for the faithfuldischarge
of his duties as such . The president and board of man-
agers shallbe elected annually ; notice of the time and
place of which electionshallbe as now providedby law
forthe electionof officersof corporations.
§ 4. Said company ,when so formed ,shallhave power
to operate, keep up , and maintain said turnpike road ;
and to that end is hereby endowed with allthe powers ,
and made subjectto allthe pains and penalties,which,
by law ,attach to turnpikeroads in thisCommonwealth .
Itmay collecttollat the gates on said road as now au-
thorized by chapter one hundred and ten of the General
Statutes; and may condemn all land and material nec-
essary for itsuse as authorized by an act approved elev-
enth day of April, one thousand eight hundred and eigh-
ty-two ,and may exercise allother powers granted by the
general laws of thisState to other turnpike roads,and
which are necessaryto enable said company to operate
and maintain said road .
§ 5. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage..
Approved May 3 , 1884.

CHAPTER 1256 .
AN ACT toincorporatethetown of Newfoundland, inElliottcounty.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That thetown of Newfoundland ,in Elliottcounty,
be ,and thesame ishereby,incorporated,and thecorpo-
rate limitsof same shallbe as follows: Running so as to
include within said town the lands of James S. Gray ,.

880 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
William Dougherty,John B. Lemaster,Martin Flannery,
R. M. Bowling , and I.N. King ; and said town shallbe a
body-politic,under the name and styleof Newfoundland ,
with powers to contract and be contracted with,sue and
be sued in such name .

man ,or two of them ,are hereby appointedjudges of the
firstelectionfor officersof said town , who , after being
firstduly sworn fairlyand impartiallyto dischargetheir
duties, shall, on the tenth day of May , one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-four,after giving three days '
notice,by written advertisement,posted at three public
places in said town ,proceedto hold an electionforfive
trustees, a police judge , and town marshal : Provided, If
said electionis not then held,the same may be held at
any time during the year one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four .
§3 . The electionsforofficersshalltake place annually
on the firstSaturday in May , except for policejudge,
who shallhold his officefor two years.

returned to the county court of Elliottcounty at its first
term after an election for such officer; and said court
shall certifyto the Governor the resultof the election,
and the Governor shallissue a commission for the person
elected to said office.
§ 5. The police judge ,before entering on the duties of
his office,shallexecutebond and take an oath prescribed
by law to faithfullyand impartiallydischarge his duties
as such officer; the oath may be administeredby any one
authorized to administer oaths in Elliottcounty , and ex-
ecute bond beforethe county judge of Elliottcounty.
The saidpolicejudge shallhave jurisdictionwithinthe
corporatelimitsof saidtown to the same extentjustices
of thepeace of Elliottcounty now have or may hereafter
have,and shallhave the same jurisdictionof criminal
and penalcaseswhich justicesof the peace have,and
may hold courts of inquiry. Said policejudge shallhave
fulljurisdictionof all offenses arising under the ordi

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 881
mances and by -laws of said town ; he shallbe entitledto
same feesas justicesof the peace are entitledto forsim-
ikarservices,and is authorized to collect same in same
manner as are justicesof the peace .
§ 6. Appeals from alljudgments renderedby saidpolice
judge in civilcases shallbe allowed to any party under
the same rulesand regulations,and to the same tribunals,
as appeals are allowed from justicesof the peace in sim-
ilar cases .
§7 . The marshal of said town shall, within itslimits,
have the same powers,and shallperform thesame duties,
and be liableto the same penalties,that constables are
now by law or may hereafter be required to perform or be
subject to; he shall,in addition,perform such duties as
may reasonablybe imposed upon him by the trusteesof
said town . Said marshal ,before entering on his duties
of office, shallgive bond with good security,conditioned
for the faithfulperformance of his duties as such officer,
and shall take an oath before some one authorized to ad-
minister oaths in Elliottcounty that he willfaithfullydis-
charge the duties of said office.
§8 . The trustees of the town of Newfoundland shall
have same powers ,and theirduties shall be the same , as
there prescribedby articlethreeand fourof chapter one
hundred and seven of the General Statutes .
§ 9. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 3, 1884.

fork to the forks of Sandy river,and then down Big Sandy
river to the beginning . That the real estate and farming
lands within said boundary shall not be subjectto taxa-
tionfortown purposes unlessitshallhave been laidoff
into town lots .

CHAPTER 1258 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Danville and Houstonville Turn-
pike Road Company .
WHEREAS . The Danville and Houstonville Turnpike
Road is twelve and three-quarter miles in length from
itsbeginning to itsterminus; and whereas,by the pro-
visionsof is charter, which was approved March first,
eighteen hundred and forty-four, and an amendment
thereof,approved February twenty-fourth ,eighteen hun-
dred and seventy , and in consideration of said corpora-
tion having built a bridge across the Knob Lick creek,
the said Danville and Houstonville Turnpike Road Com-
pany has the right to three fullgates on its road ; and
whereas ,there is some doubt as to the true meaning and
intent of the amendment approved February twenty-
fourth,eighteen hundred and seventy ; and whereas ,there
is some doubt as to the rights,powers , and duties of said
corporation under its charter and amendments ; now ,in
order to remedy the evilsaforesaid,
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That said turnpike road be divided into three sec-
tions of four and one -quarter miles each ,and that said
corporation is empowered and authorized to collectand
receive of and from alland every person or persons using
said road,at each tollgate,foreach and every four and
one -quarter miles they may use or travel on said road ,the
toll and rates mentioned and prescribed in the charter,
and that the rates mentioned and prescribed in the char-
ter shall be understood and construed as referringto each
and every four and one -quarter miles of travel any person
may use or travel on said turnpike road .
§ 2. All persons passing to and from mill on horseback ,
with corn or wheat to be ground for family use ,and not
for sale to others, shallbe exempt from toll; but those
going to or returning from mill in any other mode shall
pay the usual ratesof toll.
§3 .Any clauseor provisionin the charterof saidturn-
pike road company ,or in any amendment thereof, which

834 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
isin conflictwith any of the provisionsof thisact,ishere-
by repealed.
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 3 , 1884.
CHAPTER 1259 .
AN ACT for the benefitof G. J. Cundiff, late tax-collectorfor Taylor
county .
WHEREAS , G. J. Cundiff was tax-collector for Taylor
county for the years one thousand eight hundred and
seventy -seven and one thousand eight hundred and eigh-
ty-one ; and whereas ,said Cundiff has paid over to the
Auditor of Public Accounts all taxes due the State from
said county for the years one thousand eight hundred
and seventy -seven and one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-one , and holds quietus evidencing same ; and
whereas , said G. J. Cundiff has settled with the county
court for all taxes due the county for said years ; and
whereas ,there are considerable sums of said taxes un-
paid by the individuals owing the same for said years ;
therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That G. J. Cundiff be , and he is hereby ,granted
the furthertime of two years,from the passage of this
act, in which to collectthe unpaid taxes of Taylor
county for the years one thousand eight hundred and
seventy-seven and one thousand eight hundred and eigh-
ty-one , with power to distrain therefor; and said G. J.
Cundiffshallhave power to appointa deputy,who shall
have the same powers that are conferredon said Cundiff
by thisact; but said Cundiffshallbe responsiblefor the
wrongful acts of said deputy .
§ 2. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 3, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1260 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Bank of Williamsburg .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there ishereby establisheda bank of deposit
in the town of Williamsburg ,with a capital stock of fifty
thousand dollars,in shares of one hundred dollars each ,
to be subscribed and paid for as hereinafterprovided for;
and the subscribers, their associates,successors,and as-
signs shallbe a body -politicand corporate,by the name
and styleof the Bank of Williamsburg , and shall so con-
tinue for twenty -five years from and afterthe passage of
this act; and shall be capable of contracting and being
contracted with , of suing and being sued , of impleading
and being impleaded , of answering and being answered ,
of defending and being defended ,in allcourts and places
whatever ; they may have a common seal,and alterand
change the same at pleasure ; they shall have power to
receive gold and silverand bank notes,and other circula-
tion or currency or deposits, and pay out and loan the
same ; to buy and sell drafts, notes, billsof exchange ,
bonds , and stocks; and all notes made payable at said
bank , and all notes or billsdiscounted or purchased by
said bank , shall be , and they are hereby ,put upon the
footing of foreign bills of exchange , and like remedy
may be had thereon , jointly and severally, against the
drawers and indorsers, and with like effect,except as to
damages ; and they may allow interest on deposits, and
allow depositors to share in the profitsin said bank ,in
such manner and on such terms as may be prescribed by
the by -laws of said bank .
§2 . The said corporationmay acquire,hold,and pos-
sess, use, occupy , and enjoy all such real estate, goods
and chattels, as shall be necessary for the transaction of
itsbusiness,or which may be conveyed to it as surety for
any debt,or purchased in satisfactionof any debt,judg-
ment , or decree,and selland convey the same ; and they
may make such by -laws fortheirgovernment as theymay
deem necessary: Provided, The same are not contrary to

886 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the Constitution and laws of this State or of the United
States .
§3 . Said bank may appoint such officersas may be
necessary for the conducting of itsbusiness,who shall be
under the direction and control of not less than five and
not more than seven directors, each of whom shall be
stockholders, and ,afterthe firstelection,shall have been
stockholders for at least three months previous to their
election, and they shall be residents of this State ; and
after the firstelection, shall be elected annually , on the
firstMonday in May in each year ; they shall elect one
of their number president,and shall hold regular meet-
ings at such times as may be fixedby the by-laws ,and
may have other meetings when deemed necessary ; and
in case of death or resignation of a director,the vacancy
shall be filledby the board of directors. The directors
shall have power to declare dividends of the profitsaris-
ing out of said business, and to fix, allow,and pay the
officers,agents, and servants such sums , wages ,and sala-
riesas they shallthinkproper and reasonable.
§ 4. The stock shallbe deemed personalproperty,and
shallbe transferableand assignable according to the by-
laws or rulesof said bank at the time ; and said corpora-
tionshallhave a lienon the stockto secure any indebt-
edness by the stockholder.
§ 5. John G. Barret, W. R. Ray ,H. V. Loving,Clifton
Rodes Barret,of the cityof Louisville,Kentucky ,and R.
N. Archer ,Dudley Hill, and C. K. Drane ,of the town of
Williamsburg, Kentucky ,are hereby appointed commis-
sioners,any three of whom may act, to open !books and
receivesubscriptionfor the capitalstock; and when one
hundred shares have been subscribed , it shall be their
duty to give notice,in one or more dailynewspapers pub-
lishedin the cityof Louisville,forten days,and appoint
a day forthe electionof a board of directors, who shall
hold their office until the ensuing annual election . The
manner of payment forthe stockshallbe setforthin the
articlesof subscription. But said corporation shall not
begin business untilthe sum of ten thousand dollarsshall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 887
have been paid up,and the presidentand directorsshall
have made oath before a competent officerthat they will
truly and honestly, to the best of theirjudgment and
ability,conduct the affairsof said bank . The board of
directorsshallhave power ,by theirby-laws,to provide
for the enforcement of payment or forfeitureof stock
subscribed and not paid for, or for any part thereof.
§ 6. Said bank shall, through itsboard of directors,
have the power to requirebond from all or any of its
officersor employes; and ifthe cashier,teller,president,
or other officeror employe,shallappropriateany of the
funds of said bank to his own use ,or shallwillfullyfail
to make correctentries,or knowingly make falseones on
the books of said bank , with intent to cheat or defraud
the corporation or any one else,or to conceal any im-
proper appropriationor funds,the officer, clerk,or em-
ploye shallbe deemed guiltyof felony, and shall,upon
conviction thereof, be sentenced to confinement in the
jailand penitentiaryof thisCommonwealth for a period
of not lessthan two and not more than ten years .
§7 . The Legislature reservesthe right to alter,amend ,
or repeal this act at pleasure, as well as at alltimes to
examine into the affairsof said corporation by any com-
mittee they may appoint .
§ 8. That an annual tax of fiftycents isassessed,and
shall be collected , on each share of stock subscribed for
in thiscorporation,which said tax the cashiershallpay
on the firstday of May in each year to the Auditor of
Public Accounts in lieu of all other taxes .
§ 9. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 9, 1884.
