CHAPTER 1269 .
AN ACT to authorize the voters of the town of Bremen , in Muhlenburg
county, to vote upon the sale of spirituous, vinous,and malt liquors in
said town .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the trusteesof the town of Bremen ,in Muh-
lenburg county , be , and they are hereby , authorized to
cause a poll to be opened , on a day to be fixedby them ,
to take the sense of the qualifiedvoters of said town
living inside of the corporate limits of the town as to
whether or not the local option now existingin said town
shallbe repealed.
§2 . That said electionshallbe held in said town ,and
according to the General Statutes governing other elec-
tions,afternoticehas been given by the trusteesby post-
ing up advertisements in said town ,in at leastfiveof the
most public places, ten days before said election, said no-
tices to be signed by the trustees, and stating the time
and place of holding said election and the question to be
voted upon ; that the judge of police court of said town
shallappointthe officersof saidelection,and qualifythe
same , and may act as one of the judges of said election.
And it shall be asked each voter , when his name is re-
corded : "Are you for or against the local option now
existing in this town ? " and his vote shall be recorded
either for or against,as he chooses to answer . And at
the close of said election the judges shallcount the vote
for and against the same ,and give a certificatestating
the number of votes cast both for and against local op-
tion; which certificateshall be delivered to the police
judge , who shall record the same in his order-book ,and
when so recorded, shall be evidence of the approval or
rejectionof thisact.
§3 . That should a majority of the qualified voters of
said town voting at said election vote for the repeal of
said localoption,then the same shallstandrepealed,and
the saleof liquorsin saidtown shallbe regulatedby the
general laws of this Commonwealth ; and should a ma

894 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
jorityof the qualifiedvotersvote againstthe repeal of
said local option,then said local option shallbe enforced
as providedforby generallaws.
§ 4. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 3, 1884.

now , therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

railroadcompany , said Louisvillechancery court, upon

to appear true,shallhave jurisdiction,and itshallbe the
duty of said court,to order the distributionof so much of
said funds as shallthen remain unclaimed by said em-
ployes,tobe paid and distributedpro rata among allthe
holders of said second or Douglas mortgage bonds of said
railroadcompany ,and thenceforward all claims to said
fund,or any part of it,by said employes,or any of them,
shall be barred by limitation.
§ 2. This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved May 8,1884 .

CHAPTER 1285 .
AN ACT to prohibit the sale of spirituous, vinous, and malt liquors in
Hardin county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person or per-
sons to sell,barter,or trafficin spirituous,vinous,or malt
liquors, in any quantity whatever , and for any purpose
whatever , either directlyor indirectly, in the county of
Hardin .
§2 . Any person violatingthe provisionsof the preced-
ing sectionshall,upon conviction,be finedin any sum not
lessthan one hundred nor more than fivehundred dollars,
in the discretionof the jury trying the case.
§3 . This act shall not take effect or be in force until the
same shallbe ratifiedand approved by a majorityof the
votes cast at the electionhereinafterprovided for in this
act .
§4 . Said election shallbe held at the regular election,
on the firstMonday in August ,one thousand eight hun-
dred and eighty-four,and itshallbe unlawful for any per-
son to vote at said election who is not eligible to vote for
representativeto the General Assembly. It shallbe the
duty of the clerk of the Hardin county court, in prepar-
ing the poll-books for said election,to make one column
marked at the head thereof, on each page ," For the saleof
spirituous,vinous,and malt liquors," and anothercolumn
marked at the head of each page ,"Against the sale of
spirituous,vinous ,and malt liquors."
§ 5. It shallbe the duty of the clerk of said electionto
propound to each individual who offersto vote at said
election the question: "Are you for or against the sale of

902 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
whisky in the county of Hardin ?" and hisvote shallbe
recorded according to his answer . The officersof the
election shall be governed by the laws now regulating
the conduct of officersof general election. The poll-
books shallbe returned,compared ,and resultcertifiedas
now required by law regulating general elections. If it
shall appear from a comparison of the polls that a ma-
jority of the votes cast at said election are against the
sale of said liquors,then the board to examine the polls
shall make a certificateof that fact, which shallbe filed
with the county clerk,who shallrecordthe same in his
office, and then this act shall be in full force and effect.
§ 6. It is hereby made the duty of the Hardin county
court to order the sheriffto give twenty days ' notice of
said election,by printed or written posters,in each of the
voting places of said county .
§ 7. Nothing in this act shall be construed to interfere
with the privilege of any person who is sellingliquors
under a licenseproperlygranted tillthe expirationof
such license .
§ 8. This act, in so far as it authorizes the vote to be
taken ,shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved May 5,1884.

CHAPTER 1288 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof wine ,malt,or spirituousliquors, or a mix
ture of either, within two miles of the Mount Lebanon Church , in Green
county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That from and afterthe passage of thisact itshall
be unlawful for the county court of Green county , or any

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 905
court,or any person ,to grant a license to any person or
persons to sellwine , malt , or spirituousliquors,or a mix-
ture of either, within two miles of the Mount Lebanon
Church , in Green county , Kentucky ; nor shallitbe law-
ful for any tavern -keeper , merchant , distiller, or other
person or persons,to sellsuch liquors,or a mixture there-
of, in quantitieslessthan a barrel, within said two miles
of said Mount Lebanon Church ,except it be for medical
use,insuchquantitiesas shouldonlybe actuallynecessary,

physician: Provided, That any personwho ,ingood faith,
may have obtained a licenseto retailspirituousliquors,as
now authorizedby law , may continue to do so untilthe
expiration of the time for which such license has been
granted only .
§2 . That any person selling such liquors,in violation
of the foregoing section, shall be fined one hundred dol-
lars for the firstoffense,and two hundred dollars for each
succeeding offense,to be recovered on an indictment of the
grand jury; the fines thus assessed and recovered to be
collected as other fines,and shall inure to the benefit of
the school fund of the common school for white children

situated; and said fines, when collected, shall be paid

missioner for Green county , who shall be liable on his
officialbond forthe faithfulapplication of the same to the
benefit of said district .
§3 . That it shall not be lawful for any physician to
grant a certificateto any person or persons forthe pur-
chase of such liquorsas a beverage,but onlyforuse as a
medicine .
§ 4. This act to take effectand be in force from itspas-
sage .
Approved May 5, 1884.

ified .
§3 . The said corporationis authorizedto receiveall

such persons may be placed in such institution,by order

ceived in said institutionin good faithforthe purpose of

mates ,and they are authorized and empowered to create
such officersas to them may seem fitforthe control of

interest,payable in such manner and form as the direct-
ors may determine; and the said corporationis author-
ized and empowered to mortgage any of itspropertyto

rationisfurtherauthorizedto receive,in any manner its

sonal, and may pay for same with its bonds or capital
stock; and may receive from any person or persons real
or personal property heretoforeor hereafterleased by

the company . In case said corporationshould in any
manner acquire improvements upon any real property
belonging to the Cave Hill Cemetery Company , it is

erty forthat upon which saidimprovements may be situ-
ated ; and said Cave Hill Cemetery Company isauthorized

to make conveyance of the propertyupon which saidim-
provements are situated,in exchange for otherproperty
which ,in the judgment of the board of directorsof said

§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 10, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1298 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Fort Hill Stone Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1.That L.M. Paine,B. Dupont ,W. M. Bent,John Bar-
ret,and Jacob Bickel,shallbe ,and they are hereby ,created
and made a body-politicand corporate,by the name of
the " Fort HillStone Company ;" and by that name have
power and authority to contract and be contracted with ,
to sue and be sued,to plead and be impleaded,in any
of the courts in this Commonwealth ; and to have a
common seal,and alterthe same at pleasure; they shall
have the right to purchase , hold, lease, and work any
lands deemed useful or necessary for the prosecution
of its business; also to acquire such real and personal
estate as is usefulor requisiteforthe same ; and itmay
at any time sell,exchange,mortgage,or convey said es-
*tate,or any portion thereof,and purchase other; said com-
pany shallhave power to erectand maintain allneeded
elevators, sheds, depots,floats, buildings, and boats, and
may carry on the business of mining and quarrying stone,
and of millingand manufacturing itintodimension stone,
macadam ,lime ,and fertilizingmaterial and cement ,and

920 LAWS OF KENTUCKY ..
disposeof the products; may transportitsproducts and
propertyby river navigating; and connect itslinesof
railway with other linesof railway or linesof river navi-
gation, and own or acquire the needed boats for that
purpose .
§2 . The capitalstock of saidcorporation shallbe divided
into shares of one hundred dollars each ; but may com-
mence business when thirtythousand dollars shall be
subscribed as stock,with the privilegeof increasing the
same to meet the need of its business , to a sum not ex-
ceeding one hundred thousand dollars, by a vote of a
majority of the stockholders; but to make said increase
a majority of the stock shall be voted therefor; the said
stock to be paid in upon callsin such way and at such
times and places as ordered by the board of directors.
§3 . The business and affairsof said corporation shall
be conducted by a board of not less than three and not
more than seven directors; the board of directors shall
from theirnumber electa president, and may electa sec-
retary,treasurer, and general manager , and such other
officersas are necessary in carrying on itsbusiness affairs;
one or more of these officesmay be combined in one per-
son, ifthe board of directorssee fitto combine said offices
in one person . The present corporators are hereby de-
claredto be the firstboard of directors; they shallhold
theirofficeas such until their successorsare duly elected
and qualified. Election of directorsshall be held on the
third Monday in November in each year ,the firstbeing
held in one thousand eight hundred and eighty four; each
share of stock being entitledto one vote,and the vote
may be cast in person or by proxy .
§4 . The board of directorsmay have power to appoint
or dismiss any officeror agent at their pleasure in the
conduct of the business of said corporation, and may
require of said officersor agents bonds ,with good surety,
for a faithfuldischarge of duty , and to account for all
money or property which may come into the possession
or under the control of said agent or officersbelonging
to said corporation .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 921
§5 . Said president and directorsshallhave fullpower
to make such by -laws and rules for the conduct of its
affairsas they shalldeem fit, and for regulating and con-
trollingits officersand agents , not in conflictwith the
Constitution of the United States or the Constitution
and laws of the State of Kentucky .
§ 6. That by a vote of a majority of the stock the said
corporation may have the right to borrow money to the
amount of one half of itspaid-up stock, and to issuethe
bonds of said company or corporation in such sums , and
payable at such times and places,as may be agreed ; and
for the securityand payment of said bonds,may mort-
gage any or allthe propertyof said corporationto the
party or corporationfrom whom saidmoney isborrowed ,
or to any other person ; and said bonds may be made to
draw interestat any rate not to exceed six per cent.per
annum , if so agreed , and the same shallbe valid in law .
§7 . That untilthe firstannual electionherein provided
for, the corporatorsherein named shallhereby be con-
stituted the directorsof said corporation, and shall hold

holders . No person shallbe qualifiedto act as president

after its passage .

CHAPTER 1299 .
AN ACT to authorizeCasey county to issuebonds to aid in building a rail-
road through Casey county.
WHEREAS , The Cincinnati,Green River , and Nashville
Railroad Company proposeto construct,build,and oper-
ate a railroad from some point on the Elizabethtown ,
Lexington ,and Big Sandy Railroad ,or on the Kentucky

922 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Central Railroad,or some road controlled by the Ken-
tucky CentralRailroadCompany ,which road runsthrough
the county of Casey; thence to the cityof Nashville, in
the State of Tennessee ,or to some railroad leading to
that city; and whereas ,it is the desire of the county of
Casey to assist in building said railroad through said
county ; and if said Cincinnati, Green River ,and Nash-
ville Railroad Company should not build said railroad
through said county ,then it is the purpose and desire of
said county to assistany other company that will con-
structand operate a railroad through said county ; there-
fore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for Casey county to issue
bonds , not exceeding fiftythousand dollarsin amount , on
the credit and responsibilityof said county and of the
tax-payers and property situatedand kept in said county ,
to aid in construction of a railroad through said county .
2. That said county may acquiretitleto,or rightof
way over, a continuous stripof land, not exceeding one
hundred feetin width ,where said railroadmay be located,
and such additionalgrounds as may be necessary to build
the same upon ,and for the building of the depots and
other necessary buildings.
§3 . Said county shallhave power to dispose of and con-
vey said lands,rightsof way ,and easements to such rail-
road or corporation as shallbuild, equip ,and maintain a
good and substantialrailroad through said county along
said line,located as aforesaid, and receive in exchange
thereforthe firstmortgage bonds of saidrailroad,corpo-
ration, or company ,at not a greater rate than the par
value thereof , which bonds shall constitute a first lien on
said railroad and all of the property and franchises of
the said railroad, corporation, or company . Said county
shallhave the rightto exchange itsbonds,or proceeds
thereof, which may not have been consumed in purchas-
ing the lands and easements aforesaid, at not less than
par , for the firstmortgage bonds of said railroad com-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 923
pany , at a rate not greater than par ,secured as afore-
said, which issuing, exchange , and purchasing bonds
shall be made on the conditions hereafter named .
§ 4. The bonds of the county shallnot be issued under
thisact until authorized by a vote of the qualifiedvoters
of said county . Said bonds shall not run longer than
twenty years, and may be redeemed at the option of the
county at such shortperiodas may be determined when
said bonds issue . Said bonds shall not bear interest at a
rate exceeding six per cent. per annum .
§5 . Before any bonds shall be issued by said county
under thisact,the same must be authorized by a majority
of the legal voters of said county voting at an election
to be held as follows: When a petition,specifyingthe
amount of bonds to be issued , and the terms of the same
in regard to the time and place of payment ,signed by
not lessthan one hundred and fiftytax-payers and quali-
fiedvotersof saidcounty,shallbe presentedto the coun-
ty court of Casey county,the saidcounty court,composed
of the county judge of saidcounty and a majorityof the
justicesof thepeace in commissionin saidcounty,may
cause an electionto be held at the several voting places
in said county , and shall cause notice of the same to be
publishedin thenewspapers insaidcounty,and shallalso
cause said electionto be advertisedby printednotices
posted up in at leastsixpublicplacesin each magisterial
districtfor at leasttwenty days before said election. At
said election the sense of the qualified voters shall be
taken for and against the proposition to issue bonds of
the county to aidinthe constructionof a railroadthrough
said county. Those who shallvote in favorof the propo-
sition shallbe so recorded; those who vote against itshall
be so recorded . Said election shall be held in the same
manner ,by the same officers,and under thesame respon-
sibilities as in case of elections under the general laws of
the State; and the poll-books shallbe returnedby the
same officers,and in the same time and manner ,as in case
of electionsunder the generallaws. The poll-books shall
be opened and comparedby theofficersatthe same time

924 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and in the same manner required in cases of elections
under the general laws of the State. Said officersso
comparing saidpollsshallmake out a certificateof the
number of votes cast for and against the proposition,
which certificateshallbe copied on the order-book of the
Casey county court, and the original certificateand the
poll-books shall be filedand kept in the county court
clerk'sofficeof said county .
§ 6. If itshall appear from said certificateof election
so made out and recorded that a majority of the votes
cast at said election were in favor of issuing bonds as
aforesaid,the county court shallproceed to have the same
carriedinto effect, and shallappoint three responsible
tax-payers of said county commissioners ,who shallhave
power to cause properbonds to be prepared,which shall
be signed by the county judge , and countersigned by the
clerk,and the county seal affixed, with coupons attached,
signed by the clerk. The commissioners shall have full
power to carry into effectthe provisions of this act in
relation to all lands and rightsof way ,and the sale or
exchange of bonds ,and the collectionof intereston the
railroad bonds , and the receiving of the taxes that may
be levied to pay principal or intereston the county bonds ,
and provide for payment of intereston said bonds , and
principal thereof,under the directionsof the county court;
but no conveyance or transfershallbe made to such rail-
road company of lands, rights of way , and easements
untilsuch company shallgive proper covenant to locate
and maintain a suitable railroad through said county as
aforesaid, nor shall the bonds , nor the proceeds of the
same , be deliveredor paid to said company untilthe
grade of said railroad shallbe completed from terminus
to terminus ,when one half may be paid over ,and the
remaining half shallbe paid over on the completion of
the said railroad, ironed and ready for operation,from
terminus to terminus . The said commissioners shall be
a body -politicand corporate, under the name of the
Railroad Commissioners of Casey County ; and under
that name may sue and be sued ,contract and be con

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 925
tractedwith ,in reference to allmatters arisingunder this
act; they shall appoint one of their number treasurer,
who shall give bond before the Casey county court, with
good and sufficientsureties, conditioned that he willdis-
charge his duties as such treasurer, and will faithfully
account for and pay over to those entitled thereto all
money which may come to his hands as such , and will
safelykeep all bonds or other propertywith which he
may be intrustedin hisofficialcapacity,and deliverthe
same as he may be legallydirected. The treasurershall
be the custodian of all bonds , contracts , deeds , and
moneys authorized by this act, or in any way accrue
under the same .
§7 . That whenever itmay be necessary to raise money
to pay intereston the county bonds to raise a sinking
fund to retireor pay the bonds ,the county court afore-
said shalllevy an ad valorem tax on allthe propertyin
saidcounty,real,personal,and mixed,which is liableto
be taxed for State revenue purposes,which levy may be
made and collectedon the assessments of property of the
county for State revenue ; but no levy shall exceed fifty
cents on the one hundred dollars in value of the property
assessed for any one year .
§ 8. It shallbe the duty of the sheriffof Casey county
to collectalltaxes levied under authority of thisact, and
he shall be liablefor the same on his bond as such ,given
for county levy and dues , and shallhave the same power
of distraint,levy, and sale of real and personal property
and attachment of choses as in cases of collection of
State revenue ; and he shallbe entitledto compensation .
for collecting the same the same commission as for col-
lecting the county levy; but the county court shallhave
the rightsto demand a special bond of the sherifffor said
revenue if it deem proper ; and may , in its discretion,
appoint a special collectorof said taxes and revenues ,
and require of him a bond , with sureties,conditioned as
in other cases of tax collections. The sheriffand special
collectors shallpay over to the treasurer of the railroad
commissioners all taxes collectedby them , and shall be

926 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
liable to the same actions and motions as lie against
othercollectingofficers. Said tax shallbe due and pay-
ableby thecollectingofficerat the same time the county
levy isdue and payable .
§ 9. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 10, 1884.

CHAPTER 1301.1
AN ACT to incorporatethe MercantileBank and Savings Association.

sand shares of the par value of one hundred dollarseach ,

not exceeding ten thousand shares of likepar value; and

ing and financialbusiness; and may loan money ,discount

gotiable and payable at its banking -house , or any other

counted by said bank ,and inland billsof exchange which

are hereby ,put upon the footing of foreign billsof ex-
change ; and likeremedy may be had thereon,jointlyand

the partiesthereto.
§ 4. The said bank may acquire and hold ,possess and
use,occupy and enjoy,allsuch real and personal prop-
erty,goods,chattels, or other things, as may be neces-
sary forthe transactionof its business, or which may be
pledged or conveyed to itas securityfor any debt of pur
chase,in satisfactionof any debts,judgments,or decree,
and selland convey,or otherwisedispose of, such per-

sonalsecuritiesas a naturalperson; and itshallbe the

receivedepositsof gold,silver,bank notes,United States

kind,orasmay be agreedupon by specialorgeneralcon-
tract, and may allow intereston deposits,not to exceed
the ratesallowed by the Commonwealth . It may issue
certificatesof credit,payable throughout the United States
and elsewhere , for the convenience of merchants and
travelers,but shallnot issue any notes or billsto circulate
as money .
§ 6. The board of directorsof saidbank are hereby au-
thorized and empowered to enact and put in forcesuch
rules,by -laws,and regulationsforthe management , gov-
ernment ,and controlof itsproperty,business,and affairs
as they may deem expedient,and alter,amend ,and re-
peal the same at will; and shallspecifythereinthe num-
ber of directorsnecessary to constitute a quorum for the
transactionof business. They may appointsuchofficers,
agents,and servantsas they may deem necessaryto con-
duct the businessof the bank ,and pay them such sums
for theirservices,and take from them bonds in such pen-
alty to securethe faithfulperformance of theirduties, as
they may think reasonableand proper; and upon any
bonds thus taken recovery may be had for breaches of
LOC . L., VOL . II- 59

930 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the conditions thereof. If any officerof the bank shall
appropriate any funds of said bank to his own use , or
shallwillfullyfailto make correct entries, or knowingly
make false ones on the books of said bank , with intent
to cheat or defraud the corporationor the person,such
officershallbe deemed guiltyof felony,and shall,upon
conviction thereof , be sentenced to confinement in the
jailor penitentiaryof thisCommonwealth for a period
of not lessthan two nor more than ten years .
§7 . The said Mercantile Bank and Savings Associa-
tion, by order of its board of directors,organize a sav-
ings department ,and may keep the same ,both as to stock,
capital,investments ,and management ,separate and apart
from its general banking business . The shares in the sav-
ings department may be such a sum as the directorsmay
fix, but it shall not exceed five hundred thousand dollars
in amount . These may be paid for in such manner as
may be provided in the order of the board of directors
establishingthe savings department . In case thisdepart-
ment isorganized,itsfunds and assetsshallbe kept sepa-
rate,and shall not be liable for the debts or obligations of
the bank ,other than such as arise in and from the said
savingsdepartment,forthe purposes of thisdepartment.
The said corporationmay purchase, hold, and control
property for banking-houses and other corporationsin
the cityof Louisville; and may issuecoupon bonds,se-
cured by mortgage on such property, in such amount ,and
payable at such time and place,as may be fixed upon by
the board of directors; such bonds ,however , not to bear
a greater rate of interestthan six per centum per annum ;
or may issue interest-bearing certificateor bonds ,which
shallpass by delivery. So long as the savingsdepositsof
other banks in the city of Louisvilleare excepted from
taxation,the savings deposits of this bank shall be like-
wise exempt ,but not otherwise.
§ 8. The said corporation may receive on deposit,
during banking hours, any sum of money not lessthan
fiftycents; and minors and married women may make
depositsand controlthe same . Such depositsshallbe
paid to each depositorat such time,and with such inter-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 931
est,and under such regulations,as the board of directors
shall,from time to time,prescribe. Such regulationsshall
be put up in some public and conspicuous place in the
room when the business of said institution shall be trans-
acted , and shall not be altered so as to affectany one
who may be a depositor previous to such alteration, as
to any sum of money on deposit at the time such altera-
tion is made .
§ 9. Stock of said bank shallbe deemed personal prop-
erty, and shall only be assignable in accordance with
such rules as the board of directors shall, from time to
time ,prescribe; and said bank shall have a lien on the
shares of any stockholder who may be indebted to it;
and such shares shall not be assignable nor transferred
until the debt shall be paid or discharged , without the
consent of the president and board of directors. This
act shall take effectfrom its passage , and remain in
force for twenty -fiveyears from and afteritspassage .
§ 10. The General Assembly shall have the right to
examine into the affairsof saidcorporationby any com-
mittee they may appoint for that purpose ; and itreserves
the right to alter, amend , modify, or repealthisact at
pleasure .
§ 11. Said Mercantile Bank and Savings Association
shall at all times pay such license as the general council
of the city of Louisville may require of banks for the
privilegeof doing a banking business; and nothing herein
shallbe so construed as to exempt any of the realestate
of said Mercantile Bank and Savings Association from
city or county taxation.
§ 12. This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved May 10, 1884.

932 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1303 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the LouisvilleCity Railway Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the LouisvilleCity Railway Company be ,and
it is hereby , authorized to make and sellits negotiable
bonds to an amount not to exceed one million dollars,
bearing interestat a rate not to exceed six per centum
per annum ,payable semi -annually ,and which bonds may
be made payable at any time not exceeding thirtyyears
afterdate,and may provide for the exchange of any such
bonds for any of the bonds or notes of said company
which may be outstanding. The said company is au-
thorized to attach coupons for the interest thereon, to
any of such bonds ; or,at the request of the holder of any
such bonds , may cause the same to be registered in his
name upon the books of the company ; in which case the
bonds so registered shall be transferable only upon the
books of the company by such holder, in person or by
attorney; and the said company may make regulations
providing for such transfers, and also for a change of
any of the said bonds ,at the request of the holder, from
coupons into registered bonds , or from registered into
coupon bonds ; and when any bond shallbe registered, it
shall be so stated in an indorsement on such bond .
§ 2. The said company is authorized to secure the pay-
ment of the bonds aforesaid, with the interestthereon ,by
a mortgage ordeed of trustupon any or allof itsprop-
erty,rights, and franchises, and may provide therein that
the bonds so secured shallbe certifiedby the trusteein the
deed of trust or mortgage to be the bonds secured there-
by ,and that the holder of any such bonds may ,by agree-
ment with the company , convert the same into bonds
of smaller denominations : Provided , That this act shall
not authorizesaid company to include in said mort-
gage the following lines, the right to build which is
claimed by and now in litigationbetween the Central
Passenger Railway Company and the saidLouisvilleCity
Railway Company , viz: the intersectionof Nineteenth

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 933
and Walnut streets, along Nineteenth street to Bank
street; thence along Bank streetto Thirty-thirdstreet to
High Avenue ; thence along High Avenue to Thirty-fifth
street; thence along to Thirty-fifthstreet to Water street
(or Portlandwhart); thence along Water street(or Port-
land wharf) to a pointnear theferrylanding,and itshall
be so expressed on the face of the mortgage .
§3 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1304 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Mammoth Cave Railway Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That John M. Culp , Wm . B. McCampbell ,Oliver
Lucas , and James C. Gilbert,their associates, successors,
and assigns, be , and they are hereby , created a body .
politicand corporate,under the name and styleof the
Mammoth Cave Railway Company ; and as such , and
under that name ,shallhave perpetualsuccession, with
power to sue and be sued in the corporate name ; to
make contracts and be contracted with; to acquire, hold,
and own such real and personal estate as may be neces-
saryforthe constructionof itslineof railway,and forthe
proper carrying into execution allitsbusiness and opera-
tions as permitted by thisact; to have and use a common
seal, and power to change and alterthe same at pleasure,
They shall have power to make all necessary by -laws
and regulationsforcarryinginto effectthe provisionsof
this act and the business of said corporation,not incon-
sistent with the Constitution and laws of this State or of
the United States .
§2 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe any
sum fixed,from time to time ,by the board of directors,
not exceeding three hundred thousand dollars, divided
into shares of one hundred dollars each ; but said com-
pany may organize ,elect officers, and commence busi

934 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ness whenever twenty -fivethousand dollarsof stock shall
have been subscribed . After said amount shall have
been subscribed, the remainder of the amount of the
capitalstock,as fixedin accordance with the provision
hereinbefore stated , shall be issued in such sums and
amounts , and upon such conditions, and at such times,
as the board of directors may determine ; and after an
organization shall have been effected,the stock subscribed
for shall be paid for in such installments,and at such
times, as may be determined and called forby the board
of directors; and where stock shall be subscribed, and
default in payment therefor be made by the subscriber,
aftergiving the person in default twenty days ' notice,in
writing, the directorsmay ,by resolution entered on their
books ,forfeitsuch stock, and sellthe same at such time
as they may deem expedient ; and partialpayments made
on any stock which shall be forfeitedshall be held for
the benefitof the stockholder,afterdeducting any unpaid
calls and costs of sale . The certificates of stock shall be
signed by the president,countersignedby the secretary,
and stamped with the seal of the corporation. They
shall be transferable only upon the stock books of the
corporation,and the surrender of the certificate, with the
proper assignment thereon by the partyto whom itwas
is ued . Said certificate shall be taken in and canceled ,
al a new one issued instead , and said stock shall be
personal property.
3. The business of said corporation shallbe conducted
by a board of fivedirectors,threeof whom shallbe a quo-
rum for the transaction of business , and all of whom
shallbe stockholders,a president,a secretary,and a treas-
urer,and such other officersas may be provided for by
by -laws ; allof whom shallbe electedon the second Mon-
day in January of each year ; shall hold their officesfor
one year ,and untiltheirsuccessors shallbe elected. The
president shallbe a member of the board of directors,and
shall be elected by the other directors,and the secretary
and treasurer shall also be elected by the directors,but
shall not be members of said board . The offices of sec

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 935
retaryand treasurermay be at any time consolidated,and
thedutiesperformed by the same person. The directors
may provide for and require the officersof the company
to give bond ,with security,for the faithfulperformance
of their duties; and they may fillany vacancy that may
occur in their own number . The board of directors first
elected , after an organization is effected, shall serve until
the next annual election; and they shall,by proper by-
laws ,by them to be made , provide for the subscription
and payment of stock, the electionof officers, and all
other matters belonging to the management and business
of the company ,not in conflictwith and not provided for
by thisact, which by -laws shall have the same force and
effect as if herein enacted .
§ 4. Said corporation is hereby granted the power to
construct and operate a railway from any point on the
Louisville and Nashville Railroad to a point at or near
the entrance of the Mammoth Cave in Edmonson county ,
which road may be eithera narrow -gauge or road of stand-
ard gauge ,to be operated by steam or any other motive
power , and for that purpose may receive releasesof the
right of way ,by donation or purchase ; and when from any
cause such releases cannot be obtained by agreement ,
said company shall have the power and authorityto con-
demn such lands, as also such materials necessary for the
construction of its road -way , buildings , and structures
of allkinds permitted by this act,for its use,under and
by virtue of an act, entitled "An act to prescribe the
mode of condemning land for the use of railroad and
turnpike companies," approved April the eleventh, one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-two .
§5 . Said company may buy , own , hold, and sell all
real estate,eitherin fee-simple or by lease,thatmay be
necessary or convenient for the transaction of its business ,
and itshallhave power upon any of itsproperty to erect
and maintain allsuch buildingsas itmay see proper for
the entertainment of its visitors, and to conduct such
hotels,liverystables,and pleasuregrounds,and to keep
and maintain for the use of itsguests all the usual amuse

936 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ments ,refreshments,and attractionsusuallykept at water-
ing places and summer resorts, and not prohibitedby law ..
§6 . Said company , for the transportationof each pas-
senger over its line of road , including reasonable bag--
gage ,may charge any rate to be fixed by the board of
directors,not exceedingone dollarand twenty-fivecents,
and in proportion for lessthan the whole distance.
§ 7. By consent of the board of directors, any lands
purchased or leased, building erected, or property of any
kind purchased ,may be paid for in the stock of the con-
pany ,where such stockisacceptedin payment .
$
§ 8. Said company may , for the purpose of raising,
money to enable it to carry into effectthe purpose of its
organization, may issue itsbonds to an amount not ex-
ceeding one half itscapital stock; said bonds to be pay-
able in such a lengthof time,at such place,and to bear
such a rate of interest,not exceeding six per cent. per
annum ,as the board of directorsfixand determine ; and
to secure the payment of such bonds and theirinterest,
the company may execute a mortgage or mortgages upon
alltheirreal and personal property and franchises.
§ 9. This act shall take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 10 , 1884 ..
CHAPTER 1305 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Hayden's Corner and Pinchem Turnpike Road
Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a corporationishereby created,with a cap-
ital stock of not exceeding three thousand dollars per
mile , divided into shares of one hundred dollars each , for
the purpose of constructing a turnpike road from a point
at or near Hayden's Corner, in Clark county, by such
routeas itsdirectorsmay choose,to a pointon the exten-
sion of the Kentucky Central Railroad,running as near
as may be to Forest Grove Church ,on the Boonesboro

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 937
pike,and with the privilegeof extendingsame to a point
on the Winchester and Muddy Creek Turnpike Road .
Said corporationshallbe known as the Hayden's Corner
and Pinchem Turnpike Road Company , and shall have
the powers and franchises enumerated in section three of
chapter fifty-six of the General Statutes of this Common .
wealth .
§ 2. Said company shallconstructand operateitssaid
road as is provided in chapterone hundred and ten of
said General Statutes,and may receive and collect toll
thereon as isprovided in said chapter .
§3 . Said company , in case it failsto agree with the
owner thereof,may condemn land or material needed in
the construction or operation of said road , as is now pro-
vided by law with reference to railroadsand other corpo-
rations .
§ 4. John F. Bush,Perry Browning ,J.L. Brown ,R. M.
Nunnelly,C. B. Stewart,Jas.A. Bybee , and J. T. Quis-
enberry,or any three of them ,may open books and re-
ceive subscriptionstothe capitalstock of saidcompany ,
and call a meeting to elect officers,who shallconsistof
a board of five directors chosen from the stockholders,
with power to electone of theirnumber president. Said
board may create such offices, and appoint persons to fill
same ,as they may deem proper in building and operating
said road ; and may adopt by -laws and rules not incon-
sistent with the laws of the land .
§ 5. This act shall take effect from and after itspas-
sage .
Approved May 5 ,1884.
CHAPTER 1306 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe RockcastleSprings and Mining Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That John S. Hughes , F. J. Campbell ,John Speed ,
F. W. Varrier,and E. R. Chenault ,and their associates,
successors,and assigns,be,and they are hereby,created.

938 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
a body -corporate and politic, under the name and style
of the Rockcastle Springs and Mining Company .
§2 . Said company shall,by that name and style,have
perpetual succession,and be capable of suing and being
sued , pleading and being impleaded , in all courts and
places ; of contracting and being contracted with ; of
having a common seal , and the same to break or alter at
pleasure; to acquireand hold realand personalproperty
by purchase or otherwise, and the same to sellor dispose
of whenever they may deem itexpedient or proper .
§3 . That the capitalstock of said company shallbe
thirtythousand dollars; but said stock may be increased
to fiftythousand dollarsby a majority vote of the stock-
holders; and the said capitalstock shallbe divided into
shares of one hundred dollars each ,which may be sub-
scribed and paid for in money or property, either real or
personal,in such manner as may be determined on by
said company . Such shares shallbe deemed personal
property, and may be transferredon the books of the
company when fully paid up . When any stockholder
shall have paid up his stock in full,he shallbe entitled
to a certificatefor same ,to be issued in conformity to
the by -laws of said company , and said stockholders shall
not be subject to any further call; and each share shall
entitlethe holderthereofto one vote in any meeting of
stockholders .
§ 4. That the principalofficeof businessof said com-
pany shallbe at the old Rockcastle Springs,in Pulaski
county, Kentucky ; and the businessof said company
shallbe that of keeping a hotel for springs boarders and
other customers , a store of general merchandise , farm-
ing, and mining coal, iron, and other minerals. They
may adopt by-laws for theirgovernment; but said by-
laws shall not be in conflict with the Constitution and
laws of the United States or of this State .
§5 . The incorporatorsherein named ,or any of them ,
may , at any time, open books for the subscriptionof
stock; and for all subscription of stock made in real
estate, at an agreed value ,deeds in fee-simple therefor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 939
shallbe properly signed, acknowledged ,and delivered,
and recorded in the county clerk'sofficeof the county in
which the property is situated; and when paid in per-
sonal property, the value thereofmust firstbe agreed
upon,and same deliveredto the company at theirprin-
cipal place of business, at the Rockcastle Springs, in
Pulaski county ,Kentucky .
§6 . The business of said company shall be conduct-
ed by a president, treasurer, secretary, and such other
officersand employes as may be agreed upon by the
stockholders at their firstmeeting to be called by said
incorporators. Said officers, managers , and employes
may be selected from the stockholders or other persons
as may be deemed best by the stockholders. The duties
of the officersand employes shallbe designated and reg-
ulated by the by -laws of the company .
§ 7. The individual property of the stockholders shall
not be liablefor the debts of said company .
§ 8. This act shall take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 5, 1884.
CHAPTER 1307 .
AN ACT to regulatethe working of publicroads in Adair county, and pro-
vide for levying and collecting a road tax .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshall be the duty of the county judge of
Adair county,in the month of September next,and in
the month of January ,one thousand eight hundred and
eighty -five,and every two years thereafter, to appoint a
road commissioner for said county , who shall hold his
officetwo years,or untilhis successorisappointed and
qualified ; and said commissioner shall, before entering
upon the discharge of the duties of his office, take an
oath in the Adair county courtthathe willfaithfullyand
to the best of his abilitydischarge allthe duties of his
office according to law ; and shallat the same time enter

940 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
into bond ,with good surety,to be approved by said court,
and payable to the same ,for the faithfulperformance of
all his duties,and to properly account for all money and
propertythat may come into his hands by virtueof his
office .
§2 . Said commissioner shall have control of all the
public roads in said county ,except roads belonging to
incorporated companies ; and shall see that allthe public
roads are put in good repair,and kept so during the term
of his office .
§3 . The firstroad commissioner appointed under this
act, after taking the oath of officeand executing bond ,
shallproceed to lay offthe county into convenient road
districts,fixthe boundary of hands thatshallwork or pay
tax in each district, and select a suitable person in each
districtto be surveyor ,and number the road districts, and
report the same to the county court,and said court shall
examine and approve said report, after making such
changes as itmay deem proper ; and appoint as survey-
ors the persons named in said report; and the clerk of
said court shall record the report of the commissioner ,
when approved by the court,and shalldelivera copy of
the order to the sheriff, who shall proceed to notifythe
surveyorsof theirappointment as now provided by law .
§4 . That each surveyor, upon notice of his appoint-
ment ,shallproceed to take charge of the public roads
in his district, and shall see that they are kept in good
repair,and shallsee thatthe road isput in such order as
may be directed by the commissioner ; and shall hold his
officefor a term of two years ,or until his successor is
appointed and qualified.
§ 5. That allmale personsover sixteenand under fifty
years of age who are able to labor,except licensed min-
istersof the gospel,shallbe taxed a capitationtax of not
lessthan two dollarsnor more than fivedollarseach year ,
payable in money or work on the public roads in their
respectivedistricts, to be performed at any time,upon
two days ' notice by the surveyor ; and persons so taxed
shallbe allowed one dollarper day foreach day's labor of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 941
eighthours . Any hand may pay his tax by furnishing an
able-bodied substituteatone dollarperday of eighthours'
labor,or a team and plow ,or team and wagon , at two
dollarsand fiftycents per day of eight hours'labor.
§ 6. That there shall be a road tax of not more than
ten nor less than three cents levied and collected on each
one hundred dollars' worth of taxablepropertyas shown
✓ by the assessor'sbook each year; saidtax to be paid in
work or money as provided herein for capitation tax .
§ 7. That itshallbe the duty of the road commissioner
to visiteach road districtas often as may be necessary ,
and shallgive to the surveyors in writing directionsas to
the work that shall be done on the roads to put them in
good repair, and direct the modes of drainage and the
character of culvertsand indexes,and so forth; and shall
see that his directionsare complied with .
§ 8. The countycourtof saidcounty,in themonth of
October each year ,a majority of the justicesof the peace
in commission in said county being present and concur-
ring therein, shall levy a capitation tax of not less than
two nor more than six dollars on each male subject to
taxation as provided in section fiveof thisact, and an ad
valorem tax of not less than three nor more than ten
cents on one hundred dollarsof taxablepropertyin said
county as provided in section six of thisact.
§ 9. Itshallbe the duty of the clerkofthe county court
to furnish each surveyor the amount of ad valorem tax due
from each person in their respective districts, and said
clerk shallbe allowed twenty cents for each district,to be
paid out of the county levy,and saidsurveyorshallkeep
a correct account with each person taxed in his district,
charging each with the amount of tax assessed against
him as providedby thisact,and shallcrediteach with all
the money or work paid; and said surveyor shall have
the right to receive any money from tax-payers in his
district and receipt for same , and such receipt shall be
evidence of the payment of such tax; and expend any
money paid into hishands as tax to the best advantage in
hiring workmen to blastand build culverts,and in pur

942 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
chasing such tools as may be needed ; such contracts in
allcases to be approved by the commissioner .
§ 10. It shall be the duty of each surveyor to make a
settlement with the commissioner in the month of De-
cember each year , and report alldelinquents in his dis-
trict,and account forallmoney paid into his hands ,either
capitation or ad valorem tax ; and the commissioner shall
make out a listof all persons so reported failingto pay
theirtax in work or money , and shallgive a copy of the
same to the sheriff,who shallproceed to collectthe same
according to law , as other revenue is collected; and the
sheriffshall add ten per cent, to the amount of tax due
from each person so reported,and collectthe same as
other tax as his fees .
§ 11.That the surveyorshall,duringthe months of May
to the thirtiethof October , notifyallpersons taxed under
this act in their districts to work on the road , and that
thosepaying theirtax in work shallbe requiredto do
efficientwork ; and the surveyor shallkeep a time -table,
and credit each person working eight hours per day by
one dollar($ 1),and deduct twelve and one -half cents per
hour for each hour lessthe eight; and said surveyor shall
cause the work to be done in accordance with the direc-
tion of the commissioner ; and in case the commissioner ,.
for any reason,has given no direction,the surveyor shall,
withinthe time above named ,see thatthe roads are put
in good condition. The surveyor shallhave the right to
contract for timber and other materials necessary for re--
pairing the roads,bridges,or culverts, the same to be ap-
proved by the commissioner. The surveyormay contract.
forteams and plows , or teams and wagons ,with any per-
son within his districtsubject to road -tax,and shallcredit.
such person two dollarsand fiftycents per day foreach
day's work of eight hours,and shalldeduct thirty-one and
one-fourthcents foreach hour lessthan eighthours per
day .
§ 12. Said surveyors shall keep theirrespective roads.
open the width requiredin theirorders,and remove all
leaning and dead trees near the roads, and overhanging

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 943
brushand limbs; and they shall,withoutany unnecessary
delay, remove alltimber or obstructionsin the road ,and
may require the aid of any hand ,and give him creditby
the time he is so employed . Each surveyor shall be al-
lowed one dollarper day foreach day they are employed
in actual servicein the interestof said road ,afterdeduct-
ing the amount of tax they are liableto pay .
§ 13. That it shall be the duty of the sheriffof Adair
county , annually , on or before the firstMonday of Octo
ber , to make his settlement with the road commissioner
of said county , and pay to said commissioner all moneys
due the road fund collected under the provisions of this
act,taking his receiptfor the same . The sheriffshallbe
responsibleon his county levy bond for the collection
and settlement of said taxes, and shall be allowed ten
per cent .commission for collectingsaid tax as provided
in this act .
§ 14. That the commissioner shallnot expend a greater
sum of money in any one year than shallbe collectedfor
thatyear under the provisionof thisact; but shallappro-
priate the money collected, as near as can be ascertained,
without minute calculation ,in the districtwhere collected ,
to be paid out by the surveyor as directedby the commis-
sioner, after firstdeducting from said amount of money
herein allowed the surveyor, and the sum of two dollars
for the commissioner's services in each district: Provided ,
That the public roads leading out of the town of Colum-
bia , for a distance of one and a fourth miles, shallnot be
under the control of said commissioner ; but said roads
shall be kept in repair as provided in the fifteenthsec-
tion of this act .
§ 15. That the public roads leading out of the town of
Columbia ,for the distance of one and one fourth miles
from the court-house in said town on each road (the dis-
tance to be ascertained by measuring each road as it
runs ),shallbe under the controlof the board of trustees
of said town ; and the said board of trustees is required
to keep said roads in repair as the streetsand alleysare
required by the laws incorporatingsaidtown to be kept;

944 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and forthatpurpose said board of trusteesshalllevy on
the persons residingin said town ,and on the taxable
propertyin saidtown ,the same capitationand ad valo-
rem tax as provided in sectionsfiveand six of this act.
The said tax shallbe a lien on the property of the tax-
payer,and shallbe collectedin the same manner by the
same officers as the taxes now levied in said town are
collected; saidofficersshallhave alltherightsand powers
in collectingsaid taxes that they have in collectingthe
other taxes in said town : Provided, That the capitation
tax may be paid as providedin the fifthsectionof this
act .
§ 16. That the road commissioner shall,in the month
of March or April each year afterthe year one thousand
eight hundred and eighty- four, visiteach road districtin
the county ,and give direction to the surveyor as to the
work that shall be done that year , which shall be in
writing, and shallbe such as willrequire the roads to be
thrown up in the center, and ditched on both sideswhere
it can be done . The roads shall be graveled where con-
venient,and fine rock shallbe used in such places as may
be required; and said commissioner shall report to the
county court .
§ 17. That itshallbe the duty of the road commissioner
annually , on or before the firstMonday of January in
each year,to make a settlementwith the countyjudge ,
in which settlement he shall file the settlement of each
surveyor made with him , and produce and filevouchers
for allmoneys paid into his hands ; and the firstcommis-
sioner appointed under this act shallbe allowed in said
settlement the sum of two dollars for each road district in
the county , said amount to be retained by him_in said
settlement; and each subsequent commissioner shall be
allowed one dollar and fiftycents for each road district
in saidcounty. The county judge shallreturnsaid set-
tlement , with allvouchers and papers filed,to the court
at itsnext regularterm,when it shallbe filed,and lay
over one month for inspection and exceptions ; and if
no exceptionsis filed,the same shallbe approved and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 945
ordered to be recorded. If any exceptionsare taken,
theyshallbe in writing,and triedby the county court;
and the settlement, when corrected, shall be recorded.
The said judge shallbe allowed one dollarfor each set-
tlement,and the clerk one dollarand fiftycents for record-
ing the same ,to be paid out of the saidfund.
§ 18. Any commissioner failing to perform his duty
according to the provisionsof thisact shall,upon con-
viction, be finednot less than twenty -fivenor more than
fiftydollars; and any surveyor failingto perform his
duty shallbe finednot lessthan ten nor more than twen-
tydollarsfor each failure,to be recoveredby indictment
in each case by grand jury.
§19 . That itshallbe the duty of the officersholding
the election, at the several voting places in said county ,
on the firstMonday in August next,to open a column
in the poll-book headed " For the road law ;" and another
headed " Against the road law ;" and to propound to each
voter at said election the following question : "Are you
for or against the road law ?" Those who answer for the
law shall be so recorded; those who answer against the
law shallbe so recorded. The officersholding said elec-
tion shall state in their certificate the number of votes
cast for the law and the number of votes cast against it.
The officerswho compare the poll-books of said election
shall, at the time they compare the same ,count up and
compare the vote cast for and against the road law , and
shall make out and sign a certificateshowing the number
of votes cast for and the number of votes cast against,
which certificate shall be filed in the office of the clerk of
the Adair county court; and at the next regular term of
said court,aftersaid poll-books are compared ,said cer-
tificate shall be recorded on the order -book of said court .
If it shall appear from said certificatethat a majority of
the votes cast at said election are in favor of the road
law , this act,from the time said certificateisso entered
of record in said court , shall be held to be in full force
and effect, and the county court shallproceed to carry
LOC . L., VOL . 11-60

946 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the same into effectby appointing the commissioner and
levying tax,and proceeding as provided forin thisact;.
but ifitappearing from said certificatethat a majority
of the votes cast at said electionwere against the road
law , then this act shall not be held to be in force, and no
furtheraction or proceeding shallbe had under it. That
the sheriffof said county shallcause the electionprovided
forin the preceding sectionto be advertisedforfifteendays
preceding the election in a newspaper published in said
county ,and by printed advertisements posted in at least
four public places in each voting precinct in said county ,
The cost of said advertisements to be paid out of the
county levy .
Approved May 5, 1884.
CHAPTER 1308 .
AN ACT to enable George T. Schoolfield to perform the marriage rite of
deaf -mutes .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Geo .T. Schoolfield,a deaf-mute and teacher
in the Kentucky Institutionfor the Deaf and Dumb , be ,
and he is hereby , authorized to perform the marriage o
deaf-mutes in the State of Kentucky : Provided, Sai
Schoolfield take the oath and execute the bond now re
quired by law by persons authorizedto solemnize mar-
riage .

sage .
