CHAPTER 1309 .
AN ACT to incorporate the London Land and Immigration Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That A. W. Harman ,Archer Harman ,J. H. Stuar
A. G. Stuart, and Fred Hager , their successors and as-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 947
signs,are hereby createda body -politicand corporate,by
the name of the London Land and Immigration Com-
pany ; and by that name may contract and be contracted
with,sue and be sued,plead and be impleaded,answer
and defend ,in all courts and places as a natural person ;
and may have a common seal, and alter the same at
pleasure; and have allother lawful powers usual and in-
cident to corporations, and which may be requisiteand
necessaryforcarrying out the objectsand purposes for
which it is created and established .
§2 . That the capitalstock of said company shallbe
divided into shares of not less than five nor more than
fiftydollars each , as may be fixed by the board of di-
rectors, and may consist of any number of shares not
exceeding three hundred thousand , as may be decided
on by said board of directors, and may be subscribed
for by individuals or corporations upon such terms and
conditions as may be stipulatedin books of subscription
to be opened by said corporators or a majority there-
of; and the stockholders in said company shall,once in
each year ,electa board of directorsof not lessthan three
nor more than nine in number ,and when so elected,shall
elect of their number a president,vice president, treas-
urer , and secretary of the said company ,who shallcon-
tinue in officeuntiltheirsuccessorsare elected and quali-
fied; and in cases of vacancy in any of said offices,the
board of directorsshall have power to fillany and all
vacancies by appointment ; and the president and board
of directors may adopt by laws ,rules and regulations,in
which they shall provide for the transfer of stock and
the form of proxies to vote the same in stockholders'
meetings , and mode of calling such meetings , and the
transaction of business at such meetings , and amend or
repeal the same at pleasure; and shallhave the manage-
ment ,government ,and control of the property and affairs
of said company ; and may create such officesas may be
proper in transactingthe business of said corporation.
Said directorsshallbe stockholdersin said company ,and
a majority of saidboard shallbe a quorum to transact

948 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
business. Said board may create an executive committee
of said board , and prescribeitspowers and duties.
§3 . That said company may purchase ,hold , and sell,
and convey by deed executed by itspresident and secre-
tary,realestatein Kentucky and elsewhere,and buy and
sellsuch real estate on commission ; may encourage im-
migrationby the circulationof maps ,books,pamphlets,
and circulars, or other printed matter ; may make con-
tractsfor the settlement of colonies of emigrants on any
land itmay acquire or control, and for that purpose may
lay off and plat towns ,districts,or villagesin any county
of this Commonwealth ; may loan money or negotiable
paper in any manner not prohibitedby law . In sales
made by it said company may receive in payment there-
for current money ,stocks,or bonds of other corporations,
or the payment due iton any and all sales may be upon
time , evidenced by billsof exchange or promissory notes,
and may secure the payment of debts due itupon sales of
itspropertyby reservinga bindinglienupon the property
sold and conveyed by it; and itmay secure the payment
of any debts due it by mortgage ; and the company may
issue itscoupon bonds and secure the payment thereof
by executing one or more mortgages or deeds of truston
itscorporate property and franchisesto one or more trus-
tees for the common benefit of the holders of the bonds :
Provided, The said bonds shall not exceed the amount of
capitalstock authorized herein. Said company may sell
timber on any of itslands,or manufacture the same into
lumber , and all articlesof wooden -ware , and sellsame ;
and may build railroads and tramways from itslands to
riversand to other linesof railroad; and may operate any
mines ,or mine minerals contained in any lands itmay at
any time own or control,and selltheoreorproductstaken
therefrom; and may operatemillsforsawing or manufac-
turinglumber; and may alsoerectand operate furnaces
for the reduction of ore , and manufacture said ore into
articles of use , and sell said ore or articles.
§ 4. That said company shallestablishitsprincipal
officeas the board of directors may determine upon , and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 949
branch offices and agencies in such towns or cities as said
board may deem necessary. The corporatorsherein
named shall have and exercise all the powers and func-
tions of the board of directorsof said company untilsaid
board shall be elected and is organized .
§5 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 3 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 1310 .
AN ACT to incorporateSupreme Council Laborers' Mutual Union .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That John W. Hunter,Albert Montgomery ,Wm .
Briggs , Edward White ,Albert Harris, Thomas Price,T.
B. Brown , and their associates and successors , be , and
they are hereby ,created a body -politic,to be known by
the name ,style,and titleof the Supreme Council Labor-
ers'Mutual Union ; and by such name and titleshallhave
perpetualsuccession,and be capable in law of suing and
being sued ,pleading and being impleaded ; and of pur-
chasing , leasing,holding, and granting, in its corporate
name ,property real, personal, and mixed ; of instituting
grand and subordinate branches of said union as itmay
see fit, under such laws ,rules,and regulations as the cor-
poration may enact ,not in conflictwith the laws of this
State or of the United States .
§2 . The objects of the corporation shall be to unite
fraternallyallpersons who labor for a livelihood; to give
all possible material aid in its power to itsmembers ,by
encouraging each other in business and assistingeach
other to obtain employment ; to promote benevolence and
charity by creating a permanent fund for the support of
itsmembers when overtaken by sickness or infirmity,and
for their decent interment after death .
§3 . The said supreme council shall have a common
seal for the making and delivering of all legal acts and
proceedings , with power to break or alter the same at
pleasure .

950 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. The saidsupreme councilmay provideforholding
annual meetings at such time and place as a majorityof
itsvoting members may select.
§5 . The private property of the members of the cor-
poration shallbe exempt from corporate debts.
§ 6. Said supreme council shallprovide for the election
of such officersas itmay deem necessary to transact the
businessof thecorporationand to furtheritsobjects,who
shallhold officeuntiltheirsuccessorsare duly elected and
installed into office .
§ 7. This act to take effect from and be in force after
itspassage .
Approved May 5, 1884.
CHAPTER 1311 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Hickman , Fulton, Crittenden, Anderson , Mc-
Cracken ,and Graves counties, amending and reducing into one the acts
relatingto working roads in said counties.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The county judges of the Hickman ,Fulton ,Crit-
tenden ,Anderson , McCracken , and Graves county courts
shall divide all of the roads in said counties into pre-
citats and ,as often as may be necessary, appoint a sur-
vevor in each precinct, whose duty it shallbe to superin-
tend the road therein , and see that the same is worked
and kept in such repair as the agreement of the con-
tractor therein with the county court requires. No sur-
veyor shallbe allowed , unless by consent of the court, to
resign such place under two years , except he remove
from the precinct. Any surveyor may be removed at any
time by said court.
§2 . The clerk of the county court shall, within ten
days afterthe appointment of a surveyorof a road,de-
livera copy of the order to the sheriffof the county,
containinga fulland complete descriptionof the pre-
cinct,and take his receipttherefor. The sheriff, within
fifteendays thereafter,shalldelivera copy of the order,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 951
and a descriptionof the precinct,to the surveyor,and to
return the original to the clerk's officeof said county
courts, with the time of the service indorsed , which shall
be evidence of the fact stated therein . The clerk shall
post up in his office, once in every year, a listof the
names of allof the surveyors in the counties,designating
the precinct of each surveyor. The clerks or sheriffsfail-
ing to perform the duties prescribed in this section shall
be fined two dollars and a half for each neglect .
§3 . Each surveyor of a public road shallbe furnished
with a copy of the contractforthe working of the road
in his precinct. He shallreport to the county judge any
failureupon the part of the contractor therein. He shall
certifyto the county judge when the contract has been
properlycomplied with. He shallbe exempt from jury
service, and from poll-tax for road purposes , only during
his continuance in said office. For a failure to discharge
any of his duties prescribed by this act, said surveyor
shall be fined therefor not less than five nor more than
fifteendollarsfor each offense,to be recovered by war-
rant in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ;
and the quarterlycourt shallhave exclusivejurisdiction
of all the proceedings against surveyors of roads .
§4 . The countyjudges shall,at thecourt-house door in
said county sites, on the firstMonday in April in each
year , and such other times as he shall find necessary, let
out the working and keeping in repair the public roads
in said counties to the lowest and best bidder for such
length of time as he may choose , not exceeding his
own term of office, after due advertisements of two
insertionsin the county paper,ifone be published,and
by a printednotice posted up for at leastfifteendays
before such letting,at the voting place in each precinct
in the county : Provided, That the fund raised by the
levy court under this act shall be sufficientto work all
of the public roads in said county ; and if not sufficient,
then it shall be used at such places and for such roads
as the county court may think best,having due regard
to the public good and the wants of the differentparts
-of the county .

952 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§5 . Each contractor shall execute to the Commor--
wealth a bond ,with good security,to be approved by the
county judges ,in double the amount he isto receive for his
contract,forthe faithfulperformance of the said contract ;
and said bonds shallbe filedat the next regular term of the
said county courts afterthe letting,which shall be noted
of record,and the bonds kept on fileby the clerkof the
court in hisofficein bundles , marked " contractors'bonds ;'
and certifiedcopies of said bonds shallbe competent as
evidence ; and the county judge shalldeliverto each sur-
veyor a copy of the bond of the contractor on the road
in the surveyor'sprecinct. Any contractorwho shallfail,
in whole or in part,to complete his contract within the
time and in the manner prescribedtherein,or who ,having
contracted to keep any part of the public roads in good
order for traveling or hauling ,or any bridge or culvert in
good repair,and who shall failto do so,shall,for every
failure, be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon
conviction shall be fined not less than ten nor more than
one hundred dollars .
§ 6. All fines assessed under this act shall be collected
as other fines, and shall inure to the benefit of the road
fund of said counties, and shallbe paid out by the sheriff
under the orders of the county judge .
§ 7. The county judges shallprovide work on the roads
of the counties,or on the streetsof towns ,for such per-
sons convicted of penal offenses as may ,by order of the
proper authorities,be required to work on roads or streets,
or to work out fines assessed against them on the roads
or streets,under the provisionsof general or special laws ;
and may , when necessary, put a ball and chain upon any
such persons to prevent their escape ; and if necessary
may employ some suitable person to oversee and guard
them while at work .
§ 8. The county judges shallletout the construction or
repairingof the bridgesin Hickman ,Fulton,Crittenden,
Anderson , McCracken , and Graves counties not included
in the agreement of any contractor,or the levy courts
may ,in theirdiscretion.appoint a special commissioner

LAWS OF KENTUCKY 953
to letout any bridge or bridges, and provide for his com-
pensation therefor; and such county judge or special
commissioner shall be held liable for any failure in re-
gard to any bridgeso letby them respectively; and the
court may requirebond , with surety, from the special
cemmissioner .
§ 9. That alldelinquent capitationtax-payers of Hick-
man , Fulton , Crittenden, Anderson , McCracken , and
Graves counties shall be required to work out their taxes
at such times on the roads in theirseveral precincts as
the surveyors thereof may require, for which work such
delinquents shall have creditat the rate of one dollar for
each fullday'swork performed by him . For failingor
refusing to comply with the orders of said surveyors,
when notified, all such delinquents are made liable to
the penaltiesnow imposed by law forpersons failingor
refusing to work on roads . The several surveyors are
authorized and empowered to collectthe taxes due from
such delinquents in their several precincts,if they shall
elect to pay the same rather than work on the roads,
and the same shallbe held and applied as a part of the
road fund , and reported and paid over by said surveyor
to the county judge ,taking and filingwith the county
clerk his receipt therefor; and said clerk shall give to
said surveyor a certifiedcopy of said receipt, which shall
have the same effect in his hands as the original .
§ 10. The county courts of Hickman , Fulton , Critten-
den , Anderson , McCracken , and Graves counties may
open roads not exceeding sixtyfeet in width ,or may in-
crease the width of established roads to sixty feet, any
thing in the general laws to the contrary notwithstanding .
§11 . That the courts of the justices of the peace in
and for Hickman , Fulton , Crittenden, Anderson , Mc-
Cracken , and Graves counties shallhave jurisdictioncon-
current with the quarterlycourtsof allproceedings against
road contractors under this act; and it shall be the duty
of any justiceor the countyjudge,upon hisown knowl-
edge,or upon complaint of any surveyor,or upon infor-
mation ,under oath,of any citizen,that any part of the

954 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
public road is out of repair,to issuehis warrant against
the delinquent contractor,returnable to his own or some
other justice'scourt,or to the Hickman , Fulton ,Critten-
den , Anderson ,McCracken , and Graves quarterlycourts;
and upon the execution and return thereof such proceed-
ings shallbe had as are had in other Commonwealth cases.
In proceedings under thisact the defendant shallhave no
right to claim his district; and both the Commonwealth
and defendant shallhave the right of appeal from justices'
courts to the quarterlycourts and from the quarterly court
to the circuit court .
§ 12. It shall be the duty of the county attorneys to
prosecuteallwarrants under thisact,and forsuch prose-
cutions he shallreceive twenty -five per cent. of thefines
recovered , and upon his failurethe court may appoint
some attorney in his stead,who shallreceive saidcommis-
sion for his services . All fines assessed under this act
shall be collected as other fines and shall inure to the ben-
efit of the road fund of Hickman , Fulton , Crittenden ,
Anderson , McCracken , and Graves counties,and shallbe
paid out by the sheriffunder orders of the county judges .
§ 13. It shallbe unlawful for the county judges ,county
attorneys,sheriffs, collectorsof the revenue ,or any sur-
veyor ,to become ,directlyor indirectly,interestedin any
contract forworking roads or building or repairingbridges;
and it shallbe unlawful for eitherof said officersto buy
or become interested in any claim growing out of said
work or contract. For a violation of this section, either
of said officersshall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and ,
upon indictment and conviction thereof, shall be fined
not less than fiftynor more than five hundred dollars.
This section shall be given in charge to the grand jury
by the judges of the Hickman , Fulton , Crittenden, Ander-
son ,McCracken , and Graves circuitcourts.
§14 . Any person who shall willfullyor negligently
injure,destroy, or obstruct any of the said public roads
or bridges,or any of the culvertsor ditches in said roads ,
he shallbe fined not lessthan fivenor more than twenty-
five dollars , to be recovered in like manner as the fines

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 955
prescribed herein for surveyors, and shall also be liable
in double damages to the county ,or any person aggrieved
or injured,to be recovered in any court having jurisdic-
tionof the amount . It shallbe the duty of the survey-
orsto reportpromptly to the county judges any violation
of this section .
§ 15. The county court,a majority of the justicesagree-
ing,shall, on some regular county court day , assess a tax
of not more than fifteen cents on each one hundred dol-
lars' worth of property assessed for revenue purposes ,
and a capitation tax not exceeding two dollarson each
person in said counties required by the general law to
work on roads .
§ 16. The ad valorem and capitationtax provided for in
section fifteen of this act shall be collected as other rev-
enue due the counties is collected , and under the same
obligationsby the collectingofficer, and shallbe paid
out by said collectingofficerto road contractors on the
order of the judges of the Hickman , Fulton , Crittenden,
Anderson , McCracken , and Graves county courts.
§ 17. Chapter ninety-four of the General Statutes is
hereby adopted and made part of thisact,except when
it comes in conflictwith the provisions of this act, and
all other road laws for Hickman , Fulton , Crittenden , An-
derson ,McCracken , and Graves counties are hereby re-
pealed .
§ 18. There shallbe a poll opened in each voting pre-
cinct in said counties at the August election, one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-four,prepared as pollsare
prepared in State elections; and the officersof the elec-
tions then held shall ask each of said voters," Do you
vote for or against the new road law ? " and the vote
shall be so recorded; and if a majority of those voting in
said election vote for said law , then , in that county , it
shall be adopted ; if against said law , then it shall not
be adopted in said county .
§19 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afterthe first
day of January,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
five .
Approved May 1, 1884 .

956 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1312 .
AN ACT to establishand promote drainage in Henderson county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 .That whenever any owner or owners of lands which
would be benefited by drainage , which cannot be accom-
plishedinthe bestand cheapestmanner withoutaffecting
other lands,shalldesiresuch drainage, he, she,or they
may apply for such drainage by petition to the county
court of the county in which the lands of the petitioner
or petitionersare situated. The petitionshalldescribe
as accurately as practicablethe tracts of land of the pe-
titioneror petitioners,and of all others whose lands are
to be affectedby such proposed drainage ,giving the num-
ber of acres owned by each , so far as known ,together
with the names of owners thereof , if known , and if un-
known , shall so state. Such petitionshall be sufficient
to give the court jurisdictionover the lands described
therein,and power to fixa lienthereon,ifthey are de-
scribed as belonging to the person who appears to be the
owner according to the last assessor'sreport, or record of
transfer, as may appear upon the records in the officeof
the county clerk of the county of Henderson , where the
same is situated. Itshallalso statethat,in the opinion
of the petitioner or petitioners, either that the public
health will be improved ,or that one or more of the pub-
lichighways of the county ,or streetor streetsof a town
or city,willbe benefitedby the proposed drainage,or that
the proposed work willbe of public utility; and itshall
state,generally,the method by which it is believed such
drainage can be accomplished in the cheapest and best
manner , and the beliefof the petitioneror petitioners
that the costs,damages , and expenses of such drainage
will be less than the benefits which will result to the
owners of the lands likelyto be benefitedthereby. Such
petitionshallbe verifiedby the affidavitof some peti-
tioner or owner of real estate in the vicinity of the
lands .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 957
§2 . That whenever the petitioneror petitionersshall
filehis or theirpetitionin theclerk'sofficeof the county
court ,he shallfixand note thereon the day set fordocket-
ing thereof,and if it appear to the court that notice has
been given of the filingof said petition by posting up
notices thereof, in three public places near the line of the
proposed work ,and at the court-house door in the county
or counties in which said lands are situated,not lessthan
twenty days before the day noted thereon , and set as the
day for the docketing the same ,the court shall order the
same placed on the docket of said court as an action pend-
ing therein. Every person owning land shall have ten
days aftersaid petitionisdocketed to filewith said court
any demurrer , remonstrance , or objection he may have
to the form of said petition, or as to why the commis-
sioners or any of them , on account of their interestin
said work , or kinship to any person whose lands are af-
fected thereby,should not act in said matter ; aftersaid
ten (10) days have elapsedthe court shallconsidersaid
remonstrance ,and ifitfind said petition defective,shall
dismissthesame at thecost of the petitioneror petition.
ers,unless the same be amended ,which may be done ; and
when the court deems said petitionsufficient,itshallmake
an order referring the same to three commissioners to be
appointed by the court,who shallbe freeholdersand in
no way interestedin the proposed work ; and not related
by blood or marriage to any one of the parties who may
be interestedin said work ; and unless some valid objection
is made to one or more of said commissioners ,in which
case the court may appoint some other qualifiedperson or
persons to act as commissioner or commissioners in said
action ,which order shall be final, all objections to the
petition or the acting of eitherof the commissioners , not
made within said ten days , shallbe deemed waived . In
the order of referringsaid matter to said commissioners ,
the court shallfix a time and place forthe meeting of said
commissioners,and a time when they shallreport. The
clerk shall deliver to them a copy of such petition and
order ,and they shall meet accordingly; they shall make

958 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
personalinspectionof the landsdescribedin the petition,
and ofallotherlandslikelyto be affectedby the proposed
work , and consider-
First. Whether the drainageproposed ispracticable.
Second. Whether ,when accomplished, it will improve
the publichealth or benefitany publichighway in the
county ,or streetof a town or city,or be of public utility.
Third. Whether the costs, damages , and expenses of
effectingthe drainage will be lessthan the benefitsto the
owners of the lands likely to be benefited by the pro-
posed drainage ; and if they find any of these inquiries
in the negative,they shall make a report of such finding
to the court,and thereupon the petitionshallbe dismissed
at the cost of the petitioners; but if they find otherwise ,
they shallproceed,and definitelydeterminethe bestand
cheapest method of drainage ,the termini and route,lo-
cation and character of the proposed method ,and fixthe
same by metes and bounds ,courses and distances, and
description; estimate the cost thereof, assess the benefits
or injury,as the case may be,to each separate tractof
landtobe affectedthereby,and to easements thereinheld
by railway or other corporations, and make report to the
court as directed under oath; they may determine that
the method of drainage shallbe by removing obstructions
from a natural water -course, by deepening , widening ,
straightening,or changing its drain, open or covered , or
by making levees, or by any or all such methods com-
bined . Any two commissioners may act without the pres-
ence or concurrence of the third. The county surveyor
shall be the engineer, and make the necessary surveys :
Provided, That the court,for any valid or satisfactory
reason ,may appoint some competent civilengineer to act
insteadofthecountysurveyor,insuch case: And provided,
That in allcases when lands are named in said report as
affectedby such proposed work ,which are not named in
the petitionhereinbefore provided for,the court shallfixa
time forhearing the report; and it shallbe the duty of the
petitionerto give ten days'notice to the owners of such
lands of the filingof such report,by posting up written

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 959
1
or printed notices in three public places on or near the
lands which are named in such reportas affected,and
which were not named in the originalpetition, and near
the lineof the proposed work ,and one at thecourt-house
door of the county in which such lands are situated,
which notice shall name the time set for hearing such
report; and in such case the court shall,ifnecessary,con-
tinue the hearing of the report as to the other lands un-
tilsuch notice has been given as above provided . The
same proceedings shallbe had in regard to such report as
if all the lands mentioned therein , and the owners there-
of, had been named in the originalnotice of the filingof
the petition.
§3 . That ,upon the making of such report to the court,
ten days shallbe allowed to any owner of lands affected
by the work proposed,to remonstrateagainstthe report,
and any such remonstrance shallbe verifiedby affidavit,
and may be for any or allof the following causes :
First. That the report of the commissioners is not ac-
cording to law .
Second . By any person or persons whose lands are
assessed for benefitsthat the damages assessed to any
other specifiedtractare exorbitant.
Third. By any person or personswhose lands are as-
sessed for benefitsthat his,her,or theirspecifiedlands
are assessed too much , as compared with other lands
assessed as benefited,specifying the same .
Fourth . By any person whose lands are assessed that
some tract,specifying the same ,is assessed too low ac-
cording to the benefitsreceived by it.
Fifth. By any person whose landsare assessedthatthe
same are not affectedby , nor will the same be benefited
if,the proposed work be constructed.
Sixth. By a person to whom damages are assessed that
the same are inadequate .
Seventh. That it is not practicableto accomplish the
proposed drainage , without an expense exceeding the
aggregate benefits.

960 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Eighth. That the proposed work will neitherimprove
the publichealth,nor benefitany publichighway of the
county ,nor be of public utility.
Ninth . That the work decided upon is not sufficientto
properly drain the land to be affected. If the court be
of the opinion that the firstcause of the remonstrance
above enumerated exists , it shall direct the commission-
ers to amend and perfect their report,or the court may ,
in itsdiscretion, set aside said report,refer the matter
back to said commissioners , or other qualified commis-
sioners, for a new report. In making said order, the
court shall fix a time and place of their meeting , and
when they shall report, to which report remonstrances
may be filedin the same manner and for the same causes
as if itwas the firstreport; and the same shallbe tried
and disposed of in the same manner . Remonstrances
founded on the second , third, fourth, fifth, sixth, seventh ,
eighth,or ninth causes of remonstrance shallbe triedby
the court without a jury; and iffound for the remon-
strant upon the second ,third,fourth, fifth, or sixth causes
of remonstrance ,the court shall modify the assessment
and equalizethe same as justicemay require, and as
thus modified and equalized it shall stand and be ad-
judged valid; and if the finding be against the remon-
strance on the second ,third, fourth, fifth, or sixth causes,
the assessment made by the commissioners shallbe con-
firmed, and the order of confirming shall be final and
conclusive. Ifthe findingbe in support of the remon-
strance on the seventh or eighth causes of remonstrance
above enumerated, the proceedings shallbe dismissed.
If the finding be in favor of the remonstrance on the
ninth cause ,the court shallrequire the commissioners to
review the matter ,and make a new report,which , upon
the said ninth cause , shall be final. If there be no re-
monstrance,or ifthe findingand judgment shallbe in
all respects against the remonstrance , the court shall
make an orderdeclaringthe proposed work established,
and approving the assessments, and shall direct some
one of the commissioners to construct and make the pro-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 96
posed work ; said commissioners so appointed shallgive
bond to the satisfactionof the court forthe faithfulper-
formance of his duties. When the finding of the court
isagainst the remonstrance for any cause,or when in
favor of the remonstrance on the second, third, fourth,
fifth, or sixth causes , and the assessments of benefits or
damages is not changed fifteenper centum in favor of
theremonstrant,he shallpay the costsoccasionedby the
remonstrance ; and in all other cases the costs shall be
paid as part of the expenses of constructionof the pro-
posed work ,except thatwhen the remonstrance is sus-
tained on the seventh or eighth causes, the costs shallbe
paid by the originalpetitioneror petitioners.
§4 . The commissioner charged with the execution of
the work ,as providedin the lastpreceding section,shall
proceed to have the same constructed as ordered. He
shallpay the costs not otherwise adjudged ,and expenses
incident to establishingthe same and incurred in prepa-
rationof reports,and any expense which the petitioner
may have incurred in the preparation or presentation of
his petition, and such other expenses as the court shall
deem a proper charge upon the funds in the hands of
such commissioner and the damages assessed and the
cost of construction . He shall assess, from time to time ,
upon the lands benefited,ratably upon the amount of ben-
efitsas adjudged by the court,such sums of money as may
be necessary therefor,and as have been determined ac-
cording to the directions of sections two and three hereof ,
not exceeding the whole benefits so adjudged upon any
one tract,and require the same to be paid in installments
not exceeding twenty per cent.per month ,at such time
as he shall fix, after thirtydays ' notice thereof, to be
given by personal notice to the owner of such land ,or by
one publication in a newspaper published in the county
in which the lands benefited are situated, stating when
and where such payments shallbe made . He may divide
the work into such parts as he may deem best,and letthe
same to thebestadvantage,insuch parts,or altogether,as
LOC . L., VOL . II- 61

962 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
he may determine,and contractforthe constructionthere-
of. He shallcollectthe assessments so made by him and
apply thesame ashereinprovided; and forthepurpose of
making such collections,if not paid as above required,he
shallhave allthepower to distrainand sellpersonalprop-
erty which isconferred by law upon sheriffsforthe collec-
tion of taxes, and for so doing shallbe allowed the same
fees and costs. He may ,if he so determine , bring suitin
the name of the State of Kentucky forhis use as commis-
sioner,in any courtof competent jurisdiction,to enforce
a lienupon any tractor tractsof land forthe amount
so assessed by him ; and alljudgments obtained in such
cases may include reasonable attorney'sfees for services
in prosecuting the same .
§5 . The filingof the petition shallbe deemed notice
of the pendency of the proceedings to allpersons whose
lands are named in the petition, and the filing of the
report of the commissioners locating the work ,and fixing
the amount of the assessments,shallbe deemed notice of
the pendency of the proceedings to all persons whose
lands are named therein , and not named in the original
petition; and the amount of the assessments , as made or
approved and confirmed by the court,shallbe a lien up-
on the lands so assessedfrom the time of filingthe peti-
tion, except where lands are omitted in the petition and
afterwards assessed and reported by the Commissioners ;
and as to such lands, the assessment shallbe a lienfrom
the date of filingthe report of the commissioners . The
commissioner charged with the construction of the work
shall, as soon as may be after he has been directed to
construct the work ,make out a notice,wherein he shall
state that the work has been establishedby the court ,
also the several assessments to the several tracts of land
as the same have been finallyconfirmed by the court
and cause the same to be recorded in the office of the
clerk of the county where any such lands may be situated.
§ 6. Such commissioner shallkeep an accurate account
of all moneys collectedby him on account of the work ,
and of allpayments made by him on account thereof,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 963
and shalltake vouchers for such payments . He shall
also keep an exact account of the time occupied by
him in the performance of his duties. He shall, as
often as once in six months , make fullreport of said
matters, under oath, to the court ; and the court shall
allow him for his services not exceeding three dollars
per day for the time actuallyand necessarilyemployed.
He shall at all times be under the control and direc-
tion of the court, and shallobey such directions; and
forfailureso to do,shallforfeithis compensation,and be
dealt with summarily as for a civilcontempt , and may
also be removed from officeby the court. Suit may also
be brought upon his bond in the name of the State, and
the amount recovered shallbe applied to the construction
of the work . The court may , at any time when occa-
sion may require,direct another one of the commission-
ers to proceed with the construction of the work ; and
may , at any time ,discharge therefrom the commissioner
appointed thereto.
§ 7. This act shallbe liberallyconstrued, and the col-
lectionof assessments made under itsprovisionsshall
not be defeated by reason of any informalityor irregu-
larityin the proceedings: Provided, They shallshow the
court had jurisdictionto render the judgment or judg.
ments confirming and establishingthe assessment of ben-
efitsand injuries. Nor shallany personbe permittedto
take advantage of any error,defect,or informality,unless
the person complaining thereofisdirectlyaffectedthere-
by , at any stage of the proceedings ; and if the court
should deem itjust to releaseany person,or modify his
assessments or liability, it shall in no manner affectthe
rights or liabilityof any other person . Any person inter-
ested may filewith the court a supplemental petition,
showing that lands not mentioned in the original report
are affected,as he believes,by such drainage; in which
case the court shallrequiresuch person to give such
notice as itmay deem proper and sufficientto the per-
sons affected thereby, and shall refer the same to the
commissionersfora report,and any and allproceedings.

964 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
may be had thereon,and orders and decrees made there-
in,the same as ifitwere an originalpetition; but the
proceedingsthereinshallnot affectthe originalpetition,
unless the court shall, for good reason ,order the same
consolidatedand made a part of the orginalpetition,in
which case the court may make such orders therein as
are herein authorized .
§ 8. Whenever any such drainage will benefitany
publichighway , the value of such benefitmay be as-
sessedagainstthe county, and paid for out of the road
fund of the county,providedthe courtdeems itproper to
so order; said work to be done under the management
and direction of the commissioner in charge of the work .
Assessments on account of improvements of streets in
incorporated towns and citiesshallbe against such towns
or cities. Any person whose lands are assessed under
this act,for the construction of any work ,may apply to
such commissioner for and have leave to work out his
assessment, upon such fairand reasonable terms as may
be agreed upon by the commissioner and the party so
assessed .
§ 9. After the constructionof any such work ,itshallbe
the duty of the owner orowners of such lands as were as-
sessedforconstructingthe same tokeep the same open and
in good repair,freefrom such obstructionsor fillingup as
may hinder free drainage , as contemplated in the build-
ing and making of such drain or ditch; and the amount
paid by each shallbe in ratableproportionas was as-
sessed in the originalassessment ; and if any one or more
interestedas above should refuseto pay his or her rata-
ble proportionforrepairingthe same ,or in the event it
should be necessary to widen or deepen the said ditch or
drain,and if any one or more shallrefuse to pay his or
her ratable proportion,then , and in eithercase ,any one
or more of the partiesdesiringto have the work done
may proceed,by petition,to have itdone and completed
as providedin former sectionsof thisactforthe original
laying out and constructing of such ditch or drain : Pro-
vided,however,That if such repairs or removal of obstruc-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 965
i
tions shall have been rendered necessary by the act or
negligence of the owner or occupant ,or the employe or
agent of either of any land,then the costs thereof shall
be assessedby the commissioners against such lands alone.
§ 10. For theirservices under the third section of this
act,the commissioners ,engineer , and the chain -men , ax-
men ,and rod-men by them employed, and the commis-
sioner charged with the construction of the work , shall
receive such reasonable compensation as the court may
allow ,paid out of funds raised for itsconstruction.
§ 11. Any person who shallobstruct,injure, or destroy
any work constructed in pursuance of any law of this
State forthe drainage of lands,or shallobstructa natural
water -course below the point at which any such ditch or
drain enters the same , shall, on conviction, be fined in
any sum not less than ten nor more than one hundred
dollars, to which may be added imprisonment in the
county jailfor a period not exceeding three months ;
and shall also be liable for damages to such person or
persons as may have suffereddamages thereby .
§ 12. In addition to the notices hereinbefore provided
for, it shall be the duty of the clerk,upon the filingof
the commissioners'reportpreliminaryto the finaljudg-
ment ,to issueprocessagainstthe owners of the lands to
be affected,to show cause why the said reportshallnot
be confirmed ,and to make such orders as to non -residents
and persons under disabilitiesas requiredby the Civil
Code of Practice in actions against them in the circuit
court .
§ 13. This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved May 1, 1884.

right to redeem said bonds at any time before maturity

§2 . Before any issue of bonds the board of directors

CHAPTER 1315 .
AN ACT to createa new votingprecinct in Bullittcounty .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby created a new voting pre-
cinct,in Bullittcounty , the boundary of which shall be
as follows,to wit : Beginning at the Jefferson and Bul-
littcounty line,at Floyd'sFork,to Joel Balee'sfarm on
the south bank ; thence across in a straightline to Jerry
Stalling'sfarm to said fork,down said fork again to the
mouth of Brooks'run, up said run to G. C. Shanklin's
and John Rogers ' line, next with said line to S. B. Sum-
mers ' and Rogers '; thence with said line to J. Brooks ',at
the Blue Lick pike; thence with said Brooks ' line to the
Louisville and Nashville Railroad ; thence with the rail-
road to the Jeffersonand Bullittcounty line; thence with
said lineto the place of beginning : including all within
said line, except the farms and houses of G. C. Shanklin
and S. B. Summers .
§ 2. Zoneton shallbe the voting place in said precinct,
and Zoneton the name of the precinct; and allvoters in
said boundary , including those whose names are men-
tioned,with the exceptions mentioned in section one ,and
those who may hereafter reside where those whose names
are mentioned now do , can vote in all elections either at
Zoneton or intheprecinctinwhich theyvotedbeforethe
passage of thisact.
§3 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 3, 1884.

CHAPTER 1319 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Gravel Switch and Liberty Turnpike Road
Company , in Marion and Casey counties.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a company be,and itishereby,incorporated,
under the name and styleof the Gravel Switch and Lib-
erty Turnpike Road Company ; and by said name and
styleshall have perpetual succession; and may sue and
be sued ,plead and be impleaded ; and as such is hereby
vestedwith all the powers enjoyed by similarcorpora-
tionsin the Commonwealth forthe purpose of construct-
ing a turnpike.
§2 . The followingpersonsare appointed commission-
ersto securesubscriptionof stockin said company : Dr.
D. C. May , M. W. Penn , Milton May , and Geo . A. Wil-
liams ; and said persons are authorized to open books and
secure subscriptionsat any time and place they may
deem proper .
§3. The capitalstockof saidcompany shallbe divided
into shares of one hundred dollars each ; and the entire
capital stock shall not exceed the amount necessary to
construct said road and toll-houses , at which tollmay be
collectedat the rates allowed by general law .
§4 . When a sufficient amount of stock shall be sub-
scribed to justifythe beginning of said road , the com-
missioners, or such of them as may act, shall,at such
time and place as they deem proper ,call a meeting of
the stockholders, and hold an electionfor a president
and five directors,to hold their officesfor one year , or
untiltheirsuccessors are qualified.
§ 5. The width of said road shall not be under fifteen
feet or over thirty feet; the material or macadamized
part shall not be less than twelve feet; the steepness of
the grade shallbe regulatedby the company .
§ 6. That in the event the said company may not be
able to procure the right of way or material for the con-
structionof theirroad,they are authorizedand empow-
ered to apply to the county court for a writ of ad quod

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 971
damnum ,and have the right of way and material for the
construction of the road condemned by a jury as now
provided by general law for roads and passways .
§ 7. That it shall be the duty of the president and
directorsto appoint one of theirnumber treasurer,who
shall execute bond , with approved security,and shallbe
responsibleforallmoneys of the company subjectto the
order of thepresident; and shallalsokeep an account of
the receiptsand expenditures of said company .
§ 8. That the county court of Casey county are hereby
authorized to subscribe to the capital stock of said com-
pany , in an amount not exceeding seven hundred dollars
per mile,should they deem ittheirdiscretionnecessary
to be done .
§ 9. That said road is to be constructed from Gravel-
Switch ,in Marion county , to Liberty,in Casey county , to
be constructed the most practical route between the two
points .
§ 10. That this act take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved May 3 , 1884 .

CHAPTER 1322 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Locust Fork Turnpike Company , in Scott.
county .

of Kentucky :

name of the Locust Fork Turnpike Company ; and by
that name shallhave perpetual succession; may sue and
be sued,plead and be impleaded,and is hereby vested
with allthe powers conferredon similarcorporationsin
this Commonwealth .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 973
§2 . The following persons are appointed corporators
to receive subscriptions of stock to said company , to-
wit : J. W. Duvall,Sam .Kemper ,William Graves,John
Henry ,and they are hereby authorizedto open books
and receive subscriptionsat such time and place as they

ceed the amount necessary to construct a turnpike road

sage .
Approved May 3, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1327 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Boyle County Telephone Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Samuel G. Boyle , Chas .E. Kincaid , Samuel
D. Bottom ,and their associates, successors,and assigns,
be , and they are hereby , created a body -corporate and
politic, under the name and style of " The Boyle County
Telephone Company ;" and by that name shallhave per-
petual succession,with power to adopt and use a com-
mon seal, to change the same at pleasure; to contract
and be contracted with , to sue and be sued , and to have
all the rightsand privilegesof a natural person .
§2 . The capital stock shall be twenty -five thousand
dollars, divided into shares of twenty -five dollars each ,
which may be increased at any time by a majority vote
of the stockholders. A book for the subscriptionof stock
in saidcompany may be opened at any time afterthe pas-
sage of thisact by the persons named herein,or any two
of them , which book shallremain open untilat least fifty
shares be subscribed, when an organization may be ef-
fected as ishereinafterprovided ; and aftersaid organiza-
tion itshallremain open at the option of the company .

976 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . When fiftyshares shallhave been subscribed,the
said subscribers,or a majority of them ,may meet forthe
purpose of electinga president and fivedirectorsto man-
age the affairsof the company . Persons having sub-
scribed as stockholders shall be entitled to one vote for
each share of stock,and may castthe same in person or
by proxy upon any questionthat may come before said
president and board of directors,and upon allelections.
The president and board of directors firstelected shall
hold their offices until their successors are elected and
qualified,and a president and five directorsshall annu-
ally thereafterbe elected on the firstMonday in May in
each year , and shallhold their officesfor one year , and
untiltheirsuccessorsshallbe elected and qualified.
§ 4. No person not a stockholder in said company shall
hold any officetherein; nor shallany stockholderwho has
not paid allcalls made by the president and board of di-
rectorson the stock subscribed or held by him vote at any
election or in any matter .
§5 . The private property of the stockholders shallnot
be subject to, nor in any way be liable for,the debts of
the company .
§6 . The business of said company shall be to erect
and maintain and operate a lineof telephone from Dan-
ville, in Boyle county , to any town or towns in said
county, and Springfield, in Washington county, and to
Lebanon ,in Marion county , and to Nicholasville,in Jes-
samine county ,and to any town or towns in any county
adjoining said Boyle county , and between , and to any
points between such towns ; and the privilege shall be
exclusive,and shallcontinue for (20) twenty years .
§7 . The president and board of directors may ,if they
deem best,electa person(who shallbe a stockholder),to
servefora period of not more than one year at a time,
as a secretary and treasurer; and he shallgive bond for
the faithfuldischarge of his duties,and he may be at any
time removed at the pleasure of the said board .
§ 8. The president may call meetings of the board of
directorsat any time as he may think proper. Two di

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 977
rectorswith the president,or three directorsin the ab-
sence of the president,shall constitute a quorum for the
transaction of business. The board of directors may
establishsuch by -laws for the government of the com-
pany as they may deem proper, not inconsistentwith
law. The company ,and the engineers,surveyors,chain-
carriers,and employes thereof,shallhave authorityand
power to enterupon the lands and inclosuresand public
roads through and over which it may be proper to run
said line or lines; and in the event that said company
cannot agree with the owner or owners of the land
through which the line or lines are proposed to be run
as to the compensation to be paid for the use of the
same , then the company shall have power to condemn
such use accordingto law . Any person who shallwill-
fullyor maliciouslyinjure, obstruct, or destroyany of
the property of said company shallbe confinedin the
county jailnot less than three and not more than twelve
months .
§ 9. This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved May 5, 1884.
CHAPTER 1328 .

of Kentucky :

liquors , or any mixture of the same ,within two miles of

CHAPTER 1329 .

a
§1 . That D. Parish, Wm . Williams ,Jacob S. Hughley ,

body-corporate,by the name of Otter Creek and Stoney
River Turnpike Road Company ,withperpetualsuccession,
and fullpower and authority to build,own ,maintain ,and
manage a turnpike road,running from a point near the

pike Road , five miles from Richmond , running to the
mouth of Stoney River branch , in Madison county ; and
may contractand be contractedwith,sue and be sued,pur-
chase,acquire, own , sell,and convey any real or personal
estate,rights or intereststhat may be necessary for the
purposesof saidroad company in buildingand maintain-
ing said road . The capital stock of the company shall
not exceed seven thousand dollars($7,000),dividedinto
shares of one hundred dollars each , and shall entitlethe
owner to one vote upon each share in all elections held
by the stockholders.
§2 . The said corporators,or such of them as shallact,
may open books for the subscriptionof stock, and call
meetings to be held in said county to organize said com-
pany .
§3 . The stockholdersmay , from time to time,adopt
rules and by -laws for holding theirmeetings and for elec-
tions. It shall be the duty of the company to hold a
stockholders' meeting each year ,on the firstSaturday in

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 979
February , for the election of directors; and a special
meeting of the stockholders may be held at any time ,
upon a call of the board and reasonable notice to the
stockholders . In case of a failure to hold said annual
meeting or election upon said day , it shall stand ad-
journed ,from week to week , until such annual meeting
and election shalltake place; and in the meantime the
directors in office shall hold over until their successors
are elected and organized for business . The board of
directors shall consist of not less than five nor more than
seven ,and shall be those receiving the highest number

shall be eligibleas president or directors. Said board
shall electa president; but in his absence may call one
of their own number to preside at their meetings ; and
may appoint or employ a secretary and treasurer, and
such other officers, agents, and employes ,from time to
time , in the business of said company , as deemed neces-
sary; and may dischargethem ,or any of them ,at pleas-
ure; and may fillallvacancies occurringin said board,
or in any of said offices. They may borrow money ,but
the bonded debt shall not exceed one thousand one hun-
dred dollars at any one time ,and at a rate of interestnot
exceeding six per centum per annum ; and may execute
notes or bonds of the company forthe same ; and to secure
the payment thereof, or of any indebtedness, may mort-
gage or pledge allor any part of the propertyor income
of the company . In case of defaultin payment of said
bonds , or any of them , or interestthereon,for twelve
months after maturity thereof, the whole of such bonds
shall thereupon become due , and the holders thereof, or
any of them , may proceed by suit to enforce the lien,
and sellthe property and franchises of the company to
pay , firstthe liensaccordingto theirpriority,and then
the generaldebts of the company ; and the purchaseror
the purchasersof saidpropertyand franchisesshallsuc-
ceed to allthe rightsof the company under the charter,
and may reorganize the company accordingly. The
right of way of said road shall be thirtyfeet wide , ex

980 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
cept at such places as the company may finditbest to
dispense with said width ; but the same shall not be
reduced to less than fifteenfeet upon any part of said
road . If found necessary,the company may proceed by

hundred and ten of the General Statutes , title " Turn-
pike,Gravel,and Plank Roads ," to acquire any land for
such way ,or for rock quarries or toll-houses ; such writ
to be issued from and returned to the county court,which
court shallmake all such orders as may be necessary to
carry out such proceedings , and to have the same to
appear of record in said court.
§ 4. The company may have one half gate upon its
road , and charge and collectthe same rate of tollsas
prescribed and allowed by the charter of the Richmond ,
Otter Creek , and Boonesborough Turnpike Road Com .
pany ; but in no event to exceed the rate fixed by the
General Statutes .
§ 5. This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved May 5 , 1884 .

CHAPTER 1334 .
AN ACT to amend an act to incorporatethe Cincinnati,Green River, and
NashvilleRailroad Company ,approved February twenty -third, one thou-
sand eighthundred and eighty-two .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the firstsection of an act,entitled"An act
to incorporate Cincinnati, Green River, and Nashville
Railroad Company ," approved February twenty-third,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-two ,be amended
as follows,viz: That said company shallhave power to
build, equip,operate, and maintain a railroad, with sin-
gle or double track, with all necessary turn-outs, side-
tracks,switches,and depots,from a point on the Tennes-
see line,in the counties of Allen, Monroe ,or Cumberland ;
thence through any one or all of said counties, and
through any of the followingcounties,namely : Barren,
Metcalfe ,Adair , Green , Taylor , Russell, Casey , Marion ,
Lincoln ,Boyle , Garrard ,Jessamine , Madison , and Clark ,
so as to connect with the Kentucky Central Railroad,or
any other railroad in said counties; and the said com-
pany shall have the right to build branch or branches
from its said railroad through any of the said counties,
and through any of the followingcounties,viz: Wash-
ington,Nelson,Spencer,Bullitt,and Jeffersonto the city
of Louisville; and may ,by and with the consent of the
mayor and common council, extend their road into the
city of Louisville.
§2 . That section second of said act be amended as
follows: that the affairsof said company shallbe man-
aged by a board of nine directors, who shall be elected
annually by the stockholders; that said board of directors
shall elect one of theirnumber president, and may elect
or appoint a secretary and treasurer, and such other offi-
cers and agents and servants as they may deem neces-
sary and proper ; and may take from them such bond ,
with such surety,as they may deem proper. No person
shallhold the officeof directoror president unless he be
a stockholder .

)
§3 . That section four of said act be repealed,and the
following be inserted and substituted in lieu thereof :
That when any lands,right of way ,stone,earth, gravel,

constructing said road or branches cannot ,for want of
agreement with the owner or forother causes, be pur-
chased or obtained, the same may be condemned and
taken by said company as provided by an act,entitled
"An act to prescribe the mode of condemning lands for
the use of railroadand turnpike companies," approved
April the eleventh,eighteenhundred and eightytwo .
§4 . That the present organizationof said company
shallbe deemed legaland valid; and the presentboard
of directorsshallhave the right to provide the manner
and terms of subscribing to the capital stock of said
company .

Approved Mяу 12, 1884.

CHAPTER 1339 .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be the duty of the county judge of

a road commissioner for said county ,who shallhold his
officetwo years,or until his successor is appointed and
qualified, and who shall, before entering upon the dis-
charge of the duties of his office, take an oath in the
Russellcounty court that he willfaithfully, and to the
best of his ability, dischargeallthe dutiesof his office
according to law , and shall, at the same time ,enter into
bond, with good surety, to be approved by said court,
payable to the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,forthe faith-
fulperformance of his duties, and to properly account
forallmoneys and propertythatmay come to his hands
as commissioner aforesaid . Said commissioner shall have
control of all the public roads in said county , except
turnpikesand otherroadsbelongingto incorporatedcom-
panies, and see that allof the public roads are put in
good repair, and kept so during the term of his office.

act,aftertaking the oath of office, and executing bond ,
shallproceed to lay offthe county into convenient road

CHAPTER 1345 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Cumberland River and Tennessee Railroad
Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
§ 1. That J. A. Brents, B. M. Yates ,J. W. Tuttle, T. Z.
Morrow , R. Burnett, G. W. Hurt , and T. V. Stephenson ,
theirassociates and successors,be , and they are hereby ,
created a body -corporate and politic,under the name and
styleof the Cumberland River [and Tennessee] Railroad
Company , with perpetual succession; and shall have
power to contract and be contracted with ,sue and be
sued ; have and use a common seal ; and to have and
exerciseallpowers common to such corporations. They
shallhave power to construct,maintain,and operate a
railway ,with a single or double track, broad or narrow-
gauge ,commencing at a point south of the Cumberland
river, in Pulaski county , and on the Cincinnati Southern
Railway ; thence through Pulaski, Wayne , and Clinton
counties to the Tennessee State line in Clinton county ,
or to some point in Cumberland county on the Tennes-
see State line .
§2. The affairsof saidcompany shallbe managed and
controlledby a board of fivedirectors,to be elected by
the stockholders,in such time and manner as may be pre-
scribedby the by -laws of said company .
§3 . The capital stock of said company shall not ex-
ceed five millions of dollars , to be divided into shares of
one hundred dollars each , and shall be held to be per-
sonal property .
§4 . That where any lands or rightsof way , stone,
earth, gravel, or timber may be required by said com-
pany forthe uses and purposesof constructingtheirroad,

maintaining and operating it,and the same cannot be
obtained by agreement or purchase, the same may be
taken and obtained as provided by the provisions of an
act,entitled"An act to prescribethe mode of condemn-
ing land for the use of railroad and turnpike companies ,"
approved April eleven,one thousand eighthundred and
eighty-two .
§ 5. The said company is hereby authorizedto borrow
money and issue bonds therefor, at a rate of interestnot
exceedingeightper centum per annum ,payable at such
times and placesas they may deem expedient; and may
execute mortgages upon any or allof theproperty,rights,
and franchisesof the company .
§ 6. The said company shall have the right to extend
itsmain line,and locate,construct,maintain, and oper-
ate branch roads from the main line to the Cumberland
river, the Tennessee State line,or to any other point they
may deem expedient .

operate telegraphand telephone linesalong the main
line and any branch or branches , and make connection
with other lines .
§ 8. The said company may make connection with
other railway companies upon such terms as they may

Fork and any other stream which itcrosses.

counties ; bút,remove ,manufacture timber,and sellsame ;

eral oil,and mine for,obtain,remove ,sell, and dispose of

manufacturing ; and may lay and operate pipe lines to

ers shall not be liableto corporate debts.

CHAPTER 1346 .
AN ACT to aid in developingtheagriculturaland mineral resourcesofKen-
tucky,and to that end to provide foran exhibit thereof at the Southern
Exposition at Louisville, Kentucky , and at the World's Industrialand
Cotton Centennial Exposition at New Orleans, Louisiana.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the sum of twelve thousand fivehundred dol-
larsishereby appropriated,to be paid out of the Treas-
ury with any moneys not otherwise appropriated,to aid
in the development of the agriculturaland mineral re-
sources of the State,by an exhibitionof itsresources at the
Southern Exposition at Louisville,Kentucky ,during the
year one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four, and at
the World's Industrialand Cotton CentennialExposition
at New Orleans , Louisiana ,in eighteen hundred and eigh-
ty-four-five. Not exceeding ten thousand dollarsof said
sum isto be used and expended in the collectionand exhi-
bition of the agriculturaland mineral products,and cattle
and other stock of the State of Kentucky , at said Exposi-
tion at Louisville,Kentucky ; and not exceeding two thou-
sand five hundred dollars of said sum is to be used and
expended in removing to,and exhibitingat,New Orleans,
Louisiana , such part thereof as will best promote the
development of the resources of thisState.
§ 2. A board of commissioners is hereby created and
established,whose duty it shallbe to provide for the col-
lection and exhibition of said products as contemplated
in the first section of this act ; and said board shall be
composed of the Commissioner of Agriculture,the State
Geologist, and three other persons , to be appointed by
the Governor and confirmed by the Senate ; and one of
said persons shallbe selectedfrom each of the Superior
Court districtsof this State. The State Geologist shall
be the chairman of saidboard; and theAuditor ishereby

sums of money , and in favor of such persons as may
have certificatesthereforsignedby said chairman : Pro-
vided, That said sums ,in the aggregate ,shallnot exceed
the sum hereinbefore appropriated .
§3 . Said board shall keep a record of the sums for
which it may issue certificates,to whom issued, and on
what account,and shallmake reportthereofto the next
General Assembly
§4 . This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1347 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof John W. Partin,of Bell county .
WHEREAS , On the eightday of July,one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-three,Richard Murray shot and killed
F. M. Siler, in Bell county ,Kentucky , and the factshav-
ing been communicated to His Excellency L. P. Black-
burn,then Governor of Kentucky ,with the information
that said killing occurred in Whitley county ,Kentucky ;
and whereas ,said Governor did, on the thirteenthday of
July, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-three, issue
his proclamation offering a reward of one hundred and
fiftydollars for the apprehension and delivery of said
Richard Murray to the jailerof Whitley county; and
whereas ,on the twenty-fifthday of September,one thou-
sand eighthundred and eighty-three, the said John W.
Partin apprehended said Richard Murray and delivered
him to the jailerof Bell county ,in which county the of-
fense was committed , and whose circuitcourt has juris-
diction to try the said offense; and whereas ,the grand
jury of Bellcounty indictedsaidMurray at itsNovember
term , one thousand eight hundred and eighty-three, for
the murder of said F. M. Siler; and whereas , said Rich-
ard Murray is now in confinement in the jail of Bell
county to answer said charge ; and whereas ,the regular
judge of the Bell circuitcourt has refused to allow the
claim of said John W. Partin for the reward of one hun

1004 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
dred and fiftydollarsofferedby said Governor for the ap-
prehension and delivery of said Richard Murray to the
court having jurisdictionof the offense; therefore,
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and is
hereby,directedto draw hiswarrant on the Treasurerfor
one hundred and fiftydollarsforthe benefitof saidJohn
W. Partin,and to pay him the reward offeredas afore-
said,forhis servicesin apprehending and deliveringsaid
Richard Murray to the jailerof said Bell county .
§2 . This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884.

dered as hereinbeforeprovided for as if renderedby a

§3 . If the policejudge or marshal of said town ,or
magistrate or constable of said county ,having knowledge
or information of a violation of the first and second sec-
tions of this act, shall willfullyneglect or fail to exe-
cute the duties required of them respectively,they shall,
upon indictment of a grand jury and conviction in the
circuitcourt,be fined not lessthan fiftynor more than
one hundred dollars, or imprisonment in the county jail
not lessthan thirtydays nor more than ninety days for
each offense, or both so fined and imprisoned , at the dis-
cretion of the jury .
§ 4. The provisionsof thisactshallnot apply to wines
tobe used onlyforsacramentalpurposes.
§ 5. All fines collected under this act,except such as
may be collected from officersfailing to perform the
dutiesprescribedin thisact,shallbe paid intothe treas-
ury of said town .
§ 6. This act,in order to become a law after its pas-
sage , must be ratifiedby a majority of the legalvoters of
the town of Princeton , and within three miles thereof ;
and the officersof the regular election to be held the first
Monday of August ,one thousand eighthundred and eigh-
ty-four, are hereby authorized to take the vote at the
regular voting places in the town of Princeton, and cer-
tifyto the same .
§ 7. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 12, 1884.

of Kentucky :

striking therefrom the word " eight," in the second line
of said section, and inserting in lieu thereof the word
" six," and by striking from the third line thereof the
word " five," and inserting in lieu thereof the word
" seven ."
§2 . That section sixteen of said act be repealed, and
that all contest of officerselective by the voters of said
town be governed and decided as provided for by the
General Statutes , as in cases of contested elections for
any officerelectiveby the voters of the county .
§ 3. Section seventeen of said act is so amended that
the outgoing clerkshallnot issue a certificateof election
to any one pending a contest of said election for said
office .

officeprovided for by said act shall fail,for thirtydays

cant .
§5 . The trustees hereafter elected at the regular annual

the exceptions herein named . Three of those elected at

year and three for two years. The six elected at said

ous,vinous,and malt liquorsin the county of Greenup ."

CHAPTER 1359 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Logan.county to aid in building
turnpike roads in said county .

of Kentucky :

ityof itsmembers concurringtherein,shallhave power
to levy annually,for one or more years,an ad valorem
tax , not to exceed twenty cents on the one hundred dol-
lars' worth oftaxablepropertyin saidcounty,and a cap-
itationtax of one dollaron each voter in the county
subject to poll-tax, for the purpose of raising a fund to
