CHAPTER 1361 .
AN ACT to provide forthe working of the public roads in the countiesof
Pulaski, Rockcastle,and Laurel.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . The judge of the county court shall divide all of
the roads in the county into precincts,and ,as often as
may be necessary,appoint a surveyor in each precinct,
whose duty it shall be to superintend the road therein,
and see that the same is worked and kept in such repair
as the agreement of the contractor thereon with the
county court requires. No surveyor shallbe allowed,
unless by consent of the court,to resignsuch place un-
der two years,except he remove from the precinct. Any
surveyor may be removed at any time by said court.
§2 . The clerk of the county court shall, within ten
days afterthe appointment of a surveyor of a road ,de-
liver a copy of the order to the sheriffof the county ,con-
taining a fulland complete descriptionof the precinct,
and take his receipt therefor. The sheriff,within fifteen
days thereafter,shall deliver a copy of the order,and a
description of the precinct, to the surveyor, and return
the originalto the clerk'sofficeof the county court, with
the time of the service indorsed ,which shall be evidence
of the factsstated therein. The clerk shallpost up in his
office,once in every year ,a listof the names of allof the
surveyors in the county ,designating the precinct of each
surveyor. The clerkor sherifffailingto perform the du-
ties prescribed in this section shallbe fined two dollars
and a half for each neglect .
§3 . Each surveyorof a public road shallbe furnished
with a copy of the contractforthe working of the road
in his precinct. He shallreport to the county judge any
failureupon the part of the contractorthereon . He shall

1018 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
certifyto the county judge when the contracthas been
properlycomplied with. He shallbe exempt from jury
service,and from poll-tax forroad purposes,only during
his continuance in said office. For a failureto discharge
any of his dutiesprescribedby this act, said surveyor
shall be fined therefor not less than five nor more than
fifteendollarsforeach offense,to be recoveredby war-
rantinthe name oftheCommonwealth ofKentucky ; and
the quarterly court shallhave exclusivejurisdictionof all
proceedings against surveyors of roads .
§4. The county judge shall, at the court-house door,
between the firstMonday in April and the firstMonday
inMay in each year (and such othertimes as he shallfind
necessary),letout the working and keeping in repair the
publicroadsin the county to the lowest and bestbidder,
forsuch lengthof time as he may choose (not exceeding
his own term of office), after due advertisement of two
insertionsin the county paper,ifone be published, and
by a printednoticepostedup forat leastfifteendays be
foresuch lettingat the voting place in each votingpre-
cinct in the county : Provided , That the fund raisedby the
levy court under thisact shallbe sufficientto work allof
the publicroadsin saidcounty; and ifnot sufficient,then
itshallbe used at such places and forsuch roads as the
county court may think best,having due regard to the
public good and the wants of the differentparts of the
county .
§ 5. Each contractor shall execute to the Common-
wealth a bond ,with good security, to be approved by the
county judge,in double the amount he is to receivefor
his contract,forthe faithfulperformance of the said con-
tract; and said bonds shall be filedat the next regular
term of the county court afterthe letting, which shallbe
noted of record,and the bonds kept on fileby the clerk
of the court in his office,in bundles,marked " contractors'
bonds ;" and certifiedcopies of said bonds shallbe com-
petent as evidence ; and the county judge shalldeliver to
each surveyor a copy of the bond of the contractor on the
road in the surveyor'sprecinct. Any contractor who shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1019
fail,in whole or in part,to complete his contractwithin
the time and in the manner prescribedtherein,or who ,
having contractedto keep any partof the publicroads in
good order for traveling or hauling ,or any bridge or cul-
vert in good repair,and who shallfailto do so,shall,for
every failure. be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor ,and ,
upon conviction,shallbe fined not lessthan ten nor more
than one hundred dollars .
§ 6. All fines assessed under this act shall be collected
as other fines,and shallinure to the benefit of the road
fund of the county,and shallbe paid out by the sheriff
under the orders of the county judge .
§ 7. The county judge shallprovide work on the roads
of the county,or on the streetsof towns ,forsuch persons
convicted of penal offenses as may ,by order of the proper
authority,be required to work on roads or streets, or to
work out fines assessed against them on the roads or
streets under the provisions of general or special laws ,
and may , when necessary,put a ball and chain upon any
such persons to prevent their escape ; and it necessary,
may employ some suitable person to oversee and guard
them while at work .
§ 8. The county judge shalllet out the construction or
repairing of the bridges in the county not included in the
agreement of any contractor,or the levy court may , in
their discretion, appoint a specialcommissioner to letout
any bridge or bridges,and providefor hiscompensation
therefor; and such county judge or special commissioner
shall be held liablefor any failurein regard to any bridge
so letby them ,respectively; and the court may require
bond with surety from the special commissioner .
§ 9. That the courts of the justices of the peace shall
have jurisdictionconcurrent with the quarterly court of
all proceedings against road contractors under this act;
and itshallbe the duty of any justiceor the county judge ,
upon hisown knowledge ,or upon complaint of any sur-
veyor ,or upon the information under oath of any citizen
that any part of the public road is out of repair,to issue
his warrant against the delinquent contractor,returnable

1020 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to his own or some other justices' court,or to the quar-
terlycourt; and upon the execution and return thereof,
such proceedings shall be had as are had in other Com-
monwealth cases. In proceedings under thisact, the de-
fendant shallhave no rightto claim his district,and both
the Commonwealth and defendant shallhave the right of
appeal from justices' courts to the quarterlycourt,and
from the quarterly court to the circuitcourt.
§ 10. It shallbe the duty of the county attorney to
prosecuteallwarrants under thisact,and forsuch prose-
cutions he shall receive twenty -fiveper cent.of the fines
recovered; and upon his failure, the court may appoint
some attorney in his stead, who shall receive said com-
mission for his services . All fines assessed under this act
shall be collected as other fines,and shall inure to the
benefit of the road fund of the county ,and shallbe paid
out by thesheriffunder ordersof the county judge.
§ 11. It shallbe unlawful for the county judge ,county
attorney,sheriff,collectorof therevenue,or any surveyor,
to become ,directlyor indirectly, interestedin any con-
tractfor working roads or building or repairing bridges;
and itshall be unlawful for either of said officersto buy
or become interestedin any claim growing out of said
work or contract. For a violationof this section, either
of said officersshall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and ,
upon indictment and conviction thereof, shall be fined
not less than fiftynor more than five hundred dollars.
This section shall be given in charge to the grand jury
by the judge of the circuitcourt.
§ 12.Any personwho shallwillfullyor negligentlyin.
jure,destroy,or obstructany of the saidpublic roads or
bridges,or any of the culverts or ditches on said roads ,
he shallbe fined not lessthan five nor more than twenty-
five dollars, to be recovered in like manner as the fines
prescribed herein for surveyors,and shallalso be liablein
double damages to the county , or any person aggrieved
or injured,to be recoveredin any court having jurisdic-
tion of the amount . It shallbe the duty of the surveyors
to reportpromptly to the county judge any violationof
this section .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1021
§ 13. The county court,a majorityofthejusticesagree-
ing,shall,on some regular county court day , assess a tax
of not more than fifteen cents on each one hundred dol-
lars' worth of property assessedfor revenue purposes,
and a capitation tax not exceeding two dollarson each
person in said county required by the general law to
work on roads .
§ 14. The ad valorem and capitationtax providedfor
in section fifteen of this act shall be collected as other
revenue due the county is collected, and under the same
obligationsby the collectingofficer,and shallbe paid out
by said collectingofficerto road and bridge contractors,
on the order of the judge of the county court. The said
court,at the same time ,may assess a further capitation
tax, not exceeding two dollars on each person , which
shall be collected in same manner and under same obli-
gations as road tax herein provided , which shallbe paid
out by the said collecting officer, on the order of the
county judge ,forthe constructionand repairing of bridges
inthe county; and saidofficershalltake and produce, at
his annual settlement with the county ,the order under
which the money was paid ; and a certificateof the con-
tractor(indorsed" approved" by the county judge) set-
ting forththe work ,materials,and so forth,used in the
construction or repair of any bridge designated by name
or locality,as fixed by the road on which said bridge is
erected; and unlessthe orderis accompanied by the cer-
tificateaforesaid, said officershall not be allowed in his
settlement the amount paid .
§ 15. Any capitationtax-payer of the county may
work out the capitationtax providedfor in thisact on
the roads in his precinct,at such time and on such terms
as may be agreed on between him and the contractor for
such road ; and the contractor'swritten receiptfor such
work shallbe received by the sheriffor tax collector in
payment of such capitationtax. Said receiptshallbe
charged by the sheriffor tax collectorto the contractor
who signs itas so much paid to him on his contract.
§ 16. No capitationtax-payer under the provisionsof
this act shallbe returned a delinquent unless it appears ,

1022 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to the satisfactionof the county court, that he has no
property out of which the tax can be made ,and also that
he is physicallyunable to work on the road; and any
person not returned a delinquentwho shallfailto pay
saidtax,or to work on the road,as hereinprovided,shall
be finedfivedollarsbeforeany justiceof the peace or
the judge of the county court.
§ 17. Chapter ninety-four of the General Statutes is
hereby adopted and made part of this act,except when
it comes in conflictwith the provisions of this act it is
hereby repealed.
§ 18. This act shallonly apply to the counties of Pu-
laski, Rockcastle, and Laurel .
§19 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 9, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1362 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Madison and Southern Railway Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Prentis Meade and W. F. Howe ,of Carroll
county , W. F. Peak ,of Trimble county ,John D. Carroll,
Wm . Cravens , Jno . W. Mathews , and R. W. Master-
son,of Henry county,be,and theyare hereby,appointed
a board of directors,under the direction of whom sub-
scriptionsmay be received to the capital stock of the
Madison and Southern Railway Company ,which ishere-
by incorporated; and they may cause books to be opened
at such time and place as theymay directforthe purpose
of securingsubscriptionstothe capitalstockof saidcom-
pany ,afterhaving given such notice as they think proper :
Provided, That any subscriptiontendered at any time or
place ,other than those advertised, may be received by
said directors, or their successors .
§2 . That the capital stock of Madison and Southern
Railway Company shallbe not over three millionsof dol-
lars,or not over twenty thousand dollars per mile, in
shares of one hundred dollars each ,which may be sub-
scribedby any individual,company ,or corporation. As

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1023
!
soon as two thousand shares of said stock shall be sub-
scribed,the subscribers, theirsuccessors,and assigns,shall
be,and are hereby declared to be , incorporated into a
company by the name of the Madison and Southern
Railway Company ; and by thisname shallbe capable of
purchasing ,holding ,leasing, and conveying real estate,
not exceeding three thousand acres,and personal estate,
so far as the same may be necessary for the purpose of
the corporation; and shallhave perpetual succession;and
by said corporate name may sue and be sued ,contract
and be contracted with ; and may have and use a com-
mon seal, and alterand renew the same at pleasure; and
shall have and enjoy and use all the privileges which
other similarcorporate bodies lawfully have and use.
§3 . The subscriptionsforstock in said company shall
be payable in installments, at such times as may be re-
quired by the board of directors of said company ; but
no such payment shall be demanded until at least ten
days'[notice] shall have been given by publicationin
one or more newspapers publishedon the lineof said
road; and ifany subscribershallfailto pay any install-
ment ,when so demanded ,the same may be recovered by
an action, in the name of the corporation, before any
court having jurisdictionin such cases; and in all such
actions,itshall not be necessary to prove any other de-
mand than publication provided for in thissection; or in
case such a failureto pay any installmentor part of in-
stallment so demanded shall continue for the space of
sixtydays afterthe time the same is requiredby such
demand to be paid ,the board of directors may ,at their
discretion , order the same to be forfeited to the com-
pany ; and may , if they think proper , sellsuch share or
shares for the benefitof the company ,or in the event of
the highest bid being less than the unpaid balance and
intereston saidsubscription,thenthe company may be-
come purchaser,and shallretiresaid subscription; but
the board of directors,by a majority of the whole ,may
remit such forfeitureson such terms as they may think
proper: Provided, Itshallbe lawful forthe board of di

1024 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
rectorsto receive subscription to said capital stock,pay-
able in contracts, well secured ,to build any part of said
road ,or any bridge or bridges on same ,or perform any
kind of work , or furnish materials, which may be ac-
cepted by the company : And providedfurther, That sub-
scriptionstosaidcapitalstockmay be made in realestate
situated in Kentucky , or any other State, if said sub-
scriptionshallbe tendered to the board of directors; said
real estate to be taken at its cash value , to be assessed
at the time by three commissioners,on oath, of whom
two shall be selected by the company , and one by the
person proposing to subscribe. Upon their report, in
writing, describingthe land, assessingits cash value,
they may receive the same at itsvalue , and issue a cer-
tificateof stock, and may take a conveyance in fee-
simple; and therealestatereceivedforstocksubscription,
and which the company is hereby authorizedto receive,
shall be over and above the three thousand acres men-
tioned in section second of this act .
.
§ 4. Thus seven directorsshall serve until their suc-
cessorsare elected,on the second Tuesday in May ,one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-five; and shallhave
power to electone of theirnumber as presidentof the
company , and allow him such compensation as they may
think proper. That, to continue the successionof the
president and directors of said company ,seven directors
shallbe chosen annually ,on the second Tuesday in May ,
at such place as the president and directorsmay appoint ,
by the stockholdersof said company : Provided, That the
president and directorsmay change the time and place of
holding elections,upon publishing such change not less
than thirtydays priorto the election, in one or more
newspapers along the line of the railway ; and that the
directors,or a majority of them , shallhave power to ap-
pointjudges of allelections; to electa presidentof said
company from among themselves,and to allow him such
compensation as they may deem proper; and ifany va-
cancy shalloccur ,by death ,resignation,or refusalto act,
of any presidentor director,beforethe year forwhich he

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1025
was electedshallhave expired,a person to fillsuch va-
cancy shall be appointed by the board of directors, or a
majority of them ; and that the president and directorsof
said company shall hold and exercise their officeuntil
theirsuccessors are elected and enter upon the discharge
of theirduties; and allthe electionswhich are by thisact,
or by the by -laws of said company , to be made at a par-
ticulartime , if not made at such time ,may be made in
ninety days thereafter,upon notice in newspapers afore-
said ; and at all elections and all other occasions where a
vote of stockholders of said company istaken ,each stock-
holder shallbe allowed one vote for every share of stock
owned by such voter; and any stockholder may , in writ-
ing,depute any otherperson to act as proxy forit,him ,or
her .
§5 . That a general meeting of stockholders of said
company may be calledat any time during the interval
between the annual meeting by the president and the
directors,or a majorityof them ,or by stockholdersown-
ing one half of all the stocks subscribed, upon thirty
days ' notice of the time and place of holding the same
in the newspapers aforesaid; and where any such is called
by the stockholders,such notice shall specify the object
of the call; and if at any such callmeeting a majority of
allthe stockholdersare not presentin person or by proxy,
the same shallbe adjourned from day to day without
transacting any business for any time not exceeding five
days ; and if within said fivedays stockholders having a
majority in value of allstocks subscribed do not attend
in person or by proxy ,such meeting shallbe dissolved.
§ 6. That the presidentand directorsof said company
in officeforthe preceding year shall,at the regularan-
nual meeting of the stockholders,exhibita clear and dis-
tinct account of the affairsof said company ; that at any
call meeting of the stockholders,or a majority in value
of the whole of the stockssubscribedbeing present,they
may demand and require similar statements from the
president and directors,whose duty itshallbe to furnish
such statements when required.
LOC . L., VOL . 11-65

1026 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§7 . That the president and directorsof said company ,
before he or they act as such, shall swear or affirm, as
the case may be , that they will well and truly discharge
the duties of their respectiveofficesto the best of their
skilland judgment ; and the said president and directors,
or a majorityof them, shallhave power to elector ap-
point a treasurer of said company , and require and take
of him a bond ,in such penalty and with sureties as they
may prescribe,payable to saidcompany ,conditionedfor
the faithfulkeeping and disbursing of allsuch money as
may come into his hands , and with such other conditions
as may be prescribed. Upon every bond recoverymay
be had for a breach of the conditions thereof by suit in
the name of the company ,and in any court having juris-
diction .
§ 8. That if any of the stock authorized by this act
shallremain unsubscribed at any time ,the said president
and directors, or a majority of them ,shallhave power to
open the books and receive subscriptions to the stock
which shall remain unsubscribed, or to sell and dispose
of the same for the benefit of the company ; and the sub-
scribersor purchasers of such stock shall have allrights
of original subscribers, and be subject to the same regu-
lations and liabilities.
§ 9. That said president and directors,or a majority of
them, may appointallsuch officers, agents, [and] ser-
vants as they may deem expedient for the business of
the company ; and may remove the same at pleasure,
or said board of directors may delegate to their presi-
dent thepowers to appointor remove any or allsuch
employes,subjectto theirapproval at theirfirstmeeting
thereafter. That they, or a majority of them , may de-
termine ,by the contract,the pay of such officers, agents ,
or servants,and regulate,by by -laws ,the manner of ad-
justingallaccountsagainstthe company ; and they shall
have power to erect,carry on ,conduct ,and control work-
shops, eating-houses,warehouses, and buildingsor edi-
fices necessary to or convenient for the use of said
company ; and they shallhave the power to directand

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1027
regulate in what manner , and by what evidence, stock in
said company may be transferred; and to pass allby-
laws they deem necessary and proper for exercising the
powers hereby vested in said company , and for carrying
into effectthis act, and to alter the same at pleasure :
Provided , The same be not contrary to the Constitution
and laws of Kentucky or of the United States.
§ 10. That if the capital stock of said company shall
be deemed insufficientfor the purpose of this act, itshall
be lawful for the president and directorsto increase the
same as much as they deem it necessary , not exceeding
three millions of dollars, and not over twenty thousand
dollarsper mile, notice as hereinbefore required; and the
said company may borrow any sum of money not exceed-
ing three millions of dollars,and secure the payment of
same by mortgage upon the said road and its branches ,
or in such way as may be agreed upon .
11.That the president and directorsof said company
are hereby vested with allthe powers and rightsnecessary
to the construction of a railroadfrom Madison ,Indiana ,
through Trimble , Carroll, Henry , Shelby , Franklin , An-
derson , Mercer ,and Boyle counties,to Danville,in Boyle
county ,Kentucky , or from Madison ,Indiana , or a point
on the Kentucky side of the Ohio riveropposite,or as
nearly so as may be convenient or practicable, to Dan-
ville,Kentucky,by the best and most practicableroute:
Provided, That the line of said road shallrun through or
within one -fourth of a mile of the towns of Prestonville,
in Carroll county , and New Castle,in Henry county , and
they may connect with ,by intersectingand using parts of
eitheror all branches owned or operated by the Louis-
villeand NashvilleRailroadCompany ,upon such terms
as may be agreed on between the companies interested.
The president and directorsmay cause to be made con-
tracts,which shall be signed by the president,with any
corporations, companies , or individuals for making said
road or any part of it; and they may purchase or lease
any road or roads connecting with said road ,or any of its
branches ; and they, theiragents, engineers, or employes ,

1028 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
or those with whom they contractfor surveying said road
or any part thereof,may enter upon and use and exca-
vate any land which may be necessary to said road forits
use,or for any purpose necessaryin the constructionor
repairs of said road or itswork and appurtenances ; and
they may build bridgesand constructtunnels, and may
use timber, earth,gravel,stone, and other materials nec-
essary or usefulin the constructionor repairs of said road ;
and may condemn and acquire such lands as may be
necessary or useful in the construction of said road , or
as sitesfor itsdepots or such otherbuildingsas may be
necessary or usefulto said road ; such condemnation and
acquisition to be made as provided for in the general
laws of this State .
§ 12. Said company may allow intereston allcash sub-
scriptionsof stock from the time of paying for said stock
up to the time of making the firstdividend,and issuethe
holders stock therefor .
§ 13. That itshallbe the duty of the president and each
directorof said company , and each engineer engaged in
the service of the company , each for himself, to swear
that he is not,and will not during his continuance in of-
ficeor in the service of said company ,become interested,
directlyor indirectly,in any contractforthe construction
of said road or any of itsbranches,or any part thereof,
which has or may be made by the company or any other
person . The engineers shall also swear that they will
well and truly,to the best of theirskilland judgment ,
make a fairand impartial estimate of the work done ,the
material furnished according to contract,and otherwise
faithfullyperform the dutiesof engineer,so long as they
continue in the service of the company . A certificateof
such oath,taken before an officerauthorizedby law to
administer oaths,shall be filedwith the records of said
company . That if, in judicialproceedings and judg-
ments of any court of competent jurisdiction, itshall be
adjudged that any engineer or any of the officersafore-
said shall, at any time aftertaking such oath, have been
interested with any contractor in any contract for the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1029
construction of said road ,or any part of it,or for equip-
ping said road or its branches , no recovery in law shall
be had on such contracts,and said engineer interested,or
officer,shallbe deemed guiltyof the crime of perjury.
§ 14. That it shallbe lawful for said railway company
to acquire,by purchase or donation, and hold land for
stone quarries and timber , and coal and other mineral
lands that may be necessary for the construction and re-
pairs of the road and its business .
§ 15. That said company may connect itssaid road ,or
any of itsbranches , with the railroadof any other com-
pany in or out of thisState,and may purchase or lease
and operate any railroadconnecting with the road or
branches of said Madison and Southern Railway Com-
pany ; and it may consolidatewith, and make running
and operating arrangements with ,any other railroadcom-
pany ,upon such terms as may be agreed upon by the con-
tractingparties; and it shallbe lawful for the franchises
and subscriptionof stock granted and subscribed to any
other railroadcompany , to be assignedand transferred
to the Madison and Southern Railway Company ,and the
likepower and privilegesare hereby grantedto any rail-
road company with which itmay contract.
§ 16. Itshallbe lawful forthiscompany to unitetheir
road ,branch or branches , with any other railroad or rail-
roads,and to acquire interestin other roads or parts of
roads,and use the same as parts of theirmain line,branch
or branches ,with the consent of the directorsof such com-
panies. The said railroadcompany shallhave the same
rightsand privilegesof prosecution,and any person or per-
sons shall be liableto the same penalties and forfeitures
forinjuriesdone and committed upon thepropertyof said
company ; and shall be entitledto collectsuch tollsand
ratesfortraveland transportationover theirroad and its
branches as are provided for in the laws incorporating
the Madison and Southern Railway Company , and acts
amendatory thereto; and shallhave allthe powers and
privilegesconferredupon said Madison and Southern
Railway Company by thelawsofKentuckyforconstruct

1030 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ing and operating theirsaid proposed railroad,not herein
specifiedand granted , and not in conflictwith the terms
of this charter .
§ 17. The board of directorsof said company are em-
powered to appoint an executive committee of fiveper-
sons from among the directorsof the company , of whom
the presidentshallbe one,and authorizesaidcommittee
to conduct and manage the affairs and business of the
said company during the intervalsof the meetings of the
board,and may ,at any time,discontinuesaidcommittee,
or remove any member thereof.
§ 18. That in order to create and secure a fund for the
finalpayment of the indebtednessof saidcompany ,said
company ishereby authorizedand empowered to create
and form a sinkingfund for that purpose, in that way
deemed more advantageous and practicableby the board
of directorsto said payment .
§19 . The propertyof the company ,includingitsline,
cars,rollingstock, and property of allkind and charac-
ter, real,personal, and mixed , shall be exempt from all
taxation for a period of ten years from the completion of
that section of the road lying between a point opposite,
or nearly opposite, Madison , Indiana , and New Castle,
Henry county, Kentucky . Said railway company may
constructor purchase a telegraphlinenear thisroad and
branches , or become owners of stock in any company
which may constructsuch line of telegraph; and may
establishany express company over theirlineof railway ,
branches , and connections , and become owners of stock
in such a company , with the usual privileges of such
companies ,and subjectto the usual restrictionsupon
them . The said company may acquire any corporate
rights,franchises, and privilegesin other States,and use
the same ,which they may receive from the Legislatures
thereof,or which they may acquire therein,not inconsist-
ent with theircharteror the laws of Kentucky . They
shall begin work on their road in good faithwithin five

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1031
years ,and complete the same within ten years from the
passage of thisact,or forfeitthe powers herein conferred.
§ 20. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 9 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 1363 .
AN ACT to incorporatetheCascadeCamp-ground Association,in Webster
county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Dr. John T. Bethel , F. C. Denton , George E.
Bell,John S. Geiger , and John A. Powell , and their suc-
cessors, be , ard are hereby , created a body -corporate,
under the name [and] styleof the Cascade Camp -ground
Association , situated on the Henderson Division of the
Louisville and Nashville Railroad ,near the cityof Sebree ,
in Webster county ; to have perpetual succession, with
right to obtain, hold, sell, and convey and dispose of
every class of property necessary for the uses of said
company ,not to exceed one thousand dollars; and may
contract and be contracted with ,sue and be sued ,plead
and be impleaded ,in all the courts of the Commonwealth ;
and may make by -laws for itsgovernment not inconsist-
ent with the laws of this Commonwealth .
§2 . The company shall have authority to forbid all
transient persons from erecting booths,and sellingcon-
fectioneries,tobacco , and cigars, at any place within one
fourthof a mile of said camp ground,without the writ-
ten permission to do so of said company .
§3 . If any person or persons violatesectiontwo of
thisact,he or they,upon conviction,shallbe finedin the
sum of not less than ten dollars nor more than twenty
dollarsfor each offense,to be recovered by warrant before
any court of competent jurisdiction.
§4 . Itshallbe the duty of said company to post at
leasttwenty printed copies of theirrules and regulations
at the most publicplaceson said camp -grounds,at the
beginning of every protractedmeeting.

1032 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§5 . Nothing in the foregoing act shallbe construed as
to prevent or restrictany person or persons attending
services on said camp -grounds from bringing theirown
diet and eating it on the grounds .
§ 6. This act to take effectfrom and after its passage .
Approved May 5 , 1884.
CHAPTER 1364 .
AN ACT to encouragetheraisingof sheep,and to providea fund forpayment
to owners of sheep killedor injuredby dogs,in Campbell county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Campbell county,at its
October term at Alexandria ,one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four , and annually thereafter, a majority of
the justices of the peace residing in that part of Camp-
bellcountysouthof the court-house districtin saidcounty
sittingwith the county judge as a courtforthe purpose,
shallhave authorityand power to levy and cause to be
collecteda tax on alldogs in said district,which tax shall
be paid by the owners of said dogs ,of not more than sev-
enty-five cents for each dog so owned in said districtin
any one year . Said tax shallbe collectedby the sheriff
of said county at the same time ,by the same proceed-
ings,and under the same penaltiesunder which said sher-
iff collectsthe county levy of said county . Said tax ,
when levied,shallbe a lien on the property of the owners
of such dogs for its payment ,as other taxes are. The
sheriff,before collectingany of said tax,shallgive a good
and sufficientbond ,with security,in the Campbell county
court, forthe collectionand payment over of said tax as
herein provided for. In case of refusal or failure of the
sheriffto give said bond for the collectionof said tax,
said court may appoint a collectorof the same . The said
Campbell county court shallappoint a treasurer,whose
duty it shallbe to receive and receipt for said tax, and
safelykeep the same . Said treasurershall pay out the
money so received under the orders of said court as here

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1033
inafterprovided , and settlehis accounts when required
by said court. Said treasurershall,before he shallreceive
any of said money ,give, in said court, a good and suffi-
cient bond for the safe-keeping and disbursement of the
same . The said fund raised by said tax shallbe calledthe
sheepfund. The sheriffor collectorof saidtax shallhave ,
ascompensation,the same rateper cent.as forcollecting
the county levy; and the treasurer shallhave ,as compen-
sation forallservices,eight per cent.on amount received.
§2 . The assessor of said county shall,afterthe levy of
saidtax annually,at the same time he assessesthe prop-
erty in said districtfor State revenue ,take a listof the
number of dogs within said district, with the name of the
owner or owners , which listshe shallenter on his books
of assessment , and make return of same when he makes
hisreturnof assessmentsof propertyfor the purposes of
State revenue . Said assessor shall have the same author-
ity to administer oaths to persons, when necessary to
ascertain the number and ownership of dogs in said dis-
trict,as he has under the law in assessing property for
general revenue purposes ; and it shall be his duty to
administer such oath in any case where he has doubts as
to the number and ownership of the dogs . For taking
each list, the name of the owner and number dogs being
considered as one list, he shall have three cents , which
shallbe paid out of the sheep fund .
§3 . Any residentof said districtwho may sufferloss
or damage by having his or her sheep killedor injured by
dogs shall,if he ascertain the owner or owners of said
dog or dogs that did said killingor damage ,shalluse due
diligence to secure compensation from the owners of such
dog or dogs for the damage sustained; and if not other-
wise obtained ,shallby law pursue such persons untilpay-
ment shallbe made ,or untilreturn of no property shall
be had on the process by the officer.
§ 4. If such person so suffering loss or damage , and
failingto recover the same from the owners of such dogs ,
and all persons in said districtsufferingloss or damage
by the killing or injuring to his or her sheep by dogs ,

1034 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
whose owner or owners such persons so losing shall, after
due diligence, failto ascertainor identify, shallhave the
rightto recover and receive, out of the sheep fund herein
provided for,the damage sustained by injury to sheep
wounded ,and the value of such as may have been killed,
and pro rata. If the fund should be insufficientto pay in
full,upon proof of such loss as herein provided for, and
such persons who may have failedby suitto recover,
shallbe allowed the fees actuallypaid out in such suit as
part of his claim : Provided, That no attorney'sfee shall
form part of said claim .
§5 . All claims for loss under this act shall be sworn to
by the person sufferingthe loss or damage ,who ,in such
claim , must set out the circumstances , and bring his right
to receive pay within the provisions of thisact; and such
person must satisfythe county courtby satisfactoryevi-
dence of the value of the sheep lostor damages sustained,
if the claim be for damage alone; and upon allowance of
any such claim,the saidcourt shallgivethe claimantan
order on the treasurerforthe amount ,who shallpay the
same out of the saidsheep fund,taking hisreceiptthere-
for .
§ 6. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 9 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 1365 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Southern and Western Light,Fuel, and Motive-
power Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Lewis E. Harvie ,George W. Craddock ,John
S. Harvie , Joseph Robinson , B. W. Duke , William S.
Dehoney , and Joshua F. Bullitt, their associates, suc-
cessors, and assigns,be, and are hereby ,created a body-
corporate, by the name of the Southern and Western
Light, Fuel , and Motive -power Company ; and by that
name shallhave perpetual succession; and may contract

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1035
and be contracted with, sue and be sued, plead and be
impleaded, the same as a natural person; and may
manufacture and furnish any city, district, locality, or
individual, or any public institution, manufacturing es-
tablishment , or private premises with wood or other gas
or light, or fuel, or heat ,or motive -power , either or all,
for such time, and upon such terms, as may be agreed
upon by the parties; and for the purposes of said busi-
ness may , from time to time , acquire, by purchase or
other lawful mode ,any real or personalpropertywhich
the company may require in its business; and may sell
and dispose of the same , or any part thereof,when not
needed by the company ; and also of any and all other
products climated or leftfrom itsdifferentprocessesof
manufacture .
§2 . By and with the consent of the proper legal au-
thoritiesof any city, town , municipality, or other public
organized body having jurisdictionover the same , said
company shallhave power and authorityto lay pipes
and erect posts and other fixtures of every necessary
kind ,in and through the streetsand alleys thereof,and
along the line of any road or thoroughfare, eitheror all,
as may be agreed with said authorities; and for that
purpose to take up the pavements and streetor road

stock in any other corporation. The company may have

stock to the amount of five thousand dollars or more

quired by the company ,and shall have a firstlien upon

wise incur such indebtedness as itmay deem necessary or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1037
expedient in the prosecution of the business of the com-

and benefitofthecompany ; and to securepayment there-

annual electionafter the firstone , which may take place

CHAPTER 1371 .
AN ACT to prevent idlenessand vagrancy in Fleming county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. If any person be found in Fleming county idling,
rambling,or loiteringabout,as provided in chapterone

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1045
hundred and eleven of the General Statutes,the county
judge, or any justiceof the peace of Fleming county,
shall issue a warrant for the arrest of such person ,who
shall be given a reasonable opportunity to prepare for
trial; and if found guilty of the offensecharged in said.
chapter,shallbe sentenced to hard laborfora periodof
not lessthan fiftynor more than one hundred days .
§ 2. Itshallbe the duty of the jailerof Fleming coun-
ty to see that any person convictedof the offenseof va-
grancy,as provided above,shallbe furnishedwith work
and compelled to labor eighthours a day ; and any per-
son convicted as aforesaid refusing to labor, shall be
compelled to live on bread and water until said person
is willing to work ; but said time not to be deducted from
said person'sterm of labor.
§3 . Any person convicted as aforesaidwho shallhave
a wife or childrendependent on him for support,and who
iscompelledto laborunder sentenceofconvictionas pro-
vided herein, his wife or children shallreceive and be al-
lowed the sum of twenty- five cents per day ; the amount
provided forto be paid by the county should the county
receive the benefitof the labor; but if any town or city
corporation,or individual,procure or receive the benefit
of said labor,then such corporation or person must pay
the wife or childrenthe sum of twenty-fivecentsper day
during the time said person so labors.
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved May 10, 1884.

cents for each day's labor of eight hours' duration ; and

CHAPTER 1376 .
AN ACT for the benefit of J. F. Bullitt.
WHEREAS ,Joshua F. Bullittbecame bound as suretyfor
the Kentucky River Navigation Company ,upon a note to
the Commissioners of the Sinking Fund ,dated November
nineteenth,one thousand eight hundred and seventy -two ,
for three thousand eight hundred and one dollars and
eighty cents for rents due upon a lease of the State'sim-
provements upon the Kentucky river; and whereas , on
the thirteenth of February ,one thousand eight hundred
and eighty, said commissioners obtained a judgment of
the Franklin circuitcourt against said Bullitt for the
amount of said note , with interest from November nine-
teenth, one thousand eight hundred and seventy-two ,on
which judgment said Bullitt paid three thousand four
hundred dollars on May twenty -first, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty; and whereas , by an act approved
April twenty-first, one thousand eight hundred and eigh-
ty-two ,said Bullittwas released from furtherliabilityon
said judgment ; and whereas ,in view of the factsstated
in said Bullitt'saddress to thisGeneral Assembly , and in
letters to him from the gentlemen who constituted the
board of Commissioners of the Sinking Fund at the times
when said lease and said note were executed , viz : Ex-
Governors John W. Stevenson and Preston H. Leslie ,
ex -Secretaries of State Samuel B. Churchill and George
W. Craddock, ex-Attorney General John Rodman , ex-
Auditor D. Howard Smith , and Treasurer James W.Tate ,
itappears that said Bullittisequitablyentitledto have
saidthree thousand four hundred dollarsrefunded ; there-
fore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor is directed to draw a warrant
upon the Treasury in favorof saidBullittforthe sum of
three thousand and four hundred dollars .
Approved May 9,1884 .

tain counties .

CHAPTER 1381 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe LouisvilleCharity Organization Society.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1, That P. Joyes, John Callahan, N. G. Rogers,
B. Seymour ,W. T. Rolph,John H. Weller, W. C. Hall,
Andrew Cowan ,F. D. Carley,Garvin Bell,Geo . H. Hull ,
L. N. Dembitz , N. Bloom ,R. H. Thompson ,J. K. Good-
loe,E. G. Hall , R. M. Kelley, N. Finzer, L. Richardson ,
A. G. Munn , A. P. Humphrey ,and their associates and
successors, are created a body politicand corporate,by
the name and style of the Louisville Charity Organiza-
tion Society; and by that name may sue and be sued,
contract and be contracted with; may have a common
seal, and change same at pleasure; and may have per-
petual succession; itsoffices[shall] be in Louisville,and
itswork shallbe confined to that city.
§2 . The objectsof the societyshallbe-
First. To form a center of inter-communication be-
tween the various churches and charitable agencies in
the city of Louisville,to foster harmonious co -operation
between them , and to check the evilsof the overlapping
of relief.
Second. To investigatethoroughly,and without charge ,
the cases of all applicants for reliefwhich are referred
to the societyfor inquiry, and to send the persons having
a legitimate interest in such cases full reports of the
results of investigation; to provide visitorswho shall
personally attend cases needing counsel and advice .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1053
Third. To obtainfrom the proper charitiesand char-
itable individuals suitable and adequate for deserving
cases .
Fourth . To procure work for poor persons who are
capable of being wholly or partiallyself-supporting .
Fifth. To repress mendicity by the above means .
Sixth. To promote the general welfare of the poor ,by
social and sanitaryreforms,and by the inculcationof
habits of providence and self-dependence; to encourage
the establishment of such provident institutionsas shall
tend to the physical, moral ,and intellectualimprovement
of the poor .
§3 . The management of the societyshallbe vested in
a central council .
§ 4. The societyshallhave power to make rules for its
own government ,and the method and details of carry-
ing on its operations; it may provide for the electionof
officers,from time to time , and the appointment of com-
mittees , and fix their duties .
§5 . Said corporationmay receive,take,and hold, by
gift, bequest,and otherwise, money and personal prop-
erty,from time to time; and may take and hold,by pur-
chase ,devise, and gift, real estate in said cityfor the
purposes of this incorporation, not exceeding in value
fiftythousand dollars.
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 9,1884 .

CHAPTER 1384 .
AN ACT to incorporatetheBig Sandy Manufacturingand Pipe Line Com-
pany .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
ofKentucky:
§ 1. That R. G. Hemingray , R. Hemingray ,J. A. Bar-
rett,O. J. Wiggins ,and J. P. Orr ,jr.,and their associates,
successors, and assigns,be , and they are hereby ,declared
a body -politicand corporate, under the name and styleof
the Big Sandy Manufacturing and Pipe Line Company ;
and by such name shallhave perpetualsuccession; may
sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded ,in any court as
nay a natural person ; may have and use a common seal,
and may ordain and put in force such by -laws ,rules and
regulations,for the good government of said company ,
and the efficientmanagement of its affairs, as may be
deemed expedient ,not inconsistentwith the laws of this
Commonwealth .
§2 . The capital stock of said company shall be five
hundred thousand dollars,divided into shares of one hun-
dred dollarseach,which shallbe deemed personal prop-
erty, and shallbe transferableonly on the books of the
company . The presidentand directorsshallhave power ,
from time to time , to declare dividends out of the net
profitsof theirbusiness, and for the purpose of extending
their works ,and the convenience of theirbusiness,by a
vote of the board of directors, the capital stock of the
company may be increased to such an amount as may be
deemed necessaryto carryout successfullythe business
of the company , not, however , to exceed in the whole
amount of stock the sum of one million five hundred
thousand dollars. They may borrow money and issue
bonds therefor,bearing interestat any ratenot exceeding
ten per cent.per annum ,and secure the payment thereof
by a mortgage or mortgages upon the realand personal
estate of the corporation. The said corporation,in addi-
tion to the specificpowers hereby given and enumerated ,
may exerciseany and allthe power conferredupon sim-
ilar corporations by the general laws of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky .

1056 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . The business of the corporation shallbe to furnish
such persons,parties,corporations, or corporate authori-
tiesas itmay contract with,gas -light,natural or artificial,
heat and fuel,and crudeor manufactured oilsofany kind,
and dispose of the residuum of the products,together with
such articlesof common fuel as may be deemed expe-
dient. The said company may purchase and hold, and
may sellor exchange the same and purchase again for
said purpose ,such lands, oil-rights,leases,or real estate
as may be necessary for their business; and may erect
such buildingsthereon as may be deemed for the interest
of the corporation. Said company may dig, bore , and
open wells of any kind , and use the products thereof,
whether oil,natural gas ,or other matter or fluidfound in
theearth ; and may lay down pipes or conduits forconduct-
ing the products of said well or wellsfrom the same to any
pointor place in the Stateof Kentucky ; they may make
contracts to supply natural or manufuctured gas to and
with any person , city, town , or corporation within or
without the State of Kentucky ; and said corporation may
own and hold such otherpersonaland realestateas may
be necessary to carry out the object of thisincorporation,
and do any other lawful thing necessary for the proper
management of the affairsof the corporation.
§ 4. The said corporation may construct, erect, and
maintain roads, narrow -gauge railways, and tramways ,
from any of its works to any riveror railway contiguous
thereto .
§5 . Said company may buy ,lease,or rent any suitable
lands, mines , mining rights, rights of way , and other
propertyand rightsnecessaryto theirbusiness; and may
dispose of the same , or any part of the same ,by sale or
exchange ; and it may receive real or personal estate,
leaseholds,mining rights,and rightsof way ,in payment
of the whole , or such parts of any stock subscriptions
made to the capitalstock of said corporationas they
may deem advisable.
§6 . The affairsof said corporation shall be managed
by fivedirectors,all of whom shallbe stockholders, and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1057
any three of whom shall constitute a quorum for the
transaction of business. The five persons firstmen-
tioned as incorporators in this act shall constitute the
directors for the firstyear ,and shall act untiltheir suc-
cessors are chosen and qualified. After the firstyear the
stockholders[shall],on the firstMonday in Aprilof each
year ,meet together at the officeof the corporation, and
elect fivedirectorsfor the ensuing year ,who shall hold
their offices until their successors are chosen . Elections
fordirectorsshallbe by ballot,and may be advertised, if
so ordered,by the board of directors.
7. The directors shall choose from their own number a
president,vice president,secretary,treasurer,and general
superintendent, and may appoint such other agents as
they may see proper . They may prescribe the salary or
compensation of any officeror agent appointed by them ,
and fixand prescribe the duties of such agent ,officer, or
servant of the company . Bonds shallbe required of the
treasurer,and the periodof theirofficesand allthe offi-
cers or agents of the company shallalso be fixed.
§8. The incorporatorsherein named ,or any three of
them ,may ,afterthe passage of thisact,open books for
the subscriptionof stock, and when fifteenthousand dol-
larsof the capitalstockhereinprovidedforissubscribed,
the company may be organized accordingto the provis-
ions of this charter and begin operations. The stock
that may not be subscribed for at the organization may
be held,sold,or disposed of as the directors may select;
and the directors may make such callsfor the unpaid
subscriptionsas, in theirjudgment , the interestof the
company may demand ,untilthe whole arrearages are
paid.
§ 9. No stockholder in said company shallever be held
liableor made responsibleforitsdebts or liabilitiesin a
largeror farthersum than the amount of any unpaid
balance due to the said company forstocksubscribedfor
by said stockholder.
§ 10. The principalofficeof the company shallbe at
Covington , Kentucky ; but other officesand agencies
LOC . L., VOL . II- 67

1058 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thereformay be establishedelsewhere in any State in
the Union for the transactionof any businesspertaining
to said company .
§ 11. The directorsof said company are hereby em-
powered to pass any rules and by -laws ,not inconsistent
with thisact and the laws of thisState,that they may
deem necessary, to theirfulland competent working
under thischarter; and any rules,regulations,or by -laws ,
not inconsistent with this charter and the laws of this
State, so adopted and passed by said directors to carry

§ 12. Any person who shallunlawfully,willfully,know-
ingly, tear up , destroy, or injure any pipe or conduit
belonging to said company shall, upon conviction there-
of,be deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor ,and finedin any
sum not less than one hundred nor more than one thou-
sand dollars .
§ 13. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 10, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1387 .
AN ACT to incorporate Home College,in Henry county,and to createa
board of curators therefor .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That thereishereby establishedin Campbellsburg ,
in Henry county , an institutionof learning,to be known
as Home College; and said college shall have power to
confer any and alldegrees that may lawfullybe conferred
by any college in thisState,and to attestthe same by a
diploma under the seal of the college,signed by the fac-
ulty, and attested by the president and secretary of the
board of curators : Provided , That no honor shall be con-
ferredor diploma granted to any student except those who
shallhave completed the prescribed curriculum ,passed a
satisfactoryexamination ,and has sustained a good moral
character .
§2 . D. F. Caplinger , Wm . O'Brien ,J. S. Smith ,L. M.
Sandford ,C. T. Scott,Ben .Lamaster ,James Boyer ,T. W.
Barker , Joseph Oren , and J. S. Stapp , and their success-
ors, are hereby created a board of curators for Home
College; and they shallprovide by by -laws forthe fillingof
vacancies in the board ,elect one of their number presi-
dent and another secretary,who shall hold theirrespect-
ive offices for such time , and their successors shall be
elected in such manner ,as the by -laws of said board may
provide. But said board shallhave no power to manage
or interferein any way with the business or educational
affairs of said Home College .
§3 . The board of curators or faculty of the college, or
eitheror both ,or as many of them as may be necessary,
shall have the right and authority to arrest any person
guilty of misconduct in the college building or on the col-
legecampus ,and shalldeliverany personso arrestedto
the proper authoritiesas soon as practicable.
§4 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 12, 1884.

CHAPTER 1389 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Lincoln County Building and Savings Asso-
ciation .

of Kentucky:
§1 . That John S. Hughes , William H. Miller, William
Dougherty, Richard C. Warren , John W. Rout,William
G. Welch ,George McAlister, Stephen S. Myers , John J.
McRoberts ,John N. Menifee, A. A. Warren , Samuel H.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1061
Shanks , Thomas P. Hill,sr., Thomas P. Hill.jr., J. W.
Hayden ,James P. Bailey,EllisW. Brown ,James F. Pey-
ton ,John Blain, M. C. Saufley, Thos . D Newland , Wm .
P. Walton ,D. R. Carpenter,GabrielRainey,M. G. Nevins,
M. Huffman , John A. Allen, John B. Higgins , Robert S.
Little,Geo .D. Wearen ,A.T. Nunnelly,M. B. McRoberts,
Lee F. Huffman ,J. W. Alcorn, Joseph Severance,D. B.
Edmeston , Steele Bailey, Mrs. SallieC. Trueheart, B.
Mattingly,J. M. Bruce,and Thomas R. Walton ,and their
associates,successors, and assigns,be ,and they are here-
by ,created a body -corporate and politic,under the name
of the Lincoln County Building and Savings Association;
with power to sue and be sued ,to contract and be con-
tracted with ; to have a common seal, and the same to
alter or break at pleasure: and to have perpetual suc-
cession within the period of time allowed by thisact.
§2 . The place of business of thisAssociation shallbe
at Stanford, Lincoln county ,Kentucky , where itshall at
alltimes keep an officefor the transaction of business,at
which office it shall meet for the transaction of business
at leastonce every week .
§3 . The capitalof said associationshallconsistof not
lessthat three (3) hundred nor more than two thousand
shares of one hundred dollars each .
§4 . The objectof the associationis to enable the
owners of the shares of stock to aggregate their weekly
savings into a common fund , and to loan the same at
interest .
Second . To enable its share -holders to obtain loan of
money upon mortgage without being required to give
personal security.
Third . To enable the association to erect for its share-
holderscomfortableand healthfulhomes ,and to permit
the share-holders to pay forsame in weekly installments.
§5 . Any person may become a member of the associa-
tionby the purchase of one or more sharesof itscapital
stock, thepayment of an initiationfeeof twenty -fivecents
for each share,and by signing the constitution and by-
laws . The constitution shall be this charter .

1062 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 6. The stock of the association shallbe paid for as
follows: any person becoming a member shallexecute to
the associationa promissory note for each share he shall
purchase . Said notes to be for one hundred dollarseach ,
and to be payable in weekly installmentsof not lessthan
twenty -five cents nor more than one dollar upon each
note, without interest. The amount of the weekly in-
stallments to be fixed at the beginning of every three
months forthe three months next ensuing by the associa-
tion, and to be an amount sufficientto carry out the ob-
jects of the association. The association shall, upon the
execution and delivery of such note or notes,deliver to
the share-holder a certificatefor one share of its capital
stock for each note received from him . Alien shall be
retained in said notes upon the shares of stock to secure
the prompt payment of said notes according to their
terms , and also to secure all fines and assessments which
may be imposed on the share-holder for failureto keep
his said obligation. Said liens shall be superior to all
other claims against said stock,or against the interestof
the share -holder in the association .
§7 . Five shares of the capitalstock of the association,
the propertyof any bonafidemember with a family,shall
be exempt from sale under execution, attachment , or
judgment of any court,exceptto foreclosea mortgage or
liengiven on them by the owner ,or forpurchase-money
due thereon;but thisexemption shallnot apply to debts
or liabilitiesexisting prior to the purchase or acquisition
of such shares. Any member desiring the benefit of this
exemption shallgive to the secretaryof the associationthe
number of his sharesto which he wishesitto apply,and
the secretary shallenter the same ,and the name of the
owner , in a book to be kept for that purpose ; and there-
upon the share-holdershallbe entitledto the benefitof
this provision. The said book shall, at all reasonable
times,be open to the inspection of allpersons whomso-
ver : Provided, however, That no person claiming the
benefitof thissectionshall,in additionto the exemption
herein provided for,be entitledto a homestead exemp-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1063
م
tion of more than five hundred dollars. The exemption
herein provided for shallsurvive to the wife and infant
children,one or both, of any deceased share-holder.
§ 8. The association,by successiveorganizations,may
distribute among its members series of shares in any
number ,firstcompleting , under each separate organiza-
tion,the fullpayment of allthe shares subscribed, when
such organization shallterminate . After the firstorgani-
zation of the association , and after it has accumulated
and paid to itsmembers the amount due on the share or
shares held by each ,the incorporators or share-holders,or
any of them ,and theirassociates,may organize again for
a likepurpose of accumulating and saving for itsmem-
bers sums of money in shares; and the same may be fol-
lowed by successive reorganization without number ; but
the rightsand privilegesgranted by thisact shallcease at
the end of fiftyyears from the firstday of March ,anno
domini one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four.
§ 9. Married women may become share-holders in this
association, and the shares bona fideheld and paid for by
them , out of their own property or money ,or theirown
earnings , or paid for by others for the use and benefit of
such married women , shallnot be liablein law or equity
to the debts of theirhusbands , and shallbe the separate
estate of such married women ,and shallbe held by them
free from the control of their husbands . The association
may have and assertagainstthe sharesso heldthe same
lien as against other shares,and by the same proceedings
as against femes sole.
§ 10. Infants may become members and share-holders
in thisassociation, and the shares bona fide held and paid
for by them out of theirmoney or property, or out of
theirearnings,shallbe theirown property,and shallbe
held by thern free from the debts or control of their pa-
rents or guardian , except that the parent or guardian
may ,in writing,requirethe associationto hold and not
pay over to the infantany part of his share or shares
until afterthe marriage or majority of the infant. The
associationshallpreserveallsuch writingsas partof its

1064 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
records,and shallhave them at allreasonabletimes open
to the inspection of allpersons whomsoever .
§ 11. The money of the associationshallbe invested in
loans secured by mortgage upon real estate situated in
Lincoln county,Kentucky . The realestatemust be worth
such an amount in proportionto the amount loanedupon
itas shallbe fixed by the by -laws ,clear of all other in-
cumbrances . The money shall not be loaned outside of
the association,exceptwhere no share-holderisan appli-
cant therefor .
§ 12. The money shall be loaned in sums of not less
than three hundred dollars, and whenever there shallbe
in the treasury enough unappropriated to make a loan ,
the directors shall make the same to the member who
shallhave firstmade and filedin writing an application
therefor .
§ 13. When the real estatesecurityhas been approved ,
the loan shall then be made upon the execution of a
promissorynote by the borrower forthe amount loaned,
payable to the order of the associationin accordance
with the provisionsof thischarter,and bearing interest
at the rate legal when the loan is made ,the interestto be
payable in weekly installments; he shall also execute or
cause to be executed to the associationa mortgage upon
the approved realestateby such personsas the associa-
tion may require,to secure the payment of such note.
§14 . The share of stock of the association may be
issued at any regular meeting as of the date of issue,
upon the dues being paid up thereon from the date of the
organization of the association ; and the association may ,
in addition to such dues ,prescribethe amount of premium
to be paid on the shares issued,making the dues and pre-
miums to equal the paid-up and accumulated value of
the other shares at the date of such issue,so as to keep
all the shares in the association of equal and uniform
value .
§ 15.The profitsof the associationshallbe ascertained
at the end of each halfyear (the lastday of June and
lastday of December ),and shallat that time be entered
to the credit of the share-holders, each to receive credit

1
of the profit earned by the money paid in by him on

tion .

stock; but no share shallbe purchased untilit has been

priated funds in the treasury to the purchase of shares.

ithas a mortgage or lien,or in which it has any interest;

organization ,and the directors then elected shall continue

be upon the lastSaturday in December of each year.
The directorsshallelecta presidentand vice president
from theirnumber , and a treasurerand secretaryfrom
their number , or from among the other share-holders.
The treasurerand secretaryshallbe removable at any
time by the directors.
§19 . Directors may fillany vacancy in the board . The
directors and other officersand agents of the associa-
tion may be removed by the vote of a majority of all
the share-holders at any regular meeting , and after one
month's noticepostedprominentlyin the officeof the as-
sociation, or published in any newspaper published and
circulatedin the city of Stanford.
§ 20. The presidentshallpresideat allmeetings of the
associationand directors,sign allorders issued by the as
sociation or directors, attend to securing the execution
and recording of all mortgages , and , with the treasurer,
execute allreleases, and otherwise look to the best inter-
ests of the association . He shall receive a fee of two dol .
lars for each mortgage executed to the association and
leftfor record by him ,to be paid by the mortgagor . He
shallalsoview each pieceof propertyofferedas security
for an advance ,and report if the same be of the assessed
value,or ifithas sufferedin value,and any other fact
bearing upon itsworth as security. The directorsmay
appointa committee from among themselvesto act with
the president in viewing and reporting on the value of
property. In the absence or inabilityto act of the presi-
dent ,the vice president shallperform his duties.
§ 21. The secretary shall be present at, and keep a
strictrecord of the doings of,each meeting of the asso-
ciation and the directors, and shallhave his record-book
at each meeting . He shallkeep an accurate account be-
tween each share-holder and the association, and sign,
with the president, all orders issued by the directors on
the treasurer; and shallsafelykeep his recordsand all

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1067
T
1
books and papers belonging to the association, except
mortgages, deeds, and policiesof insurance, and other
obligations for money ,and deliver the same to his suc-
cessor. He shall,at all times,have the financialcondi-
tion of the associationready forexhibitionor examination ,
and report the same four times a year at regular meetings .
He shall execute bond ,with good security,satisfactoryto
the directors,in the sum of three thousand dollars,for the
faithfulperformance of his duties. He shall receive a
salary, payable monthly ,to be fixed by the directors.
22. The treasurershallreceiveallmoney paid to the
association, and pay all orders issued by the directors,
and countersignedby the presidentand secretary,and
keep a record of the same . He shall safely keep all
deeds , mortgages ,powers of attorney, contracts, bonds ,
notes, policies of insurance , and other obligations be-
longing to the association. He shall give bond in the
sum of five thousand dollars, with good securities, ap-
proved by the directors,for the faithfulperformance of
hisduties,and deliverto hissuccessorallpapers,effects,
money , and other property of the association in his
charge. All moneys receivedby the treasurershallbe
deposited by him in some solvent bank in the city of
Stanford ; and he shall make his check for any amount
paid out by him ; and his books and accounts shall at all
times be subject to examination by the directors and
officers of the association. He shall make quarterly
reports to the associationof the stateof his accounts .
§ 23. Suits may be institutedand recovery had upon
any bond executed to the association,for itsbenefit,in
any court of competent jurisdiction.
§24 . The directorsmay ,when necessary,employ and
fix the pay of an attorney at law ,to institutesuits or
transact any other business for the association demand-
ing the servicesor counsel of such attorney.
§ 25. Any person borrowing money of the association,
upon mortgage,shallbe requiredto pay in advance for
the recording of such mortgage , and all other expenses
incident thereto .

1068 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§26 . No officerof the association shall receive any
compensation for his servicesby vote of the directors,
except as provided for by thisact; but the association,by
a vote of allitsshare-holders,may make a compensation
to any or allofficers,the amount of which shallbe levied
in like manner .
§27 . Shares of stock shall be transferable, and the
manner of making such transfersshallbe fixedby the
by -laws . Such transfersshall not be valid, except as
between the partiesthereto,untilthe same are regularly
entered upon the books of the association,so as to show
the name of the person, by whom and to whom the
transfer is made ,the numbers or other designations of
the shares,and the date of the transfer. A regular trans-
ferbook,plainlyshowing allthese things,shallbe kept
by the associationas part of itsrecords.
§ 28. The privateproperty of the shareholdersshall
not be liableforany of the corporatedebts of thisasso-
ciation .
§29 . The association shall have the rightto make all
by -laws necessary to carry out the purposes of thisact,
not in conflictherewith , and not contrary to the Consti-
tution and General Statutes of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky .
§30 . The association may at any time ,by a majority
of allthe share-holders,expel any member for any bad
moral conduct detrimental to the interests of the associa-
tion; but any such action against a member shallbe after
notice,warning him to appear at a time and place fixed
in such notice ,to answer charges against him .
§31 . In addition to the loans upon real estate herein
authorized,the associationmay loan itsmoney upon per-
sonal or other securitiesconsidered as safeby the direct-
ors; and loans may be made anywhere in the State of
Kentucky ,but not upon real estate,except in Lincoln
county; and applicationsfor loans shallbe preferredin
the following order:
First. To members of the association who want the
money forinvestment in building or repairing theirdwell-
ing -houses .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1069
Second. To members for building other houses .
Third . To members who want the money for any other
purpose than the buildingof houses.
Fourth . To persons not members for any legitimate
purpose whatever.
§32 . Should the association become the owner of any
real estate, itmay be leased and rents collectedthereon
by the association as natural persons might do in such
cases under the laws of Kentucky ,except that such prop-
erty shall not be leased for a longer period than one
year .
§33 . As soon as three hundred shares of stock shall be
subscribed,the associationmay organize for the transac-
tion of business; but the subscription-book may be kept
open and the stock increased, from time to time ,untilit
shall reach an amount limited by a vote of the associa-
tion,and aftersuch limitshallbe reached,subscriptions
shall cease; but the associationmay at any time increase
itscapital stock to any amount allowed by thisact.
§34 . In the event of the death of a member of this as-
sociation,his heirs or personal representative shall re-
ceive from the association the value of his shares , to be
estimated as directed in the case of the withdrawal of a
member ,
§ 35. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 12, 1884.
CHAPTER 1390 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheKentucky Coal, Iron, Timber,and Transporta-
tion Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That George M. Adams ,John H. Wilson ,James T.
Gibson , of Knox county , and H. F. Finley ,Ansil Gatliff,
and Nathan A. Richardson , of Whitley county ,and M.
J. Moss , of Bell county , their associates, successors, and
assigns, be, and are hereby , constituted a body -corporate
and politic,by the name and styleof the Kentucky Coal ,
Iron,Timber ,and TransportationCompany ; and by that

1070 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
name and style shall have perpetual succession, with
power to contract and be contracted with ; to own prop-
erty,both real and personal, and to sue and be sued in
allcourts and places; to have a common seal; to engage
in mining for coal,iron,and other minerals ,and cutting
and sawing timber , and preparing such minerals and
timber formarket , and transporting and sellingthe same
within and without this State ; and to do other acts and
have allother powers needful for the successfulprosecu-
tion of their business,and for the execution of the pow-
ers herein granted .
§2 . That said corporatorsmay organizesaidcompany
by the electionof a presidentand board of directors,not
exceeding fivein number , and such other officersas they
may deem necessary, at such time and place as they may
designate by notice previously given ; and when thus
organized, the said company shall have power to make
such by -laws ,rules and regulations, as they may deem
necessary, from time to time , for the government and
prosecution of the business of said corporation,not incon-
sistent with the Constitution and laws of the United
States and of this State .
§3 . The capitalstock of the company shallnot exceed
three hundred thousand dollars,to be divided into three
thousand shares of one hundred dollars each . Said com-
pany may issue bonds not exceeding three hundred thou-
sand dollars, to bear interestat not exceeding six per
cent.per annum ,interestcoupons attached,which bonds
shallbe signed by the president, and countersignedby
the secretary of said company ; and said company may
mortgage any part or all of its property to secure the
payment of the said bonds. Said bonds shallbe made
payable in not exceeding twenty years from theirdate ,
and at such a place as may be designated in the mort-
gage .
§ 4. The said company may buy , lease, or rent any
suitablelands,timber,minerallands,mines,mining priv-
ileges, rightsof way ,saw -mills,and other property and
rights,such as they may deem necessary; and may dis-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY , 1071
pose of the same , or any portion of the same ,by sale,or
in any other legal manner . They may receive real estate,
leasehold estate,mining rights,the rights to take timber ,
and rights of way ,in payment of such part of subscrip-
tion as they may deem advisable.
§ 5. That saidcompany may constructtramways ,rail-
ways , or railroads, and operate the same from itsmines
and mills to any other railroad or navigable stream with-
in fiftymiles of its mines , mills, or business; and may
make any contract or agreement with any railroadwith
which they may connect,when they deem itproper,for
running itslocomotives and cars over such other road , or
such other road running its locomotives and cars over the
company's roads; and this company may charge such
tollsand fares for trafficover itsroad as may be allowed
by law ; and if, forthe purpose of constructing its roads
aforesaid,itbe necessary to pass over the lands of others,
and it cannot agree with such person or persons on the
amount of compensation to be paid for same ,then this
company may condemn the same for the use of said
company by writof ad quod damnum ,as the law provides
forrailand turnpike roads; but such railroadsshallbe
used to accommodate public travel and public trafficby
the owners thereof .
§6 . That saidcompany is hereby authorizedand em-
powered to erectand maintain such boom orbooms upon
the Cumberland riverand itstributaries,above Point Burn-
sides,as itmay deem proper for the purpose of stopping,
holding ,and securing saw -logs,staves,and other timber
products that may be floateddown said riverand itstrib-
utariesbelongingto saidcompany or to otherpersonsor
companies ; and as compensation to said company for the
use of any boom which may be erected by iton the said
river,itmay charge and collectfrom the owners or pur-
chasers of timber , staves, and so forth,any sum agreed
upon not exceeding the followingrates,viz: For poplar
and ash saw -logs,twenty-five(25) centsper hundred feet;
for walnut , cherry, and other kinds, forty-five cents per
hundred feet; for pipe staves,two dollars and fiftycents
4

1072 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
perthousand; forotherstaves,two dollarsperthousand,
and forheadings and spokes ,one dollar per thousand ; for
railroadties,fivecents each; and forany boom erected
by itupon any of the tributariesof saidriver,not exceed-
ing one -half the rates above named may be charged by
said company ; and in case no contract has been made ,
said company may charge the rates hereinbefore named
on all staves, logs, and other timber stopped , held, and
securedin any of itsbooms on saidriveror itstributaries
as stated: Provided,That no boom belonging to said com-
pany shallbe erected so as to impede the freepassage of
saidriveror itstributaries; and that saidcompany shall
not stopin any of itsbooms the timber,logs,staves,and
so forth, belonging to any other company ,corporation, or
individual, ifthiscompany shallhave been notifiedby
the owners or their agent not to stop the same , which
shallbe marked or branded so as to be identifiedby this
company : And itisfurtherprovided, That thiscompany
may have a lienupon alltimber, staves, and so forth,
caught in any of itsbooms , for catching and stopping
same . And forthe purpose of erecting booms ,said com-
pany may purchase land as hereinbeforeprovided.
§ 7. That said company may erect and build on any of
its lands such buildings, saw -mills, machinery , engines ,
and fixturesas may be deemed convenientand properfor
establishingand conductingthebusinessof saidcompany .
§ 8. The principalofficeof thiscompany may be at
any point in thisState said company may select,with
such branch officesas may be deemed necessary for the
transactionof itsbusiness; and the individualproperty
of stockholders of this company shall not be liable for
the debts of said company .
§ 9. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .
