CHAPTER 1391 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Drake's Creek and Bay's Fork Turnpike Com-
pany .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That William Garrison,F. M. Hardcastle,Ewing
Hardcastle,M. E. Mottley,J. M. Gillmore,J.M. Welkins,
Samuel Thomas , and F. G. Cox , and theirassociatesand
successors,be,and they are hereby,createda body -poli-
ticand corporate,under thename and styleof the Drake's
Creek and Bay's Fork Turnpike Company ; and by said
name and styleshallhave power to contract and be con-
tracted with, sue and be sued ,answer and be answered ,
plead and be impleaded ,in all courts and places in this
Commonwealth ; and may have and use a common seal,
and may alteror change the same at pleasure.
§2 . The said company is hereby authorizedand em-
powered to construct a turnpike, macadamized , or gravel
road ,in itsdiscretion,from a point at or near the termi-
nation of the turnpike road constructed by the Bowling
Green and ScottvilleTurnpike Company ,running thence
to or near the old James Gillmore place; thence in the
direction of and to Railey's Ford , on Bay's Fork ,in the
vicinityof John A. Willoughby's ; said road to be located
on the most practicable route between said termini; and
in order to raise the funds necessary to construct said
road ,said company shallhave the power to raise a sum ,
not exceeding thirtythousand dollars,by the issue and
sale of the capitalstock,to be divided into shares of fifty
dollars each .
§3 . That the books forthe subscriptionof stock may
be opened in the cityof Bowling Green ,and such other
places as may be deemed best,under the directionof the
personshereinbeforenamed ,allof whom areherebymade
commissioners for the purpose of obtainingthe capital
stockof said company ; they may insertan obligationin
the subscription book as follows : " We , whose names
are hereunto subscribed,severallypromise to pay to the
LOC . L., VOL . II- 68

1074 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Drake's Creek and Bay's Fork Turnpike Company the
sum of fiftydollars for each share of stock in said com-
pany set opposite our names , in such manner and pro-
portion,and at such times,as shallbe requiredby said
company . Witness our hands this - day of — ,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-———————————. " When
the sum of three thousand dollars shall have been sub-
scribed to the capital stock of said company ,itshall be
the duty of said commissioners named in thisact to give
notice,in such manner as they may think proper,of a
meeting of the stockholders of said company , at such
time and place as may be specifiedin said notice, for the
purpose of electing a president and five directors; one
vote shall be allowed for each share of stock ; and the
presidentand directorsshallcontinue in officeforone
year ,and untiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified.
The time and place of all elections,afterthe firstelec-
tion,shallbe fixedby the presidentand directors; and
a majority of the board shall be competent to transact
business .
§ 4. So soon as said company is organized by the
election of a president and directorsas above provided ,
they shall be a body -politicand corporate,by the name
and styleabove stated; and by that name shallhave per-
petual succession, and all the privileges and franchises
incident to a corporation; shall be capable of holding
theircapitalstock,and taking and holding,by purchase
or gift, all such lands, tenements , and hereditaments ,
real or personal property, as may be necessary for the
prosecutionofitswork or the objectsofthiscorporation.
§ 5. The president of said company may call meetings
of the board of directorswhenever he may thinkproper;
and on application of any two directorsto him , the board
of directorsshallhave power to electa secretaryand
treasurer,and such other officersas they may think nec-
essary, and to fillsuch vacancies as may occur in said
board during theirterm of office; said board shallrequire
of the treasurer,before he assumes the duties of his office,
a bond forthe faithfuland honest discharge of his duties,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1075
and to faithfullyaccount forallmoneys thatmay come to
his hands as such treasurer, which bond and security
shallbe approved by the board; saidboard shallkeep a
record of itsproceedings, which shallbe signed by the
president,and ,in the absence of the president,the board
may elect a presidentpro tem ; said board shallhave
power to make allnecessaryby -laws and regulationsfor
the government of said company ,and may sue for and
recover,in the name of said company , from delinquent
stockholders,the amount due and unpaid upon any share
or shares of stock in said company .
§ 6. The presidentand directorsshallfixand regulate
the elevation and grade of said road ,the width and part
thereof to be covered with stone or gravel; and may fix
the rates of toll,and may change and alterthe same at
pleasure; but said rates shall not exceed the rates pre-
scribedby chapter one hundred and ten of the General
Statutes ; and when three consecutive miles of said road
shallhave been completed,and examined and approved
by any two justicesof the peace of Warren county,who
shall examine the same on application of the president
of said road ,a toll-gate may be erected,and tollcollected,
under such rules and regulations as shallbe prescribedby
the president and directors, and under the general law of
the State. But until said road is completed for fivecon-
secutive miles,the tollcollectedshall be in proportion as
the length of road finished bears to five miles .
§7 . The presidentand directorsof said company and
theiremployes are hereby authorized to take the consent ,
in writing,of all persons who may consent to grant the
right of way for said turnpike road , and the use of mate-
rials for building and repairing said road ; and when
eitherthe rightof way or any of the materialsnecessary
forthe constructionof saidroad cannotbe acquiredby
consent or purchase, said presidentand directorsmay
apply to the county courtof Warren county fora writ of
ad quod damnum to assessthe damages which may be
sustainedby the owner of said land; and the jury, in
assessing damages , may take into consideration the ad

1076 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vantages resulting to the party claiming damages by the
construction of the road ; and upon the payment of the
damages assessed, or the tender thereof,it shallbe law-
fulforsaid company and itsemployes to make said road ,
and to dig and carry away any stone or other material
necessary for the construction or repair of said road ; and
said company may acquire by purchase ,or by writ of ad
quod damnum ,as above set forth,land, not to exceed two
acres,for the purpose of erectinga toll-house on said
road; and said company may procure from the Warren
county court the right of way over any road over which
the said county has control.
§8 . The president and directorsof said company may
make and declare equitable dividends of the surplus
earnings of said company among the stockholders accord-
ing to theirrespective shares,after paying the expenses
of said company .
§ 9. Certificatesof stock in said company shall be
signed by the president, and countersigned by the secre.
tary, shallbe deemed personal estate,and shallbe trans-
ferableon the books of the company in such manner as
the president and directorsshallprescribe.
§ 10.The capitalstock of said company shallbe used
in the procurement of the right of way , materials,and so
forth, and in constructing said road , and in defraying all
legitimate expenses incident to the construction of said
road ,and for no other purpose ; and the stockholders in
said company shallonly be bound to the extent of their
stock in said road .
§ 11. The said company hereby created may have
power to contract for and acquire from the Bowling
Green and ScottvilleTurnpike Company allor any part
of the road constructed on the east side of Drake's creek;
and said Drake'sCreek and Bay's Ford Turnpike Com-
pany may begin itsroad at the bridgeacrosssaidDrake's
creek,or at the terminus of the presentBowling Green
and ScottvilleTurnpike Company ,according to itscon-
tract or agreement with the said Bowling Green and
ScottvilleTurnpike Company , or it may begin at any

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1077
|
point in the vicinity of the terminus of said Bowling
Green and ScottvilleTurnpike Company .
§ 12. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after itspassage .
Approved May 8, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1392 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Homestead Loan and Savings Association.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Henry Laub ,C. E. Bush , W. H. Eaton ,Jas .
Flanagan ,and J. H. Stuart ,be , and their successors , asso-
ciates, and assigns are hereby ,constituteda body -corpo-
rate,under the name and styleof the Homestead Loan
and Savings Association; and by that name may sue and
be sued, plead and be impleaded,and shallhave suc-
cession; have a common seal, and alter or change the
same at pleasure; and may acquire and hold real and
personalestate,and selland convey the same ; and shall
possess such other powers as are consistent with the
objectsof thisassociation.
2. The officersof the associationshallbe a president,
vice president, solicitor, secretary,and a treasurer,and
any number of directors, not exceeding seven ; and in
elections for officers, and transaction of all other busi-
ness,each member present at the meeting shallbe enti-
tled to one vote for each share of stock he may hold:
Provided, That women and infant, members of the asso-
ciation, may vote by proxy ; married women and infants
by their husbands and guardians ; other women by any
member of the association selected by them ,in writing,
to act for them . The capital stock of said association
shallbe any sum ,not exceeding four hundred thousand
dollars , to be divided into shares of one hundred dollars
each,and to be paid by thestockholdersin weekly install-
ments of one dollar each week for each share of stock
untilsaid installments,with theirnet accumulations , shall
amount to said sum of four hundred dollars for each

1078 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
share,orat the expirationof sixyears from the firstelec-
tion of officers, whether said shares have amounted to
said sum or not,there shall be a distributionthereof to
the stockholders, and a liquidation of its affairs; and
afterthat,itscorporatepowers and existenceshallonly
continue to settleup itsunsettledbusiness and affairs.
§3 . The corporators above named shallopen books for
the subscriptionof said stock,and may adopt ,so far as it
has progressed,the subscriptionof saidstockin anticipa-
tion of this act ; and whenever three hundred shares shall
be subscribed, said association may go into operation .
Other shares may subsequently be subscribed, until the
whole stock is taken , the subscribers thereto being re-
quired to pay the back installmentscorrespondingwith
those paid by the original subscribers, with interest at
such rate,not exceeding sixper cent.,as the association
may prescribe in its by -laws : Provided, That no stock-
holder shallacquire or hold,at any time ,more than fifty
shares of stock . Said stock shall be transferable on the
books of said association as may be provided in its by-
laws .
§4 . The object of said association being to enable its
members ,by theirearnings and savings,to acquire homes
and other property,itsbusiness shallbe to loan its capi-
tal stock as paid in,and the accumulations thereof, only
upon mortgages and loans upon real estateample in
amount to secure the same ,preference being given in all
cases to members of said association ; and said loans shall
be made at such rate of interest,not exceeding six per
cent.,as may be agreed by the parties thereto,together
with such premiums therefor as may be offered by the
partiesto whom the loans may be made .
§ 5. As soon as practicable after this association is
ready to go into operation as provided in sectionfour of
thisact,the corporations shallcalla meeting of the stock-
holders, giving reasonable notice of time and place of
such meeting ,for the electionof officers,who shall serve
for one year,and untiltheirsuccessorsare electedand
enterupon the dischargeof theirrespectiveduties. An

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1079
mually thereafter,at such time as may be fixedby the by-
laws to be adopted by the stockholders,the officersshall
be elected; and said stockholders shallhave authority to
pass and make any by -laws for the government of said
corporation,not inconsistentwith thischarter or the laws
of this State or the Constitution of the United States .
§ 6. Said corporationmay providein itsby -laws what
penalties shall attach for failureto pay the installments
of stock at the time fixed therefor, and shall have full
power to enforce such penalties,whether the same shall
be by fine or forfeitureof such shares. It may also pro-
vide upon what terms members desiring to do so may
withdraw from the association; and it may also direct
what officersshall be required to execute bonds , with
security,forthe faithfulperformance of theirduties,and
the amount of penalty in said bonds ; and said bonds ,
when executed ,shallbe fullybinding upon the obligors
thereto; and may requirethe payment quarterly,in ad-
vance ,of fivecents per week on such share,to meet cur-
rent expenses , and also fixan initiationfee of one dollar
on each share .
§7 . The officersand directorsmay adopt any rulesfor
conducting the business of the association not in conflict
with thisact,the by -laws of said association, or the laws
of Kentucky or the Constitution aforesaid.
§ 8. Said corporation shall have power to issue and
sellor hypothecate any mortgage debt itmay hold,if at
any time itmay become necessary to do so.
§ 9. In additionto the mortgage or otherlien or security
which may be given by a stockholder to secure any loan
which may be created, a firstlien is given to the associa-
tion upon the interestand stock of such share-holder in
said association to secure it in said lien or indebted-
ness ; and the board of directors of said association is
authorized to invest,from time to time ,any part of the
money of the association that may be on hand in ex-
cess of the amount required for the time being for loans
and retirement of stocks,in bank stocks or bonds of any
kind; and may loan any part of such money to stock-

1080 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
holders upon the securityof theirstock in the association
as collateral, to secure payment of such loan or loans
respectively; and may loan any part of such money to
persons not stockholders,upon personal securityor col-
laterals, without requiring a mortgage upon real estate.
§ 10.This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884.

CHAPTER 1393 .
AN ACT to revisethe charter of the cityof Paducah , and reduce intoone
the severalactsrelatingthereto.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the boundaries of the cityof Paducah shall
be as follows,to-wit: Beginning at a pointknown as the
northwest corner of lotnumber fifteen, of the division of
the estate of William Clarke , on the south side of the
Ohio river,one hundred and seventy -four poles below the
north side of Clay street; thence south forty-three degrees
west threehundred and forty-two poles,to the corner of
Paducah Cemetery ; then south fifteendegrees sixteenmin-
utes east, with the line between said cemetery and the
addition of Flournoy , Harris, Trimble ,and Norton ,sev-
enty-threeand one-halfpoles,to an ironmonument inthe
center of Clay street; thence south thirty-four degrees
east with the line between King and Bradshaw , ninety
polesto a stake; thence north fifty-six degrees east fif-
teen poles to an iron monument ; thence south thirty-nine
degrees east with the line dividing lot number thirteen
from lotsnumbered five,four,three, and two ,of the divis-
ion of the estateof Wm .Clarke,three hundred and twenty-
ninepolesto a mulberryin the base linemarked one and
two ;thence south sixty-seven degrees east with said base
lineto the center of Island creek,at the mouth of Cross
creek; thence in the center of Island creek,and with the
meanders thereof, to its mouth on the Tennessee river;
thence with a course north fifty-sixdegrees eastto the low

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1081
water linethereof on Tennessee Island; thence down the
Tennessee river, with the low -water line thereof,binding
on Tennessee Island,tothe lower end of said island;thence
north fifty-six degrees east across the Ohio river to the
low -water line of the same on the Illinoisshore ; thence
down the Ohio river with the low -water line thereof ,bind-
ing on the Illinoisshore,to a pointwhere a linefrom the
beginning point of thisboundary above described,with a
course north forty-three degrees east, will intersectsaid
low -water line; thence south forty-three degrees west
across the Ohio river to the beginning ; and the territory
embraced within thisboundary ishereby declared to be a
city, and the inhabitants thereof are created a body -pol-
iticforever, with perpetual succession,by the name of
the cityof Paducah , and by that name retainingand
possessingthe power to sue and be sued, plead and
be impleaded , contract and be contracted with ; to have
and to use a common seal,and the same to alter,change ,
and renew at pleasure; and to hold,enjoy,lease,or sell,
or dispose of any property now vested in the city of
Paducah , or any person or corporation for said city,or
which may be hereafter acquired ,to the intent and pur-
poses forwhich the same is,was , or may be granted , not-
withstanding itslocality; and in allsuitsagainst the city
serviceor processshallbe (served) upon the mayor ,and
in his absence upon the clerk and marshal of said city.
The cityshallhave policejurisdictionin Kentucky ,out-
side the corporate limits,for one and a half miles all
around from said boundary .
§2 . The cityshallbe divided into sixwards ,as follows: Wards.
FirstWard . All that portion of the city north of the
center line of Monroe street, extended from the river out
to the corporation line.
SecondWard . All that portionof the citybetween the
firstward , as above described, and Broadway street.
Third Ward . Allthatportionofthecitybetween Broad-
way and the centerlineof Washington street,extended
from the riverout to the corporation line.

FourthWard . All thatportionof the citybetween the
third ward , as above described,and the center line of

tion line .
FifthWard . All that portion of the citybetween the
center line of Adams street and the center line of Ten-
nesseestreet,extendingfrom the riverto the corporation
line .
Sixth Ward . All that portion of the city between the
center line of Tennessee street and the southern bound-
ary line of the city, extending from the river to the
boundary line aforesaid. The boundaries of said wards
may be changed ,or the number of the wards increased,
by the council of said city. Representation shall be as
nearly equal and uniform as may be , and regulated by
the number of the qualifiedvoters.
Powers of Government .
§3 . The corporate powers of the city of Paducah shall
be divided into three distinct parts,viz : legislative, ex-
ecutive,and judicial; and no officerof one of the depart-
ments shallexerciseany power properlybelonging to
either of the others,except in the instances herein ex-
pressly permitted .
LegislativeDepartment .
§4 . The legislativepower shall be vested in the coun-
cil of the city of Paducah , which shall consist of two
members from each ward in the city,electedby the qual-
ified voters of each ward , as herein directed , and hold
theirofficesfor two years,and until theirsuccessors are
duly elected and qualified. The election for councilmen
shall be held on the firstMonday in March in each year .
§5 . No person shallbe eligibleas a councilman of the
city of Paducah who ,at the time of his election, is not
a male citizenof the United States,who has not attained
the age of twenty -four years , and who has not resided
within the corporate limitsof said cityfor two years next
preceding his election,and who is not a bona fideresident
of the ward for which he may be chosen ; and if he or

§8 . No person shall be ineligibleby reason of his

§ 9. The council shall keep , or cause to be kept , a cor-
rect journal of its proceedings , and immediately after
adjournment of each session, at which one or more or-
dinances are passed , and before enforcing same , shall
cause such ordinance or ordinances to be published at
leastonce,in some newspaper publishedand printedin
Paducah , to be selected annually by the council, or as
may be prescribedby ordinance; and itshallbe the duty

§ 12. A majority of the members of the council shall
constitutea quorum to do business; but a smaller number
may adjourn from day to day,and may compel the at-

such penalties as may be prescribed by ordinance .

liceofficersthus servingas door-keeper or sergeant-at-
arms shall not receive additional compensation for such

§ 15. The council shalljudge of the qualification,elec-
tion,and returnof itsown members ,and shallprescribe,
by ordinance,the manner in which any or allcontested
elections shall be determined .

itantsof the city.

ityat such election shallvote in favor of the ordinance ,

nance .

§ 57. The council shall have power ,at eitherof the
regularmeetings in the month of April,one thousand
eighthundred and eighty-four,to electthree persons to
be styled trustees of Oak Grove Cemetery , and said trus-
tees shall have the control and superintendence of said

council,and who shall perform the duties prescribed by

regular meetings in the month of June , one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-five,shall elect three persons

hundred and eighty-five, or until their successors are

tees by election,at the firstmeeting of the council after

§58. The councilshallhave the power to defend by
ordinance what shallconstitutevagrancy ,and the punish-
ment thereof .
§ 59. The council,in the name of the city,shallhave
power to subscribeforand hold and sellstockin any pub-
lic corporation for the building of roads and bridges in
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and in one or more
railroad companies for the building of railroads in the
northwest side of the Ohio river, terminating in Paducah ,
to be paid by the bonds of the city,bearing interestnot

exceeding fiveper cent.per annum ,the subscriptionnot

and to purchase and hold any personal or real estate

cilshallnot have the power , on behalfof the city, to
createany new debt or liabilityexceeding twenty thou-
sand dollarsfor one objector purpose,nor subscribefor
stock in any incorporated company ,or give the bonds of

sum of twenty thousand dollars; nor shall the council
have power to appropriate money ,directlyor indirectly,
for any object or purpose in amount exceeding the sun
of twenty thousand dollars, unless said council shallfirst
cause an election to be held by the qualified voters of

stock or appropriation of money for the object or pur-
pose proposed; and said propositionshallbe voted for
affirmativelyby a majority of all the qualified voters of
the city, which majority shall be ascertained by the as-
sessor'sbook made at the last assessment next previous
to the holding of such election; and it shallbe the duty
of the assessor, in his assessments of property , to dili-
gently inquire of all persons assessed, and ascertain
whether they are qualifiedvoters, and to listin a column
upon his book all qualifiedvotersof the city; and if,
upon taking such vote,thereshouldbe a majorityof all
the qualifiedvoters of said cityin favor of the proposi-
tion submitted to them ,and the council thereafter, by a
vote of two thirds of all the members in office,evidenced
by the records of the council upon a callof the yeas and
nays, shallpass an ordinance in favor of, or directing
such a subscription of stock, the creation of such new
debt or liability,or the appropriationof money exceed-
ing twenty thousand dollars: Provided, That the coun-
cilshallhave the power ,without submitting the question
to a vote of the people,to execute the bonds or obliga-
tionsof the cityrenewing any existingliabilitiesof the

city. Said council may give the bonds of the city in
such sums , and payable at such times, as they shalldeem
most expedient .
§60 . The councilshallhave power to licenseand regu-

ful regulations for taking of the votes of the qualified

persons within the citylimits, and make and declare the

in. In no case shallprivate property be taken for public
use without the written consent of the owner or owners
thereof,or unless the compensation thereforshallbe first
fixed by agreement ,or by the condemnation thereof as
herein provided ,and the compensation paid to the owners
thereof out of the treasury. The city shallnot receive or
take cognizance of any streethereafter attempted to be
laid out, unless the same be at leastsixtyfeet wide .
§ 63. The council shallhave power by ordinance ,upon

adamizing or graveling, guttering, curbing, and paving

§ 65. The council may require,by ordinance ,the dig-

§ 66. Upon the passage of any ordinancerequiringthe

ury .

Sinking Fund .
§ 80. There is hereby establisheda sinking fund for the
purpose of payment of the bonded debts of the city of
Paducah , and the payment of the intereston the same ;
and the mayor and the finance committee of the council
of the cityare hereby constituted commissioners of said
sinking fund.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1103
1. All licenses or special taxes hereinafter set forth
shall be paid into said fund ,and be held sacred to the
purposes set forth in this section; and no part of said
fund shallbe loaned or diverted,temporarilyor perma-
nently, directlyor indirectly, to any other fund or use of
said city. The treasurer of the cityshall be the treas-
urer of the sinking fund ,and shallbe liablefor the same
upon his officialbond as treasurerof the cityof Paducah ;
and said treasurershallkeep the money and accountsof
said fund separate from other money and accounts of said
city; and shall receive for his services as treasurer of
the sinkingfund one per cent.on the amount of money
that may be received and disbursed by him as such treas-
urer, to be paid quarterly out of saidfund .
2. The commissioners and treasurer of the sinking
fund shall make quarterlyreportsto the councilof the
city, commencing September one, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-four,showing the source of income ,
and the expenditures, properlytabulated,and shallac-
company the report with allvouchers for disbursements ,
and showing the balance remaining in the treasury.
They shall,during each and every quarter,keep the ac
cumulation , over and above the interestcharge ,paid out
in the purchase of the bonds of the city, where the same
ispracticable : Provided,Thatin no year shallthe amount
of bonds so purchased exceed fivethousand dollars; and
any surplus,after paying the interestand the purchase
of such amount of bonds,shallbe covered intothe gen-
eral fund of the city. The accumulations of the sinking
fund shallbe applied,first,to the payment of the interest
on the bonded debt; and any surplusthereaftershallbe
expended in the purchase of the outstandingbonds of
the cityat not exceedingpar,and the expenses of man-
aging said fund .
3. The coupons and bonds paid offby the commission-
ers shallbe deliveredto the council, and the clerk of the
council shallre-cancel and paste the coupons in the ap-
propriatecoupon-books,and the bonds shallbe burned in
the presence of the councilwhile in session. If at any

time the income herein provided forthe sinking fund shall
be insufficient to meet the interest on the bonded debts of
said city,the council shall make a levy sufficientto make
up the deficiency,or may loan the sinking fund a suffi-
cient amount until the resources are increased .
4. The councilof the cityof Paducah shall,by a gen-
eral ordinance , or by special ordinances , provide, prior
to June first,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,
for the following licenses,to be paid into the sinking fund
on and afterthat date , with adequate penaltiesfor doing

ituous, vinous, or fermented liquors, not less than five
dollarsnor more than two hundred dollarsper annum .

or performance in the cityfor pay ,or any publichall,
such sum as the council may prescribeby ordinance ; lit-
erary and charitable entertainments may be allowed by
the council without license .

or trade,not lessthan fivenor more than twenty -fivedol-
lars .
7. For every lottery office, or agency therefor,author-
ized by the laws of thisCommonwealth ,not lessthan one
hundred and not more than five hundred dollars .

table,bagatelleor pigeon-hole table,or shootinggallery,
not lessthan twenty nor more than one hundred dollars.
9. For hotels, taverns, boarding -houses entertaining
transient guests, restaurants, eating-houses , or club-

less than one quart, not less than twenty -five nor more

11. For private boarding -houses not receiving or enter-

tablishment wherein spirituous,vinous ,malt,or fermented

Executive Department.
§81 . The executive power of the city of Paducah shall
be vested in the mayor of the city of Paducah , who shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1107
be elected by the qualifiedvoters of the city for the term
of two years,and untilhis successoris duly electedand
qualified .
§ 82. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof mayor
*who isineligibleto the officeof councilman,except as to
his residence in any particularward of the city.
$ 83. The mayor shallreceive as compensation for his
services not less than five hundred dollars nor more than
twelve hundred dollarsper annum , payable quarterly out
of the citytreasury,to be fixedby the councilby ordi-
nance previous to his election,and which shall not be
increased or diminished during his term of office.
§ 84. The mayor shallbe the chiefof the police, and
may command them in the performance of executive du-
ties; shallbe a conservatorof the publicpeace,and,when
deemed necessary by him to enforce the laws to save life
or property,or to quellriotsor mobs ,he may summon into
serviceany of the citizens, eithercivilor military, and in
such cases he must be present and command in person ;
and the council shall, by ordinance , prescribe suitable
finesand penaltiesfor disobedience of the mayor's orders
and summons .
§ 85. He shall see that the laws and ordinances of the
city are enforced and faithfullyexecuted .
§86 . The mayor shall preside at all meetings of the
council, decide all points of order, and shall have the
same power during the session to enforce good order
and decorum ,and to punish contempt,thatthe presiding
judges of the circuitcourts have by law . He may re-
quire information in writing from all officersof the city
upon any subjectsrelatingto theirrespectiveoffices.
87. He shallfrom time to time give to the council in-
formation of the condition of the corporation,and recom-
mend to their consideration such measures as he may
deem expedient.
§ 88. He shallperform and dischargesuch dutiesas
may be prescribedby ordinance, not inconsistentwith
this charter .

1108 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§89.He shallcallspecialsessionsofthecouncil
whenever , in his judgment ,it is necessary, or upon re-
quest of fivecouncilmen in writing.
$ 90 . He shall give the casting vote when the council
may be equally divided upon any question or ordinance
not requiring a larger vote than a majority of the mem-
bers present.
( 91. He shallhave power to administeroaths of of-
ficeand give certificatestherefor.
§ 92. In case of death ,resignation,removal from office,
or inabilityor refusalto act,or absence of the mayor from
the cityat the time of a regular meeting ,the clerk of the
council shallcallthe council to order and proceed to hold
an electionfor mayor pro tem. The councilshallthen
electone of theirnumber as mayor pro tem.,who shallex-
ercisethe powers and discharge the duties of mayor dur-
ing the absence of the mayor from the city,or untilthe
disabilitybe removed ,or a new mayor shall be elected
and qualified; and in case the mayor is absent from the
meetings of the council,but in the city,the council shall
electfrom theirown body a presidingofficer,who shallex-
ercise none of the other powers of mayor . In case of
death ,inabilityto act,or absence of the mayor from the
city,the clerk of the councilshall,upon the writtenre-
quest of seven members ,call a meeting of the council,
and upon its assembling proceed to organize the body
by holding an electionas above set forth.
§93 . The mayor pro tem. shall receive as compensa-
tion for his services at the same rate allowed to the
mayor forthe time he thus acts,payable out of the treas-
ury,and a likeamount may be deducted from the salary
of the mayor .
§94. Every proposed ordinancewhich has passed the
council shallbe presentedto the mayor ,and ifhe ap-
proves the same he shallsign it,and itshallthen become
an ordinance; but ifhe disapprovesit,he shallreturn it
to the council,with his objections in writing,which shall
be entered at large upon the journal; and the council
shallproceed to reconsiderthe proposed ordinance,and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1109
ifit is then adopted by a majorityof allthe members
elected,it shallbe an ordinance ,the mayor's vote to the
contrary notwithstanding . In such cases the vote shall
be taken by yeas and nays , and recorded in the journal ;
and if the mayor hold up a proposed ordinance beyond
the next regular meeting of the council,it shall be an
ordinance as though signed by him .
§95 . The mayor shall have power to fillvacancies in
any of the executive officesof the cityby appointment ,
which shall expire when such vacancies are filled, as
herein provided. He shallhave power to appoint, by
and with the adviceand consentof the council,allpolice-
men and watchmen . He may , in case of emergency ,
appoint supernumerary policemen , without the advice
and consent of the council,to serve not longer than the
next meeting of the council. All watchmen and police-
men thus appointed may be removed at the pleasure of
the mayor .
JudicialDepartment .
§96. The judicialpowers of the corporationshallbe
vestedin and exercisedby a court,to be styledthe Padu-
cah City Court ,which shall be held by a single judge ,
styledthe Judge of the Paducah City Court,who shall
be elected by the qualifiedvoters of the cityon the first
Monday in August , who shallhold his officefor the term
of fouryears,and untilhissuccessorshallbe dulyelected
and qualified; and he shall take the same oath that cir-
cuit judges are required by law to take .
§97 . When ,from any cause ,the judge of the Paducah
city court fails to attend, or if in attendance cannot
properlypresidein a cause pending in court,the attor-
neys of the court who are present shallelect one of
theirmembers then in attendance,who shallpossessthe
same qualificationsprescribedfor the judge,to hold the
courtforthe occasion,who shallpresideand adjudicate,
having firsttaken the oath as prescribedfor the judge.
The election shall be held by the judge of the court if
present; ifnot, by the oldestmember of the bar then
present .

1110 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 98. The person thus elected shallqualifybefore the
judge of said court,or some magistrate or notary public;
and during the period he acts shallhave all the power ,
and be liableto allthe responsibilities,of the judge of
said court. The period of service of such special judge
shall be certifiedto the council by the said court; and
the councilshallprescribeby ordinancethe amount that
shallbe paid to such specialjudge ,and in what cases,if
any ; the amount paid to the specialjudge shallbe de-
ducted from the salary of the judge .
§ 99. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof judge
of the citycourt unless he be a male citizen of the city,
at leasttwenty-fiveyears of age, and been a licensed
practicing lawyer five years next preceding his election,
and who has resided within the city at least one year
next preceding his election.
§100 . The Paducah city court shall have exclusive
originaljurisdictionin all proceedings or prosecutions
for the violationsof ordinances of the city, and concur-
rent jurisdictionwith the circuitcourt of all violations
of the penal laws of thisCommonwealth arising within
the limits of the city of Paducah , or occurring within
one mile and a half of the citylimits, except cases of fel-
ony ; and said court shall have power and authority to
take recognizances from persons charged with offenses
cognizable before said court to appear and answer , and
to take recognizance for good behavior and to keep the
peace,and power to take the same as forfeited,and pro-
nounce judgment thereon ,and to enforce fullcompliance
with the same ,and have the power of two justicesof the
peace as an examining court.
§101 . This court shallhave jurisdictionof alleases of
vagrancy arisingwithin the citylimits.
§102 . The judge of the citycourt shallbe a conserva-
tor of the public peace ,and may order arrestsfor allof-
fenses against the laws of the State or ordinances of the
city, and for those committed within his presence may
order arrestswithout warrant ; the person offending to be

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1111
dealt with according to the laws of the State and the or-
dinances of the city.
§103 . The citycourt shallbe holden at such places
within the cityas the council shall designate,or, itfail-
ing to designate or provide a suitableplace,at such place
as the judge may select. The judge shallhave the power
to fix the time of holding court as the business before him
may seem to require.
§104 . The Paducah citycourt shallbe a court of rec-
ord,and have a seal, which shallbe provided by the coun-
cil,and which shallbe and remain in custody of the judge
of said court,to be used and affixedby him as the seals of
the circuitcourt are; and certifiedcopies of the records
of said court,over the signature of the judge ,shallbe evi-
dence in any court of this Commonwealth .
§ 105. All proceedingsin, and process from, the city
court, when the fines are imposed under the laws of
Kentucky ,shallbe in the name of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky ,and when the finesand penaltiesare under
the ordinancesof the city,proceedingsand processshall
be in the name of the cityof Paducah . The process shall
be made returnable to the citycourt,and may be directed
to any sheriff, coroner , marshal ,jailer, constable,or po-
liceman in this State,or the assistantchief of police of
the city of Paducah ; and the person executing the pro-
cess of said court shall be entitled to the like fees for like
services,and to the likeremedy for the collectionthereof,
as the sheriffof McCracken county ,and shallbe subject
to the likefinesand penaltiesfor like offensesand failure
of duty , recoverable in the same manner .
§ 106. The fines and forfeituresrecovered in the city
court in the name or in favor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , or in the name of the cityof Paducah , and
also allfinesand forfeituresin allcases that are appealed
from said citycourt to any court of this Commonwealth ,
recovered in such court appealed to, are hereby granted
to the cityof Paducah .
§ 107. All fines and forfeituresrecovered in the city
court shallbe collectedby the assistantchief of police,
Assessments .

1112 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
or other officerto whom execution or capiasprofine is
directed,and be paid by him to the treasurer; but before
the issue of the execution or other final process,the de-
fendant may pay the fine or forfeitureto the assistant
chief of police.
§108 . The defendant shall have the right to replevy
forthree months any judgment , fine, or forfeitureof the
city court, or writ of execution, or other finalprocess
therein,by giving bond ,with good security,in the same
manner allowed by law in similar cases in favor of the
Commonwealth .
§109. A returnof " notfound " on a capiasprofine,and
of " no property found " on a writ offierifaciasissued
on any judgment in the city court, shall authorize an
attachment in favor of the Commonwealth , or the city of
Paducah ,against the choses in action or effectsof the
defendant or defendants , in the same manner that a re-
turn of " no property found " authorizes an attachment
on judgment in the circuitcourt.
§110. Upon alljudgments for finesrendered by the
citycourt,whether in favor of the Commonwealth or of
the cityof Paducah ,it shallbe lawful for the attorney
of the cityto cause a fi. fa. to be issued,to be levied on
the estateof thedefendant,or to take a capiaspro fine,
requiring the imprisonment of the defendant in the city
work -house or county jail,or confined at work upon the
streetsuntilthefineispaid; but the person so imprisoned
shallhave the rightto discharge the same by labor in the
city work -house , if there be one , or on the streets or
publicworks of the city,at the rate of one dollar per
day ; and , after agreeing so to do , every such person
shall conform to the rules of the work -house or regula-
tions for working on the streets,as regards labor or dis-
cipline,unless, afterhe shallhave made such election,he
replevy or pay the judgment ,or such part thereof as re-
mains unpaid .
§111 . In all cases where fines shall hereafter be im-
posed by the judgments of the Paducah city court for
violation of the laws of this Commonwealth , or the ordi-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1113
nances of saidcity,any defendant who shallnot,at the
time of rendering such judginents,have property out of
which the judgment can be made ,or who shall fail to
immediatelypay or replevythe same , and when such in-
solventpersonso finedmay be confinedinthe cityprison,
work -house,or county jail,or worked upon the streetsof
the city,no officerof the cityshallhave, charge,or be
allowed any costs,fee,or part of such fine,for any ser-
vicessuch officeror officersmay render in such prosecu-
tions; in all such cases,when insolvent persons shallbe
made to work out theirfinesupon the public streets,no
officerofthe cityshallhave any claim or demand against
the cityfor or on account of any fees or part of such
fine,or for or on account of the fact that such persons
shallhave paid their fines by working on the streetsor
public work of the city.
§ 112. The Paducah city court shall always be open
for the hearing and determining of such cases as may
be heard and determined without the intervention of a
grand jury ; and the court may make such reasonable
rulesand regulationsforthe speedy hearing and determin-
ing of cases as to affordreasonable notice to the parties.
§113 . The judge of said court shallhave the power to
grant injunctions and attachments the same as county
judges of the Commonwealth ,and shallbe entitledto a
fee of one dollar for every injunction or attachment .
§114 . The judge of Paducah city court shall be the
clerk of his own court,and shallkeep the records of his
court, and do and perform all duties of clerk; and all
copiesof recordsof hiscourt,duly certifiedby him ,shall
be legal evidence in allcourts; and the judge may charge
and tax as costs, and have the same fees for his services
in prosecutions and for copies, as allowed by law to the
clerk of the McCracken circuit court for similar services .
§115 . The judge of the Paducah citycourt shallreceive
a salary of sixhundred dollarsper annum ,payable quar-
terly out of the treasury; and in addition thereto, fees
from his officenot exceeding four hundred dollars per
annum ; and itshallnot be diminished , after being fixed,

1114 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
during his term of office; which salary shallbe fixedby
the council within one month prior to the election of
such judge every four years. The excess over and above
the sum of four hundred dollars in fees,which he may
actuallycollect per year in cash , shall be paid to the
treasurer of the city,so that he shall not receive in all
exceeding one thousand dollarscompensation per annum
for his servicesas judge of said court,
§ 116. All appeals from the Paducah citycourt shallbe
to the circuitcourt of McCracken county .
MinisterialDepartment- CityAttorney.
§ 117. There shallbe electedby the qualifiedvoters of
said city, at the times and places provided for the regu-
lar election of city judge , one city attorney, who shall
hold his officefor the term of four years, and until his
successor is duly elected and qualified; and he shall
receive a salary not to exceed four hundred dollars per
annum ,to be paid out of the treasuryin such manner as
the council may prescribe by ordinance ; and shall be
entitledto have and receive thirtyper cent.of all fines
and forfeituresrecovered in the Paducah city court,or in
any court to which a case originating in the city court
may be appealed during his officialterm ; in which cases
the saidcityattorneyshallprosecutethe offenders,which
shall be in full of all compensation .
§118 . Itshallbe the duty of thecityattorneyto pros-
ecute before the citycourt all cases for violationof the
laws of the State, or ordinances of the city, of which
said court has jurisdiction; and to prosecute all officers
formalfeasance or misfeasance in office,or willfulneglect
of duty ; and to perform other dutiesas may be prescribed
by ordinance .
§ 119. It shallbe the duty of the city attorney to cause
-and carry on prosecutions against allpersons guiltyof any
violationof the penal laws of thisCommonwealth with-
in one mile and a half of the citylimitsof Paducah in the
illegalsales or giving of spirituous, vinous , malt ,or fer-
mented liquors; and in addition to the oath now required

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1115
by law he shall take the following oath before entering
upon the duties of his office,to-wit: " I do solemnly swear
(or affirm) that,without fear, favor, or affection,as city
attorney,I willin good faithreasonableand trueinquiry
make ,and , as far as in my power lies,ascertain allviola-
tions of the penal laws of thisCommonwealth ,in the ille-
gal saleor giving of spirituous,malt,or vinous,or ferment-
ed liquors within the city,or within one mile and a half
of the city of Paducah ; and I will in good faith, and to
my best skilland ability, cause the issue of warrants
against allsuch offenders, and prosecute the same ; and
that I will in no instance compromise or compound such
prosecutions,but I willdiligentlyprosecutethe same to
judgment . So help me God ."
§ 120. No person shall be eligibleto the officeof city
attorney who is not at the time of his election a male
citizen, twenty four years of age , and a bona fide resi-
dent of the cityforat leastone year next preceding his
election, nor unless he shall have been a licensed prac-
ticing lawyer for more than two years next preceding his
election ; and if, after his election ,he shall remove from
the city,or do any act which would render him ineligible
to such office,he shallthereby vacate the same .
§ 121. In the case of the failureof the cityattorney to
attend the Paducah citycourt and prosecute for the city,
said court shallhave the power to appoint a cityattorney
pro tem.,who shallprosecutein the absence of the city
attorney,and be entitledto the same fees therefor that
the cityattorneyisallowed herein.
§ 122. For any malfeasanceor misfeasancein office,or
any willfulomission or neglect of duty , the city attorney
shall be subject to indictment, and fined for each and
every offense not less than twenty -five nor more than
one hundred dollars; and , upon conviction thereof, his
office shall be vacant .
Assistant Chief Police.
§ 123. The council shall elect an assistantchief of
police,whose dutiesshallbe to attend all meetings of
the council when required, and to attend all sessions

1116 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of the citycourt. He shall, within the limitsof the city,
in allmatters of a criminal nature arisingunder the ordi-
nances of the city,or under the laws of this Common-
wealth ,possess the same power and perform the same
duties as sheriffor constable. He shall execute allpro-
cess,civilor criminal, issued and to him directed by the
mayor , the council, Paducah city court, the city judge ,
the presiding judge of the McCracken county court, or
any justiceof the peace. He shallcollectallexecu-
tions and final process to him directed by any of the
officersabove named ,and pay over all moneys collected
by him to the parties entitledto the same ,in the same
manner ,and under the same responsibilitiesand penal-
ties, as sheriffsare by law authorized and required to do .
His powers for discharginghis officialduties shallbe
co -extensive with McCracken county .
§ 124. The council shallhave the power to regulate,by
ordinance ,furtherduties of said assistantchief of police;
and forcompensation he shallbe entitledto the same fees
allowed by law to sheriffsfor similarservices,the collec-
tion of which may be enforced as provided for the collec-
tion of sheriff'sfee bills, and he shallalso be entitledto
have and receive eight per cent. of all fines and forfeit-
ures recovered in the city court of Paducah collectedby
him ; and the council shallhave the power to allow said
assistantchief of police a sum not to exceed fiftydollars
per month , to be paid out of the citytreasuryin such
manner as the councilshallprescribeby ordinance.
§ 125. The councilshallhave power ,by a two -thirds
vote of all the members composing the entire council,
forgood cause,to remove said assistantchiefof police.
The assistantchief of police shall be elected at the first
meeting in June , and hold his officefor the term of two
years. The term of the present incumbent is extended
until June ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-five,
and until his successor is elected and qualified .
§ 126. Any person injured by a breach of the assistant
chiefof police'scovenant may ,in any court having ju-
risdiction,prosecute suit on motion thereon against the

assistantchief police and his securities,as now provided
by law for sheriffs.
§ 127.For any malfeasanceor misfeasancein office,or
any willfulneglect or omission of duty ,the assistantchief
police shall be subject to indictment, and fined for each
and every offensenot lessthan twenty fivedollarsnor
more than one hundred dollars, and ,upon conviction, his
office shall become vacant .
City Assessor.
§ 128. The councilshallhave the power ,and itishere
by made its duty , to elect, at its last regular meeting in
December in each year ,an assessor,to hold his officefor
one year,and untilhissuccessorisdulyelectedand qual-
ified. The compensation of the assessor shall be fixed
by the council at their last meeting in December , and
prior to his appointment .
§ 129. The assessorof the city of Paducah shallassess
at cash value all the property within the citysubject to
taxation under this charter,against each owner thereof,
owned by him or her,or any corporation,on the tenth day
of January of each year , except stores, and it shall be
the duty of said assessorto assess against the respective
owners thereof,on the firstday of Aprilof each year,all
stores owned by them at that date,at a faircash valua-
tion of their respective goods ,wares ,and merchandise .
The assessor shall assess property at a reasonable cash
valuation at the time of the assessment ,and as near equal
and uniform ,according to the cash value of every man's
property , rich or poor , much or little, as the assessor's
bestjudgment willenable him to fix; and beforeacting
as assessor he shall take an oath ,in addition to the oath
now prescribed by this charter," " that he will perform
the duties of his officewithout fear,favor,or affection;
and that he willdo justicealiketo the rich or poor,
according to his best skilland judgment ." The assessor
shallmake allassessments in each year between the tenth
day of January and the fifteenthday of April; itshallbe
the duty of the assessor to listwithin the same time all

1118 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
male citizensoverthe age oftwenty-one years,who shall
residein the cityon the day of the assessment ,and return
the names and number thereof upon his book , noting the
colored persons on his book .
§ 130. It shall be the duty of the assessor to make a
complete book of allassessments,showing the names of
allpersons assessed, in alphabetical order, and a fulland
complete description of all the kinds of personal prop-
erty of each assessed, and the separate and aggregate
valuation thereof against each owner . It shall be the
duty of the assessor, in assessingthe real estate in Padu-
cah , to give the name of each owner as aforesaid, and
describeeach and every lot, block, or piece of ground
owned by him or her which shallbe assessed. He shall,
in his assessment ,describe by number of lot and block of
the real estate, and give other description thereof in all
cases of fractional lots, when the number of lot and
block will not sufficientlyidentifyit,that will, with rea-
sonable certainty, designate the same , and apprise the
owner thereof upon examining the assessor'sbook of
the assessment of the same ; and the assessor shall fix
the value of each lotor parcel of ground , and the aggre-
gate value of each person's whole real estate assessed;
and the assessor shall return his book to the office of the
clerk of the council, with the whole assessment of the
citycomplete,on or beforethe fifteenthday of April of
each year .
§ 131. After the assessor shall have returned his book to
the council,and the same shallbe revisedby the super-
visors of tax, and shall be ,by order or resolutionof the
council,received and ordered to be filed, the assessor's
books shall become and be a public record in the office
of the clerk of the council ; and after the council shall
have fixed, by order or resolution, the ad valorem tax
upon allthetaxablepropertyof thecity,and theamount of
poll-tax forthe year ,which per centum and rate of taxation
theyshallfixby orderor resolutionin the month of April
of each year , the assessor'sbook and the order of the
councilfixingthe per centum or rate of taxationupon

the assessedpropertyfor the year,shallhave the effect
and operation,to allintentsand purposes , of a judgment

ively, as shown or ascertained by the assessment and

Supervisors of Tax .
§ 132. The council shall,at itslast regular meeting in
December of each year , appoint three discreetcitizensof
the city as supervisors of tax,shall meet at the council
chamber ,withinfivedays afterthe returnof the assessor's
book to the council , and examine with care the assessor's
report of the taxable property returned for the year ,and
correct any errors of the assessor, whether in fact or in
relation to the valuation of the estate listed; and in cases
where they shallbe of opinion that the estate has been
incorrectlyvalued,to fixthe same at itsproper value;
but they shallnot increasethe valuation made by the
assessor without summoning the owner or owners , if
residingin the city,to appear before them to show cause
why the change should not be made .
§ 133. Itshallbe the duty of the assessorto attend the
sessionsof the supervisors of tax, and give evidence and
informationconcerning the businessbeforethem ,upon
oath , if required; and they shall have power to compel
the attendance and examination of witnesses before them
to enable them to discharge theirduties.
§ 134. The supervisors of tax shall keep a record of
their proceedings, and correct the tax-books thereby .
They shall make fullreports of theiracts to the council.
They shallalso report and place upon the assessor'sbook
the names of all persons liable to taxation who have
been omitted by the assessorin his book .
§ 135. The council may ,by ordinance , prescribe any
further duties of the supervisors of tax, and fix the
amount of compensation for theirservices.

§136 . The council shallhave power at any time ,within
three months afterthe report of the supervisors, and not
afterwards,to hear complaints , and to change ,reduce , or
correctthe tax-listof any person ; and in case of reduc-
tion,if the person has paid his tax,to order the proper
porportionthereofto be refunded.
§ 137. There shall be but one assessor to assess the
taxablepropertyof Paducah ,and there shallbe but one
collector to collect the taxes under such assessment ; and
so much of the acts and amendatory acts chartering the
New Orleans and Ohio Railroad Company , or of any
other acts, as required the appointment of an assessor
and collectorof Paducah to assessthe property of the
city,and collectthe taxes thereunderto pay the interest
or principal of the bonds of the old town of Paducah ,
issued and paid to said New Orleans and Ohio Railroad
Company forstockin saidcompany ,be,and the same are
hereby , repealed, and the sinking fund as herein estab-
lished shall be in lieu thereof .
§ 138. It shall be the duty of the council, when they
make their levy and fix the rate of taxation upon the
taxable propertyof the cityto meet the aggregate lia-
bilitiesof said city, to designate,in the order fixing said
rate of taxation, what per cent. thereof is intended for
the benefitof the schools of the city,and shalldesignate,
in the order, what part of said aggregate rate of taxa-
tionisintendedto meet and defrayallthe otherexpenses
and liabilitiesof the city; and it shallbe the duty of the
council to furnish the treasurer , as soon as said order
shall be inade, with a copy thereof, and it shall be the
treasurer'sduty , as he receives money from the collector
of taxes,to enter in a book ,by him to be kept for the
purpose ,the true proportion of money as he receives it
according to the order fixing the rate of taxation,defray
the ordinary expenses of the city government ,and other
liabilitiesnot herein named ; and the treasurer shall be
governed accordinglyin hisdisbursements.

Collector of Taxes .
§139 . The council shall elect, at their firstregular
meeting in June in each year ,a collectorof taxes,whose
duty itshallbe to well and faithfullycollect,account for,
and pay over to the cityof Paducah alltaxes leviedby
the city,and who shall hold his officefor one year ,and
untilhis successor shallbe elected and qualified.
§ 140.No one shallbe eligibleto the officeof collector
of taxes unless he be a male citizenof Paducah ,at least
twenty-one years of age,and a bonafideresidentof said
city; but no collectorof taxes shallbe eligibleto re-ap-
pointment or re-electionto the same officeuntilhe shall
have made settlement with the council of allhis accounts,
and paid over allmoney in hishands,and have obtained
from said council a quietus .
§ 141. As soon as the assessor'sbook shallbe revised,
received, and filedin the officeof the clerk of the coun-
cil, and the rate of taxation and the poll-tax shall be
fixed,the clerk of the council,as soon as practicable,and
within thirtydays thereafter, shall make out, in a well
bound book suitablefor the purpose ,the names ,in alpha-
beticalorder,of alltax -payers as shown by the assessor's
book ,and the amount in value according to said assess-
or's book of the real estate and the personal estate as-
sessed against each tax-payer , and the exact amount of
taxes , including poll-tax,if any ,against each tax-payer ,
in favor of the city; which book shallbe called the " col-
lector'stax-book ;" and the same shall,at the end thereof,
be certifiedby the clerk of the council that said collect-
or'stax-book correctlysetsforththe true amount of taxes
for the year due from each tax-payer therein named to
the city of Paducah , which shallbe signed by the clerk.
§ 142. The collector'stax-book shallhave,inthe hands
of the collector,allthe effectand operation,to allintent
and purposes,of an executionat law in favorof the city,
againsteach and everytax-payer and hispropertyforthe
payment and satisfactionof the amount of tax against
each tax-payer respectively. The collector,by virtueof

to levy upon the propertyof the respectivetax-payers to
satisfythe amount due from each tax -payer respectively,

upon thepropertyoftax-payers upon which the cityshall
have a lien for taxes,as upon any other property of the
tax-payer; and he may levy on and sellrealestate to
which the owner may have only an equitable title.
§ 143. It shallbe the duty of the collector,when the

debted to him ,to proceed by attachment to collectsaid

the city shall,to the amount and value of the rent or use
of said lot or lots,if the rents are collected by him , or
the lot or lots are used or occupied or controlledby him ,
pay the taxes on such lot or lots, and so much of his
personalpropertyas may be necessaryto pay the same ,
may be levied on and sold by the collector,in the same
manner as if the said lot or lots were listed in the name
of such husband or owned by him . In like manner may
the taxes be collectedfrom the fatherof a child,guardian ,
agent,trustee,or committee,or receiver,or other person
who may use,enjoy,or collectrents from the property
situatedin said cityof the child, the ward , principal,
cestuique trust,or person of unsound mind .
§145 . If there is no personal property out of which
the collector can make the taxes , and the same are not
paid within three months afterhe receivesthe tax-books
forcollection, he may levy on any real estatein the said

city belonging to or listedto said delinquent tax-payer ,
and sell so much thereof, for cash , as will pay the taxes

cution shallbe applicable to the collector,and regulate

§153 . The collector shall return to the office of the

as other public records,together with his written report
showing the delinquents who shall have failed to pay

by said collectorunder said tax-book upon propertyre-

in which the taxes have not been paid ,shallbe recorded

cordance with the foregoing provisions; and the collector

cil within six months after the expiration of his official
term ,in every case of his sales of property, which sales
itisdeclared his duty to make in allcases when the taxes
are not paid ; which last returns of the collector shall
likewisebe recordedby said clerk at the end of the pre-
viousreturnsrespectively,in the book tobe kept by him
for that purpose .

any such property, with a descriptionand ownership

quent tax-payers for the satisfactionof such taxes,and

or interestof the tax-payer in any property,under the

§ 158. No propertywhatever,except one bed and bed-
ding , and the wearing apparel of the tax -payer ,shallbe
exempt from levy and sale for satisfactionof taxes.
$ 159 . After the tenth day of January in the year the
property subject to taxation under this act shall be as-

uary of the year in which the assessment shallhave been

§ 160. The collectorshall receive a salary of not less
than eight hundred dollars ($ 800) nor more than one
thousand dollars($ 1,000) per annum ,payable quarterly
out of the treasury,for collecting and accounting for the
taxes and public dues, twenty-fiveper cent. of which
shall be retained until the collector shall make his final
settlement, and be entitledto his quietus. No cost or
commission shallbe allowed the collectoron any sales of
propertywhich may be purchased at a tax sale by the
city of Paducah .
§ 161. The fiscalyear of the city of Paducah shall
commence on the firstday of June of each year .
§ 162. The council may electa back tax-collector, and
may prescribe his duties and term of office, and require
him to execute covenant forthe faithfuldischarge of his
duties .

1130 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Auditor .
§ 163. The council shall have the power to elect an
auditor of the cityof Paducah , and shallby ordinance fix
the time of appointment , define his duties,fix the dura-
tion of his office, his compensation , and enforce obedi-
ence to all ordinances or orders of the council regulating
his duties; and the auditor shall have the power to ap-
point a deputy auditor, and he shall be responsible for
the acts of said deputy .
City Treasurer.
§ 164. The council shall,at theirfirstregular meeting
in the month of June of each year ,elect a treasurer of
the cityof Paducah , who shallhold his officefor the
term of one year,and untilhissuccessorisduly elected
and qualified: Provided,however, That the present treas-
urer may hold his officeuntilthe firstregularmeeting
of the council in June ,one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-five, and until his successor shall be duly elected
and qualified.
§ 165. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof treas-
urer unless he be a male citizen,over the age of twenty-
four years,and a bona fideresidentof the cityfor at least
one year next preceding his appointment ; and if, after
his appointment ,he shallremove from the city,his office
shallthereby become vacant .
§ 166. The treasurer shall receive and safelykeep all
the money belonging to the city,and pay out the same
under appropriations made by the council; and he shall
only pay out money upon warrants drawn by the mayor ,
and countersigned by the clerk of the council, with the
seal of the city affixed thereto, and registered and ap-
proved by the auditor, and the warrant to designate the
fund out of which the amount is to be paid . He shall
keep a fairand accurate account of allthe receipts and
expenditures of the city, showing when , from , and to
whom , and for what purpose , and on what account such
moneys are paid out. He shallexhibit to the council at
the firstregular meetings in the months of June ,Septem-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1131
>
ber,December ,and March in each year , a fullstatement
of the receiptsand expendituresof the city, showing
from whom money was received, from what source, and
to whom paid ,and on what account ,during the previous
quarter, and of the state and condition, for the time
being ,of the treasury,and the fiscalconcerns of the city.
His lastreport to June firstin each year shall show the
whole amount of receipts and disbursements of money
for the whole year . He shall perform all other duties
required by ordinance not inconsistentwith this charter.
§ 167. The treasurershallnot be eligibleto re-election
to the same ,or any other officecreatedby thischarter,
until he shall have made settlement with the council of
allhis accounts,and paid over allmoney in his hands
belonging to the city,or any one , by virtue of his office,
to such persons as the council may appoint to receive it,
and obtained from the council his quietus.
168. For malfeasance or misfeasance in office,or any
omission or willfulneglect of duty ,the treasurer may be
removed from officeby the council,and the officedeclared
vacant .
§ 169. The treasurer,upon the expiration of his term
of office,or upon his removal or resignation,shalldeliver
to his successor, or to such person as the council may
direct,allthe public money ,books,property,and effects
belonging to the city of Paducah .
§ 170. The council may , in their discretion, elect as
treasurerand custodianof the funds of the cityany in-
corporated bank under the laws of thisCommonwealth ,
or under the laws of Congress,having itsprincipalbank
and doing business in the city of Paducah ; and it shall
be lawful for such bank to become and be the treasurer
and custodian of the money of the city of Paducah ,
and for such incorporatedbank , by its presidentand
directors, to accept election from the council of Padu-
cah , and to execute , by the president and directors of
such bank , a good and sufficientbond and surety, to
be approved by the council, for the faithful perform .
ance and discharge of the duties of treasurer or custo-
dian of the money of the city; and the same liability

that would against a natural person who might be elected
treasurer and execute covenant ; and the same remedies
are hereby given against such bank corporationfor a
breach of its covenant as are given in this charter or
exist by law against natural persons in like cases fora
breach of such covenant . The council may fixthe com-
pensation to be allowed such bank , not exceeding one
half of one per cent. upon the whole amount received
and disbursedby the bank . In case no bank acceptthe

ed , the council shall fix his compensation for his services
at not exceeding one per cent .on the amount of money
that may be received and disbursed by him as treasurer.
Clerk of the Council .
§ 171. At the firstregular meeting in June the council
shall elect a clerk,who shall be styledthe Clerk of the
Council of Paducah , and who shall hold his office for
two years ,and until his successor shall be duly elected
and qualified. No person shall be eligibleto the office
of clerk of the council unless, at the time of his ap-
pointment ,he be a male citizen over the age of twen-
ty-four years,and a bona fideresidentof the cityfor at
leastone year next precedinghisappointment.
§ 172. The clerk of the council shall keep a fair and
correctrecord,in such books as may be designatedby the
council,of alltheproceedings,ordinances,and resolutions
of the council; keep and preservein hisofficethe seal of
thecity,recordsand papersofthecitynot properlybelong-
ing to another office; to prepare papers and copies from
his office,and records thereof forpublication; to make out
a poll-book for each ward in the citybefore the day of
each election,and deliverthe same to the proper officers
of the election. He shallprepareand make ready forthe
signature of the mayor all orders on the treasury, and
countersignthe same ; make out alllicenses,allcopiesof
the recordsand papers in or appertainingto his office,
upon the requestof the persons entitledto the same ;

and to perform allotherdutiesrequiredof him by order
or ordinance of the council and this charter .
§ 173. Upon applicationof any person to the clerkof
the council,he may obtain from the clerk a copy of any
recordor paper in hisoffice,paying to the said clerkhis
fee therefor, which copy attest" by the clerk shall be
evidence in all courts of this Commonwealth .

§174 . The clerk shall be entitledto charge , and shall
receiveforsuch copies,fourcentsforeverytwenty words
in such copy , and fifteencents for each attestationthere-
to; and forthe collectionof which he ishereby given the

175. The council shall fix by ordinance the amount
of the clerk'sfees for issuing licenses, and for other ser-
vices not enumerated in the lastsections,and the council
shall also,prior to the electionof every clerk of the coun-
cil,fixhissalary,to be paid monthly out of the treasury,
at a sum not less than five hundred dollars nor more
than two thousand dollars .
§ 176. The clerk of council shall have power to ad-
minister oaths or affirmationsin such cases as justicesof
the peace are now allowed by law ,and to make certifi-
cates thereof,under seal of the city,when necessaryor
required; and shallhave the power to appointa deputy
clerk at his own cost, and to require bond for the faithful
discharge of the acts of such deputy .
CityEngineer.
§ 177. The council, at theirfirstregular meeting after
a generalelectionof councilmen,shallelecta cityengi

neer, who shallhold his officefor one year , and untilhis
successor is elected and qualified, whose duties, and the

sation shall be fixed by the council, at not exceeding
one thousand fivehundred dollarsper annum , payable
monthly out of the treasury,and the fees of the office.
§ 178. No person otherwise eligibleto an officeunder
thischarter shallbe ineligibleto any officeby reason of
his having filledan officefor one or any number of times
next preceding his electionor appointment . All officers
under this charter are declared incompatible : Provided,
That one person may be weigher and also measurer of
wood and other articles,except tobacco .
Officersof Elections.
§ 179. The council shall,in the month of January each
year , appoint suitablepersons as officersof the election
in each ward - two persons as judges , one person as
marshal , and one person as clerk- and who shall hold
theirofficesfor one year ; and ,if practicable, the officers
for each voting place shallbe selectedin equal numbers
from the two politicalparties. No person shallbe ap-
pointed an officerof any election who shall not be a
qualifiedvoter in the ward for which he is appointed .
Officers of elections shall perform similar services, and
be entitledto like pay , as officersof State elections, and
be governed in all matters not inconsistent with this
charter, and liable to the penalties imposed by the laws
of this Commonwealth .
Elections .
§ 180. All electionsunder this charter by the qualified
votersof the cityshallbe vivavoce: Provided, Dumb per-
sons entitledto suffragemay vote by ballot.
§ 181. All electionsby the qualifiedvotersof the city
shall be held between the hours of six o'clock in the
morning and seven o'clock in the evening .
§ 182. Every male citizen of the United States of the
age of twenty-one years,who has residedin the State

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1135
two years,or in the county of McCracken one year next
preceding the election,shall be a voter; but such voter
shallhave been,forsixtydays next preceding the elec-
tion , a resident of the ward in which he offers to vote ,
and shall vote in said ward and not elsewhere .
§183 . Any laws in force in this Commonwealth pun-
ishing illegalvoting at the State election shall apply to
illegalvoting in the cityof Paducah for any cityofficers,
subject, however , to all the provisions of this charter
touching the qualifications of voters .
§ 184. All vacancies in officeselectedby the people
shallbe filledby the council untilthe next March or Au-
gust election (whichever shall transpire first) held for
election of any officersoccurring thereat,at which elec-
tion in March or August the remainder of such vacancy
shallbe filledby the qualifiedvoters of the city or ward
in which such vacancy shallhave occurred, except when
a vacancy shalloccurin the trusteesof the publicschools
of said city,that said vacancy shallbe filledby the trus-
tees of the public schools untilthe next regular election.
§ 185. It any vacancy shall occur in any officewhich
the council have the power of election or appointment
for the balance of the term , then it shall be the duty of
the council to do so at the next regular meeting after such
vacancy shallhave been declared .
§186 . When ,in electionsby the qualifiedvoters of the
cityor of any ward , two candidates are equal and high-
est in votes for the same office, the election shall be
determined between the two by lot,as the council may
prescribe by ordinance .
§ 187. The clerk shall, immediately after the clection,
certifyto the Secretary of State the name of the person
elected forjudge of the Paducah citycourt,and the Gov-
ernor of the Commonwealth shall issue a commission to
said officer .
§ 188.The poll-books of allelectionsin the cityshall
be returned by the several marshals of the electionsto
theclerkof the councilon the next day afterevery elec-
tion, who shallkeep them on fileas part of the records of
his office .

§ 189.The mayor , assistantchiefpolice, and clerk of
the council ,shall constitute a board for the examination
of the poll-books of allelectionsby the people. Any
two of them may constitute a board ; but if either of
them is a candidate ,he shall have no voice in the decis-
ion of his own case. If,from any cause ,two of the afore-
named persons cannot,in whole or in part,act in com-
paring the polls,theirplacesare to be filledand supplied
by the judge of the citycourt and the cityattorney.

board shall meet in the council chamber , between the
hours of ten and twelve in the morning , and compare
the polls,and ascertainthe correctnessof the summing
up ofthe votes,and issueduplicatecertificates,over their
signatures,to those who have received the highest num-
ber of votes for each officevoted for at said election, one
copy of saidcertificateto be retainedin the officeof the
clerk of the council,and one copy to each of the persons
elected .
§ 191. The certificateof electionof a cityofficershall
be in substance as follows : " City of Paducah , sct.: We ,
and , duly authorizedto compare the poll-
books for the cityof Paducah ,do certify, that at an elec-
tion held in said city on the day of
was dulyelectedto fillthe officeof - . " The certifi-
cate of electionof the councilmen or trusteesof public
schools shall be altered to show that an election was held
in a named ward .
§ 192.The oath of officemay be administeredby the
judge of the citycourt,the mayor or clerkof the coun-
cil, or any justiceof the peace of McCracken county,
and a certificatethereof shallbe filedamong the archives
of the city.

use in said cityfor wharves , streets, alleys, sidewalks ,
parks,publicgrounds,and forany otherpublicpurpose,
the said council may so declare,by ordinance or resolu-

with the owner of any such property, ifa residentof

rial, be a non -resident of said city, an infant, a feme

shall confirm the report of said commissioners, which
shall vest in said citythe titleto the ground or material
