material valued ,and the titleto the same ,and the inter-
est and duration of interestvalued by said commission-
ersor assessedby the jury; and itshallnot be necessary,
afterthe report of the commissioners upon the land or
material as hereinbefore provided for,in order to the use

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1139
and occupation of the same ,to await the issue of the
proceedings,in case exceptions are filedto the report;
and the report of the commissioners , afterthe payment
or tender to the owner ,or deposit in bank ,of the amount
assessed, shallbe a bar to allactions fortaking and using
such property .
§ 194. All costs,contracts, and proceedings of the coun-
cilof the cityof Paducah ,from the eleventh day of Feb-
ruary,one thousand eighthundred and seventy-one, up
to February fifth, one thousand eight hundred and seven-
ty-three,be ,and the same are hereby ,legalizedand made
valid .
§ 195. In any case of litigationnow pending , or that
may hereafter be instituted, wherein the records, or a
certifiedcopy of any proceedings of the council, which
proceedingwas had priorto the eleventhday of Febru-
ary,one thousand eighthundred and seventy-three,may
lawfully be introduced as evidence ,itshall be lawful for
any party interestedin such suit or litigation, either as
plaintiffor defendant, to introduce,eitherorallyor by
deposition, the mayor , clerk of the council, or any one or
more of the councilmen who were in office at the time
of the making up of the record so offered as evidence ,
to explain and testifyas to the realaction of saidcoun-
cil touching the matters in litigation, and to show that
the charter has in fact been complied with, whether the
records thereof show the fact or not : it being the intent
of thisact that the records of said cityin regard to public
improvements within the citylimitsshallnot be conclu-
siveproof of the actionof said council,but shallbe sub-
ject to contradictionor modification,just as any other
evidence by the mayor ,clerk,or councilmen .
§196 . All actionsagainstthe cityof Paducah ,or any
officerthereof,to recover taxes or assessments which have
been or may be illegallyassessedor collected,shallbe
prosecuted within one year after the cause of action
arose ,and not afterwards .

1140 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Public Schools .
§ 197. The public schools of the cityshallbe managed
and controlled by two trustees elected from each ward ,
styled " trusteesof the public schools," who shallpossess
the same qualificationsas to age , sex , residence, and
property as councilmen ; and one trustee shallbe elected
from each ward at the general electionin March in each
year ,whose term of officeshall be two years,and until
theirsuccessors are elected and qualified.
1. The trusteesof the public schools shallhave power
to elector appoint such officersas may be necessary for
their own government , and to require covenant , with
surety,from any or allofficersforthe faithfuldischarge
of their duties; to make by -laws , not in conflictwith
this charter, for carrying out the duties of their office,
and for the government of itsown officers,schools,teach-
ers,pupils,and employes;to determinetheirown rulesof
proceeding ,and to appoint superintendents,teachers,and
other officersand employes , and regulate and fix their
terms, duties, and compensation, and remove them at
pleasure .
2. The trusteesshall, before entering upon the duties
of theiroffice, make oath or affirmationas prescribed in
this charter,and shall meet at least once a month . A
majority of all the trustees elected shall be a quorum
for the transaction of business; and they shall keep a
correct record of all their proceedings, open to inspec-
tion of the citizens .
3. The councilof the cityof Paducah shallhave power
to levy and collecta tax in each year upon allthe taxa-
ble property of the city,for the support and maintenance
of the public schools, not to exceed twenty -five cents on
each one hundred dollars' worth of taxable property ,and
a head or poll-tax not to exceed one dollar; and the col-
lectorof taxes shall,without unnecessarydelay,pay to
the treasurer of the public schools the amount of taxes
collectedby him under the levy for school purposes,
and take his receipt therefor, which shall be a sufficient

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1141
voucher in any settlement of the collectorwith the coun-
cilor trusteesof the public schools.
4. The trustees shall have power to receive from the
school commissioners of McCracken county , or from any
other source,any and allsums of money provided by law
for school purposes .
5. The trusteesof the public schools shallprovide and
maintain ,out of the funds levied or otherwise provided
for the purpose , suitable buildings, teachers, and other
employes , sufficientfor the education of all children of
the city between six and twenty years of age ,and shall
provide separate buildings and schools for education of
white and black pupils.
6. The trustees of the public schools shallbe trustees
of the Male University and the Female Seminary of the
city.
7. The trusteesof the public schools shallmake annual
reports to the council,showing all items of income and
expenditure , and the condition and attendance of the
schools,classifyingthe same so as to make itconvenient
for reference,which report shallbe published.
Miscellaneous .
§ 198. Each officer of the city elected at a general
election by the people , or elected by the council (except
councilmen and trusteesof the publicschools),shallex-
ecute a covenant to the city of Paducah , with three or
more good and sufficientsureties,to be approved by the
council,conditioned that he will well,truly,and faithfully
execute and discharge all the duties of his officeaccord-
ing to law ; that he will faithfullyand promptly make
and file all settlements,and account for and pay over,
according to law and the orders and ordinances of the
council ,all money and moneys which shall come to his
hands as such officer; and that he will turn over to his
successor,or to such person as may be designatedby the
council, allbooks, papers, and propertyof any descrip-
tion, belonging to his office, or the city of Paducah ,or
shall have come to his hands by virtue of his office.

1142 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Upon the failureof any officerto execute such covenant
for thirtydays ,his officeshallbe declared vacant .
§ 199. The city of Paducah shall have a lien upon
all property, real and personal, subject to execution,
owned or belonging to the treasurer,collectorof taxes,
or assistantchief of police respectively, at the date of
the execution of their respective covenants , or which
may thereafterbe acquiredby him,which lienshallnot
be discharged until such officershall have obtained
quietus for any public dues for which he may be bound .
No covenant of any officershallbe satisfiedor void upon
the first,or any other recovery,untilthe city of Paducah ,
and every person aggrieved by the acts or omissionsof
the officer,shallhave been recompensed .
a
§ 200. The councilmay ,at any time,requireadditional
suretiesupon the covenant of any officer,or may require
an officerto execute a new covenant ; and upon failure
of such officerto furnish additional sureties,or execute
a new covenant when required to do so, and within the
time prescribedby the council,hisofficeshallbe declared
vacant .
§201 . Each officerof the city,before entering on the
discharge of the duties of his office, shalltake and sub-
scribe the following oath or affirmation , to-wit : " I do
solemnlyswear (or affirm, as the case may be)that I will
support the Constitutionof the United States and the
Constitutionof the State,and be faithfuland trueto the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , so long as I continue a
citizenthereof; and that I will faithfullyexecute ,to the
best of my ability, the officeof of the city of
Paducah ,accordingto law ; and I do solemnly swear (or
affirm) that, since the adoption of the present Consti-
tution,I,being a citizen of this State,have not fought a
duel with deadly weapons withinthe State,nor out of it,
with a citizen of this State; nor have I sent or accepted
a challengeto fighta duel with deadly weapons with a
citizen of this State ; nor have I acted as second in car-
rying a challenge,or aided or assistedany person thus
offending: so help me God ;" which oath or affirmation
shall be filedwith the clerk of the council.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1148
| §202 . All ordinances or parts of ordinances of the
cityof Paducah now in force, not in conflictwith this
charter, shall continue in force until repealed by the
council; and alllaws vestingrightsin,or imposing du-
tiesupon ,the cityof Paducah ,not herein supersededor
repealed,shallremain in forceuntilrepealedby the Gen-
-eralAssembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
§203 . The recognized records of the trustees of the
town of Paducah , and of the city of Paducah , and all
bonds and contracts executed to the said town or city,
shallremain in fullforce; and remaining in the proper
offices of said city, are hereby declared to be public
records; and copies thereof, certifiedby the proper offi-
cer or keeper thereof,shallbe evidence in all courts to
the same extentthat the originalwould be on due proof.
§ 204. If, from any cause ,the council shallfailto ap-
point or electany officerat the time fixedhereinforthe
appointment or election,it shallbe lawful for the coun-
cil to make such appointment or election at any time
thereafter in all cases where the council has the power
of appointment or election.
§ 205. This act shall be designated and cited as the
general charter of Paducah .
§ 206. The officersof the city of Paducah now in office
shall hold and discharge the duties thereof untilthe elec-
tion and qualificationof their successors as provided
herein, and the firstelection for the following officers
shallbe: formayor ,firstMonday in March ,one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-five; one councilman and one
trusteeof public schools, firstMonday in March , one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-five; for judge of
Paducah citycourt, firstMonday in August, one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-six; for cityattorney, first
Monday in August, one thousand eight hundred and
eighty-six; for clerk of the council and treasurer, first
Monday in June,one thousand eighthundred and eighty-
five .
§ 207. All laws or parts of laws in conflictwith this
act are hereby repealed.

1144 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 208. This act shallgo into effectand be in fullforce
from and afterthe date of itspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884..
CHAPTER 1394 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Fidelity Trust Company , of Lexington .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That M. C. Johnson,R. A. Thornton,Thos . Mitch-
ell,R. S. Bullock ,W. D. Nicholas ,S. Bassett,E. D. Sayre ,
J.W. Appleton, R. T. Anderson, and allother persons
who shallhereafterbecome stockholders in the company
hereby incorporated, shallbe a body-corporate, by the
name of the FidelityTrust Company ,of Lexington,and
by that name have perpetual succession; and may con-
tractand be contracted with, sue and be sued , and have
allthe powers hereinaftergiven ; and may have and use
a common seal, and alterthe same at pleasure.
§2 . The capital stock of said corporation shallconsist
of twelve hundred and fiftyshares of the value of one
hundred dollarseach,with the privilegeto increasethe
same by a vote of the stockholders(those holding two-
thirds of all the capital stock assenting thereto),at an
annual or specialmeeting ,to any sum not exceeding one
milliondollars; and the corporators,or the majority of
them , named in the first section of this act, shall have
power to open books forsubscriptionsat such times and
places as they may deem expedient ; and when not less
than one thousand shares have been subscribed , and ten
per centum thereon shallhave been paid in (activebusi-
ness shall not be commenced , however , until at least one
hundred thousand dollars shall have been paid on the
capitalstock),the shareholdersmay electa board of not
less than nine directors, who may at any time be in-
creased,by a vote of the stockholders owning two -thirds-
in value of all the stock,to any number not exceeding
fifteen; the directors to serve until the next annual elec-
tion, or until their successors shall be duly elected and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1145
qualified; and the directorsso electedof saidcompany ,
when it shall have been organized , may , and they are
hereby authorized and empowered ,to have and to exer-
cise,in the name and in behalf of the company ,allthe
rights and privileges which are hereby given , subject
only to such liabilitiesas other share-holders are subject
to,which liabilitiesare more than for the payment to
the company of the sums due or to become due on the
sharesheldby them; and shouldthe capitalstockat any
time be increased , the stockholders at the time of such
increase shallbe entitled to a pro rata share of a such
increaseupon the payment of the price fixedby the
board of directors .
§3 . The principal officeof the said company shallbe
in the city of Lexington . All the directors of said com-
pany shall be citizens of the State of Kentucky , and
residetherein, and shall keep a record of theirproceed-
ings .
§4 . The board of directorsshall divide itself, by lot,
into three classes as nearly equal as may be . The term
of officeof the firstclass shallexpire at the end of one
year ,that of the second class at the end of two years ,
and that of the thirdclass at the end of three years ; and
at the expiration of the firstyear , and annually thereaf-
ter, there shall be chosen a number of directors equal to
the number of directors in the class whose term will then
expire,who shallhold theirofficefor three years, or until
their successors are elected and qualified. Vacancies
occurring in the intervalsof electionsshall be filledby
the board .
§5 . The directors shall elect from their number , at the
firstmeeting of the board aftertheir election, and annu-
ally thereafter, a presidentand a vice president, who
shallhold theirofficefor the term of one year , and until
their successors are elected and qualified, and may ap-
point a secretary and treasurer, and such other officers,.
clerks,agents,and employes as the businessof the com-
pany may require. All elections for directors shall be
by ballot, and every stockholder shallbe entitledto one

1146 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vote for each share of stock held by him ; but no person
shallbe eligibleas directorwho is not a stockholder to
the amount of twenty shares. At any meeting a quo-
rum of the stockholders shall consist of stockholders
owning at leastone thirdin value of the capitalstock.
§6 . All lands purchased by thiscorporationin satis-
faction of debt shallbe disposed of by said corporation
within five years from the date of purchase ; and if not
disposed of within that time,must be exposed to public
sale at the end of five years from the date of said pur-
chase .
§ 7. The board of directorsshallmake allby-laws
necessary forconducting the business of the corporation.
The directors shall have power to require payment of
the amount remaining unpaid on the stock of the cor-
poration, at such times and in such proportions as they
shallthink proper: the said assessmentto be made as
the by -laws of said corporationshalldirect. The cor-
poration shallhave a lien on the stockfor any amount
that may be due thereon.
§ 8. The capitalstock to the extent of sixty-two thou
sand fivehundred dollars,and in the event of an increase
over one hundred and twenty -fivethousand dollars,then
one halfof the capitalstockpaid in, shallbe investedin
such securities, at such ratesof interestas they prom-
ise to pay , as are authorized by law for trustees, and
from time to time shall be reinvested in the same ,and
shallbe kept separate and apart from the other invest-
ments ,and designated on the books of the company as
" investments of trustcapital;" and it shallbe the duty
of the proper officersof the company , annually, on or
before the fifthday of January,or oftenerifrequiredby
the court, to filein the Fayette circuitcourt a detailed
statement ,under oath or affirmation, of said investments ;
and said trustcapitalshallbe speciallyand primarily
liable for the obligations of said company as executor ,
administrator,guardian , receiver, assignee,or trustee in
any othercapacity,and shallnot be liablefor any other
-debt or obligation of the company untilallitsliabilities

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1147
in regard to its trusts as aforesaid shall have been first
fully met and discharged . The residue of the capital
and othermoney in hands ofthe company may be loaned
or invested in such manner as may be determined by the
by -laws of the corporation,except itshallnot be invest-
ed in real estate; but nothing herein shallbe construed
to permit said company to discount paper ,or to engage
in the business of banking .
§ 9. The capitalof said company shallbe taken and
consideredas the only securityrequiredby law for the
faithfulperformance of itsduties,and other securityshall
not be requiredupon itsappointment toany ofthe offices
or duties mentioned herein,except when required by the
courts or by parties in interest; but any court having
jurisdictionto make or approve such appointments shall
have power ,before making or approving the same , and
from time to time afterwards ,to examine the officers of
the company ,under oath or affirmation,as to the security
aforesaid, and appoint a suitable person to investigate
the affairsand management of said company ,who shall
report to such court the manner in which its investments
are made , and the security offered to those by or for
whom its engagements are held, the expense of such
investigationto be defrayed by the company .
§ 10. The said corporation may be appointed ,and may
act as guardian of infants, as executors or administrator,
or committee of lunatics,as receiver, assignee, or other
trustee,whether appointed by deed,by lastwilland tes-
tament ,by any court within this Commonwealth , or in
any other manner not inconsistent with law . It may
receiveand hold in trustestate,realand personal,includ-
ing the notes,bonds ,obligations, and accounts of estates
and individuals,of companies and corporations,private or
public; and may ,in itsfiduciarycapacity,purchase,col-
lect,adjust,and settle,selland dispose of the same , in
thisState or elsewhere . Itmay act as agent or attorney
in the leasing, conveying,and managing real and per-
sonal estate, receiving and collecting rents and other
moneys ,in the issuingand countersigningcertificatesof

1148 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
stock, bonds, or other obligationsof any corporation,
association, or municipality, State or public authority,
and may receive and manage any sinking fund therefor;
and may accept and execute all such trusts of every
description,not inconsistentwith the laws of this State,
as may be committed to it by any person or persons , or
by any corporation,or by any court of record of this or
any other State. It shall have a trust department ,and
all itsbusiness of a fiduciarycharacter shall be kept in
a separate and specialset of books .
§ 11. In the exercise by said company of the powers
herein authorized as guardian , executor, administrator,
committee of lunatics, or of any officeor duty imposed
by the court,said company shallbe subjectto the same
duties and responsibilities,shall have the same powers ,
and shallreceive the same compensation ,as may be fixed
by law with relationto individualsholding similar offices-
or trusts,except as herein otherwisespeciallyprovided.
The exercise of the other powers , and the performance
of other duties by said company ,may be ,as to compen-
sation and otherwise,matters of contract with the parties
interested .
§ 12. Said corporationmay purchase or erect,at the
optionof the stockholdersholdinga majorityin value of
the stock,a building containing a commodious and strong
safetyvault,containing a number of small compartments
or boxes designed and adapted forrenting to persons who
desireto have a place of safe deposit for valuables; and
said corporationis hereby empowered to rent out the
boxes in such safetyvault to such persons, and for such
sums of money ,and forsuch time,as may be agreed be-
tween such corporation and such persons as may jointly
or severallyrent one or more of said boxes or compart-
ments .
§ 13. Said corporationis hereby empowered to receive
for safe-keeping any valuables that may be intrustedto it,
receiptingforthe same by any of itsofficersdesignated
by itsby -laws so to do,and may guarantee such special
deposits .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1149
§14 . Said corporationmay ,untilit secures a safety
vault for the valuables intrusted to it, rent or hire boxes
in the safety vault of any other company or corporation,
or rent a room or rooms for its use until it shall erect or
purchase a building.
§ 15. Said corporationmay declare and pay semi-an-
nually dividends from the net earnings of the company ;
but before declaring said dividends , it shall reserve from
said net earnings not less than ten per cent.,and shall
continue so to do until the amount thus reserved equals
twenty per cent. of the paid -up capital stock.
§ 16. This act shalltake effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 12, 1884.
CHAPTER 1395 .
AN ACT to establish a road district in Jefferson county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That that part of Jeffersoncounty lying north and
east of the Preston streetroad ,excluding the cityof Lou-
isville,is hereby constituted" The Eastern Road District,
of Jeffersoncounty ."
§ 2. L. L. Dorsey ,Hamilton Ormsby ,H. M. Bullitt,Jno .
L. Herr ,S. O. Wetherbee ,T. J. Murphy ,Ike Williams ,W.
W. Young ,D. S. Taylor , A. B. Duerson , William Barae-
man ,Nat . Ragland , S. Beard ,Thomas Bridwell,Richard
Waters ,F. S. Dravo , Richard S. Veech ,Jack Gallagher ,
James Trigg , Edward Monohan ,J. Decker ,John C. Sher-
ley, S. L. Gaar , Lewis Shallcross, W. H. Frederick, E.
R. Simcoe , Thomas Thixton , Edward Briscoe, and E. J.
Hikes ,are hereby constituted commissioners of said dis-
trict .
§3 The powers given to said commissioners by thisact
may be exercised by a majority of them .
§ 4. They shallmeet at the court-room of the county
court of said county , and select from their number , or
other tax-payers residing in said district,three persons ,

1150 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to be known as " The Trustees of the Eastern Road Dis-
trict,of Jeffersoncounty ."
§5 . The clerk of said court must be present at said
meeting ,and must record the proceedings and the names
of the commissioners present,and of the trusteeselected;
and he shall be allowed two dollarsfor each meeting of
the commissioners, and for recording proceedings and
other servicesrequired by thisact; he shallhave the fees
now allowed by law for similar services.
§6 . Upon the written application of said commission-
ers,itshallbe the duty of the judge of said court to cause
said application to be recorded,and to directthe votes of
the qualifiedvoters residing in said districtto be taken
at the next ensuing August election upon the question :
"Are you for or against taxing thisdistrictfor road pur-
poses ?" Provided, First,that said application shallnot be
for more than sixty-five thousand dollars; and , secondly ,
that at least twenty days' notice of the election to be
held under thisact,and of the sum named in said ap-
plication,shallbe publishedby saidcommissionersin two
daily newspapers in the city of Louisville; said election
shall be held and the votes shall be returned and counted
by the same officers,and in the same manner ,as elections
for State officers .
§7 . Ifthe majority of the votes cast by qualifiedvoters
be in favor of said tax, said county court shall issue
bonds for the sum named in said application, and deliver
them to the trustees hereinafter mentioned ; said bonds
shallbe payable twenty years afterdate to the bearer,
with coupons attachedpayable to the bearer,forsemi-
annual installments of interest at the rate of five per
cent, per annum ; shall be for one hundred or fivehun-
dred dollars each , and shallbe the same as if issued by
the county , except that each shall state on its face the
districtfor which it is issued; and the persons residing
and property situatedin said districtshallbe solelyliable
therefor. Said bonds shallbe payable afterfiveyears
from theirdate,at the option of the board of the sinking
fund hereinafter mentioned .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1151
§8. Said courtshall,when countytaxesareimposed,
impose taxes upon the realpropertyin saiddistrict,and
upon the personalpropertyof thoseresidingtherein,suf-
ficientto pay saidcoupons as they mature,and to redeem
saidbonds at or beforematurity,not,however ,to exceed
fifteen cents on one hundred dollars; and said taxes shall
operate as a lien,and be collectableand collectedin the
same manner ,and by the same officers, and at the same
time ,as State taxes; and the said court,before imposing
said taxes, shall require the sheriffor other collecting
officerto give bond ,with good sureties, payable to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , for the faithfuldischarge
of his duties under this act. Said collectingofficershall
receive such compensation as may be allowed by the
county court,not exceeding three per cent.on the amount
collected; and he shall account to, and settlewith ,the
board of the sinking fund hereinafter mentioned within
the same time as is required to State taxes,and for any
delinquencyhe shallbe punishableto the same extentas
a collector of State taxes .
§ 9. Said commissioners shallappoint three resident
tax-payers of saiddistrict,who shallconstitutethe board
of the sinking fund of said district,and who shall hold
office at the pleasure of said commissioners , and who
shallappoint one of theirnumber treasurer. He shall
execute bond to the Commonwealth , with suretiesto be
approved by saidcommissioners,forthe faithfulperform-
ance of his duties; and the said court may ,at any time ,
on theirmotion , require him to give additional sureties.
He shallreceive such allowance as may be made by said
court, not exceeding two per cent. on money received
and paid out by him .
§ 10. Ifthe tax be sustainedby the voters,the commis-
sioners aforesaid shall meet at the aforesaid court -room ,
between ten o'clock in the forenoon and twelve o'clock
noon ,on the twentieth day aftersaid election, and elect
three persons residing in said districtas trusteesthereof,
who shallbe known as " The Trustees of the Eastern Road
Districtof Jeffersoncounty," and shallhave thepowers

1152 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
usually belonging to private corporations. They shall
hold officefor three years ,and untiltheirsuccessors are
elected,which shallbe done by said commissioners ,at the
room aforesaid, on the firstMonday of September next
ensuing said three years; but if a vacancy should occur
within three years,itshallbe filledby the other trustees.
Said trusteesshall elect from their number a treasurer,
who shall give bond , with sureties to be approved by
said commissioners , for the faithfulperformance of his
duties; and he shall receive such allowance as may be
made by said court,not exceeding two per cent.on money
passing through his hands .
§ 11. Said bonds ,in order to be valid,must be counter-
signed by the trusteesacross theirface; and said trustees
may sellas many of them , at not lessthan par , with the
interestaccrued , as may be necessary in order to carry
out the provisions of this act. The funds realizedfrom
the sale of said bonds shallbe held by the treasurer of
said trustees,who shallhold the same subject at alltimes
to the joint order of the trustees. Such joint order shall
be a voucher to the said treasurerfor all money by him
paid out.
§ 12. The said trustees shallhave the power , in their
discretion, to subscribe,in behalf of said district,to the
stock of any company or corporationan amount not
exceeding the sum of one thousand dollarsper mile to
any public road lyingwholly in said district,to be con-
structed of macadamized or gravel in accordance with
the specificationsand requirements setforthin this act :
Provided , however , That said road so subscribed to shall
have a width of not less than ten feet and a depth of
not less than fifteen inches of metal , and shall not be
less than one mile in length : And provided further, That
said subscriptionshallnot be due and payable untileach
mile or fractionover a mile has been fullycompleted.
Specificationsof the location, name , constructors, and
length of said road so subscribed to shallbe reported by
said trustees to the county court, and recorded by the
clerk of said court in a book by him kept for that pur

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1153
pose . Said trustees shall each receive for their services
the sum of fifty($50) dollarsper year, and in addition
thereto, the sum of two ($2) dollarsfor each day that
they are actuallyengaged in inspectingthe construction
of said road priorto paying the subscriptionby them
made thereto : Provided, however, That no trustee shall
be paid for more than twenty-five(25) days' servicesfor
inspecting said roads in any one year.
§ 13. It shallnot be lawful for the county court of Jef-
ferson county to levy any tax,nor shallit be lawful to
enforce the collectionof any existing tax on the prop-
erty(realor personal) situatedwithin the said eastern
road districtof Jeffersoncounty , for the purpose of con-
structing or improving any roads not within said district
while this act is in force .
§14 . Should the question of the right or authority of
the county court to subscribe the sum of money , and for
the purpose named herein,failto be carriedby a major-
ity of the legal voters in said districtvoting on the same
at the next ensuing August election,the question may
be re-submitted to the legal voters of said districtat any
and all of the regular August elections occurring within
two (2) years from the passage of thisact.
§ 15. The clerk of the Jeffersoncounty court shallkeep
a record of the bonds issued and deliveredby the county
court to the trusteesas provided herein; and said record
shall contain an accurate description of said bonds ,in-
cluding the date of issue,the date of maturity,and the
numbers and amounts of said bonds . The said trustees
shall give theirwritten receipt for the bonds so issued
and delivered to them by the county court; and said
receiptshallbe filedin the officeof the clerk of said
court,and recorded therein,and be by him kept in said
office .
§ 16.And it shallbe the furtherduty of said trustees
to make a semi-annual report,under oath,to the county
-court,on the firstMonday of October and April in each
year,of allbonds sold by them ,and the sum of money
realizedfrom said sales,and allamounts paid out by
LOC . L., VOL . II- 73

1154 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
them ,and to whom paid; and they shallreport the bal--
ance in money and bonds in theirhands at the date of
said report; and the clerkof said courtshallrecord the
same in a book kept by him forthatpurpose; and itshall
be the duty of the treasurersnamed herein to make a
semi -annual report to the county court of all money
received by them , and allamounts by them paid out,and
to whom paid,and for what purpose,and whether paid
out in meeting the intereston said bonds or in retiring
the principalbonds. Said reportsshallbe made at the
same time that the trustees named herein shall make
theirreport,and the clerk shall record said reports in the
same book that the trustees ' books are recorded .
§17 . That when the board of the sinking fund shall
take up and retireany of said bonds ,they shall cancel
and destroy the same in the presence of the judge of the
county court, and filea report, in writing,accompanied
with the affidavitsof themselves and the said judge ,
setting forth the numbers , dates, and maturities of the
same ; and the clerk of said court shall record the same
in his office in the book mentioned in section sixteen of
this act .
§18 . Elections of members of the board of sinking
fund or of trustees(whether to fillvacancies or other-
wise ) shall be certifiedto the county court,and recorded
by the clerk thereof in the book aforesaid; and bonds of
treasurersof the board of sinking fund and of the trus-
tees shallbe deposited in said court, and recorded in said
book .
§ 19. Said board of the sinkingfund may purchase,as
investments ,bonds of the United States,or of any State
therein, or of the city of Louisville, or firstmortgage
bonds of any railroador railwaycompany charteredby
the Legislatureof Kentucky , and now in operation.
Bonds so purchased shallbe depositedin the Fidelity
Trust and Safety Vault Company ,in Louisville,at the
cost of the district.
§20. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1155
CHAPTER 1396 .
AN ACT to amend , revise,and reduce into one the severalacts in regard
to the town of Taylorsville.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. The boundary of the town of Taylorsville,in Spen-
cer county ,ishereby declared to be as follows: Beginning
at the southeast corner of the cemetery lot on Salt river;
and thence with the easternboundary of same ,in a straight
line,to Brashear's creek ; and thence with said creek to
Salt river; and thence up the river to the beginning , on
the low -water mark line. The said plat to embrace allthe
streetsand alleys, and all lotswithin the said boundary ,
whether includedin any previous survey or platof same
or not . The inhabitants of said boundary are hereby de-
clared to be a body -politicand corporate, with perpetual
succession; with power to sue and be sued,contractand
be contracted with ,plead and be impleaded ,defend and
be defended,by the name of the town of Taylorsville;
and to have a corporate seal.
§ 2. It shall be the duty of the trustees of said town ,
in officewhen thisact shalltake effect,to cause a plat of
said town to be prepared ,showing the various divisions
of said town into lots,streets,and alleys. The names of
the streets and alleys,and the numbers of the lots, as
heretoforeestablished,shall,as faras practicable,be pre-
served; and such land as may be included in said bound-
ary,not heretoforeembraced in the corporate limitsof
said town , shall be designated upon said plat by the
names of the owners . The trusteesshallcause said plat
to be recorded in the county clerk'sofficein Spencer
county , and a certifiedcopy thereof, under the hand of
the county clerk, shall be admitted in evidence in all
cases .
§3 . The municipal government of said town shall
consist of five trustees, one of whom shall be selected
by the board to preside over their meetings ,and shall
be styledchairman of the trusteesof Taylorsville.
§4 . Said trustees and their successors in office shall be
a body -politicand corporate,under the name and style

1156 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ofthetrusteesofTaylorsville;and by thatname theymay ,
forand on behalf of said town ,contract and be contract-
ed with ,sue and be sued ,plead and be impleaded ,defend
and be defended ; and in all courts and places may do all
things which a body -corporate,with perpetual succession,
may lawfully and rightfullydo within the limits of the
powers here granted .
§5 . The other officersof said town shallbe a police
judge , marshal , attorney, clerk, treasurer, assessor, and
collector, and such other officersas the trustees may ,
from time to time ,directand appoint .
1
Elections .
§6 . An election shall be held in said town on the first
Saturday in June in each year , at such place as the trus-
tees may appoint , of which ten days ' previous public
notice shallbe given by written or printed notices,posted
in at leastfive public places in said town by the town
clerk .
§7 . At the annual electionthere shallbe electedby
the qualifiedvotersof said town fivetrustees,a police
judge , a marshal , and an attorney forthe town ; and the
person receiving the highest number of votes for any of
said offices shall be declared elected .
§ 8. The officerselectedby the people under the pro-
visions of this act shall hold their respective officesfor
one year ,and untiltheirsuccessors are elected and qual-
ified. All other officers shall hold their offices at the
pleasure of the trustees.
§ 9. No person shall be eligibleto any officein said
town who is not a citizenthereof, and a qualifiedvoter
under the laws of the State; and no one shallbe eligible
to the officeof policejudge ,trustee,marshal ,or attorney,
who has not,at the time of his election, resided in said
town at leastsixmonths precedingsuch election.
§ 10.Every person electedor appointedto officeunder
the provisionsof thisact,beforeenteringupon the duties
thereof, shalltake and subscribe,before some authorized
officer,the oath of officeprescribedby the Constitution

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1157
of thisCommonwealth ,and filethe same ,duly certified
by the officerbefore whom itwas taken , with the clerk
of the town .
§ 11. Whenever a vacancy shall happen in any office
filledby election,it shallbe filledby appointment of the
trustees until the next annual election , or until his suc-
cessor iselected and qualified.
§ 12. When two or more candidates for any election
officeshall have an equal number of votes, the person
elected shallbe determined by lot,in such a manner as
the trusteesmay direct, in the presence of the trustees
and at leastthree other persons .
§13 . The trusteesshall,before every annual town elec-
tion,appoint two judges,a sheriff, and a clerk to hold
the same . The compensation of said officersshall be
fixedby the trustees; but theirliabilitesshallbe the same
as those officersof State electionsunder the general laws
of this Commonwealth .
§ 14. The electionsherein provided for shallbe held
between the hours of eight o'clock in the forenoon and
fiveo'clock in the afternoon; and alllaws in force in this
Commonwealth punishing illegal voting at the State
electionsshallapply toillegalvoting at the town elec-
tionsin Taylorsville, subjectto the provisionsof thisact
as to the qualificationof voters.
§ 15. All qualified voters under the laws of this Com-
monwealth who shall, at the time of offering to vote,
have residedin said town sixtydays previousthereto,
shallbe qualifiedvoters at any electionfor said town .
§ 16. The board of trusteesshalljudge of the qualifi-
cations,elections, and returns of itsmembers ; and cases
of contested elections, for any town officerselective by
the people,shallbe heard and determined by the trus-
tees,under such rules and regulations as they by ordi-
nance may prescribe.
§ 17. The outgoing town clerk shallcertifyto the Sec-
retaryof State the name of the person electedto the
officeof policejudge, and the Governor of this Com-
monwealth shall issue a commission to such officer.

1158 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 18. The poll-books of all electionsheld in said town
shallbe returned by the sheriffof electionto the clerk of
the town , who shallpreserve them as part of the records
of his office, examine and compare the same ,and issue
certificatesof election to the officerselected, which cer-
tificates,before being delivered,shallbe approved by and
spread upon the records of the board of trustees.
§ 19. The firstelection under thisact shallbe held on
the firstSaturday in June , one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four,and the government of said town prior
to that time shall consist of the various officers now in
office,subject to the other provisions of thisact.
Powers and Duties of Officers.
§20 . The clerkshall keep the corporate seal,if there
be one , and keep a complete record of the proceedings
of the board of trustees, whose meetings it shall be his
duty to attend; and transcriptsfrom the record of the
proceedings,certifiedby him ,shallbe evidenceinallthe
courts of this Commonwealth . He shall draw all war-
rants on the treasury directed by the board of trustees,
and shall countersign the same , and keep an accurate
account thereofin a book to be providedforthe purpose.
He shall issue licenses, when directed by the trustees to
do so,and perform all other duties which the trustees
may lawfully direct.
§21 . The treasurer shall receive all money belong-
ing to the town , and keep an accurate account of re-
ceiptsand expenditures. He shallpay out money only
on the order of the board of trustees . The warrant
for such payment shall be signed by the chairman or
acting chairman of the trustees, and countersignedby
the clerk; and itshall specify the purpose for which the
amount named thereinis paid. The treasurershallex-
hibitto the board of trustees, at least fifteendays before
the annual electionin each year , and oftener if required,
a detailed account of all receiptsand expenditures after
the date of the last annual report,and shall state the
condition of the treasury. Such accounts shall be pre

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1159
served by the clerk,who shallspreadthe same upon the
records of the board of trustees,and cause a copy there-
of to be published in some newspaper of the town one
time .
§22 . The marshal shallattend allsessionsof the police
court, and give to the town attorney notice of any pro-
ceeding in said court which it is his duty to attend. He
shall execute and make due return of all process di-
rected to him by the Taylorsvillepolicecourt,or by any
other court of thisCommonwealth . He shallpossess the
power and authorityof a constable,and his powers for
performing the duties of his officeshallbe co -extensive
with Spencer county . He shall be entitled,as compen-
sation,to the same fees allowed by law to sheriffsand
-constables for similar services ,to be collected in the same
manner . For other services, he shall receive such fees
and compensation as the trusteesmay by ordinance pre-
scribe. He shall be liable in the same cases,way , and
manner that sheriffsand constables are liable,and may
be sued and proceeded against in the same manner .
may or may not be appointed collectorfor said town .
He
§23 . The collector must collect all taxes and assess-
ments which may be leviedby the board of trustees; and
forthatpurpose he shallhave allthe powers of a sheriff
to make sales of personal and real estate; he shallpay
over to the treasurer all taxes and other money which
may come into his hands,as the board of trusteesmay
-direct. When the tax -listisplaced in his hands ,he shall
proceed at once to collectthe same ; and to enforce pay-
ment , may levy on and sellpersonalproperty,afterad-
vertisingthe same by written or printed notices posted
up in at leastthree public places in the town for not less
than ten days . Within three months from the time he
receives said list, he shall make out and return to the
board of trusteesa report,showing the amount collected,
the names of those who have paid,and those who are
delinquent; but he shall report no person delinquent, nor
shall he be credited in his settlement with the board of
trustees by the listof any person who owns personal

1160 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
property in said town out of which taxes can be made .
Upon the return of the collector'sreport as above pro-
vided,the board of trusteesshall,by order duly entered
of record,directa warrant to be made out, signed by the
chairman , and countersigned by the clerk, commanding
the collectorto levy upon and sellso much of the real
property of the delinquents situated in the town as may
be necessaryto pay the taxes due and the costsof sale;
on receiptof saidwarrant,the collectorshallproceed to
make levies as thereby commanded , and shall sell the
property levied on for cash at public auction, to the
highest bidder, after advertising the time and place of
sale for at leastfifteendays ,by written or printed notices
posted up at the court-house door, and at three other
public places in said town . On receiving the price bid,.
he shallgive to the purchaserof thepropertya certificate
of such purchase ,describingthe property in such a man-
ner that itmay be identified; such certificate, on presen-
tation to the board of trustees, shallentitlethe purchaser
to a deed from said trustees to any real estate so pur-
chased ,unless the same be redeemed as hereinafterpro-
vided . The collectorshall, within such time as they may
direct,make to the board of trusteesa full and complete
return of the manner in which he has executed said .
warrant,which return shallbe spread upon the records-
of the board .
§ 24. The attorney for the town shallprosecute offend-
ers against the by laws and ordinances of said town , and
give written opinions upon subjects submitted to him by
the board of trustees or its committees .
§25 . The assessorshallperform allthe dutieswhich
the trustees may ,by ordinance or by -laws ,prescribe,in
relationto the assessmentof propertyfor the purpose of
imposing or levying taxes.
§ 26. The trusteesmay ,by ordinance or by -law ,at any
time , require the performance of other duties, and fixthe
compensation of allofficerswhose dutiesare hereinafter
prescribed; and they may prescribethe dutiesand fixthe
compensation of allofficerselectedor appointed under

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1161
thisact,whose duties and compensation are not specifi-
callymentioned.
§27 . The police judge , treasurer, marshal , collector,
and such other officersas the board of trusteesmay re-
quire,shallseverally,before they enter upon the duties of
theirrespective offices, execute a bond to the trusteesof
Taylorsville,conditioned that they will faithfullyexecute
thedutiesof theirrespectiveoffices,and pay over tosuch
persons, and at such times as they may be entitledto re-
ceive the same ,allmoney that may come into theirhands
as such officers. The bonds so executedshallbe spread
upon the records of the board of trustees,and the origi-
nals carefullypreserved by the clerk.
§28.The trusteesof Taylorsville,or any person injured
by the breach of said bonds , may , in any court having
jurisdiction,prosecute suitor motion thereon against the
officerby whose breach of duty such injury is occasioned ,
and his sureties,or any of them , or the personal repre-
sentatives, heirs, or devisees of any or all of them , as
provided by law concerning sheriffs,constables,and other
like officers . No such bond shall be void until the trus-
tees of Taylorsville, and every person aggrieved by the
acts,omissions,or any neglect of such officers,have been
recompensed .
§29 . Any person having been an officerin said town ,
who shallnot, within ten days afternotificationand re-
quest,deliverto his successorin officeallthe property,.
papers,and effectsof every descriptionin hispossession
belonging to said town ,or appertaining to said officehe
held, shallforfeitand pay for the use of the town one
hundred dollars,besides allcosts and damages caused by
his neglect or refusalto do so,to be recovered by suitor
motion upon his officialbond .
Police Court .
§30 . There is hereby establishedin said town a court,
to be styledthe Taylorsvillepolicecourt. Said court
shallbe heldby one judge,who shallbe styledthe "Judge
of the Taylorsville Police Court ," who shall be elected
and qualifiedas hereinbefore provided .

1162 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§31 . The Taylorsvillepolice court shallhave exclusive
jurisdictionof all prosecutions for all violations of the
by laws and ordinancesof saidtown ,and concurrentju-
risdictionwith the other courts of Spencer county of mis-
demeanors committed in said town ,where the penalty
imposed by law does not exceed one hundred dollarsand
imprisonment for fiftydays . Said court shallhave power
to issue allwarrants and other process in the name of
the Commonwealth to compel the attendance of wit-
nesses; to take bonds and recognizances from persons
charged with offensescognizable before it, to appear and
answer the same ,and the same power to declare the same
forfeitedand to pronounce judgment thereon,and enforce
full compliance therewith, that the circuitcourts have .
It shallhave power to enterjudgment and award ,and
to issue writs for the purpose of enforcing its orders and
judgments , to the same extent, and in the same manner ,
as other courtsof thisCommonwealth of likejurisdic-
tion. The police judge shall be a conservator of the
peace ; he shallhave power to take recognizances to keep
the peace , and do all other acts and things necessary
and proper to carry into full and complete effect the
powers and jurisdictionconferred on said judge and
said court .
§32 . The police court shallbe held at such place as
the trustees shall designate,or,they failingto designate
and provide a suitableplace, at such place in said town
as saidjudge may select. Said courtshallbe open at all
times forthe trialof criminal and penal causes within its
jurisdiction.
§33 . Said court shall be a court of record , and the
judge thereof shall act as clerk; and copies of the rec-
ords thereof, over the signature of the judge , shall be
evidencein any courtin thisCommonwealth .
§ 34. Said court shall have no power to empanel a
grand jury , nor shall any indictment be necessary to
the exercise of the jurisdictionhereby conferred on said
court. It may punish contempt by a fineof not more
than five dollars and imprisonment not exceeding twen-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1163
1
ty-fourhours. No person shallbe triedin saidcourt for
any offensepunishableby a fineof more than fifteendol-
lars without the intervention of a jury,unless he shall
waive the privilegeof such trialby jury.
§ 35. The jurisdictionand duties of the police judge
in holding examining trialsshallbe such as are now pre
scribed by the general laws of this Commonwealth for
other likeofficers,and he shallhave power to hold exam-
ing trialsin cases of felony; and in allthings pertaining
thereto his jurisdictionshallbe equivalent to that of two
justicesof the peace in such cases.
§36 . All process from said police court shallbe in the
name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and may be
directed to any officerauthorized by law to execute such
process .
§37 . All fines and forfeitures recovered in said court
are hereby granted to the town of Taylorsville, and shall
be paid by the officercollectingthe same to the treasurer
of the town . Before the issuing of any execution or
other finalprocesson a judgment of saidcourtfora fine
or forfeiture, the defendant may pay the same to the
judge of the court. The defendant may also,beforeor
afterthe issuingof any execution,or capiasprofine,re-
plevy for three months any judgment ,fine,or forfeiture
of the police court in the same manner as is allowed by
law in similarcases within the jurisdictionof other courts
of this Commonwealth .
§ 38. A returnof not found on a capiasprofine, and of
on propertyfound on a fierifacias,issuedon any judgment
of the police court,shall authorize an equitable proceed-
ing in favor of the trustees of Taylorsville against the
estate of the defendant ,for the purpose of enforcing sat-
isfactionof such judgment ,as a return of no property
found authorizessuch proceeding in civilcauses.
§39 . Upon alljudgments for finesrendered by said
court, and which shallnot be paid or repleviedat the
time of rendering the same , an execution against the
estate of thedefendentmay be issued,on a capiasprofine
may be issuedrequiringthe imprisonment of the defend

1164 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ant in the town or county jail or work -house , until the
same shall be paid or replevied; but the imprisonment
shall not exceed one day for each dollar of such fine:
Provided, That said court may , in its discretion, assign
such defendant so fined to hard labor for the benefit of
the town until he shall pay the fine and costs adjudged
against him at the rate of one dollar per day ; such de-
fendant shall not be required to labor more than eight
hours per day . While at labor such defendant shall be
under the control of the town marshal , who shall be re-
sponsible, on his officialbond ,for the escape of thede-
fendant. When not at labor,such defendant shallbe im-
prisonedas providedin thissection; and forthe purpose
of putting him in jail or the work -house ,and taking him
out to labor, a certifiedcopy of the judgment assigning
him out to labor shall be sufficientauthority to the mar-
shal, jailer, or keeper of the work -house . All persons
sentencedtoimprisonmentby thejudgmentof saidcourt
may be assigned to labor in likemanner as those fined.
When persons imprisoned as contemplated by this sec-
tion, or confined in the county jail,the jailer'sfees shall
be paid by the town .
§ 40. The Taylorsvillepolice court shallhave jurisdic-
tion of civilcases for the recovery of money or personal
property, when the amount in controversy,exclusive of
interest and costs, does not exceed one hundred dollars .
When the amount in controversyexceeds fiftydollars,
exclusive of interestand costs,the pleadings in said court
shallbe regulated by the provisions of the CivilCode of
Practice applicable to circuitcourts; otherwise,the plead-
ings of said court shallbe oral. The judge of said court
shallhave power to grant orders of attachment , injunc-
tions,and restraining orders, orders of delivery, writs of
habeascorpus,distresswarrants,and writs of forcibleen-
try and detainer, under the same circumstances, rules
and regulations, as justices of the peace or judges of
courts with like jurisdictionand powers . Quarterly
terms of said court for the trial of civil causes shall be
held at such times as the judges thereofmay directby

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1165
an orderenteredupon the order-book ofsaidcourt. The
times forholding such terms shallbe on the second Mon-
days in March , June, September, and December , until
the same are changed in the manner indicated in this
section. The trusteesof Taylorsvilleshallprovide the
judge of said court with an order-book ,execution-book ,
and docket ; and it shall be his duty to keep a record of
allthe proceedings in his court,in the same manner and
to the same extent as is required of judges of the quar-
terly courts by the laws of this Commonwealth .
shallalso carefullypreserve allpapers and records per-
taining to his office, and deliverthem to his successor in
good order .
He
§41 . Appeals shallliefrom decisions and judgments of
the Taylorsvillepolice court to the Spencer circuitcourt
in criminal and penal cases,when the fine exceeds ten
dollarsor the imprisonment exceeds fivedays; but no
judgment shallbe suspended by such appeal unless the
defendant ,in five days after the rendition thereof, shall
filea copy of said judgment with the clerk of the circuit
court, and execute bond as requiredby sectionthree
hundred and sixty-four of the Criminal Code of Practice.
§ 42. Appeals shallliefrom thejudgment of saidpolice
court to the Spencer circuitcourt in civilcases when the
amount in controversy, exclusiveof interestand costs,
exceeds ten dollars .
§ 43. The manner of taking appeals ,and allproceed-
ings thereon,except as herein otherwiseindicated,shall
conform to the provisions of the Civiland Criminal Codes
of Practice of this Commonwealth .
§ 44. The feesof the policejudge shallbe the same in
all cases as are now ,or may hereafterbe,given by law
to other officersperforming likeservices,subjectto the
same regulations as to taxation and collection.
Taxation .
§45. The trusteesof Taylorsvilleshallhave power ,
subjectto the exceptionshereinaftermentioned, to an-
nually levy and collecttaxes,not more than twenty-five

1166 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
centson each one hundred dollars' worth of property in
said town ,including everything that is now or may be
hereafter subject to taxation by the State for revenue
purposes ; but personal property not situated within the
town ,and choses in action owned by citizensof the town ,
are not to be taxed. They may alsolevy a head or poll-
tax, not exceeding two dollars,upon every male inhabi-
tant of said town over the age of twenty -one years , and
a tax of not more than one dollar upon every dog that
any person may keep or sufferto be kept on hisor her
premises in said town .
§46. There shallbe exempt from taxation in said town
allpropertyexempted by the laws of thisCommonwealth
from taxation .
§47 . All taxes imposed by said trustees under this act
shall be a lien upon the property,real and personal , of
the persons against whom the same is assessed untilthe
same is paid; and no sale or transferof such property.
shall affect the lien thereon .
§48 . The right of redemption in all cases of sales of
real estate for taxes shall exist to the owner or his heirs
fortwo years from the date of sale, on payment of the
amount for which the same was sold, with twenty -five
per cent.thereof added ,and all the taxes accruing sub-
sequent to the sale. If the owner at the time of the
sale be an infant, married woman ,or person of unsound
mind , the right to redeem shall existtwo years afterthe
removal of such disability. If not redeemed within the
time specified,the board of trustees shall,on presenta-
tion of the certificateof sale,convey such real estate to
the purchaser .
§49 . All fines,forfeitures,taxes,and assessments here-
in contemplated and provided for shallbe appropriated
exclusivelyto the use and benefitof said town .
§50 . The trustees shall make such rules and regula-
tions concerning the assessing of property in said town
as they may see proper ; and also for the revising and
correcting of the listsreturned by the assessor.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1167
General Powers and Duties of the Trustees.
§51 . The board of trusteesshallhold stated meetings ,
and the chairman , or, in his absence ,the town clerk, may
callmeetings by notice to each of the members . A ma-
jorityof the members elected shall constitute a quorum .
In the absence of the chairman ,any member of the board
may be selectedto discharge his duties for the time .
§ 52. The board of trusteesshallhave power to make ,
obtain, and publish all such ordinances , by -laws , police
and sanitaryregulations,not contraryto the Constitution
of the United States or the Constitution of this State , for
the good government of said town ,and to promote the
health, security, trade,and commerce thereof,that they
may deem necessaryor expedient. They may enforce
the observance of allby-laws, ordinances, and regula-
tions made in pursuance of this act,by penalties not to
exceed one hundred dollars' fine and fiftydays' imprison-
ment .
§53 . The board of trustees shall have the manage-
ment and control of the finances,and all the property,
real, personal, and mixed , belonging to the town ; and
shallhave power to provide forthe erection,repair,main-
tenance ,and government of all public buildings for the
town . They shallhave power within the local jurisdic-
tion of said town by ordinance or by by -law-
First. To restrainand prohibitalldescriptionsof gam-
ing,fraudulent devices,playing at cards or other games
of chance, whereat money or property of value is bet,
won , or lost.
Second. To license, tax,regulate, or prohibit billiard-
tables, pool-tables, pigeon hole and similartables, and
nine or ten-pin alleys,or likealleys.
Third. To license, tax, regulate, or prohibit all con-
certs, exhibitions, shows, circuses, theatricalperform-
ances , and all other exhibitions and amusements .
Fourth. To restrainor suppressdisorderlyhouses,gro-
ceries,saloons,halls,and houses of prostitution.
Fifth. To restrain, suppress, and punish indecent,ob-
scene, or profane language or conduct, and to restrain
and punish vagrantsand prostitutes.

1168 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Sixth. To restrain,regulate, or prohibitthe running at
large of horses,swine , and other animals , and to impose
penaltieson the owner of any such animal for a violation
of any ordinance or by -law in relationthereto.
Seventh. To prohibitthe running at largeof dogs,and
to authorize the destruction of the same when found at
largecontraryto by-law or ordinance, and to impose
penalties on the owners thereof for permitting them to
run at large .
Eighth. To appoint watchmen or policemen, and to
prescribe theirduties,powers ,and pay .
Ninth . To establish and maintain a jail or watch-
house,a hospitalor pest-house,an engine house, work-
house ,and market -house ,and make rules and regulations
for the government thereof, and to appoint officersfor the
conduct and management of the same .
Tenth. To authorizethe clerkto grant and issuelicense,
and directthetime and manner of issuing and registering
thereof, and the feesto be paid him therefor; but no
licenseshall be granted for more than one year , and the
clerk'sfee for issuingthe same shallnot exceed fifty
cente .
Eleventh. To license, tax, and regulate auction sales

CHAPTER 1398 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe EquitableManufacturing and TransitCompany .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That T. N. Sheppard,John W. Tuttle,A. P. Simp-
son,James R. Hindman , C. W. Stone , J. C. Wells ,A. J.
Hazeltine , C. H. Noyes , Fred . Morck ,J. O. Marshall, and
Goldsmith B. West , and their associates,successors, and
assigns,be,and are hereby,declareda body-politic,under
thename and styleof the EquitableManufacturing and
TransitCompany ; and by such name shallhave perpetual
succession, and may sue and be sued ,plead and be im-
pleaded,in any court,as may a naturalperson; may have
and use a common seal; and may ordain and put in force
such by -laws ,rules and regulations,for the good govern-
ment of said company , and the efficientmanagement of
itsaffairs,as may be deemed expedient,not inconsistent
with the laws of this Commonwealth .
§2 . The capital stock of the company shall be five
hundred thousand dollars,divided into shares of one hun

1174 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
dred dollarseach,which shallbe deemed personalprop-
erty,and shallbe transferableonly on the books of the
company . The presidentand directorsshallhave power ,
from time to time ,to declare dividends out of the profits
of theirbusiness; and for the purpose of extending their
works and the convenience of theirbusiness,by a vote of
the board of directorsthe capitalstock of the company
may be increased to such an amount as may be deemed
necessaryto carry out successfullythe businessof the
company , not,however , to exceed in the whole amount
of stock the sum of one million five hundred thousand
dollars. They may borrow money and issuebonds there-
of, bearing interest at any rate not exceeding six per
cent.per annum , and secure the payment thereofby a
mortgage or mortgages upon the real and personal es-
tate of the corporation,not exceeding two -thirds of the
paid up capital. The said corporation, in additionto
the specificpower hereby given and enumerated, may
exercise any and all the powers conferred upon similar
corporationsby the general laws of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky .
§3 . The business of the corporation shallbe to furnish
such persons ,parties,corporations,or corporate authori-
ties as itmay contract with , fuel and motive power from
natural gas ,which may be obtained by boring or exca-
vating the earth; also refined oils for illuminating,and
natural and manufactured oilsfor lubricating purposes .
The said company may purchase and hold ,and may sell
or exchange the same ,and purchase again for said pur-
pose ,such lands,oil rights,leases,real estate,as may be
necessaryfortheirbusiness,and may erectsuch buildings
thereon as may be deemed necessary for the interestof
the corporation. Said company may dig,bore , and open
wells of any kind,and use the productsthereof,whether
oil, natural gas , or other matter or fluid found in the
earth,and may lay down pipes or conduits,with branch
lines,for conductingthe products of said well or wells,
eitheroil,gas,or fluids,from the saine to any point or
place in the State of Kentucky .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1175
§ 4. Said corporation may buy ,lease,or rent any suita-
ble lands for the purpose of boring thereon for oil,gas ,
or fluids, and may dispose of the same by sale or ex-
change ; and itmay receive real or personal estate, lease-
holds, mining rights, and rightsof way ,in payment of
the whole or such partsof any stock subscriptionsmade
to the capitalstock of said corporation as they may deem
advisable .
§5 . The affairsof said corporation shallbe managed
by ten directors,all of whom shallbe stockholders,and
two thirds of whom shall constitute a quorum for the
transaction of business. The ten persons mentioned as
incorporatorsin thisact shallconstitutethe directorsfor
the firstyear , and shall act until their successors are
chosen and qualified. After the firstyear the stockhold-
ers,on the firstMonday in April of each year ,shallmeet
together at the officeof the corporation, and elect ten
directorsforthe ensuing year , who shallhold their offices
until their successors are chosen . Elections for directors
shall be by ballot,and may be advertised if so ordered
by the board of directors.
§ 6. The directors shall choose from their own number
a president,vice president, secretary, and treasurer,and
general superintendent; and the treasurer shall be re-
quired to give a bond ; and the period of their officeand
allthe officesof the company shallbe fixed.
§7 . The incorporators herein named ,or a majority of
them , may , after the passage of this act,open books for
the subscriptionof stock; and when fifteenthousand dol-
larsof the capital stock herein provided for issubscribed,
the company may be organized according to the provis-
ions of this charter, and begin operations. The stock
that may not be subscribed for at the organization may
be held or disposedof as the directorsmay select; and
the directors may make such calls for the unpaid sub-
scriptionsas,in theirjudgment ,the interestof the com-
pany may demand ,untilthe whole arrearsare paid; but
no stock shallbe disposed of nor transferred at any time
without the consent of a majority of the directors first

1176 LAWS OF KENTUCKY ..
obtained at a meeting calledfor that purpose; and for
any and allelectionsthe directorscan vote by proxy,
excepting that of the annual meeting and applications to
transfer stock .
§ 8. The general officeof the company shallbe at some
othersuitablepoint in the Stateof Kentucky ,where the
corporation shall have their works or refineriesestab-
lished for the manufacturing of refined and lubricating
oils.
§ 9. The private property of the stockholders shallnot
be liablefor corporate debts.
§ 10. The directors of said company are hereby em-
powered to pass any rules and by -laws ,not inconsistent
with this act and the laws of this State,that they may
deem necessary for theirfulland complete working under
thischarter; and any rules,regulations,and by -laws ,not
inconsistent with this charter and the laws of this State ,
so adopted and passed by said directors to carry out the
powers herein granted ,shallbe as lawful as though em-
bodied in this act .
§ 11. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1400 .
AN ACT in relationto publicroads in Butler county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That there shall be levied a tax of ten cents on the
one hundred dollars' worth of property in Butler county
subject to taxation,for road and bridge purposes , to be
applied as the county court of claims may direct.
2. That each able-bodied man between the ages of
twenty -one and fiftyyears shallbe required to work four
days in each year,or pay to the road surveyorof hisdis-
trictone dollarfor each day he failsto work ; and in the
event of the failure of any person to work ,the surveyor
shall make out an account , in the nature of a fee-bill,
against the person or persons so failing,at one dollar per
day for each day he or they so failto work or pay , and
listit with the sheriff, who shall collect it as other taxes
are collected,and pay itifover to the saidsurveyor,to be
by him used for the benefitof the road . The sheriffshall
collect ten per cent, additional on each claim so listed
with him to pay the expense of collecting.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1179
§ 3. This actshallbe in forceafterthe second Monday
in August : Provided, A majorityof the votersvoting at
a poll to be opened at the August election,one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-four, forthe purpose of
- testingthe sense of voters,vote for it. Proper poll-books
shallbe furnished the officersof election,who shall ask
each voter whether or not he votes for this act , and his
vote shallbe recorded for or against,as the case may be .
Approved May 12, 1884 .

powers , privileges, franchises, and rights necessary for
the carrying out of the provisions and purposes of this
act,whether forthe condemnation of property,the con-
structionof toll-houses , or other things requisiteand nec-
essary .
§3 . The capitalstock of said corporation shallbe one
million dollars, and shall be divided into shares of one
hundred dollars each ,and be made payable in such way
and at such times as the board of directors may require.
The affairsof the corporation shallbe under the manage-
ment and control of a board of directors composed of
nine members ,chosen at an annual meeting to be held

1184 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
on the firstMonday in May of each and every year , who
shall hold office until their successors are elected and
qualified; and in case of a vacancy ,the board shall fill
the same . Said directorsshall,from among their num-
ber choose a president, and shallappoint a secretary and
treasurer,and such other officersand employes as they
may deem proper , and may prescribe their duties, and
require bond for the faithfulperformance of theirduties;
and may make allby-laws and regulationsforthe gov-
ernment of saidcorporation andits officersand employes .
§ 4. Said corporation is hereby authorized to borrow
any money for the purposes named in this act, and to
pledge itspropertyand franchisesto securethe payment
of the same , and to issue bonds in any amount not ex-
ceeding fifteenhundred thousand dollars,itsstockholders
may determine ,and secure the same by mortgage on its
property and franchise.
§5 . Said corporation may contract with any railroad
company ,in or out of thisState,to constructa railroad
trackor tracksover saidbridge,and to operatethe same ,
on such terms as the directors may deem proper ; and
may charge such tollsas may be usuallycharged by sim-
ilarcorporations,and in accordance with the laws of this
State and the United States,forrailroadengines and cars,
street-railway cars,vehicles for hauling or riding,horses
and otheranimals,footmen,and allotherthingspassing
over the said bridge .
§ 6. That the private property of the stockholders shall
not be responsiblefor the debts or liabilitiesof said com-
pany ,except to the extent or amount of stock subscribed
by each .
§ 7. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
-afteritspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1408 .

,

and assigns,be,and they are hereby,incorporatedinto a

§3 That said company shallhave the rightfor them-
selves, or as agents upon commission or otherwise , to
lease,purchase ,sell, or otherwise acquire, and dispose of
oiland oillands,mine or bore foroil,store,insure,ship,
and transportpetroleum and itsproducts,and may con-
struct and erect tanks of iron or other suitable material for
itsstorage of oil,and to lay down ,construct,and connect
ironor otherpipesforthatpurpose from and to any point
in thisCommonwealth ,when thebusinessofthiscompany
may require such lines; and the said company shall also

have the right to acquire,use,and occupy such lands as

tanks to hold the oilor petroleum,and forthe purpose of
laying down pipes or tubing forthe conveyance of the oils
from the wells or reservoirsof thiscompany ,or any other
similarcompany ; and to make and construct branch pipe
linesor branches to said branches ,and for the erection of
such machinery as may be required to carry on the trans-
portation or storage and manufacturing business of said

§ 4. That the corporators herein named shall, when

minating or heating purposes .

sage .

CHAPTER 1409 ,
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kentucky and Ohio Bridge Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That John C. Benton , B. W. Foley ,A. D. Bullock,
Henry Hanna ,and Walter Cleary,and theirassociates,
successors, or assigns, be , and they are hereby , consti-
tuted and declared a body -corporate, under the name and
style of the Kentucky and Ohio Bridge Company ; and
by that name they are hereby vested with allthe powers
incident to corporations.
§2. That said company be ,and it is hereby ,invested
with all the rights,powers ,and privilegesnecessary for
the construction of a bridge across the Ohio river, ex-
tending from some convenient point within the corporate
limitsof the city of Covington, in Kenton county, to
some convenient point within the corporate limitsof the
cityof Cincinnati; and said corporation ishereby author-
izedto purchase or condemn ,by writof ad quod damnum ,
according to law , and hold such real estate as may be
necessary for the site of said bridge,including its piers,
abutments , tollhouses , and approaches : Provided, That
said bridge shall be constructed so as not to obstruct nav-
igation further than the laws of the United States shall
allow .
§3 . That said corporation, its successors or assigns,
may extend a railway over said bridge,with as many
tracks as may be deemed expedient ; and shall have the
rightto fixreasonableratesof tollforpassing over said
bridge, and collectthe same from all persons passing
thereon ,and for all goods and chattels,vehiclesand ani-
mals passing or carriedthereon; and for thispurpose
toll-gates may be erected on said bridge,and the rates
of tollshallbe posted up in conspicuous places where
toll is demanded .
§4 . The capitalstock of said company shallbe two
millions of dollars, divided into shares of one hundred
dollarseach;but the same may be increasedat any time
by a majority vote of the stockholders. The manner of

1188 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
subscription,and the time ,terms ,and condition of pay-
ment shallbe determined by the corporators named here-
in,or theirsuccessors or assigns.
§5 . The said corporation shallhave power to borrow
money , and execute such evidences of indebtedness as it
may deem proper,and secure the same by mortgage or
otherwise ; and itmay sellitsbonds at such rates of dis-
count as itmay deem expedient .
§6 . The business of said corporation shall be con-
ducted by a board of directors of not less than three nor
more than seven in number ,who shall,of theirnumber ,
elect a president,whose duties and compensation shall
be prescribed by the board ; and the board shallalso elect
or appoint such other officersand agents as it may con-
sider necessary .
§7 . The president and directors shall have power to
make all rules and by -laws which they may consider
necessary for the government of the corporation, not
inconsistentwith law ; and they shallalso have power
tobuy ,sell,and hold realand personalproperty, and to
do any and allthings,not speciallyprovided forherein
which they may regard as beneficialto the corporation :
Provided always, Such acts shallnot be in conflictwith
the provisionsof the Constitutionof the United States
or the Constitutionand laws of the State of Kentucky .
,
§8. The corporatorsnamed herein,or those of them
who shallact,theirsuccessors or assigns,shallconstitute
the firstboard of directors. They shallhold officeuntil
theirsuccessorsare elected according to the provisions of
the by -laws which may be prescribed.
§ 9. This act shall be in force from and afterits pas
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884.
