CHAPTER 1412 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Citizens'Savings Bank , of Owensboro .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there ishereby establisheda bank of discount
and deposit in the cityof Owensboro ; and the subscrib-
ers,their associates, successors, and assigns, shall be a
body -politicand corporate,by the name and styleof the
" Citizens'Savings Bank ;" and shallbe capable of con-
tracting and being contracted with,of suing and being
sued , of impleading and being impleaded ,and of answer-
ing and defending ,in all courts and places whatever ; it
may have a common seal, and change and renew the
same at pleasure.
§2 That said bank may be under the control and
directionof three or more directors,to be elected by the
stockholders, and shall serve one year ,or until theirsuc-
cessorsshallbe elected and qualified. The directorsmay
select,annually, a presidentfrom their number . The
stockholders may meet and elect the said three or more
directors when five hundred shares shall be subscribed,
and fiftyper cent, thereof paid : J. H. Hickman , J. M.
Fetter, E. J. Halsey , J. D. Powers , W. C. Nones , P. T.
Watkins ,and J. B. Alexander,or any two of them ,may ,
at such time , place,and in such manner as suits their
convenience ,open books for subscriptionfor the capital
stock,which shall be in shares of fiftydollarseach ; and
said corporation may commence business so soon as the
saidfiftyper cent.of fivehundred shareshave been paid
in,and the presidentor cashiershallhave made oath to
the fact of such payment before some judge ,justice,or
notary .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1191
§3 . That the directorsshallhave power toreceivesub-
scriptions for so many shares of the stock of said com-
pany remaining unsoldas they may ,at any time,choose
to sell; to declaredividendsof the profitsarisingout of
the business; and to appoint such officers, agents, and
servants as they may deem necessary to conduct the
business of the bank ,and pay them such sums as wages
and salaries, and take from them such bonds to secure
the faithfulperformance of theirduty ,as they may think
reasonable and proper. In case of the death or resigna-
tion of any director, the vacancy can be filledby the
board of directors. The stock shallbe deemed personal
property,and shallbe assignableonly on the books of
the bank , upon such rules as the board of directorsshall,
from time to time, establish; but said corporationshall
have a lien on the stock to secure any indebtedness by
the stockholder, as principalor otherwise,to said corpo-
ration. For the contracts of the corporation each stock-
holdershallbe individuallyresponsibleto the amount of
stock held by him in said corporation. The capital stock
of saidbank may be increased,from time to time,to two
hundred and fiftythousand dollars, as the president and
board of directorsmay deem advisableand proper, but
not to exceed that amount .
§4 . That said bank may transact business as a natural
person; may loan money , discountpromissory notes or
billsof exchange ; may buy and sellgold and silvercoin,
exchange stocks or other securities, and all other evi-
dences of indebtedness ; and may do a general banking
business, except that it shallnot have power to make or
issue notes of its own . The promissory notes and the
billsof exchange made negotiable and payable at its
banking -house , or at any other banking -house , when dis-
counted by thisbank ,shallbe,and they are hereby,put
upon the same footing of foreign billsof exchange ; and
like remedy may be had thereon,jointly and severally,
against the makers , drawers , and indorsers . The said
bank shallhave the power to make advances on ap-
proved securities, upon agriculturalproducts, including

1192 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
growing crops, and upon all manufactures and fixtures-
as personalproperty. It may receivein pledge, or as
securityfor money loaned or debts owing it,bonds ,stocks,
produce, and merchandise,United Statesvouchers, cer-
tificatesor bonds of indebtedness , or the bonds of this or
any other State,city,or county ,or company incorporated
by the United States or any State,and sellthe same on
the non -payment of the debt or demand by ordinary
course of judicialproceedings.
§5 . That said bank may receive deposits of gold sil-
ver,bank notes, or other notes which may be circulating
as money , and repay the same in such manner , at such
times, and with such interest, as may be agreed upon
with the depositorsby special or general contract. Said
bank may receive deposits from minors and married wo-
men ,and the receipts and acquittances of such minors
and married women shall be valid .
§ 6. That the said bank may acquire,hold,possess,use ,
occupy,and enjoyallsuch realestate,goods and chattels,
as may be convenient for the transaction of itsbusiness,
or to furtherthe objectsof itscreation,or which may be
conveyed to itas surety for any debt,or purchased to aid
in the satisfactionof any debt,judgment , or decree , and
selland convey the same ; and said bank may make such
by -laws for their government as they may deem neces--
sary,provided the same be not contrary to the Constitu-
tion and laws of this State or of the United States .
§7 . That it shallbe the duty of the cashier,on the
firstday of January ,in each year ,to pay into the State
Treasury ,for the benefitof revenue proper, fiftycents on
each one hundred dollars of stock held and paid for in
said bank , which shall be in full of allstax or bonus-
thereon of every kind .
§ 8. That if the cashier,or a clerk,teller,or other offi-
cer of said bank ,shall appropriate any of the funds to
his own use , or shallwillfullyfailto make correct entries-
on the books of the bank , with intent to cheat or defraud
the corporation,or any other person ,or to conceal any
improper appropriationof funds,the officerso offending:

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1193
shallbe deemed guiltyof felony,and shall,upon convic
tionthereof,be confinedin the jailand penitentiaryof
thisCommonwealth fora periodof not lessthan two nor
more than twenty years ,
§9 . That it shall not be lawful for said bank to issue
any note or billto be passed or used as currency .
§ 10. That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage,
and continue in force thirtyyears .
Approved May 12,1884.
CHAPTER 1413 .
AN ACT to amend chapterone thousand fivehundred and thirtyof Session
Acts, one thousand eight hundred and eighty, entitled" An act estab-
lishing and incorporating the town of Bonnieville, in Hart county ,"
approved May fifth, one thousand eight hundred and eighty.
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That section eleventh of an act , entitled " An act
establishingand incorporating the town of Bonnieville,
in Hart county," approved May fifth,one thousand eight
hundred and eighty,be,and the same ishereby,repealed,
and the said act is so amended that power and authority
is hereby granted to the trustees of the town of Bonnie-
villeto issue a license to keep a house for retailingspir-
ituous, vinous , or malt liquors in said town : Provided,
They shall not grant said license at a lower rate than
one hundred dollars for one year , and on condition the
party receiving said license shallkeep an orderly house ;
and said license shall give complete immunity to the
holderwithoutthe grant of the county court. Said board
of trusteesmay charge a greater rate than one hundred
dollarsfortwelve months,ifthey deem proper: Provided,
however ,That nothing herein shallbe construed to operate
as a repeal of any general law now in force or hereafter
enacted ; and any and allapplicants for licenseshallpay
the same license to the county judge and clerk for the
State of Kentucky ,and comply with alllaws as to State
license now in force or hereafter enacted .

1194 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 2. Before a license shall be granted to any person
to keep a house to sellspirituous,vinous.or malt liquors
by retailin said town ,he shallenter into bond ,with good
security,to be approved by said board of trustees,to and
with the Commonwealth , that he will keep an orderly
house ; that he will not keep his place open on Sunday ;
and that he will not furnish, sell, or give such liquors to
persons under twenty -one years of age ; and for each vio-
lation of said covenant he will pay to the Commonwealth
of Kentucky the sum of fiftydollars,to be recovered by
action or indictment in any court of competent jurisdic-
tion; and the suretiesshallbe jointlyliablewith the prin-
cipal.
§3 . Any person ,whether druggist or not,who shall sell
spirituous, vinous or malt liquors, except native wine of
his own manufacturing , within the corporate limits,with-
out having firstprocured a license so to do from said
board of trustees, shall, for each offense, be fined not
lessthan fortydollarsnor more than one hundred dollars,
to be recovered by indictment,penal action, information,
or warrant , in any court having competent jurisdiction
thereof .
§ 4. All money arising from the issual of license, and
from the inflictionof the penalties denounced by any of
the provisions of this act, shallbe paid, when collected,
to the treasurer of said town , and shall be used for the
benefit of the said town .
§5 . The treasurer of said town shall be required to
enter into bond ,with good security,approved by the said
board of trustees , to and with the Commonwealth of
Kentucky ,conditioned that he willkeep an accurate and
correct account of all money received and disbursed by
him , and that he willpay to any and allpersons entitled
thereto the money that may come into his hands and due
such person ; and any person aggrieved by his failure
may bring and prosecute suit on said bond ,in the name
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,for his or her benefit.
§ 6. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afterMay fifteenth, one thousand eight hundred and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY , 1195
eighty-four,and shallcontinue in forcefor two years only,
-at which time itshallcease and be inoperative.
Approved May 12, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1414 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Manufacturers'Mutual Fire InsuranceCompany .
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Samuel L. Avery ,Jacob L. Smyser , Andrew
Cowan , Arthur Peter,John G. Baxter ,T. J. McCoy ,John
B. McFerran , Charles Bremaker ,Thos . H. Sherley,J. M.
Atherton , Irvine Eddy ,Jordan Giles, Dennis Long , and
other persons who may hereafter become members of
said corporation, in the manner herein prescribed, be,
and they are hereby , incorporated and made a body-
politic,by the name of the Manufacturers ' Mutual Insur-
ance Corporation,for the purpose of insuring,upon the
plan of mutual insurance,manufactories and other prop-
erty,both personal and real,againstlossor damage by
fireor other casualties. The firstnine persons signing
the articlesof incorporation,being residentsof thisState,
and owning in the aggregate property within thisState of
the value of not lessthan one hundred thousand dollars,
shall be directors of same until the first annual meeting .
The articlesof association having been filedwith the com-
missioner, with proof of publication of notice and proof
that policiesof the requisitenumber and amount have
been applied for, shallbe examined by the Attorney Gen-
eral, and if found in conformity with law ,the commis-
sioner shallissue his certificatethat said corporation is
duly organized , and is entitledto do business under the
laws of this State. This corporation shallhave the fol-
lowing powers :
First . To make contracts of insurance in this State
and elsewhere with any person against loss or damage
by fire, lightning,or storm , on allspecies of property,for
such premiums or consideration, and under such regula-
tions and restrictionsas such corporations in its by -laws
may provide .

1196 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Second. To purchase ,receive,hold,possess,and convey
allsuch real estate and personal property as shallbe nec--
essary for its accommodation , and the convenient trans-
action of its business , as in the cases where a general
fireinsurance company may take and hold the same .
Third. To prescribethe manner and form forthe admis-
sion of members and the withdrawal of the same .
Fourth. To make all necessary rules and regulations
concerning insurance on property, and the appraisement
and payment of losses, and alter and amend the same at
pleasure ,subject to the restrictionshereinafter provided ..
Fifth. To fix the compensation of itsofficers,and their
duties and obligations; and to requirebond for the faith-
fulperformance of their duties.
Sixth. To exercise such other powers as shall be nec-
essary to effectthe objectsof such corporation.
§2 . This corporation, when duly organized , and on
receivingthe certificateof the commissioner,may issue
policieson any and allspeciesof realand personalprop-
erty for any time not exceeding fiveyears, and not to
extend beyond the time such corporation is to continue ,
and for an amount not to exceed ten thousand dollars on
any one risk. This corporationmay classifythe property
insured therein under differentrates, corresponding as
nearly as may be to the greater or lessriskfrom fireand
losswhich may attach to each of the several buildings
insured and theircontents. All persons so insured shall,
give their obligations to the corporation binding them-
selves, theirheirs and assigns, to pay their pro rata
assessment on notes given for premiums to the corpora-
tion for the necessary expense and losses by fireor other
casualty which may be sustained by any member thereof
during the time for which their respective policieswere
written; and they shallalso,at the time of effectingthe
insurance ,pay such percentage in cash, and such other
chargesas may be requiredby the by-laws of the corpo-
ration; but no policy shallbe issued untilagreements
have been entered into for insurance with not less than
one hundred applicants, on which the aggregate pre

LAWS OF KENTUCKY 1197
miums shall be not less than five thousand dollars ; and
if, at any time after such corporation shall have been
organized and begun business,the number of policiesin
force shall have become less than one hundred , the secre-
tary shallat once notifythe Commissioner of Insurance,
and saidcorporationshallwind up itsaffairsin the man-
ner provided by law . This corporation may be proceeded
against and dissolved as provided by law in case of
other insurance corporations organized in thisState.
§3 . The president or vice president and secretary of
thiscorporation shallannually , within the month of Jan-
uary , prepare and deposit in the officeof the Commis-
sioner of Insurance a statement , verified by their oaths ,
of the business of the corporation during the year , and
of its condition on the thirtyfirstday of December the
next preceding ,in such form ,and containing such items
or factsin regard to itsbusiness or condition,as the Com-
missioner may require.
§4 . Every member of this corporation who may sus-
tain lossby fireor other casualty,as may be stipulated
in his policy of insurance ,shall immediately notify the
secretary, who shall proceed at once to determine the
amount of loss or damage , and ,if possible, make settle-
ment . If the partysustaininglossshallnot acquiescein
the decision,theclaim(when no contestof fraudismade )
shallbe submitted to referees, mutually agreed upon ,
whose award shallbe final. If the amount awarded by
the referees shall be greater than the amount awarded
by this corporation,this corporation shall bear the ex-
pense of the reference; if less,the party procuring the
reference shallbear the expense . Whenever the amount
of any loss has been ascertained which requires an as-
sessment to be made ,then the president and officersof
the corporationshallmake an assessment upon allpre-
mium notes held by said company sufficienttopay such
loss. The secretary,when any such assessment shallhave
been completed, shallimmediately notifyevery person
composing such corporation,by lettersent to his usual
post-officeaddress,of the factof a loss,theamount there

1198 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of,and the sum due from him as his share thereof, and
of the time when and to whom such payment is to be
made ; but such time shallnot be lessthan thirtynor
more than sixtydays from the date of such notice. If
the assured neglectpayment of any assessment within
sixtydays from date of notice,then his policyshallbe
null and void untilsuch assessment is paid , and also the
assessinentswhich may be leviedduring the suspension
of the policy or policieson account of the non -payment of
a previous assessment. Actions may be brought against
any member of this corporation who shallrefuse or neg-
lectto pay any assessmentmade upon him by the provis-
ions hereof .
§5 . Any member of this corporationmay withdraw
therefrom by giving notice,in writing, to the secretary
thereof, and paying up alldues,and his ratableshare of
all loss or damage by fireor other casualty suffered by
the corporation up to the date of his withdrawal . The
officersshall have power to annul any policy by giving
notice , in writing , to that effect to the holder thereof , and
paying any sums which may be due from the corporation
to such member .
§ 6. This corporation shall bear in its title, and upon
the face of each policyand renewal receipt,the word
" mutual ;" and all notes given in payment of premiums
of insurance shall show upon their face that they were
so given , and statethat same are not negotiable.
7. This act shall take effectfrom its passage .
Approved May 10, 1884.

body -politicand corporate,under and by the name and

or otherwise,estate and property,both real and personal,
and hold,use ,and own , or selland convey the same at
pleasure; to have and use a common seal,and to break
or alterthe same . The businessof said company shall
be the mining , and preparation for market and shipment
and sale of coal and other substances , and conversion of
coal, or the substance thereof, into coke , and the ship-
ment and sale thereof . The board of directors shall have
power to adopt and put in execution such by -laws and
ordinances as they may deem proper for the govern-
ment of the affairsof said company , not inconsistent
with the laws of this State or of the United States .
§2 . The amount of the capitalstock of said company
shall be fixed by the board of directors at any amount
not exceeding three hundred thousand dollars, and may
be increased by said board to any amount not exceeding
aforesaid amount ,and shallbe divided into shares of one
hundred dollars each . Said stock may be issued and
soldby the board of directorsof said company at such
times and places as they may deem proper; and said
stock shallbe personalestate, and pass as such. Pri-
vate property of stockholders shallbe exempt from cor-
porate debts .
§3 . For the management of the affairsof said com-
pany a board of seven directorsshall be chosen by the
stockholderstherein,who shallhold theirofficefor twelve
months ,and until their successors are duly elected and
enter upon the dischargeof the duties of theiroffice.
The time for the election of board of directors shall be
fixedby the board of directors. The persons named in
the firstsection of this act , or such of them as may act,
and such persons as may be chosen in place of those
who may decline to act,shallconstitutethe firstboard of

1200 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
directors , and shall hold their office until their successors
shall be elected and enter upon the discharge of the
duties of their office. The board of directors shall choose
one of their number president,who shallbe president of
the company ,and shalldischarge the usual duties of such
office. The said persons named in the firstsection of
this act,or such of them as may act,may organize said
company ; and if any of those named shalldecline to act,
those remaining shallchoose other person or persons to
act in place of such as may decline; and they shallelect
a president of said company ,who shall hold his office
until his successor shallbe elected and enter upon the
discharge of the duties of his office. The president and
directors shallhave power to fillallvacancies that may
occur in theirboard,and to appoint such officersand
agents of said company as they may deem proper , fix
their compensation , and remove them at pleasure. In
all elections the stockholders shall be entitled to one vote
for each share of stock; and stockholdersmay vote either
in person or by written proxy .
§ 4. Books forthe subscriptionto the capitalstockmay
be opened at any time or place and closed again at
pleasure. It shall be lawful forthe president and board
of directors to borrow or obtain loan of such sums of
money , and on such terms ,as they may deem proper ,for
the use of said company ; and as security therefor to
mortgage orpledge allor any partof theproperty,estate,
improvements ,privileges,effects, and assetsof said com-
pany forthe repayment thereof,at such time or times as
may be agreed upon .
§5 . In the event of the failure of any stockholder to
pay forhis stock in accordance with terms of subscrip-
tion,or the failure to pay any call thereon,the board of
directorsmay forfeitthe stock of such stockholder or sub-
scriberto the company ,or if they so elect,they may sue
forand recover the subscriptionprice of such stock.
§ 6. The said company may buy , lease,or rent such
lands as they may deem suitable for mines ; and may
acquire mining privileges, right of way , and other

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1201
property rights, such as they may deem necessary to
their business; and may dispose of same or any por-
tion thereof by sale or otherwise . They may receive
real estate,leasehold or mining rights,or rightsof way ,
in payment of subscriptionfor stock,or such partthere-
of as they may deem advisable.
§7 . Said company may erect on any of theirlands
such buildings, engines , machinery ,coke -ovens , and fix-
tures of any kind as may be deemed convenient and prop-
er for the conducting of their business; they shall have
power to construct coal chutes and screens,and such ap-
pendages as may be found necessary and convenient ; and
may construct and operate railways,tramways ,and other
structures for the carrying on of their business to and
from any railroad or navigable stream in the vicinityof
their mines .
§8 . This company may consolidate with any other
coal or iron company doing business in thisState, a ma-
jorityin value of the stockholders agreeing thereto.
§ 9. This act shallbe in forcefrom itspassage .
Approved May 12 , 1884.

CHAPTER 1416 .
AN ACT to regulatethe transportationof explosivecompounds .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. No railroadcorporation,streetrailway company ,
steamboat company ,or other association,copartnership,
person or persons , engaged in the transportation of pas-
sengers within thisCommonwealth ,shallknowingly trans-
port between personsor placeswithinthe territoriallim-
its of thisCommonwealth ,or transport into such limits
forsale,storage, or use therein, any explosive compound
in quantitiesexceedingthe amounts hereinafterprovided
for,in any vehicle containing passengers ,or in any ve-
hicle attached to any railroad train or vehicle carrying
passengers ,or in any case,unless the said explosive com-
LOC . L., VOL . II- 76

1202 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
pounds be plainly and legiblymarked with the names of
such compounds , and the words " explosive," " danger-
ous." It shallbe the duty of the Board of Railroad Com-
missioners,from time to time,to make rules fixingthe
maximum amounts of various explosivecompounds which
may be so carried in any public vehicle or in a railroad
traincontainingpassengers,or in a vehicleattachedto
such train,or in any car or public vehicle,whether freight
or passenger ; and said rules,subscribed by said Railroad
Commissioners ,shallbe published for the period of four
weeks in a daily paper published in Louisville, Lexing-
ton ,Paducah , and Covington each , and a copy of said
rules, certifiedby eithermember of said board ,and a like
certificateof the factof theirdue publication,shall in all
casesbe legaland conclusiveproof of said rules,and of
the proper publication thereof.
§2 . Any railroadcorporation, streetrailway company ,
steamboat company ,or other association, copartnership,
or person,thatshalltransportany explosivecompound
contrary to the rules prescribedas aforesaidby said
board , afterthey shallhave been published as herein re-
quired, or who shall send or receive such explosive com-
pound not marked as aforesaid,or who shall send such
explosive compound not packed as required by said rules,
or any conductor of a trainof cars on any railroad,or
captain of a steamboat ,or person in charge of any public
vehicleengaged in transportingpassengers or freight,who
shallfailto observesaidrulesin transportingan explo-
sive compound , afterthe same shallhave been published
as aforesaid,shall be fined not less than fiftynor more
than fivehundred dollars,or imprisonedinthecounty jail
not lessthan ten nor more than one hundred days,or
both, within the discretionof a jury .
§ 3. This actshallbe in effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884.

river; number one to be builtat any point on said Licking

ration line of said city of Covington and the Three -mile

mile Riffleand a point not more than one mile above or
southof theSix-mile Riffle;and saidcompany ,forthepur-
pose of building and maintaining said bridge or bridges,

venience , and approach or approaches ,and also to obtain

ent with the laws of this State and the United States , as

1204 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 2. The capitalstock of said company shall be three
hundred thousand dollars, divided into three thousand
shares of one hundred dollars for each share .
§ 3. Any three(3) of the persons named in thisact,
aftergivingtendays'notice,by publicationin any paper
in Kenton or Campbell county, shallbe authorized to
open books of subscription, at such time and place as

manner ,or for any kind of property they may determine ;
and when two hundred shares of stock has been disposed
of,then the said incorporators acting, are hereby author-

ceed to electfrom theirnumber fivedirectors; whereupon

on the creditof the corporation,and to secure the same ;

ter,and at such rates of discount and interestas the said

dollars, and not to be payable at a longer date than
twenty -five years from the date of issuing, and not to

bear more than seven per cent, interest, payable semi-

§5 . That said corporation shall have the right to fix

Kenton county,through itscounty court,shallhave au-

CHAPTER 1429 .
AN ACT to authorize the trustees of the Pikeville common school district
to execute bonds and sellthe same forthe purpose of buildinga school-
house in said schooldistrictin Pikeville, Pike county.

of Kentucky :

the county of Pike , shall have the right to issue bonds
in the sum of from fiftyto one hundred dollars, so that
the whole amount for which said bonds are issued shall
not exceed the sum of five thousand dollars. The bonds
shallbe made payable to bearer,and bear interestat

They shallbe signed by the chairman of the board of
trustees,and made payable in fiveyears,redeemable at

the chairman of said board ,upon the order of said board ,

of said bond shallbe used by said board in the building
and furnishingof a school-house for said district.

bond , and theirallowed redemption, the said board of
trustees of said districtto be ,in Pike county ,may levy

of the propertysubjectto taxationin said district, and

tion of levying same shallbe submitted to the voters of

Approved May 12 , 1884 .

1212 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1430 . :
AN ACT to establisha road law forGrayson and Breckinridgecounties.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1.That itshallbe the duty of the Grayson and Breck-
inridge county courts,at the next regular meeting of said
courts,to elect a general supervisor of public roads in
said counties,who shalltake an oath to faithfullyper-
form the duties of his officeas hereinafter prescribed;
and filehis bond ,with good and sufficientsecurity, and
payable to the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,in the sum
ofone thousand dollars,to be approved by and filedwith
the judge of the Grayson and Breckinridge county courts.
§2 . The general supervisor shall be responsibleon his
bond for any fine or finesthat he may be adjudged to
pay for failingto keep the public roads in said counties
in good repair.
§3 . It shall be the duty of the general supervisor of
public roads in said counties, immediately after he is
qualified, to lay off allpublic roads in said counties into
suitable and convenient districts, and appoint district
supervisors of same , and apportion the road hands to
the several road districtsin proportion to the amount
of work to be done on the road ; and thereafter visit
allroads in his county as often as practicable,and cause
the districtsupervisors to keep their sections or districts
of road in good repair; and he may , after six months
from the appointment of a districtsupervisor, and the
apportionment to him of his road hands ,prosecutehim
by summons or warrant or indictment in any court in
the county having jurisdictionof the same ; and if the
districtsupervisor shall be found guilty of neglecting or
failingto put and keep his road in good order,he shall
be finedtwenty-fivedollarsfor each month or part of
a month he allows his road to remain out of good order .
4. District supervisors of roads, as mentioned in
thisact,shallhave the power to compel the road hands
apportionedto them to appear and work when required
to do so,as isprovidedby sectiontwenty-fiveof chapter

LAWS OF KENTUCKY 1213
ninety- four of the General Statutes,and itshall be their
duty to see that the provisionsof said section is enforced .
All fines collected under this act shall be paid over to
the county judge, who shallreceiptfor the same , and
enter it in a book to be kept by him for that purpose ,
and held as a road fund ,from which drafts may be made
by the general supervisor,to pay forthe necessarytools
and powder for use on said roads; and if there is not
enough of said fund to buy said tools and powder ,then
they shallbe paid for out of the county levy, on order of
the general supervisor.
§5 . No allowance shallbe made by said county courts,
or any court of said counties,for hauling or plowing on
the public roads of said counties; that is,for hauling or
plowing done afterthisact shall take effect; but the dis-
trictsupervisorsshall,when hauling or plowing isneces-
sary,cause itto be done by the road hands , giving them
credit for two days ' work for each day they furnish a
team and wagon and driver,or plow ,team ,and driver.
§6 . It shallbe the duty of the general supervisorto
furnish the necessary toolsfor removing ,and powder and
fuse for blasting rock, to the districtsupervisors, when
they make demand on him for same ; and keep a book
in which he shallkeep a record of alltoolsso furnished;
and shall cause said districtsupervisors to account for
and hand over same to their successors,or pay for them .
When the said general supervisor shall have furnished
powder ,fuse, and tools to districtsupervisors as herein
provided, he shalllay before the county court of his
county a billof same ,and the said court shallallow the
account and order itpaid by the sheriffon demand .
§ 7. The general supervisorshall be allowed and paid
out of the county levy fivehundred dollarsforeach year's
service,payable as follows: two hundred and fiftydollars
at the end of the firstsix months ' service,and two hun-
dred and fiftydollarsat the end of the year's service; and
the county judge shall draw his warrant on the sheriff
in favor of the said general supervisor for the sums and
at the times herein provided; and the sheriffshallpay

1214 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
same on demand ,and saidwarrant shallbe good to the
sherifffor said sum in his settlement with the county
court .
§ 8. In case a vacancy should occur in the office of
generalsupervisor,thecounty judge shallappoint a gen-
eral supervisorto qualifyas providedin firstsectionof
thisact,and serveuntilhissuccessoriselectedand quali-
fied .
§ 9. The electionof the generalsupervisorprovidedfor
in thisact shallbe by a majorityof the magistratespres-
ent and voting , unless there should be a tie; then the
county judge shallgive the casting vote.
§ 10. All law now in force in said counties relative to
public roads, and not in conflictwith this act,shall re-
main in force .
§ 11. Ifthe general supervisorof publicroads,as pro-
vided in thisact,shallfailor neglectto put the public
roads in his county in good repair, or cause them to be
put in good repairand kept so,shallbe liableto indict-
ment by the grand jury of his county forfailingto per-
form the duties of his office, and on conviction in the
circuitcourt forsaid county,be finednot lessthan fifty
dollars nor more than one hundred dollars for each of-
fense, in the discretionof the court or jury trying the
case; and any districtof road,or a part of any districtof
road ,being out of repair in his county , shallconstitutea
separate offense: Provided,however, That ifthe general
supervisor of public roads can show to the satisfactionof
thejurytryingthe case thathe has used allthe power at
his command to put the road in good repairand keep it
so,then he shallbe acquitted. The finesimposed by this
sectionshallbe collectedoff of the defendant ,if he owns
the amount of propertyin his county subjectto execu-
tion; ifnot,then offof the suretieson hisbond ,as pro-
vided in first section of this act .
§ 12. Any citizenof saidcounties,of sober and steady
habits,and who is a qualifiedvoterin generalelections,
shallbe eligibleto the officeof general supervisorof
public roads in said counties,and shallhold his officefor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1215
the period of two years from the date of his election,or
untilhis successorisduly electedand qualified; histerm
of officeshallbegin immediately upon hiselection.
§ 13. It shallbe the duty of the judge of the Grayson
and Breckinridge circuitcourts to give thisact in charge
to the grand jury of said counties; and said grand jury
shall,if they think itnecessary,summon before them wit-
nesses to testifyas to the condition of the roads in said
county ; and if out of order, as provided in this act,they
shallfindan indictmentagainstthe generalsupervisorof
public roads in said counties for failing to put his roads
in good order and keep them so .
§ 14. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 12,1884.
CHAPTER 1431 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof common schooldistrictnumber forty-three,in
Graves county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That all territoryembraced within the limits of
common school districtnumber forty-three, in Graves
county,and the white personslivingin said district,be,
and they are hereby , created and incorporated as school
districtnumber forty-three, Graves county , which shall
be under the control of the trustees of said district elect-
ed as now provided by law .
§2 . The trustees of said districtshall have power to
make allby -laws and rulesfor the government of them .
selves and their appointees, and for the management ,
control, and government of the school, and to enforce
such by-laws and rulesby suitablefinesand penalties,
to be recoveredin any courthaving jurisdictionof the
amount ,to be paid, when collected,to the trustees, and
be a part of the schoolfund forsaiddistrict.
§3 . The trusteesof said district,in addition to taking
the oath prescribed by the common school law , shallexe-
cute a bond to thecommon schoolcommissionerofGraves

4216 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
county ,conditioned for the faithfulperformance of their
duties as trustees, and to pay over to the proper person
in due time allmoney which shallcome into their hands
as trustees .
§4 . The said trustees shallcause to be kept a regular
and correctjournal of alltheirproceedings in a well
bound book ,which shallat alltimes be open to the in-
spection of any one of said trustees, or any citizen of
said school district .
§ 5. Said trusteesshallappoint allteachersand other
persons necessary to the successful carrying on of said
school, fix their salariesor compensations ,and for good
cause dismiss or suspend any teacher or other person
appointed by them ,and prescriberulesfor the govern-
ment of the school; and in no case whatever shall any
other than a graduate of some college or university of
good reputation be appointed as superintendent or prin-
cipal of said school; and in no case shalla teacher be
appointedto act in saidschoolwho has not had previous
experience in teaching,and who does not hold a first-
class firstgrade certificatefrom commissioner of common
schoolsof Graves county. The trusteesshallalso pre-
scribe the branches of education to be taught in said
school, which may , in addition to the common school
courseprescribedby theState,includethe usualcurricu-
Jum of studiestaught in the high schools and colleges of
this or other States, or such parts thereofas shallbe
deemed fitby the trusteesof said school.
§6. That allwhite childrenover sixand under twenty
yeas of age ,within said district,shallhave equal rights of
admission to the school of said district, and shall not be
charged anything for tuition; but said trusteeshave the
power to admit any white non -resident pupil into said
school upon such terms and conditions,and upon the
payment of such charges,as the trusteesmay deem suita-
ble ; and all sums received for such tuition shall consti-
tutea partof the schoolfund of saiddistrict.
§7 . The said trusteesshallhave the powers and rights,
in addition to the powers herein expresslygranted , which

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1217
pertain ,by the general common school law ,to trusteesof
common schools. They shall make all such enumera-
tions and census returns to the common school commis-
sioner of Graves county as are or may be required by
the general common school law ; and shallhave the right
to receiveand appropriateto the said schoolthe funds or
taxes due or to become due from the State and all other
sources to the common schools; and itshallbe the duty
of the common school commissioner of Graves county to
pay the same to them at such times as payments are
prescribed in the revised school laws of the Common-
wealth .
§ 8. The trustees shallhave power to regulate and de-
termine the lengths of terms and sessionsof said school
for each year ; and for what length of time such schools
shall be taught in each department of said school, and
the number of hours in the day : Provided,however, They
shall have taught a five months ' term free every year .
§ 9. No books , tracts, papers , or catechisms,or other
publications of a sectarian, denominational , or infidel
character , shall be used or distributed in said school , nor
shall any sectarian or infideldoctrine be taught therein.
§ 10. The said trustees, at the end of each scholastic
year,shallcause to be made out a report,in writing,
showing the amount of receiptsand expenditures for the
year ,and the amount of funds on hand , if any , and the
condition of the property belonging to the district, and
the value thereof; the number of pupilsin each depart-
ment of the school,the number of pay and free pupils
being separate in said report, and such other facts and
information as the cause of public education requires.
§ 11. In addition to the fiftycents now allowed by law
to be assessedagainsteach patronof the schoolby the
trusteesthereof, an additional fiftycents may be assessed
againstsaid patrons by saidtrustees,for the purpose of
furnishing fuel for said school and repairing the school-
house and other school property.
LOC . L.,VOL . II- 77

1218 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§12. In additiontothepowersand dutieshereingrant-
ed the trustees,the trusteesof said school shall have all
rights and powers that are now given by the common
school laws of this State .
§ 13. That this act shall take effectfrom and after its
passage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1432 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the Central Passenger Railroad Company ..
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth

Railroad Company to operate itscars of allkinds by the

CHAPTER 1433 .
AN ACT to transferto the Warren county court that portion of the Louis-
villeand Nashville Turnpike lying in Warren county,and to authorize
saidcounty to manage and repairthe same .
WHEREAS , That portion of the Louisvilleand Nashville
Turnpike lyingwithin the limitsof Warren county has
long since ceased to yield any revenue to the State,and
has been abandoned ,except a part thereof, which has
been repaired by lessees; and whereas ,the part of said
road not so repairedhas become so badly out of repair
as to be unfitfor travel; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That,subjectto the lease of that portion of the
Louisvilleand NashvilleTurnpike lyingin Warren coun-
ty,heretofore made by the State, the management and
controlof said road is hereby transferredand assigned
to the Warren county court.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1219
§2 . The Warren county courtishereby authorizedand
empowered to assume control and management of all,
or such portions of said road as said lessees may con-
sent to surrender ; and said lessees are authorized and
empowered to surrenderallor any portionsof saidroad
thatthey may desire.
§3 . If the Warren county court shallelectto take con-
trolof said road under thisact,itis hereby authorized
and empowered to repair said road ; and to that end it
may appropriate not exceeding four hundred dollars per
mile,out of any fundsitmay have on hand or may here-
afterhave on hand not otherwiseappropriated.
§ 4. Ifthe Warren county court so desires,afteritshall
have repaired as much as three consecutive miles of said
road,itmay erecta toll-gate,and collecttollsat any rate
to be fixed by it,not exceeding ,however , the rates fixed
by chapter one hundred and ten of the General Statutes ;
said tollsshall be kept in a separate account ,and shall
be for the use and benefitof the county untilthe full
amount has been repaid her which she expended in
repairing said road ; and thereafter the private stock-
holders in said road shall be entitled to receive their
share of the net earnings of said road , and the said
county the residue; but until as much as five miles of
road is repaired, the rate of toll collected shall be in
proportion as the length of road finished bears to five
miles ,
§5 . For the purpose of repairingsaid road,the War-
ren county court ishereby authorized and empowered to
contractforallmaterialsnecessary,and to employ such
contractors and laborers as may be necessary to do the
work ; and is empowered to purchase or lease not ex-
ceeding one acre of ground for each toll-house erected
on said road .
§ 6. If the saidWarren county court shallelectnot to
put said road in good repairas a turnpike,itmay have
said road ,or as much thereof as itgets possession of from
the saidlessees,worked and repairedas county roads are
worked under the general law of the State; but if itis

1220 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
so worked ,no tollsshallbe collectedby the county,as
provided in the foregoing sectionsof thisact.
§7 . Before tolls are collected on said road it shall be
examined by two justicesof thepeace forWarren county,
and approved by them ; and if at any time said road be-
comes out of repair,any person may apply to two jus-
tices of the peace to inspect same ; and if in such
inspectionsaid road is found to be out of repair, said
justices of the peace shall deliver to the judge of the
Warren county court a certificateto that effect; and ten
days after the service of such notice, no further tolls
shallbe collectedon saidroad untilthe same isrepaired;
and afteritshallhave been repairedand inspectedby
any two justicesof the peace on application of the coun-
ty judge of Warren county, and approved by them ,the
collectionof tollsmay be resumed as hereinprovided.
§ 8. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after itspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1434 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe sale of spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors, ale
or beer,within one and one fourthmiles of Moreland Station, in Lincoln
county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallnot be lawfulforany person or per-
sons to vend , sell,or loan any spirituous, vinous ,or malt
liquors,ale or beer,or the mixtures of either,within one
and one fourth miles of Moreland Station, in Lincoln
county,Kentucky ; thedistanceto be reckoned from the
depot as a central point.
§2 . Any person or persons violating the provisions of
the firstsection of this act shall be deemed guilty of a

rant of a justice of the peace ,police judge , or county

than fiftydollars.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1221
§3 . This act to take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 12, 1884.
CHAPTER 1435 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act incorporatingthe South Lou-
isvilleand Pleasure Ridge Turnpike Road Company , of Jeffersoncounty ,"
approved February the fourteenth, one thousand eight hundred and sev-
enty-eight.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the name of South Louisville and Pleasure
Ridge Turnpike Road Company , of Jefferson county ,be ,
and the same is hereby , changed to Southwestern Turn-
pike Road Company ,of Jeffersoncounty ; and the said
company shallbe known by that name only,and occupy
in everyrespectthe same statusas itwould under the
former name .
§2 . That an act, entitled"An act incorporating the
South Louisvilleand Pleasure Ridge Turnpike Road Com-
pany ,of Jeffersoncounty," approved February the four-
teenth, one thousand eight hundred and seventy-eight,
but now known as the Southwestern Turnpike Road
Company , of Jeffersoncounty, as amended by an act
approved April the twenty-fourth, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty,be,and the same is hereby,further
amended ; that the board of management of the said turn-
pike road company , as created by the said act approved
February the fourteenth, one thousand eight hundred
and seventy-eight,shallhave the power ,at theirdiscre-
tion, to change the northern terminus of theirroad , as at
present designated , and locate it at or near the point
where Fourth street of the city of Louisville intersects
the southern boundary line of said city,and that the said
turnpike road shallthence run in a southeasterlydirec-
tion to Third street,extended through South Louisville;
thence with or near said Third street southwardly , and
then southwestwardly ,so as to leave intact the Jockey
Club grounds ,to the southern terminus of the said turn

1222 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
pike road as now established; and shallhave the right
to cross and re-cross other turnpike roads leading from
the city without paying tollor tax to such road or roads
thus crossed,nor shallsuch road or roads charge travel-
ers for thus crossing them ; but the said board of man-
agement shallpay forone half the cost of buildingand
maintaining so much of said road or roads as is used by
said Southwestern Turnpike Road Company : Provided,
That in case the said board of management should select
and build upon the route above described,the toll-gate
nearest to the city of Louisville shall not be nearer to
the city limits than the line running east and west
through the southernmost corner of the Harris tract in
the eastern line of the Mooney tract.
§3 . Said company shall have the right to build and
maintain branches of itssaid turnpike road ,and acquire
the land therefor in the manner as authorized for the
main road .
§ 4. All acts and parts of acts passed and approved
beforethe passage of thisact, in conflictherewith,are
hereby repealed.
§ 5. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1436 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to incorporatethe Covington
Mutual Life Insurance Association ."
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth

sembly ,be ,and the same ishereby ,amended so that the

ciation shallhave power to establisha class of member-
ship for insurance upon the assessment plan against loss

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1223
adopt such a plan or system of membership dues and

may be deemed the bestforeffectuatingthe purpose of this
amending act, and to generally make such regulations
and rulesas may be found necessaryor convenientforthe
establishment and continuation of the business named in

CHAPTER 1437 .
AN ACT to prevent stock from running at large in the Chatham and
Brooksville magisterialdistricts, of Bracken county.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. Any person being the owner of stock,or having
the custody or control thereof, who shallintentionallyor
negligentlysufferor permit the same to run at largeor to
be outside of his or her own inclosure,or premises under
his or her charge ,shall, for each offense,be fined in any
amount not less than one nor more than twenty dollars,
to be recovered by warrant in the name of the Common-
wealth , before any justice of the peace within the dis-
trictin which said stock are sufferedor permitted to go at
large .
§2 . Any person whose premises or property may be
injured by stock running at large with the knowledge or
permission , or by the negligence or carelessness of the
owner or custodian, shall,notwithstanding the premises
so injured may not be inclosed by a lawful fence as now
required by law ,have a right of action against any owner
or custodian of such stock in any magistrate's court in
the districtin which the injury may have been done , for
any damages he may have sustained; and may alsohave
execution issued upon said judgment as now prescribed
by law .
§3 . Should any stockbe sold as providedin the pre-
ceding section,the amount realizedthereforshallfirstgo

1224 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to the satisfactionof the execution , including costs, and
the remainder be paid over to the owner of such stock.
§4 . Whenever stock of any kind shallbe found run-
ning at largeon any of the publicroads of saiddistricts,
or on any of the streetsor alleysof any town therein, or
on the premises of another without his consent,it shall
be the duty of the sheriffor any of his deputies residing
in the precinct in which said stock may be found at large
or trespassing,and of the constable thereof,or the mar-
shal of any town therein, to seize upon and impound
such stock, and provide for theircare and keeping until
the owner or custodian of such stock shall have redeemed
them by the payment of allfees,costs,and proper charges .
incidentto such procedure ,or untilhe shallhave disposed
of them as hereinafterprovided.
§5 . The officershall be entitled to the following fees
fortaking up stock running at large or trespassing: For
each horse,jack , mule ,or colt,one dollar; for each cow ,
bull,steer,or calf, fiftycents; for each sheep ,goat , hog ,
orpig,twenty-fivecents,and allexpensesincurredby him
in impounding and taking care of the same : Provided,
The owner is not known to be in the immediate pursuit
of same .
§6 . The officermaking the seizure shall,if practicable,
within two days thereafter,give notice to the owner of
thestockhe has so taken up,togetherwith the costsand
charges due ; and if the same is not reclaimed by the
owner within five days from said notice, the officer
having the custody thereof shall advertise the same , at
two or more publicplacesin hisdistrict,forsale,by post-
ing notices; and unlessthe stockshallbe reclaimed by
the owner or custodian within fivedays afterthe posting
of said advertisement,itshallbe the duty of such officer
to expose said stock to sale at public auction,at some
public place in said districtmost convenient to the owner .
Said sale shall be made on a credit of three months . The
officermaking the saleshalltake bond , with sufficient
surety, for the purchase -money , payable to himself, in
hisofficialcapacity,which shallbe returnedto and filed

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1225 .
with one of the magistrates in the districtin which said
salewas made ,and upon which such other proceedings
may be had to enforce the collectionthereof as are now
had to enforce the payment of other salebonds taken by
officersfor proceeds of personal property sold under exe-
cution. The proceeds of said sale bond , when collected,
shallbe firstapplied to the satisfactionof all costs and
charges incident to such seizure, keeping , and sale of
said stock. Any balance remaining thereafter shall be
delivered to the owner of such stock,if in the district,
if not,to the custodian of such stock.
§7 . Should any of the officersherein named neglect,
fail,or refuseto perform any of the dutiesimposed upon .
him by thisact,on conviction thereof before a justiceof
the peace in said district,or any police judge thereof, or
the presiding judge of the county court, shall be fined
in any sum not less than one dollar nor more than ten
dollarsfor each act of negligence or failureor refusal.
§ 8. The officersherein named , and theirsureties, shall
be responsible on theirseveral officialbonds for allvio-
lationsof duty imposed upon them by thisact.
§ 9. It shall be the duty of the judge of the Bracken
county court to give public notice of this act by having
the same insertedin one or more of the newspapers pub-
lished in Bracken county , at his option ; the same to be
allowed and paid for out of the county levy.
10. This act to apply to Chatham and Brooksvillemag-
isterialdistricts,in Bracken county .
§ 11. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afterits ratificationby a vote of the qualified elec.
tors of said districts; and the question of its enforce-
ment shallbe submitted to a vote at the August election,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four; and the
vote for and against the same shallbe counted ,canvass-
ed , and certifiedby the county board of canvassers ; and
if a majority of those voting shall be in favor of it,the
same shall be enforced as above stated .
Approved May 12, 1884.

1226 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1438 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Maysville Training and Breeding Association.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Perry Jefferson, George T. Simonds , S. S. Ri-
ley , W. H. Yancy , Horace January , J. D. Kehoe , and
J. H. Wilson , and their associates , successors , and as-
signs, are hereby created a body-corporateand politic,
with perpetual succession,under the name of the Mays-
villeTraining and Breeding Association; and by that
name itmay contract and be contracted with ,sue and be
sued ,plead and be impleaded , in all courts of law or
equity; may acquire by purchase , lease, or otherwise ,
Jand ,or a right to the use of land,in Mason county , for
the purpose of the association; may improve it; may sell
and convey the same , or any part thereof, at pleasure,
and acquire and improve other in itsstead,provided that
quantity of the land held by the associationshallnot ex-
ceed two hundred acres; may acquire,hold,and dispose
of such personal estate as it may deem necessary and
proper for promoting the improvement in the various
breeds of cattle, horses, hogs , and other live stock, by
holding exhibitions, awarding premiums in the various
classes for excellence, and by such other means as it
may deem proper; itmay adopt and use a common seal,
or use such a seal as itmay from time to time adopt .
§2 . The capital stock shall not exceed one hundred
and fiftythousand dollars,divided into shares of not less
than twenty -fivedollars,payable in such installments as
the board of directorsmay directand provide. Payment
of allsubscriptionsmay be enforced by suit in the name
of the society, or the shares upon which any payment
shall be in arrear, after due call made therefor, may be
forfeitedby suit,in any court of competent jurisdiction,
at the option and by the order of the board of directors,
in which cases payments thereformade upon such for-
feitedsharesshallremain the propertyofthe association.
§3 . The board of directorsshall provide for issuing to
stockholders certificatesof their stock when paid up .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1227
The stock shallbe held to be personal estate,and shallbe
transferableonly on the books of the association, in ac-
cordance with the by -laws upon thissubject to be adopted
by the board of directors; and the board of directorsmay
make all necessary and proper by -laws in reference to
the transfer and surrender of stock that it may deem
necessary. The stockholders shall not be responsible
personally for any debts or liabilitiesof the society.
§ 4. The firstmeeting of the society shallbe held in or
near the cityof Maysville, at such time as the person
named in the firstsectionshereof, or a majority of
them , shall determine , and give due notice of, by ad-
vertisementin one or more of the public newspapers
printed in said city; at which meeting there shall be
elected, afterthe acceptance of thisact of incorporation,
a board of directors, consisting of a president, a vice
president, a treasurer, a secretary, and five directors.
The regular annual election shall be held in the city of
Maysville,on the firstMonday in March next, and in
each year thereafter,or upon some other day in each
year thereafter that the board of directors may fix and
direct. At any regular annual meeting it may change
the number of directors. If no election be made at the
appropriate time ,the board of directorsmay call a meet-
ing of the societyfor that purpose .
§5 . The president and directorsshallhold theiroffices
until the next regular meeting of the society, and until
their respective successors are elected. Each holder of
stock shall be entitled to one vote in the election of offi-
cers and directors, and in all meetings of stockholders,
upon , all questions that may be proposed to be voted
upon , for each and every share of stock he may hold.
Called meetings may be held at any time by order of the
board of directors, upon notice published two weeks , for
specifiedobjects. At regular meetings , or meetings held
by adjournment over from regularmeetings,the power of
the society over it shallbe absolute, except as controlled
by thischarter and the law of the land .
§6 . At called meetings nothing shall be done ex-
cept in relation to matters specified in the call. The

1228 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
orassociation may lay out upon its grounds a track
tracks , course or courses , for running or trotting
races, and may conduct such races, and award such
stakes, premiums ,or purses as itmay determine; and
itmay sellor permit to be soldon itsgrounds pools on
any and all races that may be run or trotted upon its
course or grounds ,and shall be responsiblefor the pay-
ment of the same , when presentedduring the races;
and on all race days this association shall have the
same police rights that are now allowed by law to the
Maysville Agriculturaland Mechanical Association. The
prudential,fiscal, and other concerns of the society, to-
gether with allitsestate or interestsof every kind , shall
be under the control of the board of directors aforesaid ;
and they shallhave power to appoint such officersas they
may deem necessary in carrying out the purposes of the
society; to take bond ,with security,from any of them , as
well as of the treasurer, for the faithfulperformance of
their duties; to fillvacancies in any of these officers, and
in the board of directors; to regulate,at theirdiscretion,
as they may think proper, for admissionto the grounds
and participationsin its fairs and races ; and to make ,
alter,and modify such rules, regulations, and by -laws ,
and do all such acts not inconsistent with the Constitution
and laws of this State or of the United States ,as ,in their
opinion,are requisitefor the accomplishment of the ob-
jects of the association, and for the good order and
proper management of itsexhibitions.
§ 7. Three directors,with the president or vice presi-
dent , shall be a quorum for the transaction of business.
Said association may issue the bonds of the corpora-
tion hereby created to any amount ,not in all exceeding
fivethousand dollars,and not at any time to an amount
exceeding one half the value of its real estate, in sums
of one hundred or five hundred dollars, made paya-
ble in not more than twenty years from date of issue,
bearing interestof not more than six per cent. per an-
num , payable semi-annually, at such place as may be
designated ; and for said interest coupons may also be
issued, attached to said bonds ,to be signed by the presi

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1229
dent of said association, and countersigned by the secre-
tary thereof, and the coupons by the secretary; and to
secure the payment of said bonds and interest, said as-
sociation shall have full power to execute a mortgage
upon itsrealestate. Said associationmay open and keep
an officefor the conduct of its business in the city of
Maysville .
§ 8. This act shallbe in forcefrom itspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884.
CHAPTER 1439 .
AN ACT to incorporatethetown of Sulphur,inHenry county.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the town of Sulphur , in Henry county , be
incorporated, with the following boundary : Beginning
at what isknown as the Hayes house,includingit; and
running thence a westerlydirectionby the Strothermill,
includingit,to the Trimble county line; thence a south-
west course with said line to a house on G. W. Abbott's
farm , including it; thence a southeast direction to Wil-
liam Abbott's house, includingit, and F. Spillman's;
thence an easterlydirectionto Wm . Sparks, including
said Sparks ; thence north to the beginning .
§2 . That on the firstSaturday in June,one thousand
eighthundred and eighty-four,and everytwo yearsthere-
after,there shallbe electedby the qualifiedvotersresid-
ing in,or owning real estatewithin thecorporatelimits
of saidtown ,fivetrustees,a policejudge,and town mar-
shal for said town ,for the term of two years,whose term
of officeshallcommence on the Monday aftertheirelec-
tion,and continuefortwo years,and untiltheirsuccess-
orsare duly electedand qualified.
§3 . Said policejudge shallhave the same jurisdiction,
concurrent, criminal, legal, penal, and equitable, with
justicesof the peace of said county within the limitsof
saidtown ,except in examining courtshe shallhave the
same jurisdictionas two justicesof the peace,and shall

1230 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
have the same fees,rights,duties, and be under the same
liabilities, as any justice of the peace in said county .
He shallhave exclusive jurisdictionin allcases arising
under , or violationsof, the by -laws and ordinances of
said town .
§4 . Said trustees may enact ordinances and all need-
fulby -laws and regulations for the government of said
town , and fixfinesfor theirviolation,not exceeding five
dollars for each violation of said by laws . They shall
have power to lay and collecta tax on each tithenot ex-
ceeding one dollar,and on real and personal estatein said
town not exceeding twenty -fivecents on each one hundred
dollarsof taxable property in said town in any one year ,
for the purpose of improving the streets,cross-ways , and
sidewalksin said town . They shallhave power to ap-
point an assessor to assess the property of said town ,
and fix his compensation therefor, not,however , exceed-
ing the amount now allowed by law to assessorsforState
revenue . The town marshal shall collect all taxes levied
by saidtrusteesunder thisact,and pay the same over as
directedby them ,for which he shallbe liableon his bond .
The trusteesshallfixhis pay forsame ,not exceeding ten
per cent,on the amount collectedand paid over.
§5 . The marshal of said town shall give bond , with
sufficientsecurity, to be approved by said trustees,for
the faithfuldischarge of all his duties,and that he will
pay over alltaxesand othermoneys which may come to
his hands by virtue of his officeto the person entitled
thereto; said marshal shallhave the same powers ,duties,
fees, and liabilitieswithin the limits of said town and
county as any constable of said county .
§ 6. The trusteesof said town shall have power to fill
vacancies in theirnumber by appointment until the next
election for said town officers; and they shall cause an
electionat any time ,afterten days'previous notice posted
at fiveconspicuousplacesin said town ,to fillvacancies
in the officeof police judge or town marshal .
§7 . Any person failingto perform any duty enjoined
by thisact may be finedin any sum ,not exceeding twen

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1231
ty-fivedollars,to be recovered as other fines before said
police judge or any magistrate of said county .
§ 8. It shallbe the duty of said trusteesto open and
keep the streetsof said town in repair,whenever neces-
sary ; and in so far as the money obtained by taxation,
fines, and other ways ,collectedin pursuance of judg-
ments of said police judge for violations of by -laws or
ordinances of saidtown ,allsuch sums shallbe held under
the control of the trustees,and shall be used for the afore-
said purposes,and for other improvements that may in
time be necessary .
§ 9. All persons entitledto vote forRepresentative shall
be qualifiedto vote,if living or owning real estateof the
value of one hundred dollars or over , in said town .
§ 10. Said trusteesmay electone of theirnumber chair-
man of the board ,and a secretaryand treasurer,and may
require bond of the treasurer. They shall hold regular
monthly meetings , and oftener, if necessary ; and shall
fix the time and place of their meetings, and keep a
record of theiracts in a book kept for that purpose .
§ 11. The election provided for in this act,except the
first, shallbe held by the chairman and secretary, or they
may appoint any two citizensof said town to hold the
same , who shall,before acting,be duly sworn faithfully
to dischargetheirduties; and the firstelectionprovided
for shall be held by the two justices of the peace in the
districtin which said town is located, or any persons
appointed by them . They shallgive ten days' noticeof
the time ,place,and purpose of same ,by written notices
posted at fivepublic places in said town .
§ 12.The policejudge shallkeep a record,and have
the same rightsand powers , and be governed by the
same laws in allother respects,not provided forin this
act,as justicesof the peace.
§ 13. This act shall take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1440 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Stevenson Campground Association,in Grant
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 .That Edward O. Stevenson ,G. W. Young ,and such
persons as may associatewith them ,and theirsuccessors,
be , and are hereby ,created a body -corporate, under the
name and style of Stevenson Camp -ground Association,
in the county of Grant , near the town of Corinth , on the
Cincinnati,New Orleans , and Texas PacificRailway ; to
have perpetual succession, with rightto obtain,hold, sell
and convey ,and dispose of,every class of property nec-
essary for the uses of theirassociation, not to exceed in
value thirtythousand dollars; and may contract and be
contracted with, sue and be sued ,plead and be implead-
ed ,in allthe courts in the Commonwealth ,and transact
allbusiness in itscorporate purposes ; and may make by-
laws for itsgovernment ,not inconsistentwith the laws of
the land .
§2 . Said association shall have power to select and
appoint a police force of such numbers as it may deem
proper,who shallhave allthe powers and authorityin
preserving order,preventing the commission of offenses
of every character,and arrestingand disposing of offend-
ers,for the time they may be so appointed ,that sheriffs,
constables,town and city marshals , or other like peace
officershave ; and theirjurisdictionin making arrestsof
persons guiltyof any offensesdenounced by thisact,or
any offensesagainstthe laws of thisCommonwealth ,or
to prevent the commission of any offenses, shallbe co-
extensive with the counties of Grant ,Owen ,and Scott.
§3 . It shallbe the duty of said associationto post at
leastten printed copies of theirrules and regulations at
the most public places on said camp -grounds at the be-
ginning of each protracted meeting .
§ 4. The object and purpose of this association is to
hold religiousworship .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1233
§ 5. This act shall(take) effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1441 .
AN ACT to authorize the county court of Boyle county to subscribeto
the capitalstock of turnpike roads in saidcounty .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Boyle county ,the presid-
ing judge and a majority of the justices of the peace in
said county concurring therein,be ,and it is hereby , au-
thorizedto subscribeto the capitalstockof any turnpike
road company in said county heretoforeor hereafter
incorporated, in any sum not exceeding one thousand
dollarsper mile; and saidcourtmay pay saidsubscrip-
tions by issuing the bonds of said county , in denomina-
tions of one hundred dollars and five hundred dollars,
with coupons thereto attached for the interest thereon ,
payable semi -annually; said bonds to be due and paya-
ble ten years after date ,and to bear interestat a rate
not exceeding sixper centum per annum ; and saidcourt
may provide for the payment of the interest on said
bonds,and the principalat maturity,by levying an an-
nual tax on the property of said county taxable for State
revenue of not exceedingfivecents upon the one hun-
dred dollars of taxable value .
§2 . This act shallnot take effectunless a majority of
the qualifiedvoters of said county , voting at a regular
electionheld at the severalvotingplacesin saidcounty,
shall vote in favor of making said subscription; and
before any electionheld for saidpurpose,it shallbe the
duty of the judge of the county court of said county to
publish notice thereof for four weeks in some newspa-
per publishedinDanville,Kentucky,by weekly insertions
therein .
§3. The sheriffof said county shallcollectthe taxes
herein provided for as said county court may levy and
LOC . L., VOL . II- 78

1234 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
order , and he and his sureties shall be liable therefor as
for State revenue .
§ 4. This act to be in forcefrom itspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884.
CHAPTER 1443 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Richard EliasGeorge , of Ballard county .
Be itenactedbytheGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Richard Elias George,an infant,in Ballard
county,be,and he is hereby,relievedof the disabilityof
infancy; and allthe powers and privilegesof a person
of the age of twenty-one years of age are hereby con-
ferred upon him .
§2 . Nothing in thisact shallbe so construed as to con-
fer upon said George the rightof suffrage.
§3 . This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12 ,1884 .

CHAPTER 1446 .
AN CT to amend an act, entitled" An act to iscorporatethe Citizens' Gas
and ElectricLight Company , of Owensboro ,Kentucky ."

of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled" An act to incorporate the
Citizens' Gas and Electric Light Company , of Owen-
boro,Kentucky ," approved March the twenty-fourth,one
thousand eight hundred and eighty two , be , and the
same is amended as follows: That the privilegeishere-
by granted to said company , and its successors and as-
signs,forthe duration of said company ,to use the streets,
alleys, and other public places within the present and
future limitsof said city,for the purpose of laying down ,

up , and repairing wires, and other apparatus and ma-
