CHAPTER 1447 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe " Pioneer Building and Loan Association," of
Paducah .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That J. W. Bernheim,E. Palmer ,J. W. McKnight ,
E. F. Bymg , W. W. Powell , George Rock , Charles
Daniels ,and Henry Burnett ,of Paducah ,Kentucky , and
such other persons as have become associated with them
by subscribingto the capitalstock of said company ,
be ,and they are hereby ,created a body- corporate,under
the name and styleof the " Pioneer Building and Loan
Association," of Paducah , Kentucky .
§ 2. Said associationshallhave power to have perpet-
ual succession; to sue and be sued by itscorporate name
aforesaid; to have a common seal,and to alterthe same
at pleasure ; to render the shares or interests of stock-
holders transferable, and to prescribethe mode of mak-
ing such transfers; to exempt the private property of
members or shareholdersfrom liabilityfor corporate
debts; to establishby -laws , and to make all rules and
regulations deemed expedient for the management of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 127
their affairs, not inconsistent with the Constitution or
laws of this State or of the United States .
§3 . The objectof thisassociationshallbe the accu- "
mulation of a fund to be loaned to its members to facili-
tate their acquiring homes ,and to affordan opportunity
for the safe investment of monthly savings by the pur-
chase or improvement of realestatein McCracken coun-
ty,Kentucky , or for the release of mortgage or trusts
on real estate, or for the redemption thereof, and for no
other purpose whatever. The amount of capitalstock
authorized by these articlesshallbe one millionof dollars,
in shares of two hundred dollars each , and no business
shall be transacted until at leasttwo hundred and fifty
shares of stock shall have been subscribed for .
§4 . The said stock,when subscribed for,shallbe paya-
ble as provided forherein and in the by-laws hereafter to
be adopted .
§5 . This corporationshallbegin businesson the twen-
ty-second day of October, one thousand eighthundred
and eighty-three,and shallcontinue for a period of twen-
ty-fiveyears,unlesssoonerdissolvedby a two -thirdsvote
of all the stockholders .
§ 6. All other questions shallbe decided by a majority
of the stockholderspresent at a meeting ,regardlessof the
number of shares held by each .
§7 . The affairsof the corporation shallbe conducted
by a president,vicepresident,secretary,and treasurer,an
attorney,and a board of fivedirectors. J. W. McKnight ,
E. Palmer ,George Rock , W. W. Powell , and Chas .Dan-
iels shall constitute the board of directors tillthe second
Thursday in January, one thousand eighthundred and
eighty -five,at which time an election by ballot shallbe
held for their successors,who shall hold their officefor
twelve months , or until their successors are elected and
qualified; and an electionshallbe held on each recurring
the second Thursday in January by the stockholders.
§ 8. The board of directors shall, from their number ,
elect the president and vice president,but the secretary
and attorney and treasury shallbe elected by the board
from among the stockholders.

1238 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
9. The board of directors shall be present at each
regular meeting of the association, and shallsuperintend
the collectionof dues ,interest,premiums ,and fines,and
transact allbusiness necessary to itswelfare. They shall
take specialcare to loan or deposit the funds of the asso-
ciationinthe safestand most profitablemanner . They
shall propose such by laws as may be necessary for the
welfare of the association. They alone shallbe the judges
of the sufficiencyof the securityto be given by the offi.
eers and agents of the association, and shallrequire such
securityfrom the president,vice president,secretary,at-
torney,and treasurer as may be deemed necessary.
§ 10. The president shallpreside at allmeetings of the
association. He shall sign all certificatesof stock,and
all drafts on the treasurer approved by the board . He
shall see that all requirements by the board from pur-
chasersand borrowers are fullycomplied with,and for
each separate incumbrance upon the securityofferedthat
issettledor paid under his supervision he shallreceive a
fee of one dollar,to be paid by the mortgagor . He shall
appoint allcommittees not otherwiseprovidedfor. He
shallgive bond in the sum of one thousand dollars.
§11 The vicepresidentshallperform the dutiesof the
president when absent, and shallreceive like compensa-
tion. He shallgive a bond of fivehundred dollars.
§ 12. The treasurer shall receive all money from the
secretary, and pay all drafts ordered by the board which
are signed by the presidentand attestedby the secre-
tary. He shallkeep correctbooks of accounts, which
shallat alltimes be subject to the inspection of the di-
rectors,or of any member of the association, and shall,
whenever requestedby the presidentor board of direct-
ors,make a fullreportof the financialaffairsof the asso-
ciation. He shall give bond in the sum of two thou
sand dollars. He shalldeliverto hissuccessor allmoneys ,
books, papers, and property of everykind belonging to
the association within one week after the election of the
successorin office,and shallreceivesuch compensation
forhis servicesas the directorsmay allow .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1239
§ 13. The secretary shall attend all meetings of the
stockholders and directors, and , when required, of all
committees , and shall keep accurate account of such
meetings . He shallkeep an accurate account with each
stockholder,and attest allorders drawn on the treasurer
forthe payment of money . He shallattend to the col-
lectionof and receive alldues ,fines,or other money paid
to the association. He shall pay the same over to the
treasurer,takinghisreceipttherefor. He shallbe charged
with the keeping of allthe books,accounts,and records
of the association. He shall also have charge of all
papers or property of the association not otherwise pro-
vided,or as the board of directors may instruct. Itshall
be his duty to see and know that allcontracts,mortgages ,
deeds , or agreements made to or with this association
shallbe properlydrawn and recorded. He shallsee that
all houses mortgaged to this association are kept fully
insured in good and responsible companies ,approved by
the finance committee , and that the policiesfor such in-
surance are deposited in his office, and made payable to
this association ; that the taxes on all real estate so mort-
gaged are promptly paid,and the receiptdepositedwith
him . Upon thedefaultof any stockholderto renew his
insurance or pay his taxes,itshallbe the duty of the sec-
retary to draw from the treasurer the amount necessary
to renew such insurance or pay such taxes, and charge
same to the stockholder,to be paid with his dues before
the next monthly meeting , or collectedas provided for in
thecase of otherdues,fines,or penaltiesdue the associ-
ation . He shall make to the board of directors at each
meeting ,or as often as may be required, a fullreport, in
writing,of the financialcondition and general affairsof
the association; and at theirfirstmeeting in each quarter
shallpresent a fullreportof businessof the preceding
quarter,and at the closeof each year shallprepare,for
publication to the stockholders, a complete statement of
the entirebusiness,condition, and affairsof the associa-
tion. At the expiration of his term of officehe shall
deliverto hissuccessorallbooks,papers,and propertyof

1240 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the association that may be in his possession. He shall
execute a bond in the sum of two ($2,000) thousand dol-
lars; and for his services shall receive such compensa-
tion as shall be fixed by the board of directors. The
premiums herein providedforshallnot exceed the legal
rate of interest .
§ 14. The attorney shallbe electedby the board of di-
rectors,and shallhold his positionuntilremoved for good
cause , or his resignation is accepted . He shall be the
legal adviser and agent of the board , and shall receive
such compensation for his services as they may agree
upon . He shallexamine alltitlesof real estate offered
as securityforloans, and answer all questionsof law
propounded by the board. For the faithulperformance
of these duties,he shallgive bond in the sum of fivehun-
dred dollars. For examining titles,and making out the
necessary papers , and having the mortgage or deed re-
corded , and for performing such other duties as may be
requiredby the associationto secure the money pur-
chased by the stockholders,shallreceivesuch compensa-
tion for his services,to be paid by the mortgagor ,as the
board of directorsmay determine. He shallprepare all
deeds ,mortgages , and agreements ,and other writings of
a legalnature,and be paid as above stipulated.
Standing Committees.
§ 15. The presidentshallannually,at the firstregular
meeting of the board of directors, appoint from their
number a finance committee of three members , whose
duty itshallbe to act in conjunctionwith the president
to examine and audit all claims against the association,
and generallyto assisthim in the management of the
financial affairs. They shall report, in writing, at the
firstmonthly meeting of the corporateyear,givinga full
statement of the financial condition of the association,
and to this end the secretary shall assistthem . A ma-
jority of the committee shall be competent to act. It
shallbe the duty of the committee to see that loans are
appliedstrictlyto thepurposeforwhich theyare made ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1241
and in furtheranceof thisthey shallrequireborrowers to
exhibit evidence of this; and when loans are awarded
for the purpose of erecting buildings, they may cause
the contracts to be exhibited to them , and require that
the money be paid only as the work progresses,and then
upon the builder'sreceiptfor same ; and in all cases
where balances to buildersor mechanics are likelyto
become due , it shall be their duty to require that said
builders or mechanics shall enter into an agreement to
so far release their mechanics ' hens as to make it second-
ary to the mortgage held by thisassociation.
Valuation Committee ,
§ 16. The president and one director,to be appointed
by thepresident,and the attorney,shallconstitutea val-
uation committee ,whose duty itshallbe to examine into
and report the value of all securitiesoffered to the asso-
ciation, and shall report, in writing, to the board of
directorstheir conclusions. A majority of this commit-
tee shall be competent to act,and the members thus in
factacting shallreceive one dollar each for theirservices
in each case of valuation, to be paid by the mortgagor .
Loans and Securities .
§ 17. At the stated monthly meetings of the association,
the funds on hand ,if to the amount of one share or more ,
shall be offered for loan , and the stockholderwho shall
bid the highest premium for the preference shallbe enti-
tled to receive a loan of two hundred dollars for each
share of stockheldby him or her. Good and ample real
estatesecurity,which shallhave been accepted in the
manner provided,shallbe given by the borrower tosecure
the repayment of the loan. For every two hundred dol-
lars,or fractional part thereof, borrowed , including the
premium , in addition to the security above mentioned ,
the borrower shall transfer, as collateralsecurity,to the
association one share of its capital stock,which , at the
time of the transfer, must be freefrom any liabilityfor
any other previously contracted indebtedness to thisasso-
ciation,or for any other purpose whatever. Borrowing

1242 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
members , who shall failto provide such securityas may
be approved within the time specified, shall pay one
month's interest, together with any expenses incurred,
and the money shallbe re-loaned at the nextstated meet-
ing . In case of non payment of installments or interest
by borrowing stockholders, for a space of six months ,
payments of principaland interest,with accrued penal-
ties, without deducting the premiums paid or interest
thereon, may be enforced by proceeding on theirsecuri-
ties according to the terms of the contract under which
the loan was made . Interest on all loans shall be at the
rate of six per cent,per annum from the time of making
said loan , and shall be paid in monthly installments in
advance , and at the same time and in the same manner
that the monthly dues are paid . All real estate or im-
provements offered as security, as above set forth, must
be situated within the county of McCracken , State of
Kentucky ,and must be free from all incumbrances , and
worth at its cash valuation such an amount over and
above the loan asked on it as the valuation committee
and the directorsshalldeem a sufficientmargin to amply
securethisassociationfrom loss. All propertyliableto
loss by firewhich is pledged to this association must be
kept fullyinsured (or to the amount borrowed ) in some
good insurancecompany ,to be approved by the finance
committee ; and the policiesshall be made payable to
this association, and deposited with its secretary. The
annual premium receiptsfor the renewal of such poli-
cies,and the annual tax receipts of all kinds for which
the propertyis liable,must also be depositedwith the
secretary so long as the loan exists. The valuation com .
mittee shall have the right to require any borrower to
increase his security,if at any time they find the value of
same has depreciated below the margin required above ,
and in case of refusal or neglect of such borrower to do
so within twenty (20) days after notice,unless the bor-
rower shallrepay so much of his loan as willre-establish
the required margin ,the board of directorsshallhave the
right to proceed against such borrower and his securities

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1243
1
according to the laws of the contract. No member shall
be allowed to borrow more than the value of ten shares
at one bidding, nor shallany member be allowed to
transferhis bid nor any part of itto any other member .
Approved securityfor loans must be offered within ten
days afterbidding in the loan or penalties will attach, as
provided forherein. All feesforthe examination of title,
registrationof deeds and mortgages , or other expenses
incurred, shall be paid by the borrower . By a three
fourths' vote of all the directors, funds not wanted by
any of the stockholders may be loaned temporarily for
the benefit of the association on first-class real estate
security, at the highest legal rate of interest.
§ 18. This association shallhave the right to issue at
any time a new serial or serialsof stock for the forma-
tion of new classes ; and such new classes shall be formed
whenever the board of directorsshall so determine ,pro-
vided that not over one thousand shares shall be issued
in one serial. Said classesshallbe numbered , and shall,
in allrespects,conform to these articlesand by -laws , and
be subject to the officersand directorsof this associa-
tion. Each class shall pay its proportion of expenses
necessary to carry on this association.
§19 . The highest amount of indebtedness or liability
to which this corporation may subject itselfat any time
shall be five hundred dollars .
§20 The liabilityof any stockholder of this associa-
tion shall be limited to the amount of his or her unpaid
stock .
§21 . Whenever the assets of this association shall
reach the par value of allshares of stock in force in any
one series,then its affairsshall be placed in liquidation,
and each and every member shall receive the full par
value of allshares held by him or her, eitherin cash or
in their own securities; and all liens of record shall be
satisfiedand discharged: Provided,however,That no stock.
holder shall be entitled to have their securities and col
lateralreturned and liensof record against them satisfied
untilhe or she has fullypaid all dues , fines,interest, or
other charges due this association .

1244 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§22. Each serialor classof shares may be placed in
liquidation,and itsaffairsseparatelywound up as pro-
vided above .
§23 . After the liquidationof the association,or of any
serialof itsshares, in the manner prescribedabove,
should there be any surplusremaining,thisshallbe di-
vided pro rataamong the stockholders,due regard being
given to the number of shares held by each .
24. The shares of stock shallbe transferableupon
the books of the associationby the holder,in person or
by proxy , upon the surrender of his certificate; but in
no case shall stock be transferred while the owner or
holder thereof is in arrears to the association. The pur-
chaser shall sign the articlesof associationand by -laws .
§25 . Shares upon which loans have been made may be
transferred,subject to the rightsof the association,in the
following manner :
§26 . When the assignee isthe purchaser of the mort .
gaged premises ,the shares may be transferred to him or
her on assuming the obligations of the assignor,when
the assignee may become a member of the association.
§27 . The purchaser of the shares may substituteother
property,satisfactoryto the board of directors,as securi-
ty for money loaned,when the firstmortgage shallbe
discharged and canceled .
28. The mortgagor , by paying the amount of the
money loaned ,with all interest,dues and penalties,may
receive back his shares , so as to transfer them .
§ 29. On the arrival of any minor or minors holding
shares to the age of twenty -one years,the guardian or
trusteemay transferstock shares to him or her in his own
proper name ,and such minor shallthereuponbe entitled
to allthe privilegesof stockholders.
§30 . Any member wishing to withdraw from the asso-
ciation, and who shall give thirty days ' notice to the
board of directorsin writing, shall be entitledto receive
back the amount actually paid in on stock installments
only by him ,together with his proportion of the accrued
profitas determined by the board of directors, less ten
per cent, of said entireamount : Provided, That the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1245
!
stock is not pledged to the association:And provided
always, That at no time shallmore than one-thirdof each
month's receiptsbe appliedto the payment of such with-
drawal claims .
§31 . On each share there shallbe paid a monthly in-
stallment of one dollar in advance . Persons wishing to
take stock after the organization of the association must
pay up allinstallmentswhich may have become due ,to-
gether with such premium as the board of directorsmay
requiretomake thecash payment on thenew stockequiv-
alent to the cash value of the old stock .
§32 . Each and every stockholder or trusteewho shall
neglect or refuse to pay his monthly dues or interestas
often as they shallbecome due and payable ,shallbe fined
fivecents per share for each week or fractionalpart of a
week in which his monthly dues remain unpaid ; or any
member failingto pay intereston money borrowed shall
pay a fineof ten centseach week or fractionalpart of a
week , as above provided,for finesfor non -payment of
monthly dues .
§33 . Any member who becomes unable to pay ,on ac-
count of sickness or misfortune, any amounts , due the
association, may so notifythe board of directors, who ,
upon investigation,through a committee forthatpurpose
appointed, may ,in theirdiscretion,allow the defaulting
member time,not exceeding two months ,in which to pay
hisdues or interest; but he shallpay sixper cent.interest
on allback payments .
§34 . All claimsforfines,dues,interest,and penalties,
shall be a lien against the stock of delinquent members
or theirassignees,and whenever such claims shallamount
to a sum equal to the dues actuallypaid,the stock shall
be forfeitedto the association,and the delinquentmem-
ber's name stricken from the roll of stockholders ,
§35 . All fines,penalties,interest,& c.,shallbe payable
with thedues,as above provided,and no member shallbe
entitledto receivea loan ortovoteon any questionwhat-
ever who is in arrears for monthly installments,interest,
finesor penalties.

1246 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§36 . Every person becoming a member of this asso
ciationshallsubscribe to and agree to abide by the terms
of these articlesof incorporation and the by -laws of said
association, and shall pay a membership fee of not less
than twenty cents for each share of stock held.
§37 . All certificatesof stock shall be signed by the
presidentand attestedby the secretary,with the sealof
the associationattached, and must be duly numbered and
recorded .
§38 . This act shall be in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1247
gain, sell,convey, and dispose of at pleasure; and to

§2 . The corporation hereby created shallhave , and it

3. The capital stock of said company shall be four

§4 . Said company shallhave power and authority,

forthe purpose of sellingand distributingwater,and of

or any of them , to use the streets, alleys, ways , avenues ,

1248 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and publicroadsof such city,town or county,forthe pur-
poseoferectingreservoirs,buildings,stand-pipes,and for
the purpose of erectingposts,stretchingwires thereon,
layingdown ,takingup,and repairingallpipes,conduct-
ing mains ,service-pipes,conduits,hydrants ,posts,and all

ducting and distributingand supplying to consumers ,

by the by laws ,who shallbe stockholders, and who shall

section of this act shall constitute the firstboard of di-
rectorsof saidcompany ,and shallhold theirofficesuntil

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1249
theirsuccessors are duly elected under thischarter. The

"per publishedin Louisville,Kentucky ,shallbe given of

ing of the stockholders,to be calledas the by -laws may
provide. All vacancies in the board of directors occa-
sioned by death ,resignation,or inabilityto discharge the

§8 The said company shallkeep a record of theirpro-
ceedings, and shallat each annual meeting of the stock-
holders make a statement of the condition of the com .
P an may declare dividends of such portions of
the earnings, at such times as the board may deter-
mine upon , or as may be prescribedby the by -laws .
§ 9. Any person duly authorizedin writing by said

who may be actingas treasureror secretarythereof,may ,

ten authority,enter any dwelling , store, room , place or

paratus for supplying and regulatingthe quantity of

CHAPTER 1449 .
AN ACT to reduce the number of magisterialor justices'districtsinMeade-
county,and to provide forlaying offthe boundaries thereof.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the number of magisterialdistrictsin Meade
county ishereby reduced to six.
§2 . That Gus W. Richardson, Z. T. Herndon , Linn
Craycroft, William Vessells, and David W. Fairleigh,be ,
and they are hereby , appointed commissioners , whose
duty it shallbe to lay off and divide said county into six
magisterial districts. Said commissioners , before enter-
ing upon the discharge of the duties required f them by
this act ,shall take an oath before some officer authorized
to administer same that they will faithfullyand impar .
tially, and with due regard to the convenience of the
citizensof said county ,discharge the duties of said office.
In the event of the failureor refusal,from any cause , of
any one of the above named persons to qualify under
this act, then it shallbe the duty of the county judge to
appoint a person to act in the place of the persons so
failing; and in the event that two or more of said per-
sons above named fail or refuse to act, then it shall be
the duty of the judge of the aforesaid county court to
appoint,and such appointments shallbe by an order of
record. Such persons so appointed shall be sworn as
herein provided , and shall discharge the duties herein
prescribed. It shallbe the duty of said commissioners,
within sixmonths afterthe passage of thisact,todivide
the said county into six magisterialdistricts,and fix and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1251
establishthe boundaries thereof; and they shall make a
report,in writing,of theiractsto the clerkof the county
court of said county,who shallrecord the same in the
order book of said county court,and thereafterthe bound-
aries of said districts shall be as established and fixed in
said report.
§3 . That at the next regular election for justices of
the peace in said county there shallbe electedby the
qualifiedvoters in each of said districtstwo justices of
the peace for each of said districts, whose powers and
dutiesshallbe the same as now providedby law forjus-
ticesof the peace .
§ 4. That at the next regular election for constables in
said county there shallbe electedby the qualifiedvoters
in each of said districts one constable for each of said
districts, whose powers and duties shall be the same as
now providedby law forconstables.
§5 . Said commissioners shall receive for their services
such compensation as the courtof claims of saidcounty
may allow , and the same shallbe paid out of the county
levy .
§ 6. This act shallin nowise affector impair the right
to hold officeor the tenure of office,or limit the power
of the present incumbents of the officesof justice of the
peace and constables in said county .
§ 7. This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved May 8, 1884.

of Kentucky :

tained in the tollowing boundaries be , and is hereby ,

Beginning at the centerlineof the Bardstown Turnpike

the Slaughter tract; thence with said Slaughter's line

ning,embracing lands belonging to Clayton S. Longest ,

terlineofthe Bardstown Turnpike,cornercommon toTil-
den and Longest ; thence running with said center line
north fifty-seven and one half degrees east one thousand
fourhundred and forty-four feetto Jos .S. Longest's seven
and one half acre tract; thence with the southeastern line

west eight hundred and fifty-two feetto Longest's west-
ern line; thence with said western line south thirty-four
and three fourthsdegrees east one thousand one hundred
and eighty-three and eighty-seven one -hundredths feet to

twenty degrees east one thousand four hundred and two
feetback to the beginning , embracing the lands of Nay-
lor and Robert K. Moore .
§2 . That Clayton S. Longest , Henry Longest , and
Robert K. Moore are hereby appointed trustees of said

in April next, at which time , and annually thereafter,
there shallbe an election by the qualifiedvoters of said

ified .
§3 . That said trustees, afterbeing qualified,shallpro-
ceed to elect one of their number chairman , who shall
preside at theirmeetings ,and have power to convene the
board when , in his opinion , the interestsof the town
require it. It shallbe his duty to see that allordinances
and by -laws of the town are duly executed . The said
trusteesand theirsuccessorsin officeshallbe a body

1254 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
politic and corporate,and shall be known by the name
and style of the Trustees of the Town of Enterprise;
and by thatname shallbe capable in law of contracting
and being contractedwith,suing and being sued,plead-
ing and being impleaded ,answering and being answered ,
and of defending and being defended ,in allthe courts of
this State, and do all matters,acts,and things which a
body -politicand corporate,with perpetual succession,can
lawfully and rightfullydo .
§4 . The said trustees,or a majority of them ,shallhave
power to make or receive all necessary conveyances in
relationto said town ; they shallhave power over the
streetsand alleys and sidewalks in said town ,or which
they may hereafteropen ,which they hereby have author-
ityto do , and may direct and order the improvement of
the same as they may deem best for the interestof said
town ; they shallhave power to levy and collectan ad
valorem tax on the property of said town ,not exceeding
fiftycents on the one hundred dollars in any one year ,
and a poll tax not exceeding two dollars on each male
citizenof said town twenty -one years old or over; they
shall have the power to tax auction sales, shows , and
exhibitions for money or profit,such sums as they may
deem just and right, or as may be provided in their
by -laws ; they shall have the power to suppress all tip-
pling and gambling houses,and fineallthosewho may
violate any of their by -laws any sum not exceeding
twenty dollars, which may be recovered by warrant
before any justiceof the peace having jurisdiction;
they shallhave power to declare what are nuisances,
and to remove the same ; to pass all by -laws and ordi-
nances necessary to give effectto allthe powers herein
granted , and that the peace and good order of said town
may require.
§5.That itshallbe the duty of saidtrusteesto appoint
a clerkand treasurer,and such otherofficersas they may
deem necessary, and by ordinance specifyand define
theirduties,which they may enforce by fines; and shall
requiresaidofficersto take an oath to faithfullyperform

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1255
their duties as such . The clerk and treasurer of said
town shallkeep a recordof the proceedingswhich prop-
erlybelong to theirrespectiveoffices.
§ 6. All fines and forfeituresfor the violation of by-
laws and ordinances of said town shall be paid to the
treasurer for the benefit of said town .
§ 7. No money shallbe drawn from the treasuryexcept
by the order of the chairman ,or in pursuance of orders
of the board of trustees of said town .
§ 8. The trustees may annually appoint an assessor
to take a listof all taxable property, inhabitants, and
owners of property in said town , and affix against each ,
separately,the amount of his or her estatein saidtown
subject to taxation under the laws of thisCommonwealth
or any ordinance of said town ,which listsare to be taken
as revenue listsare taken by law .
§ 9. Trustees authorized by this act must reside in the
town limits; but property-owners in said town ,livingout-
side of said limits and within the county of Jeffersonor
cityof Louisville, may vote at theirelection.
§ 10. This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage: Provided, That nothing herein contained shallbe
construed to interferewith the rights, privileges, fran-
chises,or toll-gates of the Bardstown Turnpike Company ,
nor with any of the rightsor franchises of the Beargrass
Railroad Company .
Approved May 8, 1884.

CHAPTER 1456 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Brandenburg and Hardinsburg Turnpike Road
Company ,in Meade county, and to authorizethe county to take stock in
said road .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby created a corporation styled
the Brandenburg and Hardinsburg Turnpike Road Com-
pany , with perpetual succession, and the right in said
name to contract and be contracted with ,to sue and be
sued ,plead and be impleaded ; to have a common seal,
and allother rights and powers incident to,and neces-
sary to,carryintoeffectthe powers hereinafterspecific-
ally granted .
§2 . That thebusinessofsaidcompany shallbe tohold,
manage , and control a graded and macadamized road
from the town of Brandenburg , in Meade county ,by way
of the Brandenburg and Hardinsburg road ,to the Breck-
inridge county line,near the residence of John L. Henry ,
on such route as itsboard of directorsmay determine on ,
and may acquire the right of way for said road , and the
necessary land for tollgates, tollhouses , stone, gravel ,
timber ,and other material for constructing and repairing
the same ,by private agreement ,or condemnation under
a writ of ad quod damnum .
§3 . That thosepersonswho may subscribeto the cap-
italstock of said company are hereby declared the stock-
holders of said company ; and in allelections each shall
have one vote forevery share of stockby him subscribed
and paid ,in accordance with the requirements of the
board of directors .
§4 . That thebusinessof saidcompany shallbe under
the direction and control of a board of five directors,
who shallbe elected annually by the stockholders on the
firstMonday in May ,and shallhold officefor one year,
and untiltheir successors are elected and qualified. In
case of a failurefrom any cause to hold the election for
directorson the day named ,an electionforthat purpose
may he held at any time,on twentydays'noticeby the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1259
board of directors, or the owner or owners of twenty
shares of stock. The notice shallbe written or printed,
and posted conspicuously at the company's principal
office, at each toll-gate on its road ,and at such other
places as may serve to give public notice.
§5 . That the board of directors shall, on their own
election, immediately elect one of their number presi-
dent of the board, who shallpresideat theirmeetings,
and be the executive of said board . The board shall
have power to selectfor said company a secretary and
treasurer, and such other officersand agents as they may
deem necessary; to prescribe their duties and fix their
compensation .
§ 6. That itshall be the duty of the secretary to keep
the books and accounts of said company ,and to attend
the meetings of said board, and keep in a substantial
book an accurate record of allitsproceedings .
§7 . That the treasurerof said company shallbe the
custodianof allmoney belonging to it,and shallpay the
same on the order of the board of directors,certifiedto
him by the secretary; but before enteringon hisduties
he shall execute a bond to the company ,with sureties
approved by the board of directors,conditioned that he
will faithfullyperform all the duties of treasurer, and
pay over as directedby the board of directorsany money
in his hands belonging to the company ; and willmake
fullsettlementof his accounts when required by said
board ; and on going out of officewill deliverto his suc-
cessorallbooks,papers,property,money ,or other thing
in his hands belonging to said company . This bond shall
be recorded in his record-book , and preserved by the sec-
retary .
§ 8. That said company shallhave the rightto estab-
lish toll-gates on said road , and charge and collecttolls
as authorized by the General Statutes.
§9 . That the capital stock of said company shall be
divided into shares of fiftydollars or twenty -fivedollars
each , as the first board of directors elected hereunder
shall determine , which determination shall be entered on
their record -book .

1260 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 10. That said board of directorsshallhave power to
make allby -laws ,rules and regulations,for the govern-
ment of themselves and officers and affairs of said com-
pany ,not inconsistentwith publiclaw.
§ 11. That at any general election the stockholders
may change the time of holding the regular election for
directors .
§ 12. That John L. Henry , Richard H. Wathen ,Thos .
Ditto,Mike Laydon ,and K. H. Herndon are hereby made
commissioners to organize said company ; and they , or
any of them choosing to act,may open books forthe sub-
scriptionof stock,call an electionfor the electionof the
firstboard of directors, and do such other acts and things
as may be necessaryto completelyorganize said com-
pany .
§ 13. The Meade county courtof claims,a majorityof
the justicesthereof being present and concurring therein,
may subscribenot more than seven hundred and fiftydol-
larsof stock to each mile of said turnpike road; and for
the purpose of paying the same said court of claims may
make a levy upon the taxable property in said county .
§ 14. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved May 8 , 1884 ..
CHAPTER 1458 .
AN ACT to reviseand amend the tax laws of the cityof Louisville.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I.
Sources of Revenue .
§1 . The revenues of the cityof Louisvillearising from
sources other than taxation are not affectedby this act.
All taxes alreadyleviedor imposed under existinglaws,
and not yet paid,remain payable as ifthisact had not
been passed,unlessthe contrarybe hereinafterprovided.
§2 . Hereafter the following taxes shallbe levied: An
ad valorem tax on lands and improvements,and on.cer

tain kinds of personal property; a licensetax on certain
trades and occupations ,and the use of certain articles; a
head tax of two dollars a head on all resident males more
than twenty -one years of age . The ad valorem and the
head tax shallbe collectedby the receiverof citytaxes,
who isto hold office,and whose successors shallbe elect-
ed ,under existinglaws . The assessment and collectionof
licensetaxes isto remain ,in accordance with those laws ,
in the hands of the commissioners of the sinking fund .
§3 . The general council shallin each calendar year ,as
earlyin the month of December as practicable,by ordi-
nance ,levy an ad valorem tax not exceeding the follow-
ing rates: For citypurposes proper ,not more than eighty-
fivecents on each one hundred dollars; for the support of
the high schools and common schools, not more than
thirtycents; formeeting theprincipaland interestof the
bonds issued to the Elizabethtown and Paducah and the
Louisville,New Albany ,and St. Louis Railroad ,not more
than thirtycents and a half; for the use of the sinking
fund,not more than fortycents; forthe House of Refuge,
not more than fivecents; for cleaning and repairing sew-
ers,not more than two cents; forcleaningand repairing
public ways ,and including foot-ways and crossings,not
more than thirty cents; for reconstruction of carriage-
ways of streetsand alleys,in conformity with section six
of the charteramendment act,approved February twen-
tieth,one thousand eighthundred and seventy-three,not
more than twenty -six cents; for bonds under the ordi-
nance and vote of one thousand eight hundred and
eighty -three, not more than twelve cents. The three
last named taxes may be levied at different rates for
the eastern and the western districts .
§ 4. If in any year the general council shallfailto pass
a levy ordinance , or if the levy ordinance in any year
shallbe invalidor inoperative,the rate of taxation for
the succeeding year shallbe as follows,and any tax-bill
made by color of an invalidlevy ordinance shallbe valid
to that extent: For city purposes proper, seventy-five
cents on each one hundred dollars'worth of property sub

ARTICLE II.
Assessments .
§1. The assessments of real and personal property
shallbe made by the assessor, who shallhold office,and
whose successors shall be elected under existing laws ..
The assessorand his assistants,clerksand draughtsmen ,
shall be compensated under ordinance of the general
council. He may ,from time to time ,appoint as many
assistantsas the general council shallprescribe; and their
appointments must be approved by the general council,
which body may also, for cause , remove them . The
general council may allow him to employ clerks and
draughtsmen ,not having the powers herein vested in his
assistants. But railroads shall be assessed as heretofore
under chapter seven hundred and sixty- four of the Acts
of one thousand eighthundred and seventy-eight.
§2 . For the purpose of assessment ,the soil shall be
known as " land ," and everything attached thereto, or
builtthereon,shallbe known as " improvements," and
such improvements,when owned by the tenant,may be
assessedin hisname apartfrom the land. The articles,
other than land and improvements,enumerated in sec-
tion five of articleone, shallbe known as " personal

and shall also listallresident males twenty -one years of
age and over .

§3 . As early as practicableafterthe passage of this

February by two per cent.,and those paid in March ,by
-one per cent .
§ 2. All tax-billsuncollected,in whole or in part, and
which remain in the hands of the receiver of the city
taxes on the firstday of May succeeding the date on which
they were listedwith him for collection, against any per-
son (not under the disabilityof infancy ,coverture,or un-

with interestat the rate of one -half of one per cent . for

given for the recovery of debts in any court of the Com-
monwealth otherwise competent for that purpose ; and
those billsassessed against an administrator, executor , or
trustee,shall be a charge against the whole succession or
trust estate, and may be enforced accordingly, aside, in
either case, from the other remedies hereinafter given .
The above rate of interestshallprevail untilthe tax -bills
are merged in a judgment .
§3 . In the months of May and June ,of each year ,the
receiver of citytaxes shall,by himself or deputy ,call on

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1275
every person assessed for taxes who residesor does bus-
iness within the city, on the husbands , guardians , and
committees of the married women ,minors, or personsof
unsound mind , who so reside or do business ,and on the
known agents of absent owners ,at the residence or place of
business of each - going to such of these places at which he
thinkshe may be most likelyto findthem and shallseek

commission . -- , Tax Receiver ;" and he
and his deputies shall report as to allbillswhich ,on the

ifany , was given for non -payment . He and each deputy
shallmake a separate listas to those called upon by each ,
which shall be sworn to and filed with the clerk of the

receivershallbe answerable to the party aggrieved for
all costs,commissions , and damages arising therefrom .
But neither the want of a proper return nor the falsityof
a return of "no goods found ," shallinvalidatesubsequent
proceedingsunder thisact,if thetax be not actuallypaid.
Between the firstand twenty-firstof August following,
upon the filingof theselists,the clerknamed shallmake
out for the tax-bills, wholly or partiallyunpaid (except
when the parties assessed appear from the face of the
billto be infants,persons of unsound mind , or fiduciaries
assessedon behalf of a trustestate),warrants substan-
tiallyin the followingform,except that no commission
shallbe allowed in a warrant upon any tax -billthat is
not fullyreported on in the list,in conformity with the
requirements of this section: And provided further, That

where the warrant calls for more than three hundred dol-
lars,itshall allow five per cent, commission on the first
three hundred dollarsonly,and one per cent. on the resi-
due : "The Commonwealth of Kentucky to the receiver of
citytaxes of the city of Louisville, greeting: We com-
mand you ,by distraint,to make of the goods and cha
telsof (party assessed),by cash sale,the sum of (amount
of tax -bill, or balance of tax -bill) due for taxes to the

or fractionof month ,from the firstof May lastpast,and

doings thereunder on or before November first, 18- .
Given under my hand thisfirst(or ifthe firstbe Sun-

C. B. C." For issuingeach warrant he shallbe paid ten
centsby the city,and the commissioners provided for shall,

§4 . These warrants shall, unless payment be made
otherwise,be levied by the receiver, or one of his depu-

tion under section five of article one of this act, and such

empt from execution by the laws now or hereafter in

The advertisement,sale,and deliveryto purchasersshall

or deputy ,shall be returned by him to the officeof the

§ 5. Every guardian ,committee , trustee,or other fidu-
ciaryappointedunder the laws of Kentucky or by a deed
or will recorded in any county clerk'sofficetherein,who

teenthday of November he and his deputies shallpro-

on the face thereof in addition thereto ; but the com-
mission shallbe disallowed in the cases in which , under

and it shall,upon the excess above three hundred dol-
lars,be reduced to one per cent. The noticesshallbe in

" The Commonwealth of Kentucky to the tenants of
—— , a tax-payer of the cityof Louisville,greeting:

of tax-payer) the rent due or accruing from you to
him to an amount not exceeding(amount due on tax-

1278 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
at the rate of one -half of one per cent, for each month
or fraction of a month , and five per cent. on the prin-
cipalsum ,and hold the same subjectto the demands of
the city of Louisville, until advised of the withdrawal
of thisgarnishment . Witness - , clerk of the
board of councilmen ,thisfirst(or second) day of Novem-
ber, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-- .
—— ,С. В. C. And they shallbe served by copy,and
the notice as returned filed with the said clerk ; and such
serviceshall,untilthe tax is paid in full,with interestand
commission,be a defensepro tantoto the tenant in any
proceeding by the landlordforthe recoveryof the rent,
and shall operate to transferpro tantoto the citythe

of any other parcel of the same owner or owners . The

the notices herein provided for shall make the receiver
answerable to the tax payer for the costs arising to him
thereby .
§7 . On the firstof May of the second year after the
assessment of city taxes, the receiver shall make out a
listof the billsstillwholly or partiallyunpaid ,on lands or
improvements ,and furnish the listto the city at-
torney,whose duty itshallbe to bring,without delay,suits
forthe recoverythereofin the Louisvillechancery court,
except that where any litigationgrowing out of distraints
or garnishments ,or against guardians and others,under

lessthere be cause to the contrary,ask for and obtain a
personaljudgment againsttheperson assessed,as well as
the enforcement of the lien hereinaftergiven . The action
herein authorized,and the judgments and subsequent pro-
ceedingstherein(except as hereinafterexcepted)shallbe

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1279
conducted in allrespectslikesuitsupon liensarisingfrom
contract, and the court shall have jurisdiction of all
amounts of twenty dollarsand over,exclusive of interest.
The sums due for taxes of laterand earlieryears shallbe
included in the petitionand in the judgment . Where all
the taxes remaining due by the same owner or set of
joint owners , exclusive of interest,amount to less than
twenty dollars,the city attorney shallcombine them with
otherbillsorsetsofbills,alsolessthan twenty dollars,hav-
ing due regard to the nearness of location in the assessed
property,in one action,so that the aggregate of the bills
embraced in one suitmay amount to fiftydollarsor over.
But citytaxes may ,in suitsbetween thirdparties,be sum-
marily determined in the judgment of sale,or allowed as
a creditto the purchaser, as heretofore, under section
seven hundred and seventy-three of the Civil Code of
Practice . New tax bills which are included with older

in sections three,four, and six of this article. The omis-
sion of the receiverto act as prescribed in sectionsthree,
four,and six,shallnot defeat any suitof the cityfortaxes.
§8 . Whenever taxes due for a year preceding the
assessment of September , one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-three,are included in an action under section
seven of this article,or are set up by pleading in any
action, the material facts making them a lien on the
propertyto be subjectedshallbe statedin the petitionor
other pleading ,and the court shall render judgment ac-
cording to the justice of the case; and this rule shall
also prevail in all actions which may be brought exclu-
sivelyforthe taxes of such former years; and such actions

a sale of the assessed premises, and a so-called purchase
thereof by the city, under section ten of the act of April
eighth,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-two ,here-
in firstmentioned ,has given to the owner a stillunex-
pired time for redemption, such purchase may , at the

defendant's option,be treated as an unmatured incum-
brance .
$ 9. The fee-simple of alllands in the cityof Louis-
ville, and the fullterm and renewal right of every lease-
hold carrying with it the value of the improvements-
thereon ,shall be subject,from and after the firstday of
September of each year, to a lienforthe citytax,to be
assessed thereon for the succeeding year ,which lien shall
be superior to homestead right,and to all incumbrances ,
whether made before or after that date , except State
taxes, and shalltake precedence of dower , curtesy, re-

shaling of burdens as against third parties, as the rules
of equity may require; and the court may allow a pur-
chaser or incumbrancer to releaseany parcel on pay-
ment of itstax , with interest and share of costs.
§ 10. The goods of infants or persons judiciallyfound
to be of unsound mind shall not be distrained for the
taxes assessed on theirlands or improvements ; nor shall
their lands,during their disability,be sold for less than
two-thirdsof theirappraisedvalue on any judgment of
sale rendered for taxes and costs alone , when those lands
or improvements have come to them through descent ,
distribution, or devise,or the gift or settlement of some
person then deceased , or have belonged to persons of
unsound mind before they became such ; nor shall, for
taxes chargeable to the owner of the particularestate,the
entire estate be sold for taxes and costs alone at less than
two-thirds of the appraised value,so as to defeat rever
sions,remainders , or future estates,while any future es-
tates are outstanding (unless when the reversioners or
remaindermen are ascertained, and are of fullage ); nor
shallsuch entireestatesbe put up to saleunlessthe par-
ticularestate of the tax-payer has firstbeen put up and
has failed to bring the amount of the taxes and costs .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1281
§ 11. Any court of competent jurisdiction,not inferior
to a circuitcourt,may ,in the establishedmodes of pro-
ceedings,and upon existingprinciplesof law or equity,
give redress against unlawful distraintsor garnishments
of rents that may be wrongfully made or threatened
under color of thisact; but no injunctionshallbe granted
in such proceeding,exceptby the courtor itsjudge,nor
otherwisethan upon noticeto the cityof Louisville,and
after a hearing .
§ 12. The receiver of the Louisville chancery court,
and allother ministerialofficersin Jeffersoncounty , shall
pay directlyto the receiverof citytaxes,for the time
being, the amounts recoveredby the city of Louisville
for taxes in any judicial proceeding on his own receipt,
without the intervention of an attorney; and where ,from
any cause, moneys so recovered anywhere have come
into the hands of an attorney on behalf of the city,he
shall deliver them to such receiver of city taxes .
§ 13. The officeof back tax-collectoris hereby abol-
ished, and all uncollected tax-bills, books , papers , and
otherpropertyin the officeof the back tax-collector,shall
be taken charge of by he city tax receiver, who shall
collect said uncollected tax -billsso taken from the back
tax-collector'soffice; but thisshallnot change the appro-
priation of such back taxes heretoforemade .
ARTICLE V.
Miscellaneous Provisions .
§ 1. No person whose property or head tax,hereafter
assessed,remains whollyorpartiallyunpaid,shallbe given
a licenseto carry on any business under thisact,or qualify
as an alderman , councilman , city official,deputy or as-
sistant,or policeman ,or be employed by the city, or have
any contract,or sellany supplies to it,untilsuch taxes are
paid ; and any creditor of the citymust receive his tax-
bills with accrued interesttowards the payment of any
demand : Provided ,In either case that the tax -billshave
not been annulled or enjoined by the judgment or order

of a competent court: And provided, That nothing herein
contained shall cause the invalidityof any officialact or
contract .
§ 2. Aside of the duties imposed by the charter of one
thousand eight hundred and seventy, the city attorney
shall,at all times,even unasked ,point out to all depart-
ments of the city government all steps to be taken by
each to make the tax-levy and assessment , both in the
aggregate and in itsdetails,binding and effectiveaccord-
ing to itspurpose ; he shall,by himself or assistant, repre-
sent the cityin all actions or proceedings to which itisa
partyin name or in effect,thatgrow out of her demands
fortaxes; he shall,with the approval of the general coun-
cil, from time to time , appoint an assistant, who shall
receive a salary fixed by ordinance of not over fifteen
hundred dollars a year from the city treasury, together
with alldocket -fees actually recovered .
§ 3. On the firstThursday of July ineach year the city
attorney shallreport to the general council allactions for
taxesbegun inthe precedingyear,withname of defend-
ants, amounts sued for, form and nature of action, and
name of court; also alljudgments , dismissals, and col-
lections made by suit or process; also a listof the bills
which ,under section seven of articlefour, were ripe for
suiton the firstday of the preceding May ,and not yet
sued upon ,with his reasons for the delay; and in bringing
any class of suits for taxes, except under sections seven
and eightof said article,he shallfollowthe directionsof
the council .
§4 . The receiver of city taxes shall, on or before
the thirty-firstday of December ,in each year , state and
settlehis accounts with ,and receive a quietus from ,the
finance committee of the general council; and in such
settlementhe shallbe allowed no creditforany uncol-
lected tax-bills,unless such committee believe,from the
evidence offered,that said uncollected tax-billscould not
have been collectedby him (for the want of goods to dis-
trainand of rentstogarnishee); and foreveryuncollected
tax -bill which such committee refuses in such settlement

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1283
toallow,he,and the suretieson hisbond ,shallbe liable,
and shallpay the same as ifsuch tax-billhad been col-
lected; and he shall be subrogated to the rights of said
cityupon such uncollectedtax-bills. If the receiverfail
to obtain a quietus at such settlements,such failureshall
be reportedtotheboard of aldermen attheirnext meeting
thereafter,whereupon that board shallat once declare the
officeof receiver of citytaxes to be vacant ,and order
the treasurerof said cityto take possessionof allthe
moneys ,books ,bills,registers, accounts ,et cetera,of the
office,and at the next meeting of the general council
thereaftera receiver of city taxes shall be elected, in
jointsession,by viva voce vote, for the unexpired term .
§5 . The laws regulating the officialbonds of any of the
cityofficialsintrusted with the collectionof taxes are not
affected by this act. The general council may ,by ordi-
nance , not in conflict herewith , regulate the officesof
assessor and receiver . And all ordinances now in force
on thissubject, as far as compatible with this act, shall
remain in force until repealed .
§6 . All laws and parts of laws in conflictwith this
act are repealed . The second and eighth and ninth sec-
tions of an act , entitled " An act to amend the charter of
the city of Louisville," approved March twenty -ninth,
one thousandeighthundred and eighty-two ,are repealed.
§ 7. An act , entitled "An act to revise and amend the
tax-laws of the cityof Louisville," approved Apriltwen .
ty-five, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four , is
hereby amended so as to make the same in allrespects
conform to the foregoing provisions of thisact.
§ 8. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas- .
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884.

CHAPTER 1459 .
AN ACT to createan additionalvoting place in the Charlestondistrict, in
Hopkins county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
1. That there is created an additionalvoting place
in Charleston district,in Hopkins county , at the city of
Dawson ,in said district; and any and allqualifiedvoters
residinginsaiddistrictarepermittedtovote at saidplace;
but they shallnot vote at any other place at the same
election .
§2 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 18,1884 .

sage .

CHAPTER 1465 .
AN ACT to establishan institutionof learning and parochialchurch in or
near the town of Beattyville,in Lee county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That an institutionof learning be ,and the same
ishereby ,establishedin or near the town of Beattyville,
in the county of Lee ,to be known by the name and style
of Cumberland College, and to be under the exclusive
control of the Protestant Episcopal Church .
§2 . That the Reverend C. H. Lockwood ,E. M. Pryse,
and Jno .S. Mahan are hereby appointed a board of trus-
tees and body -politic,and as such shall have perpetual
succession; may sue and be sued ,plead and be implead-
ed ,in any of the courts of thisCommonwealth ; may con-
tractand be contractedwith; and as such may purchase,
hold,and otherwise acquire,convey and dispose of any
property, real, personal, or mixed , money , or chose in
action , for the benefit of said institution.
§3 . That said trustees may attach a parish church to
or adjoining the school buildings; may receive donations ,
gifts,or contributions of money or property,real or per-
sonal,for the purpose of erecting the necessary buildings,
or forthe purchase of libraries,chemicalor philosophical
apparatus,or any other apparatus,furniture,or fixtures
forthe purposes of the school,employ teachers,or any
otherpurpose connected with saidinstitution.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1287
§ 4. They may purchase lands, lay out grounds, and
erect allnecessarybuildingsforsaidpurposes,and shall
have fullcontrolover the buildingsand propertyof said
institutions; may pass any by -laws or rules necessary for
itsprotection and government , not in conflictwith the
Constitution and laws of the State .
§5 . Said board may appoint one of itsmembers chair-
man ,and a majority shallconstitutea quorum to do busi-
ness; and when a vacancy in said board may occur from
any cause , it shall be the duty of those remaining in
office,or the survivors,to fillsaid vacancy or vacancies
within ten days thereafter. Said board shallappoint a
secretary and treasurer; may require bond ,with security,
from the treasurer,payable to themselves,for the faithful
discharge of such duties as may be required of him by
said board in receiving and disbursing any money or
thing belonging to said institutions.
§ 6. Said board shallkeep a record of the proceedings
of each meeting,to be recorded,read,and approved by
the board , signed by the chairman ,and attested by the
secretary, which record shallbe legal evidence in allthe
courts of thisState; and copies thereof,attestedby the
secretary,shall be entitledto as fullfaith and credit as
the original. Said board at any time , for good cause
shown , may remove said secretary or treasurer,and put
others in theirstead; may make provision for their sala-
ries,the purchase of books , stationery,et cetera, neces-
sary to carry out the objectsof thissection.
§ 7. The rectorin charge of the church shallbe presi-
dent of the school,and he ,together with a majority of
the board of trustees,are empowered to employ teachers
for said school,prescribe the branches to be taught , and
regulate the fees for tuition ; make all rules and regula-
tions necessary for the good government of said school;
may grant diplomas, certificatesof merit, or other evi-
dence of advancement or credit to students, or confer
other honorary appointments .
§ 8. All officescreatedby thisact shallbe filledby
members ofthe ProtestantEpiscopalChurch; and ,before

1288 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
entering upon their duties,shall take an oath faithfully
to discharge the same . The board of trusteesappointed
under thisact shall,immediately after its passage , qual-
ifythereunder,and holdtheirofficeduring good behavior,
unless they resign or move out of the county , or are
removed by a majorityof theirbody for a violationof
any of the rulesor by-laws adopted by the board. The
secretary and treasurer shallhold theirofficeduring the
pleasure of the board of trustees.
§ 9. All male members of the ProtestantEpiscopal
Church over the age of twenty -one years, who have
resided in Lee county for one year next preceding his
election or appointment , shall be eligibleto hold the
officesprovided for in this act.
§ 10. It shallbe the duty of each trustee, treasurer, or
secretary,within ten days afterhe or they go out of office,
to surrender to theirsuccessors any books ,papers ,money ,
or propertyof any kind belongingto saidinstitutions.
§ 11. The board of trusteesis empowered to make all
needful by -laws and regulationsfor erecting and keeping
in repair the necessary buildings and fencing, and laying
out the grounds; but allappropriationsfor paying cur-
rent expenses of the schools, or expenditure of money
for any purpose ,must be entered upon theirrecords. A
copy of thesame,certifiedby thesecretary,shallauthor-
ize the treasurerto pay itoff: Provided , That in no case
shall the treasurer pay out money or do any other act
only upon the order of the board of trustees, passed ,
signed,copied,and certifiedas provided in thisact, which
copies shallbe his only legal voucher in a settlement
with the board : Provided further, That no deed convey-
ing real estatebelonging to said corporation shallpass
titleuntilit is signed and acknowledged by allthe mem-
bers of the board of trustees: And provided further, That
the property used for school or church purposes shall not
be subject to State, county , or municipal tax.
§ 12. Said corporation may or not ,in theirdiscretion,
use a common seal .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1289
§ 13. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .

secretary of Ingleside Lodge , number one hundred and

cah, shallpurchase a lot, and agree to erect a lodge-

CHAPTER 1467 .

ad valorem tax and fixthe county levy, in Russellcounty .

of Kentucky :

dollars' worth of taxable property,to be collectedin the

passage .

mon schools for Pulaski county ,seventeen pupil children

trict number twenty -four, for the school year ending

ty-four; therefore,

of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 1470 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Frankfortand Belle Point Bridge Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. C. Herndon , J. N. Crutcher , James M.
Wakefield , R. E. Gaines , Louis Mangan , W. L. Pence ,
and John L. Sneed,be,and they are hereby,created a
body-politicand corporate, with perpetual succession,
under the name and style of the Frankfort and Belle
PointBridge Company ; and by thatname and stylemay
sue and be sued,plead and be impleaded,contractand be
contractedwith,as fullyand to allintentsand purposes
as a natural person in all matters and things connected
with, and pertinentto, the objectsand purposes for
which said corporation is created.
§ 2. That said corporationis hereby authorized and
empowered to locate,construct, maintain ,and operate a
bridge across the Kentucky river,connecting the town of
Belle Point and the cityof Frankfort,at any point said
company may select; and shall have the power to pur-
chase ,receive ,and hold as much real estate on both sides
of said river as may be necessary or convenient for the
siteof said bridge, abutments ,piers,toll-houses , and suit-
able avenues leading to and from the same ; and if said
company cannot contractfor such sitesand ways upon
such terms as they may deem proper ,they shallhave the
rightto condemn the same by proceedings according to
the provisions of the law now in force in this State in
regard to the writ of ad quod damnum ,and proceedings
thereunder .
§3 . That the capitalstock of said company shall not
be lessthan ten nor more than fiftythousand dollars, of
shares of one hundred dollars each ; and said stock may
be subscribedforby individualsand by corporationsand
by the town of Belle Point and the city of Frankfort .
§ 4. Itshallbe the duty of the above named corpora-
tors,or such of them as may act,to open books for the
subscriptionof the stock in said company , and the same
to keep open untilstock sufficientto construct said bridge

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1293
shall have been subscribed ; and in order to ascertain this
fact, said corporators shallcause an estimate to be made
of the costs of constructing a safe and substantial bridge
across said riversufficientfor the passage thereof of all
ordinarytraveland vehiclesby some practicaland ex-
perienced architect or bridge-builder; and when stock
equal to such estimate shall have been subscribed,then
itshallbe the duty of the said corporators to calla meet-
ing of the stockholders to elect five directors and such
other officersas they may deem proper,who shallpro-
ceed at once to have such bridge constructed in such
manner and upon such terms as they may deem best.
The saiddirectorsshallremain in officefor one year ,and
until their successors shall have been elected .
§5 . The said company shallhave power to make any
by -laws ,rules and regulations,in regard to the manage-
ment of the affairsof the company ,and for the protec-
tion of theirproperty,not inconsistentwith the Constitu-
tion and laws of this State .
§6 . That said corporation shall have the right to fix
and alterat pleasure the rates of tollfor allpersons and
property passing over said bridge, whether on foot or
horseback , or in vehicles of any kind : Provided, Said
tollsshallnot yieldmore than a dividendof ten per cent.
semi-annuallyon the capitalstock; and for the purpose
of collecting said toll, toll-houses may be erected and
maintained at either end of said bridge .
§7. That saidcorporationshallhave power to borrow
money to construct and operate the work above indi-
cated, at such rates of interest, for such times , in such
places,and to such amounts ,as the directorsmay deem
best,and may issuebonds or other evidencesof indebt-
edness therefor,and secure the payment of the same by
mortgage or pledge of the saidworks hereby authorized
to be constructed,or any part thereof; and saidcorpora-
tion may sell and dispose of said bonds and other evi-
dences of indebtedness upon such terms and discounts as
may be agreed upon .
§ 8. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas .
sage .
Approved May 12,1884 .

CHAPTER 1471 .
AN ACT toprohibitthe saleof spirituous,vinous, or malt liquorsat or
within one fourthof a mile of Lemon's Mill, in Scott county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person to sell
any spirituous,vinous ,or malt liquors at or within one
fourth of a mile of Lemon's Mill,in Scott county .
§2 . Any person violating the provisions of this act
shallbe fined fiftydollarsfor each offense.
§3 . This act shalltake effectsixtydays afteritspas-
sage, but shallnot be construed to affectany license
already granted .
Approved May 12, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1472 .
AN ACT for the benefit of John B. Plummer , late sheriffof Simpson
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :

county ,be given tillthe firstday of January ,one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-six,to collect all uncol-
lectedtaxes due him ,under the same pains, penalties,
and legal restrictionsand rights and remedies as now
provided by law , with power of distraint.
§2 . This act shall be in force from and after its pas-
sage .

CHAPTER 1473 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous,vinous, or malt liquors in the
town of Elkhorn , or within three miles thereof, in Taylor county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawfulforany person or persons
within the town of Elkhorn,in Taylor county,Kentucky ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1295
or within three miles thereof,to sellspirituous,vinous ,or
malt liquors,or a mixture of either.
§2 . Any person violatingthe firstsectionof thisact
shall,upon conviction,be finednot lessthan twenty-five
nor more than fiftydollars for each offense, recoverable
by warrant before a justiceof the peace ,or on indictment
by a grand jury ; and each sale shallbe deemed a sepa-
rate offense .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 12 , 1884.
CHAPTER 1474 .
AN ACT to authorizeand empower the trusteesof the town of Greenville,
in Muhlenburg county,to examine said town as to defectiveflues, and to
order the same repairedand made safe.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the trustees of the town of Greenville are
hereby authorized and empowered to examine any or all
fluesin said town that said trustees may deem defective
or dangerous ,and to order the owners of such defective
or dangerous fluesto repair the same and put theirflues
in a safe condition .
§2 . After said flues have been examined as above
stated , and are found to be defective , and the owner or
occupant notified of same by the trustees, and such
owner or occupant failingto repair the same in ten days
after being notified, in writing, by the trustees to do
so, and upon such failure, the trusteesshallcause the
same to be repaired at the cost of the owner ; and upon
said owner refusing to pay for said repairs,the trustees
shallhave fullpower to bring suitagainst such owner or
owners for the amount of said repairs and cost in the
policecourt of Greenville; and saidcourtshallhear the
proofsadduced by both the trusteesand the owner ofsaid
defective flues,and if said court is of the opinion,from
the evidence ,that said flueswere defective and danger-
ous and the charges for repairing reasonable,the court

1296 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shall enter judgment against the owner for the amount
of repairing and cost of suit. The proceedings shallbe as
in otherordinaryactionsforthe recoveryof money .
§ 3. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 12, 1884.
CHAPTER 1475 .
AN ACT forthebenefitof WilliamW. Hazelip,of Warren county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

one years of age , be , and he is hereby , invested with
all the rights, powers , and privileges of a man over
twenty -one years of age ; and fulleffectis to be given to
allhis officialacts,as though he was twenty-one years
old. But nothing in this act shall be construed to em-
power the said Hazelip to exercisethe right of suffrage.

CHAPTER 1476 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county court of Cartercounty to pay forfuelused
in the jailof saidcounty .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Cartercounty,sittingas

CHAPTER 1477 .
AN ACT to prohibit the cutting of timber into and leaving the same in
Panther creek, in Daviess county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

diction .

be used for the benefit of the schools of said county .

ling,Kentucky .

CHAPTER 1479 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the Covington ElectricLight Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :

Light Company may ,at any time, be increasedby the

of dollars .

AN ACT to authorizethe Logan county court to borrow money .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

ty of the justicesconcurring) is hereby authorized and
