LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1299
CHAPTER 1481 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Falmouth and Double Beech and the Berlin
and Double Beech Turnpike Road Companies .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That John M. Pearl,H. B. Moran ,Chas .T. North-
cutt,Jerry Glenn , Adam Roschi , W. W. McAtee , and A.
P. McClanahan ,for Bracken county ; and Monroe Lantry ,
PatrickMurphy ,Anton Leisman ,William Dunstall,John
Cullen, Conrad Stitz, and James Wood ,for Pendleton
county ,and their associates,successors,and assigns, be ,
and they are hereby,createdbodies-politicand corporate,
with perpetual succession,under the corporate names and
styles, the firstas the Falmouth and Double Beech , the
second as the Berlin and Double Beech Turnpike Road
Company , for the purpose of constructing and main-
taining a turnpike road from Falmouth ,in Pendleton
county,by way of the Double Beech,to Berlin,in Brack-
en county,at such pointsas may be agreed upon by the
two boards of directors; and by their names respectively
may sue and be sued,contractand be contractedwith,
and plead and be impleaded,in allthe courts of this
Commonwealth .
§2 . The capitalstock of said companies shallbe raised
by individual subscription, and the subscriptionsof the
Bracken and Pendleton county courts as now regulated
by law ; and said capital stock shall not exceed thirty
thousand dollars .
§3 . The capital stock shall be divided into shares of
fiftydollarseach .
§4 . Any two of the incorporators of either company
hereinbefore named may open a book for subscriptions
in each county to the stock of the company they rep-
resent,at such time and place as they may determine ;
and the subscriber therein shall sign an obligation in
substance as follows : We , whose names are hereunto
subscribed, respectivelypromise to pay to the company
the sum of fiftydollarsforeach share of stocksetoppo-
site our names , in such proportion and at such times

1300 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
as shall be determined by the presidents and directorsof
said companies .
§ 5. The private property of members of these com-
panies shall be exempt from the corporate debts of said
companies .
§6 . On the firstSaturday in August ,one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-four, in the towns of Falmouth
and Berlin, a meeting of the stockholders of said com-
paniesshallbe held for the purpose of electinga presi
dent and fivedirectorsfor each company ; one vote shall
be allowed for each share of stock. Any two of the
corporators named may call a meeting at any time for
the purpose of electingdirectors,and so forth; and the
officersthen electedshallcontinue in officeone year,
and untiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified; and
thereafter,on the firstSaturday of May in each year,an
electionof a presidentand fivedirectorsfor each com .
pany shallbe held in the towns of Falmouth and Berlin
respectively. No one who does not own stock in said
road shallbe eligibleto the officeof president or directors
in said companies .
§7 . The presidentand directorsof each company shall
elect a secretaryand treasurer, and shallfillsuch vacan-
cies as may occur in said boards during their term of
office. Said boards shallrequireof the treasurer, before
he assumes the duties of his office,a bond for the faithful
discharge of the duties of treasurer, and to faithfully
account for and pay over all moneys that may come to
his hands as treasurer, which bond and securityshallbe
approved by the boards respectively.
$ 8 . The boards of directors shallhave power to make
allnecessary by -laws and regulations forthe government
of said companies .
§ 9. The presidentsand directorsof each company
shall have the exclusive control of the construction and
maintaining of itspart of said road ,the erection of toll-
gates and toll-houses ,and the collectionof toll as now
regulatedby law ; and the provisionsof chapter one
hundred and ten of theGeneral Statutes,title" Turnpike ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1301
Gravel ,and Plank Roads ," shallapply to the road built
by the said companies ,and to the rights,franchises, and
liabilitiesof the companies .
§ 10.The corporate company of each county shallhave
jurisdictionof so much of said road as may be in their
respective counties, and no more ; and that each com-
pany be entitledto erect one toll-gate on said road in
theirrespectivecounties.
§ 11.The president and directorsof each company shall
deliver a certificate,signed by the president and secre-
tary,to each share-holderforthe number of sharespaid
for by him , which certificateshall be transferable on the
books of the company , either in person or by attorney,
upon the return of the original certificate; but no share
or shares of stock shall be issued or transferred whilst
there isany arrearage due or unpaid thereon .
§ 12. The presidents may call meetings of the boards
at any time and placethe businessmay requireit. Said
boards shallkeep a record of itsproceedings,to be en-
teredupon a book provided forthat purpose; and after
said recordhas been read and approved by the board,the
same shallbe signedby the presidentand secretary. In
the absence of the president or presidents, said board or
boards shallselectone of theirnumber who shallpreside
over their meetings .
§13 . The boards shallfix and regulate the elevation
and grade of said road , and the width and part thereof
to be covered with stone .
§14 . That said two companies may ,by a wote of two-
thirds in interestof the stockholders of each company ,at
a regularmeeting,or at a meeting held forthe purpose,
authorize their consolidation , which consolidation shall
be carried out and consummated by a contract or con-
tracts, signed by the presidentsor some other persons
authorizedby the boards of directors,on behalfof the
companies ; and in case of consolidation, the name of
the company thus created shallbe known and styled
the Falmouth,Double Beech,and BerlinTurnpike Road
Company ; and in thatname shallsue and be sued,con

1302 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tract and be contracted with ; and shall be bound for all
the debts,and succeed to allthe property, rights,privi-
leges, and franchisesof the said two companies .
§ 15. That this act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 9, 1884.
CHAPTER 1482 .
AN ACT authorizingcertainnoticesof salesof land and of sittingsof com-
missionersto be publishedin any newspaperof generalcirculationin
Campbell county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the circuitand chancery courts of Campbell
county may ,in any judgment ,decree,or order directing
the sale of real or personal property,require notices of
such sale to be advertised in any newspaper of general
circulationpublished in said county ; and in such judg
ments ,decrees,and orders may fixthe number of inser-
tionsthat the noticesshallbe publishedin said paper,
whether of the weekly or daily issue,before the time of
such sales. Said courts may ,in like manner ,directthat
notices of the sittingsof the master commissioner or spe-
cialcommissioner to hear proof in settlement of estatesof
deceased persons,or of insolvents,or to adjust and settle
accounts ,shallbe insertedin said newspaper ; but in all
such cases,one printed or written notice of such sale or
sittingsof commissioner shall be posted on the court-
house door nearest the place of sale,or of the sittingsof
the said commissioner ; and if the sale be of land , a sim-
ilarnotice must be posted on the land to be sold, or near
thereto,and both of said noticesfor the sale of land shall
be posted fifteendays before said sale. The notice of
the sittingsof the commissioner shallbe posted a reason .
able length of time before the sittingof the commissioner ,
and be continued as long as necessary .
§ 2. In allcasesof salesto be made under decrees,ex-
ecutions,and orders of sale,where the amount of money
to be made shall exceed one hundred dollars ,and in cases

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1303
of the sittingsof either of said commissioners for the
purposes mentioned in the firstsection of thisact, where
no direction shall have been given in the judgment ,
decree,execution, or order, as to the mode and time of
giving notice of sale, or sittingsof the commissioner , the
plaintiffor defendant in any such cause , or the attorney
of eitherparty, where such party shallreside out of
Campbell county , may cause notice of such sales and
sittingsof said commissioners to be advertised in said
paper. The noticesmust specifythe time, place, and
terms of sale ,the case in which it is to be made , and a
description of the property to be sold. If of the sittings
of the commissioner ,the time ,place, and purpose of his
sittingsshall set forth, as well as the cause in hand .
Such notices for the sale of land shall be inserted in said
paper,twice in the weekly issue,and in the dailyissue,
if such be published, four times. Notices of the sittings
of the commissioner ,for purposes mentioned herein, shall
be insertedas soon as said commissioner fixesthe day for
his sittings,and continued up to the time the same shall
take place,and as often thereafteras said commissioner
may deem proper. In case of the sale of land ,in addi-
tion to said advertisements in said paper ,one printed or
written notice of such sale shallbe posted on the court-
house door nearest the place of sale,and one on or near
the land to be soldfifteendays before such sale. Nothing
in thisact shallaffectthe legalityof notices of sales and
sittingsof master or special commissioners ,as authorized
to be given by the General Statutes or Code of Practice
of this State .
§3 . The power given the said courts in the firstsec-
tion of this act is a discretion power , to be exercised
whenever such courts may deem itproper .
§4 . The costs of said advertisements shall form part
of the costs of the case,and be collected as other costs
are collected; and the charges for said advertising shall
not exceed the usual rates for similar work .
§ 5. This act shallbe in forcefrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 9, 1884 .

1304 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1483 .
AN ACT to incorporateSacramento College,McLean county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That L. E. Almon ,T. M. Smith ,and S. W. Rhoads ,
of McLean county,Kentucky ,and George R. Henry ,of
Muhlenburg county, Kentucky , the present trusteesof
Sacramento College, an institute of learning situated
near the eastern limit of the town of Sacramento , Mc-
Lean county , Kentucky , and theirsuccessorsin office,be ,
and they are hereby ,created a body -politicand corporate ,
under the name of Sacramento College; and by that
name shall have perpetual succession, with power to
adopt and use a common seal,and change same at pleas-
ure; to make contractsfor the benefitof said college.
§2 . The trusteesof said college shallcontinue in office
until they resign or remove from said counties of Mc-
Lean and Muhlenburg , or are deposed according to the
by -laws establishedunder this act,and shallhave power
to choose from theirown body a president,secretary, and
treasurer, and to fillvacancies in theirown body .
§3 . The president of the college and assistantteach-
ers, together with the president and secretary of the
board of trustees,shall have power to confer upon the
studentsof saidcollegeany and allthe diplomas of de-
grees conferredby any similarinstitutionin the State :
Provided, That no degrees be conferred upon any of the
students except those who shallhave completed the pre-
scribed course of study, and passed a satisfactoryexam-
ination in the same , and who shall have maintained a
good moral character. Said diplomas shallbe signedby
the president of college and the assistantteachers,and by
the presidentand secretaryof the board of trusteesof
said college .
§ 4. The trusteesshallhave power to electthe presi-
dent and assistantteachers in case of death or resig.
nation or removal of present presidentand assistant
teachers. The president shall have power to suspend
any studentforremissnessof duties,disobedienceor vio

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1305
lationof rules; and said president, togetherwith the
trustees, shallhave power to expel such studentsfrom
saidcollegeforpersistencein disobedience,remissnessof
duty ,and immoral practice.
§5 . The trusteesshallhave power to make such by-
laws , rules, and ordinances, not inconsistent with the
laws of the State and the United States, as they shall
deem proper for the supervision and government of said
college .
§ 6. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved May 10, 1884.
CHAPTER 1484 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof James Reed ,of Washington county.
WHEREAS , James Reed ,of Washington county,twenty
years of age ,being competent to transactbusiness; there-
fore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

may be in the hands of said guardian belonging to said
infant
§ 2. It is furtherenacted ,that said James Reed be , and
he is hereby , empowered to contract and be contracted
with,sue and be sued, as though he were twenty-one
years of age .
§3 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and

Auburn High School," passedand approvedFebruarytwenty-third,one
thousand eighthundred and seventy-four.

of Kentucky :

act to incorporate the Auburn High School ," be ,and the

CHAPTER 1489 .
AN ACT to authorize the levy and collection of additional tax in aid of
public schoolsin common school districtnumber sixteen, Breckinridge
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the trustees of common school district num-
ber sixteen, Breckinridgecounty (the Hardinsburg dis-
trict), shall annually cause to be levied and collectedan
ad valorem tax of not exceeding twenty cents on each
one hundred dollars worth of taxable property, and a
capitation tax of not exceeding one dollar and fiftycents
on each person over the age of twenty -one years within
said district: Provided, That only white persons and
property belonging to white persons shallbe taxed .
§ 2. Said trustees shall,annually , on or before the first
day of May in each year,by an entryon a recordbook
to be kept by them,declarewhat amount of tax,both ad
valorem and capitation,shallbe collectedin saidyear.

1303 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . Said trustees, in the collectionof said tax ,shall
be governed by the law as now embraced in the General
Statutes, and amendments thereto, except that the col-
lectorof said tax (who may be appointed by said trus-
tees) shallpay same to the chairman of said board of
trustees, and shall make settlement with said board .
§4 . The taxes collected hereunder , after paying ex-
penses of collection,shall be used and expended for the
improvement of and keeping in repairthe common school
propertyin saiddistrict,forthe payment of teachers,for
the extension of the school term beyond the term of five
months ,if the same shall be sufficient, and for the aid
and maintenance of public schools in said district; and
shallbe in addition to, and supplemental of,the pro rata
share of the general school fund due said district.
§5 . The chairman of said board of trustees,before he
shall receive any money under this act, and before the
collectorshallpay him any,shallgo beforethe Breckin-
ridge county court, and execute an approved covenant ,
with good security, a bond for the faithfulperformance
of his duties hereunder ,which bond shall be filed with
and preserved by the clerk of said court among the rec-
ords of his office .
§ 6. This act shall take effectfrom its passage ; but
shall not be enforced until it has been submitted to the
qualifiedwhite voters of said district, and a majority of
those voting on the question shall have voted in favor
of said tax. Said questionshallbe submitted,and the
vote thereon shall be taken , in the same manner as now
prescribed by law for voting upon question of taxation
forschoolpurposes; and may be submitted, and the vote
thereon taken,at any time designatedby the board of
trustees,the noticenow prescribedby law having first
been given .
Approved May 10 , 1884 .

CHAPTER 1490 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Paynesville, inMeade county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That thecitizensof Paynesville,in Meade county,
be,and are hereby,createda body-politicand corporate,
under the name of the town of Paynesville,with power
to contract and be contracted with, sue and be sued , to
plead and be impleaded ,to answer and be answered ,and
to do allsuch acts and things,either at law or in equity,
as bodies-politicand corporatehaving perpetualsucces-
sionmay rightfullydo and perform.
§2 . The limitsof said town shallbe as follows : begin-
ning at a papaw bush,near a cave on M. B. Stiles'land;
thence west one hundred and sixtypoles to a stone on
Wright's land ; thence north one hundred and sixty poles
to a stake on Jas .King's land; thence east one hundred
and sixtypoles to a stake on Mrs. A. Huff'sland ; thence
south one hundred and sixtypoles to the beginning.
§2 . On the firstSaturday in September next,and on
the firstSaturday in June in each succeeding year there-
after, an election shall be held in said town for the
choice of five trustees of said town . Said trustees,when
elected, shallhave the same powers and authority which
is given under the general laws of this Commonwealth
to trustees of towns .
§3 . Said trustees shalltake the oath trulyand faith-
fullyto perform the dutiesimposed upon them by law
as trustees . Said trustees shall meet and choose from
among theirnumber a president of the board , who shall
hold his officeuntilthe next annual election, and whose
duty itshallbe to convene the trusteesfrom time to time
to preside at theirmeetings ,and to sign allordinances and
by -laws of said town by them made and enacted . Said
trustees shall also elect a clerk , who shall continue in
officefor one year,whose duty it shallbe to keep and
preserveallpapers belongingto or filedwith said board,
and shallattend the meetings thereof, and record allthe
proceedingsin a book to be kept by saidboard for that

1310 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
purpose. Said trusteesshallhave power to appoint a
treasurer, who shall keep the funds of said town safe,
and pay the same over by order of said board to such
person or persons as he shall be directed by the said
board .
§4 . Said trusteesshall have power to assess a poll-tax
of not exceeding one dollar, and an ad valorem tax not
to exceed ten cents on the one hundred dollars ' worth of
taxablepropertywithinthelimitsof saidtown forrepair-
ing and keeping in order streetsand sidewalks. Said
trusteesshallhave power to appoint annually an assessor,
who shallbe sworn to faithfullyand impartiallydis-
charge his duties. It shall be his duty to make out and
furnish to said trustees,at such time as they may direct,
listsof the taxable property of each individual in said
town , with the value thereof , also the tithes in said town ,
and the property in said town subject to taxation owned
by personswho do not residetherein, and alsothe names
of the owners . Said board shall also have power to
annually appoint a collectorto collectthe taxes assessed
in said town ; and said collector shall be sworn to truly
and faithfullydischarge his duties as collector, and give
bond to the trusteesof said town for the faithfulperform
ance of hisduties; saidcollectorshallhave allthe pow-
ers in the [collection] of said tax by levy,distress, sale,
and otherwise,that isgiven by law to sheriffsin the col-
lection of State tax and county levy.
§5 . A police judge and town marshal shallbe elected
by the qualifiedvoters of said town ,on the firstSaturday
in September , one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
four, and on the same day in every second year there-
after; said police judge shall be commissioned by the
Governor ; he shallhave jurisdictionof penal and crim-
inal cases within the limits of said town , and not else-
where , and shall also have exclusive jurisdictionof all
violations of the ordinances of said town . The fees of
thepolicejudge shallbe the same as the feesnow paid
to magistratesin criminaland penal cases. The town
marshal shall take an oath to faithfullydischarge his

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1811
duties as marshal of said town ; and shallexecute bond in
the county court in the same manner and with the same
covenants as is now requiredby law of constables; he
shallbe a peace officer, and shallhave the same powers
and jurisdictionin said town ,but not elsewhere,as are
conferred by law on constables; and shall also execute
allwarrants of arrest, writs,summons ,process,and sub-
pœnas for any and all violationsof the ordinances of
said town , and shall be entitled to the same fees as are
allowed to constables for similar services . He shall be
under the same responsibility, and may be proceeded
against in the same manner as constables.
§6 . Vacancies in the officeof trusteemay be filledfor
the remainder of the term by the remaining trustees.
Vacancies in the officeof police judge , clerk, assessor,
collector,and town marshal shall be filledin the same
manner .
§7 . A majorityof the trusteesshallconstitutea quo-
rum forthe transaction of business; and when the presi-
dent of the board is absent ,the trusteesmay electone of
theirnumber as president pro tem ,whose acts shallhave
the same force and effectas those of the president.
§ 8. The election mentioned herein shall be conducted
by a sheriff, clerk, and judge of election, who shall be
appointed by the board of trustees. The judge of the
county court of Meade county shall,ten days before the
electionto be held for trustees and police judge , appoint
the officersof said election, and thereafterthe said elec-
tionofficersshallbe appointedas above provided. The
sheriffof the election shall,within three days aftersuch
election, filethe poll-book in the Meade county court
clerk'soffice,and the county judge of said county shallex-
amine said book ,and issue certificatesof election to those
elected in said town . The town marshal of said town
shall have power to execute warrants of arrest, writs,
and serve subpœnas in allcriminal or penal cases within
the jurisdictionof the police judge , not only in the town
limits,but in Meade county .
§9. For the purpose of submittingthisactto thevoters
livingwithin the corporatelimits,thereshallbe a poll

1312 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
opened on the next regularelectionday inAugust ,by the
electionofficersof the Paynesvilleprecinct,which poll-
book shall have two columns , one headed " For charter "
and the other "Against charter." Only those persons liv-
ing within the corporate limitsas setforthin sectionsec-
ond of this act shall be entitledto vote . Said pollbook
shallbe returnedwiththe otherpoll-bookstothecounty
clerk,and the vote counted in the same way and in the
same manner as the vote for county officers. If a major-
ity of those entitledto vote cast their vote for charter,
then this act shall be in force and effect; but if a ma-
jorityof the qualifiedvoters in said limitscast theirvotes
against charter, then this act shall not be in force and
effect .
§ 10. This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 10, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1492 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof J.L. Plummer ,of Simpson county .
WHEREAS , J. L. Plummer , sheriff of Simpson county
was charged with one hundred and eighty-one dollars
for school tax due the State ; and whereas , the said J.
L. Plummer advanced the money and paid said sum to
the State; and whereas , afterwards,the law under which
said tax was levied, and by virtue of which he was
charged with same , was declaredunconstitutionaland
void, and he was unable to collectthe same from the
persons assessed,and the State has received the benefit
of same ,and the said J. L. Plummer is entirelyloser;
therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be ,and he is
hereby , authorized to draw his warrant on the Treasurer
in favor of J. L. Plummer for the sum of one hundred
and eighty-one dollars,to be paid out of any money in
the Treasury not otherwise appropriated.

§ 2. This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .

CHAPTER 1493 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act to amend and reduce into one
the several acts relating to the charter and amendments thereto of the
town of Winchester," approved February second, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-two .
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That when the board of councilmen of Winches-
ter shall be unable to contract with the owner of any
land situated within the limits of the town of Winches-
ter,Kentucky ,which is necessary,or which may be re-
quired , to enable said board of councilmen to open ,
lengthen , straighten, alter, or improve any street, alley,
or sidewalk in said town , said board shall file in the
office of the clerk of the Clark county court a particular
description of the land sought to be condemned , and
may apply to said county court to appoint commission-
ers to assess the damages the owner or owners thereof
may be entitledto receive; and thereupon the court shall
appointthreeimpartialhousekeepersof saidtown,who
shall be sworn to faithfullyand impartially discharge
their duties under this act .
§2 . It shallbe the duty of said commissioners to view
the land sought to be condemned , and to award to the
owner or owners thereof the value of the land , which
shallbe stated separately; and they shallalso award the
damages , if any ,resulting to the adjacent lands of the
owner or owners ,consideringthe purposesforwhich it
istaken; but they shalldeduct from such incidentaldam-
ages the value , if any , of the advantages and benefits
that willaccrue to such adjacent lands from the construc-
tion,lengthening,altering,or improving of the proposed
streetor alley. Said commissionersshallreturna report,
in writing,to the officeof the clerkof saidcourt,stating
LOC . L., VOL . II- 83

1314 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
their award ; and they shall describe in said report the
land condemned ,and shall state therein the names of the
owners , and whether said owners are non -residents of
thisState,infants,of unsound mind ,or married women .
§3 . Upon the applicationof said board of councilmen ,
afterthe returnof said commissioners'report,the clerk
of said court shall issue process against the owners of
said land to show cause why said report should not be
confirmed ; and shall make such orders as to non -resi-
dents and persons under disabilityas are required by the
Civil Code of Practice in actions against them in the cir-
cuit court .
§ 4. At the firstregular term of the county court after
the owners shallhave been summoned the length of time
prescribedby the CivilCode of Practicebefore an an-
swer is required, it shall be the duty of the court to
examine said report; and if it shallappear to be in
conformityto thisact,and to the extent that no excер-
tions have been filedthereto by eitherparty, it shallcon-
firm said report as against the owners not excepting .
§5 . When exceptions shallbe filedby eitherparty,the
courtshallforthwithcause a jury to be empaneled to try
the issues of fact made by the exceptions. In assessing
thedamages ,the jury shallbe governed by the rulepre-
scribed in the second section of this act; and upon the
requestof eitherparty, said jury may be sent by the
court,in charge of the sheriff,to view the land sought to
be condemned . If sufficient cause be not shown for set-
ting aside the verdict,the court shallrender judgment in
conformitythereto,and shallmake such ordersas may be
proper forthe conveyance of the titleto saidland upon
the payment, or tender of the payment ,of the damages
assessed. Said jury shall possess the qualificationsre-
quiredby the laws of thisState of a petitjury, and no
citizen of the town of Winchester shall be incompetent
to situpon said jury by reason of or because of said citi-
zenship .
§ 6. Either party may appeal to the circuitcourt,or
other court of similarjurisdiction,of Clark county , from

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1315
said judgment of the county court, within thirtydays
from the renditionthereof, and the appeal shallbe tried
de novo .
§7 .Upon the confirmationof the reportof the com-
missioners by the county court,or the assessment of dam-
ages by said court as herein provided ,and the payment
or tender to the owners of the amount due as shown by
the reportof the commissioners when confirmed, or as
shown by the judgment of the county court when the
damages are assessed by said court, and all costs ad-
judged to the owner ,said board of councilmen shallbe
entitledto take possessionof said land,and to use and
control the same for the purposes for which itwas con-
demned ,as fullyas if the titlehad been conveyed to said
board of councilmen or to said town of Winchester . But
when an appeal shallbe taken from the judgment of the
county courtby saidboard of councilmen,itshallnot be
entitledto take possession of the land condemned ,unless
it shall execute to the owner of said land a bond ,with
suretyto be approved by the county court,in double the
amount of the damages assessed, conditioned to perform
the judgment of said court, and of any court to which
the case may thereafterbe appealed ,which bond shallbe
filedwith the papers in the case .
§8 . The appeal from the county court shall be taken
by filingwith the clerk of the court to which the appeal
liesa statement of the parties to the appeal and a tran-
scriptof the orders of the county court; and thereupon
the clerk of the court to which the appeal is taken shall
certifyto the clerk of the county court that said appeal
has been filed, and the clerk of the county court shall
immediatelytransferthe originalpapers to the clerk of
the court in which the appeal ispending .
§ 9. Subsection fourteenth of section six of the act to
which thisis an amendment ishereby repealed .
§ 10. The board of councilmen of Winchester shall
have power and authorityto controland grant license
for the sale of spirituous, malt, and vinous liquorsby
retailwithin the limitsof said town ; and to control and

1316 LAWS OF KENTUCKY
licenseten-pin alleys, shooting-galleries, billiard, pool,
and pigeon -tables,and all other tables which are used or
kept by the proprietorsthereof for profit, and upon which
games are played ; and to control and license all games
whatever which are set up or operated by the owners or
controllersthereof for profit; and to control and license
allflyingDutchman ,swings ,or any other contrivance set
up for amusement or pastime which isoperated or carried
on forhire or profit; and to control and licenseallshows ,
circuses ,concerts , theatrical entertainments , and all exhi-
bitions or entertainments at which an entrance fee is
charged . Said board of councilmen ishereby authorized
and empowered to fixthe amounts to be paid for said
licenses as follows,viz : For coffee-house license, not ex-
ceeding two hundred dollars ($ 200) per annum ; for
licenseto merchants to sellspirituousliquors,including
druggists who sellby the quart, and not exclusively for
medical purposes , not exceeding one hundred dollars
($ 100) per annum ; for billiard, pool, or pigeon-tables,
and allothertablesupon which games are played,which
are used or kept for profit,not exceeding fiftydollarsper
annum for each table; and any such tablekept in the
same house with a saloon, coffee-house,or other place
where liquorsare soldby retail,or kept in any house or
place contiguous thereto,shall be deemed a table kept
forprofit; forcircuses,not exceeding one hundred dollars
for each day's performance ; and for any side-shows , not
exceeding twenty-fivedollarseach, for each day's per-
formance ; for all other shows or exhibitions,including
concerts and theatricalentertainments,not exceeding five
dollars($5) per day ; and for allother licensesauthor-
ized by thissection, and not above provided for, not
exceeding ten dollars($ 10) per week : Provided,however,
That nothing in thissectionshallauthorizeany person
or persons to sellliquorsby retailin said town with-
out firstobtaining from the county court the license or
licensesnow required to be obtained by the laws of this
State; but the license or licenses last mentioned shall
confer no authority to sell spirituous, vinous , or malt

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1317
liquorswithin the limitsof said town ,unless a license
has also been obtained from said board of councilmen as
provided in thissection: And provided further, That said
board of councilmen shall, at any time ,have the power
and authorityto refuseto grant any license,and to sus-
pend or revoke any licensegranted under or by virtueof
the authority conferred by this section,when said board
shalldeem it necessary so to do ,in order to preserve the
peace or good morals of said town ; and said board of
councilmen shall be the exclusive judges of said neces-
sity.
§ 11.No person shallpresume to sellspirituous,vinous,
or malt liquors by retailwithin said town of Winches-
ter, nor to set up or operate within said town any
of the tables, alleys, games , or contrivancesnamed in
the tenth section of this act ; nor to exhibit any of the
shows , circuses, concerts, theatrical or other entertain-
ments named in said section,without firstobtaining the
license therein required; and said board of councilmen
shall have fullpower and authority, by the enactment
of ordinances or by -laws ,to fix penalties for the failure
to comply with the above provisions of this section,as
follows,to-wit : For sellingsaid liquorswithin said town
without firstobtaining said license, not exceeding fifty
dollars($50), and each sale of liquorshallbe deemed
a separate offense; for exhibiting a circus within said
town without firstobtaining said license,not exceeding
three hundred dollars ($300), and each exhibition shall
be deemed a separate offense; for exhibiting any other
of the above named shows ,concerts,theatrical,or other
entertainments, within said town , without firstobtaining
said license, not exceeding twenty-fivedollars($25) for
each day's exhibition; forsettingup or operating within
said town any of the tables, alleys, games , or contriv-
ances aforesaid,without firstobtaining said licenses,not
exceeding twenty-five dollars($25) for each day the
same shallbe so set up or operated .
12. Said board of councilmen shall have the ordi-
nances and by -laws of the town of Winchester which

1318 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
are of a general nature ,printed and published in a book
or pamphlet ; and any amendments or additions to such
ordinances or by -laws of a general nature which shall
be adopted or ordainedby saidboard, afterthe publica-
tionof saidbook or pamphlet,shallbe printedin some
newspaper published in said town ; and the ordinances ,
rules and regulations for the government of said town
which went into effecton the firstday of July,one thou-
sand eighthundred and seventy-four,subjectto changes
and alterationsmade therein subsequent to said day ,
shallconstitutethe by -laws and ordinances of said town
untiltheprintingand publicationof the ordinancesand
by -laws above provided for shall be completed . Section
(32) thirty-two of the act to which this act is an amend-
ment ishereby repealed.
§13 . The recorder's court of Winchester shall have
exclusivejurisdictionto try all breaches and violations
of the ordinances and by -laws of said town of Winches-
ter .
§14.This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884.
CHAPTER 1494 .
AN ACT to establishand incorporateSouth Covington districtin Kenton
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That so much of the county of Kenton as may be
embraced within the following boundary ,to-wit : Begin-
ning at a point on the Licking riverwhere a ravine,just
north of the Blick and Phillips' coal elevator, empties
into the same ; thence in a westerly direction to the
center of the Bank Lick Turnpike Road where same
makes an abrupt curve near said ravine; thence with
the center of the same southwesterly to the intersec-
tion of Latonia avenue with said pike; thence along
the center of said avenue to the center of the Louisville
and NashvilleRailroad track; thence southwardly with

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1319
the center of the same to Bank Lick creek ; thence with
the meanders of the same to where it empties into the
said Licking river; thence from the same to the place
of beginning,ishereby establishedas a separatejustices'
and election districtin said county ; and the inhabitants
thereof are created a body -politicand corporate,by the
name and style of the " South Covington District," in
Kenton county, Kentucky , for the purposes hereinafter
mentioned .
§2 . Two justicesof the peace and one constable shall
be elected for said districtby the qualifiedvoters thereof,
at the times and in the manner and having the qualifica-
tion requiredby the Constitutionand laws of thisCom-
monwealth ; and the elections for said district shall be
held at a suitable place, to be selected by the trustees
of said district; said voting place may be chosen by the
trustees, and noticethereof shallbe given by posting
written noticesin fiveor more public places in said dis-
trictfor ten days before said election is to be held. The
electionforjusticesand constable hereunder shallbe held
on firstMonday in August , one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four .
§3 . The government of said corporation shallbe con-
finedto a board of five(5) trustees,having the qualifica-
tion of owners of real estate,and resident within said
district,who shall be chosen ,once in two years,on the
firstMonday in August,by the qualifiedresidentvoters
within said district,and who shallserve for two years,
and untiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified; the
firstelection hereunder for trusteesto be in August , one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-six. Said trustees
may fillvacanciesin theirbody by appointing persons
qualifiedto fillthe office,and shallenter upon the duties
of their officeafter being sworn to faithfullydischarge
the duties devolving upon them .
§ 4. Said trusteesmay selectone of theirnumber chair-
man ,who shallpreside at theirmeetings ; and in case of
his absence ,they may select a chairman pro tem. They
shall select a clerk,treasurer,collector, and other corpo

1320 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
rationofficers,and appoint policemen fortheplace,and
remove same at discretion, and appoint others in their
stead. They may meet at such times and places as they
may deem proper,or as the chairman may appoint,upon
petition from two (2) of the board ; and a majority of
those elected shall constitute a quorum to do business.
They shallkeep a record of theirproceedings .
They may pass such by -laws ,rules and regulations for
thepreservationof the health,good government ,and po-
liceprotection of the persons and property of the district
as they may deem proper , and not in conflictor incon-
sistent with the Constitution or laws of the State , and
provide for theirobservance by adequate penaltiesfor a
violationof the same ,to be enforced before a justice of
the peace ; and allfines and forfeiturestherein collected
shallbe paid to the treasurerof the board,to be used in
improving the public thoroughfares within said district.
They may make regulations to prevent stock of any
kind from running at large in said district,provided they
think it necessary ; and may provide a pound for im-
pounding stock that may be taken up , and the cost of
impounding shallbe a lien on the stock,and to be dis-
charged by the owner thereof, and the same may be
enforced before a justice of the peace in said district, in
such manner as the by laws thereof may provide .
They shall have the management and control of the
public roads in said district, except turnpike roads oper-
ated by companies running into or through said district,
and shallkeep the same in good repair,and forthat pur-
pose a tax of ten (10) cents on the one hundred ($ 100)
dollars' worth of all the real estate in said district, which

sage .

CHAPTER 1506 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Deep Creek Bridge and Gravel Switch Turnpike
Road Company , in Washington and Marion counties.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That a company be,and is hereby,incorporated,
under the name and styleof the Deep Creek Bridge and
Gravel Switch Turnpike Road Company ; and by said
name and styleshall have perpetualsuccession; and
may sue and be sued , plead and be impleaded ; and as
such is hereby vested with allthe powers , rights,and
privilegesenjoyedby the most favored of similarcorpo-
rations in this Commonwealth .
§2. The capitalstockof saidcompany shallnot exceed
the amount necessary to construct said road and toll-
houses , and shall be divided into shares of twenty -five
dollars each .
§3 . The following persons are appointed commission-
ers to receive subscriptionof stock to said company , viz :
S. H. Coyle , William Best,G. W. Russell,J. D. Harmon ,
Nathan Pipes, M. T. Harmon , Nelson Hays , and S. H.
Pirtle; and said persons,or any four of them ,are author-
ized to open books and receive subscription at any time
or place they may deem proper . They shall insert in a
book providedforthatpurpose the followingobligation:
" We ,whose names are hereunto subscribed,promise to
pay to the president and directors of the Deep Creek
Bridge and Gravel Switch Turnpike Road Company the
sum of twenty-fivedollarsforeach share of stockby us
subscribed and set opposite our respective names , in
such proportions,and at such times,as shall be required
by said corporation." When they think a sufficient
amount of stock has been subscribed to justifythe be-
ginning of said road,they shall,at such time and place
as they may deem proper , call a meeting of the stock-
holders , and hold an election for a president and five
directors. One vote in person or by proxy shall be al-
lowed for each share of stock; and the officersso elected
shall continue in officefor one year ,or until their suc

1332 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
cessorsare elected and qualified. The manner of giving
the notice, and the place of elections, after the first,
shallbe fixed by the president and directors; but the day
of electionshallbe the second Saturday in May each
year .
§4 . The county courts of Washington and Marion
counties shall have power , a majority of the justices in
each being present and concurring therein,to subscribe a
sum not exceeding one thousand dollars per mile to the
capital stock of said road ,to be regarded as other stock.
§ 5. The beginning of thisroad shallbe from a point
on the Mackville and PerryvilleTurnpike Road ,at or near
the Deep Creek Bridge ,in Washington county ,and shall
be located on the most practicable route to a point on
the Lebanon and PerryvilleTurnpike Road ,near Gravel
Switch ,in Marion county .
§6 . The land belonging to this road shall not be less
than thirtyfeet wide , the grade shall not be less than
eighteen feet wide , and the metal , stone or gravel, or
both ,shall not be less than fourteen feet wide ,and not
less than nine inches deep .
§7 . The president and directorsshall have power to
begin work at either or both points,as they may think
best ; and whenever two and one -half continuous miles
have been completed of said road ,from eitherpoint, and
receivedby them ,they shallhave power to erecta gate
and collecttolls, and likewise for every two and one -half
continuous miles thereaftercompleted and receivedby
them; but the ratesof tollshallnot exceed those pre-
scribedby general law ; said tollsto be used for the com-
pletionof and keeping up of said road: Provided, The
tollscollectedat the gate or gates in Washington county
shallnot be appliedtothecompletionof saidroad,or any
portionthereof,in Marion county,and viceversa. If,after
the road is completed and kept up,there should be any
profits,then saidprofitsshallbe paid annuallyto the
stockholders in said road .
§ 8. Every presidentand directorof said road com-
pany shall,beforehe enterson the duties of his office,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1333
swear or affirm,beforesome person authorizedto admin-
isteroaths,that he willwell and trulydischarge the duties
of his said officeto the best of his judgment ,without par-
tialityor prejudice.
§9. Itshallbe the duty of the presidentand directors
to appoint one of theirdirectorstreasurer and secretary,
who shallexecute bond ,with approved security,and shall
be responsiblefor all moneys of the company ,subject to
the order of the president; and he shallalso keep an ac-
count of the receipts and expenditures of said company
in a book kept forthat purpose,which shallbe open to
the inspection of any stockholder in said company .
§ 10.The presidentand directorsshallhave power and
authority to agree with and appoint all such survey-
ors,engineers,and other officersand agents necessary to
carry on to the completion of said road , and itskeeping
in repair ; they may adopt such by -laws as may be nec-
essary for the control and proper management of said
work during its progress, and after itscompletion : Pro-
vided, The said by -laws shall not be inconsistentwith
the Constitution and laws of this Commonwealth or of
the United States or this act .
§ 11. The president may call meetings of the directors
at such time and places as he may think proper . Three
of said directors and president, or four of the directors
in the absence of the president,shallconstitutea quorum
for the transactionof business. They shallkeep a record
of alltheirproceedings,to be entered in a book provided
for that purpose,and they shallbe signed by the presi-
dent , after firstbeing approved by the directorspresent ;
and in case of the death or absence of the president, the
directors shall elect a president pro tempore; and they
may adjourn from time to time as they may think proper .
§ 12. No person or personsshallbe requiredto pay toll
at any of the gates erected going to dr returning from
religious worship on the Sabbath ,or in going to or re-
turning from a funeral,and no person or persons over
whose land the said road may run shallbe charged toll,

1334 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
in theirdailyoccupation,forgoing through the gates,in
passing from one part of the farm to another .
§ 13. The president and directorsshalldeliver a certifi-
cate,signed by the president and treasurer, and sealed by
the seal of the corporation, if they have any , to each
share-holder, for each share by him or her subscribed and
held, whenever the full amount of each has been paid ,
which certificate shall be transferable on the books of
said company in person or by attorney. The original
certificateof the share or shares transferred shall be sur-
rendered, and a new certificategiven to the purchaser ,
who shall be entitledto all the benefits and privileges
that would have accrued to the original owner .
§ 14.No person who is not a share-holderin saidroad
corporationshallbe allowed to vote in any election,nor
shallany person be eligibleto the officeof presidentor
director of said road who is not the owner of at least
one whole share of stock in said road .
§ 15. Itshallbe the privilegeof each person who may
have subscribed stock to work out the value of his said
stock on the road -bed , at the customary prices for such
work . They must,however ,work under the directionof
the president and directorsof said company ,or their au-
thorized agents .
§ 16. Wherever the words the president and directors
appear herein,they shall be understood as construed in
section eleven of this act .
§ 17. This act shall take effectand be in force at and
from the date of itspassage .
Approved May 10 , 1884 .

or the county courts,in cases of a county or precinct,"
after the following words in same section : " it shall be
the duty of the county judge or courts,in cases of any
town , city, or county ," and before the words " and the
president of the board of trustees,in case of any town :"
Provided , however, This amendment shall apply only to
the county of Jefferson: Provided, No precinct under
this act shall subscribe for more than twenty thousand
dollarsof the stock of said corporation.
§ 2. If said railroad,as provided forherein,shallnot be
located on or before January first, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-seven , all rightsunder this act and
amendments shall lapse .
§ 3. This actshalltakeeffectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 10, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1509 .
AN ACT relatingto the outstanding railroad tax receiptsof Simpson
county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1.That allpersonsholdinggenuine receiptsfortaxes
paid on propertyin Simpson county in aid of the Lou-
isvilleand Nashville Railroad , which have not been
converted into the stock of the company ,or previously
adjustedby the county,may ,on or beforeNovember first,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four, filesuch
receiptswith the clerk of the Simpson county court, and
have them registeredfor the purpose of applying to
the county court for the adjustment thereof.
§2 . The clerk shall make a record of all such claims
so presented in a book , or upon a listto be kept for
that purpose ,showing the date and amount of each re-
ceipt,the originaltax-payer ,and the present claimant or
owner .
§3 . The county court of said county ,at itsNovember
term ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,may
deal with and provide for the adjustment of claims so
filedand registeredas to itmay seem just and equitable;
this act in nowise binding the county to the payment
thereof,furtherthan itis already bound .
§ 4. That all persons or claimants who shall fail to
so present and have registeredtheir claims for taxes as
here provided by November first, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-four, shall be deemed as having
abounded and surrendered same , and the county court
of said county shall not be held in law or equity com-
pelledto recognizethe same ,or provide in anywise for
the payment of the same .
§ 5. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 10, 1884..

CHAPTER 1514 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the town of Cave City, in Barren county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be the duty of the trustees of Cave
City,in Barren county, Kentucky ,upon the petitionof
ten or more voters of said town , addressed to them ,re-
questing the election hereinafter ordered, to submit to
the qualifiedvoters of said town the question whether
spirituous,vinous , or malt liquors shall be sold in said
[town ] under a licenseissuedtherefor.
§2 . That upon the presentationof the petitionmen-
tioned in the preceding section to the board of trustees
of said town ,they (the said trustees) shall,within thirty
days thereafter,and on a day to be by them fixed,by
order on theirrecord-book,directand cause said elec-
tionto be heldby the qualifiedvotersof saidtown ,upon
the question of licenseor no license, for the retailing
spirituous, vinous, or malt liquorsin said town . Said

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1341
trustees shall,by order, appoint the officersof said elec-
tion .
§3 . That it shall be the duty of the judges holding
said election to return to said board of trustees a certi-
fiedstatement of the resultof said election,together with
the poll-book thereof, within two days aftersaid election;
and the said board shall,upon the third day thereafter,
compare the poll-book of said election, and by order on
their order -book announce the result of said election .
§ 4. Before said election shall be held,itshall be the
duty of said board of trustees to cause notice of the
time and place of said election to be given by written
notices posted at fiveor more public places in said town .
§5 . That any officerholding said electionor voter
votingthereinshallbe subjectedto allthepenaltiesnow
denounced by the laws of thisStateforcorruptlyreceiv-
ing and permitting an illegalvote to be knowingly cast,
or for casting an illegal vote .
§ 6. That if a majority of the votes at said elec-
tion be cast in favor of license,then it shallbe the duty
of the trusteesof said town to grant to any applicant
therefor a licenseto retailvinous or malt liquors within
said town for one year ; but before granting the same ,
said trusteesshallrequire of the applicant therefor proof
of said applicant'sgood character for honesty and good
demeanor .
§7 . That the trusteesof said town shall,in addition to
the State tax,charge for such license not lessthan fifty
nor more than one hundred and fiftydollars.
§ 8. That allmoney accruing from said licenseshallbe
paid over by saidtrusteesto the common school commis-
sioner of Barren county , to be held by him under the
bond given by him as common school commissioner for
said county,and be paid out by him in the supportand
maintenance of the common school in the common school
district in which said town is located .
§.9. Before said board of trusteesshallgrant said license,
it shallrequirethe applicantthereforto enterintobond ,
with approved security, before them ,to the Common-

1342 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
wealth of Kentucky ,conditionedthat the applicantwill
keep an orderlyhouse,thathe or she willnot permit any
one to tipple or drink to excess in said house wherein he
or she islicensed to conduct his or her business ; that he
or she will not sell,loan, or give to any minor or known
inebriateany spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors, or the
mixture of either ; and that in default thereof that the
applicant will pay to any one aggrieved on account
thereof any damages which he or she may sustain there-
by .
§ 10.That any one aggrievedby a violationof the pro-
visionsof said bond ,eitherfather or mother (if the father
be dead ) of an infant,or the wife of an inebriate,or the
Commonwealth of Kentucky ,may institutean action on
said bond to recover the damages that may resultto any
of said partiesby reason of a violationof said bond ,and
the State by itsattorney may institutea penal action on
said bond to recover any penalty that may have been
incurredby the party licensedby a violationof his said
bond .
§ 11. That in any action on said bond by the father or
mother of an infant , or the wife of an inebriate , it shall
be competent forthe plaintiffto introduceand prove,by
way of ascertainment of the damages ,the mortification
and sufferingin mind occasioned by the drinking and
tipplingof the minor or husband , as well as any loss that
may have occurred by reason of losttime from labor.
§ 12. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage ; and
allacts in conflictherewith are repealed .
Approved May 8 ,1884 .

byterian Church , of Louisville," approved February twentieth, one thou-

CHAPTER 1517 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Woodbine and Cumberland Gap RailroadCom-
pany .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That John H. Wilson ,M. J. Moss ,and W. G. Coul-
son , of Pineville, Kentucky ; Henry Skiles and G. U. Mc-
Elroy, of Bowling Green , Kentucky ; Grant Green , of
Frankfort, Kentucky ; J. T. Edgerly, John Brogan , P.
Hinkle, J. T. Gibson, and Wm . Lock , of Barbourville,
Kentucky ; and J. B. Walton , of Detroit, Michigan, and
theirassociatesand successors,be,and they hereby are,
createda body -politicand corporate,under the name and
styleof the Woodbine and Cumberland Gap Narrow-
gauge RailroadCompany , for the purpose of construct-
ing, equipping, and operatinga narrow-gauge railroad
from Woodbine ,or some point near Woodbine , on the
Knoxville Branch of the Louisville and Nashville Rail-
road,to Cumberland Gap ,on the most practicableroute

1344 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
through the counties of Knox and Bell, in the State
of Kentucky , to be determined by the president and
board of directors of said company ; and as such , in its
corporate capacity,sue and be sued , plead and be im-
pleaded , in allcourts; and to have and exercise, in addi-
tionto those hereby expresslygranted, alllawful rights
and privilegesusual and incidentto such corporations,
and requisiteto carry out the objectsand purposes of
this act .
§ 2. The business of said company shallbe conducted
under the immediate direction of a board of directors,
consisting of not less than five (5) members nor more
than nine (9) members ,to be electedby the stockholders
from among themselves ; and said board of directors shall
elect one of their number who shallbe president of the
board , and also president of said railroadcompany .
§3 . The board of directorsmay elect a vice president,
secretary,and treasurer,and such otherofficers,and may
appoint such agents as they may deem necessaryand
proper forthe management of the business of said com-
pany ; and they may fixthe term of officeand salaryof
each,and may requiresuch officersto providebond ,with
security,forthe faithfulperformance of his duties: Pro-
vided, That no officershallhold formore than two years,
or untilhis successor iselected and qualified.
§ 4. The board of directors may , from time to time ,
frame and adopt such by -laws for the regulation of the
business of said company ,and fixthe rates of freightand
passenger transportationover said road, as they may
deem necessary and proper ,subject to such general laws
of thisState and the United States as do now or may
hereafter exist or be enacted for the regulation of such
rates .
§5 . The company may receive,by donation or by pur-
chase, alllands necessaryfor its road-bed, side-tracks,
depot and warehouse grounds , and all other legitimate
purposes , and also all material necessary for the con-
structionand maintenance of said road and itsappur-
tenances; and ifthe lands and materialsnecessary for

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1345
itsroad-bed,side-tracks,depots,and warehouse grounds,
waste-banks,and borrow-pitscannot be procuredby vol-
untary agreement with the owners thereof, said owners
being competent to contract, but if the owners , being
competent ,refuse to contract,or any of them being femes
covert,or minors ,or non compos,or are out of the county
in which the property wanted is situated,applicationmay
be made by thecompany or itsagent to the county court
of any county into or through which itis proposed to
construct said railroad; said court shall appoint three
commissioners ,one of whom shallbe an engineer of said
company ,and the remaining two shallbe residentsof the
county in which the property is situated,who shallbe
sworn to dischargetheirdutiesunder thisactjustlyand
impartially,to the best of their skilland judgment . It
shallbe the duty of said commissioners to view the land
or materials required and fixthe amount of compensation
to which the owner or owners may be entitled,and make
out and return to the officeof the county clerk of said
county a report in writing, particularlydescribing the
lands or materials valued and the amount of damages ,if
any ,assessed. The clerkshallreceivesuch reportand
filethe same ,indorsing thereon the time of itsbeing filed;
and if such owner or owners residein the county ,he shall
forthwith issue a summons commanding the owner or
owners of such lands or inaterialsto appear and show
cause why the reportshallnot be confirmed,which sum-
mons shallbe forthwith executed by the sheriffor other
officerin whose hands it shallbe placed. The report
shall be docketed in the county court,and shallstand for
trialat any term of said court,commencing at any term
not less than ten days after service of the summons ,it
being understood that the county judge may act in all
cases where the county court is mentioned in this chap-
ter. If the owner is not a resident of the county , the
court may ,at the firstterm afterthe reportis filed,ap-
point an attorneyto defend forsaidowner ,who may file
exceptionsat any time not laterthan the followingterm ;
LOC , L., VOL . 11-85

1346 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and upon exceptions being filedby the owner or his or
her attorney,or by the company ,the court shallforthwith
order a jury to be empaneled to try the question of com-
pensation or damages ,unless,forgood cause shown ,time
is given for preparation. If no exception is filed, the
report shallbe confirmed . The jury and commissioners
shall,in estimatingthe damages to any proprietorof land
or materials, after fixing a just compensation therefor,
take into considerationthe advantages accruing ,or which
may accrue ,to said owner from the construction of the
proposed railroad. Said corporation shallnot be delayed
in progressingwith itswork by proceedingsrequired in
this section,but may enter upon or use the land or real
estate, and take and use the earth,gravel,stone,timber ,
and other materials necessary for its use , as well before
as after such inquest of damages , firstnotifying the
owner or owners of the same , and causing an account
to be kept of the material used to date of inquest of dam-
ages .
§6 Said company may adopt a common seal,to be
affixedby the secretary to all documents requiring the
signature of the president, and they may alteror change
the same whenever a majority of the board of directors
may see proper to do so.
§7 . The capitalstock of the said company shallbe
seven hundred and fiftythousand ($750,000) dollars, to
be divided in sharesof one hundred ($ 100) dollarseach .
§8 . The corporators,or a majorityof them heretofore
named ,are authorized to open books for subscriptionto
the capital stock at such places and times, and may
continue them open for such time as they may deem
necessary. The capitalstock of said company is here-
by declaredto be personalproperty,and may be issued,
certified,transferred,and registeredin such manner ,and
at such places as may be ordered and provided for in
the by -laws of said company .
§9. When seventy-fivethousand ($75,000) dollarsof
bona fide stock shall have been subscribed, the stock-
holders,or a majority of them ,shallcalla meeting ,which

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1347
shallproceed to electa board of directors, and shall
be entitledto one (1) vote for each share so subscribed:
Provided, That where a county shall take subscription
to the capitalstock itsvote shallbe cast by the pre-
siding judge ; at such meeting of the stockholders they
may transact such other business as may be necessary
for a perfect organization of the company and the ad-
vancement of the enterprise.
§ 10. Upon writtenapplicationof the board of direct-
ors to the county court of any county along the said line
of road,and especiallythe countiesof Knox and Bell,it
shall be the duty of said court to order an election to
take the sense of the qualifiedvoters as to whether said
county shallissue bonds to be exchanged for stock of
said railroad,to an amount to be specifiedin such appli-
cation of said road ; but in no case to exceed one hun-
dred thousand ($ 100,000) dollarsforeach county. The
county reserving a right to exchange said stock at any
time the county court may deem necessary for an equal
amount of firstmortgage bonds of said railroad com-
pany . These electionsshallbe held in the same manner
as the electionsfor county officers, and the returns shall
be made and the records kept in the same way as in such
elections. Should a majority of the votes cast be in favor
of theproposition,thecountyjudge shallcausetobe issued
such bonds ,and shallsign them , and they shallbe coun-
tersigned by the clerk and treasurerof said county ,and
shall not run for more than thirty(30) years,nor bear
interestat a greater rate than eight (8) per cent.,payable
annually .
§ 11. When authorized to do so,by a majority of the
stockholdersat any regular or called meeting of the
stockholders, the president and board of directors may
issue the bonds of the said railroadcompany , bearing
interestat any rate not exceeding eight(8) per cent.per
annum . The payment of the principaland interestof
said bonds shallbe securedby such hypothecationof the
property of said company as may be necessary; but the
aggregate amount of such bonds shall in no case exceed
the capitalstockof saidcompany .

1348 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§12. The saidcompany ,when authorizedby a majority
of the stockholders,may change the gauge of said road
as they may deem proper; and may increasethe capital
stock of said company to any sum not exceeding two
million($ 2,000,000) dollars.
§ 13. The saidWoodbine and Cumberland Gap Rail-
road Company may lease theirsaid road to any other
railroad company , or may connect with or lease other
roads, so as to form connecting lines in this State , or
with roads in other States .
§ 14. Said railroad company may take and hold all
real estate that may be donated to it by individuals, or
subscribed as capital stock,and may dispose of the same
by the board of directorsby sale, by lease,or by mort-
gage , as may seem best to said board of directors.
§ 15. Said company may construct, or purchase and
own , telegraph lines near the line of their road , or may
become owners of stock in any company which may con-
struct the same .
§ 16. The directors of said company may make con-
tracts,which shall be signed by the president, and at-
testedby the secretary,with any corporation,individual
or individuals,for constructing or equipping said railroad
and branches ,or any part thereof,and shallpay for the
same wholly or partiallyin stock,bonds ,or other prop-
erty of said company ,in such manner and upon such
conditionsas may be deemed for the best interestsof said
company .
§ 17. That copies from the minutes of the proceedings
of the board of directors,certifiedby the secretary,and
copies of the by -laws adopted by the board ,when so cer-
tified, shall be received in all tribunals in the State as
evidence as fullyand to the same extent as would the
books of the company .
§ 18. The general officesof the company shall be lo-
cated at Barbourville,Kentucky .
§19 . The privatepropertyof the stockholdersshallbe
exempt from liabilitieson account of the debts of said
company .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1349
§20 . The president and directors of said company
may , if they so desire, construct, as herein provided , a
narrow -gauge road in place of a standard gauge .
§ 21. This act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved May 12, 1884.
CHAPTER 1518 .

trict, of Fayette county .

of Kentucky :

Road . The said place of voting shallbe known as poll-

electionsof said county .

Lexington Railroad ,are required to vote at polling place

side of said railroad shall vote at pollingplace number

4 The sheriffof Fayette county is required to cause
fiftynotices to be printed, in large type ,and cause said
notices to be posted throughout said magisterialdistrict
for at least ten days before the next regular election,

of time in at leasttwo papers publishedin the cityof
Lexington .

sage .

pany shallhave a lienon the stockto secure any indebt-

and open books and receive subscriptionsto the capital
stock; and when shares to the amount of twenty thou-
sand dollarsshallhave [been] subscribed,itshallbe their
duty to give notice and appoint a day for the election of
a board of directors, who shall hold their office until the
succeedingannual election. The payment forthe shares
subscribed shall be as follows : one dollar on each share
at the time of subscribing,and the residue in installments
of twenty -fiveper cent.on the amount : Provided, That
when ten thousand dollars shallbe paid in,the directors
may ,ifthey thinkproper,extend the time forpaying the
remainder , and may thereupon commence business- the
president firstmaking affidavitthat such sum has actu-
allybeen paid in.
§ 4. The capitalstock of said company shallnot ex-
ceed one hundred thousand dollars, and shall be divided
into shares of one hundred dollars each .
§5 . Said company may receivedepositsofgold,silver,
and bank notes ,and other notes which may be circulating
as money lawfully,and repay the same in such time and
manner ,and with such interest,as may be agreed upon
with the depositorsby specialor general contract; may
loan money ,buy and sellgold,silver,exchange,promis-
sory notes, bonds , stocks, billsof exchange , and other
evidencesof debt, merchandise,and produce; take per-
sonal and other securitiesfor the payment thereof,and
dispose of the latter in all respects as a natural person
may do on the non -payment of the debt or demand ,sub-
ject to the provisions of an act,entitled"An act for the
benefitofthe incorporatedbanks ofKentucky ," approved
March sixteenth,one thousand eight hundred and seven-
ty-one; and the promissorynotesor billsmade negotiable
and payable at its office, or at any other officeor bank-
ing-house, which may be discountedby said company ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1353 .
and inlandbillswhich may be discountedby it,shallbe,
and they are hereby,put upon the footingof foreignbills
of exchange ,and likeremedies may be had thereon,jointly
and severally,againstthe drawers and indorsers.
§ 6. The saidcompany may acquire,hold,possess,use,
enjoy,and occupy allsuch real estate,goods and chat-
tels, as may be convenient for the transactionof its
business,or which may be conveyed to it as surety for
any debt,or purchased in satisfactionof any debt,judg-
ment ,or decree, and selland convey the same ; and they
may make all needful by -laws for the government of
said company ,not inconsistentwith the Constitutionof
the United States or of the State of Kentucky .
§ 7. It shall be the duty of the president,on the first
Monday in January of each year,to pay into the treas-
ury of the State fiftycentson each one hundred dollars
of itspaid -in capital in fullof alltaxes.
§ 8. Said cocompany shallhave power to make ad-
vances on approved securitiesof every kind ; upon the
agricultural,mechanical , and other products of the coun-
try, includinggrowing crops,merchandise,and personal
property ; it may receive United States vouchers , ware-
house receipts, billsof lading, bonds , stocks, notes, pro-
duce, and merchandisein pledge for the securityof
money loaned , debts owing , or liabilitiesthat may be .
come due to said company , and sell the same on the
non -payment of the debt or demand ,subject to the pro-
visions of an act , entitled " An act for the benefit of the
incorporatedbanks of Kentucky ," approved March the
sixteenth, one thousand eight hundred and seventy-one .
§ 9. Every stockholder shall be individually liable to
the creditorsof said company to the full amount of the
stock subscribed by him ; but when the same shallhave
been paid for in full by him , there shall be no further
individualliabilityto the stockholder.
§ 10. The indebtednessof this company over and
above that incurred for depositors of money shall at no
time exceed their paid-up capital stock.
§ 11. No loan or discount shall be made to any stock-
holder to pay any call or balance due on stock; nor

1354 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shall it be lawful for said company to issue any note
or billto circulateas money .
§12 . That when stockholders failto make the pay-
ments for stock subscribed, or failto pay the calls at the
time and in the manner prescribed by the directors, the
board may , afterthe expirationof thirtydays, upon
giving due noticeto said delinquentstockholders, pro-
ceed to sellthe shares of stock of said delinquents; and
allpayments which may have been made thereon shall
be forfeitedto the use and benefitof said company .
§ 13. It shall be lawful for said company to buy its
own stock in payment of debt,judginent,or decree,but
shall resell the same as soon as it is convenient .
§ 14. This act shall take effectfrom its passage , and
remain in force for thirty years from the time of its
approval ; but if said company is not organized within
two years thereafter, thisact shallbe void.
§ 15. The Legislaturereservesthe rightto alter,amend ,
or repeal thischarter at pleasure.
Approved May 9, 1884.
CHAPTER 1522 .
AN ACT to incorporatetheKiddvilleand Thomson's StationTurnpike Road
Company ,of Clark county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the persons who may subscribe stock for the
purpose of constructing a turnpike road ,from a point at
or near J. H. Goff's residence , on the Winchester and
Red River IronWorks Turnpike Road ,by way of Kidd-
ville,to Thomson's Station,on the Elizabethtown,Lex-
ington,and Big Sandy Railroad,and on to Sewell'sShop ,
if the directorsso determine, be, and they are hereby ,

and style of the Kiddville and Thomson Station Turn-
pike Road Company ; and by thatname sue and be sued,

privileges incident to corporations created by the laws

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1355
of this Commonwealth for the purpose of constructing
turnpike roads .

ment of five directors, one of whom shall be chosen

such other officesas may be needed in the conduct of

fill same . Said directors shall be stockholders in said
company , and shall be elected annually by the stock-
holders on a day appointed by the commissioners herein
named , and afterwards on such days as may be desig-
nated by the board of directors.
§3 . That Samuel T. Hadden ,J. A. Eubank ,E. Y. Beall,
Stephen Jett, A. F. Duckworth , H. P. Thomson , Robert
Nelson ,W. F. Prewitt,and T. J. Wilkerson ,or any three
of them ,be ,and they are hereby , appointed commission-
ers to open books and receive subscriptionsto the capital
stock of said company ,which shallconsistof not exceed-
ing three thousand dollars per mile,divided into shares
of one hundred dollarseach . Said commissioners ,or any
three of them ,may call a meeting to organize said com-
pany whenever they so please. Said company may con-
demn land or material needed in the construction of its
turnpike road as is now provided by law with reference
to railroadsand other corporations. Said company may
erect a toll-gate on its turnpike road when so much as
two and one half miles thereofiscompleted,and collect
tollsthereat,according to the rates now established by
law for such companies .
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 9, 1884.

CHAPTER 1523 .
AN ACT to prevent the sale, loan,exchange ,barter, or gift of spirituous,
vinous, or malt liquors,or any mixture thereof, within common school
districtnumber thirty-five, including the town of Murray , in Calloway
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1.That itshallbe unlawfulforany person or persons
to sell,loan,exchange,barter,or give away any spiritu-
ous ,vinous ,or malt liquors,or any mixture thereof,within
common school districtnumber thirty-five,including the
town of Murray ,in Calloway county .
§2.That ifany person or personsshallviolatethe pro-
visions of this act,such person or persons shall, upon in-
dictment and conviction thereof in the Calloway circuit
court,for each offense, be fined the sum of one hundred
dollars, or confined in the jail of said county for the
period of thirtydays ,or both so fined and imprisoned , in
the direction of the court or jury trying the case; but if
the fine be for money alone ,and the defendant fails to
pay or replevythe same ,he or she shallbe committed
to the jail of said county ,there to remain until, at the
rateof two dollarsper day,saidfineisfullydischarged.
§3 . That ,in addition to the penalties provided in the
second sectionof thisact,every husband,wife,child,pa-
rent,brother,sister,guardian ,or employer who shall be
injuredin person,property,or means of support,by any
intoxicatedperson ,or in consequence of the intoxication
of any person ,in whole or in part,shallhave a right of
actiontherefor,and may enforcethe same in any court
of thisCommonwealth having jurisdictionthereof, in his
or her name ,severallyor jointly,againstany person or
personswho shall,by selling,loaning,exchanging, bar-
tering,or giving away such intoxicatingliquors,or any
mixture thereof, within said district,have caused the in-
toxication,in whole or in part,of such person,and shall
recover of such person or persons so selling,loaning , ex-
changing,bartering,or givingaway any such liquorsor
the mixtures thereofsuch damages to person and prop-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1357
ertyor means of supportas shallbe sustainedby reason
thereof; and any married woman shallhave the right to
sue under this act as if she were a feme sole, and recover
and control,in her own name ,and for her own use and
benefit,any money recovered by her under this act, and
make any and allcontractsin regard to same as a feme
sole .
§4. The provisions of thisact shallnot operate against
any person now sellingliquorsin said districtunder a
license untilthe expiration of such license.
§ 5. This act shall not take effect or be in force until it
be submitted to, and ratifiedby , the voters of the said
districtat a special electionto be held at the court-house
in Murray ,on the thirdSaturday in June,one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-four, and between the hours of
Sıx o'clock in the forenoon and seven o'clock in the after-
noon . The officersfor holding said election shall be
chosen in the same manner as officers are now selected
for holding general elections . At which time it shall be
submitted to the legal voters of said district; and it shall
be the duty of the officersholding said electionsto open
a poll for the purpose of taking the sense of the legal
voters in said districtupon the proposition whether or
not any spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsshallbe sold,
loaned ,exchanged ,bartered, or given away therein; and
it shallbe the duty of the sheriff,or other officerholding
said election,to propound to each voter who may vote
this question : " Are you in favor of the sale, loan , ex-
change , barter, or gift of spirituous, vinous , or malt
liquorsin common school districtnumber thirty-five?"
and his vote shall be recorded for or against it, as he
directs. The pollshallbe returned as otherpollsin reg-
ular elections,and shall be examined and compared by
the same officers .
§ 6. If it shall be found that a majority of the legal
votes cast at the said election were given against the
sale,loan ,exchange ,barter, or gift of spirituous, vinous ,
or malt liquorsin saiddistrict,itshallbe the duty of the
examining board to certifythat lact; which certificate

1358 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shallbe deliveredto the clerk of the county court,and by
him duly recorded(and a certifiedcopy thereofshallbe
prima facieevidence in all proceedingsunder this act),
and then this act shall be in full force and effect.
§ 7. It shallbe the duty of the sheriffor officerholding
said electionto firstadvertiseand give notice of the time ,
place ,and object of said electionfor at leasttwenty days
before the day of the election by posting printed notices
thereof at the court-house door , in Murray , and at at
leastfiveother public places in said district.
§ 8. All acts or parts of acts in conflictwith this act
are hereby repealed.
Approved May 9 , 1884.
CHAPTER 1524 .

after be , conferred upon the sheriffsof this Common-
wealth .
§3 . The marshal shall have the same power as con-
stable to execute warrants and collect debts within the

the courtsof Warren county: Provided, He shallnot be

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1359
marshal; and forallactsof such deputy the marshal shall

late and prescribe the duties and salary of the marshal
and fees for all services rendered by him for said city,
