chiefof police,such person shallbe vestedwith likepow-
ers as are herein conferred upon the marshal of said city.
§8 . All appeals from ordersof the board of council.
men opening new streetsor widening old streetsshallbe
taken to the circuitcourtof Warren county,withinsixty
days from the passage of the resolution by the board of
councilmen opening or widening such street: Provided,
That nothing herein shallaffectany appeals now pending
untilsixtydays afterthe passage of thisact.
§ 9. The board of councilmen shall,on the firstMonday
in January ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty five,
electsix persons as water commissioners ,and the persons
thus elected shall be divided by lot into two classes, so
that the term of officeof three of them shall expire in
one year ,and the term of officeof the other three in two
years; and the board of councilmen shall,on thefirstMon-
day in January ,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
five,and annually thereafter,electthree persons as water
commissioners to succeed those whose terms of office then
expire,who shallhold theirofficesfortwo years and un-
tiltheirsuccessors are elected and qualified. The board
of councilmenshallby ordinanceprescribethedutiesand
qualificationsof the water commissioners .

1360 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 10.All laws orpartsof laws conflictingwith the pro-
visionsof thisactbe,and the same are hereby,repealed.
§ 11. This act shall take effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved May 9 , 1884.

CHAPTER 1526 .
AN ACT to establisha toll-gate on the Wilderness Turnpike Road , in
Bell county .
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Bell county , within the
limitsof said county,is hereby authorizedto erect and
maintain a gate upon the Wilderness Turnpike Road ,
at which tollsmay be charged and collectedas regulated
in subsection three of section three of chapter one hun-
dred and ten of the General Statutes .
§2 . It shallbe the duty of the judge of the Bell county
court,on the firstday of a regular term of said court,
afterhaving given ten days'writtennoticeposted in a
conspicuousplace in at leastfivepublicplaces in Bell
county,to sellat the court-house door in Pineville,Ken-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1361
tucky ,the aforesaid gate to the highest bidder, for the
period of twelve months , and to make a similar sale
every twelve months thereafter; the saidjudge shalltake
the purchaser'sbond ,with approved security,conditioned
that said purchaser will pay over to said county court,
every three months thereafter, of the year for which he
purchased,one fourthof the sum he agreed to pay per
annum for said toll-gate ; and if at the end of any
quarter said purchaser shall fail to comply with the
conditions of said bond , the said county court shall
enter an order removing such keeper or purchaser,and
shall republish and sellsaid gate again for the residue
of the year ; and shall cause suit to be institutedin the
name of the Bell county court against such purchaser
for the amount due upon said bond .
§3 . All moneys derived from the sale of said toll-gate
shallbe appliedexclusivelyto repairingand keeping in
good repairthe saidWildernessTurnpike Road ,between
Cumberland Gap and the Knox county line; and the
county court of Bellcounty shallhave no power to bor-
row money and pledge forthe payment thereofthe pro-
ceeds of said toll-gate .
§4. Every male citizenin Bell county,Kentucky ,be-
tween the age ofsixteenyears and forty-fiveyears,living
within one mile of said road ,shall, under the directionof
an overseer, work at least four days out of each year
in repairingand keeping in repair the aforesaidroad,
bridges, and so forth; but the said county court may
excuse any person from working on said road on account
of disease or infirmity.
§ 5. All personsshallbe charged tollwho pass through
said toll-gate except citizensof Bell county , and the rates
of tollshallremain as now fixedby the General Statutes
in regard to turnpike roads.
§ 6. This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved May 10, 1884.

county poor -house thereon .

ble in not lessthan fivenor more than fifteenyears, and

§3. The countycourtof saidcountyshallhave power

CHAPTER 1529 .

said corporationshallhave power to contract and be
contractedwith,sue and be sued,plead and be impleaded,

corporationshallhave power to transacta general mer-

ried on and managed by a board of trustees,consisting

cember ,one thousand eighthundred and eighty-four,the

tees .
-§3 . That the trusteesshall keep or cause to be

ers vote therefor. Said company may commence busi-

porate powers herein granted , and the same may be

ber president of said board , and one of their number

tionor appointment shallbe made immediatelyupon the

after .

§ 8. No officerof said company shallmake any con-
tractson behalf of saidcorporation,or incurany liability

§ 11. Said corporationmay have a common seal,and

sage .

of Kentucky :

lic buildings, and furnishing the same , the county court

pose .

curringtherein,may increasethe countylevynot exceed-
ing one dollar and fiftycents on each tithable in the

of the county,in additiontothe ad valorem tax herein-
before authorized ; and the sheriff shall collect and ac-
count forsame in the same manner as the county levy

1368 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and revenue tax are now collected and accounted for by
law ,having the same right to distrain therefor; and he
and his suretiesshallbe liablein like manner , and shall
pay the money upon orders of court,which shall apply
the same to the payment of the interestand liquidation
of said bonds .
§ 4. That thecounty courtshallhave power to appoint
three commissioners to prepare plans and specifications
forcourt-house and clerks'offices,who shallreceivepro-
posals for building same ,and have power to superintend
the erectionthereof, and when completed to accept same
from the contractors, which commissioners shall also be
authorized to sellthe bonds hereby authorized tobe issued,
which bonds shallnot be soldforlessthan theirpar value ,
and in denominations of not less than one hundred nor
more than one thousand dollars; and shallhave power
to collectthe taxes collectedby the sheriff, and pay out
the same to the bondholders . Said commissioners shall
be required to execute bond , with good security, for the
faithfuldischarge of their duties,which bond shall be
executed to the county court of Knox county,and on
which suitmay be brought in the name of said court.
§5 . That in case the money may not be raised by the
sale of bonds ,the money arising from the increase county
levy, and ad valorem tax authorized by this act,may ,by
the orders of said county ,be applied to the indebtedness
incurred by said court in erecting and furnishing a court-
house and clerks' offices.
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 10, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1533 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Donansburg, in Green county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
* of Kentucky :
§1 . That there ishereby incorporated the town of Do-
nansburg, inGeen county,with the followingcorporate

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1369
limits, viz: Beginning at the residence of Wm . Chuning ;
thence with the Osceola and Greensburg road to the school-
house ; thence a due south line to Trammel's creek,and up
the same to the crossing of the old Brewersburg road ;
thence with said road to the beginning ,including the res-
idence of Wm . Chuning in corporate limits.
§2 . There shallbe electedannually,on the firstMon-
day in August,by the legallyqualifiedvotersofsaidtown ,
two trusteesof said town ,who shallbe styledthe Trustees
of the Town of Donansburg ,with perpetual succession,
who ,in theircorporate name aforesaid,shallhave power
and capacity to sue and be sued ,implead or be impleaded ,
grant and receive by their corporate name , and do all
other acts as natural persons may do ; and as such ,make
and enforce allneedful by -laws and rulesfor the govern .
ment and regulation of said town ,not inconsistentwith
the laws of the State or United States .
§3 . Said trustees shall have and use a common seal ;
may purchase and hold lotsfor a grave -yard ,church ,and
seminary , and provide for the improvement of the same ;
may have and hold,by purchase or devise,or by gift,land
or other property for the benefit of said town ; and shall
establishand keep in repair allstreetsin said town .
§4 . There shallbe elected biennially,on the firstMon-
day in August (at the time of holding general elections
for the State),by the legally qualifiedvoters of the town ,
a policejudge and town marshal ,to be styledpolicejudge
of Donansburg and town marshal of Donansburg . The
policejudge to have the same jurisdiction, power ,and
capacity ,entitledto the same fees and emoluments ,and
subject to the same liabilitiesand responsibilitiesas a jus-
tice of the peace for Green county . The town marshal
to have the same power and capacity, entitledto same
fees,and subjecttothesame liabilities,duties,and respon-
sibilitiesas a constable for Green county . The police
judge to be commissioned by the Governor of thisState.
The town marshal to execute bond in the Green county
court ,with securityto be approved by the court, with the
same conditionsand penalties as the bond of a constable.

1370 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
The policejudge shallhave also the rightand power to
take depositions the same as an examiner for Green
county,who may be appointedby the Green circuitcourt
or the judge thereof.
§5 . All the officershereinmentioned shallbe firstduly
sworn by some courtorjusticeof the peace thatthey will
faithfullydischarge the dutiesof theirofficebefore they
proceed to act. All resignationsof the same shall be to
the Green county court,and all vacancies in the same
shallbe filledby said court until the next election; and
allofficersmay continue to act until theirsuccessors are
qualified. All electionsshall be held by a clerk to be
appointed by the trustees, who shall certifythe same
to the Green county court,and said court,as to the police
judge ,shallcertifyto the Governor of thisState.
§6 . Until the time of holding the firstelection, and
untiltheirsuccessorsshall quality,the following shall be
the officersof said town : Wm . Chuning , police judge ;
Henry Bishop and Edward Clark,trustees,and Jas. Mat .
Jones , town marshal .
§7 . This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage ; but the Legislature reserves the right to change ,
amend ,or repeal the same at any time.
Approved May 10, 1884 .

Building on back streetto Campbellsvilleand Greensburg Turnpike , be-
tween lots numbers sixtynine and one hundred and nine, in town of

lor county , running from Hodgen Gate and Old Office
Building on back streetto Campbellsville and Greensburg

CHAPTER 1535 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Sulphur Well and Hanly Turnpike Road Com .
pany , in Jessamine county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company is hereby formed and created a
body -corporateand politic,under the name and styleof
the Sulphur Well and Hanly Turnpike Road Company ,
for the purpose of constructing a turnpike road ,begin-
ning at the Sulphur Well and Paint Lick Turnpike Road ,
near the old John Davis residence ; thence along the
present dirtroad to the Danville, Lancaster , and Nicho-
lasvilleTurnpike Road ,to a pointat or near the old Jno .
Butler residence, a distance of about two and one half
miles;tohave perpetualsuccession; and by the aforesaid
name and style may sue and be sued ,contract and be
contractedwith, plead and be impleaded,in any of the
courts of this Commonwealth .
§2 . That the capitalstock of said company shallbe
two thousand dollars, and may be increased or dimin-
ished at the pleasure of the company , and shall be di-
vided into shares of fiftydollarseach .
§3 . That William H. Hoover , jr., Wilson Baker , and
Frank Miller,be , and are hereby , appointed commission-
ers to open books for the subscriptionof stock aforesaid,
at such time and in such places as any two of said com-
missioners may deem expedient .
§4 . That when a sufficient amount of stock shall be
subscribed to justifythe beginning of said road ,the com-
missioners or any two of them shall,at such time and
place as they may deem proper, call a meeting of the
stockholders,and hold an election for a president and
three directors,for one year , or untiltheir successorsare
elected and qualified.

1372 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§5. That no person shallbe eligibleas presidentor
director who is not the owner of one or more shares of
stockin said road; and in the electionof presidentand
directors, each stockholder shall be entitled to one vote
foreach share of stockso owned ,and may be castin per-
son or by written proxy .
§6 . That the president and directors shall have the
power to appoint a treasurer,gate-keeper ,and allofficers
and agents which may be deemed necessary to effectthe
purpose of thisact,and to remove them at pleasure; and
they may require the treasurer,and allother officersand
agents appointed by them ,to execute bond and security
in such a sum as they may deem proper, conditioned for
the faithfulperformance of the duties incumbent on them
as such .
§7 . That the county court of Jessamine county are
hereby authorized to subscribe to the capital stock of
saidcompany in any amount ,not exceedingone thousand
dollarsper mile,that the said court may see proper to
subscribe .
§ 8. That the grade and width of the said road shall
be determined by the president and board of directors.
§9 . That the stock of the said company shallbe trans-
ferableonly upon the books of saidcompany .
§ 10. That so soon as the said road is completed the
president and directorsmay erect one toll-gate anywhere
on said road they deem proper , and may collect and
receive one -half fulltollat the rate now allowed by the
General Statutes .
§ 11. That the presidentand board of directorsmay
make all by -laws necessary for the transaction of its
business, not in conflictherewith nor in conflict with
the laws of this State .
§ 12. That said company may acquire by deed ,gift, or
conveyance,or by writsof ad quod damnum ,ground for a
toll-house (not exceeding two acres),rock,timber,quar-
ries,and other material as may be necessary to build said
road and bridges on said road .
§ 13. This actto take effectfrom and afteritspassage.
Approved May 10, 1884 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1373
CHAPTER 1536 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe sale of spirituous, vinous, and malt liquors, in
common school districtnumber thirty, in Graves county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful for any person ,in any
quantity whatever , to sellor give to another any spirit-
uous ,vinous, or malt liquors,or a mixture of either,with-
in the limitsof common school districtnumber thirty,in
Graves county , Kentucky .
§ 2. Any person found guilty of a violation of thisact,
shall,upon conviction,be finedin the sum of not more
than sixtydollarsand not lessthan twenty dollars,recov-
erable either upon a warrant before any justice of the
peace in saidcounty,or upon indictmentin theGraves
circuit court .
§ 3. All finesand forfeituresarisingunder thisact,after
payment of officers'fees,shallbe appropriatedto thepub-
lic school in said district .
§ 4. If persons of color should violatethe provisions of
this act, the fines for same shall be appropriated to the
benefit of the colored school in said district .
§5 . This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
after the firstday of May ,one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four .
Approved May 8 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 1537 .
AN ACT toauthorizetheturnpikecommissionersof Lagrange and West-
port precincts, in Oldham county, to compromise for taxes due or to
become due .

of Kentucky :

Westport precincts,in Oldham county , or in eitherof said
precincts,be,and they are hereby,authorizedto settleby
compromise with any person or corporationforany taxes
due or to become due ,and any such compromise shallbe

office .

CHAPTER 1538 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe East End Park Company , of Mason county .
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That C. P. Dietrich, Casper Jacobs ,Micajah Hutch-
inson,H. C. Dietrich, and Jacob Allspanach,and those
who may associatewith them , and their successors, shall
be , and they are hereby ,created a body -politicand cor-
porate, by the name of the East End Park Company ,
with power , and in that name ,to contract and be con-
tracted with,to sue and be sued ,to plead and be im-
pleaded; to have and use a common seal,and to change
and alterthe same at pleasure; to ordain and put into
execution such by -laws , rules and regulations, for the
government of itsaffairsas it may deem expedient and
proper, not inconsistentwith the Constitutionof the
United States or of this State .
§2 . That the capitalstock of thiscorporation shall not
exceed the sum of fiftythousand dollars, divided into
shares of fiftydollarseach. The shares shallbe per-
sonal estate, and transferable on the books of the cor-
poration. Each share of capitalstock shallentitlethe
holderto one vote,eitherin person or by writtenproxy,
in all elections of officers, and at all meetings of stock-
holders . When the sum of two thousand five hundred
dollars issubscribed and paid up ,said corporation shall
have authorityto organize and commence business.
§3 . That the objectof thiscorporationshallbe to
open and maintain a park and pleasure garden in the
neighborhood of Maysville and Chester,in Mason coun-
ty,Kentucky ; and forthatpurpose itmay purchase,rent,
or lease any real or personal property; and may build

LAWS OF KENTUCKY .. 1375
such buildings and furnish the same as it may deem
proper .
§4 . That the business and affairsof said company shall
be managed by a board of five directors, who shall be
elected annually by the stockholders,and who ,from their
number ,shall select a president,a secretary and treas-
urer, and a superintendent. They shall hold regular
meetings , and in the case of the death or resignation of
a director,the vacancy shallbe filledby the board from
the stockholders . Said board of directors shall have
power to open and maintain said park and pleasure
garden at alltimes; and they may employ such officers
and servants as may be necessary ; and they shall have
power to employ and appoint police to keep and main-
tain order in said park , who shallbe authorized to make
arrestsand take offendersbefore the mayor of Chester or
any justiceof the peace of Mason county, to be dealt
with according to law . They shallbe authorized to have
in connection with said park concerts,dancing platforms,
swings ,and other means of amusement ; and when said
park is open they may offerfor sale on the premises ,eat-
ables,lemonade , soda water ,mineral water , beer,ginger
pop ,cigars,cigarettes, and tobacco.
§5 . That itshall be unlawful for any person to bring
into said park , or to drink therein, any spirituousliquors,
or to play at any game formoney or property, or gamble
in any way in said park ; and any person violating this
provisionmay be arrestedby the officersof said com-
pany , and taken before the mayor of Chester or any
justiceof the peace of Mason county,and,upon hiscon-
viction, he may be finedin any sum not exceeding fifty
dollars .
§6 . That the indebtednessof thiscorporationshallat
no time exceed the sum of five thousand dollars.
Approved May 9 , 1884 .

court,a licensed attorney at law , be,and he is hereby ,

monwealth ,outsideof the county of Anderson .

ground .

CHAPTER 1541 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Elders and Trusteesof the Presbyterian Church
of Stanfordto transferthe old Buffalo Spring Cemetery to the Buffalo
Spring Cemetery Company .
WHEREAS , The Eldersand Trusteesof the Presbyterian
Church of Stanford have exclusive control of the lands of
the old Buffalo Spring Cemetery , and which is now en-
tirelysurrounded by the lands owned by " The Buffalo
Spring Cemetery Company ;" and whereas,saidchurch is
willing to surrender the entire management and control
of said old cemetery grounds to the said " Buffalo Spring
Cemetery Company ;" therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That the Elders and Trustees of the Presbyterian

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1377
Church of Stanfordbe,and they are hereby,empowered
to transfer,by deed or otherwise,the lands of the old Buf-
falo Spring Cemetery ,to the " Buffalo Spring Cemetery
Company ."
§2 . This act shall be in force from and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 8 , 1884 .
CHAPTER 1542 .
AN ACT toincorporatethe St. PhillipsEpiscopalChurch , at Harrodsburg .
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the wardens and vestry of the Protestant
Episcopal Church ,known and called St. PhillipsChurch ,
at Harrodsburg ,Kentucky , and theirsuccessorsin office,
are hereby created a body -politicand corporate,by the
name and style of " St. PhillipsProtestantEpiscopal
Church ,of Harrodsburg ,Kentucky ."
§2 . The wardens and vestry of said church , selected
and chosen according to the canons and usages of the
ProtestantEpiscopalChurch in the dioceseto which the
same isor may be hereafter attached, shall, from time to
time ,be the managers of the secular and financialaffairs
of the said corporation,and ,so far as the said corporation
is concerned ,shall exercise all the rightsand privileges,
and perform all the duties, and be invested with all the
titlesto church property by the laws of the Common-
wealth conferred on trustees of religious societies.
§3 . The said wardens and vestry,in the name of the
said corporation,shall have power to do and perform all
such acts and things as are permitted by the laws of the
land to other corporations of a like character ; and the
saidcorporationshallhave perpetualsuccession,and may
use a common seal .
§4 . Be it furtherenacted,That thisact shalltake effect
from the day of itspassage and approval .

Approved May 8,1884.

1378 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1543 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to incorporatethe Knights of
Honor Temple Company , at Louisville," approved February twentieth,
one thousand eighthundred and eighty-four.

proved February twenty , one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four , be , and the same is hereby , amended
as follows, viz : The name of said company shall be

§2 . Amend section fiveof said act by insertingtherein
the words , " firstWednesday in May , one thousand eight
hundred and eighty four," in lieu of the words , " first
Wednesday in April, one thousand eight hundred and
eighty -four," therein .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 8, 1884.
CHAPTER 1544 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Ohio county, relating to the public roads in
said county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county judge of Ohio county shalldivide
all the roads of said county into precincts, and as often
as may be necessary appoint a surveyor in each precinct,
whose duty itshall be to superintend the roads therein,
and see that the same are worked and kept in such repair
as the agreement of the contractor or contractors thereon
with the county court as said court may require. No
surveyorshallbe allowed,unlessby consentof thecourt,
to resignsuch place under two years,except he remove
from the precinct. Any surveyor may be removed at
any time by saidcourt.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1379
§2 . The clerk of the county court shall, within ten
days after the appointment of a surveyor of said road ,
delivera copy of the order to the sheriffof the county,
or to a constable if there be no sheriff, containing a full
and complete description of the precincts,and take his
receipttherefor. Such officershall, within fifteendays
thereafter,delivera copy of the order and a descriptionof
theprecinctto the clerk'sofficeof the Ohio county court,
with the time of the service indorsed , which shall be evi-
dence of the facts stated therein. The clerk shall post
up in his officeonce in every year a listof allthe sur-
veyors in the county , designating the precinct of each
surveyor; theclerk,sheriff,or otherofficerfailingto per-
form the duties prescribed in this section shall be fined
two dollars for each neglect .
§3 . Each surveyor of a public road shallbe furnished
with a copy of the contract for the working of the road
in his precinct; he shall report to the county judge any
failureupon the part of the contractor thereon ; he shall
certifyto the county judge when the contract has been
properly complied with; he shallbe exempt from jury
service, and from poll-tax for road purposes during
hiscontinuance in said office. For a failureto perform
any of his duties prescribed by this act said surveyor
shall be fined therefor not less than five nor more than
ten dollars for each offense,to be recovered by warrant
in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and the
county court shallhave jurisdictionof allproceedings
against surveyors of roads.
§4 . The county judge shall, at the court-house door in
Hartford,on the firstMonday in September in each year,
and at such othertimes as he shallfindnecessary,letout
the working and keeping in repair the public roads in
Ohio countyto thelowest and bestbidder,forsuch length
of time as he may choose , not exceeding his own term of
office, after due advertisement of two insertionsin the
county paper, and also by written or printed notices
posted up for at least fifteendays before such lettingat
the voting places of each voting precinctin the county :

1380 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Provided, That the funds raisedby the levy courtunder
thisact shallbe sufficientto work allof the public roads
in said county , and if not sufficient,then it shallbe used
at such places and for such roads as the county court may
thinkbest,a majorityof thejusticesconcurring,they hav-
ing due regard to the public good and the wants of the
differentparts of the county .
§5 . Each contractor shall execute to the Common-
wealth a bond with good security,to be approved by the
county judge ,in double the amount he isto receive for his
contractforthefaithfulperformanceofsaidcontract; and
said bond shall be filed at the next regular term of the
Ohio county court afterthe letting,which shallbe noted
of record,and the bonds kept on fileby the clerkof the
court in his officein bundles marked contractors' bonds ,
and certifiedcopies of said bonds shallbe competent as
evidence , and the county judge shalldeliverto each sur-
veyor a copy of the contract for working the roads in
such surveyor's precinct. Any contractor who shallfail,
in whole or in part,to complete his contract within the
time and in the manner prescribedtherein,or who ,having
contracted to keep any part of the public roads in good
order for traveling or for hauling , or any bridge or cul-
vert in good repair, and who shallfailto do so,shallfor
every failure be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and
upon conviction shallbe fined not lessthan ten nor more
than fiftydollars.
§ 6. All fines assessed under this act shall be collected
as other fines,and shall inure to the benefit of the road
fund of Ohio county,and shallbe paid out by the sheriff
under the order of the county judge .
§7 . The county judge shallletout the construction or
repairingof the bridgesin Ohio county,by and with the
concurrence of a majority of the justices agreeing , or
they may ,in their discretion,appoint a special commis-
sioner to letout any bridge or bridges,and provide for his
compensation therefor;and such county judge or special
commissioner shall be held liable for any failure in re-
gard to any bridge so let by them respectively, and the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1381
court may requirebond with securityfrom the special
commissioner .
§ 8. That all delinquent capitation tax payers of Ohio
county shall be required to work out their taxes at such
times on the roads in their several precinctsas the sur-
veyor thereofmay require, for which work such delin-
quent shall have creditat the rate of one dollar for each
fullday'swork performed by him . For failingor refus-
ing to comply with the order of said surveyor when noti-
fied, allsuch delinquents are made liableto the penalties
now imposed by law for persons failing or refusing to
work on roads. The several surveyors are authorized
and empowered tocollectthetaxesdue from suchdelin-
quents in theirseveral precincts,if they shallelectto pay
the same rather than work on the road , and the same
shall be held and applied as part of the road fund , and
reportedand paid over by said surveyorto the county
judge , taking and filingwith the county clerk his receipt
therefor; and said clerkshallgive to said surveyor a cer-
tifiedcopy of said receipt, which shall have the same
effect in his hands as the original .
§ 9. The county court of Ohio county may open roads
where they may think best for the public good , not ex-
ceeding sixty feet in width ; the damages ,if any ,in so
opening new roads,to be settledas the general law now
directs .
§ 10. That the courts of the justicesof the peace in
and for Ohio county shall have jurisdictionconcurrent
with the quarterly court of all proceedings against road
contractorsunder thisact, and itshallbe the duty of any
justiceor the county judge ,upon his own knowledge or
upon complaint of any surveyor ,or upon the information
under oath of any citizen that any part of the public
road is out of repair,to issue his warrant against the
delinquent contractor, returnable to his own or some
otherjustice'scourtor theOhio quarterlycourt; and upon
the execution and return thereof, such proceedings shall
be had as are had in otherCommonwealth cases; in pro-
ceedings under this act, the defendant shallhave no right

1382 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to claim his district,and both the Commonwealth and de-
fendant shallhave the right of appeal from justices'.
courts to the quarterly court, and from the quarterly
court to the circuit court .
§ 11. It shall be the duty of the county attorney to
prosecuteallwarrantsunder thisact,and forsuch prose
cution he shallreceive twenty -fiveper cent.of the finesre-
covered ,and upon his failurethe court may appoint some
attorney in his stead,who shall receive said commission
for his services . All fines assessed under this act shall be
collected as other fines,and shall inure to the benefit of the
road fund of Ohio county , and shall be paid out by the
sheriff or other collecting officer under orders from the
county judge .
§ 12. Itshall be unlawful for the county judge ,county
attorney,sheriff, collectorof the revenue ,or any surveyor ,
to become ,directlyor indirectly,interestedin any contract
for working roads or building or repairing bridges, and it
shall be unlawful for either of said officersto buy or be
come interestedin any claim growing out of said work
or contract . For a violation of this section either of said
officersshallbe guiltyof a misdemeanor , and upon in-
dictment and conviction thereof, shall be fined not less
than fiftynor more than two hundred dollars.
§13 . Any person who shallwillfullyor negligentlyin-
jure,destroy,or obstructany of the said publicroads or
bridges,or any of the culvertsor ditcheson said roads,
shallbe finednot lessthan fivenor more than twenty -five
dollars,tobe recovered in likemanner as the finespre-
scribed herein for surveyors,and shall also be liable in
double damages to the county ,or any person aggrieved or
injured,tobe recoveredin any courthaving jurisdictionof
the amount . It shall be the duty of surveyors to report
promptly to the county judge any violationof thissec.
tion .
§14 . The countycourt, a majorityof thejusticesagree-
ing,shall, on some regular county court day , assess a tax
of not more than fifteen cents on each one hundred dol-
lars'worth of propertyassessedforrevenuepurposes,and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1383
a capitation tax not exceeding two dollars on each per-
son in said county requiredby the general law to work
on roads .
§ 15. The ad valorem and capitation tax provided for
in section[fourteen] of thisactshallbe collectedas other
revenue due the county is collected,and under the same
obligations by the collecting officer,and shall be paid
out by said collectingofficerto road contractors on the
order of the judge of the Ohio county court.
§ 16. Chapter ninety-four of the General Statutesis
hereby adopted and made a part of thisact,except when
it comes in conflictwith the provisions of thisact ; and
all other road laws passed for Ohio county are hereby
repealed .
§ 17. That an electionshallbe held in Ohio county on
the firstMonday in August , one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four ,at which two pollsshallbe opened- one
" For the road law ," and the other " Against the road
law ;" and if it shall be found that a majority of those
who voted upon the question have voted for the law ,
then this act shall be in full force . Should there not
be a majority for said law at said election, then the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1389
§4 . That said company shall have the right,through

town not exceeding fiftycents on the one hundred dol-

fee-house keepers before granting said license,which said

town . The said board of trusteesshallhave power to levy
a tax and make appropriationsof money out of any funds
on hand forthe purpose of buildinga jail.
§ 10. All acts or parts of actsin conflictwith thisact
are hereby repealed.
§ 11. This act to take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1564 .
AN ACT in relationto the Franklin county court.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafter it shall be unlawful for the Frank-
lin county court to make any appropriations forbridges
and turnpikes,unless a levy he made the same year to
provide for payment of same ,nor shallsuch levy for said
purposes exceed ten cents on the one hundred dollars*
worth of property for any one year .
§2 . This act to take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 9,1884 .
CHAPTER 1565 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Frankfort Tobacco Warehouse and Manufac
turing Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That John Kiernan , W. L. Jett, John Rodman ,S.
V. Pence , and G. W. Craddock , or such of them as may
act, are hereby appointed commissioners , and they , or
such of them as may act, are authorized and empow-
ered to organize a company ,under the name and styleof
the Frankfort Tobacco Warehouse and Manufacturing
Company . The said company shallbe a body -corporate
and politic, under the name and style aforesaid; and
shall have perpetual succession; and shall have power
to contract and be contracted with , sue and be sued ,
plead and be impleaded , and to transact and perform all
such business as a naturalperson could do ; and may
adopt a common seal, and alteror abolish the same at
pleasure.
§ 2. The capitalstockof saidcompany shallbe fifteen
thousand dollars, in shares of fittydollars each , to be
subscribed for as herein prescribed: Provided, That the
board of directors of said company may ,if at any time
deemed advisable, increase the capital to the sum of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1401
twenty -five thousand dollars: And provided further, That
the company may organize and commence business when
there shall have been subscribed for stock in said com-
pany the sum of fivethousand dollars.
§3 . That the commissionershereby appointed,or such
of them as may act,shallorganize by electingone of
their number chairman , and shall cause books to be
opened for the subscription of stock in said company .
The following shall be the form of the covenant of the
subscribers for the stock of said company : " We , the
undersigned,do hereby subscribeforthe number of shares
set opposite to our respective names of the stock of the
Frar.kfort Tobacco Warehouse and Manufacturing Com-
pany , of fiftydollarseach ; and we respectivelyagree to
pay forthe same as may be required by the board of direct-
ors of said company ." After a subscriptionof fivethou-
sand dollarsor more shallhave been made for the capital
stock of said company ,the commissioners shall,by notice
of at least ten days ,published in the Tri-weekly Ken-
tucky Yeoman , in the city of Frankfort, of the time,
place, and object of the meeting ,call a meeting of the
stockholders,for the purpose of organizing the said com-
pany by the electionof fivedirectors,who shallhold their
officeforone year ,and untiltheirsuccessorsare thereafter
elected, a majority of whom shallconstitutea board .
§ 4. That the board of directorsshall have power to
selectand appoint a secretaryor clerk,and allsuch other
officersand agents as may be deemed necessary, and to
fixtheirrespectivesalaries,and to prescribe theirrespect-
ive duties,and to remove them , or any of them ,at pleas-
ure,and appoint others in their stead, and may require
bond , with good security,from them or any of them , for
the faithfuldischarge of their respective duties; and for
any breach of the covenant or covenants of any of such
bond , the company may bring suit in any court of com-
petentjurisdiction.
§5 . The board of directorsshallhave power to adopt
such by laws as they may deem proper, subjectto the
approval of the stockholdersat any meeting of the stock--

1402 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
holders : Provided, That the same shall not conflictwith
the Constitution or the laws of this State or of the United
States .
§ 6. That the company shall have the power and au-
thorityto purchase and hold such real estateas itmay
deem necessary to conduct itsbusiness,and the same to
sellat pleasure, and to purchase other real estate for the
same purpose .
§7 . The business of the company shall consist in the
purchase and sale, and the handling of tobacco ,and in
the manufacture thereof, in any or all of its branches ,
and warehousing , storing, and selling for others : Pro-
vided, The company may confine itself to any one or
more of said branches of business .
§ 8. That there shallbe held an annual meeting of the
stockholdersfor the purpose of electingfivedirectors,and
for the transaction of any other business deemed nec-
essary or proper by the stockholders; and itshallbe the
duty of the acting board of directorsto call such meet-
ingsby noticeas requiredin the thirdsectionof thisact:
Provided , That at any time any three of the stockholders
may , in writing, demand a stockholders' meeting ; and
when any such demand shallbe made ,the board of direct-
ors shall call the same by notice as aforesaid, within a
reasonable time thereafter.
§9 . That the board of directorsshallcause to be kept
a stock-book , in which shall appear the name of each
stockholder and the number of shares he holds ; and the
sale or transferby any stockholder of his stock, or any
part of it, shallnot be deemed as complete or binding
until a transfer thereof is made in said stock book .
§ 10. The board of directorsshalldeclare annual divi-
dends to the stockholdersof the net earnings of the com-
pany at such time as the board may appoint,or as may
be fixedby the by -laws of the company .
§ 11 That thisact shalltake effectand be in force from
and after its passage .
Approved May 10 , 1884 .

CHAPTER 1566 .

number twenty , in Lyon county.

trictnumber twenty,in Lyon county,employed a teacher
and had taught a three months ' school in said district for

selves ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :

hereby ,directed to draw his warrant on the Treasurer
forthe sum of twenty-fivedollarsand eighty-fivecents
in favor of Gabriel Glover ,to be paid out of the unbonded
surplus school fund of said county .
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 10, 1884.

of the LouisvilleSouthern Railroad Company ," approved one thousand

CHAPTER 1572 .

liquors, or mixture of either, in two miles of any house used by a congre-
gation or denomination in which persons assemble to worship God , or

of any house used by a congregation or denomination in

CHAPTER 1573 .

Turnpike Road Company .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the Springfield, Mack-
ville,and WillisburgTurnpike Road Company ,in Wash-
ington county , and said company shall have the power ,
to charge and collecttollsof all persons traveling over
or using their road at half the rates now fixed and
established,and fixed by law for said road at their gate
on said road nearest Mackville , and between Mackville
and Springfield.

any court of said county having competent jurisdiction.

CHAPTER 1575 .

R. T. Anderson, R. S. Bullock, M. G. Thompson , J. M.
Graves , W. J. Loughridge , J. T. Slade , R. J. O'Mahony ,
Dennis Mulligan , W. B. Emmal ,J. M. Duff,George Lan-
caster, John L. Barclay , John B. Wilgus , W. H. Cassell,

1406 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Alex . Pearson , Asa Dodge , M. C. Johnson , W. D. Nich-
olas,and David James ,and the subscribersto the stock
of this association and their successors, shall be , and

swer and be answered , defend and be defended , in all
courts of law and equity and elsewhere ; with fullauthor-
ityto acquire,hold and possess,use,occupy,and enjoy,
and the same to sell,convey ,and dispose of, allsuch real

and renew at pleasure; and generallyto do and perform
allsuch things relativeto the object of thisinstitution.
§2 . That the capitalstock of thiscompany shall be

increased at the willof the stockholders,and in conform-
itywith the provisionsof the generalinsurancelaws, to
any sum not exceeding one milliondollars.

ance Commissioner after examination made by him ac-
cording to law ,the company shallbe competent to trans-
act all kinds of business for which it is established .

holder,or his legal representatives,subjectto such restric-

make and establish, and subjectalsoto the provisionsof
the general insurance laws .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1407
more than thirteendirectors,allof whom shallbe stock-
holders . Within one month after the amount of one

after,the stockholdersshall hold a meeting for the elec-

in office until others shall have been chosen to succeed

in case of a vacancy in the board of directors,itshall be
filledby the directorsfrom the stockholders for the re-
mainder of the year.
§6 . That itshalland may be lawful for said company
to insure houses and other buildings, and personal prop-
erty contained in any house or building of whatever
kind , against loss or damage by fire, water , lightning,
wind storms ,or otherwise ,in and out of this State ; and it
shallbe liableto make good alllossessustained by fireor

ident from among themselves , and in case of the death

ing of the stockholders(a majorityof the stock consent-
ing),it may be determined to declarea portion of the

such policy-holdersas the stockholdersat such meeting
may direct,but not to impair the capitalstock; the divi-
dends declaredby presidentand directorsshallbe made
halfyearly on the firstMondays in January and July,
and shallbe paid to the stockholderswithin ten days
thereafter .
§ 9. That the presidentand directorsshallhave power

1408 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and authorityto appoint a secretary and such other offi-
cers,clerks,and agents under them , as shallbe necessary
for transactingthe business of said corporation,and may
allow them such compensation as they may deem reason-
able ; to ordain and establish such by -laws and regula-
tionsas shall appear to them necessary forregulating and

contrary to or inconsistentwith thisact,the Constitution

stockholders .

eral meeting of the stockholdersfor any purpose relative
to the affairsof the company ,giving at leasttwo weeks '
notice thereof in some newspaper printed in Lexington ,

ever ,that the annual election of directors should not be

lated by the by -laws and ordinances of thiscorporation ;

paper printedin the cityof Lexington,
§ 12. That all policiesor contractsof insurancethat
may be made or enteredinto by said company may be

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1409
by such committee or otherwise as the board may author-
ize, and the same shallbe binding on the company .

pany ,and open books for that purpose at the city of

until the election of the first board of directors, when

companies ," approved March twelfth, one thousand eight
hundred and seventy, and the acts which have been here-
tofore,or may hereafterbe passed amendatory thereto;
and thiscompany shall,upon itsincorporation and enter-
ing upon business,be amenable to and regulatedby the
general insurance laws of this Commonwealth , as other
stock fireinsurance companies now doing business in this
State are,both those heretofore chartered by the General
Assembly , and those which have become incorporated
under the provisions of the general insurance laws of this
State .
§ 15. That this act shall be in force from and after its
passage,and shallcontinuein forceforthe space of fifty
years .
Approved May 10, 1884.

stockholders of said company , at such place as the presi.
dent and directorsmay designate,on the the firstWednes-
day in June in every year,and thepresidentand directors
may fillany vacancy in theirboard : Provided, That all
electionswhich are to be made may be made thereafter
upon thirtydays ' notice being given to the stockholders.
§ 8. That the president and directorsof said company
shall have power to elect a treasurer,or any other offi-
cers or agents that they may deem proper and necessary ,
and may require of their treasurer,or any other agent or
officers,such bond and security,under such penalties as
they may deem proper,upon which recoverymay be had
for a breach of the conditions thereof .
§9 . That the said Cumberland River Railway Com-
pany , their president, directors, and agents , are hereby
vested with all of the powers and rights necessary for
surveying , locating, and constructing a railway from a
point near Woodbine , on the Knoxville Branch of the
Louisville and Nashville Railroad, or from any point on
the line of the extension of the Kentucky Central Rail-
road ,at or near Barboursville,to Pineville,in Bell county ,
or both of said lines; thence by the most practicalroute
up the Cumberland river, or its tributaries, to Mount
Pleasant ,Harlan county ; thence up the Poor Fork of said
riverto the State line of Virginia, at or near Pound Gap ,
in Letcher county ; also a branch of said railway from
the main line,near Pineville,to Cumberland Gap ; also
a branch of said railway from the main line,near Pine-
ville,up Straight creek to the head of the same ; also a
branch of said railway from the main line, near Mount
Pleasant , up Martin's Fork of said river or itstributaries
to the State line of Virginia; also a branch from the
main line of said railway , at or near Mount Pleasant, up
the Clover Fork of said river,to the Virginia State line.
And should the president and directors of said company

1416 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
deem itto be expedient, in order to develop the resources
of any landsthey may acquireby purchase or otherwise,
said company may construct and operate other branches
from the main line of said railway or its branches up
any of the creeks, in the counties of Bell,Clay , Leslie,
Harlan , or Letcher , which railway or branches may
be constructed with as many sets of tracks,side-tracks,
turnouts,or switches,as the said president and directors
may deem necessary, and in such a manner as to secure
a road -bed not exceeding one hundred feet in width .
And the said company ,theirofficers, agents, engineers,
and servants,may ,subjectto the provisionsof sectionsten
and eleven , enter upon , survey , use,and excavate any
land which may be required for the siteof said road and
branches ,or the depots, warehouses , and machine shops
thereof, and for the construction of, use or repair of the
same , or itsworks and appurtenances ;and may take and
use any earth,timber,stone,or gravel,or othermaterials,
which may be used and usefulfor the construction,com-
pletion,use,or repairof the same ; and may construct and
have turretsand bridges : Provided, That in building any
bridge or bridges,the same shallbe constructed so as not
to interferewith or obstruct the free navigation of any
navigable stream .
§ 10. That upon the application of said company , or
its agents , to the county courts of the several counties
through which the road or itsbranches may be surveyed ,.
such court shallappoint a civilengineer,and two impar-
tialcommissionerswhose duty itshallbe totake from the
owners and proprietorsof land , or those having an inter-
est therein, and through which itis proposed that said.
road or any part thereof shall pass, a grant of the use
and rightof way through the same ,and of grounds for
depots, warehouses , machine shops , and buildings; and
said engineers and commissioners ,or eitherof them ,shall
have the same power to take acknowledgments of the
grantors that clerks of the county court now have ; also
to take privyexaminations of married women ,and cer-
tifysuch acknowledgments ; and it shall be the duty of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY , 1417
the county clerk to receive and record the said grants and
certificates,the same as other deeds ; and grants so cer-
tifiedand recorded shallvest in said company allthe
rightsthereby intended or expressed; and any or all.
grants to the said company may be for the fee of the
lands granted , any lesserestate, and may include the full
and absolute right and disposal of all the stone, earth,
gravel,timber ,fuel,and materials so granted to said com-
pany : Provided, That said company may acquire said
grants by contract or purchase on such terms as may be
agreed upon with the proprietors: Provided, That the
county court clerk or his deputy shallbe one of the com-
missioners appointed by the court : And provided further,
That the commissioners shallreceive fivedollars per day
for theirservices,in addition to the legal fees of the clerk,
for taking acknowledgments of sale, rights of way ,& c.,
allof which fees shallbe paid by the officersor agents of
said company ; and the money to pay the commissioners
and clerk shallbe deposited with the court, if demanded
by the commissioners , before they act or serve as herein
provided; and no one but the clerkor his deputy shall
have authority to take acknowledgments or conduct
privy examinations .
§ 11. That in allcases where the owners and proprie-
tors of lands shallfailor refuse to grant or sellthe right
of way , or grounds for the depots , warehouses , work-
shops ,side-tracks,turnouts,bridges,& c.,required for said
road ,it shall be lawful for said engineer and commission-
ers, or any two of them ,and it shallbe theirduty,from
theirown views , and such proof as shallbe presented,to
value the land requiredfor the road separately,and the
advantage the road willbe to the adjoining lands of the
proprietor separately,and the disadvantage to the adjoin-
ing lands separately,and make a report of the same , to-
gether with a description of the land so required,and a
map of the road through such county ,to the clerk of the
county court of such county , with the names of the own-
ers of such land , and their residence if known , and
whether they are adults or infants; and it shallbe the

1418 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
duty of the clerkof such county court to issue a sum-
mons against all such owners , to show cause why the
grant of the rightof way ,or use of such lands forthe
purposes aforesaid,shallnot be made on the payment of
the balance ,if any , of the value of the land so reported ;
and upon the service of such summons upon the owner ,
if in the county , and ifnot,upon the agent in the county ,
ifsuch owner has any ,ten days,the county court shall
have jurisdictionon said report to order the balance , if
any , of the assessed value of the land to be paid , and a
grant of the right of way to the use of the land , for the
purposes aforesaid,to be executed to the company by a
commissioner to be appointed by the court for that pur-
pose : Provided, That if the owner is out of the county ,
and have no known agent in it,the court shallmake an
order of appearance by a day named , not lessthan four
weeks ,and appoint an attorney to correspond with the
owner and detend for him ; and when the owner is an
infant, or lunatic,the court shall appoint a guardian ad
litemto appear for such infant or lunatic, and make de-
fense; and itshall be lawful for the court to take up the
case as to any one owner who is before the court, with-
out waiting until all are served : Provided, In no case
shallthe actual value of the land taken be set off by the
estimated advantages to the balance of the land .
§ 12. That it shall be lawful for the company or for
the owner to traverse the report,or for both to traverse
it, and claim a jury to value the land for any of the
purposes mentioned in the foregoing section,and to assess
the damages to any of the adjoining lands, and the ad-
vantages the road will be to the same , all of which shall
be returned separately, and thereupon the court shall
cause a jury to be empaneled to try the traverse or trav-
erses,in open court, and under itsdirections;and itshall
be the duty of the court,on motion of eitherparty,to or-
der an actual view by the jury , and under charge of the
sheriff, of the premises required, and to order the dam-
ages assessed by the jury to be paid, and also the dam-
ages to the adjacent land of the proprietor,firstdeducting

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1419
therefrom the amount of estimated advantages so assess-
ed ,to be paid upon the takingof the same by saidcom-
pany ; and the court shallhave the right to grant to either

trialsare ordinarily granted at law ; and either party
may appeal to take writ of error,but no appeal or writ of

structtheirroad or branches : Provided,That if the pres-

§ 13. That so soon as the company shall have com-

ously , they may commence and prosecute theirbusiness
as though the whole work were completed ; and they
shall have the right and power to charge and collectfor

any kind, along said railway or itsbranches ,any sum or
rates which the president and directors may establish
from time to time : Provided, The rates fixed be not in-
consistent with or repugnant to any general law of this
Commonwealth now in existence,or that may be hereaf-
ter enacted .
§14. That the said president and directorsshall, annu-
ally or semi -annually ,declare and make such dividend as

be dividedamongst the stockholdersin proportionto
theirrespectiveshares. But said president and directors
shallannually pay intothe treasury of thisCommonwealth
such tax upon the road and property of said company as
shallbe provided by law ,which tax shallnever be greater
than the rate of tax imposed upon other railroad com-
panies in the State,and to be estimated upon the prime
cost of the road and works ,deducting wear and tear.

1420 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 15. That the presidentand directorsshallhave power
to enact such by -laws ,rules and regulations,not incon-
sistent with the laws of this Commonwealth or of the
United States,as they may deem necessaryand proper
for the government of said corporation; and prescribing
the manner and form in which transfersmay be made of
the capital stock of said company .
§ 16. That general meetings of the stockholders of said
company may be calledat any time in the intervalsbe-
tween the anual meetings of the presidentand directors,
or a majorityof them , or by stockholdersowning at least
one fourth of the whole stock subscribed, upon giving
thirtydays ' public notice of the time and place of holding
the same ,which shallbe at the placethe principaloffice
of the company iskept ,named in such advertisements ;and
when any such meetings are called by stockholders, the
advertisements shall specifythe objects of the call; and
ifat any such meetings a majority in value of the stock-
holdersare not present,in person or by proxy,such meet-
ing shallbe adjourned from day to day without transact-
ing any business,for any time not exceeding three days ;
and if within that time stockholders holding a majority
of the stock in value shall not so attend,such meeting .
shall be dissolved .
§ 17. That at the regular meetings of the stockholders
of said company ,itshallbe the duty of the president and
directorsin officeforthe preceding year to exhibita full
and clear and precisestatement of the affairsand finances
of said company ; and at any called meetings of the
stockholders,a majority in value of the attending stock-
holders may require similar statements from the presi-
dent and directors, whose duty itshallbe to furnishthem
when required; and at any general meeting of the stock-
holders, a majorityin value of allthe stockholdersin
said company may remove from officethe president, or
any of the directors of said company , and fillthe va-
cancy or vacancies so created in the same way , and to
the same extent,thatthey might by electionat any stated
meeting .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1421
§ 18. That itshallbe lawful forthe said company to
unite theirsaid road or branches with any other railroad
company in or out of the State; and saidcompany shall
have power to leaseor purchase and hold any otherrail-
road in or out of the State ; and may agree upon terms
of consolidation with any other railroad company in or
out of the State, or subscribe for stock in any other rail-
road company in or out of the State,now or hereafter
incorporated, with the consent of the presidentand di-
rectorsof thiscompany ; which union ,consolidation,lease,
-or purchase shall and may be consummated upon such
terms and conditions as may be agreed by and between
the directorsof the respective companies ; and the com-
pany shallhave fullright and power to run theirrailway
and branches through or across any highway , without
injuring the same ,or the streetsof any town or city on
the route thereof,and to such point in any city or town
as they may deem advantageous : Provided , Such cityor
town by their authorities consent thereto .
§19 . That said company shallhave the right to estab-
lishtelegraph or telephone linesalong theirline of rail-
road and branches ,and may erect and use any apparatus
for conveying intelligence with rapidity by means of

§20 . That said company ,by itspresident and board of
directors, may contractwith any person or persons or
corporation to construct its said railway or branches ,or

company ,or in the bonds of said company ,or in both, in
such manner and upon such terms as may be agreed
upon .
§21 . That shares of stock in said Cumberland River
Railway Company shallbe heldtobe personalproperty,
and pass as such,and the privatepropertyof stockhold-
ers shallbe exempt from the corporate debts of the com-
pany .
§ 22. The said corporation shallhave no power to in-
terferewith the free navigation of any riveror water-

course over which said road or roads or branches of same

§ 23. This act shallbe in force from and afterits pas-
sage .

ary sixteenth,one thousand eight hundred and sixty eight,

CHAPTER 1584 .
AN ACT to regulate and fix the jurisdictionof the policejudge of the
town of Earlington, in Hopkins county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the jurisdiction of the police judge of the
town of Earlington be concurrent with the justices of
the peace of the county of Hopkins , and not exclusive
jurisdiction,as now provided by the charter of said town ;
and in all cases for a violation of the penal and criminal
laws or by -laws of the town of Earlington,the accused
(or defendant) shall have the privilegeand right to have
his trialbefore the justicesof the peace of his district.
§2 . In all trialsfor infractionsof the by -laws of the
town of Earlington,where trialsare had and finesimposed
by justices of the peace , all such fines shall go to the
town of Earlington, in same manner as if imposed by the
policejudge instead of the justicesof the peace .

§3 . This act to take effect and be in force from itspas-
sage .

and by-laws , rules, regulations, for the government of

1424 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .

§3 . That the capital stock of said company shall be

of the stockholders,each stockholder shallbe entitledto
one vote for every share of stock up to three. No stock-

may work out his road tax at the rate of one dollar per
day under the directionat the time specifiedby the con-
tractoron the precinctto which he belongs,for which the
contractor shall give every person so working a state-
ment showing number of days so worked , which shallbe
good to the tax-collectorin his settlement with the tax-
payer .
§ 4. The electionherein provided for shall be held by
order of the county judge , issued upon application to
him in writingby twenty or more tax-payers of Lyon
county ; due notice of said election to be given by writ-
ten or printed notices posted at not less than twenty
public places in said county , at least thirtydays before
the day of election. And should the majority of votes

cast at the August election,one thousand eighthundred
and eighty-four,be againstsaid tax,then at any regular
annual electionthereafterthe same questionsmay be
submitted as provided for in thissection.

Approved May 10, 1884.
CHAPTER 1594 .

ciates and successors,be , and they are hereby ,created a

§2 . That said company is hereby empowered and

villeto a point on the Todd's Point and Beard's Station
Turnpike,at or near the residenceof George W. Collins;
said road to be located on the most practicable route
between said points; and in order to raisemoney to con-

and to said company ,the stock of which shallbe divided

or subscriptionswork to be done on said road by reliable

1432 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
be counted in estimating the capital stock of said com-
pany ,and shallbe recognized at all meetings and elec-

money . The capitalstock of said company shall not
exceed ten thousand dollars,but may be increased at any
time to twenty thousand , or not exceeding that sum ,by a
majority of the then stockholders voting therefor. When-
ever sufficientsubscriptionshave been obtained in money
or work ,includingany subscriptionthe county of Shelby

§3 . Said company ,by and under the name and style

eral laws of the State . When three miles of said road are

the said right of way and material cannot be obtained by

italstockof saidcompany forany purpose whatever.

allmeetings of the company ,a majorityof the number

CHAPTER 1595 .

in Grayson county, to levy and collecta tax for the benefitof the com-
mon schoolproperty in said district.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the trustees of common school district num-
ber one,in Grayson county,duringthe years one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-four and one thousand
eighthundred and eighty-five,are hereby authorizedto
levy and collectan ad valorem tax,foreach of said years ,

for taxation , under the revenue laws of this Common-
wealth , of not exceeding fifteen cents on every one
hundred dollars' worth of taxableproperty so listedfor

taxationin each of saidyears,and a capitationtax of
one dollar on each white poll in said districtliable for

CHAPTER 1596 .

Road Company .

of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a corporationishereby created,with a capi-
tal stock of not exceeding three thousand dollars per

the purpose of constructing and operating a turnpike
road from a point on the Winchester and Boonesboro
Turnpike Road , at or near McCord's blacksmith shop , to
Elkin'sDepot ,on the extensionof the Kentucky Central
Railroad ,in Clark county , and thence , if so desired by

Creek Turnpike Road ,at a pointto be selectedby them .

and Elkin Depot Turnpike Road Company , and shall

three (3) of chapter fifty-six of the General Statutes of

thereon as isprescribed in said chapter.

owner thereof,may condemn land needed in constructing
or operatingsaid road,as is now provided by law with

itspassage .

CHAPTER 1597 .

of Kentucky :
§1 . That the only costs that shall be taxed in the

ant, upon conviction of a violation of the by -laws and

dollarsfor the mayor ,one dollar for the chief of police,

be taxed unless the cityattorney be present at the trial
and prosecute the case.

§3 . That the mayor shallhave the same power as jus-

School to conferlearneddegrees.

CHAPTER 1602 .
AN ACT to prevent stock from running at large in Berlin precinct,
Bracken county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That itshall[be] unlawful forany person owning
or having care of any stock to permit the same to run at
large in Berlin precinct,Bracken county; and any per-
son so offending shallbe liableto such penalties as are
prescribed and provided for in thisact.
§ 2. That itshallbe the duty of the constable or sheriff
to seizeany stockfound running at largeinsaidprecinct,
and impound said stock, and provide for their care and
keeping until the owner of such stock shall have re-
deemed them by the payment of all fees and costs inci-
dent to said procedure .
§3 . That whenever any stock shallbe found trespass-
ing upon the inclosure of any person ,the owner of such
stock shallbe liablefor alldamages done and for allfees

1440 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and costs incidentto the prosecutionof such trespass,
recoverable before any officerhaving competent jurisdic-
tion; and the partyinjuredshallhave a lienupon such
stock for alldamages ,cost, and fees.
§4 . That the officershall be entitledto the following
fees fortaking up stockrunning at large or trespassing
upon the lands of any one : For each horse, jack , mule ,
or colt over six months old,one dollar ; for each bull,
cow ,steer,heifer,or calf over six months old, fiftycents ;
for each sheep or hog over three months old, twenty -five
cents,and allexpenses incurredby him in impounding
and taking care of the same : Provided, The owner of
said stock is not known to be in immediate pursuitof
same .
§5 . That the officermaking the seizure shalladvertise
allstockseizedby him, ifnot redeemed within fivedays
after the seizure of same ,for sale in the precinct ten
days before selling. The sale shall be made on a credit
of three months , the purchaser giving bond , with ap-
proved security, payable to the officermaking the sale,
who shall be entitled to receive the same commission and
fees as are allowed by law for sales under execution for
debt ; and when the bond shallbe collected,he shall pay
the residue,afterallexpenses are paid ,to the owner of
said stock,and the officershallbe responsible on his of-
ficial bond ,
§ 6. That this act shall be submitted to the qualified
voters of Berlin precinct at the next August election,
and itshall be the duty of the officersof said election to
open a poll,and propound to each voter the question,
"Are you for or against the stock law ?" and registerhis
vote in accordance with his response to said question.
The vote shall be certified and returned , examined , and
compared , as they are required in other elections; and if
itshallappear [from] the certificateof the examining
board that a majority of all those voting for or against
the stock law , the presidingjudge of the county court
shall cause a record of same to be made by the county
court clerk,when the provisions of this act shall take
effect; but ifthe examining board reportto saidjudge

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1441
that a majority voted against said stock law , then this
act shall not be in force .
7. That it shall be the duty of the sheriff, at least
thirtydays before the next August election,in at least
three public places in the precinct,to post notices that a
vote will be taken on the firstMonday in August , one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-four,for or against
the stock law .
§ 8. That thisact shall take effecton the firstday of
October , one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four.
Approved May 10, 1884.

CHAPTER 1604 .
AN ACT authorizing the voters of Owen county to vote at the August
election,one thousand eight hundred and eighty-four, on the proposition
as to whether or not spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors,or any mixture
thereof,may be soldin Owen county as a beverage.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be the duty of the county judge of
Owen county , as soon as practicable after the passage
of this act, upon there being deposited with him a suffi-
cient amount of money to pay forthe printing, clerk's.
fees,costs, and other expenses hereinafterprovided for,
to make an order upon his order-book,directingthe offi-
cers of the election in allthe voting precincts in Owen
county to open a poll at the August election,one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-four, for the purpose of
takingthe sense of the legalvotersin saidcounty upon
the propositionwhether or not spirituous,vinous,or malt
liquors,or any mixture thereof,shallbe sold in said coun-
ty as a beverage .
§2 . It shall be the duty of said county judge to see
that the officersof the election, at the respective voting
precincts in said county , are furnished with poll-books
at the said August election, one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-four,for the purpose of taking the sense of
the legal voters of said county upon the question indi-
cated in section first of this act .
§ 3. It shall be the duty of the clerk of the Owen
county court to furnish the sheriffof said county with
a copy of the orderas made by the judge of saidcounty
upon the order-book , and the sheriffshallhave the same
published in allof the weekly papers publishedin said
county not lessthan four weeks previous to said election,
and to advertisethe same by printed handbillsposted
not less than two weeks before said election ,in a con-
spicuous place in each of the magisterial districtsof said
county .
§ 4. It shallbe the duty of the officersof the aforesaid
election, in each voting precinct, to propound to each
voter who may vote the question, " Are you in favor of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1443
the sale of spirituous,vinous,and malt liquorsin Owen
county as a beverage ?" and his vote shallbe recorded on
the poll-books furnished as aforesaidfor or against as he
directs; the poll-books shall be returned as other poll-
books in said election , and shall be examined and com-
pared by the same officers.
§ 5. If it shall be found that a majority of the legal
votes cast at the election before provided for were given
against the sale of spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors in
said county as a beverage ,itshallbe the duty of the exam-
ining board to certifythat fact, which certificateshallbe
delivered to the clerk of the Owen county court, to be by
him safelykept untilthe next regular term of the county
court,at which time the judge thereof shallhave the same
spread upon the order-book of his court,and a certified
copy of the same shall be evidence that allproceedings
under thisact were regularlyand properly had .
§6 . After the certificateof the examining board , as
above provided for,has been spread upon the order-book
of the Owen county court, it shall be unlawful for any
person to sellin said county any spirituous, vinous ,or
malt liquors,or any mixture thereof, in Owen county as a
beverage ; and any person who sellsany such liquors or
mixture thereof in said county shall, except as provided
in the next section of thisact,be fined in the sum of not
lessthan twenty fivedollars nor more than one hundred
dollars for each offense .
§ 7. If,after the certificateof the examining board as
provided for,has been spread upon the order-book of the
Owen county court,any spirituous,vinous, or malt liq-
uors,or any mixture of either,be sold,given ,or furnished,
at any publicmeeting or gathering in Owen county,to
any personwhilstany businessorproceedingsofa general
nature isbeing performed or transacted,shallbe unlawful ,
and any person or persons having the control or manage-
ment of thesame shall,upon convictionthereof,be fined
one hundred dollarsfor each offense; and allsuch persons
may be prosecutedjointly,and upon the trialno one shall
be a competent witnessfortheother;and ifupon the trial

1444 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
itshallbe proven thatany spirituous,vinous,or malt liq-
uors,or any mixture thereof, were drunk at any such
places, it shall be conclusivelypresumed that the same
was furnished by those having the right to control the
management of said public meeting or gathering .
§ 8. The provisionsof thisact shallnot apply to any
druggist who sellsfor medicinal purposes on a prescrip-
tionwrittenand signed by a regularlypracticingphysi-
cian ; and in any prosecution,when it shallappear that
any spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors, or any mixture
thereof,has been prescribed,the burden of showing that
the prescriptionwas needed as a medicine shallbe upon
the accused ,and no physician shallmake or sign any such
prescription,except the person for whom it is made is
actually sick, and such liquor is absolutelyrequired as a
medicine ; and any physician who makes or signs any
prescriptionfor any such liquors,except as provided for
in thisact,shall be guiltyof a violation of this act,and
upon conviction,shallbe finednot lessthan fiftynor more
than one hundred dollars for each offense; and whosoever
shallsign the name of any physician without the knowl-
edge and consent of said physicianto any prescription
provided in this act, shall be deemed guilty of a misde-
meanor , and fined not less than one hundred nor more
than fivehundred dollars,or imprisoned not lessthan one
nor more than twelve months , or both so fined or im-
prisoned,at the discretionof a jury .
§ 9. If it shall be found that a majority of the legal
votes cast at said election are in favor of the sale of
spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsin the county,then, in
thatevent,itshallnot be lawfulto sellany such liquors
in any of the districtsin said county in which the law
known as the localoption law has hithertobeen voted,
and is at the time of this election in force ; but such law
shallremain and be in fullforce in said districtsjust as if
no vote under this act had been voted .
§ 10. If it shall be found that a majority of the legal
votes cast at the election herein provided for are against
the saleof spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors,or any mix

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1445
ture thereof under this act,then in that event no other
vote is to be taken under this act .
§ 11. This act shalltake effectand be in force from and
after its passage .
Approved May 9, 1884 .

county subject to taxation by virtue of the act to which
thisisan amendment ,to pay to the sheriffof said county ,

and it shall be the duty of the county attorney of said
county to instituteand prosecute said suitsfor said taxes
and damages .

todividethe said fund arisingfrom the taxes upon the

CHAPTER 1606 .

lect an ad valorem tax ..

of Kentucky :

part of the old Louisvilleand NashvilleTurnpike Road

CHAPTER 1608 .

of Kentucky :

Gatlin , M. W. Martin ,and L. C. Lewis , of Murray , Ken-
tucky ; and H. S. Hale , R. T. Allbritton,and L. Anderson ,
of Mayfield , Kentucky ; and J. R. Smith and Geo . C.
Thompson , of Paducah , Kentucky ; and W. M. Reed and
W. P. Lee , of Benton , Kentucky ; and R. L. Ellison, W.
H. Hudson ,and A. B. White , of Paris,Tennessee ,or such
of them as may choose to act, are hereby constituteda
body -politicand corporate,under the name and style of
the Murray Telephone Company ;" and by that name

1450 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shall have perpetualsuccession, may contractand be
contractedwith, sue and be sued, plead and implead,
answer and defend,in any and allcourtsand placesas a
natural person ; said company may have a common seal,
and alterand change the same at will.
§2 . The capitalstockof saidcompany shallnot exceed
three thousand dollars, in shares of twenty -five dollars
each . Said corporate members ,or any two of them , may
at any time open books and receive subscription to the
capitalstockof said company ,and whenever ten shares
of said stock shall be subscribed, they may close said
books, and by reasonablenoticecalla meeting of the
subscribers, and proceed to elect a board of directors
consisting of five persons ,who shall then elect a presi-
dent and other officersthey may think necessary, who
shall hold their officesuntil their successor shall be elected
and qualified. An electionshallbe held on the firstMon-
day in January of each year after the organization of
said company , for the purpose of electinga board of
directors; and if for any cause said electionis not held,
the then officersmay hold over until the next regular
election .
§3 . The president and directorsmay then direct when
and how much of the stock subscribed shallbe paid in.
§ 4. When the amount called for shallbe paid in,then
the president and directorsmay proceed to erect and con-
structa telephone linefrom Murray , Kentucky , to any
point or place within thisState.
§ 5. The president and board of directorsmay make ,
and from time to time change,the rulesand by -laws by
which said company shallbe governed .
§ 6. This act shall take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 8, 1884.

CHAPTER 1610 .

of Kentucky :

amend and reduce into one the severalacts incorporat

ing the town of Blandville,in Ballard county ," be , and is

CHAPTER 1611 .
AN ACT to prevent cattleor stock of any kind from running at largeupon
the Maysville and Bracken Turnpike Road .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any cattleor stock of
any kind to run on or run at large upon any partof the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1453
turnpikeroadowned and controlledbytheMaysvilleand
Bracken Turnpike Road Company .
§2 . Any person or personsbeing the owner of,or hav-
ing the custody or control of any cattle or stock of any
kind ,who shallsuffer or permit any of them to run at
large upon any portion of the lineof turnpike road be-
longing to said Maysville and Bracken Turnpike Road
Company ,shallbe finedfor each and every offensenot
less than one dollar nor more than twenty dollars, to be
recovered by warrant in the name of the Commonwealth

the fine so paid to go to the trustee of the jury fund of
the county in which the offensewas committed .
§4 . This act shall take effectand be in force on the
firstday ofJune,one thousandeighthundredand eighty-
four,and notice of same shallbe posted at each and every
toll-gate on the line of said turnpike road for two weeks
before the day thislaw takes effect.
