CHAPTER 1617 .
AN ACT to regulate the sale of spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors in
Washington county.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That it shallbe the duty of the judge of the
Washington county court,upon a written petitionsigned
by at leasttwenty of the legalvotersof saidcounty,to
make an order on his order-book ,at the next regular term
of his court afterhe receives said petition,directing the
sheriffof saidcounty to open a pollat each and allof the
voting places of saidcounty at the next regularcounty elec-
tion held therein, on August fourth, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-four, for the purpose of taking the
sense of the legal votes of said county upon the propo-
sition whether or not spirituous,vinous , or malt liquors
shall be sold in said county .
§ 2. It shall be the duty of the county court clerk to
give to the sheriffaforesaida certifiedcopy of the order
of the judge of the county court as it appears on his
order-book ,within ten days aftersaid order ismade .
§ 3. Itshallbe the duty of the sheriffto have the order
of the judge of the county court published in some weekly
or daily paper published in said county ,for at least two
weeks before the election ; and also to advertise the same
by printed or written handbills,posted at fifteenconspic-
uous places in said county , for the same length of time ;
and ifthere isno daily or weekly newspaper published in
said county,the printedor written handbillsposted as
before provided for shallbe sufficientnotice. The sheriff
shallhave the advertisement and noticesherein provided
forposted as above provided for within ten days afterhe
receives the order of the county judge , and twenty days
before the election .
§ 4. It shallbe the duty of the sheriffto open a poll at
each and allof the voting places in said county ; and the
electionofficersshallpropound to each voterwho may
vote the question," Are you in favor of the sale of spirit-
uous, vinous, or malt liquorsin thiscounty?" and his

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1457
vote shallbe entered for or against it,as he directs. The
pollsshall be returned as the other pollsin said election,
and shallbe examined and compared by the same officers.
§5 . If itshall be found that a majority of the legal
votes cast at the election before provided for were given
againstthe saleof the spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors
in said county, it shallbe the duty of the examining
board to certifythat fact,which certificateshallbe deliv-
ered to the clerk of the county court; and by him safely
kept until the next regular term of the county court,at
which term the judge thereof shallhave the same spread
on the order book of his court, and said entry of the cer-
tificatein the order-book ,or a certifiedcopy thereof,shall
be prima facieevidencein allproceedingsunder thisact.
§6 . After the entry of the certificateof the examining
board as above provided for, in the order-book of the
county court,it shallbe unlawful for any person to sell
any spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorsin said county to
any person ; and any person who sellsany such liquorsin
said county shall, upon conviction,be fined the sum of
not lessthan twenty fivedollars nor more than one hun-
dred dollars for each offense .
§7. The provisionsof thisact shallnot apply to any
manufacturer or wholesale dealer who ,in good faith,and
in the usual course of trade,sellsby the wholesale ,nor to
druggistswho sellfor medicinalpurposeson a prescrip-
tionmade and signed by a regularpracticingphysician;
but no physicianshallmake or signany suchprescription,
except the personfor whom it is made isactuallysick,
and such liquor is absolutelyrequired as a medicine ; and
any physicianwho makes or signs any prescriptionfor
such liquors,except as provided for in this act,shall be
guiltyof a violationof thisact,and ,on conviction,fined
twenty -fivedollarsfor each offense.
§ 8. The county judge shall not make the order for
said election untilthe persons signing the petitionhave
deposited with him ,in money ,an amount sufficientto
pay for printingor posting advertisementsas provided
LOC . L., VOL . 11-92

1458 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
for,and the fees of the clerk for making entries on the
order-book ,and other legal fees.
§9 . The election herein provided for shall be held at
the time provided for, and not oftener thereafter than
every two years .
§ 10.It shallbe the duty of the circuitjudge to give
thisact in charge of the grand juries of said county .
§ 11. All acts or parts of acts in conflictherewith are
repealed .
§ 12. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 8, 1884 .
CHAPTER 1618 .
AN ACT for the benefit of Dr. I. W. Kincaid .
WHEREAS , When the militia of the State was called into
activeservicein Eastern Kentucky ,in theyear one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-two , one Jno .Hurley , ser-
geant of the Emmet Rifles,was sufferingfrom a gun -shot
wound ; and whereas,Dr. I.W. Kincaid,a regular prac-
ticingphysicianand surgeon of Catlettsburg,Kentucky ,
was calledin to treatsaid Hurley,the company with its
surgeonhaving been calledaway from Catlettsburg,and
Hurley leftthere; and said Dr. I.W. Kincaid paid said
Hurley nineteenvisits, includingmedicines, dressingsof
the wound ; and whereas,thesaidsurgeonmade applica-|
tion to the Adjutant General for payment of hisaccounts
for twenty-fivedollars,which was refused, as there was
no provisionmade by law to pay the same , and no
parthas been paid; wherefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts draw his
warrant upon the Treasurerof thisStatein favor of
I. W. Kincaid for the sum of twenty -five dollars, to be
paid out of any fundsin the Treasury not otherwiseap-
propriated.
§2 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 12, 1884-

CHAPTER 1619 .
AN ACT for the protection of the students of Georgetown College .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That any person who shall sell,lend,or give any
vinous , spirituous, or malt liquors, or any mixture of
either, to any student of Georgetown College, under
twenty -one years of age , other than his own children,
without the special written directionso to do ,specifying
the person by name and the quantity,from the father or
guardian of such student,shallbe fined fiftydollarsfor
every such act.
§2 . That if any person , being the owner or controller
of any pigeon -hole, billiardor pool tables, or any table
similar thereto, shall suffer or permit any student of
Georgetown College, under twenty-one years of age,
without the written permission of the parent or guardian
of such student,to play any game thereon ,such person
so offending shall be fined the sum of fiftydollars for
every such offense.
§3 . That if any person ,being the owner or controller
of any house or room , shall permit or sufferany student
of Georgetown College, under the age of twenty -one
years,to engage in any game of chance in any such
house or room wherein any money or other things of
value iswon or lost,such person shallbe deemed guilty
of a misdemeanor ,and on conviction shall be fined in
the sum of fiftydollarsforeverysuch offense.
§ 4. All finesimposed by this act shallbe recovered
before the judge of the Scott county court,the judge of
the police court of Georgetown , or any justice of the
peace for Scott county , who may hold court at any time
for the trialof such offensesas are specifiedin thisact.
§5 . That on the trialof any person charged with any
of the offensesset out in thisact,proof that such offense
was committed at any time within twelve months next
preceding the date of the warrant ,shallbe held sufficient
to warrant a conviction under the provisions of this act,
and the judge or justiceconducting such trialshallhave

all the-powers to summons witnesses,and to punish in
cases of contempt,possessedby a judge of the circuit
court .
46. All fines[and] forfeiturescollectedunder thislaw
shall be reported to the circuitcourt, and paid over to
the trustees of the jury fund of Scott county as other
fines and forfeitures are under the general law .
§7. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1884 .

§2 . The capital stock of said corporation shallconsist
of one hundred thousand dollars in shares of five hundred
each ; and when the sum of three thousand dollars shall
have been subscribed, the said company may organize
by the election of three directors, one of whom shallbe

The form of the certificates and the manner of their

and a lien is hereby created in favor of the company on

claims,debts, deeds,or demands which the company may

stockholders in the election of officers shall be entitled to
one vote for each share of stock held by them respect-
ively.
§3 . The businessof said company may embrace the
buying,selling,feeding,raising,and dealing in allkinds
of livestock,and such other personaland realproperty
as may be necessaryfor the convenience and profitable
operation of said company .
§ 4. The said company shallhave power to borrow
money when needed in its business,not to exceed at any
time the capitalstock paid in; and may mortgage its

1462 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
property to secure payment thereof, or may issue its
bonds thereof,with interestcoupons attached,and make
the same payable at such times and places as may be
deemed most advisable ; but such bonds shall not bear a
rate of interestexceeding eight per cent.per annum , and
payable annuallyor semi-annuallyas saidcompany may
elect .
§ 5. None but stockholders shall be qualifiedfor the
officeof president or directors; and if any vacancy oc-
cur in the officeof president, itmay be filledby the board
untilthe next regularelection. The presidentor direct-
ors may appoint a clerk or agent to attend to such busi-
ness of said company as they deem advisable, and said
clerk or agent may be required to give good bond and
securitythat he willfaithfullyperform the dutiesof his
office. The presidentshallbe treasurer, and may also
be required to give a good and sufficientbond that he
will faithfullyperform the duties of said office.
§ 6. The liabilitiesof the stockholders shall extend to
and be limitedby the amount of stock held by them .
§ 7. This act shall be enforced from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 9 ,1884 .
CHAPTER 1622 .
AN ACT to prevent and prohibit the sale of spirituous, vinous, or malt
liquorswithin three miles of Hazlegreen, in Wolfe county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful forany person or persons
to vend or sellspirituous,vinous,or malt liquors,or any
mixture of the same , within the corporate limits of the
town of Hazlegreen , or within three miles of the said
town , in Wolfe county , in lessquantity than fivegallons ,
except regularlylicenseddruggistswho sellby prescrip-
tion or for sacramental purposes .
§2 . Any person or persons violating the provisions of
this act shall, on convictionbeforeany justiceof the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
county with competent jurisdiction,be finednot lessthan
five dollars nor more than twenty -five dollars for each
and every offense.
§3 . This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved May 10, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1623 .
AN ACT to amend an act incorporatingthe town of Munfordville, in Hart
county .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an act to incorporatethe town of Munfordville,
approved March the third, one thousand eight hundred
and eighty,be,and the same is hereby,amended as fol-
lows , to-wit : The boundary of said town shall hereafter
be as follows, to wit : Beginning at a point on the north
bank of Green river opposite the southwest end of the
island in said river,just above said town , and running
thence a straight line to the corner of S. M. Payton's
farm ,near the colored church ; thence with Payton's fence
to the northeast corner of the fair ground ; thence a
straightline with the north side of said fairgrounds to a
point two hundred yards west of the railroad; thence
parallelwith saidrailroad,and two hundred yards west of
same ,allthe way to the river; thenceup the riverto the
beginning .
§ 2. In addition to the present power of taxation for
the purpose of keeping thestreetsand alleysof saidtown
in repair,the trusteesof said town are hereby invested
with authority to compel all male persons over sixteen
and under fiftyyears of age to work two days of each
week on said streetsupon two days'written notice signed
by the president of the board of trusteesor the commis-
sioner of streets,to be appointed by said board of trustees;
and any person who ,having been so notified,shallfailto
attend and work , shall be fined not less than two dollars
and fiftycentsnor more than five($5)dollars; but any per-
son who shall furnish an able-bodied person to work in
thisstead shallbe relievedof thispenalty .

1464 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . Any personwho has placed or caused to be placed
any obstruction on any of the streetsor alleys of said
town shall be compelled to remove the same upon ten
days' written notice to do so from the board of trustees,
under a penalty of twenty dollars for each day he or
they shall failto remove the same after notice, to be
recovered by indictment, information,or warrant in the
police court of said town .
§ 4. All that part of establishedpublicroads leading
into said town that are within the extended corporate
limitsshallhereafterbe denominated streetsof said town ,
and are hereby placed under the controland supervision
of said trustees .
§ 5. The fencing, care,and management of the public
grave -yards in said town is hereby imposed upon the
board of trustees, and it shall hereafter be the duty of
said board to have and keep the same securelyfenced
and protected,under a penalty of twenty dollarseach for
each failureso to do ; but they shallnot incur thispenalty
forsix (6) months afterthe approval of thisact.
$ 6. For the purpose of raisingmoney to fenceand pro-
tectsaid grave -yards ,the trusteesmay levy a tax as now
providedby law forworking the streets,or they may ap-
propriatemoney arisingfrom fines,or they may warn in
the hands liable to work on the streets, whose duty it
shall be to assistin working under the same penalty as
for failure to work on streets when warned so to do :
§7 . When the officeof sheriffis vacant , the marshal
of the town of Munfordville shallhave power and author-
ityto execute bench warrants , warrants of arrestforwit-
nesses, take bail-bonds ,serve subpœnas ,summons ,orders
of attachment , notices to take depositions, to execute
capiasprofines,levyexecutionson personalproperty,sell
the same,and take replevinbonds in allpartsof the
county of Hart ; and his acts shallbe as valid as if done
by the sheriffhimself.
§ 8. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 8, 1884.

CHAPTER 1624 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheThomas James & Co.Teaming Company .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That Thomas James ,Samuel Roberts ,and Frank
W. Quast be,and they are hereby,createda body -politic
and corporate, under the name of " The Thomas James
& Co. Teaming Company ;" and in and by that name
may sue and be sued, plead and be impleaded, in all
the courts in this Commonwealth and elsewhere ; and to
contract and be contracted with ; to make and use a
common seal,and to alter or renew the same at pleas-
ure; and to have perpetualsuccession.
§2 . The general nature of the business of said corpo-
ration shall be that of purchasing and owning of carts,
wagons ,and other vehicles,and horses,mules, and har-
ness, and of the hiring out of the same for compensa-
tion; and of hauling coal, stone, and other substances
for persons and corporations ; and it may also extend to
such purchases and sales of personal and real property
as may ,from time to time ,be deemed proper ,including
the purchasing ,leasing, or renting of real estate,and of
erecting such buildings and stables thereon as will be
necessaryforthe carryingon of saidbusiness. The prin-
cipal place of business of said corporation shall be at
Louisville,Kentucky .
§3 . The amount of capitalstock authorized is twenty-
fourhundred ($2,400) dollars,and the same shallbe fully
paid up in money , or in propertyhaving that market
value,before said corporation shall commence business ;
and paid up stock shall be issued therefor. Said stock
to be valued at one hundred dollarsper share. The cap-
italmay hereafterbe increased to a sum not exceeding
twenty thousand ($20,000) dollarsby a vote of three
fourths(1) of allstock.
§4 . The affairsof the corporation shall be conducted
by a board of not less than three (3) nor more than five
(5) directors. The board of directors shall consist of a
president, secretary, treasurer,and general manager , to

1466 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
perform dutiesusuallyincidentto such offices. One or
more of these officersmay be combined in one person ,if
the board of directors see fit to combine said offices in
one person . The present corporators are declared to be
the firstboard of directors, and they shall hold their
officesas such until theirsuccessorsare duly elected and
qualified. The electionof directorsshallbe held on the
firstMonday in March in each year ,the firstelection
being in one thousand eight hundred and eighty-five;
each share of stock being entitled to one vote .
§5 . Said corporationmay , at any time,be dissolved
by a vote of the majority of allof itsstock.
§6 . The highest amount of indebtedness to which the
corporation shall at any one time subject itseltis fixed
at two thirds(3) of itspaid up capitalstock.
§7. The privatepropertyof stockholdersshallbe ex-
empt from any and allliabilityforthedebtsof said cor-
poration .
§ 8. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved May 10, 1884 .

CHAPTER 1626 .
AN ACT for the benefitof T. B. Waller,of Marshall county .
WHEREAS , T. B. Waller, of Marshall county, did,on
September first,one thousand eighthundred and eighty-
three, apply for and obtain a licenseto keep a tavern at
Gilbertsville, Kentucky; and whereas,on or aboutthe
firstday of October , one thousand , eight hundred and
eighty-three, his tavern building, with all its contents,
were destroyedby fire,therebydeprivinghim of any fur-
ther use or benefit of said license ; therefore ,
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor is hereby instructed to draw his
warrant on the Treasurer in favor of T. B. Waller for the
sum of forty-fivedollarsand eightycents,the amount of
fee for licenseof which he was deprived of using.
§2 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
[Became a law without the signatureof the Governor .]

CHAPTER 1633 .
AN ACT definingand declaringwho are the legal heirs of John Small-
wood , deceased, and empowering them to take from him by descent.
WHEREAS , John Smallwood , lateof Union county,has
departed this life at an advanced age ,without wife or
child,or fatheror mother ,or brother or sister,but leaving
kinsfolk, the descendants of his brothers and sistersby
the same mother ; and whereas , it is claimed that said
Smallwood was a bastard ; now ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the descendants of the brothers and sistersof
said John Smallwood from the same mother with him
are hereby declared to be his legal heirs at law ,and may
take and have by descent from said Smallwood , as if
said Smallwood and his said brothers and sisters had
been born in lawful wedlock of the same parents.
§2 . That thisact be in forcefrom and afteritspas-
sage .
[Became a law without the signatureof the Governor .]

CHAPTER 1637 .
AN ACT to regulatethe jurisdictionof the police court of the city of
Lebanon .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That from and after the passage of this act the
police court of the cityof Lebanon shall have concur-
rent jurisdictionwith the Marion circuit court, with or
without indictment, of all misdemeanors committed
within the county of Marion .
§ 2. The provisions of an act,entitled"An act to con-
fer on quarterlycourtsjurisdictionof misdemeanors in
certaincases," approved March eighteenth, one thousand
eighthundred and seventy-six,shallapply to transferof
cases from the circuitcourt to the police court of Leba-
non , and shall regulate the mode of trialand duties of
the police judge and city attorney in such cases ; and
casestransferredfrom thecircuitcourtto thepolicecourt,
by virtue of the aforesaid act, shall be triable at any
time ; and for the trial of other criminal causes under this
act said court shall hold regular monthly terms at the
same times as now designated for the trialof civilbusi-
nessin said courtby the charterof the cityof Lebanon .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1477
§3 . Articlesthree, four, five, and six, of chapter sixty-
two , of the General Statutes, and articlefour,of chapter
six of the Criminal Code of Practice,are enacted as part
hereof,and shall apply alone to the trialof causes by
virtue of this act .
§4 . Allfines and forfeitures,less legal fees and com-
missions, collectedor recovered by said police judge,
shall be reported to the circuitcourt, and paid to the
trustee of the jury fund of Marion county , except fines
or forfeituresfor breaches within the city limits of the
city ordinances .
§ 5. The police judge shall be allowed ,as compensa-
tion for his services, the same fees as are allowed circuit
court clerks in such cases .
§ 6. All acts or part thereof in conflictherewith are
repealed .

Approved May 9 , 1884.
CHAPTER 1638 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof John Curry , of Powell county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That John Curry , of Powell county ,be ,and he is
hereby , authorized to peddle without licensein any or all
of the counties of this Commonwealth for a period of
five years : Provided, That nothing in this act shall be
construed to allow said Curry to sellspirituous, vinous ,
or malt liquors.
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from and
afteritspassage .
[Became a law withoutthesignatureof theGovernor.]

1478 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1639 .
AN ACT for the benefit of J. H. Vandover , late clerk of the Monroe
county court .
Be it enactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That J. H. Vandover , late clerk of the Monroe
county court, be,and he is,allowed the furthertime of
two years to distrainfor and collect his uncollected fee-
bills .
§2 . That nothing in this act shall be construed to
exempt said Vandover from any of the penaltiespre-
scribedby law forcollectingillegalfee-bills.
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
[Became a law withoutthesignatureof theGovernor.]
CHAPTER 1640 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof John R. Proctor, of Anderson county .
WHEREAS , John R. Proctor, of Anderson county, is a
cripple,and has been sincehisbirth,so he isunable todo
any manual labor,and hisfatherbeing a very poor man
with a large family, and unable to give thisyoung man
money sufficientto educate him , and as he is desirous to
obtain an education that will qualifyhim for a clerkship,
he respectfullypetitionsthe Legislatureto grant him the
privilegeof peddling goods in thisCommonwealth with-
out license ; therefore ,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That John R. Proctor,of the county of Anderson ,
be , and he is hereby ,granted the privilege of peddling
goods and merchandise in this Commonwealth without
license : Provided , That in no instance and under no cir-
cumstances shallhe be allowed to sellwhisky or any
mixture thereof .
§2 . This act shall take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
[Became a law without the signatureof the Governor .]
